title: "Bum" (Stuffed Cabbage Ball)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 white cabbage head
- 1.5 kg prepared forcemeat (serves 12 people)
- butter
- sugar
- coloring
- broth or water
- flour
- sago flour

## Directions

1. Shred a white cabbage head and brown it in butter with a little sugar and coloring
2. When the cabbage is brown, add a little broth or water and make a depression in the center
3. Place the forcemeat in this depression, shaping it as round as possible
4. Pack the cabbage around the forcemeat
5. Cook both together under a closed lid over low heat for 2 to 2.5 hours
6. Make a brown sauce from the gravy, either by cooking butter and flour in it or by thickening with sago flour

title: Aal i Karri (Eel Curry)
body:
## Ingredients

- eels
- salt
- cloves
- 1 charlotte onion
- whole peppercorns
- 1 bay leaf
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1 chopped onion
- 1 teaspoon curry
- 1/2 pound Carolina rice

## Directions

1. Clean the eels and cut into pieces as mentioned above, then cook them in water with salt, a few cloves, 1 charlotte onion, some whole peppercorns, and 1 bay leaf.
2. In a saucepan, cook together 1/4 pound butter, 1 chopped onion, and 1 teaspoon curry; thin the roux with the soup in which the eel was cooked.
3. Strain the sauce, add salt to taste, and shortly before serving stir in 1 tablespoon butter. If the sauce does not taste sufficiently of curry, mix a little more curry in cold water and add it.
4. Arrange the eels in the center of a large flat dish, pour the curry sauce over them, and arrange a border of cooked rice around them, prepared as follows: After 1/2 pound Carolina rice is cleaned and blanched, cook it in as much water as to cover it well. When tender but still whole, strain it, pour cold water over it, and return it to the saucepan so the remaining moisture can evaporate.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Aal i Karry-Sauce (Eel in Curry Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel, skinned, cleaned, and cut into pieces
- Salt
- Water
- Butter
- Onion
- Flour
- Curry
- Boiled loose rice or potato puree
- Onions, roughly chopped and browned

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the eel as for Fricassee (Nr. 510), boiling in salted water with parsley
2. Melt butter and sweat a grated onion in it
3. Brown a good spoonful of flour together with curry to taste in the butter
4. Add the fish stock to create a sauce
5. Arrange the eel in a ring of loose boiled rice or potato puree
6. Pour the sauce over the eel
7. Sprinkle the rice or puree ring with roughly chopped browned onions

title: Aal à l'Anglaise (Eel English Style)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Skinned and cleaned eel, cut into 5-8 centimeter pieces
- Salt
- Water
- Parsley
- Horseradish, grated
- Butter, melted
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Place the prepared eel pieces in boiling salted water with a good bunch of parsley
2. When cooked, arrange on a serving platter garnished with grated horseradish sprinkled with chopped parsley
3. Serve with melted butter and boiled potatoes

title: Aale-Frikassé (Eel Fricassee)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Skinned, cleaned eel, cut into pieces
- Water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolks
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned eel pieces in boiling salted water with a bunch of parsley, using only as much water as necessary to make a stronger sauce
2. Strain the cooking liquid and keep the eel warm
3. Make a roux with butter and flour, then add the fish stock to create a smooth sauce
4. Thicken the sauce with a couple of egg yolks and add chopped parsley
5. Pour the sauce over the eel when serving
6. Serve with boiled potatoes

title: Aale-Ragout (Eel Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onion
- Paprika
- Flour
- Thin meat or fish stock
- Food coloring
- Skinned and cut eel
- Potato starch
- Sherry
- Simlers or puff pastry strips

## Directions

1. Brown a piece of butter with a grated onion, some paprika, and a spoonful of flour in a pot
2. Add thin meat or fish stock and add coloring to achieve a nice brown color
3. Add the cleaned and cut eel and cook until tender
4. Remove the eel pieces with a skimming ladle and place on the serving platter
5. Skim the fat from the sauce and thicken with a little potato starch if needed
6. Finish the sauce with sherry
7. Pour the sauce over the eel pieces
8. Serve with Simlers or puff pastry strips, or arrange in a puff pastry or potato puree ring

title: Aale-Suppe (Eel Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) eel
- butter
- flour
- a mixture of eel and white meat broth
- carrots
- bunch
- flour dumplings
- cooked apples
- prunes
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. After the eels have been slaughtered, skinned, cleaned, rinsed and the fins cut off as shown in No. 508, cut them into 5 centimeter (2 inch) long pieces, which are cooked in salted water.
2. When the eels are cooked, remove them and keep them warm.
3. Brown butter and flour as for Kræse Soup (No. 372).
4. Dilute it with a mixture of eel and white meat broth, in which carrots and bunch have been cooked.
5. Cut the carrots in half and add them to the tureen together with the eels, flour dumplings, as well as cooked apples and prunes.
6. Season the soup with vinegar and sugar.
7. It must be served very hot.

title: Aalesuppe (Eel Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 til 4 Pund mellemstore Aal (3 to 4 pounds of medium-sized eels)
- Salt
- Vand (water)
- 5 Potter tynd Kjødsuppe (5 pots of thin meat broth)
- Rene, ituskaarne Supperødder (clean, cut-up soup vegetables)
- En halv Snes Peberkorn (half a dozen peppercorns)
- Et Laurbærblad (a bay leaf)
- Skrællede, ituskaarne Æblestykker (peeled, cut-up apple pieces)
- Smaa Kjødboller (small meatballs), optional
- Sukker (sugar)
- Eddike (vinegar)

## Directions

1. Skin the eels, clean them, remove the head and cut off the fins.
2. Cut the eels into pieces about one inch long and sprinkle with salt.
3. Place the eel pieces in a little salted water, bring to a boil, skim well, and drain in a colander.
4. Heat 5 pots of thin meat broth with clean cut-up soup vegetables, half a dozen peppercorns, and a bay leaf over gentle heat.
5. When the vegetables are half tender, add peeled, cut-up apple pieces and, if desired, small meatballs.
6. When these are almost tender, add the eel pieces and bring to a boil until completely tender.
7. Remove from heat and season with salt, sugar, and vinegar to taste.
8. Note: the soup will become discolored if vinegar is added while it is boiling.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Aalesuppe 1 (Eel Soup 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large eels
- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound flour
- 4 quarts strong bouillon
- About 1 pint port wine
- 1 tablespoon chopped onion
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- 2 tablespoons Harvey sauce
- 1 tablespoon anchovy essence
- Salt
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from the bones along the entire length of the eel and cut into pieces of 1 inch square.
2. Brown the butter, onions and flour over gentle heat for 5 minutes and dilute with good bouillon.
3. When it boils, add the eel bones, let them cook for a few minutes, then skim the soup and strain it into a casserole.
4. Add the eel pieces and at the same time add the Harvey sauce, parsley, port wine, salt and sugar.
5. Return the soup to the heat, boil for a few minutes, skim well and serve.

title: Aalesuppe 2 (Eel Soup 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Several small eels
- Clear, strong fish soup
- A handful of chervil
- A handful of sorrel
- A handful of celery leaves
- Butter
- Toasted bread
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clean the eels and cut into pieces and cook in the fish soup.
2. Cook the vegetables separately, then let them drain.
3. Add them to the soup together with a piece of butter and the whisked egg yolks.
4. Place some toasted bread slices in the soup tureen and pour the soup over.

title: Ablekage Ar. 2 (Apple Cake No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 apples
- wine
- sugar
- water
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- 8 eggs
- 8 rusks

## Directions

1. Peel, slice, and cook 20 apples to a pulp with wine, a little sugar, water, cinnamon, and lemon zest
2. Whip 8 whole eggs well with sugar
3. Mix the whipped eggs with 8 crushed rusks and the hot apple pulp
4. Bake slowly, being careful to avoid too strong an oven heat

title: Aborre (Perch)
body:
## Ingredients

- perch
- salt
- boiled peeled potatoes
- melted butter
- parsley
- eggs
- cream
- breadcrumbs
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Scrape the fish on a grater to remove the scales and clean it. If boiled, serve with boiled peeled potatoes, melted butter, and parsley.
2. If they are to be fried, which is best for the smallest ones, fill them with a little parsley and butter, dip in eggs, a little cream and breadcrumbs, then fry hard in browned butter and serve with browned butter and lemon slices.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Aborre-Frikassé (Perch Fricassee)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg perch
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolks
- Sherry or Graves
- Lemon juice
- White pepper
- Mushrooms or oysters (optional)

## Directions

1. Skin and split the perches, remove the backbone, and cut into smaller pieces
2. Rinse the pieces well and let them stand sprinkled with salt for one hour
3. Cook the fish in very little water with salt so the sauce becomes better
4. Brown the butter and flour together and dilute with the fish stock to a white, smooth sauce
5. Thicken the sauce with a few egg yolks and season with Sherry or Graves, a little lemon juice, and ground white pepper
6. The sauce may be varied with the addition of mushrooms or oysters
7. Pour the sauce over the fish and garnish with puff pastry strips

title: Abrikos-Gelé (Apricot Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg apricots, not fully ripe
- 2 1/2 dl water
- 1/2 kg sugar (or corresponding weight after straining)

## Directions

1. Split the apricots in half; remove the pits, and place the fruit over heat with 2 1/2 dl water until it begins to release its juices.
2. Pour the pulp into a scalded and tightly wrung jelly cloth.
3. The next day, weigh the drained juice, and after the sugar has been cooked dry (see Sugar Cooking for Jelly), add the juice to it.
4. Skim during cooking, which lasts approximately 10 minutes, and fill immediately into jelly cups.
5. Jelly may be tied up as soon as it begins to set. White paper dipped in cognac is usually used to place over the jelly, but it may be omitted.
6. Tie with parchment paper.
7. What remains in the jelly cloth is pressed through a sieve or Passermachine, cooked with sugar, and used for Russian marmalade, see No. 146.

title: Abrikos-Grød (Apricot Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg (1 S) dried apricots
- 1 1/2 L (1 1/2 pots) water
- sago flour for thickening
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Rinse the fruit and soak it for 2 days before cooking it in the water in which it has been standing.
2. Thus soaked, it needs only a few minutes of cooking before it is thickened with stirred sago flour, sweetened, and poured out.
3. Serve cold with cream or an inexpensive cream made as follows: to 1 L (1 pot) of sweet or semi-skimmed milk, use 1 tablespoon maizena, which is stirred into some of the cold milk; the rest is brought to the boil, and the thickening is given one boil in it.
4. 2 eggs are stirred with 75 g (15 quintal) powdered sugar, the milk is poured in, and the cream is poured into the pot to be brought to the boiling point.
5. It is whisked well with a whisk, poured out, and flavored with vanilla essence.
6. A slight pinch of salt is stirred in.

title: Abrikos-Is (Apricot Ice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 to 25 ripe apricots
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) powdered sugar
- 3 3/4 dl (1 1/2 cup) water
- Juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Split the apricots and remove the pits and crush them, as the kernels must be cooked with the syrup.
2. Cook the powdered sugar to a syrup with the water, the crushed apricot kernels, juice of 2 lemons, and the flesh of the apricots which has been grated through a sieve.
3. Pour the whole mixture through a fine sieve into the freezing container.
4. If the fruit is not sufficiently ripe, it must first be cooked and the water from this should be used to cook the sugar in.

title: Abrikos-Kompot (Apricot Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 to 20 ripe apricots
- 250 grams (1/2 pund) powdered sugar
- 375 milliliters (1/2 pægel) water

## Directions

1. Split the fruit, remove the stones and crush them; the kernels are cooked together with the apricots, which must not cook longer than until the fruit is beautifully whole when removed.
2. The syrup is reduced and poured over the fruit.

title: Abrikos-Kompot (Apricot Preserve)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Not fully ripe apricots
- 125 grams powdered sugar
- 1.25 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Split the apricots and remove the stones.
2. Prepare a thin syrup by cooking the sugar and water together.
3. Give the fruit only a good boil in the syrup and place in the sulphured jars.
4. Reduce the syrup for a few minutes before pouring it boiling over the fruit.
5. Tie the jars immediately as instructed above.
6. The stones can be crushed, and the kernels can be scalded and cooked for 5 minutes, either together with the fruit or by themselves in a little of the syrup.
7. Some of the kernels can be mixed with the preserve when serving; the preserve keeps just as well without being preserved together with the kernels, which easily cause it to ferment.
8. Another use of apricot kernels is to use them instead of almonds; the stones are crushed as the kernels are needed.

title: Abrikos-Marmelade (Apricot Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe fruit
- water

## Directions

1. Scald the ripe fruit for a few minutes in boiling water until the skin can be easily removed.
2. Split the apricots and remove the stones.
3. Place the fruit in the pot after the bottom has first been covered with water.
4. Cook the jam as previously described, pour it out and cover with paper, then seal.
5. Alternatively, split the fruit, remove the stones and cook the fruit completely; but first the bottom of the pot must be covered with water.
6. Pass the mass through a coarse sieve, then pour it back into the pot to reduce under continuous stirring as previously explained.
7. What remains in the sieve can be covered with water, boiled and used in fruit soup.

title: Abrikos-Suppe (Apricot Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g dried apricots
- 4½ L water

## Directions

1. Rinse the fruit and soak it the day before it is to be cooked in the water in which it has been standing.
2. When the fruit is tender but not overcooked, remove some whole fruits to add to the soup; dust them with powdered sugar and place in the serving bowl.
3. Cook the remaining fruit. Strain the soup, sweeten it and thicken it with sago flour stirred in water.
4. Serve with rusks or baked French bread cubes.

title: Aegge-Sauce (Egg Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1 heaping teaspoon flour
- Juice of 1 lemon and rind of half a lemon
- 1 teaspoon vinegar
- Powdered sugar
- A piece of butter the size of a small walnut
- 1 pint water

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks until thick
2. Add the flour
3. Add lemon juice and rind, vinegar, powdered sugar, and butter
4. Add water
5. Place on heat and stir until it boils
6. This portion is suitable for 4 pounds of cooked fish

title: Afbrændt Vin (Flamed Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart white or red wine
- 12-14 ounces sugar
- 1 stick cinnamon
- 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Mix the wine with sugar that has been dipped in water, the cinnamon, and the peel of 1 lemon. Heat over the fire, stirring until warm, and strain through a fine sieve. Add the two sliced lemons after the seeds have been removed. Instead of lemons, many prefer a few tablespoons of Kirschwasser.

title: Agerhøns med Champignons (Partridge with Mushrooms)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted partridges
- Mushrooms
- Madeira
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Roast the partridges as directed
2. When tender, strain the sauce
3. Simmer some mushrooms in this sauce
4. Season the sauce to taste with Madeira and a little lemon juice

title: Agurke-Salat (Cucumber Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 medium cucumbers
- 2 spoonfuls oil
- 4 spoonfuls vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Let the cut cucumbers stand beforehand, sprinkled with salt, under a light press.
2. Mix together the oil, vinegar, salt and pepper and pour over the pressed cucumbers.
3. Sprinkle chopped parsley on the salad.

title: Agurke-Salat (Cucumber Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin cucumbers without seeds
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Powdered sugar
- White pepper
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers and cut them into thin slices
2. Sprinkle with a little salt and place under a light press for one hour
3. Press off the liquid from the cucumbers and pour vinegar over them
4. Add a little powdered sugar and sprinkle with white pepper and chopped parsley
5. Can be arranged in small hollowed-out tomatoes

title: Agurke-Salat (Salted Cucumber Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- firm, green cucumbers without seeds
- salt
- kitchen salt
- parchment paper

## Directions

1. The cucumbers must be firm and green and preferably without seeds.
2. Peel them and cut into thin slices, as cucumbers are otherwise handled for salad.
3. Sprinkle with salt and let stand until the next day.
4. Partially squeeze out the brine.
5. Pack the cucumber salad tightly in stone jars in layers with a little kitchen salt.
6. Place a light press over them and seal the jar with parchment paper.
7. As you need them, remove the cucumbers and rinse them in fresh, cold water before preparing them with pepper, vinegar, or oil.

title: Agurke-Sauce (Cucumber Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Plain brown sauce
- Small pickled cucumbers, cut in pieces
- Wine
- Paprika
- Sugar
- Salt
- Fresh cucumber slices
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. A plain brown sauce is given a boil with small, cut pickled cucumbers, wine, paprika, sugar, and salt.
2. Slices of fresh cucumbers can be cooked in the sauce, which is then finished with a little lemon juice.

title: Agurkefalat (Cucumber Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large, green cucumbers
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers and cut them into slices as thin as possible.
2. Lay the slices on a plate with coarse salt between them and place a press over them; let them stand for several hours, then press them hard between the hands so no salt water follows.
3. Place them in a dish and add vinegar and pepper.
4. If they must be made immediately before the meal, sprinkle salt on the slices, lay them between two deep plates and shake them strongly so the salt spreads around, then press and prepare them.

title: Agurker (Cucumbers) med raa Eddike
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 store, gule agurker
- Salt
- Koldt vand
- Raa eddike
- Friske laurberblade
- Spansk peber
- Muskatblomme
- Helt hvidt peber
- Charlotter

## Directions

1. Skræl agurkerne, flek dem og udhul dem
2. Ksast dem 24 timer i koldt, helst brøndvand
3. Tag dem op og tør dem
4. Stro dem godt med salt, læg dem tæt inden i hinanden og lad dem stå således igen i 24 timer
5. Tag dem op, læg dem på en sigte for at aflobe, tør dem og tryk dem igen i leder
6. Nedlæg dem i krukker lagvis med nogle friske laurberblade, spansk peber, muskatblomme og et par spiseskeer helt hvidt peber og charlotter
7. Hæld raa eddike på, indtil den står rundelig over agurkerne, og bind dem med blere

title: Agurker (Pickled Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers, large or small
- Strong salt brine
- Vinegar
- Dill seeds or sprigs
- Red and black pepper
- Bay leaves
- Black peppercorns

## Directions

1. Place the cucumbers in strong salt brine
2. The next day, remove them and dry them thoroughly
3. Bring vinegar to a boil and give the cucumbers a quick boil, a few at a time
4. Remove them with a fish slice and add new ones
5. Continue until all cucumbers have had one boil
6. Discard the vinegar
7. As the cucumbers are placed in the crock, add dill seeds (which may be dried), red and black pepper, and some bay leaves between them
8. Pour new vinegar over in the proportion of 23 quarts to one peck small cucumbers, boiled with black peppercorns and poured hot over the cucumbers
9. Add dill on top (or you can have sprigs of dill throughout, as it looks nice when served)
10. Place a cloth over and tie the crock after the vinegar has cooled

title: Agurker (Pickled Gherkins)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized gherkins
- Cold water
- Strong salt brine
- 2 1/2 liters (2 1/2 pots) good vinegar
- 1/4 kg (1/2 skålpund) powdered sugar (Melis)
- 4 pods Spanish pepper
- 2 tablespoons whole yellow mustard
- About 20 small peeled shallots
- A little dill
- A couple fresh bay leaves

## Directions

1. Soak the gherkins in cold water for one day
2. Remove and place in strong salt brine for a couple of days
3. Remove from brine, dry, and cover with boiling vinegar
4. Heat 2 1/2 liters good vinegar with powdered sugar, Spanish pepper pods, whole yellow mustard, small peeled shallots, dill, and fresh bay leaves
5. Bring the gherkins to a boil in this mixture, remove, and place in crock
6. Pour the vinegar with spices over them
7. Cover the crock immediately with cloth and bind with parchment paper the next day

title: Agurker å l'anglaise (Sliced Pickled Gherkins)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Gherkins not yet seeded
- Salt
- 2 liters (2 pots) vinegar
- 50 grams (10 quintals) whole black pepper
- 50 grams (10 quintals) whole ginger

## Directions

1. Cut the gherkins on the bias into slices 2-3 centimeters (about 1 inch) thick
2. Sprinkle with salt and set aside until the next day
3. Drain the gherkins in a sieve until the brine is completely drained before placing in crock
4. Cover after pouring boiling vinegar over them
5. After 8 days, drain the vinegar and bring it to a boil with whole black pepper and whole ginger
6. Pour the boiling vinegar over the gherkins, cover and bind when they are partially cooled
7. These sliced gherkins are suitable for use after a few days

title: Agurkesalat (Cucumber Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- vinegar
- pepper
- oil

## Directions

1. Use white, long cucumbers if possible; whether using white or green, make sure they are not spotted as these are bitter
2. Peel thinly and cut into thin slices across the cucumber
3. Place the slices on a plate, sprinkle with salt, cover with another plate and let stand for one hour
4. Gently press the slices and prepare with vinegar, pepper, and salt, and with oil if desired, added before the vinegar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Agurkeſalat (Cucumber Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large green cucumbers
- Salt

## Directions

1. Peel large green cucumbers and cut into as thin slices as possible.
2. Then place them on a plate.

title: Alliance-Marmelade af Stenfrugt (Mixed Stone Fruit Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar for every 1/2 kg fruit without stones
- Various types of stone fruit such as apricots, plums, peaches, and cherries

## Directions

1. For cherries, remove the stones; for other types of fruit, scald in boiling water until the skin comes off easily
2. Halve the fruits and remove the stones, crush them, and scald the kernels before cooking them together with the fruit and sugar for about 1/2 hour
3. Alternatively, halve the fruits, remove and crush the stones, scald the kernels, and after the jam has cooked, press them through a coarse sieve
4. Add the kernels to the strained finished jam and cook for 5 minutes before filling into glasses, covering with paper, and sealing while warm
5. The kernels may be omitted

title: Alliance-Marmelade af Stenfrugt (Stone Fruit Alliance Marmalade)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) pounded fine sugar to 1/2 kg (1 pound) fruit without pits
- various types of stone fruits such as apricots, plums, peaches, and cherries

## Directions

1. Different varieties of stone fruit such as apricots, plums, peaches, and cherries can be cooked together.
2. Remove the pits from the last-mentioned fruits, whereas the other varieties are peeled or scalded in boiling water until the skin can be easily removed.

title: Alliance-Tærte (Alliance Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 whole eggs
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 3 quarter-pounds blanched sweet almonds, finely ground
- 10 quintals bitter almonds, finely ground
- half pound washed butter
- half pound flour
- 1 egg
- a little white rum
- a little crushed rusks
- vanilla cream
- jam

## Directions

1. Beat the 9 whole eggs well for a quarter hour, then beat again for a quarter hour with 1 pound powdered sugar and lastly with 3 quarter-pounds finely ground sweet and 10 quintals finely ground bitter almonds.
2. Make a butter dough (see No. 186) from half pound washed butter, half pound flour, 1 egg and a little white rum.
3. Line the form with this butter dough, then sprinkle with a little crushed rusks.
4. On the bottom of the form, lay a layer of vanilla cream, upon that a thin layer of crushed rusks; then a layer of jam, so a thin layer of crushed rusks, after which fill the form with the aforementioned mixed dough.
5. Bake immediately at even good heat.
6. Test by inserting a straw; when it comes out dry, the cake is sufficiently baked.
7. When it has rested a little, remove it from the form.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Alliance-Tærte (Alliance Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 whole eggs
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 3 quarter-pounds pounded sweet almonds
- 10 quintal bitter almonds
- 0.5 pound softened butter
- 0.5 pound flour
- 1 egg
- white rum
- vanilla cream
- rusks
- jam

## Directions

1. Stir 9 whole eggs well for 0.25 hour and then again for 0.25 hour with 1 pound powdered sugar and finally with 3 quarter-pounds pounded sweet almonds and 10 quintal bitter almonds
2. Make a butter dough (see recipe 186) of 0.5 pound softened butter, 0.5 pound flour, 1 egg, and a little white rum
3. Line the form with this butter dough and dust it with a little pounded rusks
4. On the bottom of the form place a layer of vanilla cream, over it a thin layer of pounded rusks; then a layer of jam, then a thin layer of pounded rusks, after which fill the form with the aforementioned mixed batter
5. Bake the tart immediately at even good heat
6. Test it by inserting a straw; when this comes out dry, the tart is done
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Amalies Kringler (Amalie's Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 2 pounds butter
- 1 spoonful yeast
- cream, as the dough will take

## Directions

1. Mix flour, butter, and yeast with as much cream as the dough will take
2. Mix the dough together the evening before the rings are to be baked
3. Let it stand overnight in a covered bowl to rise
4. The next day, roll the dough into small rings
5. Bake light brown
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Amalies Kringler (Amalie's Twists)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 2 pounds butter
- 1 spoonful yeast
- cream as the dough will absorb

## Directions

1. Mix the flour, butter, and yeast with as much cream as the dough will take. Knead the dough together the evening before the kringler are to be baked and let it stand overnight in a covered dish to rise. The next day, roll out the dough into small kringler and bake them light brown.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: American-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams powdered sugar
- 250 grams margarine
- 5 eggs
- 10 lemon drops
- 125 grams cleaned currants
- 125 grams sultana raisins
- 65 grams finely chopped preserved orange peel
- 4 teaspoons baking powder
- 875 milliliters semi-skimmed milk
- 1 kilogram Pillsbury flour

## Directions

1. Mix powdered sugar, margarine, and eggs well
2. Add lemon drops, currants, sultana raisins, and finely chopped preserved orange peel
3. Dissolve baking powder in semi-skimmed milk and add to the mixture
4. Stir in Pillsbury flour last
5. Bake in a greased form for approximately 1 hour 30 minutes

title: American-Cake (American Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) powdered sugar
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 lb) margarine
- 5 eggs
- 10 lemon drops
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) cleaned currants
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) Sultana raisins
- 65 gr (13 quintals) chopped, candied

## Directions

1. Mix the powdered sugar, margarine, and eggs well
2. Add the lemon drops, cleaned currants, Sultana raisins, and chopped candied citrus peel

title: Amerikansk Abrikos-Kage (American Apricot Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 125 grams powdered sugar
- 1/4 teaspoon cream of tartar
- lemon or orange essence
- 75 grams flour
- egg whites, beaten stiff
- 500 milliliters semi-skimmed milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 100 grams powdered sugar
- 4 sheets isinglass
- 125 grams cold butter
- 3 egg whites, beaten stiff
- 125 grams dried apricots

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg yolks well with powdered sugar, cream of tartar, lemon or orange essence, and flour
2. Finally add the beaten egg whites
3. Divide the dough into 2 parts and bake in a greased layer cake form
4. Bake only a few minutes until set, then immediately remove from the form and place on the dish on which it will be served
5. Boil the semi-skimmed milk and add 3 egg yolks beaten well with powdered sugar
6. Bring the cream to a boil, remove from heat, and add the isinglass sheets rinsed in cold water to melt
7. Blend cold beaten butter into the cooled cream, then the 3 beaten egg whites, and use this to coat the cakes
8. Soak dried apricots and cook them in compote, sweeten well, and only when cold, coat the cakes with them
9. Place the half fruits with the rounded side up
10. The cakes can be baked the day before, but the fruit must only be added the day the cake is served

title: Amerikansk Sølvblomme-Suppe (American Prune Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g prunes
- 4½ L water
- 125 g small crushed macaroons
- ½ L cream

## Directions

1. Rinse the prunes, soak them for one day and cook them in the water in which they have been standing.
2. Pass the fruit through a coarse sieve, dilute to the specified measure, sweeten and thicken with sago flour.
3. Whip ½ L cream in which 125 g small crushed macaroons have been stirred, and serve with the soup.

title: Ananas-Fromage (Pineapple Mousse)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can pineapple in syrup
- 10 egg yolks
- 175 grams (35 quintals) sugar
- 18 sheets isinglass
- 1¼ dl (½ pægel) boiling water
- juice of 2 lemons
- juice from the canned pineapple
- 10 stiffly beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks well with sugar.
2. Dissolve isinglass in boiling water and add the juice of 2 lemons as well as the juice from the canned pineapple.
3. Cut the pineapple into small cubes and stir into the eggs along with the isinglass solution and the stiffly beaten egg whites.
4. Pour the mousse into a wetted mold, which is turned out at serving and decorated with whipped cream.

title: Ananas-Gelé (Pineapple Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can pineapple
- wine
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Mix the pineapple juice with wine and prepare as Wine Jelly.
2. Layer the fruit cut into small cubes in the jelly and let sit until the next day.
3. Serve either with cream sauce, whipped cream, or garnish with pineapple slices and can be decorated with meringue.

title: Ananas-Is (Pineapple Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled pineapple
- Sugar
- Water
- Juice of 1 or 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Cut peeled pineapple into very small pieces and cook with sugar and water.
2. Strain the syrup, add the strained juice of 1 or 2 lemons and freeze as explained in the previous recipe.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ananas-Marmelade (Pineapple Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar for every 1/2 kg peeled fruit
- Ripe pineapples

## Directions

1. Peel ripe pineapples, cut into slices, and pound in a mortar
2. Cook sugar and fruit together for 25 minutes with constant stirring
3. Fill into glasses, immediately cover with paper, and seal with parchment paper

title: Anchiovis Smör (Anchovy Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound anchovies
- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- ½ pound washed butter

## Directions

1. Remove the spines from 1 pound anchovies and weigh the meat
2. Mince the meat finely and mix with 3 hard-boiled egg yolks and ½ pound washed butter
3. When well combined, spread on toasted French bread

title: Anchiovis-Rouletter (Anchovy Roulettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 hard-boiled egg yolks
- softened butter
- 20 anchovies
- parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Mash 20 hard-boiled egg yolks with softened butter, then take the scraped meat of 20 anchovies, chop it finely, and stir it in. When it is cold, form roulettes from it in the usual manner as in No. 203. When serving, garnish the platter with curly parsley.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Anchiovis-Sauce (Anchovy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 to 8 anchovies
- butter
- 2 to 3 spoonfuls cooked water
- flour
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pound 6 to 8 anchovies in a mortar together with a piece of butter.
2. Add 2 to 3 spoonfuls cooked water and strain.
3. Put butter and flour on heat, bake together and thin with the anchovy mixture.
4. When it has come to a boil, thicken it with 3 egg yolks.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Anchovy Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- French bread
- butter
- broth
- flat anchovies
- poached eggs
- meat gravy sauce

## Directions

1. Slice French bread and bake in butter and broth
2. On each piece place a flat anchovy with the bones removed, and beside it a poached egg
3. Arrange them side by side on a platter and serve with a strong meat gravy sauce

title: And med Oliven (Duck with Olives)
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck
- Olives
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Wheat flour
- Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Prepare and roast the duck as described in Nr. 163.
2. Remove the duck from the pan and add peeled olives to the sauce; it should not boil but only stand briefly over the heat.
3. Cut the duck into pieces and place on a platter with peeled olives distributed among them.
4. Skim the fat off the sauce and brown with flour until it becomes sufficiently thick; add some peeled olives and a glass of Madeira, and pour the sauce over the duck.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Andel Kage (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- half a cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1.5 cups flour
- whites of 3 eggs
- 1 tablespoon cream of tartar
- half a teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Stir sugar and butter first in the usual way
2. Add the rest, and sift the soda with the flour, adding it last
3. Between each cake layer spread a filling made like the coconut jelly cake filling but omitting the coconut
4. Over each layer sprinkle split and blanched almonds approximately one inch apart
5. Bake in layers

title: Annanas-Koldskaal (Pineapple Cold Fruit Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pineapple
- 2 Bottles white Wine
- 1/2 Bottle Sherry
- a little Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut a preserved pineapple into slices, which are cut into 4 pieces, sprinkle with a little powdered sugar and let stand with it for a couple of hours, after which it is poured over with the wine and placed on ice to be properly cold when it is to be served with small Macaroons, Flutes or Ice Wafers
2. The cheap, white country wine can be used for this
3. The cold soup can be diluted with Soda Water or Apollinaris to taste

title: Ansjos- eller Sardel-Sauce (Anchovy or Sardine Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Soup
- Anchovy or sardine butter
- Filleted anchovies
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Brown butter and flour and thin with soup
2. Just before serving, add a good spoonful of anchovy or sardine butter and stir until it melts
3. Cut some filleted anchovies (with the bone removed) into small pieces and add to the sauce together with chopped parsley
4. This sauce can be prepared as a brown sauce by browning the butter, in which the flour is baked, and thinning it with gravy

title: Ansjos-Smør (Anchovy Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat from 15 anchovies or sardelles
- ¼ kg (½ pound) butter

## Directions

1. Pound the meat of 15 anchovies or sardelles in a mortar and pass through a sieve to mix together with ¼ kg butter.

title: Ansjos-Toast (Anchovy Toast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 anchovies
- 50 grams butter
- 2 hard-boiled egg yolks
- Paprika
- Lemon juice
- Bread

## Directions

1. Pound the meat of 10 anchovies, butter, hard-boiled egg yolks, paprika, and a few drops of lemon juice well in a mortar
2. Spread on warm toasted bread

title: Appelsin-age (Orange Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites
- 5 egg yolks
- 2 cups sugar
- half a cup butter
- half a cup cold water
- 2.5 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Whip 3 egg whites and 5 egg yolks separately
2. Stir the yolks with 2 cups sugar and half a cup butter, add half a cup cold water and 2.5 cups flour with 2 teaspoons baking powder, then fold in the whipped egg whites

title: Appelsin-Essens til Drikkevand (Orange Essence for Drinking Water)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin yellow zest of oranges
- Cognac or wine spirits
- 1 kg (2 S) ground icing sugar
- 1/4 l (3 pægles) water
- 25 gr (5 qvints) citric acid
- 1 1/4 l (1 pægl) hot water
- 1/4 l (1 pægl) of the orange essence

## Directions

1. Cover the thin yellow zest of oranges with cognac or wine spirits.
2. After 6 weeks, strain off the extract.
3. Boil 1 kg (2 S) ground icing sugar with 1/4 l (3 pægles) water to make a thin syrup.
4. When cool, blend the syrup with 25 gr (5 qvints) citric acid dissolved in 1 1/4 l (1 pægl) hot water, and with 1/4 l (1 pægl) of the orange essence.
5. Fill into bottles and cork. Can be used in soda water or ordinary cold drinking water.
6. The zest can be poured over with cognac or spirits again, but since the essence becomes less potent, a larger quantity of the sugar syrup must be added.

title: Appelsin-Fromage (Orange Mousse)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 375 grams (¼ pound) powdered sugar
- 16 sheets isinglass
- 1¼ dl (½ pægel) boiling water
- juice of 6 oranges, pressed and strained
- juice of 1 lemon
- 10 stiffly beaten egg whites
- ¼ liter (½ pot) heavy cream for whipping
- orange pieces

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks well with powdered sugar.
2. Dissolve isinglass in boiling water and add the pressed and strained juice of oranges and lemon.
3. Pour this mixture into the eggs along with the stiffly beaten egg whites.
4. Pour the mousse into a wetted mold to be turned out and decorated with whipped cream and with some orange pieces that have been left covered with sugar for an hour.
5. The oranges should be peeled before pressing and the peel can be used for essence.

title: Appelsin-Gelé (Orange Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Half the peel of the oranges to be used
- Juice of oranges
- Corresponding weight of sugar to the juice
- 1 1/4 dl water

## Directions

1. Use only half of the peels from the oranges to be used in the jelly.
2. Rub the outer thin yellow peel on some sugar and place together with the pressed juice in the jelly cloth.
3. Weigh the juice after it has run through the cloth.
4. Cook the corresponding weight of sugar with water according to instructions in No. 93, and add the juice when the sugar has been cooked dry.
5. Cook and skim for 10 minutes, pour into jelly cups moistened with cognac, and tie up when the jelly has set.
6. The remaining peels may be preserved as No. 29.

title: Appelsin-Gelé (Orange Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 oranges (approximately 14 oranges total)
- 375 gr (3/4 S) powdered sugar
- 1/4 L water
- 10 sheets isinglass

## Directions

1. Boil 375 gr powdered sugar with 1/4 L water.
2. Pour in the squeezed and strained juice of 8 oranges and strain through a jelly bag.
3. To each liter of juice add 10 sheets isinglass dissolved in a little boiling water.
4. Peel the remaining 6 oranges, cut into slices, remove the seeds, and layer them in the mold with the jelly.

title: Appelsin-Gelé (Orange Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g (3/4 S) powdered sugar
- 1/4 L (3 pints) water
- 8 oranges, juice extracted and strained
- 10 sheets isinglass per liter (pot) of juice, dissolved in a little boiling water
- 6 oranges, peeled and sliced
- green grapes for garnish

## Directions

1. Cook the powdered sugar with 1/4 L (3 pints) water.
2. Pour in the extracted and strained juice of 8 oranges and pass the whole through a jelly bag.
3. For each liter (pot) of juice, add 10 sheets of isinglass dissolved in a little boiling water.
4. Peel the remaining 6 oranges, cut them into slices, remove the pips, and layer them in the mold with the jelly; but the previous layer must be stiff before a new one is laid over it.
5. Place the mold on ice or set it in a cold place until the next day.
6. Turn out, garnish with green grapes, and serve with cream sauce or whipped cream.
7. Serve with macaroons.

title: Appelsin-Is (Orange Ice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg (1 1/2 lb) powdered sugar
- 1 L (1 pot) water
- Juice of 8 oranges
- Finely grated zest of 4 oranges
- Juice of 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Cook the powdered sugar with the water.
2. When it is cold, add the juice of 8 oranges and finely grated zest of 4 oranges, as well as the juice of 4 lemons.
3. Strain and pour into the freezing container.

title: Appelsin-Koldskaal (Orange Cold Fruit Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Oranges
- 1/4 Kg (1 1/2 lb) Powdered Sugar
- Wine as in the preceding Cold Soup

## Directions

1. Rub the peel of 1 orange on a piece of sugar, which is placed in the tureen together with the pressed juice of 2 oranges, whereas the remaining fruits are peeled, cut into thin slices, freed of the pits and sprinkled with sugar
2. Otherwise treated as the preceding cold soup
3. Serve with Sugar Biscuits
4. Dilute with Apollinaris

title: Appelsin-Kompot (Orange Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oranges
- 250 grams (1/2 pund) powdered sugar
- 375 milliliters (1/2 pægel) water

## Directions

1. Cook a syrup from the sugar and water.
2. Peel the oranges, remove the white pith, slice them, and remove the seeds before placing them in bowls and pouring the syrup over them.

title: Appelsin-Likør med Cognac eller Sprit (Orange Liqueur with Cognac or Spirits)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Thinly peeled yellow zest of 6 oranges
- 1 l (1 pot) cognac or wine spirits
- 1 kg (2 S) icing sugar
- 1/2 l (1/2 pot) water
- Vanilla essence to taste

## Directions

1. Pour 1 l (1 pot) cognac or wine spirits over the thinly peeled yellow zest of 6 oranges and let it stand for one month.
2. Boil 1 kg (2 S) icing sugar with 1/2 l (1/2 pot) water to make a syrup.
3. Blend the syrup with the strained orange essence and flavor to taste with vanilla essence.
4. Pour the liqueur into bottles, cork them, and seal with gypsum.

title: Appelsin-Likør med Cognac eller Sprit (Orange Liqueur with Cognac or Spirits)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peel of 6 oranges (thinly peeled, yellow part only)
- 1 liter cognac or spirits

## Directions

1. Peel the thin yellow outer layer of 6 oranges
2. Pour 1 liter of cognac or spirits over the peel

title: Appelsin-Likør med Rom (Orange Liqueur with Rum)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Thinly peeled yellow zest of 8 oranges
- 2 l (2 pots) white rum
- 1 kg (2 S) powdered sugar
- 1/2 l (1/2 pot) water

## Directions

1. Place the thinly peeled yellow zest of 8 oranges in a jar and pour 2 l (2 pots) white rum over it. Tie the jar and set it in the warmth for 3 to 4 days.
2. Strain the rum through a clear but fairly tight piece of cloth.
3. Prepare a syrup by boiling 1 kg (2 S) powdered sugar and 1/2 l (1/2 pot) water together.
4. Pour the syrup over the rum, then fill into dry bottles, cork them, and seal with gypsum or lacquer.

title: Appelsin-Likør med Rom (Orange Liqueur with Rum)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peel of 8 oranges (thinly peeled, yellow part only)
- 2 liters (2 pots) white rum
- 1 kg (2 pounds) lump sugar
- 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) water

## Directions

1. Place the thinly peeled yellow peel of 8 oranges in a crock
2. Pour 2 liters (2 pots) of white rum over the peel
3. Tie the crock closed and place in warmth for 3 to 4 days
4. Strain the rum through a clear but fairly tight piece of cloth
5. Make a syrup by boiling 1 kg (2 pounds) of lump sugar with 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) of water
6. Pour the syrup over the strained rum
7. Fill into dry bottles, cork them, and seal with plaster or lac
8. See rules for sealing and storing preserves

title: Appelsin-Limonade (Orange Lemonade)
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- spirits
- 1½ pounds fine sugar
- 2 pots boiling water
- 4 quintals citric acid
- 2 drams orange essence
- 14 quintals cochineal drops

## Directions

1. Peel oranges very thinly and place the peel in a bottle in which you pour so much spirits that it stands somewhat over the peel
2. Let it stand corked for 3 months to infuse
3. When you want to make the lemonade, dissolve 1½ pounds fine sugar in 2 pots boiling water with 4 quintals citric acid
4. When it is dissolved and cooled, add 2 drams of the orange essence with 14 quintals cochineal drops
5. This lemonade is especially suitable for children
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Appelsin-Marmelade (Orange Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oranges
- 2 fresh bitter oranges
- 1 lemon
- At least 1/4 kg powdered sugar per 1/2 kg fruit meat
- 1/4 liter of the last cooking water in which the fruit was boiled

## Directions

1. Cook the fruit in water until completely soft so it can easily be pierced with a straw or stick
2. Change the water 2 to 3 times during cooking, which lasts about one hour
3. Dry the fruit and cut the yellow peel as thin as possible, then cut into thread-fine strips
4. Remove the bitter white pith and cut the fruit open, carefully removing the seeds
5. Weigh the fruit meat with the juice
6. Cook sugar and water for 10 minutes, then add the orange mixture and cook for another 10 minutes
7. Finally add the finely cut peel and cook the jam for 10 minutes more until ready to pour into glasses and seal
8. The best time for making this jam is February, March, or April when fresh bitter oranges are available

title: Appelsin-Sauce (Orange Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Dl (1/2 Pægl) water
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 tb) powdered sugar
- Zest of 3 oranges
- Juice of 6 oranges
- Graves or Rhine wine
- A very little arrowroot flour

## Directions

1. Make a sugar syrup cooked from 1 1/2 Dl (1/2 Pægl) water and 1/4 Kg (1/2 tb) powdered sugar
2. Stir in grated zest of 3 and juice of 6 oranges together with Graves or Rhine wine to taste
3. The sauce can be thinned with water if desired
4. Smooth it with a very little mixed arrowroot flour and can be served both warm and cold

title: Appelsiner med Makroner og Flødeskum (Oranges with Macarons and Whipped Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 oranges
- sugar
- 20 macarons
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) heavy cream

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the oranges, removing the seeds.
2. Sprinkle with sugar and let sit for one hour.
3. Layer the orange slices in a glass bowl with 20 macarons and let sit for 1/2 hour so the macarons soften slightly.
4. Just before serving, top with whipped cream made from 1/2 L (1/2 pot) heavy cream.

title: Appelsingelée (Orange Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Zest of 5 oranges
- ½ pound white sugar
- Juice of 5 oranges
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 2 pints water
- 2 pints white wine
- 12 quarters dissolved isinglass
- 3 to 4 beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Rub the zest of 5 oranges on ½ pound white sugar.
2. Strain the juice of 5 oranges and 2 lemons and pour into a saucepan with 2 pints water, 2 pints white wine, 12 quarters dissolved isinglass, and 3 to 4 beaten egg whites.
3. Set over a brisk heat and bring to a boil, lift off for a moment, bring to a boil again, and repeat this a third time, and after skimming it should appear clear.
4. Pour it through a filter bag to clarify and fill what drains into clear glass bowls, which are set on ice or in a cold place.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Appelsinkage (Orange Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites
- 5 egg yolks
- 2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup cold water
- 3 1/2 cups flour
- 2 tablespoons baking powder
- rind of 1 orange with all juice on a spoonful

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites and yolks separately.
2. Stir the yolks with 2 cups sugar and 1/2 cup butter.
3. Add 1/2 cup cold water and then 3 1/2 cups flour with 2 tablespoons baking powder and the beaten whites and grated orange rind with juice on a spoonful.
4. Bake in two square pans.
5. For the filling: beat 2 egg whites with 2 small cups sugar and the remaining orange juice.
6. When the cake is cold, spread the filling between the layers and over the top.

title: Appelsinkage med Mandeldejg (Orange Cake with Almond Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oranges
- Powdered icing sugar
- Half a pound clarified butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered icing sugar
- 1 pound sweet almonds, crushed with 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Remove the peel and white pith from 6 oranges and divide into small pieces.
2. Lay the pieces on the bottom of a pan and sprinkle with powdered icing sugar.
3. Whisk the clarified butter to a smooth paste and add 4 egg yolks, 1 pound powdered icing sugar, and the sweet almonds crushed with 2 whole eggs.
4. Mix well together and finally fold in the whipped egg whites.
5. Pour the dough over the oranges.
6. Place the pan in the oven and bake the cake at good heat from above.
7. Serve with whipped cream.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Appelsinsaft til Tø (Orange Juice for Tea, Russian Style)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Orange peel
- Powdered sugar (1/4 kg or 1/2 lb powdered sugar or farina for juice of 3 oranges)

## Directions

1. Rub the orange peel on a piece of sugar
2. Press out the juice, strain it through a fine sieve into the saucepan and boil it with the sugar for at most 5 minutes
3. Skim it and reheat it the next day for another boiling
4. Pour the juice into bottles and seal them while still warm

title: Appelsintærte (Orange Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered icing sugar
- Half a pound butter
- 1 pound flour
- Zest of 5 lemons
- Egg whites, whipped to stiff peaks
- Orange or lemon cream (prepared as Nr. 273)
- White sugar glaze with orange juice
- Orange slices
- Powdered sugar glaze for decoration

## Directions

1. Whisk the egg yolks with 1 pound powdered icing sugar.
2. Melt half a pound butter in a warm oven and sift 1 pound flour on white paper.
3. When the sugar and eggs are whisked completely white, add the warm butter, then the flour, and finally the whipped egg whites and the grated zest of 5 lemons.
4. Cut paper to fit the pan size as molds, crimp the edges and brush with cold butter.
5. Distribute the mixture into these molds and bake until light brown.
6. Between the layers from the molds, place the orange or lemon cream.
7. Carefully trim the edges and cover the entire tart with a glaze of white sugar dissolved in water and mixed with a little orange juice.
8. Place in the oven to dry.
9. Dry orange slices (with seeds removed) in a lukewarm oven, glaze them by dipping with a thin pointed stick into boiled white sugar, and when dry, arrange in a ring on the outer edge of the cake's top layer.
10. Decorate the whole cake with decorative sugar glaze in figures, in whose larger spaces you can place jelly.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Appelsín-Gelee (Orange Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce gelatine
- 1/2 pint cold water
- juice of 3 lemons
- 2 pounds sugar
- 1 quart boiling water
- 1 pint orange juice
- 3 oranges

## Directions

1. Let 1 ounce of gelatine stand for one hour in 1/2 pint of cold water.
2. Then add the juice of 3 lemons, 2 pounds of sugar, and 1 quart of boiling water.
3. When everything is dissolved, add 1 pint of orange juice.
4. Strain carefully and set on a cold place, preferably on ice, until it is to be used.

title: Apple Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- Cider
- All sorts of apples, if only they are juicy
- Cinnamon and cloves (optional)

## Directions

1. Fill a kettle with cider and let it boil until about one-third is boiled away.
2. Peel the apples, cut them into small pieces, free them from the cores and put so many in the kettle that the cider covers them.
3. Let it boil evenly with frequent stirring. When the apples are fully soft, take them up with a fish slice so that no juice comes with.
4. Put a new batch of chopped apples in the cider in the same way as before, and treat them in the same manner.
5. Then remove the kettle from the fire and pour everything into a spacious crock, where it stands until the next day under a well-fitting lid.
6. Then pour it back into the kettle and cook again with constant stirring until it is brown and like soft soap. Can be seasoned with cinnamon and cloves to taste.
7. Pour into stone crocks and will, in a dry, cool place, keep through the winter.

title: Apple Pie (Æble Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Lemon juice
- Pie dough
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. The apples for this should preferably be stewed like cooked apples.
2. Lemon juice is dripped between them; sometimes they are also merely cut into slices, but the first is to be preferred, because the pie in the latter case is apt to become too hard.
3. When the plate is lined with dough, spread a thick layer of apples over, and a lid of dough is spread over again.
4. Holes are pricked here and there in the lid so the steam can escape.
5. Just before it is done, take it out a moment, sprinkle with powdered sugar, and set in for 5 minutes.

title: Apple Slices Fried in Batter
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping cup flour
- 2 large spoonfuls butter, clarified
- 1 gill ale
- as much milk
- 4 eggs, whites whipped and stirred in firmly
- apples

## Directions

1. Mix 1 heaping cup flour with 2 large spoonfuls clarified butter, 1 gill ale, and as much milk
2. Whip the egg whites and stir them in firmly
3. Peel and core the apples, cut them into slices
4. Give them a quick boiling in sugar and water
5. Dip each slice in the batter and cook

title: Apricots (Abrikoser)
body:
## Ingredients

- barely ripe apricots
- 3/4 pot cold water per pound of fruit
- as many pounds of powdered sugar as the fruit weighs
- as many measures of water as sugar
- juice of one lemon
- a little brandy or spirits

## Directions

1. The fruit must be barely ripe and dried before placing in a preserving kettle with 3/4 pot cold water per pound.
2. When the apricots are tender, remove them and lay on clean linen to dry.
3. Cut them in half, remove the stones, and peel the skin if desired.
4. Cook equal parts powdered sugar and water with the sugar measure of water into a syrup, skim well, and add the juice of one lemon.
5. Cook the fruit in the syrup until clear, then remove and place in a preserving jar.
6. Pour the syrup over them and add a little brandy or spirits on top.
7. Note: Apricots and peaches tend to ferment quickly and require frequent monitoring, and if necessary must be cooked again with more sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Arme Riddere (Poor Knights)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of French bread
- milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter or Palmin
- jam

## Directions

1. Dip slices of French bread in milk and let them stand covered for 1/2 hour.
2. Before frying, sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon, dip in egg and bread crumbs, and fry on a pan in butter or Palmin.
3. Serve hot, covered with jam.

title: Arrak-Sauce (Arrak Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a liter (1/2 Pot) milk
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon arrowroot flour
- 5 tablespoons arrak

## Directions

1. Boil half a liter (1/2 Pot) milk and whisk it into 4 egg yolks, which are whisked white with 4 tablespoons powdered sugar and 1 teaspoon arrowroot flour
2. Place the sauce back over heat and blend with 5 tablespoons arrak and whisk well until it is smooth, but without boiling

title: Artis-Suppe (Root Vegetable Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large carrots
- 1 celery
- 2 leeks
- 1 small parsnip or parsley root
- 4 medium potatoes
- 1 parsnip
- 2 ounces butter
- Salt
- A few whole peppercorns
- 2.5 quarts water

## Directions

1. Cut all the vegetables into pieces, approximately one inch long and of the thickness of a finger.
2. After the vegetables are washed, drain the water from them on a colander.
3. Put 2 ounces of butter on the fire; when it is melted, add the vegetables and cook for a quarter hour with constant stirring, so that it does not brown.
4. Then add salt, a few whole peppercorns and 2.5 quarts of water, but only 0.5 quart at a time, and in such a way that it boils between each addition.
5. In total the soup must cook for about two hours, and to prevent it from boiling over, it must simmer evenly and with a lid on.
6. If you find that there are too many vegetables, some of them can be held back during serving.
7. It is served with toasted bread and is for 3 to 4 persons.

title: Artiskokbunde (Artichoke Hearts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Lightly salted water
- Lemon juice
- Salt (10 gr or 2 Kvint per 1 liter)
- Cold water

## Directions

1. After the artichokes have been treated as instructed, remove the flower parts from the bases
2. Give the bases a good cooking in lightly salted water with a little lemon juice added to prevent darkening
3. Remove and pour cold water over them and when cooled place them in dry, clean jars
4. Pour over them a cold solution of 10 gr (2 Kvint) salt to 1 liter boiling water, close the jars, place in a pot with cold water and cook for about 35 minutes
5. Cool in the water in which they were cooked, or remove and cover with a woolen blanket

title: Artiskokker (Artichokes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Salt
- Water
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the outermost tips and trim the stems.
2. Rinse the artichokes and place them upside down in a pot.
3. Pour boiling salted water over them.
4. Most of an hour's cooking time is needed.
5. Serve them upside down on a platter with a napkin as warm as possible.
6. Melted butter is served with them.

title: Asier (Pickled Cucumbers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Short, thick, heavy green cucumbers
- Salt
- 5 liters (5 pots) vinegar
- 1/2 kg (1 skålpund) powdered sugar (Melis)
- 50 grams (10 quintals) long pepper
- 50 grams (10 quintals) whole white pepper
- 50 grams (10 quintals) whole yellow mustard seeds
- 6 pods Spanish pepper
- A little dill
- A couple dozen small peeled shallots

## Directions

1. Choose short, thick, heavy green cucumbers for pickling
2. Peel them, split lengthwise, and scrape out the seeds with a silver spoon
3. Sprinkle with salt and set aside until the next day
4. Remove from the brine, drain well, and dry in cloth
5. Heat the vinegar with the powdered sugar, long pepper, whole white pepper, whole yellow mustard seeds (wrapped in a clear piece of cloth), Spanish pepper pods, dill, and peeled shallots
6. Boil all of this in the vinegar, then remove after 2 minutes before the cucumbers are added
7. Arrange the cucumbers in a crock and after all are arranged, bring the vinegar to a boil before pouring it over the cucumbers
8. Place the mustard seeds on top of the crock, cover with cloth, and the next day bind with parchment paper
9. Cucumbers pickled this way become hard, crisp, and very durable

title: Asier (Preserved Celeriac)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- celeriac
- salt
- vinegar
- spices

## Directions

1. Peel the celeriac and split them.
2. Remove the cores with a silver spoon.
3. They can be salted in autumn and stand with a light press on.
4. As needed during winter, prepare a smaller portion at a time.
5. Boil spices as for the recipe No. 232 in vinegar.
6. Add the well-dried celeriac pieces just long enough to heat through, then remove them immediately.

title: Asier (Preserved Rowan Berries)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Rowan berries
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Spices (as in recipe Nr. 232)

## Directions

1. Peel and split the rowan berries, and scrape out the seeds with a silver spoon.
2. They can be salted in the autumn and left with a light press on top.
3. As needed during winter, prepare a smaller portion at a time.
4. Cook the spices (as in recipe Nr. 232) in vinegar, then add the well-dried rowan berries briefly and remove them immediately.
5. Bring the vinegar to a boil before pouring it over.
6. Cover the jar with a cloth and bind it with parchment paper the next day.
7. This tastes excellent as freshly preserved rowan berries during winter.

title: Asier med Ingefær (Pears with Ginger)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 gr whole ginger
- ¾ dl water
- ¾ dl vinegar
- ½ kg powdered loaf sugar
- peeled pears

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, split them, hollow them out with a silver spoon and dry them in a cloth, before cutting them across into narrow strips, either with an ordinary knife or with an herb knife.
2. Soak the ginger in water until the next day; then scald, scrape, rinse and cook it together with the vinegar and sugar to a thin syrup, into which the pear pieces are placed.
3. Cook for about 5 minutes and drain on a sieve, and the syrup is cooked until smooth, together with the ginger.
4. The pears are put back into the pot to blend with the syrup, before they are filled into the jar, which is sealed when the preserve has cooled.
5. The ginger may remain in the jar among the pears, or it may be removed and wrapped in a clear cloth to be placed on top of the jar.

title: Asier med Kirsebærsaft (Pickled Cucumbers with Cherry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- Vinegar
- Raw cherry juice
- Sugar
- Cloves
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, hollow them, weigh them and put them on the stove with enough vinegar to cook them in
2. When they have had one boil, remove them, press in cloths and cut into strips
3. If they are small cucumbers, you do not need to cut them into strips
4. For 1/2 pound of cucumbers, take 1 gill raw cherry juice, 2 small spoonfuls of vinegar, 1/2 pound sugar, 5 cloves and some small pieces of cinnamon
5. Put this on the stove and boil until it just simmers
6. Add the cucumbers, and when it boils again, remove them and place in the crock
7. Boil the liquid for a few more minutes and pour it hot over
8. Cover with a cloth and tie up the next day

title: Asier med raa Eddike (Pickled Cucumbers with Raw Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- About 20 peeled, split and hollowed cucumbers
- Spices as in the previous recipe
- A couple handfuls of salt
- Raw vinegar to cover

## Directions

1. Arrange the cucumbers in layers in a crock with spices as in the previous recipe and a couple handfuls of salt
2. Pour enough raw vinegar to cover the cucumbers
3. Place a light press on them
4. Bind the crock

title: Asparges stuvede i Mælk (Asparagus Stewed in Milk)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salt
- Water
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the asparagus into small pieces, and sort the tips separately and cook them a few minutes later in the water, as they otherwise boil away easily.
2. Blanch the asparagus in boiling salted water, and when cooked, place them on a colander so the water can drain off well so it does not thin the sauce.
3. Brown the butter and flour together and add milk to make a white, smooth sauce, which is seasoned with a little sugar and salt.
4. Toss the asparagus in the warm sauce.
5. The water in which they were cooked can be used in soup and will have good effect.

title: Asparges stuvede i Suppe (Asparagus Stewed in Broth)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salt
- Water or asparagus cooking liquid
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolk
- Sugar
- Maggi bouillon cubes

## Directions

1. Drain the asparagus after it has been cooked as described above.
2. Brown the butter and flour and add the water in which the asparagus was cooked to make a smooth sauce, which is thickened with one egg yolk for every half pound of asparagus.
3. Add salt, a little sugar and Maggi bouillon cubes to the sauce.
4. Warm the asparagus in this sauce, without letting the sauce come to a boil.

title: Asparges til Suppe eller Stuvning (Asparagus for Soup or Braising)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled asparagus
- Lightly salted water
- Salt solution (10 gr or 2 Kvint per liter)

## Directions

1. Cut the peeled asparagus into small pieces and rinse
2. Cook in lightly salted water for 5 minutes and pour cold water over them
3. Place in jars and pour a salt solution (as described above) over them
4. Cook the closed jars for 1/4 hour and then place to cool in the pot or cover

title: Asparges à la Pompadour (Asparagus à la Pompadour)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Short-cut green asparagus
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Short-cut green asparagus is peeled, tied in bundles and cooked as in recipe 586.
2. Arrange them on a warm platter with the tips to the same side.

title: Asparges, som Slikasparges (Asparagus as Sauce Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Rinse the asparagus in cold water
2. Peel from top to bottom to remove the woody outer part at the lower end
3. Trim the bottom
4. Tie them in bundles of about twenty pieces
5. Cook in water with a little salt for about a quarter hour until tender, but not so tender that the heads fall off
6. Remove the ties from the bundles and arrange on a flat platter with a napkin underneath
7. If they are barely tender enough, cover them with a warm napkin and let them stand for a moment until done
8. For sauce asparagus, always choose the largest and most tender with light heads
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Asparges, stuvede (Stewed Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Smaller asparagus
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Asparagus cooking water
- Nutmeg
- Milk
- Cream
- Sugar
- Shrimp (optional)
- Shrimp shells

## Directions

1. Peel the asparagus and cut into pieces about one inch long
2. Cook in water with salt or in thin broth
3. The stalks can be cooked longer than the heads
4. Make a roux of flour and butter baked together and thin with a little of the asparagus water, add the drained asparagus pieces with a little salt and nutmeg, or alternatively make a roux of flour and butter baked together and thin with milk and cream
5. The second method is preferable when asparagus is stewed with shrimp
6. Cook and shell the shrimp, crush the shells, bake in butter, cook in water and strain, then skim off the butter, bake with flour, thin with cream and a little asparagus water, add the shelled shrimp and cooked asparagus pieces along with a little sugar and nutmeg
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Asparges-Budding (Asparagus Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 grams margarine
- 225 grams Pillsbury flour
- 1 liter boiling milk
- 14 egg yolks
- 0.5 kilogram cooked asparagus, drained, or equivalent fresh-cut cooked asparagus
- salt
- a small amount of sugar
- egg whites, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. In 150 grams margarine, bake 225 grams Pillsbury flour, which is diluted with 1 liter of boiling milk.
2. When cooled, gradually stir in 14 egg yolks and 0.5 kilogram of cooked asparagus, from which the water has been well drained, or else use an equivalent amount of fresh-cut cooked asparagus.
3. Finally, stir in a little salt, an imperceptible amount of sugar, and the stiffly beaten egg whites.

title: Asparges-Budding (Asparagus Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 kvint flour
- 28 kvint butter
- 12 kvint powdered sugar
- 3 pægle sweet milk
- 12 egg yolks
- a little ground nutmeg
- egg whites
- 15 large asparagus
- salt
- water
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Bake together the flour, butter, powdered sugar and sweet milk.
2. Stir in 12 egg yolks, two at a time, a little ground nutmeg, and finally the beaten egg whites.
3. Cook 15 large asparagus until tender in salted water and place on a colander to drain.
4. Cut each asparagus into 3-5 pieces.
5. Layer the asparagus pieces with the batter in a buttered and breadcrumb-lined pudding mold.
6. Cook the pudding for 2 hours and serve warm with melted butter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Asparges-Gratin (Asparagus Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 190 Gr (38 Kvint) Margarine
- 190 Gr (38 Kvint) Pillsbury-Mel
- 3/4 L (3 Pægle) kogende Mælk
- 9 Æg
- Salt
- Melis
- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) iluskaarne, kogte Asparges

## Directions

1. Smør og Mel bages sammen og spædes med den kogende Mælk.
2. Når det er afsvalet, røres 9 Æggeblommer, lidt Salt, ubetydeligt Melis samt de meget stift piskede Æggehvider deri.
3. 1/2 Kg huskaarne, kogte Asparges vendes varsomt i Dejgen, som derefter hældes i Genstanden, hvori Gratin'en skal bages.
4. Formen må være smurt godt og bestrøet med Rasp.

title: Asparges-Omelet (Asparagus Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- good asparagus
- water
- 12 drams butter
- 15 drams flour
- 1 measure cream
- salt
- sugar
- nutmeg
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Peel good asparagus, cut into thumb-length pieces and cook in water until tender
2. Make a sauce of 12 drams butter and 15 drams flour, thin with 1 measure cream
3. Add the cooked asparagus with salt, sugar and a little nutmeg
4. Stir well together with 6 egg yolks
5. Carefully fold in the beaten egg whites
6. Pour the entire mixture into a buttered and breadcrumbed dish
7. Bake for half an hour in a warm oven
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Asparges-Salat (Asparagus Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled asparagus
- Water
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) butter
- 2 tablespoons oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt
- Asparagus water

## Directions

1. Peel and cut asparagus small and cook tender in a small amount of water.
2. Set aside until cold.
3. Cream 125 grams butter and mix in 2 tablespoons oil, a little vinegar, pepper, salt and enough asparagus water to make the sauce properly smooth.
4. Turn finely cut chicken, veal or lamb meat together with the asparagus in the sauce.
5. Alternatively: turn the finely cut, cold asparagus in a true or false mayonnaise, top with lobster meat and garnish with salad leaves.

title: Asparges-Sauce (Asparagus Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Asparagus water
- Finely cut, peeled asparagus
- Egg yolks
- Lobster meat, peeled shrimp, and crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Prepare a baked white, even, thickened sauce as for false Hollandaise sauce
2. For thinning this use the water in which the finely cut, peeled asparagus has been cooked
3. Serve together with asparagus, lobster meat, peeled shrimp and crayfish tails
4. For sauce for 12 persons use 3 to 4 egg yolks for thickening

title: Asparges-Sauce (Asparagus Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus water in which finely cut, peeled asparagus has been cooked
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolks
- Salt

## Directions

1. Make a baked white, smooth, bound sauce as a mock hollandaise sauce (see No. 777)
2. Use the asparagus water, in which the finely cut, peeled asparagus has been cooked, to thin this sauce
3. Bind with egg yolks - use 3 to 4 egg yolks per sauce for 12 persons
4. This sauce pairs well with lobster meat, shelled shrimp, and crayfish tails

title: Asparges-Suppe (Asparagus Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 L (5 pots) veal or lamb stock, or economical stock
- 1 kg (2.5 lb) fresh, medium asparagus
- 125 g (1/4 lb) butter
- 125 g (1/4 lb) flour
- 1 bunch of herbs
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 spoonful cold water

## Directions

1. Brown the butter with the flour and dilute with the stock in which a bunch of herbs has been cooked
2. Peel the asparagus and cut into pieces 3 centimeters (about 1 inch) in length and cook for a quarter hour in the stock
3. Add the asparagus heads a few minutes later, as they require less cooking than the rest of the asparagus
4. Whisk the egg yolks with a spoonful of cold water and then blend with some of the hot stock before stirring it into the pot with constant stirring
5. Do not allow the soup to boil while the eggs are being stirred in, nor afterwards, as it will curdle
6. If using canned asparagus, use 1 kilo (2.5 lb) tin, blend the water with the stock, and add the asparagus to the pot just before serving
7. Serve with toasted or baked bread

title: Aspargeskildpadde-Suppe (Turtle Soup, Authentic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 L (8 pots) good brown stock
- Paprika
- 1/2 bottle Madeira
- Cognac to taste
- Turtle meat (pre-cooked)
- Whole hard-boiled egg yolks
- Chicken, veal, or fish forcemeat balls
- Sago flour (optional for thickening)

## Directions

1. Reduce 8 L (8 pots) good brown stock without fat to approximately half its volume.
2. Season with paprika, 1/2 bottle Madeira, and cognac to taste.
3. Cut the turtle meat into appropriate pieces and bring to a boil in the soup.
4. Serve with whole hard-boiled egg yolks and forcemeat balls made from chicken, veal, or fish.
5. If desired, thicken the soup with sago flour mixed with water.

title: Aspargeskoldpaddesirup (Asparagus Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.5 L (3.5 pots) good white stock
- 1 kg (2 lb) asparagus
- 100 grams (20 quintal) butter
- 100 grams (20 quintal) flour
- 4 egg yolks
- Sherry or Madeira to taste
- Sugar
- Salt
- 0.5 kg (1 S) cooked medium asparagus

## Directions

1. Wash thin asparagus well and cut into small pieces.
2. Cook tender in 2 L (2 pots) water.
3. Strain the asparagus and pass through a sieve with 3 L (3 pots) good soup along with the asparagus water.
4. Brown 100 grams butter and 100 grams flour together and dilute with the strained soup.
5. When it boils, bind with 4 egg yolks.
6. Season with sherry or Madeira, sugar, and salt.
7. Serve with 0.5 kg of cooked, cut medium asparagus and serve with toasted bread.

title: Aspargessuppe (Asparagus Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pounds cleaned asparagus
- a little water
- a little salt
- butter
- 3 Pots good bouillon
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons good sweet cream

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned asparagus into pieces about two inches long and cook quickly in a little water with a little salt and butter until tender.
2. Dilute with 3 pots of good bouillon and bring to a boil.
3. In a tureen, beat 6 egg yolks with 6 tablespoons of good sweet cream.
4. Under continuous beating, carefully add the soup a little at a time to the egg mixture.
5. Serve with toasted French bread cut into cubes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Asta-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams margarine
- 2 eggs
- 375 grams powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon ground cloves
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- zest of 1 lemon
- 250 grams cleaned currants
- finely chopped candied orange peel to taste
- 250 milliliters semi-skimmed milk
- 500 grams cake flour

## Directions

1. Mix margarine well with eggs, powdered sugar, cinnamon, ground cloves, baking soda, and lemon zest
2. Add cleaned currants and finely chopped candied orange peel to taste
3. Stir in semi-skimmed milk and cake flour
4. Bake in a greased form at moderate heat

title: At afklare Smor (Clarified Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Place the butter on the fire and boil it a little
2. Remove it from heat and let it stand for a while so the salt can sink
3. Remove the foam
4. Pour the butter off carefully so that only the salt remains behind

title: At bage Mel og Brød til Buddingers og Boller (Baking Flour and Bread for Puddings and Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- milk or water (boiling hot)

## Directions

1. The butter must boil before the flour is stirred into it, and the flour must be well baked before the milk or water, which must be boiling hot, is added little by little.
2. Bake it again over an even fire.
3. If there is too little butter to mix with the flour, a little milk or water can be added to the butter before the flour is stirred in.
4. Do not remove it from the fire during baking, as the dough would become heavy.
5. The common sign that the dough is baked enough is when it releases from the pan and spoon, but if this happens very quickly, bake it until the butter shows mostly on the outside and until it becomes lighter and rises again when pressed with a spoon.
6. To be safest that the dough turns out good, when everything is baked, let it bake another hour in the oven, so you only need to turn it occasionally.

title: At farve Boller (Coloring Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal forcemeat
- cooked and strained spinach
- raw beetroot
- saffron solution

## Directions

1. Meatballs made from veal forcemeat can be colored green with a little cooked and strained spinach, which is stirred into the forcemeat before the balls are cooked.
2. Color the forcemeat red by grating raw beetroot over it until the color is as desired.
3. Color it yellow by adding a little dissolved saffron in boiling water.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: At koge Kjød frisk igen (Cooking Meat Fresh Again)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat (raw, cooked, fresh, or salted)
- charcoal

## Directions

1. In warm weather, if meat and flesh develop what is called a 'taint' in kitchen language, place it on the fire in a little water along with some charcoal.
2. Boil for a quarter hour with the charcoal, which will restore the meat to freshness.
3. To prevent the charcoal from leaving marks on the meat, wrap it in a very thin piece of cloth.

title: At koge sur Suppesaft igen (Cooking Sour Stock Again)
body:
## Ingredients

- sour meat stock
- raw or cooked, chopped meat bones (fresh and good)
