## Directions

1. If preserved meat stock from one day to the next becomes sour in summer, boil the soup for a quarter hour or longer if needed with some raw or cooked, chopped meat bones.
2. The sourness will be boiled away by the chalky substance contained in the bones, and the soup will become good and usable.
3. Alternatively, let the sour food boil quite dry with a teaspoon of magnesia, which will remove all sourness.

title: At koge ved Damp (Cooking by Steam)
body:
## Ingredients

- vegetables, meat, fish, or poultry to be cooked
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Obtain a simple, deep tin pot with a lid that fits tightly and well.
2. Place a tin sieve inside that goes only one quarter of the way down into the pot (it must not have a handle or shaft).
3. Fill one quarter of the space below with water.
4. Place the sieve in, add what is to be cooked, sprinkle with salt, place the lid on, and keep the water in continuous boil until the vegetables are tender.
5. The water becomes a strong stock that can be used in many ways.

title: At koge Æggekelvide (Cooking Egg Whites)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- egg whites (beaten to stiff foam)
- sugar (optional)
- vanilla or cinnamon (optional)

## Directions

1. Place milk on the fire in a shallow pan; when it boils, add the egg whites, which have been beaten to stiff foam, one spoonful beside the other.
2. They need only boil a moment to become large, then remove them and place them on an inverted sieve so the milk can drain off.
3. Sugar, a piece of vanilla or cinnamon can be added to the milk, which gives the whites a good taste.
4. The milk can then be strained off and used for egg milk and the like.

title: At lave Glasér-Sukker (Making Icing Sugar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg white
- 3 ounces white sugar (finely ground and sifted)
- juice of one lemon or half a lemon
- rose water (a little)

## Directions

1. Beat the egg white to foam and stir it well with 3 ounces of very good, finely ground and sifted white sugar.
2. If it does not become as thick as a thin porridge, more sugar should be added, as otherwise it will not be able to cover the cake.
3. Stir in the juice of a lemon or half a lemon and a little rose water.
4. To make the cake, which is to be iced, even, rub it with flour before coating it with the icing.

title: At nedlægge Spegesild (Preserved Spiced Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good, fat autumn herring
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean the herring by removing the gills
2. Sprinkle generously with salt and let stand for 3 days
3. Remove the herring and brush off the salt
4. Layer the herring in a small barrel or half-barrel with the belly side up, packed tightly together
5. Between each layer of herring, place a good layer of salt, and top with salt
6. Once layered, place a weight on top
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: At piske Fløde til Skum (Whipping Cream to Foam)
body:
## Ingredients

- cream (preferably cooked)
- lemon juice or vinegar (optional)
- ground vanilla or vanilla extract (optional)
- white sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. The cream should preferably be cooked, as long as it is good, because thin cream cannot become foam.
2. Whip it in a roomy pan, especially in warm weather, place it in cold water both before and while the cream is whipped.
3. If it still does not become foam, a little lemon juice or vinegar can be whipped with the cream.
4. If it does not become entirely foam throughout, let it stand still, take the foam from the top and place it on an inverted sieve so the liquid can drain off; whip this again along with the remaining cream; when it has become foam again, place it on the sieve again and so forth.
5. Ground vanilla or a few drops of it, when dissolved in spirits, and white sugar can be whipped with it.

title: At skolde Mandler (Blanching Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- hot water
- cold water

## Directions

1. After the almonds have been in hot water for a little while, the brown skin can be slipped off with the fingers.
2. Rinse them in cold water and dry them in a cloth before grinding or grating.
3. Bitter almonds should always be ground fine.

title: At skolde Risengrvn (Blanching Rice Grains)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice grains
- cold water
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Pick over the grains and place them in a little cold water to draw out the sourness.
2. Afterwards pour boiling water over them three times, letting them stand each time until nearly cold.
3. Finally, drain well through a colander.

title: At udtage Skroget og farsere en Kalkun (Boning and Stuffing a Turkey)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey, plucked, cleaned and washed
- salt
- stuffing (see options below)

## Directions

1. Place the plucked, cleaned and washed turkey on a cloth with the breast facing down toward the table.
2. With a pointed, sharp knife, slit the skin along the back.
3. With great care, cut out the back and breast bone in such a way that only the thighs and wings remain attached to the meat, but this must be done without damaging the skin.
4. When the turkey is opened in this manner, remove the entrails at the same time as the skeleton.
5. Spread the meat out on the cloth, sprinkle with a little salt and cover with enough stuffing that the turkey, when sewn together along the back, has taken on a beautiful round shape.

title: At udvaske Smor (Washed Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- cold water

## Directions

1. Press the butter with the hands in a bowl of cold water
2. Flatten it into pieces and let it stand in water until it is to be used
3. Then press the pieces in a cloth so that no water remains

title: At varme Kalvesteg og Steg af Vildt (Reheating Veal Roast and Game Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal or game roast, sliced
- butter
- gravy or cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into slices and place it in browned butter on the pan
2. When it is well heated, remove it and place it on the serving dish
3. Remove the pan from heat while you stir a little gravy or cream into the sauce
4. Return the pan to heat and stir until it boils, then pour it over the meat
5. Serve with stiff currant jelly
6. For as much meat as serves 5 persons, use a gill of good cream
7. It does not matter if the cream is sour

title: Aubain (Aubain Meatloaf)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.25 kg (0.5 lbs) butter
- 0.25 kg (0.5 lbs) flour
- 8.5 dl (3.5 pints) boiling milk
- 12 egg yolks
- 1 onion, grated
- Salt
- Pepper
- 0.5 kg (1 lbs) chopped meat
- Egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and bake the flour in it.
2. Add the boiling milk gradually to the flour mixture.
3. When cooled, stir in the egg yolks, grated onion, salt, pepper and chopped meat.
4. Finally fold in the beaten egg whites.
5. Pour into a buttered and floured pudding mold.
6. Boil for 2.5 hours.
7. Alternatively, bake in the oven in a gratin dish for approximately 1 hour or in a ring mold.
8. Serve with a brown gravy sauce made with gherkins, anchovies, pickles, pearl onions, chutney, Maggi bouillon cubes or lemon juice.

title: Aubin (Meat and Cream Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 lbs. cooked meat
- some onions
- 0.5 lbs. butter
- 0.5 lbs. flour
- 3 pints cooked milk
- 15 egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Chop the cooked meat very finely with some onions.
2. Bake it with 0.5 lbs. butter, 0.5 lbs. flour and 3 pints cooked milk.
3. When baked, pour it into a dish, and when it has cooled, stir 15 egg yolks into it.
4. Whip the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in last.
5. Put the mixture in a pudding mold greased with butter and sprinkled with breadcrumbs and cook for 1.5 hours.
6. Serve with a strong sauce of meat broth, to which a very little Sherry can be added.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Bagt Brød (Fried Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Sugar
- French bread, cut into small cubes

## Directions

1. Place butter and sugar on a frying pan
2. Brown small cubes of French bread in the mixture
3. Serve with sweet soups, or for beef soup, fry the bread in butter only

title: Bagt Risengröd og Nistager (Baked Rice Porridge and Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover milk rice porridge from previous day
- eggs
- melted butter
- bread crumbs
- sugar
- cinnamon
- sauce

## Directions

1. If you have milk rice porridge left over from the previous day, you can use it as follows: If you have enough porridge for four persons, stir it with a spoon, 2 whole eggs and a piece of melted butter
2. Put it into a deep dish that has first been buttered and sprinkled with stiff bread crumbs
3. Smooth the top, pour melted butter over, and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
4. Bake until light brown, and serve hot with sauce
5. Serve on the same dish with a napkin around it
6. Alternatively, instead of baking the whole porridge, after preparation it can be fried on a griddle in flat cakes
7. Serve hot with sugar, jam, boiled apples or mushrooms

title: Bagte Kartofler (Baked Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small potatoes of equal size
- Cold water
- Butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. Select small potatoes of equal size.
2. Wash them well in cold water and clean with a stiff brush.
3. Place them in a hot oven, which will bake them quickly.
4. They are ready in 20 to 35 minutes depending on their size and ripeness.
5. They are done when they give way easily when pressed between the fingers.
6. Serve immediately as soon as they are finished and never cover them; if they stand after finishing, they will become heavy, and if covered, they become watery.
7. Eat with cold butter and salt.

title: Bagte Snegle i Brøddejg (Baked Snails in Bread Dough)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams mushrooms, finely chopped
- 1 onion
- 125 grams butter
- Grated French bread
- Strained snail broth, reduced
- Salt
- Pepper
- White wine or lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- Snail shells, cleaned
- Cooked snails (from No. 572)
- Breadcrumbs, finely sifted
- Melted butter
- Salad for garnish
- Lemon for garnish

## Directions

1. Finely chop 125 grams mushrooms together with 1 onion
2. Sauté in 125 grams butter
3. Add as much grated French bread as the butter can accommodate
4. Thin with the strongly reduced, strained snail broth until it reaches the consistency of a not too thin batter dough
5. Season with salt, pepper and a little white wine or lemon juice
6. Stir in 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
7. Ensure snail shells are well cleaned inside with boiling and cold water and well drained
8. Place a little of the dough in each shell
9. Add the cooked snail (from No. 572)
10. Cover with dough and smooth it
11. Sprinkle with finely sifted breadcrumbs
12. Drizzle with melted butter
13. Place in a moderately warm oven
14. Bake for about 15 minutes on a baking pan covered with a layer of kitchen salt, or on a flat dish
15. Arrange very hot on a napkin, decorated with salad and garnished with lemon, whose juice is eaten on the snails

title: Bagte Æg (Baked Eggs) (Yankee Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- Butter
- Pepper
- Salt
- A little butter
- 3 tablespoons cream

## Directions

1. Break 8 eggs into a well-buttered bowl
2. Add pepper, salt, a little butter, and 3 tablespoons cream
3. Bake in the oven for 20 minutes and serve hot

title: Bajerske Pølser (Bavarian Sausages)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-3 sausages per person
- Water
- Parsley
- Horseradish, grated
- Mustard
- Butter, melted

## Directions

1. Place the sausages in boiling water and boil for 3-4 minutes.
2. Remove them and serve as hot as possible.
3. Garnish with parsley.
4. Serve either with grated horseradish, mustard and melted butter or with scrambled eggs.

title: Bajerske Pølser (Bavarian Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- Filling as for Medisterpølse
- Fine lamb intestines

## Directions

1. These sausages are prepared like Medister (No. 510), only with the difference that the filling is put into fine lamb intestines and the sausages are smoked.
2. They are boiled 3 minutes in boiling water with salt in it or in boiling bacon soup and are served with coarse, grated horseradish and toasted French bread.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Baked Eggs (Yankee Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- a little butter
- 3 tablespoons cream

## Directions

1. Break 8 eggs into a bowl well-buttered with butter
2. Add pepper, salt, a little butter and 3 tablespoons cream
3. Set in the oven, bake 20 minutes and serve warm

title: Bakkelse (Layered Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds wheat flour
- 1/2 pound crushed powdered sugar or light farina
- 1 pound butter
- 4 eggs
- a little crushed cinnamon
- cloves
- grated lemon zest
- 1 egg for brushing
- 4 pounds finely cut almonds
- candied citrus peel

## Directions

1. Work 2 pounds wheat flour, 1/2 pound crushed powdered sugar or light farina, 1 pound butter, 4 eggs, a little crushed cinnamon, cloves and grated lemon zest together well.
2. Roll out the dough on a baking board.
3. Brush the dough with 1 egg and sprinkle with 4 pounds finely cut almonds.
4. Cut candied citrus peel into thin slices and press them here and there on the surface of the dough.
5. Bake until beautifully light brown.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Banan-Kompot (Banana Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Banana
- Sugar syrup
- Lemon juice
- Maraschino essence

## Directions

1. Slice the banana and pour cold sugar syrup over it, seasoned with lemon juice and maraschino essence.

title: Bankekjød (Beaten Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef from the tenderloin, sliced finger-thick
- Grated bread
- Wheat flour
- Finely chopped onions
- Ground pepper
- Butter
- Chard onions
- Cloves
- Ginger
- Bay leaves
- Bouillon
- Browned flour
- Port wine
- Lemon juice
- Grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cut beef into finger-thick slices and beat and chop lightly with a knife.
2. Mix grated bread and wheat flour with finely chopped onions and ground pepper.
3. Coat the beef in this mixture.
4. Heat butter in a pan over the fire until slightly browned.
5. Place the beef in the pan and fry it half-tender under a lid.
6. Transfer the beef to a casserole with grated Charlotte onion, cloves, pepper, a little ginger, and a few bay leaves over even heat.
7. Add broth or water until the beef is covered.
8. When the beef is tender, thicken the sauce with browned flour and butter mixed in bouillon.
9. Shortly before serving, add a glass of Port wine, some lemon juice, and a little grated lemon zest to the sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Bankekød (Braised Beef)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg beef chuck or mixed cut
- butter, lard, or Palmin
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- water or thin stock
- coloring
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into slices, which are beaten out with a meat mallet and dipped in a mixture of salt, pepper, and flour.
2. In a pot, brown a good slice of butter, lard, or Palmin, and in it brown a dessert plate of finely chopped onion, after which the floured meat is placed in the pot.
3. Pour over it 1 liter water or thin stock and coloring, sufficient to color the sauce.
4. Simmer the meat over low heat and under a closed lid for about 3 hours.
5. Add Maggi bouillon cubes in this and the following 2 dishes if the sauce is not sufficiently piquant.
6. Serve with boiled potatoes or mashed potatoes.
7. When the meat is about tender, raw, peeled, cut potatoes can be added to cook in the sauce.

title: Bankekød (Pounded Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef, preferably from the hind leg
- Butter
- Onion
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Cloves
- Bay leaves
- Flour
- Bouillon or water

## Directions

1. Cut beef into slices as thick as a finger, pound with a mallet and hack slightly with a knife.
2. Place a piece of butter on the bottom of a casserole, then layer beef slices sprinkled with grated onion, salt, ground pepper, cloves, a couple of bay leaves and a little butter; repeat layers of meat, spices and butter.
3. Place over even heat and cook until the meat releases its juices, then gradually pour in boiling water until it barely covers the meat.
4. After cooking for about an hour, the meat should be tender. Mix a little flour with a little bouillon or water and add it.
5. Alternatively, you can flour the meat, brown it in butter, add the same spices and enough boiling water to cover the meat. Cook for a couple of hours until tender.

title: Bavarol (Bavarian Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 sheets isinglass
- 1⁄3 dl (½ pægel) boiling water
- ½ kilo (1 pound) powdered sugar
- 1 liter (1 pot) cold rhubarb juice
- ¼ liter (3 pægel) heavy cream

## Directions

1. Dissolve isinglass in boiling water along with powdered sugar and pour into cold rhubarb juice.
2. When it begins to set, stir in whipped cream made from the heavy cream.
3. Pour it into a wetted and sprinkled mold.
4. Place in a cool location or on ice for a few hours.
5. Serve with small macaroons.
6. Either red currant, raspberry, strawberry, or cherry juice can be used instead of rhubarb.
7. If the juice is sweet, use less sugar.
8. Can be decorated with preserved fruits around the pudding.

title: Bearnaise-Essens (Béarnaise Essence)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty peeled shallots
- 2 cloves of garlic
- 20 whole peppercorns
- 10 allspice berries
- 5 cloves
- 1 bay leaf
- parsley
- chervil
- horseradish
- paprika
- 1/4 L (3 pægle) wine vinegar
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) tarragon vinegar
- 1/4 L (1 pægl) Graves wine

## Directions

1. Cook the peeled shallots, garlic, peppercorns, allspice, cloves, bay leaf, parsley, chervil, horseradish and a little paprika in a mixture of 1/4 L (3 pægle) wine vinegar, 1/2 L (1/2 pot) tarragon vinegar and 1/4 L (1 pægl) Graves until the onions are tender and the essence has reduced to approximately 1 L (1 pot) during cooking.
2. Strain and pour cooled into bottles, which are stoppered.
3. Use for sauce. Can be purchased ready-made.

title: Bederyg tillavet som Vildt (Beef Loin Prepared as Game)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef loin, 5 to 6 kg (10 to 12 pounds)
- Cold strained beef blood, 5 liters (5 pots)
- Vinegar, 1/2 liter (1/2 pot)
- Salt, a good handful
- Parsley for larding
- Cream, thin
- Butter for paper
- Paper

## Directions

1. The loin, which should preferably have hung for a few days, is prepared like a veal or lamb loin and placed in a marinade for a couple of days.
2. Place a loin of 5 to 6 kg in a trough and cover it with 5 liters of cold, strained beef blood mixed with 1/2 liter of vinegar and a good handful of salt.
3. In this marinade, which must completely cover the loin, it can stand during winter for approximately a week.
4. When ready to use, rinse and dry the loin well.
5. Remove the membranes and lard the roast thickly and place it in a hot oven.
6. Initially, baste it with gravy, soup, or water and later with thin cream, and to prevent it from becoming too brown, cover it with paper smeared with butter.
7. Roast for 2½ hours.
8. Make the sauce as game sauce (see Nr. 772).
9. Carve the loin and place it back in position as described for veal and lamb loin, garnish with salad and browned potatoes or glazed chestnuts.
10. Red currant and lingonberry jelly, as well as apple sauce and compote, are suitable accompaniments.

title: Bederyg à l'anglaise (Beef Loin à l'Anglaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef loin
- Butter
- Paper
- Flour
- Reduced gravy
- Food coloring
- Pepper
- Salt
- Lemon juice
- Cooked vegetables (Nr. 733)
- Glazed onions (Nr. 671)
- Potato croquettes (Nr. 14)
- Purée (Nr. 725-732)
- English sauces

## Directions

1. Remove the loin, roast and carve it like lamb loin (Nr. 858).
2. The flaps must remain attached to the loin and be tucked underneath it, and the whole is bound with a band, covered with butter and wrapped with paper.
3. During roasting, which takes approximately 2 to 3 hours depending on the size of the loin, it must be frequently basted.
4. Make a sauce by cooking butter and flour together, which is diluted with the strained and skimmed gravy.
5. Give the sauce color with food coloring and season with pepper, salt, and lemon juice.
6. Garnish with cooked vegetables, glazed onions, potato croquettes, and purée.
7. Serve English sauces mixed with the gravy sauce.

title: Beef Tea (Beef Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound lean beef, finely chopped
- 1 pint cold water
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Chop the lean beef finely and place it in 1 pint of cold water for one hour
2. Set over the fire in the same water and bring slowly to a boil
3. Simmer for 20 to 30 minutes
4. Strain and season with a little salt
5. Note: The longer the meat can lie in cold water, the more nourishing matter is extracted

title: Beef Tea No. 2 (Beef Broth, Second Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound lean beef, finely chopped
- salt and other seasonings, if the doctor permits

## Directions

1. Chop the lean beef finely and place it in an enameled pot with a lid, without water
2. Place in a moderately warm oven for 4 hours
3. Strain off the juice and season, if the doctor allows it
4. Note: Beef prepared this way retains all the nourishing properties of the meat

title: Beefsteg på Panden (Pan-Fried Beef Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Porterhouse or sirloin beef
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Wheat flour
- Onion
- Bouillon or water

## Directions

1. Use Porterhouse or sirloin. Have the butcher cut it into thick or thin slices as you prefer.
2. Pound thick slices lightly on both sides, then sprinkle with salt and pepper and fry in brown butter.
3. When done, which varies according to taste, place on a platter.
4. Put a piece of butter on the pan, stir in 1 teaspoon wheat flour, some grated onion and a little ground pepper, then thin with boiling water.
5. If you have bouillon, make a better sauce as follows: When the meat is taken from the pan, add butter and stir well with a spoon; when it is brown, add grated onion and cook a little. Then stir in good bouillon and ground pepper.

title: Beefsteg på Rist (Grilled Beef Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Porterhouse beef
- Butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. Use Porterhouse beef for steak. Skin it and scrape it from the sides; make cuts here and there in the meat so that it cooks through more easily.
2. Place it on the grill and cook over clear coals of hardwood. When you think it is cooked enough on one side, turn it.
3. Do not let it become too dry; it should be red inside.
4. When done, place it on a platter, lay cold butter and salt over it and let it rest a moment in the oven, but serve it before the butter is completely melted. Eat it with potatoes.

title: Benlose Fugle (Boneless Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin slices of beef
- Beef forcemeat
- Carrot or fatty meat
- Spices
- Butter
- Onion
- Water
- Broth
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Salt
- Flour

## Directions

1. Thin slices of beef are cut and beaten very little.
2. On each piece is spread a spoonful of beef forcemeat, into which is placed a narrow piece of carrot or fatty meat, which is first dipped in spices.
3. The meat is folded together and formed in the shape of a bird without head and legs.
4. The meat is packed together with meat and bound with twine.
5. The birds are rubbed with flour, pepper, a little crushed cloves and salt.
6. A piece of butter is browned, in which a grated onion is boiled, then the birds, until they are slightly brown.
7. This is thinned with water and broth until it stands over them, and boiled gently for a couple of hours or until they are tender, then they are placed on a platter and kept warm, while the sauce is strained, put back on the fire with a little cream and flour if it is not strong and even enough, and poured over the birds.
8. These can be made in the same way without forcemeat, only with a piece of carrot or meat in the middle.

title: Benløse Fugle (Beef Rolls)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of good beef
- farce
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- flour
- brown butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Slices of good beef (not freshly slaughtered and preferably inner loin) are beaten out, and on them is spread a thin layer of farce.
2. In the center place a strip of bacon, which is dipped in a mixture of salt, pepper, and cloves.
3. Roll up the meat, shape like the body of a bird, and fasten together with a row of lard strips.
4. Rub the birds on the outside with the aforementioned spices and dip in flour.
5. In a pot with brown butter or margarine, [preparation continues but is cut off]

title: Benløse Fugle (Boneless Birds)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of good beef (not too freshly slaughtered, preferably inner loin)
- Ground meat forcemeat
- Bacon strips
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Butter or margarine
- Onions
- Flour
- Stock or water
- Coloring
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Beat out slices of beef and spread a thin layer of forcemeat on each.
2. Place a strip of bacon in the center, dipped in a mixture of salt, pepper, and cloves.
3. Roll up the meat, shape it like a bird's body, and join together with bacon strips.
4. Rub the birds externally with the spices and dip in flour.
5. In a pot with browned butter or margarine into which a couple of grated onions have been added, brown the birds.
6. Pour in enough stock, broth, or water so that it reaches the level of the meat.
7. Add coloring to the liquid and cook the birds for 2 hours.
8. Brown flour in the fat skimmed from the liquid, then thicken the sauce with it.
9. Arrange the birds in the sauce and serve with boiled, peeled potatoes.

title: Benløse Fugle (Roulade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender beef from the tenderloin or sirloin
- Bacon strips
- Flour
- Ground pepper
- Cloves
- Salt
- Butter
- Onions
- Hot water and bouillon
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into long slices of a hand's width and a finger's thickness.
2. Beat the slices lightly.
3. Place a strip of bacon in the middle of each slice and wrap the beef around it.
4. Coat in flour mixed with ground pepper, cloves, and salt.
5. Tie each roll with string.
6. Brown butter with a couple of chopped onions.
7. Place the beef rolls in the pan and brown them lightly.
8. Add enough hot water and bouillon to cover the meat.
9. Boil until tender and remove, keeping warm.
10. Strain the sauce and thicken it with egg yolk.
11. Pour the sauce over the roulades, removing the string.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ber (Preserved Berries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Berries
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Bring slowly to a boil in a spacious pot
2. If they give off much juice, remove it with a cup when they begin to boil; when they are almost dry, add sugar, 1 spoonful to each quart of berries
3. Cook for a quarter hour and fill as usual
4. This method can be used for all kinds of berries

title: Bergfisk (Bergfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into pieces, clean it, and soak it overnight.
2. Place in boiling water and cook for a short time.
3. When the pot is removed from the heat, add a little salt and some cold water, then immediately take the fish out and place it on a dish with the skin facing up.
4. Serve with melted butter and mustard, and with potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Berkes-Horn (Horn-Shaped Rolls with Berkes Topping)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g margarine
- 1 kg Pillsbury flour
- 1 teaspoon salt with 25 g yeast dissolved in it
- 2 teaspoons icing sugar
- a very small amount of cardamom
- just over 6.5 dl half-skimmed milk
- egg for brushing
- berkes for coating
- butter for serving

## Directions

1. Crumble the margarine into the flour
2. Add salt with dissolved yeast, icing sugar, cardamom, and milk
3. Shape the dough as horns and place on the baking sheet
4. Brush with egg and sprinkle with berkes
5. Let rise for approximately 1.5 hours
6. Bake at good heat for circa 20 minutes
7. Split and serve spread with butter

title: Berkesbrød (Caraway Seed Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- French bread dough
- caraway seeds
- egg beaten and mixed with milk

## Directions

1. Form French loaves and shape
2. Make a twist of dough and lay along the length of the loaves
3. Brush with beaten egg mixed with milk
4. Sprinkle with caraway seeds
5. Let rise before placing in oven

title: Betty Hennings-Kage (Betty Hennings Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams flour
- 100 grams margarine
- 25 grams powdered sugar
- 1 egg yolk
- Cream filling
- apple compote
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Knead together flour, margarine, powdered sugar, and egg yolk
2. Roll out into a thin sheet and bake on paper cut to the size of the dish
3. Cover this base with a layer of cream
4. Over that a layer of apple compote and again cream
5. Before serving, decorate with whipped cream tops

title: Biksemad (Maritime Hash)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover cooked or fried beef, fatty beef with some bacon
- Potatoes, cooked and diced
- Onions, sliced
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter or cooking fat

## Directions

1. Cut the meat small and brown it well in a pan.
2. Cut the potatoes into small cubes; they should be cooked, firm, peeled potatoes, not mealy.
3. Slice the onions in a good proportion to the meat and potatoes, and brown them as for beef steak.
4. Brown all three components together and warm through on the pan.
5. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Serve very hot.

title: Billig Punsch-Extrakt (Inexpensive Punch Extract)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pail water
- 1 pound sugar
- 2 quarts citric acid (obtained from pharmacy)

## Directions

1. Boil water with sugar and citric acid
2. When the sugar is dissolved, skim it and remove from the fire
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Billig Punsch-Extrakt (Inexpensive Punch Extract)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot water
- 1 pound sugar
- 2 quintals citric acid
- 1 drop citron oil
- 1 drop Peruvian balsam
- 1 pot white rum

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pot water with 1 pound sugar and 2 quintals citric acid
2. When the sugar is dissolved, skim it and remove from heat
3. Add 1 drop citron oil and 1 drop Peruvian balsam, and 1 pot white rum
4. Filter the extract and preserve in bottles
5. When it shall be used, add at least as much boiling water; drink it warm
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Biscuits (Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Egg yolks
- 0.75 Pound powdered sugar
- 0.5 Pound fine sifted fine or potato flour

## Directions

1. Beat 10 egg yolks for half an hour with three-quarters pound powdered sugar, constantly in the same direction.
2. Then add 0.5 pound fine sifted fine or potato flour.
3. Pour the mixture into small, oblong forms.
4. Brush with dissolved sugar.
5. Bake the cakes for one hour at even heat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Biscuits Nr. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1 tablespoon baking powder
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 pound fat
- 1 quart cold milk

## Directions

1. Sift 1 pound flour with 1 tablespoon baking powder and 1 teaspoon salt.
2. Rub 1/4 pound fat quickly into the flour in small pieces.
3. Dust the dough pan and table with flour.
4. Pour 1 quart cold milk into the dough and blend as quickly as possible.
5. Turn the dough onto the table, roll out 1 inch thick, and cut the small biscuits into squares and place on a buttered baking pan.
6. Bake in a hot oven. All of this must be done with the greatest haste so the biscuits can get into the oven before the gas from the baking powder and milk escapes. The dough is made light by this gas, which forces itself up through it, but if the biscuits are not baked before the gas escapes, they will be heavy.
7. When half-baked—after about 10 minutes—open the oven door and brush a little milk over them, then finish baking.

title: Biskop (Bishop)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 fresh bitter oranges
- 4 bottles red wine
- 13 pounds sugar
- 1 bottle Champagne
- 1 bottle soda water

## Directions

1. Peel the bitter oranges, place them with their peels in a bowl and pour 1 bottle of red wine over them. Let stand for 24 hours, then remove the fruit and pour the wine over sugar that has been dipped in water. Add the 3 remaining bottles of red wine and after 1 hour also add the Champagne and soda water.

title: Blaabær (Blueberries)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) ripe blueberries
- 250 gr (1/2 lb) Demerara sugar
- cinnamon sticks

## Directions

1. Place the ripe blueberries together with the sugar over low heat.
2. Skim them well during cooking, which takes 10 to 15 minutes.
3. Pour the berries onto a colander and boil the syrup with several pieces of whole cinnamon for at least 1 quarter hour.
4. Place the berries directly into the boiling syrup before pouring them into the crocks, which are sealed once the fruit has cooled.

title: Blaabær-, Hyldebær- og Solbærsuppe (Blueberry, Elderberry, and Blackcurrant Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pots blueberries, or 14 pounds ripe elderberries, or 12 pots blackcurrants
- 9 pots water
- a couple of sticks cinnamon
- a little lemon peel
- 2 to 3 teaspoonfuls potato flour
- a little cold water
- sugar to taste
- lemon juice or sour fruit juice
- bread balls or baked wheat bread cubes

## Directions

1. Clean the berries well and put them on to cook with the water until they are so tender that they can be mashed through a sieve. The cooking will take about an hour.
2. Strain the soup and put it back over the fire with a couple of sticks of cinnamon and a little lemon peel and cook it, then skim it.
3. Mix 2 to 3 teaspoonfuls of potato flour in a little cold water and thin it with a little of the soup.
4. Add sugar to taste, add the thickening to the soup, give it just one boil, but no more.
5. If the soup needs more sourness, add a little lemon juice or sour fruit juice.
6. Serve it with bread balls on top or with baked wheat bread cubes on the side.
7. Elderberry soup gets a good flavor when you take pieces of peeled good apples, with the cores removed, and cook them in the soup after it has been strained.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Blaabær-Suppe (Blueberry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 575 g blueberries
- 4½ L water

## Directions

1. Prepare as recipe No. 420.
2. Add rhubarb juice, lemon juice or vinegar.

title: Blaabær-Suppe (Blueberry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 kg (2 1/2 S) blueberries
- 5 L (5 pots) water
- lemon
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook blueberries with lemon and cinnamon, strain and thicken as with the other fruit soups.
2. Blackberries are cooked the same way as blueberries.

title: Blaabærsaft (Blueberry Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Blueberries
- 1 liter (1 Pot) water per 2 to 2½ kg berries (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil the berries for a quarter of an hour with frequent stirring.
2. Press the juice through a very fine sieve.
3. Pour it into bottles, which are wrapped with hay, corked, and boiled for 5 minutes in the pot.
4. When the bottles have partially cooled, wipe them dry and plaster them the next day.
5. Alternatively, the blueberries can be boiled with 1 liter (1 Pot) water per 2 to 2½ kg berries.
6. Sift off the juice and treat as described above.

title: Blaabærsaft (Blueberry Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Blueberries
- Powdered sugar or farina (1/2 kg or 1/2 lb powdered sugar or farina to 1 liter juice)

## Directions

1. Pick and rinse the berries and place them over the fire, stirring regularly so they do not burn on the bottom of the pot
2. When they release their juice well, remove the pot from the fire and press the berries through a sieve after they have cooled sufficiently
3. Measure the juice and bring it to a boil with the sugar, skim it and boil for 10 minutes
4. Pour it into dry bottles and seal them immediately with parchment paper

title: Blaabærsaft (Blueberry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Blueberries
- 1 pound of powdered sugar per pot of juice
- A little cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place the berries over the fire until they burst, then pour them out and cool a little
2. Wring them in a cloth
3. Cook the juice afterwards for 1 hour with the sugar and cinnamon
4. Skim it well
5. When it is cold, pour it into bottles
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Black Cherries, Preserving Cherries (Sorte Kirsebær)
body:
## Ingredients

- large, ripe black cherries
- vinegar
- powdered sugar equal in weight to the berries
- cinnamon sticks
- whole cloves

## Directions

1. Remove the stones from large, ripe berries and place them in a terrine, covering with enough good vinegar to cover the berries.
2. Let sit overnight.
3. The next day, pour the berries into a colander so all vinegar drains off.
4. Weigh the berries and for each pound take 1 pound powdered sugar.
5. On the bottom of a preserving jar place, for the stated quantity, 4 drams whole cinnamon and 15 drams whole cloves.
6. Over this lay a 3-fingers-thick layer of powdered sugar, then a layer of cherries, then a layer of sugar, continuing in this manner with the top layer being a 4-fingers-thick sugar layer.
7. Tie the jar immediately with a bladder and place in a cool location.
8. The drained vinegar can be bottled and used in other ways, such as for sweet soup and similar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Black Currants (Solbær)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe berries
- 1 Pd. Melis per 1 Pd. berries
- 1 Pægl vinegar

## Directions

1. Strip the ripe berries from the stem and cut off the flower
2. Boil the Melis with vinegar to a thin syrup
3. Add the berries and boil for about 1 hour until they appear clear
4. Remove from heat and shake until they begin to swell, then pour off
5. Boil the syrup and give the berries a boil in it
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Blackberries (Brombær)
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- 1/2 pound powdered sugar per pound of berries
- cinnamon sticks

## Directions

1. Clean the berries.
2. For each pound of berries, use 1/2 pound powdered sugar, which is sprinkled in layers between the berries.
3. After standing for several hours, place on the fire to cook together with some whole cinnamon sticks.
4. Cook the berries for approximately half an hour until they appear clear.
5. Skim frequently during cooking.
6. Remove the berries carefully and place in the preserving jar.
7. Continue cooking the syrup until it is like a thick syrup, then pour over the berries and cover the next day.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Blanc Manger (Almond Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pints sweet cream
- 0.5 Pound whole sugar
- 3 Pounds sweet almonds
- 1 Ounce bitter almonds
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 2 Ounces isinglass, dissolved in warm water
- 1 Quart raspberry juice
- 3 Gills cherry juice
- 1 Pint water
- 1.5 Pounds white sugar
- 1 Ounce gelatine

## Directions

1. Heat the sweet cream with the whole sugar, finely pounded sweet almonds, finely pounded bitter almonds, and lemon zest
2. When it has boiled for 2 to 3 minutes, add the isinglass dissolved in warm water
3. Cook gently for 10 minutes with constant stirring
4. Strain through a fine cloth into a stone bowl
5. For the red jelly layer: mix together the raspberry juice, cherry juice, water, white sugar, and gelatine
6. Bring to a boil, stirring, and stir in a bowl until it begins to set
7. When both parts are ready, pour some of the white mixture into the oiled mold to a height of about half an inch
8. Insert a clean stick and make a cut where the mixture ends
9. When completely cold and stiff, warm some of the red jelly so it is just fluid and carefully pour it over with a spoon so it does not fall hard on one spot and break the white
10. Hold the stick, which must not be pointed, down to the solid layer and pour, filling to the cut
11. Continue in this way as long as there is something left
12. Keep the mold in cold water the entire time, changing it often
13. This pudding is always turned out
14. Always test each mixture before putting it in the mold by pouring a little onto a saucer and letting it cool and set as much as possible
15. The white never fails, but the red depends much on the quality of the juice; homemade juice should be used; if it does not set, use more gelatine

title: Blanc-manger (Blancmange)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot thick, sweet cream
- 6 quarters isinglass
- Drops of vanilla and bergamot oil
- Sugar to taste, about 1 pound
- Brown caramel sugar, dissolved cochineal, saffron decoction, spinach decoction for coloring

## Directions

1. Beat 1 pot thick, sweet cream until very stiff foam.
2. Add 6 quarters isinglass, first dissolved in boiling water, a few drops of vanilla and bergamot oil, and sugar to taste—about 1 pound.
3. Color one part brown with brown caramel sugar, another red with dissolved cochineal, one yellow with saffron decoction, one green with spinach decoction, and thus you can vary the colors as desired in the different layers with which the jelly mold is filled; but each layer must be set before the next comes on.
4. Set the whole blancmange in a cold place to become stiff.
5. It can also be filled in small molds, but then given only one color.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Blanc-manger Budding (Blancmange Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- ¾ pound sugar
- 6 kvint isinglass
- 1 vanilla stick
- water
- jam (strawberry, raspberry or similar)
- oblong sugar breads

## Directions

1. Whip 1 pot sweet cream to stiff peaks.
2. Boil ¾ pound sugar, 6 kvint isinglass and 1 vanilla stick with a little water until all is dissolved.
3. When nearly cold, gently stir it into the whipped cream.
4. Decorate the bottom of the mold with jam (strawberry, raspberry or similar) and place oblong sugar breads around the inside of the mold.
5. Fill with the blancmange.
6. Let stand for a few hours in a cold place, turn out and serve with cold wine sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Blandet Bred (Mixed Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flour
- rye flour
- water

## Directions

1. Make good bread by taking half wheat flour and half rye flour, for example setting the dough with wheat flour and kneading it up with rye flour.
2. Set it in water and handle otherwise as usual.

title: Blandet Frugtsaft (Mixed Fruit Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal parts of pressed or strained juice, for example of strawberries, currants, and raspberries
- 375 g (3/4 S) powdered sugar Melis per 1 liter (1 Pot) juice

## Directions

1. Mix equal parts of pressed or strained juice, such as strawberry, currant, and raspberry juice.
2. Boil with the sugar until it has skimmed.
3. Pour the juice into bottles and bind immediately.

title: Blanquet af Kalvekjod (Blanquette of Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds veal breast
- Cold water
- White pepper
- Salt
- Green herbs
- Carrot
- Turnip
- Onion
- Cloves
- Butter
- Flour
- Nutmeg
- Egg yolks
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Cut 3 pounds veal breast into small pieces and place in sufficient cold water to cover them; at the same time add half a teaspoon white pepper, a teaspoon salt, a good bunch of green herbs, half a scraped carrot, a peeled turnip and an onion in which 3 cloves are stuck
2. Bring to a gentle boil and skim carefully as long as scum appears, and cook until the meat is tender
3. Strain it, keeping the broth on the heat and wash the meat in cold water
4. Meanwhile make a white sauce by melting a little butter and stirring flour into it and then gradually adding 1½ pints of the broth
5. Add a little more pepper and salt if needed, and half a teaspoon grated nutmeg
6. When the sauce comes to a boil, whisk it with a whisk, add two small egg yolks, put the meat in and cook it a few minutes while stirring occasionally
7. It can be served with a tablespoon of chopped parsley sprinkled over it after it is poured onto a warm platter

title: Bleg-Selleri, stuvet (Braised Blanched Celery)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked celery stalks, cut in pieces
- Butter
- Flour
- Celery cooking water
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Egg yolks
- Raw cream
- Salt
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Pour a white, smooth sauce made of butter, flour and celery water with Maggi Bouillon cubes over the cut cooked stalks
2. Thicken the sauce with egg yolks beaten with raw cream
3. Add salt and a little sugar to taste

title: Bleg-Selleri- og Østers-Salat (Celery and Oyster Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can preserved oysters, containing approximately 375 grams (1/4 pound)
- 1 large English celery
- Oil
- Lemon juice
- Salt
- Finely ground white pepper
- Ice
- Mayonnaise (made with 2 egg yolks)
- Watercress

## Directions

1. Marinate the oysters in a mixture of oil, lemon juice, salt and a small amount of finely ground white pepper.
2. Set aside for a couple of hours in an icebox or cold place.
3. Rinse the celery and cut out the most tender and best of it in very thin slices.
4. Place them in a bowl and add a piece of ice.
5. When the salad is to be served, drain the celery together with the oysters.
6. Place a layer in the salad bowl, cover with mayonnaise, then again celery and oysters and mayonnaise as the top layer.
7. Garnish with watercress.
8. Make the mayonnaise from 2 egg yolks.

title: Bleg-Selleri-Salat til Vildt (Blanched Celery Salad for Game)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- One blanched celery
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cream
- Mustard
- Sugar
- Lemon juice or tarragon vinegar
- Green salad leaves
- Fruit coloring (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut out all the tender and good parts of a rinsed and dried blanched celery into very small pieces
2. Lay in a salad bowl on a ring of green salad leaves
3. Whip 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cream to a foam
4. Mix the foam with mustard, sugar, and lemon juice or tarragon vinegar to taste
5. The foam sauce can be colored pale pink with a few drops of fruit coloring
6. The salad must be eaten when prepared
7. If using root celery, it must be cooked

title: Bleg-Sellerier (kogte) (Blanched Celery, Cooked)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Blanched celery
- Butter

## Directions

1. Divide and rinse the celery well
2. Cook as radishes
3. Serve on a napkin on a plate
4. Eat with melted butter

title: Blitz
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds clarified butter
- ¾ pound powdered sugar
- lemon zest, grated
- Ceylon cinnamon, ground
- 3 whole eggs
- 2 pounds wheat flour
- chopped sweet almonds
- coarsely ground white sugar

## Directions

1. Stir clarified butter with ¾ pound powdered sugar, grated lemon zest, and ground Ceylon cinnamon
2. Stir for half an hour with 3 whole eggs
3. Add 2 pounds wheat flour
4. Form a square mold from paper, grease it with butter, and place the dough in it to the thickness of a two-crown coin
5. Sprinkle with chopped sweet almonds and coarsely ground white sugar
6. Bake at even heat for half an hour until light brown
7. Cut into small square cakes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Blodpolser (Blood Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork intestines or beef intestines
- medium-fine barley grains
- 1½ gallons pork blood
- 3 spoonfuls dried, finely chopped thyme and marjoram leaves
- 1 spoonful crushed allspice
- 3 spoonfuls crushed cloves
- ¼ pound powdered sugar
- salt
- 2 pounds raisins
- pork lard, cut into small cubes

## Directions

1. Clean the intestines completely inside and out by scraping, turning and filling them repeatedly in lukewarm water
2. When they are completely smooth and clear, place them in cold water with celery leaves and let them stand overnight
3. The same day the intestines are cleaned, pour boiling water over a suitable quantity of medium-fine barley grains (or half barley grains and half rye flour)
4. After stirring well, drain off almost all the water and let the grains stand until the next day
5. The next day, pour 1½ gallons of pork blood, still warm from slaughter, through a sieve over them, and let them stand again until the next day, stirring regularly
6. Add 3 spoonfuls of dried, finely chopped and sieved thyme and marjoram leaves to the blood, along with 1 spoonful crushed allspice, 3 spoonfuls crushed cloves, ¼ pound powdered sugar, a little salt and 2 pounds raisins
7. Stir the whole to a thick consistency
8. Drain the water from the intestines and lay them in lukewarm water
9. Blow down into them to see where there are holes, and cut them there
10. Tie one end, put the sausage horn in the other end, and through it fill the sausage with a spoon while stirring often
11. For each spoonful add a couple of cubes of pork lard
12. Fill the intestines only about half full, tie them off and press the sausage mixture around in them so that the raisins and meat cubes are well distributed
13. Place them in lukewarm water, and when 4 or 6 are filled, place them in a kettle with boiling water
14. Add the thyme stalks to the boiling water
15. During cooking prick them here and there
16. Cook for a good half hour or until a fine stick inserted in them no longer becomes colored from blood
17. Serve with powdered white sugar and powdered cinnamon or sieved cloves in melted butter

title: Blodpølser, stegt med Æbler (Blood Sausages Fried with Apples)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Blood sausages
- Apples, peeled and sliced
- Sugar
- Butter, melted

## Directions

1. Slice the sausages and fry them in a pan in butter together with peeled apples that have been sliced and sprinkled with sugar.
2. Arrange the apples warm in the center of the dish and the sausages around them.
3. Serve with melted butter and sugar.

title: Blomkaal (Cauliflower)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Firm cauliflower heads
- Cold water
- Boiling water with salt
- Salt solution (10 gr or 2 Kvint per 1 liter)

## Directions

1. Divide firm cauliflower heads into smaller florets and rinse in cold water
2. Place in a pot with boiling salted water
3. Cook for 4 to 5 minutes, remove and pour the cold salt solution over them
4. Place the hermetically sealed jars in a pot and cook for 30 minutes and cool

title: Blomkaal (Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- flour
- water or milk
- butter
- cream
- milk
- nutmeg
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the cauliflower very well of the small leaves that sit between the florets
2. Cut away the stalks around the outer leaves
3. Wrap it in a piece of white cloth to prevent it from becoming reddish when cooked
4. Place it in cold water gradually as you clean it before cooking
5. Cook by steam or in water
6. Rub 2 rounded teaspoons of flour into a large spoonful of butter, add enough milk or cream to make a thick, even sauce and let the cauliflower have a brief boiling in this with nutmeg, a piece of sugar and finally a little salt
7. Alternatively, melt a good piece of butter over heat and stir a little nutmeg in, then pour it over the cauliflower when arranged on the plate

title: Blomkaal (Pickled Cauliflower)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 skålpund) cauliflower, divided into small florets
- Salt
- 1 1/2 liters (1 1/2 pots) vinegar
- 3 pods Spanish pepper
- 1 tablespoon whole white pepper

## Directions

1. Rinse the cauliflower divided into small florets
2. Give it one good boil in salted water
3. Drain in sieve
4. Boil the vinegar with Spanish pepper pods and whole white pepper
5. Place the cauliflower in a crock, cover with the boiling vinegar, and cover

title: Blomkaal au naturel (Cauliflower Natural)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower heads
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Select white and firm cauliflower heads
2. Remove the green leaves
3. Cut away the bottom of the stalks
4. Soak the cauliflower in cold water with a little vinegar to remove larvae and insects
5. Place the cauliflower in boiling salted water with the flower facing down
6. Cook for approximately 20 to 25 minutes
7. To keep it white, cook in a mixture of milk and water or wrap in a clear cloth
8. Serve either arranged alone in a pyramid or together with other vegetables such as peas, asparagus, beans, radishes, carrots and leeks, arranged around the cauliflower with carrots placed between them with points upward
9. Serve with butter

title: Blomkaal med Parmesanost (Cauliflower with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 medium cauliflower heads
- butter
- flour
- milk
- egg yolk
- grated Parmesan cheese
- sugar
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Place the cooked cauliflower on a sieve to remove water
2. When well drained, arrange the heads in a pyramid on an oven-safe dish
3. Make a smooth white sauce of butter and flour diluted with milk
4. Thicken with 1 egg yolk per cauliflower head
5. Season the sauce with grated Parmesan cheese, sugar and salt to taste
6. Pour the sauce over the cauliflower, ensuring it is completely covered
7. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs and drizzle with melted butter
8. Bake at strong oven heat for about 15 minutes until light brown
9. Serve as a vegetable or as an independent dish for a first or middle course

title: Blomkaal med Reje-Sauce (Cauliflower with Shrimp Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cauliflower
- shrimp or lobster sauce
- puff pastry strips
- lobster head

## Directions

1. Arrange the cooked cauliflower in a pyramid on a round dish
2. Pour a shrimp or lobster sauce over it
3. Garnish with puff pastry strips
4. Place a lobster head on top of the arrangement

title: Blomkaal med Tomat-Sauce og kogte Ris (Cauliflower with Tomato Sauce and Cooked Rice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- cooked cauliflower heads
- tomato sauce made with wine
- raw tomatoes
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook rice as for curry and arrange as a border on a larger dish
2. Place the rice in the oven until dry
3. Place cooked cauliflower heads inside the rice border
4. Pour a good tomato sauce made with wine over the cauliflower
5. Dip slices of raw tomato in egg and breadcrumbs mixed with pepper and salt
6. Fry the tomato slices in butter in a pan
7. Place these fried tomato slices on the rice border

title: Blomkaal og Ærter med Mayonnaise eller rørt Smør (Cauliflower and Peas with Mayonnaise or Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cauliflower heads
- cooked peas
- parsley
- mayonnaise or butter

## Directions

1. Arrange a large or several smaller cooked cauliflower heads in the center of a dish
2. Garnish with a wreath of cooked peas
3. Rinse a couple of bunches of parsley, press dry in a cloth, chop finely and sprinkle over the vegetable dish
4. Serve with mayonnaise or butter
5. For a more decorative presentation, place the mayonnaise or butter in tartlets arranged around the vegetable dish, ensuring the tartlets are cold

title: Blomkaal å la maitre d'hotel (Cauliflower à la Maître d'Hôtel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cauliflower
- butter
- lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Place the cooked, steaming hot cauliflower in the center of a dish
2. On the edge of the dish place slices of lemon
3. On the lemon place small balls of butter made with a teaspoon and dipped in hot water
4. Roll the butter balls in finely chopped parsley
5. Arrange before serving the cauliflower

title: Blomkaal, au naturel (Cauliflower, Natural)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower heads
- Salt
- Butter
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. The heads must be white and firm
2. Clean well and remove all green leaves
3. Cut off the stalk close to the head without separating it
4. Place with the stalk facing up in a little water and sprinkle generously with salt so that insects and worms fall off into the water
5. Cook the head in water with salt until tender
6. Carefully remove and serve with melted butter in which an egg yolk has been beaten
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Blomkaal, stuvet (Stewed Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Flour
- Butter
- Milk
- Cream
- Salt
- Sugar
- Nutmeg
- Egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflower first, then cut into smaller pieces
2. Make a roux of flour and butter, thin with milk or cream and add a little salt, sugar, and nutmeg
3. Whipped egg yolks can also be added to the roux
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Blomkaal-Suppe (Cauliflower Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 L (5 pots) soup
- 2 medium cauliflower heads
- Water
- Salt
- 125 g (1/4 lb) butter or vegetable margarine
- 125 g (1/4 lb) flour
- Cauliflower cooking water
- Veal stock or economical stock
- 1 bunch of herbs
- 6 egg yolks
- Ground white pepper
- A couple spoonfuls grated Parmesan cheese (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the cauliflower heads and cook in salted water, but not too soft
2. Divide into small florets and place in the tureen
3. Brown the butter or margarine with the flour and dilute with 5 L (5 pots) of liquid, a mixture of the cauliflower water and veal stock or economical stock, in which a bunch of herbs has been cooked
4. Thicken the soup like asparagus soup with 6 egg yolks and season with a little ground white pepper
5. Grated Parmesan cheese can be added to the soup if desired
6. Serve with baked or toasted bread

title: Blomkål i Gratinskaller (Cauliflower in Gratin Shells)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs
- ham (optional)
- eggs (optional)

## Directions

1. The cauliflower is cooked nearly tender in salted water.
2. It is divided into small florets, which are placed in the shells, over which melted butter is poured, and they are sprinkled with breadcrumbs and baked.
3. Around the warm cabbage, around the edge of the shell, chopped ham can be placed. Chopped egg is sprinkled over the cauliflower.

title: Blomme-Kompot (Plum Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- 250 grams (1/2 pund) powdered sugar
- 375 milliliters (1/2 pægel) water

## Directions

1. Scald the plums, prick them, and give them one boil in a sugar syrup made from the sugar and water.
2. Green plums, prune plums, and small mirabelles can be treated in the same way.

title: Blomme-Marmelade (Dried Plum Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 2 kg prunes
- 1/2 kg apples
- 1/2 kg quinces
- Peel of 1 lemon cut into fine strips
- 1 or 2 pieces of whole cinnamon

## Directions

1. Scald the plums and press out the stones
2. Peel the apples and quinces and cut into 4 to 6 pieces, removing the seed cores; then weigh the fruit
3. Cook the lemon peel and cinnamon together with the fruit and sugar over even heat, stirring, until the fruit is cooked out and the jam is like a smooth porridge
4. Place in glasses, cover with paper, and seal

title: Blomme-Marmelade med Citron (Plum Jam with Lemon)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg powdered sugar for 1 kg plums without stones
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Scald the plums, remove the skin, halve them, and remove the stones, then weigh them
2. Place the plums, sugar, and juice of 1 lemon in the pot
3. Cut the lemon peel into very fine strips and mix with the plums
4. Cook over even heat with constant stirring until the fruit is cooked out and the jam is a firm mass
5. Ladle into glasses, cover with paper, and seal

title: Blomme-Suppe (Plum Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg fresh plums
- 4.5 L water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- potato flour
- red wine or Graves wine

## Directions

1. Rinse plums and cook in 4.5 L water with sugar and cinnamon until cooked through
2. Strain through a coarse sieve
3. Thicken with potato flour mixed with water
4. Season with red wine or Graves

title: Blomme-Suppe (Plum Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg (3 S) fresh plums
- 4 1/2 L (4 1/2 pots) water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- potato starch
- red wine or Graves

## Directions

1. Rinse the plums and cook them in 4 1/2 L (4 1/2 pots) water with sugar and cinnamon until they are cooked out; then strain them through a coarse sieve and thicken with stirred potato starch.
2. Season the soup with red wine or Graves.

title: Blommer (Plums)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- Powdered sugar (optional, for serving as compote)

## Directions

1. Choice, unblemished plums must be used, and the stems must be removed carefully so as not to damage the fruit.
2. Fill the plums into jars with wide mouths, seal them, wrap with hay, and cook according to the preceding instructions for 20 minutes.
3. The jars remain in the pot until the water has cooled, or they are removed, covered, dried, and marked.
4. The plums may be served as compote, but first sprinkle with powdered sugar and let them stand for a couple of hours.
5. Suitable in grain soup or for plum soup.
6. Crags, a type of small plums, may be treated the same way, but must not cook more than 10 minutes.

title: Blommer År. 1. (Pickled Plums No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fully ripe plums
- Half gill vinegar per pound of plums
- Half pound powdered sugar per pound of plums
- Cinnamon stick
- Cloves
- Fresh bay leaves (optional, for serving with roasted meat)

## Directions

1. Plums must be fully ripe. Dry them, prick them with a pointed wooden stick or needle and place them in a crock, where they are covered with half a gill of vinegar per pound of plums and left until the next day.
2. Pour off the vinegar and add half pound of powdered sugar per pound of plums. Heat this on the fire, and when it is just melted, add the plums and turn them a couple of times around so that those that were at the bottom come to the top.
3. When it begins to boil, pour it into the crock, leave uncovered until the next day, pour into another crock so that the syrup can reach all the plums, and the day after set the whole thing back on the fire, but stop as soon as it begins to boil.
4. Two days later set it on the fire again, and now cook the plums until they are not sour by the stones, usually in half an hour. Then place them in a strainer so the syrup can drain off.
5. Cook the syrup a good quarter hour, or until it is smooth, with whole cinnamon and cloves. Pour it into a crock, and when it is cold, place the plums in it, but not too many, so there can be sufficient syrup around the plums.
6. The next day tie them up. If there is more syrup than can be with the plums, pour this into bottles. If one wishes to use these plums with roast, it is good to add a few fresh bay leaves.

title: Blommer År. 2. (Pickled Plums No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds powdered sugar per pound of plums
- Half gill vinegar per pound of plums
- 1 tablespoon water per pound of plums
- Cinnamon
- Cloves

## Directions

1. For each pound of plums, take 3 pounds powdered sugar, which is put on the fire with half gill vinegar and 1 tablespoon water.
2. When it is melted, pour it over the plums, which have first been dried, pricked and placed in a crock.
3. After 3 to 4 days, during which the plums are poured each day into another crock so that the syrup can reach around them, set them over the fire where they are cooked until they are not sour by the stones, about three-quarters of an hour.
4. Then place them in a coarse strainer and from there in a crock which is filled half full with them.
5. Cook the syrup with cinnamon and cloves for a quarter hour or until it is smooth. Pour it warm over the plums, which are tied up the next day.
6. Plums can be preserved in the same way with half pound sugar and half gill vinegar per pound, but then less syrup is obtained.

title: Blood Sausages (Blodpølser)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg (4 lbs) blood sausages
- Salt
- Water
- Melted butter
- Cinnamon
- Sugar
- Apple sauce or apple compote

## Directions

1. Place whole in boiling salted water, and boil them for a few minutes until they are thoroughly heated through.
2. Cut them into pieces, which are reassembled, and serve as hot as possible, garnished with parsley.
3. Serve with melted butter as well as cinnamon and sugar. Apple sauce or apple compote is very much eaten with blood sausages.

title: Blood Sausages Fried with Apples (Blodpølser, stegt med Æbler)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Sausages
- Apples, peeled and sliced
- Sugar
- Butter
- Melted butter with sugar

## Directions

1. Slice the sausages and fry them in butter in a pan together with peeled and sliced apples, which are sprinkled with sugar.
2. Place the apples hot in the center of the platter and the sausages around them.
3. Serve with melted butter and sugar.

title: Blåbær (Huckleberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound ripe blueberries
- ½ pound powdered sugar
- Cinnamon
- Paper dipped in cognac

## Directions

1. Place 1 pound ripe blueberries over the fire with ½ pound powdered sugar and boil for about ¼ hour
2. Pour the berries onto a strainer
3. Boil the juice for a quarter hour with cinnamon, skim it, and pour hot over the berries
4. The next day, cover with paper dipped in cognac and seal

title: Blåbær Saft (Blueberry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Blueberries
- Brandy

## Directions

1. Place the berries over the fire and boil for a quarter hour
2. Stir often so nothing sticks to the bottom
3. When slightly cooled, wring them out in a cloth
4. When the juice is completely cold, put it in small bottles that have been well filled with brandy; also pour a layer of brandy on top of each bottle, which is then immediately corked
5. This inexpensive juice keeps well all winter

title: Blåbær-Suppe (Blueberry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 kg blueberries
- 5 L water
- lemon
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook blueberries with lemon and cinnamon
2. Strain and thicken like other fruit soups

title: Blåbær-Suppe (Blueberry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart Blueberries
- 3 Pint Water
- Sugar
- 1 large Spoonful Corn-Starch

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as Raspberry Soup.

title: Blåbærsaft (Blueberry Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Blueberries
- 9 0/2 parts powdered sugar or farina per 1 liter (1 pot) juice

## Directions

1. Pick over the berries, rinse them, and place over heat, stirring regularly so they do not burn on the bottom of the pot.
2. When they release their juice well, remove the pot from heat and press the berries through a sieve after they have cooled sufficiently.
3. Measure the juice and bring to a boil with the sugar, skim it, boil for 10 minutes, and then pour into dry bottles.
4. Tie the bottles immediately with parchment paper.

title: Blødkogte Æg (Soft-Boiled Eggs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Salt water

## Directions

1. Wash the eggs in salt water and place them in an egg net, which is lowered into the boiling water. When boiling eggs, account must be taken for the size of the eggs. Very large, fresh eggs easily tolerate boiling for 4 minutes; otherwise the common rule is 3 minutes. 6 to 7 minutes are needed for the white to be firm and the yolk soft for cut eggs. The eggs must not be boiled in too much water or under a cover, as it causes them to crack easily. Fresh eggs have a greater density than water and therefore sink to the bottom of the pot, whereas less good ones float on top. Soft-boiled eggs are arranged immediately either in an egg coddler or on a platter lined with a napkin, which is folded over the eggs and garnished with parsley.

title: Boeuf a la Mode (Beef Braised in Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- Several beef rump steaks
- Bacon pieces
- Pepper
- Ginger
- Flour
- Butter
- Broth
- Whole onions
- Anchovies
- Bay leaves
- Whole ginger pieces
- Whole peppercorns
- Oysters or gravy

## Directions

1. For this several beef rump steaks are used, and since these are usually lean, many lard them with bacon pieces, done by cutting a deep slit in several places in the meat, taking small bacon pieces, dipping them in pepper and ginger and pushing them as deep in as possible.
2. If the cut gapes, it is closed together with a small skewer or with one of the bacon strips with which the roast is larded on the outside.
3. Such bacon strips must be cut lengthwise through the meat; if taken crosswise, they break apart.
4. The roast is then dusted with flour, but not more than can be well rubbed in; then it is browned on all sides in butter, and one must not take more of it than exactly necessary, for then the roast would become less brown.
5. When all sides are brown, the roast is taken up, a good spoonful of flour is browned in the butter that is in the pan; this is filled out with about 3 quarts of hot broth little by little.
6. The roast is placed back in and some whole onions, a couple of anchovies, a small handful of bay leaves, some pieces of whole ginger and whole peppercorns are added to the sauce.
7. This all boils together under a lid until the roast is tender, then the sauce is strained, thinned if it is too thick, and whatever oysters or gravy you have is added.

title: Boeuf à la Mode (Beef à la Mode)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef from the tenderloin
- Smoked bacon, half lean and half fat, the size of a small finger
- Peeled and quartered Charlotte onions
- Ground ginger
- Ground pepper
- Cloves
- Finely chopped onions
- Salt
- Butter
- Water
- Bay leaves
- Peppercorns
- Red wine or Port wine
- Flour

## Directions

1. Beat the beef well on all sides and remove the sinews.
2. Cut the bacon into strips and finely chop the Charlotte onions.
3. Mix ground ginger, pepper, cloves, finely chopped onions, and salt together.
4. Lard the meat with the bacon strips and rub it with the spice mixture.
5. Also rub the bacon in the spice mixture before larding.
6. Lard the meat on all sides.
7. Heat butter in a pot over the fire until browned.
8. When the butter begins to brown, place the meat in it and brown slowly on both sides.
9. Add enough water to just cover the meat.
10. Add a few bay leaves, some peppercorns, an onion, a few cloves, and a glass of red wine or Port wine.
11. Boil for approximately 4 hours until the meat is tender.
12. Drain the broth and skim off the fat.
13. Reduce the broth over high heat to half its volume.
14. Add salt to taste.
15. The sauce can also be thickened with butter and flour.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Boeufsteg i Gelee (Beef Roast in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef, hung several days after slaughter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Grated onion
- Flour
- Butter
- Mustard
- Tarragon vinegar
- Vinegar of preserved lemons
- Gelatine
- Egg yolks
- Egg whites
- Lemon or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut beef into finger-thick but small pieces and beat them slightly
2. Season with salt, pepper, grated onion and a little flour
3. Heat the roasting pan and add a good amount of butter in proportion to the meat
4. When it begins to brown, place the meat with the seasoned side down and fry briskly, being careful not to burn it
5. Season the other side similarly, turn when the underside is brown, and fry briskly until the meat is cooked and red inside, but not bloody
6. Remove the meat and set aside to cool
7. Put the pan back on the heat with the sauce, stirring in a little mixed mustard, a little tarragon vinegar and a little vinegar of preserved lemons until it is well-flavored but not sour
8. Stir well until it boils and becomes smooth, then pour through a sieve onto a not entirely flat dish and place the meat in it
9. Make an aspic as for roast in aspic, but without vinegar and laurel leaves
10. When this aspic has become so cold that it begins to set, pour it over the beef roast
11. When serving, decorate with egg yolks and whites laid in strips on top of the aspic, as well as with a little chopped lemon or lemon juice

title: Boghvedegrød (Buckwheat Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 625 Gr (1 1/2 S) fine, hulled buckwheat grains
- 5 L (5 Potter) skimmed milk
- 3 bottles pale ale

## Directions

1. When the milk boils, sprinkle the grains into it while stirring.
2. This porridge only needs 1/4 hour of cooking.
3. Serve with butter and sweetened pale ale.
4. Leftover porridge can be used for ale porridge.

title: Boghvedegrød (Buckwheat Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints fine, rinsed buckwheat grits
- 4 pots milk (preferably sweet)
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Heat the pot and grease it inside with lard to prevent sticking
2. Bring 4 pots of milk to a boil
3. Sprinkle the buckwheat grits quickly into the milk at the side where it boils most vigorously, stirring constantly until it returns to a boil
4. Continue stirring regularly until the porridge is done
5. Add salt at the end
6. If the porridge becomes too thick, thin it with a little boiling milk
7. Most can be served as an accompaniment to this porridge
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Boiled Fish with Oyster Sauce (Kogt Fisk med Østers-Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds cod
- 1 pint oysters
- 1 ounce butter
- 1 ounce flour
- 1 egg yolk
- ½ teaspoon salt
- Ground white pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and boil 2 pounds of cod in salt water according to instructions.
2. Once the fish is done, remove the pot from heat and set aside.
3. In the meantime, place 1 pint of oysters over heat to cook in their own sauce.
4. Once cooked, strain the oysters and return the sauce to heat to bring it to a boil.
5. Melt 1 ounce of butter in a frying pan over heat, stir in 1 ounce of flour, and when it bubbles, stir in the boiling oyster sauce.
6. Even out the sauce with 1 well-beaten egg yolk, ½ teaspoon salt, and a little ground white pepper.
7. Add the oysters and serve the sauce in a sauce boat.

title: Boiled Smoked Ham with Madeira
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- One smoked, lightly salted ham, approximately 4 kg (8 lbs)
- 1 bottle Madeira
- Butter
- Flour
- Strong broth, reduced
- Vegetables

## Directions

1. Blanch the smoked, lightly salted ham over heat in abundant boiling water, where it must boil very gently for 2½ hours, or until the rind easily comes off.
2. Transfer the ham to another pot that is no larger than strictly necessary, and cover with 1 bottle of Madeira.
3. Let it boil for ½ hour very gently under a very tightly fitting lid, so that all the wine does not evaporate.
4. At serving, cover it with a gentle, reduced, strong broth and serve hot with vegetables.
5. Make a sauce by baking butter and flour together, and thin it with a mixture of the reduced broth and the Madeira in which the ham was cooked.

title: Boiled, Salted Mackerel (Kogt, saltet Makrel)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salted mackerel
- Cold butter
- Pepper
- Lemon slices
- Fine wheat bread

## Directions

1. Soak the fish for a few hours in lukewarm water, changing the water several times.
2. Then wrap it loosely in cloth and place in cold water, set over heat and let it just come to a boil.
3. Pour out all the water at once and pour boiling water over the fish.
4. Let this come to a boil, then remove it, dry it and place on a platter.
5. Add cold butter and pepper and place in the oven for a couple of minutes.
6. Serve with lemon slices and fine wheat bread.

title: Boller i Sauce (Meatballs in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Broth and water in equal parts
- Meat or fish meatballs
- Butter
- Onion
- Black pepper
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Flour
- Oysters, mussels, mushrooms, capers, celery, or other additions as desired
- Wine and lemon juice (optional)
- Egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. Put broth and water in equal parts on the heat, in which cook meat or fish meatballs
2. When cooked, remove them and strain the broth
3. Heat a good piece of butter on the heat, in which stir some grated onion, some ground pepper, nutmeg, salt and flour, which is thinned with the broth until it has a proper thickness
4. Add the meatballs and cook them a little in it
5. In the same way the meatballs can be prepared with oysters, mussels, mushrooms, capers, celery, or whatever you wish to use; the onions can be omitted and you can then add a little wine and lemon juice and thicken the sauce with a couple of egg yolks

title: Bondepige med Slør (Peasant Girl with a Veil)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- grated rye bread
- butter
- sugar
- apple sauce or jam
- whipped cream
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Bake grated rye bread with butter and sugar in a pan and spread on a flat dish while still warm.
2. Layer with apple sauce or jam, then another layer of bread.
3. Cover completely with whipped cream and decorate with currant jelly.

title: Bordelaise-Sauce (Bordelaise Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A couple of tablespoons finely chopped shallots
- A clove of garlic
- Butter
- ¼ liter (1 pint) red wine
- ½ liter (½ pot) good brown sauce
- 125 grams (¼ pound) fresh or pickled mushrooms
- A couple of tablespoons tomato purée
- 1 glass Madeira
- Paprika
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 50 to 75 grams (10 to 15 ounces) small marrow dumplings

## Directions

1. A couple of tablespoons finely chopped shallots, among which there should preferably be a clove of garlic, are sautéed in butter and covered with a good ¼ liter red wine.
2. This boils until some of the wine has evaporated, then ½ liter good, brown sauce is added, made as usual but it must be strong.
3. In the sauce, some mushrooms (125 grams fresh or pickled) cut into thin slices are given a boil, then a couple of tablespoons tomato purée is stirred in, 1 glass Madeira, a little paprika, and 1 teaspoon chopped parsley.
4. Just before this sauce is served, 50 to 75 grams small marrow dumplings, blanched in boiling water, are placed in it.
5. The marrow can be omitted.

title: Bordure-Rand (Decorative Border)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 375 grams flour (approximately 1/4 lb)
- Egg white
- Flour for binding

## Directions

1. Mix eggs and flour to form a fairly firm dough that can be easily rolled out
2. Roll out a strip 5 centimeters (2 inches) wide, fairly thin and long enough to reach around the inside edge of the dish
3. Cut the border perfectly straight
4. Form figures on the upper edge with a border iron, or cut simply into points with a knife, and make a clear pattern with a pastry bag
5. Lay the border to dry and set it up only when it has begun to stiffen
6. Fix it to the dish with egg white mixed thick with flour
7. Suitable for meat, fish and vegetables

title: Bortfeldske Roer (Browned Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beets
- Butter
- Bouillon
- Sugar
- Salt
- Flour

## Directions

1. The beets should not be too large, but rather firm and crisp
2. Cut into strips and place in boiling water, then immediately remove to a colander and rinse with cold water
3. Brown some butter in a saucepan and cook the beets in it until they become golden brown
4. Thin with bouillon and cook until tender
5. Add sugar and salt to taste and thicken with a tablespoon of flour first mixed in cold water
