6. Do not leave them cleaned for long before using as they will otherwise become black unless well covered
7. Store best in the cellar in a pile of sand
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Boston Mais-Brod (Boston Corn Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups sour milk
- 2/3 cup molasses
- 1 cup wheat flour
- 4 cups cornmeal
- 2 teaspoons soda

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup sweet milk with 2 cups sour milk.
2. Add 2/3 cup molasses, 1 cup wheat flour, 4 cups cornmeal, and 2 teaspoons soda.
3. Bake for 3 hours.

title: Bouillon (Beef Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot water per 1 pound beef
- Beef with bones (such as shank, brisket, leg, breast, etc.)
- Salt
- Carrots
- Parsley roots
- Celery
- Onions (peeled)
- Peppercorns
- Ginger (small piece)
- Candy sugar (small piece)
- Twine for bundling herbs

## Directions

1. Rinse the beef and place it in a pot with cold water, allowing 1 pot of water per pound of beef. The water should stand slightly above the meat, and the pot should be nearly full with a tightly fitting lid.
2. Bring the broth to a gentle boil over low heat until it begins to foam. Add a little salt, which helps bring the foam to the surface more readily.
3. Keep the broth simmering under a closed lid with even heat, skimming frequently until all impurities are removed.
4. When clean, add several well-rinsed whole soup vegetables: carrots, parsley roots, and celery, as well as a bundle of herbs tied with twine.
5. Add peeled onions, a few whole peppercorns, a small piece of ginger, and a small piece of candy sugar.
6. Continue simmering the broth over even heat with gentle cooking until the vegetables are tender, then remove them. Continue cooking the meat until it is completely tender.
7. When the broth is finished cooking, strain it and let it rest quietly for a quarter hour, then skim off the fat.
8. Serve the bouillon with an egg yolk and toasted bread slices, or use it as a base for other soups and sauces.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Bouillon de Poche (Meat Bouillon)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds beef shank, chopped
- 2 pounds veal shank
- 1 not too young hen
- 8 pots water

## Directions

1. Chop the beef shank and veal shank. If the hen is to be used whole later, do not chop it; otherwise chop it as well.
2. Place all the meat over heat with 8 pots water and cook in the same manner as Bouillon No. 1.
3. When the soup is strained, pour it back into the pot without the sediment, and now cook it over gentle heat with regular stirring until it becomes brown and even, approximately like syrup.
4. It is then ready and poured onto a flat dish to cool and can be kept for a long time.
5. When you wish to use it, cut off appropriate pieces, which are dissolved in boiling water.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Bowle af Bordeaux Vin (Bordeaux Wine Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle Bordeaux wine
- 2 wine glasses Sherry
- 1 wine glass Maraschino
- Crushed sugar
- 1 half bottle soda water

## Directions

1. Mix the wine, sugar, and liqueur to taste and chill on ice, then pour into a bowl or terrine. The soda water, also chilled, is added just before serving. Instead of Maraschino liqueur, one can use a glass of Curacao and a little lemon, or instead of lemon, 1 pint of strawberries or raspberries, or 4-5 peaches.

title: Bread Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- cream
- lemon rind
- salt
- French bread
- milk
- butter
- white sugar

## Directions

1. For each egg, take a spoonful of cream
2. Whip them together and add grated lemon rind and salt
3. Dip slices of French bread in milk and fry in butter
4. When fried on both sides, pour the batter over and bake over good heat, but not over a flame from fire, until it shines on top
5. It does not need to be turned, and is arranged with white sugar on top

title: Breaded Pork Tenderloin
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork tenderloin
- Egg
- Bread
- Butter
- Parsley
- Lemon slices
- Tomato slices
- Cooked vegetables

## Directions

1. Beat the pork tenderloin flat, dip it in egg and bread, and roast it on the pan in browned butter.
2. Arrange with the butter poured over.
3. Garnish with parsley, lemon and tomato slices.
4. Pork tenderloin can be served garnished with cooked vegetables.
5. Serve with creamed butter.

title: Brioche
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg flour
- 2 eggs
- 50 g sugar
- a little cardamom
- 50 g yeast, dissolved in 1/4 L lukewarm, half-skimmed milk
- 1/4 kg margarine

## Directions

1. Mix the flour, eggs, sugar, cardamom, and yeast dissolved in milk well together
2. Roll out the dough with a rolling pin
3. Spread with 1/4 kg margarine, fold together and roll out repeatedly
4. Form into balls
5. Make a small indentation on top of each and place a small ball the size of a small nut in it
6. Brush with egg and let rise
7. Bake in a well-heated oven

title: Brissel-Gratin (Sweetbread Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Par Brisler
- Smør
- Mel
- Brisselsuppe
- Peber
- Salt
- 1 Glas Sherry
- 4 Æg
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) Fløde eller Mælk

## Directions

1. Brislerne renses og koges som i Nr. 6 og skæres derefter i Skiver.
2. Til Saucen bages Smør og Mel, som fortyndes med Brisselsuppen.
3. Deri kommer Peber, Salt og 1 Glas Sherry.
4. Saucen hældes over de varme Brisler, der dækkes med 4 Æg, godt piskede med 1/4 L (1 Pægl) Fløde eller Mælk.
5. Bages 1 Kvarter.

title: Brissel-Suppe (Sweetbread Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 L (5 pots) economical stock
- 0.5 sweetbreads
- Cold water
- 1 bunch of herbs
- 125 g (1/4 lb) butter or vegetable margarine
- 125 g (1/4 lb) flour
- 6 egg yolks
- Lemon juice
- Madeira wine
- Ground white pepper
- Toasted bread

## Directions

1. Place the sweetbreads in cold water and blanch before adding to the stock in which a good bunch of herbs has been cooked
2. When the sweetbreads are white and firm, remove them and cut into very fine, small dice, placing them in the tureen
3. Brown 125 g (1/4 lb) butter or vegetable margarine with 125 g (1/4 lb) flour and dilute with 5 L (5 pots) stock
4. Thicken with 6 egg yolks and season with lemon juice, Madeira and ground white pepper
5. Pour the soup over the sweetbreads when serving
6. Serve with toasted bread

title: Brokfugle (Roasted Ruff)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ruff
- Gizzard, heart and liver
- French bread slices

## Directions

1. Pluck and roast the ruff as for partridge (see No. 960).
2. The gizzard, heart and liver can be prepared like woodcock farce and used to stuff the birds before roasting, or the farce can be served on slices of French bread placed around the birds. (See Baking woodcock bread, No. 979).

title: Brombær (Blackberries)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) blackberries
- 375 gr (3/4 lb) powdered loaf sugar
- 1.5 dl (1/2 pint) water

## Directions

1. Once the sugar is boiled together with the water, add the berries and give them only one boil in the syrup.
2. Pour them onto a colander and reduce the syrup until it is smooth.
3. Then add the berries to blend with the syrup and give them one boil before pouring them into the crock.
4. Seal immediately.

title: Brombær- og Ribs-Gelé (Blackberry and Currant Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Blackberries
- Red currant juice, equal weight to blackberry juice
- 1/2 kg sugar (or corresponding weight to combined juices)
- 1 1/4 dl water

## Directions

1. Since blackberries are quite sweet, the best jelly is obtained by mixing blackberry and currant juice, half of each.
2. Place the blackberries in a pot and set over heat until they begin to release their juices well; then lift the pot off and crush the berries with a spoon before pouring into a scalded and tightly wrung jelly cloth.
3. The next day, weigh the juice and mix with an equal weight of completely clear currant juice without sugar.
4. After the sugar has been weighed and cooked as mentioned in No. 93, pour the juice into it: cook and skim for 10 minutes.
5. Fill the jelly into cups, which are tied up when it is partially cold and has begun to set.
6. The pulp remaining in the cloth is pressed through a very coarse sieve and used for Russian marmalade.

title: Brombærsaft (Blackberry Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Blackberries
- 3/4 kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar or farina per 1 liter (1 pot) juice

## Directions

1. Blackberries can be prepared exactly like blueberries.
2. Boil as described in the blueberry recipe.

title: Brosten
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ pound butter, clarified
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 vanilla pod, ground
- 1 pound potato starch
- 6 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Clarify the butter and stir well
2. Add 1 pound powdered sugar and stir well for 1 hour
3. While stirring continuously, add 6 egg yolks
4. Add 1 ground vanilla pod
5. Add 1 pound potato starch
6. Finally fold in 6 whipped egg whites
7. Bake in small molds at good heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brosten (Caramel Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ pound butter
- 1 pound finely ground powdered sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 stick ground vanilla
- 1 pound potato flour
- 6 beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Clarify ¾ pound butter and stir well
2. Add 1 pound finely ground powdered sugar and stir well for 1 hour
3. Add 6 egg yolks while stirring constantly
4. Add 1 stick ground vanilla
5. Add 1 pound potato flour
6. Finally add 6 beaten egg whites
7. Bake at good heat in small forms
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brovl (Brovl Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- half a pound powdered sugar
- 4 ounces butter
- 1.5 teaspoons baking powder
- 3 whole eggs
- good thick cream

## Directions

1. Blend together 1 pound flour, half a pound powdered sugar, 4 ounces butter, 1.5 teaspoons baking powder and 3 whole eggs.
2. Add as much good thick cream as it can take.
3. Roll out in whatever molds you wish.
4. Bake at even heat.

title: Brulot af Arrak (Flamed Arrack)
body:
## Ingredients

- White sugar
- Arrack

## Directions

1. Crush white sugar and place it in a saucepan or other suitable vessel. Pour the Arrack over it and ignite it, stirring and moving it up and down with a spoon. When the sugar is melted, extinguish the flame by placing a lid over it, and the drink is ready to serve.

title: Brun Flöde-Sauce (Brown Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal parts butter and thick sour cream
- 1 teaspoon flour
- Meat skimmings
- Soy sauce

## Directions

1. Take equal amounts of butter and thick sour cream
2. Brown the butter in a pan and brown 1 teaspoon flour in it
3. Add a couple spoonfuls of skimmings
4. When this boils, add the cream and stir strongly until the sauce boils
5. If you have no skimmings, do not use flour either, but then the cream must be good
6. Should it separate, take a little more cream, and if it becomes too white from this, remedy it with soy sauce
7. This sauce is for veal and poultry and can be used for mutton and beef roast if necessary
8. For poultry avoid soy sauce

title: Brun Fraase-Suppe à l'anglaise (Brown Goose Giblet Soup English Style)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 goose giblets
- 125 Gr (1/4 tt) Butter, Margarine or Palmin
- 2 chopped onions
- 6 L (6 Pots) boiling water
- Salt
- Kulør (caramel coloring)
- Paprika
- Sherry
- Sago flour or Butter and Flour
- Egg royal (Nr. 507)
- Puff pastry strips

## Directions

1. Clean the giblets as described for Giblet Soup (Nr. 372)
2. Brown the giblets in 125 Gr (1/4 tt) butter, margarine or Palmin together with 2 chopped onions
3. Add 6 L (6 Pots) boiling water, salt, kulør and a little paprika to the pot
4. Boil the giblets 2 to 3 hours
5. When tender, remove and keep warm, strain the soup, skim off the fat, and season with Sherry
6. The soup must have a nice brown color and can be thickened either with sago flour or with butter and flour browned together and diluted with the soup
7. The giblets are eaten with the soup and can be placed in the tureen or served separately on a platter, garnished with puff pastry strips
8. Top the soup with egg royal (Nr. 507)

title: Brun Kastanie-Suppe (Brown Chestnut Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 kg (1.5 S) chestnuts
- 5 L (5 pots) brown spare broth
- 75 grams (15 skein) butter
- 50 grams (10 skein) flour
- Sherry
- couleur
- glazed chestnuts or small fillets of roasted or cooked chicken, squab, doves or game birds
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Place the chestnuts in boiling water for a few minutes until the shell and the innermost brown skin are easily removed.
2. Then cook them tender in some of the brown spare broth and rub them through a sieve with constant thinning with the rest of the broth.
3. Brown 75 grams (15 skein) butter, and bake 50 grams (10 skein) flour in it, which is thinned with the chestnut soup, which then only is brought to a boil.
4. Add a little Sherry and couleur to the soup if needed.
5. It can be served with glazed chestnuts or with small fillets cut from roasted or cooked chicken, squab, doves or game birds.
6. Serve with toasted bread.

title: Brun Kjodsuppe (Brown Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean meat and pieces unsuitable for other uses
- Butter
- Boiling water
- Salt
- Small root vegetables
- Celery fibers
- Water
- Meat dumplings or fish dumplings

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces, but not thin ones. Brown them in butter with a little boiling water and add salt and roots. Use small roots as they cook apart anyway. For celery, use the fibers since the broth should not be white. Wash and rub the roots in several waters and let them soak in clean water for several hours before adding. Skim the broth well as it cooks. Thin it first with water, then with white meat broth. The roots that should be in the finished broth are cooked in white broth or water. It is most common to have only dumplings in the broth, either meat dumplings or fish dumplings.

title: Brun Sauce (Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- gravy or strong bouillon
- port wine or sherry (optional)

## Directions

1. Brown butter and flour to a light brown in a saucepan.
2. Thin with gravy or, if lacking, with strong bouillon.
3. Bring to a boil.
4. If desired, add a little port wine or sherry.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brun Sky (Brown Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/2 pound beef kidney, finely chopped
- approximately 6 pounds beef, medium cut
- peeled onions
- cleaned carrots, sliced
- celery, sliced
- meat and bone scraps
- roasting bones, chopped
- salt
- 1 soup bunch
- about 10 peppercorns
- water
- bouillon as needed

## Directions

1. In a heavy-bottomed casserole, melt 1/4 pound butter and let it run around the bottom.
2. Add 1/2 pound finely chopped beef kidney.
3. Slice approximately 6 pounds of medium-cut beef and lay it on the bottom of the casserole.
4. Over this, place peeled onions and slices of cleaned carrots and celery.
5. On top of this, layer scraps of meat and bone and well-rinsed roasting bones that have been chopped.
6. Place the whole over gentle heat for one hour, or better yet in a fairly warm oven, and watch that it does not burn. If necessary, thin the mixture with a little bouillon.
7. Then place the whole briefly over stronger heat, and pour in enough water so that it just covers the mixture.
8. Add a little salt, a small soup bunch, and about 10 peppercorns, then cook covered under gentle heat for approximately 4 to 5 hours.
9. Remove the vegetables and soup bunch, and when the gravy is done, strain it and set it aside to cool.
10. The longer the gravy cooks, the firmer it becomes, and the longer it can be kept.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brun Sky-Sauce til Steg (Brown Gravy Sauce for Roast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Gravy from the roast
- Salt
- Sugar
- Coloring (if needed)

## Directions

1. Brown the butter and bake flour in it, which is thinned with the gravy from the roast; this must be strained beforehand and all fat well skimmed
2. The sauce must simmer very gently for 1 quarter hour after it is thinned with the gravy
3. It should be made fairly thin, and the portion should be generously large, since the sauce reduces somewhat during cooking, but in return becomes thicker the longer it cooks
4. Prepare the sauce with salt, a little sugar and coloring if it needs more color

title: Brun Suppe (lieret) (Brown Thickened Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L soup
- 1 kg cooked asparagus
- 125 gr butter
- 125 gr flour
- 4 egg yolks
- asparagus water
- cold water

## Directions

1. Brown 125 gr butter and bake 125 gr flour in it
2. Thin with 4 L brown spare soup together with the asparagus water
3. Beat 4 egg yolks with a couple of spoonfuls of cold water and stir into the soup

title: Brun Suppe (lieret) (Brown Thickened Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 Gr (1/4 S) Butter
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) Flour
- 4 L (4 Pots) brown spare soup
- Asparagus water
- 4 Egg yolks
- Cold water
- Sherry
- 1 Kg (2 S) cooked asparagus

## Directions

1. Brown 125 Gr (1/4 S) butter, and bake 125 Gr (1/4 S) flour in it
2. Dilute with 4 L (4 Pots) brown spare soup together with the asparagus water
3. Whisk 4 egg yolks with a couple of spoonfuls of cold water and stir into the soup, which must not then come to a boil
4. Season with a little Sherry, and add the asparagus

title: Brun-Kager (Brown Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 13 pounds butter
- 4 pounds syrup
- 5 pounds flour
- 1/2 pound sweet almonds, sliced into fine strips
- zest of 2 lemons, chopped
- 5 quintal ground cloves
- 6 quintal potassium carbonate, dissolved in rose water
- 6 quintal chopped orange peel
- 8 quintal dried yeast, first dissolved in a cup of lukewarm milk
- 1 pound coarsely crushed candy sugar

## Directions

1. Clarify the butter and give it just one boil with the syrup, then while it is still warm, stir in: the flour, sweet almonds in fine strips, the chopped lemon zest, ground cloves, potassium carbonate dissolved in rose water, chopped orange peel, and the dried yeast first dissolved in lukewarm milk. The entire mixture must be well stirred and thoroughly kneaded the evening before the cakes are to be baked, and stand in a warm place overnight. The next day, knead the dough again with the coarsely crushed candy sugar, and then bake the cakes very brown in the oven.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brune Sirupskager (Brown Syrup Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) dark syrup
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) margarine
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) chopped almonds
- 65 gr (13 quintals) chopped candied citrus peel
- 65 gr (13 quintals) chopped orange peel
- 5 gr (1 quintal) cloves
- 10 gr (2 quintals) cinnamon
- 5 gr (1 quintal) ginger
- 1.25 dl (1/2 pint) rose water
- 15 gr (3 quintals) potash
- 1/4 Kg (1.5 lb) flour

## Directions

1. Place the syrup, powdered sugar, margarine, chopped almonds, candied citrus peel, orange peel, cloves, cinnamon, ginger, and rose water in a pot
2. Bring to a boil, then add the potash and remove from heat
3. Sprinkle in the flour and work well with a spoon, as the dough is too soft to handle by hand
4. Let it rest covered for one week
5. Work the dough well on a board and roll very thin with a little flour
6. Cut the cakes with a glass or with cake cutters in various shapes
7. Butter the baking sheet and place the cakes not too close together
8. Brush with egg and top each with an almond
9. Bake at good heat
10. Store in tin boxes for an extended period. Note: candied citrus peel and almonds can be omitted from the dough and the cakes will still be good

title: Brunede Batfeldske Roer (Browned Beet Field Roots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small beet roots
- Butter
- Sugar
- Coloring
- Broth or gravy
- Salt
- Ground pepper

## Directions

1. Usually choose the smallest roots, which are less often tough
2. Peel, rinse, and cut them into long thin strips
3. Brown them in butter and sugar
4. When they have taken on a brownish color, a little coloring can be added, as well as a small amount of broth or gravy, and cook the roots tender in this
5. Usually the liquid will have evaporated; season with salt and ground pepper

title: Brunede Kartofler (Browned Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small chestnut potatoes
- Powdered sugar
- Water
- Butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Boil the small potatoes and peel them while hot.
2. Brown a good spoonful of powdered sugar in a frying pan.
3. As soon as it is brown but not burnt, add a couple of spoonfuls of water and a small piece of butter or margarine and let it boil together with the sugar.
4. Pour in the potatoes and turn them carefully.
5. Let them boil in the sugar until they acquire a beautiful brown color.
6. For a more attractive result, use small chestnut potatoes rather than large mealy ones cut into pieces.

title: Brunede Kartofler (Browned Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Powdered sugar or syrup
- Cooked peeled potatoes

## Directions

1. Brown a piece of butter in the pan, stir in powdered sugar or syrup and cooked peeled potatoes cut into pieces and cook slowly until they are brown.

title: Brunet Hvidkaal (Browned White Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Sugar
- White cabbage
- Salt
- Meat gravy

## Directions

1. A good piece of butter is browned, and sugar is stirred into it; the cabbage must be cut very fine and cooked in it over even heat with a closed lid until it is tender, then salt is added. It is good, if you have it, to add a little meat gravy to the cabbage when it is finished.

title: Brunet Hvidkaalsuppe (Browned White Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Et Fjerdingspund Smør (a quarter pound of butter)
- 2 Spiseskefulde Puddersukker (2 tablespoons powdered sugar)
- Et ituskaaret, fast Hvidkaalshoved (a cut-up firm head of white cabbage)
- 5 Potter Suppe (5 pots of broth)
- Kjødboller (meatballs)

## Directions

1. Brown the butter with 2 tablespoons of powdered sugar until light brown.
2. Add the strips of cabbage (from a firm head with outer leaves removed) and brown under a lid, stirring carefully, until the cabbage is tender.
3. Gradually thin the cabbage with 5 pots of broth.
4. When the broth boils, top with meatballs.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brunet Smør (Browned Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Margarine
- Butter

## Directions

1. A mixture of the best margarine and good butter can be browned together in a pan just before serving.
2. It is sufficiently brown as soon as it has stopped foaming; then, if desired, the sediment can be strained out before it is poured into the sauce bowl.
3. To browned butter can be added lemon juice or vinegar, if it suits the dish it is to be served with.
4. Naturally, it is preferable to use only butter for this, but margarine is excellent for mixing with it.

title: Brunet Surkaal (Browned Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauerkraut
- Butter
- Bouillon
- Pepper
- Flour
- Brown syrup

## Directions

1. Rinse the sauerkraut and drain it, brown with butter over gentle heat
2. Add bouillon and pepper, then cook until done
3. Thicken with a roux of butter and flour that has been fried together, and add a little brown syrup
4. Bring to a boil

title: Brunkaal (Braised Brown Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- White cabbage
- Butter or lard
- Sugar
- Coloring (optional)
- Liquid (broth, gravy, or water)
- Salt

## Directions

1. Slice the white cabbage and place it in a pot in which a good slice of butter or lard has been browned.
2. Add a spoonful of sugar and brown the cabbage with regular stirring so it does not burn.
3. To speed up browning, coloring may be added.
4. Once the cabbage has sufficient color, add a little liquid, which can be broth, gravy, or water.
5. Steam the cabbage until tender under a lid.
6. Season with salt and more sugar if necessary.
7. Serve with boiled sausages, boiled and fried bacon.
8. A piece of lightly salted, striped bacon can be rinsed, placed in the browned cabbage, and cooked in it, to be eaten with the cabbage.

title: Brunkaal (Braised Red Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- white cabbage
- butter or wine
- sugar
- color
- broth, gravy or water
- salt

## Directions

1. Slice white cabbage
2. Brown a good slice of butter or wine with a spoonful of sugar in a pot
3. Add the cabbage and brown it with regular stirring so it does not burn
4. If needed, add color to make it brown faster
5. When it has sufficient color, add a little liquid which can be broth, gravy or water
6. Braise it tender under a lid
7. Season with salt and more sugar if necessary

title: Brunkaal-Suppe (Brown Cabbage Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 medium white cabbage head
- Butter
- 1 Dessertspoonful Powdered sugar
- 5 L (5 Pots) thin spare soup
- Kulør (caramel coloring)

## Directions

1. Slice the white cabbage finely
2. Brown in butter and 1 dessertspoonful powdered sugar
3. Pour 5 L (5 Pots) thin spare soup over it
4. If the soup does not have sufficient color, add a little kulør
5. Let it boil for at least 2 hours

title: Brunsviger (Danish Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine wheat flour
- 1/4 pound butter
- 12 grams sugar
- 12 grams washed raisins
- 12 grams washed currants
- 12 grams sweet, scalded and chopped almonds
- 3 eggs
- a little cardamom
- 1 tablespoon yeast
- milk

## Directions

1. Lay the flour on a baking table and add 36 grams cold butter, the eggs, cardamom, currants, yeast, sugar, and enough milk to make a soft wheat bread dough.
2. Knead well with the hands.
3. Roll out the dough to half a finger's thickness in a round or oval shape.
4. Fold up the dough around the edge into tongues; in every other tongue place almonds, in the other raisins with a small lump of remaining butter.
5. Melt the rest of the butter and brush the cake with it, then sprinkle a good layer of ground cinnamon and powdered sugar.
6. If desired, thin slices of citron peel can be placed here and there.
7. Let the cake rise for 1/2 hour before it goes in the oven.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brunsviger-Kager (Brunsviger Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams (1/4 lb) margarine
- 2 egg yolks
- 125 grams (1/4 lb) icing sugar
- 75 grams (15 quintal) ground almonds
- zest of one lemon or a few drops of lemon essence
- 125 grams (1/4 lb) flour
- 2 egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. Mix the margarine well with 2 egg yolks, icing sugar, ground almonds, lemon zest or lemon drops, and flour
2. Then mix in the 2 beaten egg whites
3. Spread the dough thinly on a cold, buttered baking sheet
4. Sprinkle with chopped almonds, sugar and caraway seeds
5. Bake quickly
6. Cut into small square cakes while still warm

title: Brystsukker (Breast Sugar / Fondant)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 2 pints water
- a little egg white
- flavoring such as cognac, rum, lemon juice, orange juice, chocolate, peppermint water, or vanilla

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar with water and a little egg white. During cooking, skim it well, and to test if it is cooked enough, dip a spoon in water and then in the sugar. When you bite the sugar you get on the spoon, it must release from your teeth; as long as it sticks, it is not cooked enough. The sugar can either be poured hot onto a buttered sheet and when it is almost cold, cut into square pieces; or it can be molded in forms created from dry starch on a sheet. Different flavors are given by the addition of cognac, rum, lemon or orange juice, chocolate, peppermint water, vanilla, and similar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brændte Mandler (Burned Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- half a pint water
- 60 quintal sweet almonds
- 4 quintal ground Ceylon cinnamon
- 1/2 quintal ground cloves
- 1/2 quintal ground cardamom
- 1/2 quintal borax
- coarse powdered sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar in water and cook to syrup. Then add the sweet almonds, Ceylon cinnamon, ground cloves, ground cardamom, and borax. Cook them with constant stirring until they become light brown and begin to crack, then spread them on a sheet and sprinkle with coarse powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Bröd-Budding (Bread Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 pounds wheat bread
- 5 ounces butter
- 10 eggs
- 4 ounces raisins
- Nutmeg, salt, and a little sugar

## Directions

1. Scrape off the crust of 14 pounds of wheat bread on a grater, cut into smaller pieces
2. Dip in cold water and let sit covered for a couple hours
3. Press the bread with a spoon, add 5 ounces butter and stir warm, not hot, for half an hour or until the batter becomes white
4. Mix in 10 well-beaten eggs, raisins, nutmeg, salt, and a little sugar
5. Cook for 2.5 hours and serve with sweet sauce

title: Brödboller (Bread Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 French bread rolls (or small wheat bread rolls)
- 5 eggs
- 1 good pound butter
- Grated bread
- Flour

## Directions

1. Remove all crust and hard edges from the bread, which is dipped in milk and stirred with the melted butter, egg yolks and finally the egg whites, which are whipped to stiff peaks.
2. Pour the mixture into a form that is greased with butter and sprinkled with grated bread, and cook in the oven.
3. When done, take it out, cut into pieces and serve in the soup.
4. Note: From the same mixture small round balls can be made, which are placed in boiling salted water and are done when they rise to the top.

title: Brød-Budding (Bread Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- one large French bread
- milk
- 200 grams margarine
- 12 egg yolks
- egg whites, beaten
- salt
- sugar
- crushed almonds, finely cut citron peel, raisins, orange essence, grated lemon zest, or 3 crushed vanilla tablets (optional)

## Directions

1. From a large French bread, cut off the crust, and cut the bread into thick slices, which are dipped in milk and left covered for a couple of hours, after which they are mashed with a spoon.
2. 200 grams of margarine is melted, and the bread is baked in it until it is well combined.
3. When the batter is slightly cooled, it is stirred with 12 egg yolks and finally with the beaten egg whites.
4. A little salt and sugar are added to taste, and the pudding can be seasoned either with crushed almonds, finely cut citron peel, raisins, orange essence, grated lemon zest, or 3 crushed vanilla tablets.
5. Serve with fruit sauces, ginger sauce, or red wine sauce.

title: Brød-Croutons (Bread Croutons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Old French bread, crust removed
- Butter or Palmin

## Directions

1. Cut the bread into slices
2. Form into various shapes using a knife or cookie cutter
3. Bake until light golden brown in butter or Palmin on a frying pan
4. Place on paper to drain the fat
5. Serve with soup or as garnish around various dishes

title: Brød-Frikadeller (Bread Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Cents Toast or equivalent amount of old bread
- 1 Pint Milk
- 3 Eggs
- 3 Tablespoons melted Butter
- grated Lemon Peel
- Powdered Sugar
- a little Salt
- a little Currants (optional)

## Directions

1. Pound the toast or bread fine and mix with 1 Pint Milk, letting it stand for one hour
2. Stir well with 3 Eggs (added 3 at a time), 3 Tablespoons melted Butter, grated Lemon Peel, Powdered Sugar, a little Salt, and if desired, a little Currants
3. Fry in clarified fat for a fairly long time so they become hard on the outside
4. Serve with powdered sugar sprinkled on top

title: Brød-Pletter (Bread Pancake Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Some slices not too fresh French bread
- Milk
- Powdered sugar
- Powdered cinnamon
- Crushed bitter almonds
- Wheat flour
- 4 Eggs
- Sweet cream
- 0.5 Pint melted butter

## Directions

1. Soak some slices of not too fresh French bread in milk.
2. After taking them up, press them apart with a spoon.
3. Add powdered sugar, a little powdered cinnamon, some crushed bitter almonds, and a couple of tablespoonfuls wheat flour.
4. Beat 4 eggs with a little sweet cream and add to the rest.
5. Finally stir in 0.5 pint melted butter.
6. Bake somewhat thick cakes from this.
7. Put butter on for each new pan full.
8. Serve with powdered sugar and powdered cinnamon.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brød-Sokkel (Bread Pedestal)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Malted bread
- Egg white
- Flour

## Directions

1. Use the bottom crust of malted bread to create the pedestal
2. Split the bread and give the pedestal the desired height
3. Glue it to the dish with egg white mixed with flour
4. Fasten the decorative skewers at the ends, decorated with flowers, lettuce or available garnish
5. Arrange vegetables on the sides

title: Brød-Suppe (Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound rye bread
- 3 quarts water
- lemon zest
- cinnamon
- 1 glass white wine
- juice and zest of 2 lemons
- sugar
- 1/2 pint thick cream

## Directions

1. Slice the rye bread small and boil in 3 quarts of water until smooth
2. Rub through a sieve and set back on heat with lemon zest and cinnamon
3. When the soup boils, add 1 glass of white wine, juice and zest of 2 lemons, and sugar
4. Whip 1/2 pint of thick cream to foam in the terrine and pour the soup over it while whisking with a whisk
5. If you do not have sliced bread to use, the bread can be grated, and then the soup does not need to be strained

title: Brød-Suppe med Flødeskum (Bread Soup with Cream Foam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Old rye bread
- ½ L cream
- Raisins
- Lemon peel
- Cinnamon
- Rhubarb juice
- Sherry
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Soak old rye bread in water for a few hours and cook it.
2. Press it through a coarse sieve with the help of boiling water until the soup becomes suitably thin and there is sufficient quantity.
3. Return the bread soup to the fire to cook with raisins, lemon peel and whole cinnamon.
4. Prepare with rhubarb juice, sherry and sugar or with jam.
5. Bread soup can be served with cooked apples, or add a well-sweetened gooseberry compote to the serving bowl.
6. Serve with cream foam.

title: Brød-Suppe med Æg (Bread Soup with Egg)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread soup
- 3-4 egg yolks
- Powdered sugar
- Ribs or rhubarb juice
- Sherry

## Directions

1. The bread soup for this should be rather thin, as the eggs thicken it considerably.
2. Stir 3-4 egg yolks in the serving bowl with powdered sugar, and add the hot soup to it while stirring constantly.
3. Prepare with currant or rhubarb juice and sherry.

title: Brødboller (Bread Balls)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 French bread loaf
- Milk
- 100 grams (20 dram) butter
- 6 eggs
- Salt
- Sugar
- Cardamom

## Directions

1. Cut away the crust from a French bread loaf and cut the bread into thick slices
2. Dip the slices in milk and let stand covered for a couple of hours
3. Rub the bread with a wooden spoon until well combined
4. Melt the butter and bake the bread in it until it is well bound together
5. When the dough has cooled slightly, stir it with 6 eggs, salt, sugar, and cardamom
6. Form into balls and cook in salted water

title: Brødboller (Bread Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 small French breads or wheat rolls
- 1/2 pound butter
- 5 eggs
- salt to taste
- sugar to taste
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scrape off the crust from the breads with a grater
2. Cut into 4 pieces, soak in water and press in a clean linen cloth
3. Melt the butter in a saucepan and bake the bread while stirring constantly until it releases
4. Pour the bread onto a dish and while still hot, stir in 5 eggs one at a time
5. Add salt, sugar and a little grated nutmeg
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brødboller (Bread Dumplings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 French Bread
- 100 Gr (20 Kvint) Butter
- 6 Eggs
- Salt
- Sugar
- Cardamom

## Directions

1. Remove the crust from a French bread, and cut the bread into thick slices, which are dipped in milk and left covered for a couple of hours, after which it is worked with a wooden spoon
2. Melt 100 Gr (20 Kvint) butter, and bake the bread in it until it is well cohesive
3. When the dough is slightly cooled, stir it with 6 eggs, salt, sugar and cardamom
4. Boil dumplings in salted water

title: Brødbudding (Bread Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound French bread
- sweet milk or cold water
- a quarter pound butter
- a quarter pound cleaned raisins
- a quarter pound ground sugar
- salt
- cardamom or lemon zest
- 10 egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Grate half a pound French bread, cut into pieces and dip very lightly in sweet milk or cold water, then let it stand covered for an hour. Then mash it with a spoon and bake with a quarter pound butter until it releases from the spoon, then place it in a deep dish to cool somewhat. When this is done, but while it is still warm, stir in a quarter pound cleaned raisins with the kernels removed, a quarter pound ground sugar, a little salt, cardamom or lemon zest, and 10 egg yolks. Lastly stir in the egg whites, which have been beaten to a foam, also carefully. The stirring takes about half an hour. Then put the mixture in a mold that is buttered and sprinkled with grated bread. Set the mold in boiling water, and the pudding must boil for 2 hours. Serve with sweet fruit sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Brødkage (Bread Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of French bread
- milk
- butter
- egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 10 tablespoons milk
- a little salt
- sugar
- egg whites
- powdered sugar
- jam

## Directions

1. Dip slices of French bread in milk and fry them in butter on a pan.
2. Beat the egg yolks and stir in 1 tablespoon of flour, 10 tablespoons of milk, a little salt and sugar, and finally the foam from the egg whites.
3. Melt a little butter on a pan before pouring the egg mixture onto it.
4. When it begins to set a little, distribute the bread slices. Turn the bread cake and bake on both sides.
5. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and serve with jam.

title: Brødvand for Syge (Bread Water for the Sick)
body:
## Ingredients

- rye bread
- boiling water
- lemon juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Slice rye bread and toast it on both sides
2. Break it into pieces
3. Pour boiling water over it
4. Prepare the bread water with lemon juice and sugar
5. When cold, strain it
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Bubble (English Dish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked Savoy cabbage, finely chopped
- Pepper
- Salt
- Butter, browned
- Cooked meat, sliced and fried brown in butter

## Directions

1. Place the finely chopped cooked Savoy cabbage, sprinkled with pepper and salt, on a pan with browned butter.
2. Turn and warm through the cabbage in the butter.
3. Place it in the center of a flat dish.
4. Arrange slices of cooked meat that have been fried brown in butter around it.
5. Either pour a strong brown gravy sauce over the dish or serve the sauce separately.

title: Butterdejg (Puff Pastry)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) cake flour
- 1/2 kg (1 pound) cold margarine
- 1/2 glass vinegar
- 1/4 liter (1 pint) cold water

## Directions

1. Place flour on a pastry board and blend half of the cold margarine with the flour until very finely crumbled, using a large knife to chop the flour and butter together to keep hands cool
2. Make a well in the center of the flour and pour in the vinegar and cold water
3. Work together with the knife until a tough dough forms
4. Gather the dough together with quick, light hands and roll out with a pastry pin into a large sheet
5. Distribute the remaining margarine in small lumps over the dough
6. Fold the dough first into 3 parts and then again into 3 parts in the opposite direction, as if folding a napkin
7. Wrap in a cloth and set in a cold place for 1 hour
8. Roll out and fold the puff pastry 4 to 5 times with a few minutes between each folding, ensuring this is done in a cool kitchen and using only minimal flour for the rolling pin and board
9. The dough is then ready for use in various baked goods
10. A larger portion of puff pastry can be made at once but must be stored in a cold place and well covered to prevent it from developing a dry crust
11. The pastry is typically baked the day it is to be served, but it can also be baked the day before and reheated in the oven before serving

title: Butterdejg til Postejer (Puff Pastry for Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. butter
- 1 lb. flour
- 1 pint good cognac (or water)
- cold water

## Directions

1. Wash the butter well in several changes of cold water until it becomes smooth and free of salt, then lay it in cold water.
2. Place 1 lb. flour on a pastry board and make a depression in the middle, into which put 1 pint good cognac (or water) together with a piece of the butter.
3. Work this together with a little cold water until it becomes a suitably thick dough; but it must be worked well together until eyes appear in it.
4. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a straw and take the rest of the butter out of the water and pat it in a dry cloth, place it in the middle of the dough.
5. Fold the dough into three and beat well with a rolling pin.
6. Thus roll out the dough 4 to 5 times after each time folding it 4 to 5 sections and set aside to cool for a couple of hours.
7. When the dough is to be baked, roll it out to the thickness of a two-crown coin.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Butterdejgs-Kranse (Butter Dough Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough (see recipe 22)
- Egg for brushing
- Chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Roll out a thin layer of butter dough
2. Cut out round cakes with a beer glass
3. Remove the center of each with a small liqueur glass to form rings
4. Brush the rings with egg, sprinkle with chopped almonds, and bake in a very hot oven
5. These cakes cannot be stored for long like other small cakes, only a few days, and must be warmed in the oven before serving
6. They can be placed quite close together on the baking sheet

title: Butterdejgs-Kringler (Butter Dough Twists)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough (see recipe 22)
- Egg for brushing
- Sugar and chopped almonds, optional

## Directions

1. Roll out a thin layer of butter dough
2. Cut out small twists using a metal twist cutter
3. Brush with egg and can be sprinkled with sugar and chopped almonds
4. Place close together on the baking sheet and bake with good heat

title: Butterdejgs-Æblekager (Butter Dough Apple Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 grams flour
- 375 grams margarine
- 175 milliliters water
- 1 shot glass vinegar
- 1.25 kilograms sour apples
- cinnamon
- sugar
- egg
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make butter dough from flour, margarine, water, and vinegar
2. Roll out a very thin layer that covers the baking sheet completely
3. Peel the apples, divide into 4 pieces, and remove the cores
4. Rinse them and place in a saucepan with a little water and cook until partly tender, but not until they become mush
5. Sweeten and cool before spreading over the dough and sprinkling with cinnamon
6. Roll out the remaining dough into a similar sheet and cover the apples with it
7. Press the cake together along the edges, brush with egg, and sprinkle with sugar
8. Bake in a well-heated oven for approximately half an hour
9. When the sheet is taken from the oven, cut the cake into small, square cakes
10. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and serve lukewarm
11. Can be decorated with whipped cream
12. These cakes can be made with different fruits, first cooked with sugar, such as gooseberries, rhubarb, plums, and dried apricots

title: Butterdejgskringler (Butter Dough Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds washed butter
- ½ pound fine flour

## Directions

1. Divide the 3 pounds of washed butter into 3 parts
2. Rub one part with ½ pound fine flour
3. Place the remaining butter in this mixture
4. Treat as with other butter dough (see No. 186)
5. Form into rings using a ring iron
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Byggryns-Vandgrød (Barley Water Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) medium-fine barley grains
- 6 L (6 Potter) water
- butter and sugar for serving

## Directions

1. When the water comes to a boil, sprinkle the grains into it under quick stirring to prevent lumps from forming.
2. Water porridge tolerates about 3 hours of cooking.
3. Serve with butter and sugar.
4. Serve with sweet milk; in summer thick milk is used a lot and is served separately in small dessert plates.
5. A few peeled and scalded berries can be cooked in the porridge a few minutes before serving.

title: Byggrynsgrød (Barley Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) fine barley grains
- 5 L (5 Potter) skimmed milk

## Directions

1. When the milk boils, sprinkle the grains into it while stirring.
2. After the porridge has cooked for about 2 hours, salt it and serve with butter and sugar.
3. Serve sweetened pale ale with it.

title: Byggrynsgrød og Vælling (Barley Grits Porridge and Gruel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 to 5 pots skimmed milk
- 1 pot medium-fine barley grits
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Heat 4 to 5 pots of skimmed milk in a pot that is greased inside with lard
2. Rinse 1 pot of medium-fine barley grits in cold water
3. When the milk is boiling vigorously, sprinkle the grits into the milk
4. Cover the pot and let the porridge cook for a couple of hours over even heat until the grits are tender, stirring occasionally
5. Season with salt to taste
6. For gruel, use the same method but use only 4.5 pots of grits to 4 pots of milk
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Byggrynssuppe (Barley Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 measures medium-fine barley grits
- 4 to 5 pots water
- peeled apple pieces
- dried fruit
- prunes
- raisins
- sour fruit juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the barley grits in cold water by stirring with your hands, then pour away the dirty water.
2. Set 4 to 5 pots of water over the fire (less water is needed if the grits are finer) and bring to a boil.
3. Add the grits and cook covered until tender.
4. Add peeled apple pieces, dried fruit, prunes and raisins that have first been rinsed in cold water.
5. When ready to serve, season the soup with sour juice and sugar to taste.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Byggyn-Suppe (Barley Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ L fine barley grains
- 4½ L water
- Lemon peel
- Cinnamon
- Prunes
- Peeled apples
- Fruit juice

## Directions

1. When the water boils, add lemon peel, whole cinnamon and the grains while stirring until it boils again; then cover to prevent skin from forming on the soup.
2. It must cook very gently for at least 2 hours.
3. Prepare the soup with juice.
4. Cook prunes and peeled, cut apples in the soup.
5. It can be given more color with fruit coloring if the juice used to prepare it does not color it.

title: Bøf i Sky (Beef in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked beef
- Good aspic
- Isinglass
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Egg yolk and egg white

## Directions

1. Refer to recipe 132 for beef preparation.
2. Deglaze the pan in which the beef was cooked with good aspic.
3. Cook the aspic while stirring constantly until it begins to thicken.
4. Pour the aspic onto the bottom of the dish.
5. Arrange the beef on top.
6. When cold, cover with good aspic mixed with isinglass.
7. When serving, once the aspic has set, garnish with chopped egg yolk and egg white arranged in strips.

title: Bøf med Brun Kapers-Sauce (Beef with Brown Caper Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Strong gravy sauce
- Capers
- Wine
- Butter dough strips

## Directions

1. In a well-made, strong gravy sauce, add capers and wine.
2. Pour over the beef at serving.
3. Serve with butter dough strips.

title: Bøf med Champignons (Beef with Mushrooms)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh or preserved mushrooms
- Strong, slightly thickened brown gravy sauce
- Wine to taste
- Fried beef
- Butter dough for garnish as border or as strips

## Directions

1. Give fresh or preserved mushrooms a simmer in a strong, slightly thickened brown gravy sauce, to which wine is added to taste.
2. Arrange the sauce over the fried beef, which is decorated with butter dough in the form of a border or as strips.

title: Bøf med Oliven og Kastanier (Beef with Olives and Chestnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Good, strong gravy sauce
- Peeled olives
- Wine to taste
- Roasted chestnuts
- Butter dough strips

## Directions

1. Pour over the beef a good, strong gravy sauce into which peeled olives and wine to taste have been added.
2. Garnish the beef with roasted chestnuts and butter dough strips.

title: Bøf med Sauce Piquante (Beef with Piquant Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.25 liters strong gravy
- 4 tablespoons preserved small onions or shallots
- Paprika
- Wine
- Salt
- Sugar
- Sago flour for thickening
- Water
- Butter dough border or strips

## Directions

1. For a sauce for 8 to 10 people, use 0.25 liters of very strong gravy, which must simmer for 5 to 10 minutes with 4 tablespoons preserved small onions or shallots, paprika, wine, salt and sugar.
2. Give the sauce a nice brown color and thicken with sago flour mixed in water.
3. On the dish place either a butter dough border or strips.
4. Pour the sauce over the beef.

title: Bøf med Sauce à la Diable (Beef with Deviled Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- 2 large shallots, finely chopped
- Butter
- Flour
- Good, strong gravy or broth
- 3 tablespoons finely cut piccalilli pickles in mustard
- 1 to 2 tablespoons of the mustard
- Paprika
- Wine
- Salt
- A little sugar
- Food coloring if needed
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Brown 2 large, finely chopped shallots in butter, and in this make a roux with flour, which is thinned with good, strong gravy or broth.
2. Add to the sauce 3 tablespoons finely cut piccalilli pickles in mustard, 1 to 2 tablespoons of the mustard, paprika, wine, salt, a little sugar and food coloring if needed.
3. Let the sauce simmer with the seasonings for a few minutes.
4. Arrange over the fried meat.
5. Serve with boiled potatoes.

title: Bøf med Skildpadde-Sauce (Beef with Turtle Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Turtle sauce
- Stock from roasted bones for cooking vegetables
- Hard-boiled eggs, sliced
- Small fish balls

## Directions

1. A good turtle sauce can be made as recipe No. 9.
2. For this, stock from roasted bones can be used for cooking the vegetables.
3. Arrange over the beef, which is garnished with sliced hard-boiled eggs and small fish balls.

title: Bøf med Tomat-Sauce og Ris-Rand (Beef with Tomato Sauce and Rice Border)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth
- Wine
- Paprika
- Tomato purée to taste
- Rice border
- Boiled rice or macaroni

## Directions

1. For tomato sauce, make a roux with butter and flour, which is thinned with broth.
2. Add wine, paprika and tomato purée to taste.
3. Arrange the beef on a dish on which the rice border has been baked, and pour the sauce over.
4. Alternatively, loose boiled rice or macaroni can be used, laid around the edge of the dish.

title: Bøf à la mode (Braised Beef à la Mode)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg beef sirloin
- Pickled shallots
- Bacon strips
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Palmin or claret
- Water
- Coloring
- Butter
- Flour
- Cooked potatoes or mashed potatoes
- Pickles or preserved vegetables

## Directions

1. The beef, which must not be freshly slaughtered, is beaten on all sides and larded with the help of a pointed knife very densely with pickled shallots and fairly thick bacon strips dipped in a mixture of salt, ground pepper, allspice and cloves
2. The meat is rubbed with the spices
3. Brown it in palmin or claret in a pot and then pour water with coloring over it
4. Roast it covered for 3 hours and turn during roasting
5. Make the sauce as usual from butter, flour and gravy
6. When serving, pour just a little sauce over the roast and serve the rest in a sauce bowl
7. Cooked potatoes or mashed potatoes and either preserved vegetables or pickles can be served with it

title: Bønner (Green Beans)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small green beans
- Water
- Salt
- Soda (in small amount)
- Salt solution (10 gr or 2 Kvint per 1 liter)

## Directions

1. Pat dry or rinse the beans; remove the ribs along the back and belly seams
2. Place in boiling water with salt and a slight amount of soda and cook for 5 minutes
3. Remove and pour cold water over them, let cool and place in jars
4. Pour over them a cold solution of 10 gr (2 Kvint) salt to 1 liter water, close the jars and place in a pot with cold water
5. Cook for 30 to 35 minutes and cool

title: Cakes on String (Kager paa Traad)
body:
## Ingredients

- raisins, cooked slightly
- blanched almonds, folded in
- small pieces of apple
- batter for Apple Slices

## Directions

1. Thread raisins, blanched almonds, and small pieces of apple alternately on a good string
2. Thread until about 1 yard is full and tight, leaving a piece of string at each end to hold
3. Dip in batter made according to the method for liquid Apple Slices
4. Place in boiling clarified fat and cook until light brown
5. Draw out the string before the cake is cold

title: Canton af Faarekjod (Stuffed Mutton Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds mutton shoulder
- Onions
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Carrots
- Parsnips
- Butter for the sauce
- Flour
- Cream
- Sugar

## Directions

1. The shoulder should not be cracked at the butcher; wash it and lay it on the table with the inner side up.
2. Remove the shoulder blade and cook it to be completely clean.
3. With a sharp, thin-bladed knife, cut out the lower joint of the bone and half of the second, breaking off halfway between the first and second joint and being careful not to tear the meat or cut through the skin.
4. Shape the end of the bone so it resembles a duck's beak.
5. Stuff the shoulder with a forcemeat or filling made as follows: peel 6 onions, cut them into pieces and cook them in three different changes of water.
6. Soak 8 ounces of breadcrumbs in lukewarm water and wring out in a clean cloth.
7. When the onions are tender, about half an hour, chop them and mix together with the breadcrumbs, a piece of butter, 10 egg yolks and a little salt and pepper.
8. After filling the skin with this, sew it and tie it together so it resembles a duck.
9. Place it in the roasting pan on some pieces of vegetables and roast in a hot oven about 20 minutes per pound.
10. Meanwhile, prepare carrots and parsnips by cutting them into small balls, cook them tender in well-salted boiling water and place them in cold water to preserve their color.
11. When the canton is done, remove it to a warm platter and keep warm without covering while the sauce is made.
12. Make the sauce from browned butter with a little flour, strain the gravy from the roasting pan with the vegetables into the sauce, and finally stir in a spoonful of raw cream and a little sugar.
13. Warm the root vegetables in the sauce and arrange them around the meat.
14. The shoulder blade, which should be scraped after being cooked and have the broad end chopped so it resembles a feather, is placed behind the canton, which is then served.

title: Capers in Raw Vinegar (Kapers i raa Eddike)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- seeds or berries
- 125 grams salt
- 1 l boiling water

## Directions

1. Place the seeds or berries used in a brine of 125 grams salt to 1 liter boiling water
2. The next day, drain them in a sieve

title: Cardinal
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 bottles Graves wine
- 1½ pounds top sugar
- peel of 2 lemons
- peel of 2 oranges
- 3 measures boiling water
- ½ bottle Sherry
- a good dram old rum

## Directions

1. Set 3 bottles Graves wine over heat with 1½ pounds top sugar, peel of 2 lemons and peel of 2 oranges
2. When it begins to boil, pour 3 measures boiling water into it
3. Remove from heat and add ½ bottle Sherry and a good dram old rum
4. Drink
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Cardonner (Cardoons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoon stalks
- Boiling water with salt
- Salt solution (10 gr or 2 Kvint per 1 liter)

## Directions

1. Cut only the fleshy stalks into finger-length pieces
2. Place in boiling salted water and cook until the outer skin can easily be removed by rubbing with salt
3. Pour the cold salt solution over the cardoons
4. Cook for 30 to 35 minutes in carefully closed jars and cool

title: Caroline med Ananas (Caroline with Pineapple)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh pineapples
- sugar
- 2 pounds fine sugar
- 1 bottle Madeira
- 1 bottle Sauternes
- 1 bottle Graves
- 1 bottle ice-cooled Champagne

## Directions

1. Cut fresh pineapples into thin slices, place in sugar and let stand 24 hours, or use pineapples cooked with sugar
2. Add 2 pounds fine sugar and cover with 1 bottle Madeira, 1 bottle Sauternes and 1 bottle Graves
3. Let the mixture stand well covered for a couple of hours on ice
4. Then strain through a fine sieve or filter
5. Immediately before serving, add 1 bottle ice-cooled Champagne
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Carp in Red Wine (Karper i Rodvin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp, cleaned, scaled and cut into pieces
- Bay leaves
- Whole pepper
- Fine cloves
- Salt
- Water
- Red wine
- Flour
- Grated rye bread

## Directions

1. Place cleaned, scaled and cut carp pieces in a casserole with bay leaves, whole pepper, fine cloves and salt.
2. Place the heads on the bottom.
3. Add a little water and enough red wine so it just covers the fish.
4. Once it has boiled a little, even it out with a little flour and some grated rye bread, which is mixed with a little of the hot soup and pressed through a sieve.

title: Catfish-Suppe (Catfish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 fish, weighing about half a pound each
- 1/4 pound salt pork
- 2 quarts water
- chopped parsley
- 1 bunch celery or celery salt
- 1 pint milk
- 2 beaten eggs
- a small piece of butter
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and cut into pieces
2. Cut 1/4 pound salt pork into small pieces
3. Put fish and pork together in the pot with 2 quarts water, chopped parsley, and 1 bunch of celery or celery salt
4. Boil until the fish and meat are in pieces
5. Strain the soup and pour back into the pot with 1 pint of warmed milk
6. Add 2 beaten eggs and a small piece of butter last
7. When it has come to a boil, it is ready and is served with toasted bread

title: Champignon-Salat (Mushroom Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small, closed mushrooms
- Butter
- Fish forcemeat formed into cubes or small balls
- Cooked, finely cut celery
- Mayonnaise
- Salad leaves

## Directions

1. Cook small closed mushrooms in butter; form cubes or small balls of fish forcemeat and cook them.
2. Mix equal amounts of mushrooms, forcemeat balls, and finely cut cooked celery together.
3. Turn in a mayonnaise.
4. Arrange the salad and garnish with salad leaves and a few mushrooms.

title: Champignon-Sauce (Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- small fresh or cooked mushrooms
- butter
- flour
- lemon juice
- bouillon
- egg yolks
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Cook small cleaned fresh or cooked mushrooms together with butter, a little flour, and lemon juice.
2. Thin with bouillon.
3. After bringing to a boil, thicken the sauce with a few egg yolks and sweet cream.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Champignons (Mushrooms)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small closed mushrooms
- Wool cloth
- Lukewarm salt water
- Clean salt water
- Boiling water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- 10 gr (2 Kvint) salt to 1 l (1 Pot) water

## Directions

1. Cut off the bottom of the stems of small closed mushrooms
2. Wash them with a wool cloth and lukewarm salt water
3. As they are cleaned, place them in clean salt water
4. Do not remove the gills from small partially closed mushrooms, but remove them from large, open ones, and also remove the skin from these
5. Take the mushrooms out of the salt water and pour boiling water mixed with a little vinegar and salt over them
6. Let them stand for 1.5 hours, then drain on a sieve
7. This treatment should be done before canning in all cases
8. Give the mushrooms a 3 minute boiling in water with salt, 10 gr (2 Kvint) to 1 l (1 Pot)
9. When the mushrooms are filled in the jar, pour the water in which they were boiled over them, but let it be well cooled beforehand
10. Attach the lid, rubber ring and clamp and boil the jars for 30 to 35 minutes
11. Cool before storing

title: Champignons (Preserved Mushrooms)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small, closed mushrooms
- Salt (100 grams per 1/2 kilogram of mushrooms)

## Directions

1. Extract the small, closed mushrooms first in boiled water as clarified in Nr. 217, then drain them on a sieve.
2. After draining well, place them in jars; for a larger portion one can use a well-cleaned butter tub.
3. Arrange the mushrooms on blocks with caps facing up and sprinkle salt between the layers.
4. Spread a piece of linen over them, then cover with a perforated wooden bottom of appropriate size to fit the tub and place a stone or other press on top.
5. Leave in this manner in a cool place.
6. Soak the mushrooms a few hours before use, changing the water several times during this period.
7. Salted mushrooms can be preserved throughout the winter if kept covered with a press on top.
8. Some of the brine that forms can be used in Mushroom-Soya (see the Cookbook).

title: Champignons i Smør (Mushrooms in Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- Vinegar
- Unsalted butter of best quality

## Directions

1. Clean and extract the mushrooms in water with vinegar (see previous recipe)
2. Bring unsalted butter of best quality to a boil, and cook the well-drained mushrooms in it for 10 minutes
3. Take out the mushrooms, place them in jars, and cook the butter for 5 minutes over gentle heat; thereby some of the moisture which the mushrooms contain will evaporate, and they will be better preserved
4. After being packed in jars, the butter must completely cover them
5. Bind the jars very carefully with parchment paper; this can be painted over with egg white
6. The butter in which they are cooked is used in the dishes in which the mushrooms are used

title: Champignons, Preserved and Pickled (Preserved and Pickled Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms (small, fresh)
- Butter
- Lemon juice (half or one lemon)
- Glass jars
- Bladder for sealing
- Salt water (light brine)
- Vinegar (best sort)
- Nutmeg flower

## Directions

1. Choose mushrooms carefully, discarding any that are dark or white on the underside of the cap, have thin stems, or have an onion-like formation at the base of the stem. Use small, underdeveloped genuine mushrooms. To preserve: Peel mushrooms carefully, clean them, and cut larger ones into pieces after removing the reddish membrane from the underside of the cap. Discard any with worms. Place peeled pieces immediately in cold water, rinse, and place on a colander then on a dry linen cloth to remove water. Melt butter in a pot, add the dry mushrooms, and immediately squeeze the juice of half or one lemon (as much as needed) over them to keep them white. Cook over gentle heat until the butter looks like oil and a sample from the bottom does not look grainy. Remove from heat immediately, and when cooled, fill glass jars with wide openings and seal with a moistened bladder. To pickle: As mushrooms are cleaned and prepared, place them immediately in cold water. Remove and place in a colander to drain. Whisk together a light brine, place mushrooms in it, and let stand for 2 hours. Remove and place on a colander again. When water has drained, boil in a pot without a lid for 10 minutes with best sort vinegar and a little nutmeg flower. When slightly cooled, fill glass jars, seal with butter, and bind with bladder. Handle mushrooms immediately upon receipt as they darken when left standing.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Champignons-Gratin (Mushroom Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Friske Champignons eller henkogte Champignons
- Smør
- Vand
- Citronsat
- Mel
- Suppe af de kogte Champignons
- Et Par Æggeblommer
- Sherry
- Brød

## Directions

1. Såfremt man bruger friske Champignons, må disse efter at være rengjorte koges nogle få Minutter i Smør og ganske lidt Vand med Tilsætning af nogle Draaber Citronsat.
2. Smørret skummes af, og deri bages Melet og opspædes med Suppen af de kogte Champignons til en lemmeligt jævn Sauce, som lieres med et Par Æggeblommer og tilsættes lidt Sherry efter Smag.
3. Stuvningen hældes i Gratinskaller og bestros med Brød.
4. Bages i 10 Minutter i en hed Ovn.

title: Champignons-Sauce (Mushroom Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Even, brown gravy sauce
- Mushrooms (fresh, pickled in butter, or pickled in vinegar, well rinsed)
- Sherry
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Salt
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Add mushrooms to an even, brown gravy sauce
2. The mushrooms can be either fresh, pickled in butter, or if pickled in vinegar, well rinsed so the sauce does not take on a strong, sour taste
3. Prepare with Sherry, Maggi bouillon cubes, salt and a little sugar
4. Bring to a boil with the mushrooms

title: Champignons-Sauce (Mushroom Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Plain brown sauce
- Mushrooms, fresh or pickled

## Directions

1. A plain brown sauce is given mushrooms, which can be either fresh, pickled in butter, or, if pickled in vinegar, well rinsed.

title: Champignons-Sauce (Mushroom Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Meat or fish stock
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sherry or Graves wine
- Lemon juice
- Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Bake butter and flour as in the preceding sauce and thin with meat or fish stock, depending on what the sauce is to be served with
2. Bind with egg yolks and season with salt, pepper, sherry or Graves wine, and lemon juice if using mushrooms that are not already sour
3. Give the mushrooms a boil in the sauce
4. Mushrooms kept in butter should be heated so the butter can melt; use it to bake the flour in

title: Champignons-Soya (Mushroom Soy Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- mushroom trimmings from pickled mushrooms
- salt
- 10 chopped anchovies
- 1 teaspoon ground white pepper
- 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves
- 2 bay leaves
- 1/2 kg (1 s) strongly browned powdered sugar
- 1/2 bottle red wine
- 1 1/2 L (1 1/2 pot) mushroom liquid

## Directions

1. If you have pickled mushrooms, all the trimmings can be used for soy sauce. Rinse them well and chop finely, then sprinkle with salt and let stand under a press for 3 to 4 days.
2. Wring out the liquid from a cloth.
3. To 1 1/2 L (1 1/2 pot) liquid take 10 chopped anchovies, 1 teaspoon ground white pepper, 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves, 2 bay leaves and 1/2 kg (1 s) strongly browned powdered sugar.
4. Cook all this and skim, before pouring 1/2 bottle red wine into it.
5. When it has boiled for at least 1 quarter of an hour after the wine is added, strain it and pour into dry bottles, which are stoppered when it is cold.
6. This soy sauce is very tasty in various dishes.

title: Champignons-Suppe (Mushroom Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-5 L (4.5-5.5 pots) soup
- 0.5 kg (1 lb) fresh mushrooms
- Water
- Salt
- 125 g (1/4 lb) butter
- Lemon juice
- 125 g (1/4 lb) flour
- 6 egg yolks
- Rhenish wine
- Bread croutons

## Directions

1. Select only small closed fresh mushrooms
2. Rinse with a woolen cloth in salted water and pour boiling water over them, allowing them to stand for a quarter hour
3. Discard the water and place the mushrooms in 0.5 L (0.5 pot) boiling water with 125 g (1/4 lb) butter and a little lemon juice
4. Give them one boil
5. Skim the butter and brown 125 g (1/4 lb) flour in it, which is diluted with the soup and the water in which the mushrooms have been cooked
6. Thicken the soup with 6 egg yolks, add a little Rhenish wine and serve with bread croutons arranged on a napkin on a platter

title: Champions-Kager (Champagne Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons finely powdered sugar
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 2 egg whites
- 1 whole egg
- cream

## Directions

1. Mix the powdered sugar and flour with the egg whites and whole egg
2. Thin with a little cream, taking care the batter does not become too thin
3. Fry in a mold iron in fat
4. Before beginning to fry, dip the iron in the boiling fat and repeat this for each cake
5. When arranging, place a little currant jelly in the bottom of each cake
6. Top with whipped cream
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Chartreuse med Bajerske Pølser (Chartreuse with Bavarian Sausages)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heads pointed cabbage
- 1/4 kg sausages
- yellow and white carrots, half-cooked
- salt
- pepper
- 125 gr melted butter
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. A Chartreuse can either be cooked in a pudding mold without a tap or placed in the oven in a mold with a solid bottom. If the latter, a piece of paper buttered with butter and with some small holes cut in it can be placed over it to allow steam to escape. The mold used must be well buttered with cold butter.
2. Yellow and white carrots, which are half-cooked, are cut into thin slices and cut into equal sizes. They are pressed firmly against the bottom and sides of the mold to form a pattern.
3. The cabbage, which can be savoy cabbage, pointed cabbage, or white cabbage, is cut into 4 parts and cooked nearly tender in water. The stalks are cut off, and the water is pressed well out of the cabbage, which is chopped very coarsely with salt and pepper.
4. The previously decorated mold is lined with a 3 centimeter (1 inch) thick layer of cabbage, and the sausages are placed in the middle. Over this cabbage is laid again, and finally the Chartreuse is poured over with 125 gr (1/4 pound) melted butter.
5. It is cooked or baked for about an hour, carefully turned out onto a serving dish and served with good, brown sauce.

title: Chartreuse med Bajerske Pølser (Chartreuse with Bavarian Sausages)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heads of pointed cabbage
- ¼ kg (½ lb) sausages
- Butter
- Yellow and white carrots, parboiled

## Directions

1. A chartreuse can be cooked in a pudding mold without a center post or baked in an oven in a mold with a fixed bottom
2. In the latter case, place a piece of buttered paper over it with some small holes cut in it to allow steam to escape
3. Butter the mold well with cold butter
4. Cut parboiled yellow and white carrots into thin slices

title: Chartreuse med Brunkaal (Chartreuse with Braised Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- white cabbage
- browned butter
- sugar
- striped, lightly salted bacon, or medister sausage, or poached duck
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. White cabbage is cut and browned in a pot in browned butter and a very little sugar.
2. When the cabbage is nicely brown, a layer of it is laid in the buttered mold.
3. In the middle, either striped, lightly salted bacon, medister sausage, or poached duck can be placed. What is used for this must be cooked and cut into suitable pieces.
4. It is baked or cooked for 1 hour. Brown sauce is eaten with it; this can be made from Maggi bouillon cubes.

title: Chartreuse med forskellige Slags Ragout'er (Chartreuse with Various Ragouts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- pointed cabbage
- ragout of game animals and game birds, or geese, hens or pigeons
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. The mold is decorated as in recipe 54. The cabbage is prepared in the same manner as directed in that recipe.
2. In the middle, ragout of game animals and game birds, of geese, hens or pigeons can be filled, and on top a layer of cabbage.
3. There must not be too much sauce with the meat. The rest is served to the Chartreuse, which is turned out onto a serving dish with great care.

title: Chaudeau (Wine Foam Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound white sugar
- 1 lemon
- cold water
- 2 glasses white wine
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Rub the zest of 1 lemon onto 1/4 pound white sugar; dip the sugar quickly in cold water and place in a saucepan with 2 glasses white wine, 3 whole eggs, 3 egg yolks and a little salt.
2. Beat all this well together and then set over heat, where it is constantly whisked until it is about to boil.
3. Remove the sauce and serve immediately.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Cherries with Cognac
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg cherries
- 250 gr powdered sugar
- 1/4 dl cognac

## Directions

1. Yellow Spanish morello cherries or ordinary table cherries can be used, but they must be completely dry, whole and without blemishes.
2. Cut off some of the stems before placing them in dry jars, which are tapped lightly on the table so that the berries can settle.
3. Stir the sugar into the cognac, pour it over the cherries, and bind the jars immediately with parchment paper.

title: Cherries with Vinegar
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg cherries
- 315 gr powdered sugar
- 1/4 dl vinegar

## Directions

1. The sugar can be chosen between powdered sugar, icing sugar or Demerara sugar, the latter gives the best juice.
2. Place the sugar in the pot in layers with the pitted berries, and pour the vinegar over.
3. The pot should initially be placed over low heat, while stirring occasionally.
4. As soon as the cherries boil, skim them and remove them on a colander, but not before they begin to wrinkle; usually one counts 10 minutes of boiling.
5. Pour the cherries, from which the juice must have drained well, into a jar, and reduce the juice until it is fairly thick.
6. Pour it boiling over the cherries, which can then be sealed immediately with parchment paper.
7. If desired, some pieces of whole cinnamon and some cloves can be boiled with the juice.

title: Cherry Marmalade (Marmelade af Kirsebær)
body:
## Ingredients

- Best quality cherries, preferably black and well-ripened
- White sugar at 1 pound per pound of berries, or 1/2 pound per pound of berries if longer cooking is desired
- Juice from the berries
- Vinegar (if for serving with meat)
- Cinnamon
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and pits using a quill, rinse the pits in several changes of warm water
2. Dry them in a cloth and spread in the air, in an oven, or in a pan over coals until dry
3. Gently crush so the kernels do not break, then remove the kernels
4. Weigh the berries
5. Put sugar on the fire with a little of the juice from the berries, or if for serving with meat, with a little vinegar
6. When the sugar is melted, add the berries and cook for about a quarter hour or until no longer sour, but not so long they become mushy
7. Pour onto a dish
8. Put the juice on the fire with cinnamon and cook another quarter hour or until thick
9. Add the berries with the kernels and a few cloves to be blended with the juice, but stop before they boil
10. Seal the next day

title: Chess Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 coconut, peeled and grated
- 1 pound sugar
- 1/2 pint water
- 1 pound sugar
- egg yolks from 7 well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound sugar and 1/2 pint water for fifteen minutes.
2. Stir in the grated coconut and cook for fifteen minutes more.
3. While hot, stir in 1 pound sugar and the yolks from 7 well-beaten eggs.
4. Bake in small, half-oval cake pans.

title: Chocolade-Kage (Chocolate Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 4 cups flour
- 4 eggs
- 3 teaspoons royal baking powder
- 1 cup sweet milk

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup butter, 2 cups sugar, 4 cups flour, 4 eggs, 3 teaspoons royal baking powder, and 1 cup sweet milk
2. Bake in layers
3. For the filling: heat half a cake of baker's chocolate with half a cup powdered sugar and enough hot water to cover, boil for a couple of minutes, cool, and add half a teaspoon vanilla
4. Spread between each layer and over the top in the usual way

title: Chocolate Cake (Chocolade-Kage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup Butter
- 2 Cups Sugar
- 5 Eggs (less than the whites of 2)
- 1 Cup Sweet Milk
- 1 Teaspoon Cream of Tartar dissolved in the milk
- ½ Teaspoon Soda dissolved in the milk
- 3½ Cups Flour, scant measure
- Whites of two eggs
- 1½ Cups powdered Sugar
- 6 large Tablespoons grated Chocolate
- 2 Teaspoons Vanilla

## Directions

1. Cream together the butter and sugar
2. Add the eggs
3. Add the sweet milk
4. Mix in the cream of tartar and soda dissolved in the milk
5. Stir in the flour
6. Bake the cake
7. When baked, cover it while hot with a layer made from the whites of two eggs, 1½ cups powdered sugar, 6 large tablespoons grated chocolate and 2 teaspoons vanilla
8. Place it in a moderately warm oven for a few minutes

title: Chokolade-Budding (Chocolate Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 L very strong coffee
- 1/4 kg vanilla chocolate
- 16 sheets isinglass
- 1/2 L cream, whipped to stiff foam
- 1/4 L semi-skimmed or sweet milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 150 g powdered sugar
- 2 teaspoons wheat flour or powdered starch
- vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Dissolve the vanilla chocolate and isinglass in the very strong coffee
2. When it has cooled and begins to set, stir in the whipped cream
3. Pour into a moistened and dusted form
4. For the cream sauce: whisk the egg yolks into the milk together with the powdered sugar and wheat flour or starch
5. Place the cream on the heat and stir regularly until it is almost cold
6. Add vanilla essence to the sauce
7. Serve the pudding with the cream sauce

title: Chokolade-Budding (Chocolate Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart cream
- 5 Ounces grated chocolate
- Powdered sugar to taste
- 6 Ounces isinglass or nearly 2 Ounces gelatine

## Directions

1. Put the cream on the heat with the grated chocolate, powdered sugar, and isinglass or gelatine
2. Let it boil for about 15 minutes
3. Pour it out and stir until it is cold, but still fluid enough to be poured into the oiled mold so that a layer forms all over it, about as thick as a finger
4. Continue to turn the mold until the mixture is so stiff that it no longer moves
5. Smooth out the last layer evenly with a knife so that a hollow space remains in the center
6. Let the mold stand in this way until the next day
