7. Then whip 1 Pint cream to stiff peaks with 2 Ounces white sugar, vanilla, and finally 1 Ounce dissolved gelatine
8. Fill the pudding with this mixture
9. After a couple of hours it can be turned onto a plate, but it must first be loosened on the sides with a knife; if it still does not slip out, place a hot cloth around the mold for a moment
10. A mold that holds 2.5 Quarts is adequate for this portion, which serves 16 persons

title: Chokolade-Budding fyldt med Ingefær-Budding (Chocolate Pudding Filled with Ginger Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 3/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) strong coffee
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) vanilla chocolate
- 8 leaves isinglass
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) cream, whipped to stiff foam
- salad oil

## Directions

1. Dissolve the vanilla chocolate and 8 leaves of isinglass in the strong coffee
2. Allow to cool and stir in 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) cream whipped to stiff foam
3. Coat the sides and bottom of the mold with this mixture in a one-inch thick layer
4. Rub the mold beforehand with a cloth dipped in a little salad oil
5. Turn the mold around until the chocolate has set in the mold
6. Fill the center either with Ginger Pudding (Nr. 1036) or with Rum Fromage (Nr. 1057), Pineapple Fromage (Nr. 1048), or Kan ikke la'  være (Nr. 1054)

title: Chokolade-Is (Chocolate Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- 36 quarters chocolate
- 2 pounds white sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 1 pot cream

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pot sweet cream with 36 quarters chocolate and 2 pounds white sugar and pour it while hot under beating over 12 beaten egg yolks.
2. Set the whole mixture over heat to smooth under continued beating.
3. Let it cool.
4. Beat 1 pot cream until stiff foam and stir it in when the rest is cooled.
5. Then freeze it.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Chokolade-Is (Chocolate Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart thin good cream
- 1 pint grated chocolate
- sugar according to the chocolate's sweetness
- 6 beaten egg yolks

## Directions

1. Place 1 quart of thin, good cream, 1 pint of grated chocolate, sugar according to the sweetness of the chocolate, and 6 beaten egg yolks on the fire and beat until it boils and then until it becomes cold.
2. Then treat it as other ice cream.

title: Chokolade-Kréme (Chocolate Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- 12 quarters sugar
- zest of half a lemon
- 25 quarters grated chocolate, not too sweet

## Directions

1. Place 1 pot of sweet cream, 12 quarters sugar, the zest of half a lemon, and 25 quarters of grated chocolate, not too sweet, over the heat and give it a boil under constant whisking, after which it is strained through a hair sieve and set to cool.
2. The cream is poured into a deep cake dish, which is set in boiling water and stands there for a few minutes until the cream has become firm, after which it is set to cool.
3. It is served with sweet bread.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Chokolade-Marengs (Chocolate Meringues)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 375 gr (1/4 lb) powdered flour sugar
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) grated chocolate
- a small amount of cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Fold in the powdered flour sugar, grated chocolate, and a small amount of cream of tartar
3. Place on a buttered baking sheet with a teaspoon
4. Bake in a warm oven

title: Chokolade-Sauce (Chocolate Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams (1/4 pound) chocolate
- 2 tablespoons cocoa
- 1 liter (1 pot) half-skimmed milk
- 2 to 3 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 2 teaspoons potato starch
- Cognac, wine, or liqueur to taste (optional)

## Directions

1. Mix chocolate and cocoa into 1 liter of half-skimmed milk and bring to a boil with constant stirring until smooth.
2. Whisk 2 to 3 egg yolks white with 2 tablespoons powdered sugar and 2 teaspoons potato starch.
3. Add some of the hot chocolate sauce to the egg mixture.
4. Under strong whisking with a whisk, pour the egg mixture into the pot with the remaining sauce, but do not allow it to boil.
5. Serve both warm and cold with puddings.
6. Add Cognac, wine, or liqueur if desired.
7. For this portion, 1/4 liter (1 pint) of cream may be whipped to a foam and placed on top in a warm sauce bowl, or stirred into the cold sauce.

title: Chokolade-Soufflé (Chocolate Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound good chocolate
- 1 cup boiling water
- 3 cups sweet cream
- 4 pounds sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites, whipped with powdered icing sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve the chocolate in boiling water.
2. Add 3 cups sweet cream and 4 pounds sugar to the chocolate.
3. Boil the mixture for one hour, stirring constantly.
4. Whip the egg yolks and pour the warm chocolate mixture into them.
5. Bring to a boil while constantly whisking until the mixture becomes thick and smooth.
6. Pour into a dish to cool.
7. When cold, fold in the whipped egg whites mixed with powdered icing sugar.
8. Place the mixture in a deep porcelain pan in a moderately warm oven.
9. When it has risen, remove and serve either without sauce or with vanilla sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Chokolade-Suppe (Chocolate Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Pound Chocolate
- 2 Quarts sweet Milk
- 3 Egg yolks
- Sugar
- a few drops good Vanilla Extract
- Egg whites (optional)

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate and cook it in 2 quarts sweet milk.
2. Stir evenly until it thickens.
3. After some time of cooking, pour it into a tureen in which 3 egg yolks have been whisked beforehand with sugar and a few drops good vanilla extract.
4. Whisk it in until the soup is poured in.
5. If desired, the egg whites can be whisked, cooked and laid on top in the tureen.
6. Serve with cooked bread or small rusks, and allow 1 pint of milk per person.

title: Chokolade-Suppe (Chocolate Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound chocolate
- 2 quarts sweet milk
- 3 egg yolks
- sugar
- a few drops good vanilla extract
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Grate 1/4 pound chocolate and boil in 2 quarts of sweet milk
2. Stir regularly until it boils
3. After some cooking time, pour it into a terrine in which 3 egg yolks have been previously whisked with sugar and a few drops of good vanilla extract
4. Whisk constantly while pouring in the soup
5. If desired, the egg whites can be whisked, cooked and placed on top

title: Chokoladeis (Chocolate Ice Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 Gr (40 Kvint) chocolate
- a little milk
- 1 L (1 Pot) cream or milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar
- cocoa (optional)

## Directions

1. Melt the chocolate over the fire in a little milk.
2. Stir 1 L (1 Pot) cream or milk into this for the cream.
3. Cook this as vanilla cream Nr. 1068, with 8 egg yolks and 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar.
4. Cocoa can be used in this recipe.

title: Chokoladekager (Chocolate Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 36 quintal finely ground and sifted powdered sugar
- 24 quintal finely ground and sifted chocolate

## Directions

1. Whip 4 egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Stir in 36 quintal finely ground and sifted powdered sugar and 24 quintal finely ground and sifted chocolate
3. Place small dollops on a wax-greased sheet
4. Bake or dry at slow heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Chokoladekager (Chocolate Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/2 pound white sugar
- 6 tablespoons sifted flour
- 1/4 pound grated chocolate
- 1 teaspoon cream of tartar dissolved in a little rose water

## Directions

1. Beat 4 eggs for a quarter hour with 1/2 pound white sugar.
2. Mix in 6 tablespoons sifted flour, 1/4 pound grated chocolate, and 1 teaspoon cream of tartar dissolved in a little rose water.
3. Butter the pan with a spatula and it must be cold again before the dough is placed on it in small lumps, not too close together as they spread out.
4. They are generally done in half an hour and are removed when they loosen from the pan.
5. Makes 50 cakes.

title: Chokoladekager med Æggehvider (Chocolate Cakes with Egg Whites)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 1/4 pound grated chocolate
- 1 1/2 pounds sifted powdered sugar
- 1 small teaspoon cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites to stiff peaks.
2. Stir in 1/4 pound grated chocolate, 1 1/2 pounds sifted powdered sugar, and 1 small teaspoon cream of tartar.
3. When all is just mixed, place the dough immediately on a buttered and cooled pan with a teaspoon.
4. The oven must be just warm enough so the cakes can stand in it overnight.
5. Makes 25 cakes.

title: Chokoladesuppe (Chocolate Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pots sweet milk
- A couple of pieces of lemon peel
- One-quarter pound chocolate
- 12 egg yolks, whisked
- 1 pot cream
- Powdered sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Boil 4 pots of sweet milk with a couple of pieces of lemon peel.
2. Grate one-quarter pound of chocolate and dissolve it in 12 whisked egg yolks and 1 pot of cream; add powdered sugar to taste.
3. When the milk boils, pour this mixture into it with continuous whisking and stirring until the whole comes to a boil again.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Chow Chow
body:
## Ingredients

- Two white Cabbage heads
- two Cauliflower heads
- a dozen Cucumbers
- four Celery knobs
- four pieces red Pepper
- a Quart small Onions
- two Quarts green Tomatoes
- two Gallons Vinegar
- 4 Pounds Mustard
- 4 Pounds Mustard Seeds
- one can French Mustard
- one Ounce Cloves
- two Ounces Turmeric

## Directions

1. Cut all vegetables into small pieces
2. Cook each type of vegetable separately until soft
3. Sieve
4. Place vinegar and spices in a kettle and bring to a boil
5. Mix the vegetables and pour the vinegar over

title: Christmas-Cake (English Christmas Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 250 grams margarine
- 125 grams fine sugar
- zest of 1 lemon or lemon drops
- cardamom
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 500 grams Pillsbury flour
- 125 grams sultana raisins
- 125 grams currants
- 500 milliliters semi-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Mix eggs, margarine, fine sugar, lemon zest or lemon drops, cardamom, and baking powder well
2. Add Pillsbury flour, sultana raisins, currants, and semi-skimmed milk
3. Pour batter into a greased form

title: Chutney (Chutney)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l vinegar
- 40 plums
- 3 pods Spanish pepper
- 0.25 kg sultana raisins
- 20 peeled shallots
- 1 small clove of garlic
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 2 teaspoons ground ginger
- 1 teaspoon cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Place 3 pods of Spanish pepper in boiling water to soften and make them easier to chop fine
2. Chop together with 0.25 kg sultana raisins, 20 peeled shallots and 1 small clove of garlic
3. Cook all of these together with the vinegar under cover for 1 hour
4. Then add the split plums with the stones removed, 1 tablespoon salt, 3 tablespoons sugar, 2 teaspoons ground ginger and 1 teaspoon cayenne pepper
5. After the plums are added, cook the mixture again under cover for approximately 2 hours
6. When the chutney is ready, it should resemble a thin porridge
7. Pour it into jars and bind with parchment paper
8. Can be stored for years

title: Chutney- eller Tartar-Smør (Chutney or Tartar Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kg (½ pound) stirred butter
- 2 tablespoons chutney
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- 1 tablespoon mixed mustard
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. To ¼ kg stirred butter, mix in 2 tablespoons chutney that has first been pressed through a coarse sieve, 1 tablespoon chopped parsley, 1 tablespoon mixed mustard, and a little lemon juice.
2. For fried flounder, hornfish, etc., the chutney can be omitted.

title: Chutney-Sauce (Chutney Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Even, brown gravy sauce
- Chutney

## Directions

1. Prepare an even, brown gravy sauce
2. Add chutney to taste
3. Can be served with roasted meat and fish dishes

title: Châteaubriand (Beef Filet Slices)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef filet slices, 3 centimeters thick
- Salt
- Pepper
- Brown butter
- Gravy
- Stirred butter or piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Châteaubriand consists of simple slices of beef filet 3 centimeters thick.
2. Pound lightly on both sides, sprinkle with salt and pepper and fry like beef.
3. Can be served as beef with stirred butter or with another piquant sauce, after which it is named accordingly.
4. Can be varied with the different additions mentioned in beef filet and pork tenderloin prepared as beef.

title: Cider-Gelee (Cider Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce gelatine
- rind of 1 lemon
- juice of 3 lemons
- 1 pint cold water
- 2 pounds whole sugar
- 3 pints boiling water
- 1 pint cider

## Directions

1. Add 1 ounce of gelatine to the grated peel of 1 lemon and the juice of 3.
2. Add 1 pint of cold water and let it stand for one hour.
3. Then add 2 pounds of whole sugar, 3 pints of boiling water, and 1 pint of cider.
4. Set to cool as usual.

title: Cinger Snaps (Ginger Snaps)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup molasses
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup water
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- 2 teaspoons saleratus

## Directions

1. Dissolve 2 teaspoons saleratus in the molasses.
2. Mix the whole together with flour until stiff, and cut into round cakes.

title: Citron Creme (Lemon Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon corn starch
- juice of 4 lemons
- 1/2 pint old wine
- powdered sugar to taste
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Whisk the egg yolks well; add the corn starch, juice of 4 lemons, 1/2 pint old wine and powdered sugar to taste.
2. Place over heat with constant stirring until it begins to boil and becomes thick.
3. Whisk 4 egg whites to stiff peaks and fold them in.
4. Pour out and continue stirring until it becomes cold.
5. This is for 8 to 10 persons.

title: Citron Saft (Lemon Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lemons
- Sugar, ¼ pound per pint of juice
- 5 to 6 lemons per pint juice

## Directions

1. Remove all the insides of the lemons and press them through a sieve or wring them out in a cloth
2. For 1 pint juice (from 5 to 6 lemons), take ¼ pound sugar
3. Put it on the fire at once, boil and skim well for 10 minutes
4. Pour into a jar and seal

title: Citron Sauce (Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Lemon
- 1 Ounce Butter
- 1 Ounce Sugar
- 1/4 Pint boiling Water
- 1 Ounce Sugar

## Directions

1. Press out the Juice from a Lemon and grate the Shell.
2. Put 1 Ounce Butter over the Fire and mix it with 1 Ounce Sugar until it boils.
3. Then stir in 1/4 Pint boiling Water, 1 Ounce Sugar, Lemon Shell and Juice.
4. The Sauce is served for Puddings.
5. It must not boil after the Lemon is added, as it will otherwise become bitter.

title: Citron som Lemon (Preserved Lemons)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 lemons
- salt
- 2 pieces Spanish pepper
- 2 tablespoons white pepper
- a few pieces scraped ginger
- 2 pots best wine vinegar
- 12 tablespoons mustard seed
- 3-4 shallots

## Directions

1. Dry the lemons well and make a cross-cut at one end down to the middle of the lemon
2. Widen the cross-cuts and fill each one with a spoonful of salt
3. Stand the lemons in a dish with the whole end down
4. After they have stood uncovered for 8 days, turn them and let them stand the same length of time on the other end
5. Place them in an enameled pot side by side
6. Pour the drained brine over them and add 2 pieces Spanish pepper, 2 tablespoons white pepper, and a few pieces scraped ginger
7. Add 2 pots best wine vinegar to the pot and place over a good heat of coals
8. Boil the lemons for half an hour over even heat until they are tender
9. Place them in a jar and pour the hot brine over them; if the brine is not sufficient, boil some vinegar and pour it hot into the jar
10. Sew a small but fairly roomy bag of white, open linen large enough to fill the upper part of the jar
11. Put 12 tablespoons mustard seed and 3-4 shallots in the bag
12. Boil this slightly while the lemons are cooking and place it over them in the jar
13. The next day, bind the jar in the usual way
14. After 3 months have passed, you can begin to use them; they become better by standing even longer
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citron- eller Appelsinskaller (Lemon or Orange Peel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) lemon or orange peel
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) powdered loaf sugar
- water
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the peel from the fruit in 2 or 4 pieces and soak them in cold water for 5 days, changing the water daily.
2. After this time, place them over heat in cold water and cook them gently under a lid, usually for almost 1/2 hour.
3. Remove the peels and cut away all the white part before weighing.
4. Boil the sugar into a thin syrup with 1/2 l (1/2 pot) water, which is only poured over the peels once it has cooled, and let them sit covered for 2 to 3 days.
5. Pour the syrup into a pot and boil it until smooth together with the juice of 1 lemon.
6. Finally, give the peels one boil in it before placing them in the jars.
7. Reduce the syrup again until it is completely smooth, pour it boiling over the peels, and seal the jars immediately.

title: Citron-Budding (Lemon Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 2 large lemons
- 0.5 Pint water for each bottle of good wine
- 12 Pounds powdered sugar (for 2 Quarts of the mixture)
- Zest of 2 lemons
- 1.5 Ounces gelatine, dissolved in warm water

## Directions

1. Mix together the lemon juice and water with the wine
2. Take 2 Quarts of this mixture and add the powdered sugar, lemon zest, and gelatine dissolved in warm water
3. Cook the mixture
4. Stir both during cooking and afterward in a stone bowl until cold
5. Pour into the mold
6. Serve with yellow Crème sauce with some finely crushed almonds

title: Citron-Cake (Lemon Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams margarine
- 375 grams fine sugar
- 5 eggs
- 4 teaspoons baking powder
- zest and juice of 1 lemon
- a little orange essence
- 1 kilogram Pillsbury flour
- 750 milliliters semi-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Mix margarine, fine sugar, eggs, baking powder, lemon zest, lemon juice, and orange essence well
2. Add Pillsbury flour and semi-skimmed milk
3. Bake for 1 hour 15 minutes in a moderately warm oven
4. Grease and line the form with paper

title: Citron-Fromage (Lemon Fromage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound powdered icing sugar
- 20 egg yolks
- juice of 6 lemons
- zest of 1 lemon
- 12 quintals isinglass
- 2 pints boiling water
- 20 beaten egg whites
- fruit sauce

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 pound powdered icing sugar with 20 egg yolks, juice of 6 lemons, zest of 1 lemon, and 12 quintals isinglass which has first been dissolved in 2 pints boiling water.
2. Bring the mixture to a boil.
3. Fold in the 20 beaten egg whites.
4. Serve cold in glass bowls with fruit sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citron-Gelee (Lemon Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ounces gelatine
- 1 quart boiling water
- juice of 3 large lemons
- 1 pint white sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve 3 ounces of gelatine.
2. Add 1 quart of boiling water, the juice of 3 large lemons, and 1 pint of white sugar.

title: Citron-Gelée (Lemon Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Zest of 3 lemons
- Juice of 5 lemons
- 2 pounds white sugar
- 2 pints water
- 12 quarters isinglass
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Make exactly as Recipe 421, except that instead of the zest of oranges use the zest of 3 lemons, and instead of orange juice use the juice of 5 lemons.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citron-Is (Lemon Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ pints water
- 1 pound sugar
- Zest of 2 lemons
- Juice of 6 lemons
- 2 egg whites
- Alternative: 2 tablespoons thick cream foam

## Directions

1. Boil 1½ pints water and 1 pound sugar and skim.
2. Add the finely grated zest of 2 and the strained juice of 6 lemons.
3. When cold, stir in 2 egg whites beaten until stiff, then freeze.
4. Instead of egg whites, you can also add 2 tablespoons thick cream foam.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citron-Is (Lemon Ice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar
- 1 L (1 pot) water
- Juice of 8 lemons
- Zest of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Make a syrup from the powdered sugar, water, juice of 8 lemons, and zest of 2 lemons.
2. Treat it as the preceding types.

title: Citron-Kréme (Lemon Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints French white wine (Graves)
- 1 pint water
- 16 beaten egg yolks
- 1 pound white sugar
- zest of 3 lemons
- juice of 3 lemons
- 8 beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, place 2 pints of French white wine (Graves), 1 pint of water, 16 beaten egg yolks, 1 pound of white sugar, on which the zest of 3 lemons has been grated, and the strained juice of 3 lemons.
2. Set all this over the heat and warm it until it begins to boil, then remove it, and stir in 8 beaten egg whites.
3. The cream is whisked until it is cold.
4. It is arranged in a deep dish and garnished with powdered sugar.
5. Biscuits are served with it.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citron-Omelet (Lemon Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- grated lemon zest
- 6 drams powdered sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons melted butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 6 egg whites
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Beat together the grated lemon zest, 6 drams powdered sugar and 6 egg yolks
2. Beat in 6 tablespoons melted butter and 2 tablespoons flour
3. Finally beat in the 6 egg whites, first beaten to stiff peaks, and a few drops of lemon juice
4. Bake the omelet in a moderate oven for 2 hours
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citron-Sauce (Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- half a bottle white French wine
- 3 bottles water
- juice of 2 lemons
- zest of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Stir together 12 egg yolks, half a bottle white French wine, 3 bottles water, juice and zest of 2 lemons.
2. Place over heat and whisk with a whisk until it boils.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citron-Soufflé (Lemon Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- Yellow zest of 4 lemons
- 4 pounds white sugar, crushed
- 20 egg yolks
- 5 pounds white sugar, crushed
- Juice of 6 lemons, strained
- 16 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Rub the yellow zest of 4 lemons onto 4 pounds of crushed white sugar.
2. Remove the white pith completely.
3. Whisk the egg yolks for one hour with 5 pounds of crushed white sugar.
4. Pour the strained lemon juice into the mixture.
5. Fold in the whipped egg whites.
6. Pour the whole mixture into a deep cake pan and place in a gentle oven heat so the soufflé rises.
7. Serve dusted with powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citronkage (Lemon Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg yolk
- Half pound powdered icing sugar
- Zest of 3 lemons
- Juice of 3 lemons
- Egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Whisk 1 egg yolk for half an hour with half pound powdered icing sugar and the grated zest of 3 lemons.
2. Add the juice of 3 lemons and the whipped egg whites.
3. Bake the cake light brown in a pan at even heat and serve with whipped cream.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citronkage (Lemon Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks
- 1/4 hour time
- 3/4 pound powdered sugar
- Zest of 3 lemons
- Juice of 3 lemons
- Egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir egg yolks for 1/4 hour with 3/4 pound powdered sugar and the grated zest of 3 lemons
2. Add the juice of the 3 lemons and the whipped egg whites
3. Bake the cake until pale brown in a pan at even heat and serve with whipped cream over top
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citronkage med Mandler (Lemon Cake with Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- zest of 6 lemons
- 3 quarter-pounds powdered sugar
- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 3/4 pound bitter almonds
- egg white or a little water
- 8 eggs
- juice of 6 lemons
- butter dough (No. 186)

## Directions

1. Rub the yellow zest of 6 lemons onto 3 quarter-pounds powdered sugar, then grind very finely.
2. Melt 1 pound butter and remove the salt from it.
3. Scald and grind 1 pound sweet and 3/4 pound bitter almonds very finely either with the white of an egg or with a little water.
4. Beat 8 eggs well and stir with the almonds, then with the sugar, until the whole is properly well mixed, which will take about 1 hour.
5. Then press the juice of the 6 lemons through a sieve into the mixture.
6. Grease a form with butter and line with butter dough (No. 186), then fill with the dough and crimp the butter dough at the edge.
7. Insert immediately in the oven and bake for 2 hours.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citronol (Lemon Ale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 fresh lemons
- 10 pounds sugar
- Water
- 0.5 pint yeast

## Directions

1. Pour the sugar together with 37 quarts of water into a clean kettle, boil for 1 hour, skim well, cool. When lukewarm, add the yeast. Cover the vessel with a lid, not a cloth, which would cause off-flavor. Let stand 2 or more days for fermentation. Carefully skim off the yeast foam, pour the ale into barrels. Slice the lemons without peeling and add them. Bung the barrel and place in the cellar. After 14 days the ale is ready for bottling. Pour 23 quarts of fresh spring water into the barrel and bottle it.

title: Citronsaft (Lemon Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lemons
- 3/4 kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar per 1 liter (1 pot) juice

## Directions

1. The pared lemon peels can be preserved as No. 29, and the juice of the lemons is pressed out and then poured through a fine sieve or cloth.
2. Boil with the sugar for 10 minutes and pour hot into small bottles, which are tied or corked with clean, scalded corks that are well dried.

title: Citronsaft til Te (Russian Lemon Juice for Tea)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 5 lemons
- 1/2 kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar

## Directions

1. The procedure is the same as for Orange Juice No. 175.

title: Citrontærte (Lemon Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- Zest of 4 lemons
- 1 pound white sugar
- 20 egg yolks
- Juice of 4 lemons, strained
- 10 egg whites, whipped to peaks

## Directions

1. Rub the zest of 4 lemons onto 1 pound white sugar, then crush it fine.
2. Whisk this with 20 egg yolks and the strained juice of 4 lemons for one hour.
3. Whip 10 egg whites to stiff peaks and gently fold in.
4. Bake the mixture in a buttered pan at moderate heat for three-quarters to one hour.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citrontærte (Lemon Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- Zest of 4 lemons
- 1 pound white sugar
- 20 egg yolks
- Juice of the 4 lemons
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Rub the zest of 4 lemons onto 1 pound white sugar, then pound it finely and stir for an hour with 20 egg yolks and the strained juice of the 4 lemons
2. Whip 10 egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in carefully
3. Bake the mixture in a buttered form at moderate heat for up to 1 hour
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Citrou-Omelet (Lemon Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound clarified butter
- 0.5 pound powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoon fine wheat flour
- 12 beaten egg yolks
- 1 quart sweet cream
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Grated peel of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Stir clarified butter with powdered sugar
2. Stir in wheat flour
3. Add beaten egg yolks
4. Add sweet cream
5. Add lemon juice, strained to avoid seeds
6. Add grated lemon peel
7. Beat well together
8. Gently fold in stiffly beaten egg whites

title: Clam Chowder (New Bedford Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 clams
- 1/2 pound salted pork, finely diced
- Onions, finely chopped
- Potatoes, sliced thin
- 3 pints water
- 1 quart hot water
- Crackers, crushed
- Sweet milk

## Directions

1. Rinse the clams thoroughly and place in a pot with 3 pints water.
2. When the shells open, remove the clams and chop them finely, reserving all the clam water.
3. Fry the pork slowly, remove the pieces, and fry or toast the onions.
4. Keep the chowder pot very clean to avoid scorching.
5. Add the onions with 1 quart hot water.
6. Pour in the clams, clam water, and pork pieces.
7. When boiling, add the potatoes.
8. When the potatoes are cooked, the dish is done.
9. Add a little crushed crackers and sweet milk before serving.

title: Cod au Fromage (Torsk au fromage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of cold, cooked macaroni, broken into small pieces
- 1 cupful of cold, cooked cod
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Cold, melted butter or milk
- Grated cheese

## Directions

1. Mix the macaroni and cod well.
2. Place in a buttered dish or bowl and lay a small lump of butter on top.
3. Season with salt and pepper, moisten with cold melted butter or milk.
4. Sprinkle several spoonfuls of grated cheese over it and bake until done, then serve in the pan.

title: Coffee Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup strong coffee
- 5 cups flour
- 1 pound raisins
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 3 or 5 eggs

## Directions

1. Dissolve the soda in the coffee

title: Cognac with Quince (Quince Preserve with Cognac)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Quince
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Vinegar
- Cognac

## Directions

1. Place over the fire together with sugar and vinegar and cook until tender, while skimming at the same time.
2. Remove the quinces and reduce the liquid further before pouring it hot over the fruit.
3. Seal as soon as the preserve has cooled.

title: Composition Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 2¾ cups sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 2 teaspoons cream of tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 4 cups flour
- beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 1 cup butter with 5 egg yolks, then 2¾ cups sugar, 1 cup milk, 2 teaspoons cream of tartar, 1 teaspoon soda and 4 cups flour
2. Finally add the beaten whites

title: Consommé Royal (Royal Consommé)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-5 L (4.5-5.5 pots) economical stock
- 0.5 sweetbreads
- Water
- Butter or vegetable margarine
- 100 g (3.5 oz) flour
- 6 egg yolks
- Paprika
- Madeira wine
- Salt sticks

## Directions

1. Blanch the sweetbreads and cook them tender in the economical stock
2. Cut half of the sweetbreads into very small dice and place in the tureen
3. Finely chop the remaining sweetbreads and sauté in a very little butter or vegetable margarine
4. Brown 100 g (3.5 oz) flour and dilute with the stock, thickened with 6 egg yolks
5. Add a little paprika and Madeira to taste
6. Press the soup through a coarse sieve into the tureen
7. Serve with salt sticks

title: Corn Meal-Brod (Corn Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts cornmeal
- 3 pints hot water
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 2 large tablespoons homemade or 1 cent baker's yeast
- 1 3/4 cups wheat flour
- 3/4 pint hot water

## Directions

1. Blend 2 quarts cornmeal with 3 pints hot water.
2. Add 1 tablespoon salt, 2 tablespoons sugar, and 2 large tablespoons homemade or 1 cent baker's yeast.
3. Let stand 5 hours in a warm place.
4. Add 1 3/4 cups wheat flour and 3/4 pint hot water.
5. Let it rise again for 1 1/2 hours, knead lightly, and divide into bread pans, letting them stand a few minutes before putting them in.
6. Bake in a moderate oven for 1 1/2 hours.

title: Corn Starch Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 egg whites
- 2 cups powdered sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup corn starch (mixed in the milk)
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- almond extract

## Directions

1. Stir 7 egg whites with 2 cups powdered sugar, 1 cup butter, 1 cup flour, 1 cup corn starch (mixed in milk), 2 teaspoons baking powder sifted with 2 cups flour, and almond extract

title: Corn Starch Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Eggs
- 4 Ounces sugar
- 3 Ounces corn starch
- 1 Teaspoonful vanilla or lemon
- 1 Quart milk

## Directions

1. Mix together the eggs, sugar, corn starch, and vanilla or lemon
2. Add the milk gradually
3. Heat until nearly boiling
4. Pour into cups and place in a roasting pan filled halfway with water
5. Place the pan in a moderately warm oven and bake the small puddings for half an hour until they are firm and light brown
6. They may be eaten warm from the cups or turned out and eaten cold

title: Corn Starch Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Eggs
- 4 Ounces sugar
- 3 Ounces corn starch
- 1 Teaspoon vanilla or lemon flavoring
- 1 Quart milk

## Directions

1. Mix together the eggs, sugar, corn starch, and vanilla or lemon
2. Gradually add the milk
3. Heat over the stove until nearly boiling
4. Pour into cups and set in a baking pan half full of water
5. Place the pan in a moderately warm oven and bake for half an hour until firm and light brown
6. May be eaten warm from the cups or turned out and eaten cold

title: Corned Beef Fogt (Boiled Salted Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Corned beef
- Water

## Directions

1. The meat is placed in water overnight if it is very salty, but if the meat is young and only lightly salted, such prolonged soaking is not necessary.
2. The salt is washed off, and cold water is poured over to completely cover it.
3. It should be placed over so slow a fire that it will only simmer, and remain thus for 4 to 6 hours according to the size of the piece.
4. If it is tough, let it stand over until the next day and bring it to the boiling point just before serving.
5. Breast pieces are simmered until the bones are easily removed, folded together and placed under a weight until cold; then it can be sliced.

title: Cotelets (Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal, lamb, or pork ribs
- Green vegetables or potatoes
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cutlets are made from veal as well as from lamb and pork
2. For this, ribs are bought
3. The butcher cuts it into slices with a bone to each; from lamb meat one must place two bones to each piece so that it becomes thick enough, but only one remains attached
4. Serve with green vegetables or potatoes and no other sauce than that which remains on the platter, for which reason a little good butter is placed on it

title: Couleur (Coloring)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound St. Croix sugar
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Moisten 1 pound St. Croix sugar in a kettle and boil with constant stirring to a thick, brownish syrup.
2. Dilute with boiling water and boil again for 1 hour.
3. When cold, pour into clean, dry bottles, cork and store in a dry place.
4. This produces a golden-brown color.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Cranberry Pie (Trane Bær Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cranberries
- Cold water
- Sugar
- 2 teaspoons corn starch
- Pie dough
- 1 peeled and cut apple

## Directions

1. Pick the cranberries clean, wash in cold water, and set over heat with cold water that covers them.
2. Let them boil gently until they are soft.
3. Press them through a sieve with a spoon.
4. Set them over heat again with sufficient sugar and 2 teaspoons corn starch dissolved in a little of the juice that has been held back and must be cold.
5. When it comes to a boil it is done.
6. Line the form with dough prepared meanwhile.
7. Pour the berries over and lay strips of dough on top in various figures.
8. Bake in a moderately heated oven.
9. To take away the bitter taste of the cranberries, one may boil a peeled and cut apple among them.

title: Cranberry Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- Large, best berries, 1 quart
- Powdered sugar, ½ pound
- Cinnamon or lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook the berries with the powdered sugar and a little cinnamon or lemon peel, and skim until they are no longer sour, usually in a quarter of an hour
2. Strain them, lay a cloth over and tie them up the next day
3. Small pieces of apple can be cooked together with the berries, which greatly removes their bitter taste

title: Cream Pie No. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 scant pint fresh milk
- 2 small tablespoons corn starch
- 2 eggs
- 1 scant teacup sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1.5 cups milk
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 8 tablespoons cold water

## Directions

1. Boil 1 scant pint fresh milk.
2. In a little of the cold milk, stir 2 small tablespoons corn starch and beat together with 2 eggs.
3. When the milk has boiled, slowly stir this in with 1 scant teacup sugar, 0.5 cup butter, and 2 tablespoons lemon juice.
4. For the cake, stir together 3 eggs with 1 cup white sugar, 1.5 cups milk, 1 teaspoon baking powder sifted together with 8 tablespoons cold water.
5. Pour into two ordinary pie plates and bake in a hot oven.
6. When the cake is done, take it out, split while still warm, and fill the cream between both layers.
7. If desired, it can be glazed again with egg white and sugar.

title: Cream Pie No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 0.5 teaspoon soda
- Beaten egg whites
- 1 pint milk
- 1 tablespoon corn starch
- A little cold milk
- 3 eggs
- A little vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg yolks thick, then stir in 1 cup sugar and then 1 cup flour sifted together with 1 teaspoon cream of tartar and 0.5 teaspoon soda.
2. When stirred together, fold in the beaten egg whites.
3. Divide into four ordinary pie plates and bake light brown. This will be sufficient for 2 pies.
4. For the cream, use 1 pint milk, 1 tablespoon corn starch dissolved in a little cold milk, 3 eggs, and a little vanilla.
5. When cold, divide into two parts and spread between two layers of cake.

title: Cream Rice Pudding (Cream Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces rice
- Water
- 4.5 tablespoons sugar
- 1 teaspoon lemon extract
- 3 quarts milk

## Directions

1. Wash the rice well in two waters and place in a form, which may be earthenware.
2. Add the sugar and lemon extract; pour 3 quarts of milk over it.
3. Place in a moderate oven to bake for 1.5 hours, or until it reaches a creamy consistency.

title: Cream Sauce with Parsley
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- cream
- chopped parsley
- white sugar
- salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Melt butter, stir flour in it and dilute with cream until smooth
2. Give chopped parsley a boiling in it
3. Add a small piece of white sugar and salt at the end
4. Milk can be used instead of cream, but then more butter is used

title: Cremaortartarikage (Cream of Tartar Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 quarter pound butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 4.5 pounds powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoon pounded bitter almonds
- 1 pound fine flour
- 3 quintals cream of tartar
- 1.5 quintals baking soda
- 2 cups boiled, cold milk
- Egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir the butter white with 4 egg yolks, 4.5 pounds powdered sugar, and 1 tablespoon pounded bitter almonds
2. Mix 1 pound fine flour with 3 quintals cream of tartar and 1.5 quintals baking soda, and gradually stir in 2 cups boiled, cold milk
3. Finally add the whipped egg whites
4. Pour the batter into a form and bake for 1 hour at even heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Creme Sauce (Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart good sweet Cream
- 7 Ounces Sugar
- a little Vanilla
- 8 well-beaten Egg yolks
- whipped Cream, about 5 to 6 Tablespoons

## Directions

1. Boil 1 Quart good sweet Cream with 7 Ounces Sugar and a little Vanilla.
2. Thicken it with 8 well-beaten Egg yolks, and whip it well.
3. When it is cold, add whipped Cream, about 5 to 6 Tablespoons, which is gently stirred in and should be seen on top.
4. This Sauce is used cold for Red Wine Pudding, Lemon Pudding, Fromage and several similar Things.
5. It is also used for Blanc-Manger; but then it is mixed with twice as much whipped Cream.

title: Creme til Is (Cream for Ice Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 whole eggs
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar, preferably superfine sugar
- 1 L (1 Pot) boiling cream or sweet milk
- vanilla beans (optional)

## Directions

1. Stir the eggs and powdered sugar together with a whisk until it is a foamy mass.
2. Whip 1 L (1 Pot) boiling cream or sweet milk into this mixture.
3. Bring the cream just to the boiling point while continuing to work it—it must not boil.
4. Whip it until it is cold to prevent it from forming a skin.
5. This cream can have the kernels of 1 vanilla bean added and can serve as the base for all the following types of cream ice cream.

title: Creme-Ablekage (Cream Apple Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine apples
- sugar
- water
- jam (raspberry or cherry)
- 1 quart cream
- 8 egg yolks
- vanilla
- half a tablespoon corn starch

## Directions

1. Peel apples and cut off a lid, hollowing them out
2. Simmer in sugar and a little water so they do not fall apart
3. Arrange them close together in a fairly deep pan and fill with jam without liquid, preferably raspberry or cherry marmalade
4. Cover with the cut-off lids
5. Make a cream from 1 quart cream, 8 egg yolks, a little vanilla, half a tablespoon corn starch, and sugar to taste
6. When cold, pour the cream over the apples
7. Place the pan in the oven and bake the cake slowly
8. When the batter begins to set and becomes light brown, it is done

title: Creme-Sauce (Cream Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight egg yolks
- 5 to 6 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1 L (1 Pot) sweet milk
- A few drops of vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Whisk together eight egg yolks white with 5 to 6 tablespoons powdered sugar
2. When 1 L (1 Pot) sweet milk boils, pour some of it into the eggs, which are poured back into the pot under vigorous whisking with a whisk
3. As soon as it comes to a boil, remove it quickly from heat and pour it out
4. Stir a few drops of vanilla essence into the cream, which must be stirred regularly until it is cold so it does not form a skin

title: Creme-Sauce med Flødeskum (Cream Sauce with Whipped Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Four egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) milk
- A few drops of vanilla essence
- 4 pieces of isinglass dissolved in insignificant water
- 2 glasses of sherry
- White wine or rhubarb juice
- 1/2 L (1 Pægl) cream for whipped cream
- Fruit coloring
- Strawberry juice
- Raspberry juice
- Cherry marmalade
- Ginger
- Orange essence
- Maraschino or cacao liqueur

## Directions

1. Whisk together four egg yolks white with 3 tablespoons powdered sugar
2. Bring 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) milk to a boil and stir it into the egg mixture, which is again poured into the pot and stirred well until it comes to a boil
3. Pour it out and add a few drops of vanilla essence
4. Stir in 4 pieces of isinglass dissolved in insignificant water together with 2 glasses of sherry and as much white wine or rhubarb juice so that the sauce gets a fresh, tart flavor
5. Stir regularly in the cream until it is cold
6. When it is to be served, stir in whipped cream made from 1/2 L (1 Pægl) cream, and if the sauce is desired thinner, it can simply be diluted with a little cream
7. The sauce can be colored pale pink with fruit coloring and served with various cold puddings
8. It can be varied with: strawberry juice, raspberry juice, cherry marmalade, ginger, orange essence, or maraschino or cacao liqueur

title: Creme-Snitter eller Creme-Stænger (Cream Slices or Cream Sticks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 Gr (1/4 S) Flour
- Cream (made from 3 1/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) semi-skimmed milk, 2 egg yolks, stirred with 1 tablespoon powdered sugar and 1 tablespoon cornstarch)
- chopped almonds or candied citrus peel and raisins
- egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Roll out puff pastry made from 375 Gr (1/4 S) flour thinly on a large baking sheet
2. Cut the dough into strips 1/4 quarter inch wide
3. Brush each strip with a thin layer of cream
4. Sprinkle with chopped almonds or candied citrus peel and raisins and cover with a thin layer of dough
5. Brush with egg and sprinkle with chopped almonds
6. Bake at strong heat
7. Cut into appropriate pieces when cooled
8. The cream is made from 3 1/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) semi-skimmed milk, 2 egg yolks, stirred with 1 tablespoon powdered sugar and 1 tablespoon cornstarch

title: Croquant-Kringler (Croquant Twists)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 354 grams (1/4 pound) flour
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) margarine
- 175 grams (35 quintals) powdered sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 egg yolk
- Sugar for dipping

## Directions

1. Knead the flour, margarine, powdered sugar, egg, and egg yolk together well
2. Roll and form into small twists
3. Dip them in sugar and bake until light brown
4. They are very crisp

title: Custard Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pint milk
- 1 tablespoon corn starch
- 3 egg yolks
- A little vanilla
- Salt
- Pie dough
- Beaten egg whites
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 0.5 pint milk.
2. Stir into it 1 tablespoon corn starch, 3 egg yolks, a little vanilla, and salt.
3. Line the plate with ordinary dough.
4. Pour in the filling and set in the oven and bake at good heat.
5. Beat the whites with sugar and place on top when done, after which set it back in the oven for a moment.

title: Cutlets à l'Anglaise
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Bones and membranes
- Madeira
- Food coloring
- Meat extract or Maggi bouillon cubes
- Boiled, smoked ham

## Directions

1. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and roast on the pan in browned butter.
2. Boil the bones and membranes to make a gravy, which is mixed with Madeira, food coloring, and meat extract or Maggi bouillon cubes.
3. Pour the gravy over the cutlets, which are arranged in the center of the platter.
4. Arrange slices of boiled, smoked ham in a ring around them and eat together with the cutlets.

title: Daisy-Cakes
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) Pillsbury flour
- 175 grams (35 quintal) margarine
- 75 grams (15 quintal) icing sugar
- a little cardamom
- a few drops of orange essence
- 2 teaspoons baking powder, mixed in 1/4 dl (1/4 pint) milk

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Roll out thinly
3. Cut with a cake cutter into long, narrow cakes
4. Brush with egg
5. Can be sprinkled either with caraway seeds or with chopped almonds
6. Bake in a well-heated oven

title: Damepunsch (Ladies' Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle best white rum
- 1 bottle white French wine (Cerons)
- juice of 1 lemon
- 6 measures boiling water
- 1 pound white top sugar

## Directions

1. Add juice of 1 lemon and 6 measures boiling water in which 1 pound white top sugar is dissolved to 1 bottle best white rum and 1 bottle white French wine (Cerons)
2. Drink warm or cold
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Dampkogt Kalkun (Steamed Turkey)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 stuffed turkey
- slices of bacon
- salt
- a little broth or water
- carrots, cut in half
- celery
- butter
- flour
- egg yolks
- turkey broth from cooking

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a pot with slices of bacon.
2. Place the turkey on top and steam until tender for approximately 3 hours in a little broth or water to which salt, a few halved carrots, and a celery stalk have been added.
3. Alternatively, cook on a rack with only a little liquid on the bottom of the pot.
4. With a fine knitting needle, one can pierce the turkey to ascertain that it is tender.
5. Make a sauce by browning butter and flour together, then dilute with the broth from the turkey and thicken with a few egg yolks.
6. The sauce can be given various additions according to recipe 935.

title: Dansk Boeuf (Danish Beef or Chopped Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef from the inner leg, approximately half a pound per portion
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut beef from the inner leg into pieces, 6 to 8 pieces per pound.
2. Remove the sinews and beat each piece well with a meat mallet and chop it with a knife like carbonade.
3. Place the pieces on a platter as they are finished, but they must not touch each other.
4. Place a frying pan over the heat with some butter, and when it is browned, add the beef to it.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Dansk Boeuf (Danish Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef from the tenderloin, approximately half a pound per portion
- Wheat flour
- Fine salt
- Ground pepper
- Butter
- Onions
- Water or beer

## Directions

1. Cut beef from the tenderloin into pieces, 6 to 8 pieces per pound.
2. Remove the sinews from each piece and beat well with a mallet.
3. Chop with a knife like a cutlet.
4. Place finished pieces on a plate without letting them touch each other.
5. Heat butter in a frying pan over the fire until browned.
6. Turn each piece of beef in wheat flour mixed with fine salt and ground pepper.
7. Place the beef in the pan and fry it.
8. As the beef is fried, place it on a warm serving dish.
9. Add a good piece of butter to the pan along with peeled onions sliced into slices.
10. Bake the onions until tender over gentle heat.
11. When done, thin the sauce with a little water or beer.
12. Add salt to taste.
13. Pour the sauce over the beef.
14. Serve with peeled, boiled potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Dansk Knækbrød (Danish Crisp Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound wheat flour
- 3/4 pound cold butter
- 1 measure milk
- a handful caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Work 1 pound wheat flour, 3/4 pound cold butter, 1 measure milk and a handful caraway seeds together.
2. Roll out into very thin cakes, cut into oblong, square pieces with a pastry wheel, double the size of a playing card.
3. Prick with a fork and bake at good heat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Dild-Sauce (Dill Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- egg yolks
- fresh dill
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Bake butter and flour together and thin with bouillon.
2. Thicken with a couple of egg yolks.
3. Finally add finely chopped dill and a little lemon juice.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Dorotheakager (Dorothy Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds butter
- 3½ pounds powdered sugar
- 2 pounds wheat flour
- 8 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Stir butter with powdered sugar
2. Add wheat flour
3. Finally fold in 8 whipped egg whites
4. Bake the small cakes light brown
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Doughnuts
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sour milk
- 1 tablespoon soda
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 3 eggs
- salt
- cinnamon (if desired)
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Mix 2 cups sour milk, 1 tablespoon soda, 2 cups sugar, 1 tablespoon melted butter, 3 eggs, salt, and cinnamon if desired with enough wheat flour to make a dough that can be rolled.
2. Bake in hot lard and dry afterwards.

title: Dried White Beans (Torrede, hvide Bønner)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg dried white beans
- Maggis bouillon cubes
- butter
- ground white pepper
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak the beans in lukewarm water overnight
2. The next day, drain the water and cook the beans in bouillon or water with Maggis bouillon cubes, adding a slice of butter
3. The beans must remain whole and not be overcooked
4. At serving time, drain the cooking liquid and pour melted butter mixed with ground white pepper over the beans
5. Season with salt and lemon juice to taste

title: Dronning-Suppe à la reine (Queen's Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- En fed Høne
- 4 Pd. Oxekjød
- 3 Pd. Kalvekjød
- en Theskefuld Salt
- 1 Selleri
- nogle Persillerødder
- 2 Løg
- nogle Korn hvidt Peber
- 2 til 3 Spiseskefulde Hvedemel
- 6 Æggeblommer
- 6 Spiseskefulde god sød Fløde
- afbagt Brød

## Directions

1. Afvadskes En fed Høne, 4 Pd. Oxekjød og 3 Pd. Kalvekjød i koldt Vand og sættes over Ilden med en Theskefuld Salt og saa meget Vand, at det netop staar over Kjødet.
2. Naar Suppen er skummet, tilføjes 1 Selleri, nogle Persillerødder, 2 Løg og nogle Korn hvidt Peber.
3. Naar Hønen er mør, tages den op; men det Øvrige koges endnu i en Time og sies da.
4. Det Fede skummes af Suppen, kommes i en anden Kasserolle med 2 til 3 Spiseskefulde Hvedemel og bages godt sammen, dog ikke saaledes, at det brunes.
5. Det spædes med den afkogte Hønsebouillon og faar et Opkog.
6. Brystet af Hønen skæres i fine Strimler paa et Par Tommers Længde, og ved Anretningen kommes nogle Stykker i hver Tallerken.
7. Piskes 6 Æggeblommer i Terrinen med 6 Spiseskefulde god sød Fløde, og den kogende Suppe hældes herpaa under stadig Piskning.
8. Til Suppen kan serveres afbagt Brød.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Duchesse Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Butter
- Salt
- White pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes and press through a coarse sieve.
2. Mix them with a piece of butter, salt, very little white pepper, a little grated nutmeg and two egg yolks.
3. Spread the dough on a plate and shape with a knife into small cakes two inches long and one inch wide.
4. Place them on a buttered plate, brush on top with egg white and place in the oven until golden brown.
5. The oven must not be very hot.

title: Duer med brun Champignons-Sauce (Squabs with Brown Mushroom Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs
- Soup or water
- Mushroom sauce (see Nr. 758)
- Puff pastry border or strips

## Directions

1. Lard and roast the squabs, basting with soup or water as described in recipe 913
2. Make a good brown mushroom sauce from the gravy
3. Pour over the split squabs
4. Garnish with puff pastry border or strips

title: Duer, stegt som Vildt (Squabs Roasted as Game)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs
- Milk
- Butter
- Flour
- Strained gravy
- Cold butter
- Redcurrant jelly
- Roasted potatoes
- Parsley
- Redcurrant jelly, lingonberry jelly, or apple sauce for serving

## Directions

1. Lard and brown the squabs, then roast as in the previous recipe
2. Baste with milk instead of soup
3. Split and arrange garnished with roasted potatoes and parsley
4. The sauce can be reduced until smooth, or brown butter and flour and dilute with the gravy it was roasted in
5. Stir in a little cold butter and redcurrant jelly at the end
6. The sauce should have a nice, light brown color
7. Serve with redcurrant jelly, lingonberry jelly, or apple sauce

title: Dyre-Fricandeau (Venison Fricandeau)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison leg
- Lard
- Butter
- Flour
- Game stock
- Sherry, Madeira or red wine
- Potatoes
- Currant jelly
- Green salad or herb salad

## Directions

1. Cut fricandeau from the leg, lard it, brown and roast as directed in Nr. 163.
2. From the pan drippings, make a brown, smooth sauce.
3. Add sherry, Madeira or red wine to the sauce.
4. Pour the sauce over the meat, which is garnished with browned potatoes.
5. Serve with currant jelly, green salad or herb salad.

title: Dyre-Koteletter (Venison Cutlets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison cutlets from the back
- Salt and pepper
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Game stock made from bones and venison trimmings

## Directions

1. Cut small cutlets from the back.
2. Beat lightly with a meat mallet.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Dip in egg and breadcrumbs.
5. Fry as veal cutlets.
6. Pour a good brown sauce made of game stock cooked on bones and venison trimmings into the center of the serving dish.
7. Arrange the cutlets in a ring around the sauce.

title: Dyre-Ragout (Venison Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison haunch
- Butter
- Flour
- Game stock
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. After the haunch has been larded and roasted, cut it into smaller pieces.
2. Make a good sauce from the pan drippings by browning butter and flour.
3. The sauce can be finished as with venison fillets (Nr. 942).
4. Leftover ragout can be put in a pastry (see Nr. 41), and bones can be cooked in milk or thin soup for sauce to the pastry.

title: Dyrekød-Suppe (Venison Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison head, neck and breast
- Claret
- Carrots
- Onions
- Water
- Salt
- Kulør (caramel coloring)
- Paprika
- Butter
- Flour
- Madeira or red wine
- Veal or fish forcemeat for balls
- Hard-boiled egg yolks

## Directions

1. Chop the venison head, neck and breast into smaller pieces
2. Brown in claret together with carrots and a couple of onions
3. Pour in enough water so it is level with the meat
4. Add salt, kulør and paprika
5. When the soup is well skimmed and the meat is cooked tender but not shredded, remove and use for hachis
6. The bones can then be put back in the soup to cook longer with it
7. Brown butter and flour and dilute with the strained soup
8. Season with Madeira or red wine and serve with balls made of either venison or fish forcemeat
9. Hard-boiled egg yolks can be added to the soup

title: Dyreryg (Roasted Venison Back)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison back
- Butter, margarine or fat
- Flour
- Skimmed milk
- Lard
- Cream or melted margarine or butter browned with flour
- Stock, soup, thin cream or milk
- Raw cream or condensed milk
- Cold butter
- Currant jelly
- Potatoes
- Tartlets
- Herb salad

## Directions

1. The animal must hang at least 4 to 5 days before preparation.
2. Remove the back as described, and with a sharp knife remove all skin at once without losing any meat.
3. Rinse the back in skimmed milk and lard in close rows.
4. Place the back in a roasting pan, cover well with small pieces of butter, margarine or fat.
5. Put in a hot oven for a quarter hour before basting with cream or with a mixture of melted margarine or butter browned with flour, diluted with stock, soup, thin cream or milk.
6. The length of roasting depends on size, but in all cases not less than 1½ hours.
7. Strain the pan drippings for the sauce, which is either browned with butter and flour or reduced and finished with raw cream or condensed milk (483), which must boil well with it.
8. Finally add a piece of cold butter and some currant jelly.
9. At serving, cut the meat free from the ribs and backbone, then cut crosswise into slices, which are again gathered and placed on the back in the order they were cut.
10. Venison back can be garnished with browned potatoes and tartlets filled with currant jelly or with herb salad in gratin shells (see Nr. 316).
11. Fasten a skewer at the neck, passed through a lettuce head.
12. If both back and legs are to be roasted, it is best to roast the legs a good hour before placing the back in the roasting pan.

title: Dyreryg (Saddle of Venison)
body:
## Ingredients

- One saddle of venison, approximately 10 pounds
- Strips of salted meat, very thin and approximately 2 inches long
- Two carrots
- One onion
- Salted meat, sliced thin
- Two bay leaves
- Two cloves
- Five grains of allspice
- Half a lemon, sliced
- Salt
- Pepper
- One quarter soup stock (Claret)
- A small lump of butter

## Directions

1. Take one approximately 10 pounds in weight and lard it with strips of salted meat, very thin and approximately 2 inches long, two rows on each side.
2. Slice two carrots and one onion and season with a little salted meat, sliced thin, in a large roasting pan.
3. Add two bay leaves, two cloves, five grains of allspice, half a lemon sliced thin, a little salt and pepper.
4. Place the venison saddle in the pan with one quarter soup stock (Claret) and a small lump of butter, and let it simmer approximately one quarter of an hour on top of the stove.
5. Set it then in a hot oven to roast, and drip with a little butter every fifth minute, until it is half-roasted, so the blood runs out when you cut into the meat.
6. Serve with wine sauce. Others prefer it completely tender. Then it must be roasted much longer. Serve with cream sauce.

title: Dyreryg (Venison Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin of a roe deer
- Lard
- Butter
- Salt
- Cream
- Bouillon
- Water
- Wheat flour
- Roasted potatoes
- Redcurrant jelly

## Directions

1. Hang the venison for several days. Lard the roast well with lard and place in a roasting pan with browned butter, sprinkle with salt and cover with butter.
2. During roasting, baste well, initially also with cream and bouillon. For each year the animal is old, add one more branch of larding.
3. Roast at even heat for approximately 2.5 to 3 hours until tender, basting the meat regularly.
4. When the roast is tender and removed, brown the sauce with a little wheat flour, strain through a sieve, and stir in a cold piece of butter quickly.
5. Serve with roasted potatoes and redcurrant jelly.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Dyrevildt i Sky (Venison and Hare in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover venison or hare roast
- Aspic or broth
- Additional good aspic or Maggi bouillon cubes
- Italian salad
- Trotters (for garnish)

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from the bones into small pieces.
2. Arrange the meat in a ring-shaped mold.
3. Fill the center with Italian salad.
4. Arrange chopped aspic around the edge of the ring.
5. Prepare the aspic by cooking it on the roasting bones with additional good aspic, broth, or Maggi bouillon cubes.
6. Cook trotters in the aspic for garnish and decoration on top of the salad.

title: Egg Cake with Bacon (Æggekage med Flæsk)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 12 tablespoons whole milk
- 1/2 tablespoon flour
- 375 grams bacon
- Leek

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks and milk into the flour together with finely cut leek and finally the beaten egg whites.
2. Fry slices of lightly salted, striped bacon from which the rind has been removed in the pan.
3. When done, remove it and pour the fat from the pan, which is cleaned of the salt that has collected on it.
4. Pour the fat back into the pan, which is again heated over the fire.
5. Pour in the eggs and arrange the bacon on top.
6. Turn the egg cake onto a lid and bake on the other side.

title: Eggetoddy (Egg Toddy)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar
- Cognac, Rum, or Arrack
- Eggs
- Boiling water

## Directions

1. Beat 1-2 egg yolks well with 3-4 tablespoons of crushed sugar. While stirring constantly, add boiling water and finally either Cognac, Rum, Arrack, Port wine, or Madeira.

title: Endivie-Salat (Endive Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Endive heads
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt
- Chopped chervil

## Directions

1. Remove the outermost coarse, green leaves and use only the inner yellow ones.
2. Rinse and shake them well dry in a cloth.
3. Cut away the stem and section the salad and place it in the salad bowl.
4. Mix together oil, vinegar, pepper and salt as for the previous salads and pour over.
5. Endive salad can be sprinkled with chopped chervil.

title: Endivien (Endive Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- endive
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves and any insects
2. Remove the stalk and cut the lettuce into fine strips
3. Rinse in water and pat dry in a cloth
4. Prepare either with vinegar and oil alone or as the oil salad recipe
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Engelsk Boeuf (English Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tenderloin or neck piece, not freshly slaughtered but aged several days
- Butter
- Finely chopped red onion
- Pepper
- Gravy or stock

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into appropriately sized pieces and place them on a dry, strongly heated pan.
2. Fry the pieces at brisk heat so they remain juicy inside, and as they are done, place them on the serving dish and pour over them the juice that has run from the meat.
3. When all pieces are fried, add butter to the pan and stir well with a spoon to loosen the gravy that still clings to it.
4. Add finely chopped red onion to this, cook it a little with it, then add more gravy and pepper.
5. Alternatively, when the meat is cut into appropriately thick slices, beat these, dip them in chopped red onion and a little pepper, and place them on a pan in which you have browned some butter.
6. Fry the slices over a brisk heat, turn them as soon as they begin to brown, and under no circumstances fry them so much that the meat becomes dry.
7. Stir up the sauce on the pan with gravy and pour it over the pieces.
8. Serve with boiled, peeled potatoes; sometimes with a fried egg on top.
9. Calculate 3 portions from one pound of meat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Engelsk Bøf (English Beef)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 to 3 kg beef from the sirloin
- Butter
- Claret or Palmin
- Onion slices
- Salt
- Pepper
- Water or gravy or broth
- Coloring

## Directions

1. The meat must have hung at least 1 week after slaughter
2. Cut the meat with the grain into thumb-thick slices, then cut across into 2 to 3 pieces
3. Brown a good portion of onion slices in butter, claret or Palmin on the frying pan
4. While this is happening, give each piece of beef a blow with a meat hammer
5. Scrape the crisp, brown onions off the pan
6. Place the pan back over the heat and on this dry, hot pan lay the beef to quickly turn it on the other side and then immediately remove from the pan
7. Brown a good slice of butter on the pan and place the beef in it, sprinkled with salt and pepper, to brown quickly on both sides
8. Place the beef in a strip on a hot plate and distribute the browned onions on the pieces
9. Deglaze the pan with a little water, gravy or broth so the meat juices can be loosened
10. Stir a little coloring and salt and about 1/4 kg cold butter into this gravy
11. After it boils, pour it over the beef on the plate

title: Engelsk Frikadelle (English Meat Ball)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds veal
- 0.5 pound pork
- 1 large cupful finely grated breadcrumbs
- 2 eggs
- Pepper
- Salt
- Grated nutmeg
- Thyme (pulverized or chopped)

## Directions

1. Mince the veal and pork finely.
2. Stir in a large cupful of finely grated breadcrumbs, 2 eggs, pepper, salt, grated nutmeg, and a small amount of pulverized or chopped thyme.
3. Place the mixture in a deep roasting pan with browned butter and brown it well on both sides.
4. Pour bouillon over it and roast slowly in the oven for about 2 hours, turning occasionally.

title: Engelsk Frugt-Pie (English Fruit Pie)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g butter or margarine
- 1/2 kg flour
- 1/4 L cold water
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- cooked apple pieces or raw peeled apples
- powdered sugar
- egg white for brushing
- egg for brushing decoration

## Directions

1. Make a pastry dough as for butter dough, using the specified amounts of butter, flour, water and baking powder
2. Roll out the dough 3 times, handling it as little as possible with the hands but working it with a broad knife
3. Roll out a very thin sheet of dough and line a buttered form with it
4. Lay cooked apple pieces or raw peeled apples in layers with powdered sugar in the lined form until almost full
5. Lay another sheet over and press the edges together; this can be crimped with the fingers
6. Roll out the remaining dough and cut into strips or shapes; arrange these over the pie, brushed with egg white; brush the decoration with egg as well
7. Bake for 1 hour at even top and bottom heat
8. All kinds of fruit can be used

title: Engelsk Harvey-Sauce til Kød- og Fiskeretter (English Harvey Sauce for Meat and Fish Dishes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 L (1 1/4 pot) water
- 1/4 L (1/4 pot) soy sauce
- 75 gr (15 kvint) crushed celery seed
- 75 gr (15 kvint) mustard seed
- 15 gr (3 kvint) ground ginger
- nutmeg
- cayenne pepper
- 20 gr (4 kvint) garlic
- 75 gr (15 kvint) pickled, chopped cucumbers
- 1 1/2 L (1 1/2 pot) vinegar
- dried peel of one lemon

## Directions

1. Place 1/4 L (1/4 pot) soy sauce, 75 gr (15 kvint) crushed celery seed, 75 gr (15 kvint) mustard seed, 15 gr (3 kvint) ground ginger, a little nutmeg and cayenne pepper, 20 gr (4 kvint) garlic, 75 gr (15 kvint) pickled, chopped cucumbers and 1 1/2 L (1 1/2 pot) vinegar in 1 1/4 L (1 1/4 pot) water.
2. Cook all this together until it has reduced to approximately half.
3. Remove from heat and add dried peel of one lemon.
4. Let the sauce stand covered for a few days and strain before pouring into dry bottles, which are stoppered well.
5. This sauce, as well as No. 740, can be used instead of the expensive English sauces available in commerce.
6. Use in sauce for meat and fish dishes and is used much for cold buffets.

title: Engelsk Melvælling (English Flour Gruel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 grams butter
- 200 grams Pillsbury flour
- 4 liters milk
- 100 grams almonds
- salt
- sugar
- almond essence (optional)
- bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Melt 150 grams of butter or margarine
2. Roast 200 grams of Pillsbury flour in the butter
3. Dilute gradually with at least 4 liters of boiling, sweet milk
4. Cook 100 grams of chopped almonds with some bitter almonds in the mixture, or use almond essence instead
5. Season with salt and sugar
6. Serve with sugar and cinnamon, or with jam or fruit compote

title: Engelsk Nougat-Creme (English Nougat Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 Gr (35 Kvint) scalded almonds
- 250 Gr (1/2 S) powdered sugar
- 4 tablespoons water
- a little butter
- vanilla cream as Nr. 1000
- cream foam for decoration
- candied fruit crowns for decoration

## Directions

1. Cut the scalded almonds lengthwise into fine strips and place in a saucepan together with the powdered sugar, water, and a little butter.
2. While it stands over the fire, stir carefully so that the almonds do not break apart or burn.
3. Once the sugar is sufficiently brown like caramel sugar, remove from the fire and quickly place in small lumps on a buttered or oiled tray.
4. When the nougat is hard, crush it and stir it into a vanilla cream as Nr. 1000.
5. Decorate with cream foam and candied fruit crowns.

title: Engelsk Nougat-Creme (English Nougat Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 gr (35 quintal) blanched almonds
- 250 gr (1/2 S) powdered sugar
- 4 tablespoons water
- a little butter

## Directions

1. Cut blanched almonds lengthwise into fine strips
2. Place in a saucepan with powdered sugar, water and a little butter
3. Stir gently over heat to prevent the almonds from breaking or burning

title: Engelsk Superlative-Sauce (English Superlative Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) port wine
- 1/4 L (1/4 pot) pickles vinegar
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) mushroom soy sauce
- 1 1/4 dl (1/8 pot) ordinary soy sauce
- 30 gr (6 kvint) finely chopped shallots
- 30 gr (6 kvint) finely chopped lemon peel
- 30 gr (6 kvint) grated horseradish
- 15 gr (3 kvint) ground pepper
- 15 gr (3 kvint) ground allspice
- 10 chopped anchovies
- cayenne pepper
- celery seed

## Directions

1. Place 1/2 L (1/2 pot) port wine, 1/4 L (1/4 pot) pickles vinegar, 1/2 L (1/2 pot) mushroom soy sauce and 1 1/4 dl (1/8 pot) ordinary soy sauce in a small barrel, along with 30 gr (6 kvint) finely chopped shallots, 30 gr (6 kvint) finely chopped lemon peel, 30 gr (6 kvint) grated horseradish, 15 gr (3 kvint) ground pepper, 15 gr (3 kvint) ground allspice, 10 chopped anchovies, and a very little cayenne pepper and celery seed.
2. Stopper the barrel well and shake it daily for a period of 14 days.
3. Strain the sauce, pour into bottles and stopper.
4. A few spoonfuls of this is an excellent addition to sauce.

title: Engelske Lakskoteletter (English Salmon Cutlets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Raw salmon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg
- Bread
- Butter
- Chutney
- English fish sauce
- Lemon juice
- Cooked potatoes
- Parsley
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Clean the raw salmon and split it from the back, remove the bone
2. Cut into slices of 2½ centimeters (1 inch) thickness
3. Beat the slices very lightly and remove the skin
4. Sprinkle the cutlets with salt and pepper, dip in egg and bread
5. Fry on the pan in browned butter
6. Serve with browned butter mixed with chutney, English fish sauce or simply lemon juice, accompanied by cooked potatoes
7. Garnish the dish with parsley and lemon slices

title: Engelske Stakys (English Salsify)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- English salsify roots
- Water
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. The roots are about the thickness and length of a small finger and very ridged
2. Scrub the thin skin off as much as possible with a brush before cooking
3. Cook in salted water
4. Arrange as steaming hot as possible, laid in a folded napkin
5. Serve with melted butter

title: Escalloped Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Bread or cracker crumbs
- 1/2 cup cream or milk
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- 1 quart oysters with liquid

## Directions

1. Butter the bottom of a baking dish and spread a layer of bread or cracker crumbs over it.
2. Pour 1/2 cup cream or milk over this, spread with a tablespoon.
3. Add salt, pepper, and butter; then add 1 quart of oysters with their liquid.
4. Sprinkle with a little pepper and butter, then another layer of crumbs and butter.
5. Cover and bake for 2 or 3 hours or until the oyster liquid bubbles up.
6. Remove the lid and place the baking dish in the hottest part of the oven for a few minutes.
7. Serve in the baking dish.

title: Essens af Sellerifro og Paprika (Celery Seed and Paprika Essence)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Celery seed or paprika
- Cognac

## Directions

1. Place the spice in a small bottle and cover it with enough Cognac so that it stands above it.
2. Shake the bottle regularly, and after a few days the essence can be strained and stoppered well.
3. In this way essence can be made from most spices.
4. A few drops of celery seed essence make a good effect in soup and sauce and can often replace celery.
5. Paprika (Hungarian pepper), prepared as an essence, is a spice which is now used much more than cayenne pepper. It is easy to have it in liquid form, as only a small amount need be used.

title: Esser
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) flour
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) icing sugar
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) margarine
- 2 eggs
- 1 small teaspoon baking soda, mixed in 1 1/4 dl (1/4 pint) water

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together into a dough
2. Pipe and shape into S's or sticks

title: Esser (Letter S Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound wheat flour
- 4½ pounds butter
- ½ pound powdered sugar
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix wheat flour, butter, powdered sugar, and eggs together
2. Form into small tea cakes shaped like an S using a pastry bag
3. Bake brown at strong heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Estragon-Eddike (Tarragon Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh tarragon leaves
- good grape vinegar

## Directions

1. Dry fresh tarragon leaves in the shade and then place them in a bottle, which is filled with good grape vinegar.
2. Store the bottle in a warm place and shake it daily.
3. Let it stand for one month's time, then strain the vinegar through a fine cloth, pour into a bottle, and stopper it.
4. It is then ready for use. Fresh vinegar can again be poured over the leaves.

title: Estragon-Eddike (Tarragon Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters finest grape vinegar
- tarragon leaves dried in shade
- lemon peel
- 5 husks Spanish pepper

## Directions

1. Use the peel of 0.5 lemon and 5 husks of Spanish pepper.
2. Pour the vinegar over the leaves and let stand for 1 month.
3. Strain through a tight cloth.

title: Faare-Frikassee (Mutton Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton, cut into pieces
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth
- Salt
- Pepper
- Green peas
- White root vegetables
- Small potatoes
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare as for calf fricassee, but as the meat is stronger, more water can be added.
2. Use green peas, white root vegetables, small potatoes and chopped parsley.

title: Faarekjodsuppe (Mutton Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton
- Water
- One quart less water than for beef broth

## Directions

1. Use one quart less water than for beef broth. Prepare following the general method for meat broths.

title: Faarekjöd med Cottin (Mutton or Lamb with Cotting)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fat mutton or lamb meat
- Butter
- Flour
- Nutmeg
- Cayenne
- Cotting (a type of coating)

## Directions

1. Cook the fat mutton or lamb meat first, but not too thoroughly, and let it cool.
2. Prepare a sauce with a roux of butter and flour, slightly thinner than for fricassee, seasoned with nutmeg and cayenne; let this sauce also cool.
3. Arrange the pieces tightly together in a deep dish so that the upper side is reasonably flat without protruding bones.
4. Pour the sauce over the meat.
5. Spread the cotting over to a finger's thickness.
6. Make a hole in the center to allow steam to escape.
7. Brush the whole with beaten egg.
8. Place paper over once the crust is golden; it must not become brown.
9. Keep more sauce in the dish than goes on the meat, as it reduces considerably.

title: Faarekjöd med Cottin (Mutton with Cotting)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fat mutton or lamb, cut into pieces
- Butter
- Flour
- Nutmeg
- Cayenne pepper
- Cotting (breadcrumb coating)
- Eggs, beaten
- Paper for covering

## Directions

1. Fat mutton or lamb meat can be used with cotting; cook it first, but not too much, and let it cool.
2. The sauce is made with a roux of butter and flour, slightly thinner than for fricassee, and with nutmeg and cayenne, and should also be cold.
3. Layer the pieces tightly together in a deep platter so the upper side is fairly smooth without protruding bones.
4. Pour the sauce over and spread cotting about a finger's thickness over it.
5. Make a hole in the middle so steam can escape.
6. Cover the whole with beaten egg.
7. Place paper over when the crust is golden; it must not become brown.

title: Falske Makroner (False Macaroons)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 5 ounces bitter almonds
- 2 pounds wheat flour
- 1½ pounds powdered sugar
- 4 whole eggs
- lemon zest, grated
- Ceylon cinnamon, ground

## Directions

1. Scald the almonds and grind them fine
2. Mix together with 2 pounds wheat flour, 1½ pounds powdered sugar, 4 whole eggs, grated lemon zest, and ground Ceylon cinnamon
3. Roll the dough into small balls
4. Press them flat as they are placed on the pan
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Farcerede Løg (Stuffed Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large yellow onions
- Meat forcemeat
- Thin cloth
- Water
- Butter
- Flour
- Black pepper
- Cucumbers or gherkins
- Bouillon, broth or soy sauce
- Meatballs (optional)

## Directions

1. When large yellow onions are peeled, cut a lid from them, hollow them out, fill with meat forcemeat, place the lids on and bind them in a piece of thin cloth
2. Place them in boiling water and cook until they are tender, then remove them and untie the binding
3. Afterwards make a brown sauce from browned butter, in which mix flour; pepper and cucumbers or gherkins, which is thinned with bouillon, broth or soy sauce
4. Give the onions a boil in it or keep them warm on the platter and pour the sauce over them
5. If you do not have enough onions, you can cook some meatballs in the water together with the onions and place them in the middle of the platter with the onions around them

title: Farcerede Sellerier (Stuffed Celery)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large celery
- Meat forcemeat
