- Water
- Butter
- Flour
- Nutmeg or curry
- Salt
- Black pepper
- Egg yolk (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare large celery in the same way as stuffed onions, but cook them in a little less water, because this shall be used for the sauce
2. Remove them when they are tender, melt a little butter, bake flour in it, thin it with some of the water in which the celery is cooked
3. Warm these in the sauce, or, if they are warm, pour the sauce over them
4. Add a little ground nutmeg or curry and salt
5. The sauce can be thickened with an egg yolk, and a little pepper can be added to it
6. The part that was hollowed out can be used to help make celery soup or for stewing

title: Farcerede Tomatos (Stuffed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized tomatoes
- Bread
- Melted butter
- Salt
- Sugar
- Paper

## Directions

1. Wash medium-sized tomatoes, cut a slice from the top of each, and hollow out this and the tomato with a teaspoon
2. Mince the cut-out portions finely and mix with an equally large quantity of wheat bread (which has been soaked in water or meat broth), a little melted butter, salt and a little fine sugar
3. Fill the tomato with the mixture and bake, covered with a little paper, in a hot oven

title: Farceret Hvidkaal (Stuffed Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage head
- Meat forcemeat
- Water
- Salt
- Large cabbage leaves
- Fine twine or thin cloth
- Butter
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. When the outermost leaves are peeled from the cabbage head, cut a lid from one side where the stalk is
2. Hollow out the cabbage head and fill with meat forcemeat, place the lid on, over it a couple of the large leaves, and bind it with fine twine or a piece of thin cloth and place in boiling salted water, in which it cooks 1½ to 2 hours depending on the size of the head
3. Serve with brown butter mixed with nutmeg
4. The cabbage that was hollowed out can be cooked in the water and minced for soaked cabbage or stewed

title: Faresteg og Sammesteg (Roasted Mutton and Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton or lamb roast
- Parsley
- Butter
- Bread dough (made from French bread, milk, butter, eggs, sugar, salt)
- Flour

## Directions

1. There is a skin on the sides of the mutton roast which must be removed before roasting.
2. The roast is washed, after which many lard it with parsley, which is done by making holes with a knife, one beside the other down the back of the leg of the roast, and pressing picked and washed parsley together with a little butter into each hole.
3. The roast can be filled with parsley and butter or with bread dough, which is made as follows: some slices of French bread are dipped in milk, left standing for about an hour, and then pounded into pieces with a spoon. A piece of cold butter is stirred until creamy, into which the bread is stirred, along with 1 or 2 eggs, a little sugar and salt.
4. When well mixed, it is filled into the roast, after this is opened from one side, so that a hand can be gotten in and with this the skin can be loosened as much as possible on the roast.
5. If there are holes in the roast they are sewn together, and when the dough is in the roast, it is sewn shut.
6. The roast is dusted with flour and roasted in a good hour.
7. Lamb is treated in all respects as mutton roast.

title: Farina-Grød (Farina Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 quarts milk
- 1 ordinary cup farina
- salt
- butter
- sugar
- milk or juice and water (as desired)

## Directions

1. When the milk boils, sprinkle the grains in; stir evenly, and it needs only very brief cooking
2. Stir in salt afterward
3. Eat it with butter and sugar along with milk or juice and water as desired
4. Without butter and juice, it is also an excellent dish for small children, as is farina gruel, for which fewer grains are needed

title: Fars af Hønsekjød (Minced Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken breast
- butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 shallot
- 2 spoonfuls wheat flour
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Chop the breast meat very finely and pound in a mortar together with butter, eggs, shallot, wheat flour, and salt and pepper to taste until it becomes appropriately cohesive and spongy
2. Stir in a little sweet cream, a little at a time
3. Form small balls to be poached in soup
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fars af Kalve- eller Lammekjød (Minced Veal or Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal or lamb from the leg or best shank
- beef fat
- salt
- pepper
- shallot
- wheat flour
- eggs
- milk or cream

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as described for Fars af Oxekjød
2. The meat is taken from the leg or the best shank and beaten strongly with a mallet before scraping
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fars af Oxekjød (Minced Beef Mixture)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound beef, preferably from the inner part, cut into strips
- a little over 1/4 pound beef fat (30 quints), picked apart and without membrane
- salt to taste
- ground pepper to taste
- 1 shallot
- 3 tablespoons wheat flour
- 1 pail sweet milk or cream
- ground spices such as nutmeg, ginger, allspice or cloves to taste
- 2 eggs or egg whites (optional if cream is used)
- marrow cut into small cubes (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into strips and scrape along the sinews to remove them before chopping (or omit if using a meat grinder)
2. During chopping, blend the beef with the fat
3. When the beef and fat are well worked together and chopped fine enough to hold together, chop in salt, pepper, shallot, and wheat flour
4. Allow the mixture to rest for several hours or longer before use
5. After resting, work it with a spoon and gradually add, at long intervals, a good pail of sweet milk or cream
6. Blend in ground spices such as nutmeg, ginger, allspice or cloves to taste
7. Stir the mixture strongly and continuously, making it more cohesive by adding a couple of eggs or egg whites if cream was not used
8. If desired, the mixture may rest for a few hours and then be stirred with a little more milk to make it firmer
9. If marrow is available, cut some into small cubes and blend these with the mixture
10. Test the consistency by forming one ball and cooking it in soup
11. To form balls: take a larger portion of dough on a large spoon, and using a small spoon dipped in soup between each time, form the balls and place them in the soup
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Farserede Aborrer (Stuffed Perch)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Perch
- Parsley
- Farce

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the fish, cut it open along the belly, and remove the backbone
2. Spread the fish out and coat it with a layer of farce made from smaller perches
3. Finely chopped parsley may be mixed into the farce
4. Sew the belly together and cook the fish in salted water
5. Arrange garnished with parsley, and remove the threads with which they were sewn
6. Serve with sauce hollandaise and boiled potatoes

title: Farserede Agurker (Stuffed Cucumbers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh medium-sized cucumbers
- Ground meat filling
- Maggis bouillon cubes
- Browned butter
- Flour
- Food coloring
- Paprika
- Wine

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, split them, and scrape out the cores with a spoon
2. Fill the cucumbers with the ground meat filling and tie them together
3. Cook them in stock or water with Maggis bouillon cubes
4. Make a sauce from browned butter, flour, the cooking stock, food coloring, and paprika
5. Add wine when the sauce is finished and pour it over the cucumbers at serving
6. The stuffed cucumbers may be sliced and used as garnish around a roast

title: Farserede Artiskokker (Stuffed Artichokes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Salt
- Lemon juice
- Chicken or veal ground meat
- Mushrooms, chopped
- Parsley
- Breadcrumbs
- Melted butter
- Strong stock with Madeira
- Butter-coated paper
- Arrowroot flour

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves from the artichokes and cut level with the base of the flower head
2. Clean and rinse them, then cook in salted water with a little lemon juice for about an hour so they do not darken
3. Remove and place on cloth to drain
4. Scrape away the fuzzy choke with a spoon handle
5. Mix chopped mushrooms and parsley into good chicken or veal ground meat
6. Fill the artichokes with this mixture, mounding it high in the center
7. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs, drizzle with melted butter, and place in the oven in a roasting pan
8. During cooking, drizzle frequently with good strong stock mixed with Madeira
9. Cover with butter-coated paper and bake for about 15 minutes
10. Strain the stock, skim it, add more food coloring if needed, and thicken with arrowroot flour
11. Arrange the artichokes on a folded napkin on a platter and serve the sauce separately

title: Farserede Kogte Æg (Stuffed Boiled Eggs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- forcemeat, preferably of veal or pork
- beaten egg
- breadcrumbs
- Palmin
- brown gravy sauce
- lettuce head
- small roasted sausages

## Directions

1. Cover hard-boiled eggs with forcemeat
2. Smooth them, roll in beaten egg and breadcrumbs
3. Fry in Palmin for a few minutes until the forcemeat is golden brown
4. Cut the hot eggs once lengthwise and arrange them with the cut side up in a circle on a hot platter
5. Place a rinsed and well-dried lettuce head in the center of the platter
6. Serve with a piquant brown gravy sauce
7. Small roasted sausages can be placed around the eggs

title: Farserede Porrer (Stuffed Leeks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked leek pieces
- forcemeat
- breadcrumbs or flour
- egg
- butter or Palmin
- browned butter
- small whole cooked carrots or browned potatoes

## Directions

1. Wrap each cut cooked leek piece in forcemeat
2. Sprinkle a cutting board with breadcrumbs or flour, place spoonfuls of forcemeat on it, flatten it, and place a cooked leek on each
3. Gather the forcemeat around the leek, roll in bread, dip in egg, then in bread again
4. Fry in a pan with butter or Palmin
5. Serve with browned butter
6. Garnish the stuffed leeks with small whole cooked carrots or browned potatoes

title: Farserede Sellerier (Stuffed Celery)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large celery stalks (serves 12 people)
- forcemeat
- water
- salt
- butter
- flour
- broth
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- white pepper
- wine
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Peel large celery stalks and boil half tender in salted water
2. Cut a lid from the top of each celery stalk and hollow it out
3. Fill with forcemeat and bind the lids on, placing them upside down in the pot
4. Cook again in the celery water, but only as much as needed for the sauce
5. Make the sauce from butter and flour, thin with the broth into a white sauce, add Maggi bouillon cubes, white pepper, and wine if needed, and thicken with 2 to 3 egg yolks
6. Preferably cook the celery in a thin broth, though this can be omitted
7. Remove the lids and arrange the celery on a napkin on the platter
8. Place small meat balls in the spaces between
9. Garnish the top with celery leaves, parsley, or salad
10. The mass hollowed from the celery can be used for celery soup

title: Farserede Spanske Løg (Stuffed Spanish Onions)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 to 8 large Spanish onions (serves 10 people)
- forcemeat
- broth or water
- salt
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- browned butter
- flour
- paprika
- lemon juice
- coloring

## Directions

1. Peel large Spanish onions and cut off the top
2. Hollow them out and fill with forcemeat
3. Place the top back on and bind with string
4. Cook until tender in broth or water with salt and Maggi bouillon cubes, using only enough liquid for the sauce
5. Make the sauce from browned butter, flour, the broth, paprika, lemon juice, and coloring

title: Farserede Tomater (Stuffed Tomatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- large red tomatoes of uniform size
- fish forcemeat
- fish stock made from trimmings
- butter
- flour
- salad or parsley

## Directions

1. Cut a cap from the top of each tomato.
2. Hollow out the tomatoes and fill with fish forcemeat, leaving space for it to rise during cooking.
3. Replace the cap.
4. Place the tomatoes in a shallow casserole with boiling water.
5. Cook until the forcemeat is thoroughly cooked.
6. Cook the hollowed-out tomato flesh in fish stock made from the forcemeat trimmings.
7. Strain the stock and use to thin the sauce made from butter and flour.
8. Garnish with salad or parsley.

title: Farseret Blomkål (Stuffed Cauliflower)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 medium cauliflower heads
- 1/2 kg (3 lb) prepared ground meat
- Clear cloth
- Sailcloth band
- Salt
- Parsley
- Stirred butter

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the cauliflower
2. Place a cloth and cover it with a thin layer of ground meat
3. Place the cauliflower head down against the meat and tie the cloth at the bottom of the stalk with a sailcloth band
4. Place in boiling salted water and cook, usually about 3 quarters of an hour until tender
5. Arrange in a pyramid, garnish with parsley or salt, and serve with stirred butter
6. The cauliflower may be boiled for a few minutes before coating with meat, which then requires less cooking
7. Cauliflower may be prepared the same way with fish ground meat and served with lobster, crab, or shrimp sauce

title: Farseret Fasan (Stuffed Pheasant)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant
- Fresh bacon for larding
- Veal forcemeat
- Truffles, chopped
- Sauce as for roasted pheasant

## Directions

1. Bone the pheasant as directed for turkey
2. Lard the pheasant and fill with a good veal forcemeat to which chopped truffles may be added
3. Sew it closed so that none of the forcemeat comes out during roasting
4. Preferably roast in the oven, basting with a sauce prepared as for roasted pheasant
5. If there are truffles in the forcemeat, small pieces may be added to the sauce

title: Farseret Gaas (Stuffed Goose)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose
- veal or pork forcemeat, neither too lean nor too fat

## Directions

1. Make a cut along the middle of the back in the goose's skin.
2. Remove the carcass as instructed for turkey.
3. Spread the meat and cover it with good veal or pork forcemeat that is neither too lean nor too fat.
4. Sew the goose closed and roast or boil it.
5. If boiled, serve with Mushroom Sauce or Tomato Sauce.
6. If roasted, prepare the sauce with mushrooms, truffles, olives, chestnuts, and pickles.

title: Farseret Gedde (Stuffed Pike)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized pike
- Salt
- Fish farce
- Parsley
- Small boiled potatoes
- Melted butter
- Grated horseradish
- Sauce hollandaise
- Mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Cut open the pike along the belly, remove the entrails, clean and rinse well
2. Cut out the backbone with a sharp knife, leaving the head and tail intact
3. Rub the inside with salt and coat with a layer of fish farce
4. Sew the belly together and cook as described in previous pike recipes
5. Remove the band when serving and garnish with parsley and small boiled potatoes
6. Serve with either melted butter mixed with grated horseradish, sauce hollandaise, or mushroom sauce

title: Faste-Kringler (Lenten Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 large tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1 pound flour
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Whip 6 eggs in a quarter hour with two large tablespoons powdered sugar.
2. Stir as much of 1 pound flour as it can take into it, and then knead it on a baking board with the rest, but hold back as much as is needed to dust the board with during kneading.
3. The dough must be worked well and kneaded so long that it completely releases from hands and board, then cut it into small pieces, approximately 18 to 20, which are equal in size.
4. Roll these with the hands, but without flour on the board, into sausages of a finger's thickness, shape them into rings or wreaths, place them in a pot with plenty of gently and strongly boiling water and boil until they rise, then remove them and bake at once very slowly until quite light.
5. Do not put so many rings in the water at once that they come to lie on top of one another.
6. If the rings do not quickly rise, they have settled on the bottom and must then be carefully loosened with a spoon.

title: Fastekringler (Lenten Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g margarine
- 1 kg flour
- a little salt and sugar
- 2 eggs
- 40 g yeast, dissolved in 1/2 L half-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Crumble the margarine into the flour
2. Add salt, sugar, eggs, and yeast dissolved in milk
3. Form the dough into elongated rings and place on a floured baking board
4. Let them rise
5. When risen, place them in a pot of boiling water
6. Remove with a slotted spoon as soon as they float to the surface
7. Place on buttered baking sheets
8. Brush with egg and bake for a quarter hour at good heat
9. Split and serve spread with butter

title: Fastelavnsboller (Carnival Buns)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 875 Gr (1 1/4 S) cornstarch flour
- 30 Gr (6 Kvint) powdered sugar
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) sultana raisins
- 75 Gr (15 Kvint) finely chopped candied citrus peel
- a little cardamom
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) lukewarm milk in which 40 Gr (8 Kvint) yeast is dissolved

## Directions

1. Mix flour, powdered sugar, sultana raisins, finely chopped candied citrus peel, a little cardamom and lukewarm milk in which yeast is dissolved
2. Roll out the dough as for Danish pastry and layer with 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) margarine
3. Fold together, roll out and form into buns
4. Brush with egg and let rise
5. Place in a very hot oven to bake

title: Feeder-Kage (Feather Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- ½ cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- a couple of grains of salt

## Directions

1. Sift the powder as always with the flour

title: Fersk Oksetunge i Kød-Rand (Fresh Beef Tongue in Meat Ring)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tongue
- Brown caper sauce or other sauce
- Veal or lean pork for the filling
- Meat ring (No. 215)
- Butter pastry ring (No. 34, optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the beef tongue and cut into slices
2. Prepare with one of the above-mentioned sauces
3. Arrange in a meat ring (see No. 215) with filling made from veal or lean pork
4. Can be further decorated with a butter pastry ring around the meat ring (see No. 34)

title: Fersk Oksetunge med brun Kapers-Sauce (Fresh Beef Tongue with Brown Caper Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tongue slices
- Brown caper sauce (No. 763)

## Directions

1. Arrange the tongue slices in a brown caper sauce (see No. 763)
2. Bring just to a boil in the sauce

title: Fersk Oksetunge med friske Agurker (Fresh Beef Tongue with Fresh Cucumbers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked beef tongue
- Brown sauce
- Paprika
- Glass of Sherry
- Fresh cucumbers, peeled and hollowed

## Directions

1. Slice the cooked beef tongue and arrange it together again in its original form
2. In a brown sauce base, add paprika, a glass of Sherry and thin slices of fresh peeled and hollowed cucumbers
3. Bring to a boil in the sauce

title: Fersk Oksetunge med Madeira-Sauce (Fresh Beef Tongue with Madeira Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Good brown sauce made from butter, flour, and stock or tongue soup
- Paprika
- Coloring
- Madeira
- Butter pastry ring (No. 34)
- Truffles (optional)
- Mushrooms (optional)

## Directions

1. Make a good brown sauce from butter, flour, stock or the tongue soup, paprika, coloring and Madeira
2. Pour the sauce over the sliced tongue
3. Garnish with butter pastry ring (see No. 34)
4. Truffles can be added to the sauce, and mushrooms also pair well with Madeira sauce

title: Fersk Oksetunge med Oliven (Fresh Beef Tongue with Olives)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh beef tongue
- Water
- Olives
- Butter
- Flour
- Coloring
- Tongue soup
- Madeira
- Maggi bouillon cubes (if needed)
- Roasted chestnuts (optional)
- Butter pastry strips (No. 34, optional)

## Directions

1. Place fresh beef tongue in boiling water
2. Skim and cook until tender and the skin comes off
3. Remove the root and cut the tongue into fairly thick slices
4. Lard one side of each slice with olives that have been peeled lengthwise and cut into strips, inserting them into the cooked slices
5. Make a good brown sauce from butter, flour, coloring and tongue soup, add Madeira and peeled olives
6. Strengthen the sauce with Maggi bouillon cubes if needed
7. Place the tongue slices in the sauce with the larded side up and bring to a boil
8. Arrange in a ring on the platter and pour the sauce over
9. Can be served with roasted chestnuts and butter pastry strips

title: Fersk paneret Oksetunge (Fresh Breaded Beef Tongue)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- One cooked tongue, skin removed
- Bread crumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from a cooked tongue and sprinkle with bread crumbs, then pour melted butter over it.
2. Place it in a hot oven to brown.
3. Slice the tongue into slices, then reassemble them so it lies whole.
4. Arrange and garnish either with endive, lettuce, or parsley.
5. Serve with one of the following sauces: Sauce piquante, Sauce à la diable, Tomato Sauce, or Brown Mushroom Sauce.

title: Fersk paneret Oksetunge (Fresh Breaded Beef Tongue)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked tongue, skin removed
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Endive, salad or parsley for garnish
- Sauce piquante (No. 768) or Sauce à la diable (No. 760) or Tomato sauce (No. 749) or Brown mushroom sauce (No. 758)

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the cooked, skinned tongue with breadcrumbs and pour melted butter over it
2. Place in a warm oven to brown
3. Cut the tongue into slices and reassemble so it lies whole
4. Arrange and garnish with endive, salad or parsley
5. Serve with one of the suggested sauces: sauce piquante, sauce à la diable, tomato sauce, or brown mushroom sauce

title: Ferske farserede Kalvetunger (Fresh Stuffed Calf Tongues)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked calf tongues, skin removed
- Veal or pork forcemeat
- Bread crumbs
- Butter, melted
- Broth or thin soup
- Sharp, brown sauce made from the cooking broth
- Optional: mushrooms, pickles, capers, or chutney

## Directions

1. Split the cooked, skinned tongues lengthwise so they remain joined together.
2. Spread a layer of forcemeat, 1 to 2 centimeters (1 inch) thick, on the split tongues.
3. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and drip with melted butter.
4. Place the tongues carefully in a roasting pan, the bottom of which is covered with broth or thin soup to prevent sticking.
5. Set in a hot oven until browned on top and the forcemeat is cooked.
6. Serve with a sharp, brown sauce made from the cooking broth, which may be seasoned with mushrooms, pickles, capers, or chutney.

title: Ferske Kalvetunger med Asparges-Sauce (Fresh Calf Tongues with Asparagus Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked, split calf tongues
- 1½ kg (1 S) asparagus
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Tongue broth
- 2 to 3 egg yolks
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Peel the asparagus, cut it into small pieces, and cook in salted water.
2. Brown butter and flour together, then thin with some of the tongue broth to make a sauce of proper consistency.
3. Bind the sauce with 2 to 3 egg yolks.
4. Arrange the cooked, split tongues in a circle on the plate, with parsley for garnish in the center.
5. Pour the asparagus sauce over the tongues.

title: Ferske Kalvetunger med Gemyse (Fresh Calf Tongues with Vegetables)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf tongues
- Bread crumbs
- Eggs
- Butter
- Vegetables
- Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the tongues as for Fresh Calf Tongues with Mayonnaise.
2. In the center of the plate on which the tongues are arranged, place the vegetables.
3. The vegetables may either be stewed or eaten with stirred butter.
4. If vegetables are stewed, serve browned butter with the tongues.

title: Ferske Kalvetunger med Madeira-Sauce (Fresh Calf Tongues with Madeira Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf tongues
- Bread crumbs
- Butter or claret
- Lemons
- Madeira broth, slightly thickened
- Compote of dried or fresh fruit
- Lettuce or parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the calf tongues, split them, bread them, and brown in butter or claret.
2. Place a star of lemons, cut into four pieces, in the center of the plate; the lemon juice is eaten with the meat.
3. Serve with slightly thickened Madeira broth.
4. Serve with compote of dried or fresh fruit.
5. Garnish the dish with lettuce or parsley.

title: Ferske Kalvetunger med Mayonnaise (Fresh Calf Tongues with Mayonnaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 calf tongues
- Water
- Salt
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Butter or claret
- Mayonnaise sauce
- Chopped anchovies
- Stirred mustard
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the calf tongues and cook them tender in water and salt.
2. Remove the skin.
3. Split the tongues lengthwise into 2 to 3 pieces.
4. Dip in egg and bread, then fry in butter or cook in claret.
5. Serve the tongues garnished with parsley.
6. Serve Mayonnaise sauce on the side, with chopped anchovies and stirred mustard added to it.

title: Ferske spækkede Kalvetunger (Fresh Larded Calf Tongues)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf tongues
- Water
- Salt
- Larding bacon
- Butter
- Broth, soup, or water
- Maggis Bouillon cubes
- Food coloring
- Madeira
- Tapioca flour
- Water
- Optional: mushrooms, pearl onions, shallots, or lemon slices
- Bread croutons or sliced bread

## Directions

1. Boil the tongues in water just enough so the skin can be removed.
2. Lard the tongues and brown in butter.
3. Pour over as much broth, soup, or water with Maggis Bouillon cubes as needed for the sauce.
4. Give the sauce a nice color with food coloring.
5. Let the tongues simmer gently and baste frequently until they are tender.
6. If desired, add Madeira to the sauce.
7. Thicken the sauce with tapioca flour mixed in water.
8. To make it more piquant, add mushrooms, pearl onions, shallots, or lemon slices.
9. When serving, pour some of the sauce over the tongues and garnish with bread croutons or sliced bread.

title: Fersken-Kompot (Peach Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1.25 dl water for 0.5 kg fruit

## Directions

1. Place the peaches in boiling water so the skin comes off more easily.
2. Split them and remove the stones.
3. Treat the fruit and kernel as described for apricots.

title: Fersken-Kompot (Peach Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- dried peaches
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak the dried fruit in water for 2 days
2. Rinse the dried fruit well in several changes of lukewarm water before soaking
3. Cook the fruit in the water in which it has been soaked with lemon juice
4. Add the sugar only after the fruit is cooked and removed, allowing the sugar and juice to boil together
5. Pour the boiling syrup over the fruit

title: Ferskener med Cognac (Peaches with Cognac)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) peaches
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) powdered loaf sugar
- 1.5 dl (1/3 pint) water
- Cognac

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water into a fairly smooth syrup in which the whole peaches boil for 3 minutes and are then placed in glass jars or crocks, which are left covered for 3 to 4 days.
2. After this time has elapsed, pour off the syrup, reduce it until it is very smooth, and pour it over the peaches.
3. The jars must not be filled completely, as after the fruit has cooled, a generous amount of good Cognac must be added before the jars are sealed.

title: Figaro (for 12 Persons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg Bavarian sausages
- Boiled cold potatoes
- Butter
- Flour
- Sausage cooking water
- 5 to 6 large onions
- Salt
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Pepper
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Rinse the sausages well and place them in boiling water to cook for 4 to 5 minutes.
2. Make a roux from butter and flour, then thin it with the water in which the sausages were cooked until it becomes a regular smooth white sauce.
3. Chop the onions coarsely and cook them tender in the sauce.
4. On a slightly deep plate, layer slices of boiled potatoes, then slices of the cooked sausages, then another layer of potato slices.
5. Season the sauce with salt, Maggi Bouillon cubes, and pepper, and pour it over generously, as some will be absorbed by the potatoes.
6. Sprinkle the dish with bread crumbs and place it in a warm oven to heat through and brown slightly on top.
7. If no warm oven is available, cut the potatoes and sausages while warm and pour the hot sauce over them.
8. Serve as soon as it is arranged. Figaro can be served as a lunch or dinner course.

title: Fildender på Skandinavisk (Wild Ducks Scandinavian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild ducks
- Butter
- Good milk
- Cream

## Directions

1. Pluck and skin the ducks completely.
2. Roast in butter for one hour, browning the butter.
3. Add enough good milk so that it comes halfway up the duck, basting constantly.
4. Add cream to the sauce before serving.

title: Fin Melkesuppe, varm eller kold (Fine Milk Soup, Warm or Cold)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- 1 small dessert spoon corn starch or rice flour
- 2 egg yolks
- white sugar
- salt
- a couple of drops of vanilla (or a couple of pieces of whole cinnamon)
- soft poached egg whites (optional)

## Directions

1. Mix the corn starch or rice flour with some of the cold milk
2. Heat the rest of the milk on the fire, and when it is hot, stir in the flour and cook the milk until it is smooth
3. Stir the egg yolks with white sugar, thin them with warm milk, and pour into the pot and stir until it begins to cook
4. Then pour it into a tureen with a little salt and a couple of drops of vanilla added (or cook a couple of pieces of whole cinnamon in the milk instead of vanilla)
5. Soft poached egg whites may be laid on top in the tureen; the milk in which they cook should contain sugar and a little cinnamon, which milk is then strained and added to the rest of the milk
6. Serve the soup with small rusks or baked bread, and one generally counts one quart of milk for 3 people

title: Fin Pinje-Gelee af Kalvefødder (Fine Paw Jelly from Calf's Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large calf's feet
- 12 quarts wine
- 3 pounds sugar
- a small piece of broken cinnamon
- juice of 12 lemons
- yellow peel of 3 lemons
- 6-8 lightly beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the 12 calf's feet according to the instructions given above.
2. Add 12 quarts of wine, 3 pounds of sugar, a small piece of cinnamon broken into pieces, the juice of 12 and the yellow peel of 3 lemons, and 6-8 lightly beaten egg whites.
3. Can be eaten from small glasses, or placed in molds that are first rinsed, and turned out.

title: Fin-Kjød eller Fiske-Fars (Fine Meat or Fish Farce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound scraped meat or fish
- 15 quints marrow (for meat fars) or 15 quints butter (for fish fars)
- 4 beaten egg whites
- 2 pails sweet cream

## Directions

1. Work the meat or fish very strongly, chop it and pass through a fine colander together with marrow (for meat) or butter (for fish)
2. Stir the farce with beaten egg whites and sweet cream
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fine engelske Pickles (Fine English Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Quart Vinegar
- 2 Pounds Mustard Seeds
- 1 Pound Long Pepper
- 1 Pound Short Pepper
- 1 Quart Wine Vinegar
- 1 small bottle Cayenne Pepper
- 6 pieces Horseradish sliced
- 2 Stalks Garlic
- Carrots cleaned and flaked
- Cabbage
- Sliced Cauliflower
- Brussels Sprouts
- Green Beans
- Small Corn
- Young Elderberry Stems
- Celery
- Slices of 2 Apples
- Cucumbers
- Small whole peeled Mushrooms
- 1-2 Bags Spanish Pepper per glass
- 1 Teaspoon whole Mustard Seeds per glass

## Directions

1. Cook all these parts and place in a large crock, let it stand covered until cooled
2. Add as many cleaned and flaked carrots as needed to color the vinegar yellow
3. Clean and polish the various vegetables such as cabbage, sliced cauliflower, Brussels sprouts, green beans, small corn, very young elderberry stems, celery, apple slices, cucumbers, small whole peeled mushrooms and the like
4. Pour boiled vinegar over them repeatedly, cooling each time, until they are soft
5. Drain on a sieve and let stand overnight
6. Place in pickle glasses and fill with the aforementioned vinegar
7. Stopper and bind with bladder
8. Cover with sealing wax
9. Add 1-2 bags of Spanish pepper and one teaspoon of whole mustard seeds to each glass

title: Fingerkage (Finger Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup granulated sugar (less than 1 tablespoon)
- 1 scant teaspoon cream of tartar
- 1/2 as much soda
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat eggs lightly and add 1 cup granulated sugar (less than 1 tablespoon).
2. Sift in a very scant teaspoon of cream of tartar and half as much soda and a little salt.
3. Sift in flour until a stiff dough is formed.
4. Roll the dough fairly thin and spread the remaining sugar over it.
5. Cut the dough into strips about the size of a finger and place on a buttered pan without touching each other.
6. Bake in a quick oven and watch that they do not burn.
7. Vanilla may be stirred into the dough.

title: Finker (Beef Ragout with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cooked or roasted beef
- half a quarter pound butter
- half as much flour
- 4 pints good stock
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- sugar
- salt
- ground pepper
- peeled apples, chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into small cubes
2. In the butter, stir in half as much flour
3. Thin with 4 pints of good stock and 1 tablespoon of vinegar
4. Add the meat, reheat thoroughly
5. Before serving, season the ragout with sugar, salt and ground pepper to taste
6. It gives a good flavor to cook some peeled, chopped apples in the stock before adding the meat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Finker (Beef Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound cooked or roasted beef
- Half a quarter pound of butter
- Flour
- Four gills good broth
- One tablespoon vinegar
- Sugar
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Apples (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut beef into small cubes.
2. In half a quarter pound of butter, mix half as much flour.
3. Thin with 4 gills of good broth and one tablespoon of vinegar.
4. Add the beef and heat through.
5. Before serving, season the finker with sugar, salt, and ground pepper to taste.
6. Optionally, cook peeled, quartered apples in the broth before adding the beef for good flavor.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Finker (Head Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat from a cooked ox head
- Ox lung
- Ox heart
- Ox liver
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Apples
- Ginger
- Whole pepper

## Directions

1. Use the meat of a cooked ox head along with lung, heart and liver of an ox.
2. These parts must be well rinsed and soak for several hours in water so the blood can be extracted, then cook in salted water and skim carefully.
3. After sufficient cooking, cut away all unusable parts; lung and heart are chopped, while liver is grated to make it easier to remove the sinews.
4. Strain the soup in which it cooked, and in it give the chopped mixture a re-boil together with the head meat cut into cubes.
5. Place in crocks and seal when cold.
6. When it is to be used, add vinegar and sugar along with sliced apples if you have them, some pieces of ginger, whole pepper and other seasonings as desired.

title: Finsk Brød (Finnish Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) margarine
- 375 grams (1/4 lb) flour
- 100 grams (20 quintal) sugar

## Directions

1. Mix together margarine, flour and sugar
2. Roll into sticks of finger thickness
3. Cut into pieces 5 centimeters (2 inches) long
4. Dip in egg, sugar and chopped almonds

title: Fint Hvedebrod paa en anden Maade (Fine Wheat Bread, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small quart milk
- 1 cent compressed yeast
- wheat flour
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 2 tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Warm 1 small quart milk and pour into the dough pan. Dissolve 1 cent compressed yeast in a little lukewarm water and add to the milk.
2. Sift in as much flour while stirring to make a thick, even dough that cannot be kneaded, only stirred.
3. Let it rise for the usual time during the day or overnight.
4. When risen, add 1 tablespoon sugar, 1 tablespoon salt, and 2 tablespoons butter.
5. Sift in enough flour to make a good, soft dough that can be handled.
6. Knead well and place in the pan, allowing it to rise again.
7. When sufficiently risen, divide into as many loaves as desired—2 large or 3 small—let stand for a quarter hour in a warm place, then bake as usual.

title: Fint Rugbrod med Kommen (Fine Rye Bread with Caraway)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cents caraway seeds
- water
- fine rye flour
- 2 cents baker's yeast
- wheat flour or rye flour

## Directions

1. The evening before baking, boil 2 cents caraway seeds in about 3 cups water until the water turns brown.
2. Pour the hot liquid over fine rye flour and stir quickly to make an even dough.
3. When cooled, stir in 2 cents baker's yeast.
4. Cover well and let stand until the next day.
5. Knead up with wheat flour (and also rye flour if desired) and set to rise again before shaping into loaves.
6. Bake with good heat.

title: Fire Species-Kranse (Four Spice Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 36 quintal sugar
- 36 quintal fine flour
- 25 quintal butter
- 2 eggs
- chopped almonds for topping

## Directions

1. Stir 36 quintal sugar, 36 quintal fine flour, 25 quintal butter, and 2 eggs into a batter
2. Form small rings with chopped almonds on top
3. Bake light brown
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fish Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Salt
- 0.5 small loaf
- Cold water
- A little pepper
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- 2 pounds fish
- Butter

## Directions

1. Boil potatoes in well-salted water.
2. Soak 0.5 small loaf in cold water and wring dry in a clean cloth.
3. Stir in a little salt, pepper, and 1 tablespoon chopped parsley.
4. Clean 2 pounds fish, cut into thin slices, and place in cold, salt water.
5. When the potatoes are sufficiently cooked, pick them, press through a colander, and season with pepper and salt.
6. Grease a pudding form on the bottom with butter and lay alternating layers of bread and fish in it.
7. On top place a neat layer of the potato mixture and bake for one hour in a not too hot oven.

title: Fish à la Crème (Fisk a la Creme)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt water fish such as cod or mackerel
- Salt
- 1 ounce flour
- 1 quart cream or good milk
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 1 bunch parsley
- 1 finely chopped onion
- Salt and pepper
- ½ pound butter
- Egg whites from 6 eggs

## Directions

1. After cleaning the fish, rub it with salt and place it whole in enough boiling water to completely cover it, then place over heat.
2. Once it boils, move it to the back of the oven where it can simmer gently for 1 hour.
3. Remove and pick out all the bones.
4. In a casserole, blend 1 ounce flour with 1 quart cream or good milk, adding it little by little.
5. Add the juice of 1 lemon, 1 bunch parsley, 1 finely chopped onion, and salt and pepper.
6. Place over heat and stir until it becomes a thick sauce, then add ½ pound butter.
7. Bring to a boil and strain.
8. Pour a little sauce into a deep dish, place the fish in it and pour the remaining sauce over.
9. Beat the whites of 6 eggs to a foam, spread over the top and bake until light brown in the oven.

title: Fisk i Gelee (Fish in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- A couple of pounds of fish
- 1½ quart water
- Salt
- 1 gill vinegar
- 3 or 4 laurel leaves
- Whole pepper
- Some cloves
- Gelatine
- A couple of egg whites
- Rind of a lemon or vinegar
- Beets (optional for coloring)

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, cut it into pieces and cook in 1½ quart water with salt, 1 gill vinegar, 3 or 4 laurel leaves, whole pepper and some cloves
2. Remove the tender pieces of fish when cooked, while other pieces cook longer for the stock
3. Strain the stock and add a little gelatine, a couple of egg whites and lemon rind or vinegar
4. Pour through a flannel cloth
5. For pike, color the aspic red by cooking beets in it; for other sorts keep it as white as possible
6. Pour a thin layer of aspic over the bottom of the dish, arrange the most beautiful fish pieces on it when set, and place the aspic between them
7. For salmon with red aspic, cut flowers from cooked egg whites; for white aspic cut flowers from beets
8. Place these decorations between the pieces or form a circle inside the bottom of the dish with these flowers and fresh parsley leaves
9. Pour more aspic over, which should not be more than lukewarm
10. When all is set, hold the dish briefly over hot water and turn out onto another dish

title: Fisk i Gelée (Fish in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon or other fish
- Cold water
- Bay leaves
- Whole peppercorns
- Isinglass (husblas)
- Egg whites, whipped stiff
- Lemon slices
- Eel or other fish as alternatives

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and cut it into appropriate slices across the grain, keeping the skin with each piece, as the skin is fatty and helps the piece hold its shape
2. Rinse the slices only once in cold water, as more rinsing will cause loss of fat
3. Bring the fish to a boil; when boiling, skim the broth and add some bay leaves and whole peppercorns; do not let it boil for too long
4. When the fish is tender, place it in a flat glass or porcelain dish
5. Cook the broth with the necessary isinglass, skim it, clarify it with whipped stiff egg whites, and strain it through a gelée sieve over the fish, laying some lemon slices among it
6. Eel and other fish may be arranged this way as well; eel must first be skinned
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fisk i Gelée (Fish in Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- water
- bay leaves
- peppercorns

## Directions

1. After the fish is cleaned, cut it into appropriate slices across, so that the skin comes with each piece, as the skin is fatty and helps the piece hold its shape
2. Rinse the slices only once in cold water so the fat is not lost
3. Set the fish to cook and when it comes to a boil, skim the broth
4. Add some bay leaves and whole peppercorns
5. Do not let it boil for long
6. Cook until the fish is tender
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fisk i Majonaise (Fish in Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole, boneless pieces of cooked, cold fish
- Mayonnaise sauce

## Directions

1. Cover whole, boneless pieces of cooked, cold fish with proper mayonnaise sauce and serve in it
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fisk-Chowder (Fish Chowder)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh fish
- Salt pork slices
- Crackers
- Red pepper
- Onions
- Water

## Directions

1. Any kind of fresh fish can be used, although many prefer cod and bass.
2. Clean in the usual way, cut into pieces 1 inch or more thick and 2 inches wide and long.
3. Fry a suitable number (for example, 8) fairly large slices of salt pork until crisp in an iron pot; remove the pork so that only the fat remains.
4. In the pot place a layer of fish, a layer of coarsely crushed crackers, some of the finely chopped pork with red pepper and chopped onions; then a new layer of fish, a new layer of crackers, etc., etc. until the pot is full.
5. Pour water over it and stew the fish until it is cooked enough; then take it up and place it in the serving dish; the sauce (the gravy) is thickened with crushed cracker, brought to a boil and poured over the chowder.

title: Fiske Salat (Fish Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1 pint cream
- Mustard
- 3 to 4 tablespoons vinegar
- White wine
- Pepper
- Salt
- Lobster or anchovy essence (optional)
- 1 large deep plate cooked, picked fish
- 1/4 pound small broken cooked macaroni

## Directions

1. Stir egg yolks in a pint of cream and cook as for cream
2. When cold, stir in mustard, 3 to 4 tablespoons vinegar, white wine, a little pepper and salt, and if desired some lobster or anchovy essence
3. In this sauce, which must be smooth, put a large deep plate of cooked, picked fish and 1/4 pound small broken cooked macaroni
4. Mix well together and should preferably stand a couple of hours before use

title: Fiske Salat med Flöde (Fish Salad with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish of various kinds
- 1 pint cream (or sour cream)
- 2 tablespoons mushrooms (or capers as alternative)
- Vinegar from the mushrooms
- A little ground nutmeg
- Salt
- 3 tablespoons oil (as alternative to cream)

## Directions

1. Pick the cooked fish from skin and bones into flat pieces
2. For a deep plate half full of picked fish, make a sauce from a pint of cream, which can be sour, stirred with 2 tablespoons of mushrooms together with the vinegar that comes with them, a little ground nutmeg and salt
3. If the sauce is not tart enough, a little vinegar can be added
4. Instead of cream, the salad can be made with 3 tablespoons oil
5. Instead of mushrooms, capers can be used

title: Fiske-Budding (Fish Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.25 kg scraped fish for forcemeat
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare the fish forcemeat as described in the fish preparation method.
2. Butter a pudding mold and dust with bread crumbs.
3. Fill with the fish forcemeat.
4. Cook as described for meat pudding, by placing in boiling water and simmering until set.
5. Serve with choice of sauce: melted butter, oyster sauce, mushroom sauce, lobster sauce, tomato sauce, caper sauce, curry sauce, chutney sauce, or shrimp and asparagus sauce.

title: Fiske-Farce (Fish Forcemeat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fish
- salt
- 2 ounces cold butter, or half butter and half marrow
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 2 eggs
- milk
- cream
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Rinse the fish and cut it open from the back
2. Rinse it again on the inside so that the fish smell does not become too strong
3. Dry it with a cloth
4. Scrape and mince 1 pound of fish first with salt, then with 2 ounces of cold butter or half butter and half marrow, 1 tablespoon of flour and two eggs
5. When it is minced well and thoroughly combined, stir it with milk, cream and nutmeg
6. If the forcemeat is not fine, it can be pressed through a fine sieve or open sieve
7. The fish can be kept until the next day by tying it at the tail so blood and slime can drain away
8. Wash it again before scraping

title: Fiske-Filet (Fish Fillet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Flatfish
- Bread crumbs dried in the oven
- Egg
- Cold water
- Fat for frying
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Fillet is made only from flatfish.
2. Wash the flatfish, dry in a clean cloth and place flat on the table.
3. Cut off the fins with a sharp, thin-bladed knife.
4. Press the knife down in the middle of the fish until it reaches the bones, then turn it with the edge toward the fins and guide it along the bones outward so that a whole quarter of the fish is loosened from the bones.
5. In the same way loosen the other quarters and place them all with skin down on the table.
6. Set one of the fingers of the left hand firmly on the end of one of the quarters and let the knife run along the skin so that the fish piece is freed from it.
7. Skin all the fillet in this way and place it on a plate.
8. The whole is a matter of skill, and what it is about is that the fillet does not fall to pieces.
9. Sprinkle bread crumbs, which have been dried in the oven, crushed fine and sifted, on the table; beat an egg with a spoonful of cold water.
10. Place the fillet on the crumbs, press lightly, turn, press again, dip in the beaten egg and take away quickly so that the crumbs are not washed off.
11. Place it again in the crumbs and press in a neat form with the knife.
12. Heat as much fat as will be sufficient to cover three or four fillets until it begins to smoke; place the fillets in one at a time, fry them light brown, take them up with a skimming ladle and place them for a moment on brown paper so that they are freed from fat.
13. Place them neatly on a plate with a few parsley leaves and serve them.

title: Fiske-Frikadeller eller Kager (Fish Fritters or Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried cod
- Water
- Boiled potatoes
- Butter
- Egg

## Directions

1. Cut the scraped and cleaned dried cod into small pieces, and place these in lukewarm water for about an hour.
2. Remove the skin and bones and pick it finely; place it in cold water and set over the heat; when it boils, change the water and let it boil again.
3. Have ready cooked, mashed and butter-stirred potatoes.
4. Stir the fish and potato mixture together while both are hot; stir in a well-beaten egg and make balls or thick cakes from the mixture, drop these in enough melted fat so they float in it, and fish them out.

title: Fiske-Gratin (Fish Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- potato flour
- wheat flour
- butter
- milk
- egg yolks
- egg whites
- cooked flaked fish
- salt
- melted butter or caper sauce

## Directions

1. Bake a quarter pound of potato flour and a quarter pound of wheat flour with half a pound of butter and 6 pails of milk
2. Stir in 20 egg yolks which have first been stirred until pale
3. Whip the egg whites to stiff peaks and add them last to the mixture along with about 3 pounds of flaked cooked fish and a little salt
4. Pour the gratin into a deep dish smeared with butter and bake for an hour in the oven
5. Serve with a napkin around the dish, and serve with melted butter or caper sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fiske-Gratin 1 (Fish Gratin 1)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Smør
- Mel
- Kogende Mælk
- 9 Æg
- Salt
- Melis
- Stødt, hvidt Peber
- 1 Tallerken kogt, pillet Fisk

## Directions

1. Brug Dejg som i Nr. 62. Sukkeret og Asparges udelades deri, men i Stedet derfor røres stødt, hvidt Peber og 1 Tallerken kogt, pillet Fisk i.
2. Fiske-Gratin kan laves af de fleste Sorter kogt, pillet Fisk.

title: Fiske-Gratin 2 (Fish Gratin 2)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Laks, Gedder, Torsk, Kuller eller Helleflynder
- Smør
- Mel
- Fiskesuppe
- Fløde
- Stødt, hvidt Peber
- Salt
- 1 Glas Graves eller Sherry
- Et Par Æggeblommer
- Brød

## Directions

1. Fisken, som bruges til denne Gratin, kan enten være Laks, Gedder, Torsk, Kuller eller Helleflynder.
2. Den kogte Fisk pilles fra Benene i små Stykker.
3. Hertil laves en jævn Sauce af Smør, Mel, Fiskesuppe, lidt Fløde, stødt, hvidt Peber, Salt og 1 Glas Graves eller Sherry; den lieres med et Par Æggeblommer.
4. Heri røres Fisken forsigtigt og kommer i Formen.
5. Gratin'en bestros med Brød og overhældes med smeltet Smør.

title: Fiske-Omelet (Fish Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound flour
- 1/4 pound butter
- 2 measures sweet milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- 2 pounds cooked fish
- oyster or mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Bake 1/4 pound flour with 1/4 pound butter
2. Thin with 2 measures sweet milk
3. When well baked, stir in 8 egg yolks
4. Finally stir in the beaten egg whites
5. Stir in 2 pounds cooked fish in large pieces
6. Bake the omelet for 1 hour, it should be light brown
7. Serve with oyster or mushroom sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fiske-Rouletter (Fish Roulettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or cod
- butter
- flour
- soup or bouillon
- capers
- mushrooms
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook pike or cod and pick it free from skin and bones. Bake butter and flour together and thin with soup, adding capers, mushrooms, pepper, and salt. When the sauce is finished, add the fish and stir carefully, but it must not boil. Then strain it to cool, and when it is cold, form roulettes from it as in No. 203. The roulettes are served hot as a side dish with mushroom or caper sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fiske-Salat (Fish Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon, pike, turbot or cod
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock from the cooked fish
- Egg yolks
- Mushrooms or mushroom vinegar
- Vinegar
- White pepper
- Sugar
- Sherry
- Cooked celery
- Watercress for garnish

## Directions

1. Make a roux from butter and flour and dilute with the stock in which the fish was cooked
2. Thicken the sauce with 2-3 egg yolks so it is like a thick porridge
3. Season with mushrooms or their vinegar, vinegar, salt, white pepper, a little sugar, and sherry to taste
4. Pick the cooked fish into fairly large pieces while still warm
5. Turn the fish gently into the prepared sauce
6. For a deep plate full of fish, take one large cooked celery, cut it into very small cubes and add it
7. The salad is best the same day it is prepared
8. It can be garnished with watercress

title: Fiske-Suppe (Fish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds fish
- 4 quarts water
- Salt
- A bundle of herbs
- Root vegetables
- A small handful of leeks
- Parsley
- 2 tablespoons flour
- Almost half a pint of sour cream
- Bread or flour dumplings
- Vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into pieces and boil in 4 quarts of water with salt, a bundle of herbs and root vegetables.
2. When these are soft, remove them, rinse them and put them back in the soup when it is strained.
3. Cook out the fish completely, crush it and strain it; pour a little hot water on it in the strainer to extract the flavor.
4. Chop a small handful of leeks and parsley finely with two tablespoons of flour, mix it in almost half a pint of sour cream, thin somewhat with the soup, which is again placed on the fire, add it when it is heated, and stir until it boils; then add bread or flour dumplings to it.
5. If you do not have sour cream, you can stir in a couple of spoonfuls of vinegar into the finished soup.

title: Fiskebudding (Fish Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Pounds Fish, preferably Pike, scraped free of skin and bone
- Half a Pound cold Butter
- 1 Pound melted Butter
- 8 Eggs
- 4 Tablespoons Wheat Flour
- Salt and Pepper to taste
- About 2 Pots sweet Cream

## Directions

1. Scrape the fish free of skin and bone, then chop it with half a pound of cold butter.
2. Place the mixture in a mortar and pound it, adding the melted butter gradually during pounding until it becomes like a tough dough and releases from the mortar.
3. Add 8 eggs one at a time, stirring constantly.
4. Add 4 tablespoons of wheat flour, salt and pepper to taste, and enough sweet cream so that the mixture becomes appropriately thick.
5. Test the consistency by placing a spoonful in boiling water; if too thin, add more flour; if too firm, add more cream.
6. Grease a pudding mold with butter and fill it with the mixture.
7. Cook for a couple of hours.
8. Serve with Caper sauce or Crayfish sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fiskebudding (Fish Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or other fish
- cold butter
- melted butter
- eggs
- wheat flour
- salt
- pepper
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Scrape 4 pounds of fish free of skin and bone, then chop it with half a pound of cold butter
2. Place it in a mortar to be pounded and thin during pounding with a pound of melted butter until it becomes like a stiff dough and releases from the mortar
3. Add 8 eggs one at a time while stirring constantly, then add 4 spoonfuls of wheat flour, salt and pepper to taste, and enough sweet cream so that the mixture becomes appropriately thick
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fiskefars (Fish Farce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound pike and 1/4 pound cod (or 3/8 pound total of one type of fish)
- 20 to 25 quints butter
- 3 small spoonfuls wheat flour
- 3 eggs
- sweet milk (approximately 3 pails, of which half can be cream)
- nutmeg to taste
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Clean and fillet the fish, remove the bones
2. Soak in cold water which is changed several times for a couple of hours
3. Carefully scrape the meat from the skin and chop fairly finely
4. Blend with butter and wheat flour, then chop finely
5. Stir the farce with eggs and sweet milk as needed
6. Add nutmeg and salt to taste
7. This makes approximately 100 small balls
8. If baked in browned butter as frikadeller, this amount will make 8 portions, served with browned butter as sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fiskefars (Fish Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg scraped fish
- 125 grams butter
- 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoons potato flour
- about 1/2 liter sweet milk
- salt
- ground white pepper

## Directions

1. Fish forcemeat can be made from pike, cod, or pollack, or half of each of the last two kinds
2. Cut the fish along the back, clean, rinse well and dry with a fish cloth
3. Scrape, chop finely and pound with the butter and flour together with salt until it becomes a tough mass
4. Then stir with quite small amounts of milk at a time
5. Use only ground white pepper as seasoning
6. By using potato flour there is considerably more forcemeat, but you can take half wheat flour and half potato flour, though the milk must then be calculated accordingly
7. It does not tolerate being as soft for balls and meatballs as for pudding and border mold
8. If the fish is cleaned the day before, bind it, hang by the tail and keep in a cool place
9. Garfish can be made into forcemeat, but then care must be taken that the dark stripe closest to the skin does not get in

title: Fiskesalat (Fish Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints cream
- 6 egg yolks
- Capers
- Chopped shallot
- Pepper
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Pieces of cold, cooked fish, freed of skin and bones

## Directions

1. Stir 2 pints cream and 6 egg yolks into a crème
2. Add capers, chopped shallot, pepper, salt, vinegar and sugar to taste
3. Place pieces of cold, cooked fish, freed of skin and bones, in this sauce
4. Alternatively, use diced cooked chicken or veal meat mixed with diced cooked celery
5. Pickled mushrooms may be used instead of capers if desired
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fiskesuppe (Fish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- cleaned soup vegetables, sliced
- chopped red onion
- parsley
- 4 pounds cod, flounder, or eel, cleaned and cut into pieces
- water
- salt
- 10 peppercorns
- 1 bay leaf

## Directions

1. Place 1/4 pound butter in a casserole together with the soup vegetables sliced clean, chopped red onion and parsley, and steam covered until the vegetables are half-tender.
2. Then add 4 pounds of well-cleaned cod, flounder, or eel cut into pieces.
3. Pour in enough water so that it just covers the fish, then boil and skim the soup.
4. Add salt, 10 peppercorns and a bay leaf, then cook the whole covered until the fish is thoroughly tender.
5. Remove the fish, strain the soup and it can be used for thinning meat soup and for fish sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fjerkræ i Mayonnaise (Poultry in Mayonnaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked poultry
- Mayonnaise
- Herb salad
- Soft or hard-cooked eggs
- Radishes
- Parsley
- Olives
- Capers
- Peas
- Carved yellow and white roots or cress
- Fruit coloring
- Gelatin
- Bordure ring

## Directions

1. When the meat is cooked and cold, cut it from the bones into small fillets (the bones can be used again for broth or sauce).
2. Dip the fillets on a fork in the mayonnaise and arrange them pyramidically on a platter.
3. Smooth the arrangement with a spoon, and use the remaining mayonnaise for herb salad, arranged as a garnish around the edge of the platter (see recipe 316).
4. The mayonnaise can also be decorated with soft or hard-cooked eggs, arranged alternately with radishes and parsley in a wreath around the platter.
5. Various other items can be used to decorate the pyramid, such as olives, capers, peas, carved yellow and white roots, or cress.
6. Mayonnaise can be served in a ring made of water, colored bright red with fruit coloring and stiffened with dissolved gelatin.
7. The ring is not eaten but provides an attractive presentation with the mayonnaise in the center.
8. A Bordure ring can also be used (see recipe 334).

title: Flageolets (Flageolets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Young beans from fully developed snap beans
- Boiling water
- Salt solution (10 gr or 2 Kvint per 1 liter)

## Directions

1. Scald the shelled beans in boiling water, pour cold water over them and fill into jars
2. Pour a cold salt solution over the beans in the jar, close and cook for 30 to 35 minutes
3. Cool the jars

title: Fleſkepandekage (Bacon Pancake)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Cream
- Butter
- Bacon
- Water
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Use, for example, 8 eggs, and to each egg take a spoonful of cream.
2. Beat the eggs first and then together with the cream.
3. Brown a little butter in a frying pan, then pour a large spoonful of the batter onto it and lay slices of bacon (which must first be soaked in water) on it.
4. Pour the batter gently over so the bacon is covered and fry the cake over gentle heat.
5. Sometimes you must insert a little butter at the edge, as you must not have too much underneath at first.
6. When it begins to stiffen on top, it is brown underneath and must be turned; it may also happen that it browns while it is still soft on top, in which case the heat is too high.
7. Slide it from the pan, preferably onto a lid with a handle; place the pan on top of the cake, on which a little butter has first been placed, and hold the lid firmly to the pan as you turn it around.
8. You can sprinkle chopped parsley on top.

title: Floating Island
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites
- preserves
- fresh cream

## Directions

1. For each egg white take a small spoonful of currant or cloudberry preserves.
2. Whisk together for a long time, even after it becomes stiff peaks, as this makes it whiter and finer in appearance.
3. From 4 whites makes a fairly large portion.
4. Pile it in a high mound on a dish into which enough raw cream has been poured so that the mound can float in it.
5. Serve with the cream in which it floats and with small cakes.
6. You can also place the whites on a dish without cream, and then serve vanilla cream alongside.

title: Flode-Sauce med Persille (Cream Sauce with Parsley)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Cream
- Chopped parsley
- A small piece of white sugar
- Salt
- Milk (optional, as substitute for cream)

## Directions

1. Melt butter and stir flour into it
2. Add cream gradually until the sauce is smooth
3. Add chopped parsley and bring to a boil
4. Add a small piece of white sugar and salt to taste
5. If using milk instead of cream, use more butter

title: Flodelapper (Cream Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint sour Cream
- 12 Tablespoons Flour
- 4 Tablespoons light Powdered Sugar

## Directions

1. Whip 1 Pint sour Cream well and stir with 12 Tablespoons Flour and 4 Tablespoons light Powdered Sugar
2. Set on a hot pan with a Spoon in small dollops and bake until light brown
3. If the Cream is good, Butter on the pan is needed only for the first cakes

title: Floielsgröd (Velvet Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- somewhat less butter than in Smörgröd
- wheat flour
- boiling milk

## Directions

1. Make in the same way as Smörgröd, but use somewhat less butter and boiling milk instead of water

title: Florentinerkage (Florentine Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarter-pounds cleaned rice grains (No. 6)
- 1 can sweet milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 4 pounds powdered sugar
- 1 quarter-pound finely ground sweet almonds
- 10 quintals bitter almonds, finely ground
- 1 pound clarified butter
- 8 egg whites, beaten
- apples, peeled and cored
- jam
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil 3 quarter-pounds cleaned rice grains in 1 can sweet milk.
2. When they are cold, stir in 8 egg yolks one by one, then 4 pounds powdered sugar, 1 quarter-pound finely ground sweet and 10 quintals bitter almonds, 1 pound clarified butter and finally the 8 beaten egg whites.
3. Make a compote from apples that are peeled and cored removed.
4. Layer in the form: first a layer of the gruel, then jam, then apple compote, then gruel again and so on until the form is full.
5. Sprinkle the cake with grated bread and bake for 1 hour.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Flydende Æbleskiver (Fried Apple Pancake Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 whole eggs
- 1 pound flour
- 4 pounds sugar
- lukewarm cream
- lemon zest
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs in a bowl and stir together with the flour and sugar
2. Thin the mixture with lukewarm cream
3. Add a little grated lemon zest and ground cinnamon if desired
4. The batter should be thick enough to cling to a spoon
5. Drop into boiling butter or fat as small balls
6. Fry and serve hot
7. Sprinkle with powdered sugar before serving
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Flydende Æbleskiver (Liquid Apple Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping Cup Flour
- 2 large Tablespoons Butter (clarified)
- 1 Gill Ale
- 1 Gill Milk
- 4 Eggs

## Directions

1. Stir 1 heaping Cup Flour with 2 large Tablespoons clarified Butter, 1 Gill Ale, 1 Gill Milk, and 4 Eggs (whip the Whites and fold in last)
2. Peel and hollow out Apples, cut into slices and they can be given a short boil in Sugar and Water
3. Dip each slice in the batter and fry

title: Flynder og Rødspætter (Flounder and Plaice)
body:
## Ingredients

- flounder or plaice
- salt
- boiled potatoes
- melted butter
- parsley
- eggs
- cream
- breadcrumbs
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and discard it along with the innards. Skin the flounder, but not the plaice.
2. Both can be boiled by placing them in boiling water with a little salt. If they are large, cut them into pieces.
3. Serve with either melted butter with parsley or sour butter sauce, along with boiled potatoes.
4. If they are to be fried, use the method described for Perch (No. 85), but without parsley filling.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Flækkede Sveskeblommer til Kompot (Split Prunes for Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1.5 dl water for 0.5 kg fruit

## Directions

1. Scald the plums in boiling water until the skin comes off easily.
2. Split them and remove the stones.
3. Give the plums just one boil in the syrup as described in the previous recipe, then place them in jars.
4. Boil the juice for a few minutes before pouring it over them.
5. Crush the stones and boil some of the kernels as described for apricots.

title: Flæsk i Kaal (Pork in Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg (4 lb) mildly salted or partly fresh striped pork
- 2 medium heads white cabbage

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of mildly salted or partly fresh striped pork first for ½ hour in water, which is then roughly poured out of the pot.
2. Cut 2 medium white cabbage heads coarsely and put them in the pot with the pork in the middle and cover with the cabbage.
3. Cook for about 3 hours under a lid over very low heat so it does not burn.
4. Turn the cabbage once in a while.
5. When done, cut the pork and arrange it in the center of the serving dish with the cabbage around it.

title: Flæskesteg (Pork Roast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork roast, 5 to 6 kg (10 to 12 pounds)
- Salt
- Boiling water
- Bay leaves (optional)
- Whole peppercorns (optional)
- Cloves (optional)
- Flour for sauce
- Food coloring
- Maggi bouillon cubes

## Directions

1. A pork roast of 5 to 6 kg must roast for approximately 4 hours.
2. The ham is the piece of pork usually used for a larger roast, but in smaller households the front rib is often roasted.
3. First, remove the shank (see its use in Nr. 263 and 868), and then scrape the roast well on the skin side.
4. With a sharp knife, score the skin either in strips crosswise or in squares, but not too deeply into the meat.
5. Wash the skin in cold water and then rub it well with a little salt to make it crispy.
6. Place the roast in the roasting pan, which is placed in a well-heated oven, and only after a few minutes pour boiling water to it along with salt.
7. A pork roast must not be basted, as this prevents the skin from becoming crispy.
8. Many use a couple of bay leaves, some whole peppercorns, and a few cloves with the roast, but this depends on taste.
9. Half an hour before the roast is done, strain off some of the gravy.
10. Skim off the fat, and some of it can be used to cook the flour in for the sauce, which is diluted with the gravy and must then cook well.
11. Season with salt, give it color with food coloring, and improve it with Maggi bouillon cubes.

title: Flæskesteg (Roasted Pork Belly)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham (with shank removed)
- bacon fat
- salt
- ground pepper
- water
- flour
- cold butter

## Directions

1. Before roasting, rub the skin well with the fatty side of a piece of bacon fat and then with salt mixed with a little ground pepper.
2. Cut scores in the skin, but not into the meat, first lengthwise and then crosswise, so that the skin appears divided into squares no larger than a 25-øre piece.
3. Place the roast in the pan, sprinkle with a little salt and ground pepper.
4. Add a little water to the pan.
5. Roast for approximately 4-5 hours.
6. The oven must be very hot when the roast is placed in it, but not so hot that the roast burns.
7. Roasting occurs at even heat, and the roast is not basted.
8. Check occasionally that there is not too much steam in the oven, as the skin will not become crispy otherwise.
9. Pour off the sauce from the roast, skim off the fat and brown it with flour in a casserole and thin with the remaining sauce.
10. Skim it, and just before serving stir a small piece of cold butter into the sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Flæskesteg i skarp Sauce og Ris-Rand (Crispy Pork Roast in Sharp Sauce with Rice Border)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Browned butter
- Flour
- Stock or soup
- Sherry
- Salt
- Paprika
- Culinary color
- Slices of crispy pork roast
- Cooked rice
- Onion slices
- Butter for browning
- Potato puree (see No. 336)

## Directions

1. Make a sauce from browned butter and flour, thinned with stock or soup
2. Add sherry, salt, paprika and culinary color to the sauce
3. Warm slices of crispy pork roast in this sauce
4. Place the warmed meat on a flat dish and surround it with a border of loosely cooked rice
5. Sprinkle the rice border with onion slices browned in butter, or with a potato puree baked on the dish (see No. 336)

title: Flæskesteg i skarp Sauce og Ris-Rand (Pork Roast in Sharp Sauce with Rice Border)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Stock or soup
- Sherry
- Salt
- Paprika
- Coloring
- Sliced pork roast
- Cooked rice, loose
- Onion slices, browned in butter

## Directions

1. Make a sauce with browned butter and flour, lighten with stock or soup.
2. Add Sherry, salt, paprika and coloring to the sauce.
3. Warm slices of pork roast in this sauce.
4. Arrange the meat on a flat dish and surround it with a border of loose cooked rice.
5. Sprinkle the rice with onion slices browned in butter or with mashed potatoes baked on the dish.

title: Flæskesuppe (Pork Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Flæsk, Ben, Flæskesvær og Saltmad (pork, bones, pork crackling, and salt meat)
- Koldt Vand (cold water)
- Et lille Stykke Ingefær (a small piece of ginger)
- Grove Byggryn eller Perlegryn (coarse barley or pearl barley)
- Selleri (celery)
- Persillerødder (parsley roots)
- Gulerødder (carrots)
- Raaskrællede Kartofler (raw peeled potatoes)
- Lidt Hvedemel (a little wheat flour)

## Directions

1. Soak the salt meat in cold water for two days before cooking, renewing the water three times daily.
2. If there are sausages among the salt meat, prick them occasionally with a fork during cooking to prevent them from bursting.
3. Place the meat in a pot with enough cold water to cover it by a couple of inches.
4. Add a small piece of ginger to the water.
5. Bring to a boil and skim the soup carefully several times during cooking.
6. When the meat is tender, strain the soup and let it rest for a quarter hour to allow fat to rise.
7. Rinse the barley in cold water.
8. Return the soup to the heat and add the barley, celery, parsley roots, carrots, and raw peeled potatoes.
9. Skim the fat that rises and mix it with a little wheat flour to thicken the soup.
10. Cook until the vegetables and barley are tender.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Flöde-Omelet Nr. 1 (Cream Omelet No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup clarified butter
- 0.25 pint cream or good milk
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon vanilla extract
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Bake flour in clarified butter
2. Gradually add cream or milk and cook with strong stirring and beating with spoon
3. Cool slightly
4. Stir strongly with egg yolks and vanilla extract
5. Finally fold in beaten egg whites
6. Bake in oven for about 3 quarters of an hour
7. Serves 8 to 10 persons

title: Flöde-Omelet Nr. 2 (Cream Omelet No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 1 small teaspoon sugar
- 1 teaspoon flour
- 1 pint cream
- A couple drops vanilla

## Directions

1. Stir egg yolks white with sugar
2. Stir in flour
3. Gradually add cream
4. Add vanilla
5. Finally fold in beaten egg whites
6. Bake for 0.5 hour
7. Serves 4 persons

title: Fløde-Budding (Cream Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Liter (Pot) cream
- 200 Gr (40 Kvint) icing sugar
- 16 leaves isinglass
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water
- 3 3/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) white rum
- cold fruit sauce

## Directions

1. Whip the cream to foam
2. Stir in 200 Gr (40 Kvint) icing sugar
3. Dissolve 16 leaves of isinglass in 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water
4. Stir the isinglass solution and 3 3/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) white rum into the cream
5. Stir the pudding until it begins to set before pouring into the mold or bowl
6. The pudding can be made a few hours before serving
7. Serve with cold fruit sauce

title: Fløde-Kranse (Cream Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram flour
- 300 grams (60 quintals) margarine
- 175 grams (35 quintals) powdered sugar
- 6 tablespoons cream
- Cream for dipping
- Powdered sugar for coating
- Butter for greasing the pan

## Directions

1. Knead together the flour, margarine, powdered sugar, and cream
2. Roll the mixture into small rings
3. Dip them in cream and powdered sugar
4. Grease the baking sheet with butter and place the rings at some distance from each other on the sheet

title: Fløde-Peberrod (Horseradish Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint cream
- sugar
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Whip 1 pint cream to stiff peaks with a little sugar.
2. Stir in 1 tablespoon vinegar.
3. Finally add the grated horseradish, which should be grated only when it is to be used so it does not become dark.
4. Serve cold.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fløde-Vafler (Cream Waffles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter (1 pot) cream
- approximately 375 g (1/4 tb) flour
- 1 good tablespoon sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat the cream to foam and then carefully beat in approximately 375 g (1/4 tb) flour, but only a little at a time, together with a good tablespoon of sugar.
2. Heat the waffle iron thoroughly and butter it with a cloth.
3. During baking, turn the iron so the waffles bake evenly light brown on both sides.
4. Sprinkle with crushed powdered sugar and serve preferably warm. Sour or sweet cream or half of each can be used. They become soft when stacked on top of each other.

title: Fløde-Vafler (Cream Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pot cream
- 60 Qvint fine wheat flour

## Directions

1. Beat the cream to stiff foam.
2. Stir in 60 qvint fine wheat flour.
3. Bake in the usual manner.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fløde-Æbleskiver (Cream Apple Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint sweet cream
- 2 Egg yolks
- 0.5 Pint fine wheat flour
- 2 Tablespoonfuls powdered sugar
- A little finely powdered cardamom
- 2 Egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 1 pint sweet cream well with 2 egg yolks, 0.5 pint fine wheat flour, 2 tablespoonfuls powdered sugar, and a little finely powdered cardamom.
2. Beat the 2 egg whites to stiff foam and fold into the rest.
3. Bake the æbleskiver in a hot pan in melted butter.
4. Serve with caster sugar over.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Flødekage (Cream Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints thick sweet or sour cream
- 8 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 8 egg whites, beaten
- a little ground Ceylon cinnamon
- a little grated lemon zest
- clarified butter
- a little raspberry or currant juice (optional)
- powdered sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Beat 3 pints thick sweet or sour cream to stiff foam, and stir in 8 egg yolks which have first been beaten well with 4 tablespoons powdered sugar and then with 4 tablespoons flour.
2. Finally stir in the 8 beaten egg whites together with a little ground Ceylon cinnamon and a little grated lemon zest.
3. Grease a dish well with clarified butter and pour in the dough, then bake for about an hour.
4. If you wish, you can place a little raspberry or currant juice in the bottom of the dish.
5. Serve warm, well sprinkled with powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Flødelapper (Cream Pancakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) cream
- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) flour
- 3 to 4 tablespoons powdered sugar
- essence of choice
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the cream well and stir in the flour, 3 to 4 tablespoons of powdered sugar, and a little essence if desired.
2. Bake in butter on a pan and set off with a spoon in small cakes.
3. Serve warm with sugar.

title: Fløderuller (Cream-Filled Pastry Rolls)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) Flour
- 3/4 L (3/4 Pot) Cream, whipped
- Butter dough (see Nr. 22)
- Egg
- Powdered sugar (Melis)
- Maraschino liqueur
- Pineapple, finely chopped
- Chocolate, dissolved

## Directions

1. Roll out butter dough as thinly as possible and cut into strips 2.5 centimeters (1 inch) wide.
2. Thin metal tubes of the oven's length can be ordered from the tinsmith; spiral the butter dough strips around these in 8 centimeters (3 inches) length and break off for each cake.
3. Brush with egg, sprinkle with powdered sugar, and bake golden brown in a good hot oven.
4. When finished, gently push them off the tubes and fill at serving with whipped cream, into which may be added maraschino liqueur, finely chopped pineapple, or dissolved chocolate.

title: Flødeskum med Frugt-Puré (Whipped Cream with Fruit Purée)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked fruits such as apples, pears and apricots, very tender
- sugar to taste
- 1 L (1 pot) cream for whipping
- raw fruits such as strawberries and raspberries
- 1/2 kg (1 S) strawberries or raspberries to 1 L (1 pot) cream

## Directions
