1. For cooked fruit: strain through a coarse sieve and sweeten to taste, then fold in whipped cream just before serving
2. Arrange high in the center of a dish and serve immediately
3. For raw fruit: strain through a sieve, sweeten, and stir into 1 L whipped cream using the proportion of 1/2 kg fruit to 1 L cream

title: Flødeskum med reven Peberrod (Whipped Cream with Grated Horseradish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ liter (½ pound) cream
- 125 grams (¼ pound) grated horseradish
- Sugar
- Salt
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. When the cream is whipped to stiff peaks, stir in 125 grams grated horseradish along with sugar, salt, and vinegar to taste.
2. The sauce is made just before serving.
3. It is eaten with boiled pike, carp, chicken, and veal and lamb meat, etc.
4. What remains of the sauce can be used for macaroni salad.

title: Flødeskum-Sauce (Whipped Cream Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) cream of the best sort
- Liqueur or vanilla (optional)
- A little sugar

## Directions

1. Keep the cream on ice or in a cold place until it is to be whipped.
2. This is done most easily when the cream is in a jug or pail, which is either placed on ice or in cold water.
3. Whip it with a cream whisk to very stiff foam.
4. Add liqueur or vanilla and a little sugar as desired.
5. Use as decoration around cheese, cold puddings and cooked fruit, or serve separately.

title: Fløjelsgrød (Velvet Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound butter
- 1 pound wheat flour
- 4 pots boiling milk
- salt to taste
- ground cinnamon
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Bake half a pound of butter and 1 pound of wheat flour together until it leaves the spoon
2. Gradually thin it with 4 pots of boiling milk, letting the porridge boil between each addition
3. Stir well during thinning to prevent lumps
4. Salt at the end
5. Serve immediately sprinkled with ground cinnamon and powdered sugar, as it becomes long and stringy if it stands
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fløjlsgrød (Velvet Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 Gr (40 Qvint) butter
- 400 Gr (80 Qvint) flour
- 3 L (3 Potter) skimmed milk

## Directions

1. For this porridge, use boiling milk; otherwise the preparation and serving is the same as for Butter Porridge Nr. 471.

title: Fløjlsgrød (Velvet Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g butter
- 400 g flour
- 3 L semi-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. For this porridge, use boiling milk; otherwise the preparation and serving is the same as for Smørgrød No. 471.

title: Forellen (Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- warm vinegar
- white paper
- napkin

## Directions

1. Lay the trout on a wet kitchen board and do not scrape it; remove the innards, being careful not to touch them too much with your hand so as not to lose the slime that produces the blue color.
2. Rinse in cold water, place on a dish, and pour warm vinegar over them, which will make them blue.
3. Cover with a clean piece of white paper and a napkin over it, and let them stand in the vinegar for about 1 hour.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Forloren Hare (Mock Hare)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg beef
- 0.5 kg pork
- 8 rusks
- 2 eggs
- salt
- a few spoonfuls of cream
- smoked bacon for larding
- milk for basting
- butter
- flour
- gravy
- raw cream
- food coloring
- browned potatoes
- parsley
- currant or lingonberry jelly

## Directions

1. Scrape and mince the meat finely together with the crushed rusks, eggs, salt, and a few spoonfuls of cream.
2. Form the forcemeat in the shape of a hare saddle.
3. Lard it deeply and densely with smoked bacon.
4. Roast in the oven for 1 hour, basting frequently with milk.
5. For the sauce, brown butter and flour, thin with gravy, and before serving add a little raw cream and food coloring.
6. Garnish with browned potatoes and parsley.
7. Serve with currant or lingonberry jelly.

title: Forloren Hollandsk Sauce (Mock Hollandaise Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Water
- Maggi bouillon cubes or fish or meat stock
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Melt 100 gr (20 Kvint) of butter
2. Bake 50 gr (10 Kvint) of flour in it
3. Thin with approximately 3/4 L (1/4 Pot) of water mixed with Maggi bouillon cubes, or with fish or meat stock
4. Bind the sauce with 6 egg yolks and season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice
5. Finally, add a little cold butter to it
6. The sauce must be bound just before serving

title: Forloren Mayonnaise (False Mayonnaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 grams butter
- 60 grams flour
- Broth
- 5 well-beaten egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Tarragon vinegar
- 4 tablespoons salad oil

## Directions

1. Make a roux from butter and flour and thin it with enough broth to make a very thick sauce, thickened with the well-beaten egg yolks.
2. Pour out the sauce and stir until it is cold.
3. Under constant stirring add salt, pepper, tarragon vinegar, and 4 tablespoons of salad oil, all to taste.
4. This mayonnaise is easy to make and is preferred by many over the genuine version since it does not contain as much oil.

title: Forloren Okse- eller Kalve-Filet (Mock Beef or Veal Fillet)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- forcemeat
- butter
- asparagus or cauliflower
- tomato slices
- onion slices
- leeks
- peas
- peeled apples
- stewed prunes
- gravy or soup
- food coloring or soya
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- lettuce head

## Directions

1. Spread a layer of forcemeat on a roasting pan buttered with cold butter, press it flat.
2. Arrange chosen vegetables over the forcemeat.
3. Cover with another layer of forcemeat and smooth it.
4. Roast in the oven for 1 hour, basting with gravy, soup, or broth from the forcemeat trimmings, seasoned with food coloring or soya.
5. Slice the roast and arrange the slices together on a platter.
6. Garnish with vegetables matching the filling and a lettuce head at the ends.
7. Make sauce from good brown gravy prepared from the drippings, adding Maggi bouillon cubes.

title: Forloren Skildpadde (Mock Turtle)
body:
## Ingredients

- Head of a fattened calf, cleaned and cooked
- Beef neck meat or good shank, 245 Pd.
- Onions
- Beets
- Carrots
- Small celery root
- Pepper
- Ginger
- Salt
- Bouquet garni
- Calf stock
- Lemon juice
- Sugar
- Soya
- Cayenne pepper
- Madeira wine, half a bottle, or half a measure of good cognac
- Madeira wine, one more measure, before serving
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Small fish balls made from fish farce of 24 Pd.

## Directions

1. Clean and cook the head of a fattened calf the day before it is to be used (see recipe 23), then place the cut meat on a platter.
2. Brown beef neck meat or good shank with abundant onions, beets, carrots, a small celery root, pepper, ginger, salt and a bouquet garni, and let the whole simmer until the meat is very tender.
3. Finally thin it with a little of the calf stock and let it cook for a long time, but gently.
4. Let it stand for a while to settle, then strain it.
5. In this sauce, warm the meat from the calf's head when it is to be used, together with a little lemon juice, sugar, soya and a small amount of cayenne pepper, and half a bottle of Madeira or half a measure of good cognac.
6. Immediately before serving, add one more measure of Madeira.
7. Serve the mock turtle with hard-boiled egg yolks and small fish balls.
8. From a good head, reckon 10-12 portions.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Forloren Skildpadde (Mock Turtle)
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's head
- Water
- Salt
- Browned roast bones
- Broth
- Vegetables and onion
- Butter
- Onion
- Flour
- Cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. When a calf's head of medium size is scalded, clean it well; remove the tongue for better cleaning; this and the head, which is flocked, are cooked in water and salt until it is so tender that the meat can be separated from the bones, which must be done while it is warm
2. The broth should be skimmed carefully
3. Spread it on a platter, carefully remove all bones, and cut the meat along with the tongue into small square pieces
4. Then take bouillon, which can be made from browned roast bones and the water in which the head was cooked, but then it must be cooked in just water with vegetables and onion — but it is better when you directly make brown broth for the same purpose
5. Brown a good piece of butter, in which cook a finely grated onion, but carefully so it does not burn
6. Then add 3 heaping tablespoons flour and a little Cayenne pepper

title: Forloren Skildpadde-Suppe (Mock Turtle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- brown stock
- cooked calf's head, cut into thin strips
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- meat and fish balls
- nutmeg
- ginger
- cayenne pepper
- raw egg yolks
- Madeira or port wine

## Directions

1. Make brown stock as previously described; add thin strips of cooked calf's head; if lacking strong stock, you can also take the soup in which the calf's head was cooked, when it is well cleaned of hair, but it is better to avoid this; add hard-boiled egg yolks, meat and fish balls, whose size should be like the egg yolks, and nutmeg, ginger and cayenne; root vegetables and parsley are not used
2. At serving, smooth it with a couple of raw egg yolks, and add Madeira or port wine according to the portion size

title: Forloren Skildpaddesuppe (Mock Turtle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hovedet af en Fedekalv
- Salt
- en Suppevisk
- 10 hele Peberkorn
- et lille Stykke Ingefær
- Et finthakket Rødløg
- et Fjerdingspund Smør
- fire Spiseskefulde Hvedemel
- 3 Potter kraftig Sky
- en lille Knivspidsfuld Kajennepeber
- en lille Spiseskefuld Kandis
- en Spiseskefuld god Cognac
- en halv Pægl Madeira eller Sherry
- 12 til 20 haardkogte Æggeblommer

## Directions

1. Lægges Hovedet af en Fedekalv en Timestid i koldt Vand.
2. Skylles det med varmt Vand, saa det bliver frit for Blod.
3. Efter at det er omhyggeligt renset, Ørene afskaarne og Øjnene udtagne, sættes det over Ilden med tilstrækkeligt koldt Vand.
4. Naar det netop staar paa Kog, tages det op og blancheres ved at overhældes med koldt Vand.
5. Flækkes Hovedet, og Hjernen udtages.
6. Suppen sættes paany over Ilden tilligemed Hovedet, lidt Salt, en Suppevisk, 10 hele Peberkorn og et lille Stykke Ingefær.
7. Den koges derpaa over sagte Ild, indtil Kjødet er godt gjennemkogt og let kan løsnes fra Benene.
8. Hovedet tages derpaa op, lægges paa et fladt Fad for at afkøles, og Suppen sies.
9. Et finthakket Rødløg brunes langsomt med et Fjerdingspund Smør og fire Spiseskefulde Hvedemel.
10. 3 Potter kraftig Sky tilsættes, og det Hele spædes med den paa Kalvehovedet kogte Suppe.
11. Naar det har kogt noget, lægges Kjødet af Kalvehovedet, der er pillet af Benene og tilligemed Tungen skaaret i smaa Stykker, omtrent en halv Tomme paa hver Led, deri tilligemed en lille Knivspidsfuld Kajennepeber og en lille Spiseskefuld Kandis.
12. Umiddelbart før Anretningen kommes en Spiseskefuld god Cognac og en halv Pægl Madeira eller Sherry i Suppen.
13. I Terrinen lægges 12 til 20 haardkogte Æggeblommer, hvorefter Suppen ophældes og anrettes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Forlorne Æg (Poached Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh eggs
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Fresh eggs are cracked on the edge of a plate and placed in a pan with boiling water, which stands over the heat.
2. You must not put too many in at once, and if you want the yolk clear, there must not be so much water in the pan that it stands over the eggs.
3. They are loosened with a knife, taken up and wiped off.
4. For breakfast, they can be served with anchovies, one of which is laid around the yolk on each egg, after the head and backbone have first been removed, or gravy can be laid over them in strips.
5. If the eggs are not newly laid, add a little vinegar to the water.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fransk Bøf med Maître d'Hôtel-Smør (French Beef with Maître d'Hôtel Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg beef tenderloin, or veal or pork tenderloin
- Salt
- Pepper
- Brown butter
- Gravy or stock
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- 0.25 kg butter
- 3 teaspoons chopped parsley
- 2 teaspoons lemon juice
- Potatoes cooked in Palmin

## Directions

1. Beef, veal or pork tenderloin can be used; in this case pork tenderloin is used.
2. Cut the tenderloin across into suitable pieces and pound flat.
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and fry in a pan with brown butter over a brisk fire.
4. Arrange the beef on a very hot dish and pour over it the butter and gravy deglazed from the pan.
5. A few Maggi bouillon cubes can be dissolved in it.
6. Stir the butter and add the lemon juice.
7. With a teaspoon dipped in warm water, form small balls; these are rolled in the chopped parsley, and one ball is placed on each piece of beef.
8. Garnish with potatoes cooked in Palmin.

title: Fransk Kød-Salat (French Meat Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons oil
- 3 tablespoons tomato puree
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- Mustard
- Salt
- Pepper
- Tarragon vinegar
- 10 anchovies
- 6 to 8 pickled shallots
- 1 deep plate finely cut meat

## Directions

1. Mix together the oil, tomato puree, chopped parsley, mustard, salt, pepper and tarragon vinegar.
2. Chop the meat from 10 anchovies and 6 to 8 pickled shallots and add to the sauce together with a deep plate of finely cut meat.

title: Fransk Omelet (French Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 6 dessertspoons powdered sugar
- juice and grated zest from half a lemon
- egg whites
- butter for the mold

## Directions

1. Stir 6 egg yolks for half an hour with 6 dessertspoons powdered sugar and the juice and grated zest of half a lemon
2. Beat the egg whites stiff and fold in gently
3. Butter a mold, pour in the batter and let stand five minutes at low heat to set
4. Then place it in a hot oven and bake one hour
5. Cannot be removed from the mold
6. Serve with white sugar
7. The eggs must be stirred and beaten well and long, otherwise it will be heavy
8. Serves 5 people

title: Fransk Postej (French Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- breadcrumbs
- veal forcemeat
- cooked macaroni
- Parmesan cheese
- fricassee of turkey or hens
- short crust dough

## Directions

1. Grease the mold with cold butter and sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
2. Fill the bottom and sides with veal forcemeat, then with a layer of cleaned, cooked, finely cut macaroni and a layer of Parmesan cheese.
3. Over this place a strong fricassee of turkey or hens.
4. Then cover the whole with macaroni and finally with a layer of short crust dough.
5. Bake the pie for a good hour in a hot oven.
6. Serve with mushroom sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fransk Sennep (French Mustard - Moutard à la ravigote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 gr (10 kvint) chervil
- 50 gr (10 kvint) watercress
- 50 gr (10 kvint) tarragon leaves
- 50 gr (10 kvint) salad burnet
- 200 gr (40 kvint) powdered yellow mustard
- water

## Directions

1. Place chervil, watercress, tarragon leaves and salad burnet in boiling water for a couple of minutes and pour onto a strainer to drain.
2. Pound the vegetables very finely in a mortar and mix into 200 gr (40 kvint) powdered yellow mustard that has been stirred in water.
3. Store this mustard in a well-stoppered and bound glass.

title: Fransk Suppe (French Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound beef, finely cut
- 1 onion
- 3 carrots
- 3 quarts water
- a large bunch of herbs
- root vegetables
- 1/2 pound meat for meat balls

## Directions

1. Cut 1 pound beef finely and cook it slowly together with 1 onion and 3 carrots in 3 quarts water until the meat can easily be pressed apart, which requires nearly 3 hours
2. To prevent the soup from boiling away, it is necessary that it cooks evenly in a pot with a tight-fitting lid
3. Add a large bunch of herbs and root vegetables and take them out when they are done
4. Strain the soup, place it back on the fire, skim it, and when it boils, add meat balls made from 1/2 pound meat
5. The soup can be smoothed with egg and eaten with toasted bread; this portion is for 3 persons

title: Fransk Æblekage (French Apple Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- small macaroons
- apple porridge
- raspberry marmalade
- 6 egg yolks
- 1.5 cups sweet cream
- 6 egg whites
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Butter a form and flour it
2. On the bottom place a layer of small macaroons dipped in melted butter, then a layer of apple porridge, then a layer of raspberry marmalade, then again macaroons, and so forth, until the form is almost full
3. Beat 6 egg yolks with 1.5 cups sweet cream and then with the 6 previously beaten egg whites
4. Pour this mixture over the rest, dust with powdered sugar, and then bake the cake 1 hour in a moderately warm oven
5. When done, turn it onto a dish
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Franskbrød (French Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 grams pressed yeast
- 13 cups lukewarm milk
- 1 pound flour
- a little salt
- egg white or lukewarm water for brushing
- poppy seeds (optional)

## Directions

1. Dissolve the pressed yeast in the lukewarm milk and stir into 1 pound flour along with a little salt.
2. Knead the dough well until it releases from the hands and shows eyes.
3. When it has risen, shape without further kneading into small loaves.
4. Brush with egg white or lukewarm water and bake light brown with good, even heat.
5. If poppy seeds are sprinkled over the loaves before baking, they become the so-called Berkesbrød.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Franskbrød bagt i Form (French Bread Baked in Form - Warm Rising)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 Gr (10 Kvint) yeast
- 2 teaspoons salt
- 2 teaspoons sugar
- 1 Kg (2 S) Pillsbury flour
- 1/4 L (3 Pægle) semi-skimmed, lukewarm milk

## Directions

1. Mix yeast with salt and sugar
2. Add Pillsbury flour and semi-skimmed lukewarm milk
3. Work well with a spoon until the dough releases from the bowl
4. Grease two French bread forms with butter
5. Place dough in forms and let rise for 2-2 1/2 hours
6. Bake in a very hot oven for approximately 1 hour

title: Franske Nathuer (French Night Caps)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 French breads
- half-skimmed milk
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- bread crusts
- butter or Palmin
- fruit sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the crust from a French bread.
2. Split them, dip in half-skimmed milk and let stand covered for a couple of hours, before they are dipped in egg and bread crumbs mixed with the removed crusts.
3. Fry them in a pan in butter or Palmin and serve hot with fruit sauce.

title: Franstbrod (French Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces good rice
- wheat flour
- lukewarm water
- salt
- 2 cents yeast

## Directions

1. Boil 12 ounces good rice in a linen cloth or bag, ensuring sufficient room for it to swell.
2. Cook for 3 to 4 hours or until it becomes a complete dough.
3. While still warm, blend with 7 pounds flour, as much lukewarm water as necessary, a little salt, and 2 cents yeast.
4. Knead the dough well and set to rise in warmth.
5. Divide into loaves, knead and handle again in the usual manner.

title: Fricandeau (Braised Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal, cut into pieces as thick as a finger
- Butter, in proportion to the meat
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- A few crushed cloves
- Broth or gravy and water
- Cream
- Flour
- White wine

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into pieces as thick as a finger, beat lightly with a kitchen knife on one side and lard on the other side.
2. Brown a suitable amount of butter in a casserole or braising pan, lay the meat with the larded side down and fry until light brown.
3. Turn the meat and cook covered until tender, generally about a quarter of an hour.
4. Remove the meat and place on a platter, which is set over coals.
5. If there is fat on the sauce, skim it off, and remove from heat while stirring in a little gravy and cream, then return to heat, stirring constantly until it begins to boil and is smooth.
6. Pour the sauce over the meat, with the larded side turned upright.
7. Serve with stiff currant jelly as for beef.

title: Fried Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pint sour milk or buttermilk
- 2 beaten eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- ½ teaspoonful soda
- 2 spoonfuls warm fat
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix together ½ pint sour milk or buttermilk, 2 beaten eggs, 1 cup sugar, ½ teaspoonful soda, and 2 spoonfuls warm fat
2. Add flour until you find it pleasantly thick so it can be handled
3. Press it together well, but do not roll it
4. Place on baking sheet and roll out with a roller, not too thin
5. Cut out cakes with a glass or similar object
6. Cut these out again with a smaller glass so they form rings with small cakes
7. Form them like doughnuts when frying
8. This dough works well for Kleiner too

title: Fried Perch (Wagte Aborrer)
body:
## Ingredients

- Perch
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Pepper
- Salt
- Parsley
- Chopped onion
- Beaten egg whites
- Bread
- Caper sauce

## Directions

1. After the fish is cut and washed, split it in two parts.
2. Remove the bones, peel off the skin and add vinegar, oil, pepper, salt, parsley and chopped onion, letting it stand for 2-3 hours.
3. Then take out the fish, dry it in a cloth, dip it in beaten egg whites, then in bread and fry in a pan.
4. Serve with caper sauce.
5. Alternatively, split cleaned perch from the back, lay them in brine or sprinkle with salt.
6. Dry them, coat with capers mixed with parsley, ground pepper and butter.
7. Put them together, spread butter on the outside, roast on a grate and serve with the same sauce.

title: Frikadeller (Danish Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds ground beef
- 1 pound beef kidney fat
- 4 eggs
- 4 hardtacks soaked in sweet milk
- finely chopped onion
- ground pepper
- salt
- sweet milk and cream for consistency
- fat or butter for frying

## Directions

1. Chop the beef very finely.
2. Mix the beef with kidney fat, eggs, hardtacks soaked in sweet milk, finely chopped onion, ground pepper and salt.
3. Work the mixture together until it becomes well aerated.
4. Thin with sweet milk and cream to achieve proper consistency.
5. Fry the meatballs light brown in a pan with fat or butter, using a spoon dipped in cold water to shape them.
6. Turn them on the pan and fry over gentle heat.
7. Brown butter in the pan, thin with a little water and serve with the meatballs along with stewed vegetables or carrots.
8. May also be made from other kinds of meat, fish forcemeat, or cooked or fried meat; in the latter case, mix the meat with 1 pound of fat, smoked, finely chopped bacon instead of kidney fat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Frikadeller (Fried Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- fars (minced meat mixture)
- double the amount of wheat flour compared to the mixture for balls
- fat or butter
- cold water

## Directions

1. Use the same fars as for balls, but with double the wheat flour
2. Heat a frying pan over the fire with a little fat or butter, and when it is light brown, place the frikadeller on the pan using a spoon dipped in cold water
3. Place as many on the pan as can fit without touching each other
4. When half cooked through on gentle heat, turn them carefully
5. When fully fried, place them on a dish and keep warm until ready to serve
6. When finished frying all the frikadeller, brown butter in the pan, thin with a little water and serve as sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Frikadeller (Meat Patties)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat farce
- Butter
- Green vegetables
- Sharp sauce
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. The farce should be as firm as for meatballs, and from it fry small round cakes in brown butter.
2. Place them on the pan with a spoon, dipping the spoon in warm water each time so the dough slips off.
3. Serve with green vegetables or with sharp sauce and potatoes.

title: Frikadeller af kogt eller stegt Kød (Meatballs of Cooked or Fried Meat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams (0.25 lbs) fatty, smoked or fresh bacon, finely chopped
- 0.5 kg (1 lbs) cooked or fried meat
- 1 onion
- 1 French bread roll, sliced and soaked in milk
- Salt
- Pepper
- 4-5 eggs
- Egg for dipping
- Breadcrumbs
- Clarified butter or Palmin oil

## Directions

1. Finely chop the bacon with the cooked or fried meat and onion.
2. When it forms a cohesive mass, work in with the chopper a small French bread roll that has been sliced and soaked in milk for one hour.
3. Finally chop in salt, pepper and 4-5 eggs until it becomes a firm dough.
4. Shape with a spoon into balls, dip in egg and breadcrumbs and form into round or slightly flattened oval shapes.
5. Fry in clarified butter or Palmin oil over low heat.
6. Serve with a sharp brown sauce and cooked macaroni or with other vegetables.

title: Frikassee af kold Steg (Fricassee of Cold Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast meat, sliced thin
- a handful chopped parsley
- 1 root onion cut into pieces
- ½ pint bouillon
- a piece of butter
- salt
- pepper
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 glass port wine or sherry
- 1 spoonful vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thin slices and cook it gently for a quarter hour together with a handful chopped parsley, a root onion cut into pieces, ½ pint bouillon, a piece of butter, salt and pepper
2. Quickly stir together 2 egg yolks, 1 glass port wine or sherry, and 1 spoonful vinegar
3. Pour this mixture into the meat

title: Frikassé-Sauce with Asparagus (Fricassee Sauce with Asparagus)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock or salted water
- Egg yolks
- Cooked, shelled shrimp

## Directions

1. Peel the asparagus, cut into small pieces and cook in very little stock or in salted water
2. Use the stock in which they were cooked for the sauce, which is bound with egg yolks
3. Cooked, shelled shrimp can be added to the sauce together with the asparagus
4. Shrimp shells can be pounded and boiled; see Lobster Sauce No. 778

title: Frikassé-Sauce with Broccoli (Fricassee Sauce with Broccoli)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Broccoli
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock or salted water
- Egg yolks
- Cooked, shelled shrimp

## Directions

1. Prepare as with asparagus
2. Cooked, shelled shrimp can also be added to this

title: Frikassé-Sauce with Parsley (Fricassee Sauce with Parsley)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Stock in which the meat was cooked
- Egg yolks
- Cooked, sliced carrots
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Calculate at least 1 L (1 Pot) of sauce for 12 persons
2. Bake butter and flour and thin with the stock in which the meat was cooked, bind with 3 egg yolks
3. Pour the sauce over the cut-up meat for fricassee
4. Just before serving, add 2 tablespoons of finely chopped parsley and some cooked, sliced carrots to the sauce

title: Frikassé-Sauce with Peas (Fricassee Sauce with Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Stock in which the meat was cooked
- Egg yolks
- Cooked, shelled peas
- Parsley
- Cooked, sliced carrots

## Directions

1. Make the sauce as described in the preceding recipes and add cooked, shelled peas
2. If desired, add finely chopped parsley and cooked, sliced carrots

title: Fromage (Fruit Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint raw fruit juice, preferably raspberries
- sugar
- 3 ounces gelatine
- 1 quart fresh cream

## Directions

1. Boil the fruit juice with sugar and 3 ounces gelatine until the gelatine is dissolved.
2. Allow to cool and fold in 1 quart fresh cream that has been whipped to stiff peaks.
3. Continue stirring until it is cold, then pour into a mold that has first been wetted, from which it can be turned out again.
4. Serve without sauce with small, dry cakes.
5. Can be made the same way from stewed juice, which then must be heated.
6. For 8 persons.

title: Frossen Fløde (Frozen Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Whipped cream
- Vanilla
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Sweeten the whipped cream and add a little vanilla.
2. Freeze it.
3. Serve in slices from the mold with a spoon dipped in warm water.
4. Arrange around a whole carved and folded pineapple and sprinkle with candied violets.
5. Frozen cream is eaten with fresh strawberries.

title: Fru Gorickes Kager (Mrs. Goricke's Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 whole eggs
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- almond drops

## Directions

1. Mix well thoroughly.
2. Add almond drops.
3. Place on a buttered pan with a teaspoon.

title: Fru Heibergs Kager (Mrs. Heiberg's Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- raspberry jam
- thick cold vanilla cream
- egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Line small forms with butter dough
2. Add a little raspberry jam
3. Top with a thick, cold vanilla cream
4. Lay a cross of butter dough over the cream
5. Brush the cakes with egg
6. Bake in a warm oven
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Frugt Pie (Fruit Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- good pie dough
- approximately 10 good large apples, peeled and sliced
- 1 pound raisins, chopped
- 2 to 3 cups sugar
- cinnamon
- small pieces of butter
- fine sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Bake preferably in a two-quart mold with roughly the same circumference at top and bottom
2. First make good pie dough
3. Place a layer of this in the bottom but not on the sides
4. Spread a layer of chopped and peeled apples two inches thick
5. Then spread a layer of chopped raisins
6. Sprinkle sugar, cinnamon and small pieces of butter over this
7. Then lay another layer of dough, then fruit and so forth until the mold is full
8. The top layer must be dough
9. Bake evenly for a couple of hours
10. When done, turn the bottom upward and before serving, sprinkle fine sugar over it
11. It is equally good when reheated

title: Frugt-Kage (Fruit Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces butter
- ½ pound raisins
- ½ pound currants
- 2 ounces candied peel
- 6 ounces sugar
- 6 eggs
- 6 ounces flour

## Directions

1. Heat 2 ounces butter near the fire until soft without becoming oily
2. Line a cake pan internally with paper that is buttered
3. Remove the stones from ½ pound raisins and wash and dry ½ pound currants thoroughly
4. Cut 2 ounces candied peel into thin small slices
5. Stir 6 ounces sugar to cream with 2 eggs; then add 2 more, stir for a couple of minutes and finally a fifth egg, then beat again slightly
6. Sift 6 ounces flour into this and stir evenly; then add the butter and fruit, and when well mixed, pour into the pan and bake in a moderately warm oven for approximately half an hour
7. Test in the usual way

title: Frugt-Pie'er (Fruit Pie)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- a pie dish with compote of dried or fresh fruit
- batter (choice of three types as specified)

## Directions

1. Place the pie dish with compote in the oven to warm through
2. Pour one of the batter types over the compote
3. Bake the pie at good heat for 35 minutes
4. Serve in the dish, wrapped with a napkin, unless the dish is too nice to need covering
5. Sprinkle with powdered sugar

title: Frugt-Sauce af friske Frugter (Fresh Fruit Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter (1 pint) water
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) powdered sugar
- 1/2 kilogram (1 pound) rinsed fruit (apples, pears, apricots, peaches, or various sorts of plums)
- Tapioca starch

## Directions

1. Set 1/4 liter water over heat with 125 grams powdered sugar, and when it is melted, add 1/2 kilogram rinsed fruit and cook until it can easily be pressed through a sieve.
2. Set the sauce over heat again and smooth it with mixed tapioca starch.
3. The amount of water and sugar must be adjusted according to the fruit's sweetness.
4. Some of the fruit may be left whole in the sauce; they are then cooked with the rest and removed before the remainder is pressed through the sieve.
5. Apples and pears must be peeled before cooking, and the cores removed.
6. Apricots and peaches are halved, the stones removed, and the kernels placed in the sauce.

title: Frugt-Sauce af Marmelade (Jam Fruit Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Preserved fruit jams (cherry, strawberry, raspberry, blackberry, or mulberry)
- Boiling water
- Potato or tapioca starch

## Directions

1. Make a thickening of boiling water and potato or tapioca starch.
2. Add the jam to this mixture.
3. The jam should preferably not boil, as the berries will lose in taste and appearance.

title: Frugt-Sauce af syltet Saft (Preserved Juice Fruit Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Any kind of preserved juice
- Water
- Sago or potato starch
- Sugar (if needed)
- Sliced or chopped almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Dilute the preserved juice with water.
2. When it boils, smooth it with mixed sago or potato starch.
3. Add sugar if needed.
4. Sliced or chopped almonds may be added to the fruit sauce.
5. Serve cold or warm.

title: Frugt-Sauce af tørrede Frugter (Dried Fruit Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried apples, pears, or apricots
- Water
- Sugar
- Sago starch
- Vanilla essence, lemon zest, or cinnamon (optional)

## Directions

1. Rinse the dried fruit, soak it the day before, and cook in water with sugar until so tender that it can be pressed through a sieve.
2. Smooth the sauce with mixed sago starch.
3. Add vanilla essence, lemon zest, or cinnamon if desired.
4. For dried cherries: crush them before cooking.

title: Frugtsauce (Fruit Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- various fruit juices
- potato flour
- red wine
- fresh fruit such as cherries or raspberries
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Use various fruit juices, boil and thicken with a little potato flour first stirred in red wine.
2. If using fresh fruit, particularly cherries or raspberries, first boil and strain, then boil the juice again with sugar to taste and thicken as mentioned above.
3. In cherry sauce one may also add a little cinnamon and lemon zest.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fruit Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup melted butter
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1 cup lukewarm water
- 2 tablespoons Dr. Price's Baking Powder
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix melted butter, sugar, lukewarm water, and 2 tablespoons Dr. Price's Baking Powder sifted together with as much flour as needed for a stiff dough.
2. Roll out very thin and cut with a round cake cutter or a glass.
3. Bake in a hot oven.

title: Frukt-Kréme (Fruit Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- apple sauce or juice of cherries, currants, raspberries, or strawberries and raspberries
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon juice
- wine
- sago starch or potato starch
- whipped cream
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Can be made from apple sauce or from juice of cherries, currants, raspberries, or strawberries and raspberries, which are cooked and strained.
2. The juice is cooked with sugar according to taste, a little cinnamon and lemon juice, and wine.
3. When the juice boils, thicken it by stirring in some sago starch or potato starch, which has first been dissolved in cold juice, such that it does not lump.
4. The cream is covered with whipped cream and over it powdered sugar, and it is served with cream.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Frølår (Frog Legs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Frog legs
- Bread crumbs
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Frog legs can be fried in a pan with bread crumbs and eggs like oysters, but require longer cooking.
2. They can also be boiled or prepared in other ways.

title: Fugle-Postej (Game Bird Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- liver forcemeat
- 0.5 lbs. veal
- 1 lb. pork belly
- a fairly large calf liver
- game birds or pigeons
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Cover the mold with a liver forcemeat made of 0.5 lbs. veal, 1 lb. pork belly and a fairly large calf liver.
2. Half-roast the birds in a casserole and set aside the sauce.
3. Place the birds in the forcemeat and cover with a layer of it.
4. Bake the pie for 1 hour.
5. Serve with a strong sauce of meat broth without onion and small puff pastry bits or strips.
6. Game birds and pigeons are best suited for this pie.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fuglereder (Bird Nests)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- white cabbage
- butter
- sugar
- salt
- broth
- forcemeat
- small round flat baking forms
- water
- paper
- brown gravy sauce
- Madeira wine
- sago flour
- small fish balls
- salad leaves

## Directions

1. Shred white cabbage finely and brown it in butter with a little sugar and salt
2. Steam it tender in a little gravy
3. Butter small round flat baking forms and line them with forcemeat
4. Place in the oven in a roasting pan covered with boiling water and topped with paper brushed with butter
5. When the forcemeat is cooked, carefully remove from the forms
6. Arrange the cabbage inside in the form of a nest with 3 small fish balls in the center
7. Serve with a brown gravy sauce to which 1 glass of Madeira has been added and which is thickened with sago flour
8. Garnish with salad leaves between the bird nests

title: Fuglevildt i Sky (Game Birds in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- game birds
- soup or gravy for basting
- isinglass
- fresh cabbage leaves
- herb salad
- apple compote or apple puree

## Directions

1. During roasting, baste the birds with soup or gravy
2. From game birds, generally only the breast is used, which is sliced and placed in the mold
3. Prepare the aspic with isinglass
4. Cut fresh cabbage leaves in the form of shells and fill them with herb salad
5. Arrange the shells around the edge of the dish
6. Apple compote or apple puree can be served with the game
7. Use the remaining meat for game salad

title: Fuglevildt i Sky (Game Birds in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Game birds
- Broth or aspic
- Isinglass (for the aspic)
- Fresh cabbage leaves
- Herb salad
- Apple compote or apple puree

## Directions

1. During roasting, baste the birds with broth or aspic.
2. For game birds, use only the breast, which is sliced and arranged in the dish.
3. Prepare the aspic with isinglass.
4. Cut fresh cabbage leaves in the shape of scallop shells and fill them with herb salad.
5. Arrange the shells around the edge of the dish.
6. Serve apple compote or apple puree with the game.

title: Fyldt Hvidkaalshoved (Stuffed White Cabbage Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- One good, firm white cabbage head
- Ground beef
- Kidney, finely chopped
- Linen cloth or twine
- Butter
- Salt
- Water
- Melted butter or brown sauce

## Directions

1. Take a good, firm white cabbage head and remove the outer loose leaves. Cut off a lid the size of a saucer and hollow out the head. Fill with forcemeat of ground beef mixed with finely chopped kidney. Replace the lid and bind with a clean linen cloth or wrap with the removed large leaves and tie around it. Boil the head in about two hours in water with a little salt and butter. Before serving, drain the water from it. Serve with melted butter or brown sauce. To brown the head: after filling and binding with cooking twine, place it with a piece of butter in a pot with a tight lid to cook in its own juice over gentle heat until golden brown and tender, checking frequently so it does not burn. Place the head on a dish and remove the twine. Stir the sauce in the pot with a teaspoon of flour and pour over the head. The hollowed-out cabbage can be browned separately.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fyldte Løg (Stuffed Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- meat forcemeat
- brown butter
- flour
- pepper
- bouillon or gravy
- salt
- thread for binding

## Directions

1. Peel the onions and cut off a lid
2. Hollow out the onions
3. Fill with meat forcemeat
4. Replace the lids and bind the onions with thread
5. Cook either in meat broth or in salted water until tender
6. Remove the thread
7. Make a brown sauce from browned butter with a little flour and pepper, thinned with bouillon or gravy
8. Give the onions a boil in this sauce
9. Arrange with the sauce poured over
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Fyldte Selleriеr (Stuffed Celery Root)
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized celery roots
- Forcemeat
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Peel the roots and cut smooth, turn them into pear shapes
2. Cut out a round lid and hollow out the roots with a knife
3. Cook until a larding needle passes easily through them
4. Fill with forcemeat and replace the lid
5. Place closely together in a low pot and cook slowly with bouillon
6. Serve with their own sauce alongside meat dishes

title: Gaas eller And med hvid Sauce (Goose or Duck with White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose or duck
- Fat from the bird
- 2 to 3 spoonfuls of fat
- 2 tablespoons flour without heaping
- Mustard
- Cayenne
- Apples cut in 4 pieces
- White root vegetables
- Sliced almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Half-boil the goose while it is whole, then remove it and let it cool slightly
2. Chop it up on a board with a sharp knife and a hammer, first removing the legs and wings
3. Split the back and breast, and chop each of these parts into suitable pieces
4. Boil the soup when the chopped meat is returned to it; when it is tender, place it in a warm dish with a lid while the sauce is strained and prepared
5. Skim the fat well, take 2 to 3 spoonfuls and place on the bottom of the cleaned pot
6. Take 2 tablespoons of flour without heaping for a goose, stir it out in the fat and then fill it with the soup
7. If sour or sweet sauce is desired, it must be prepared in a tinned copper pot or enameled dish
8. Add mustard and a little cayenne, apples cut in 4 pieces, white root vegetables, and if desired, sliced almonds
9. If making the sauce without sweet and sour, make it stronger with seasonings, and use only root vegetables and meat balls
10. For duck, cook for 1.5 to 2 hours; for a whole goose, 2 to 3 hours or slightly less if very young
11. The bird should not be covered completely as it is better to spit-roast it; salt as you would other meat

title: Gaas i Karry (Goose Curry)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whole goose
- salt
- water
- curry sauce ingredients (as per Nr. 780)
- cooked rice

## Directions

1. Cut the goose into pieces and boil in water with salt and a whisk.
2. When tender, prepare the curry sauce as Nr. 780.
3. Place the meat in the sauce and serve in a ring formed of loose cooked rice, sprinkled with curry.

title: Gaase-Lever-Postej (Goose Liver Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh goose livers
- Veal liver, up to half the amount of goose liver if needed
- Salt
- Water
- Pork fat from the loin, equal amount to liver
- White pepper
- 1/2 cup Madeira
- Truffles, finely chopped
- Salt to taste
- Melted fat, half goose fat and half pork fat

## Directions

1. Wash the fresh goose livers in cold water
2. If there is not enough goose liver, up to half veal liver may be used
3. Cook the liver in salt and water until tender
4. Remove and pound finely in a mortar with an equal amount of pork fat from the loin
5. Press carefully through a sieve so that no membrane passes through
6. Add a little white pepper
7. Stir in 1/2 cup Madeira
8. Add finely chopped truffles
9. Mix everything according to taste with a little salt
10. Fill the mixture into small jars or terrines
11. Cover with melted fat, half goose fat and half pork fat
12. Place the lid on while still warm so it seals airtight
13. Store in a cool, airy place where it will keep for a long time
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gaase-Ragout (Goose Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose
- salt
- color (culinary coloring)
- butter
- flour
- water
- wine
- paprika
- boiled apple halves
- puff pastry strips

## Directions

1. Either boil or roast the goose whole, or cut it into smaller pieces.
2. After rinsing the pieces in cold water, place them over heat in just enough boiling salted water with color so that the sauce becomes more concentrated.
3. Brown a slice of butter and bake flour in it, then thin with the strained and skimmed cooking liquid.
4. Give the sauce color with culinary coloring and add wine and paprika to taste.
5. Bring the meat to a boil in the sauce, serve with the sauce poured over, and garnish with boiled apple halves and puff pastry strips.
6. If making ragout from leftovers of roast goose, use the leftover sauce from the roast along with other good stock, meat extract, or Maggi bouillon cubes.
7. The sauce can also be made with pickled cucumbers, pickles, truffles, mushrooms, olives, and chestnuts.

title: Gaase-Suppe (Goose Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 uncut goose, approximately 5 kg (10 lb)
- Cold water with salt
- Bunch of herbs
- 4 carrots
- 1 celery
- 4 leeks
- 10 whole peppercorns
- Flour dumplings (recipe No. 497)
- 1 kg (2 lb) good apples
- Sugar
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) washed prunes
- Maggis Bouillon cubes (optional)
- Vinegar and sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. After the goose is plucked, singed, cleaned, and freed from feather follicles, cut off the feet at the knee joint
2. Cut off the neck and wings, then cut up the goose and remove the innards
3. Rinse the goose thoroughly
4. Place in a pot with enough cold salted water to reach the level of the meat
5. Scald the feet well, cut off the claws, remove the outer skin, and add to the soup along with the neck, cleaned gizzard, and heart
6. Save the liver for liver pâté
7. Once the soup is well skimmed, add bunch of herbs, 4 carrots, 1 celery, 4 leeks, and 10 whole peppercorns
8. While the soup cooks, prepare flour dumplings (recipe No. 497)
9. Peel 1 kg (2 lb) good apples, cut into 4 pieces, remove the core, cook in water with sugar until tender but whole
10. Cook 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) rinsed prunes in the same water as the apples
11. When the vegetables are tender, remove them, cut into pieces, and place in the tureen with the apples, prunes, and flour dumplings (which should be cooked separately in salted water to keep the soup clear)
12. When the meat is tender, strain most of the soup off, leaving only a little in the pot to keep the goose warm
13. Skim the fat from the soup carefully and add the water in which the apples and prunes were cooked
14. If necessary, strengthen the soup with Maggis Bouillon cubes
15. Season with vinegar and sugar to taste, if desired
16. Serve the boiled goose garnished with parsley along with the soup and gizzards

title: Gaaseleverpostej (Goose Liver Pâté)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 to 10 goose livers of medium size
- small-cut truffles
- spices as in the preceding recipe, but only half as much clove
- finely chopped pork shoulder (optional, to lighten the color)

## Directions

1. Wash the livers well and inspect them carefully; cut away any green parts often found from the gallbladder, as the pâté would otherwise taste less good
2. Prepare the farce (forcemeat) as in recipe No. 259, with small-cut truffles and spices as mentioned, but only half as much clove
3. If using Danish goose livers, add some finely chopped pork shoulder to lighten the color, though this diminishes the good taste; select the lightest livers for this purpose
4. Bake the pâté as in recipe No. 259
5. Garnish the pâté with aspic (Sky)
6. Serve as a middle course with bread and butter

title: Gaasesteg (Roast Goose)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whole uncarved goose, about 5 to 5½ kg
- 5 to 6 peeled apples
- 125 grams scalded prunes, pitted
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- water
- Maggi bouillon cubes or meat extract
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the goose by carefully removing all feather follicles and singeing it over a gas or spirit flame.
2. Remove the head with neck, wings, feet, giblets, and heart for soup or ragout; reserve the liver for liver pâté.
3. Make a crosswise cut at the rump, large enough only to remove the innards.
4. Remove the giblets and place in cold salted water along with any fat picked from the intestines.
5. Wash the goose thoroughly in cold water, rub the inside with pepper and salt, and fill with the 5 to 6 apples cut into 4 pieces each, along with the 125 grams of scalded pitted prunes.
6. Sew the goose closed, rub the outside with salt, place in a roasting pan, and put in a hot oven for a few minutes before pouring a little boiling salted water over it.
7. To ensure crispy skin, baste the goose only at the beginning.
8. Roast for 2½ to 3 hours.
9. Half an hour before serving, strain the pan drippings through a sieve and return the goose to the oven with very little water in the pan.
10. For the sauce, brown butter, add flour and bake it, then thin with the strained pan drippings from which all fat has been carefully skimmed.
11. Season the sauce with color, salt, and Maggi bouillon cubes or meat extract, as the goose drippings are not very strong.
12. When serving, remove the thread and garnish the goose with parsley (and with Danish flags if Christmas goose).
13. Serve with roasted or boiled potatoes, red cabbage, asparagus, preserves, and the apples and prunes that were in the goose.

title: Gaaſeſteg (Roasted Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- One goose
- Apples and good prunes
- Or: Eggs, bread and milk
- Or: Salt, pepper and anchovies
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. The best filling for a goose is apples and good prunes, which gives it the most pleasant taste, but others prepare a dough of eggs, bread and milk and fill with that.
2. You can also simply rub it inside with salt and pepper and then place a couple of anchovies inside; these are consumed entirely during roasting, but they give the goose a fresh and piquant taste.
3. Set it in the oven with water over it, which soon becomes fat, with which the goose is often basted.
4. It requires two to three hours of roasting depending on the condition of the oven and the size of the goose.
5. For sauce, sharp red wine sauce can be used, but in general a brown sauce prepared in the usual manner is used.
6. The fat is well skimmed off before the sauce is made.

title: Garibaldi-Budding (Garibaldi Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pægle sweet cream
- 1½ pounds white sugar
- 1 vanilla stick
- 4 pounds butter
- 14 pounds flour
- sweet milk
- 2 tablespoons powdered bitter almonds
- 12 to 14 eggs
- brown sugar for glazing

## Directions

1. Boil 6 pægle sweet cream with 1½ pounds white sugar, 1 vanilla stick and 4 pounds butter.
2. Add 14 pounds flour, first mixed with a little sweet milk.
3. Stir the whole mixture over the fire until it releases from the pan and spoon, then remove and let cool.
4. Add 2 tablespoons powdered bitter almonds and 12 to 14 eggs, stirring well until it looks white.
5. Glaze a mold with brown sugar and fill with the mixture.
6. Place the mold in boiling water and cook the pudding for 2 hours.
7. Serve with Chantilly sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gelee med alleflags Frugter (Jelly with All Sorts of Fruits)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint lemon or citron jelly
- strawberries
- cherries
- raspberries

## Directions

1. Clarify a pint of lemon or citron jelly prepared as usual and pour it through flannel onto a dish.
2. Arrange all sorts of fruits on it, such as strawberries, cherries, raspberries, etc., in the most ornamental order one can manage.
3. Let the jelly set firmly.

title: Gelé af Kalvefødder (Calf's Foot Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's feet
- cold water

## Directions

1. Scald the calf's feet, clean them and wash them well and place them overnight in lukewarm water.
2. Then place them over heat with cold water; 4 calf's feet are boiled in 4 1/2 quarts water for approximately 3 hours.
3. Skim well, then strain through an earthenware or porcelain dish and set aside until it becomes cold.
4. If there is a layer of fat on top, it must be removed before using, as well as any sediment at the bottom.

title: Gelé til Fjerkræ og Fisk (Aspic for Poultry and Fish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 L good soup, either meat or fish
- a little vinegar
- whole white pepper
- salt
- a couple of bay leaves
- 12 leaves of isinglass, rinsed in cold water
- 2 to 3 cleaned and crushed eggshells, beaten together with egg whites
- tomatoes
- cucumbers
- lemon slices
- red and white carved beets
- parsley

## Directions

1. Add a little vinegar, whole white pepper, salt, and a couple of bay leaves to 1 L of good soup, either meat or fish
2. When it boils, add 12 leaves of isinglass that have first been rinsed in cold water, and 2 to 3 cleaned and crushed eggshells that have been beaten together with egg whites
3. Stir constantly with a whisk and bring the aspic to a boil again and let it stand for a few minutes on the heat so that it does not set but remains clear
4. Pour it either into an aspic bag or through a fairly tight cloth laid in a sieve, and whatever is used for this must be wrung out in boiling water before the aspic is poured into it
5. If the first of the aspic that runs through is not sufficiently clear, pour it back again, and this must sometimes be repeated a few times
6. Pour a thin layer of aspic into the bottom of the mold; when it is set, arrange figures over it made from tomatoes, cucumbers, lemon slices, or red and white carved beets, with parsley as garnish
7. Pour another thin layer of aspic over this, and only when this is again set, place the meat or fish in it
8. Pour some of the aspic over it, which during the entire preparation has been kept near enough to the heat so that it is not set but only kept lukewarm
9. Make the aspic the day before
10. At serving time, dip the mold in boiling water if it does not otherwise release
11. Any leftover aspic can be filled into small molds, which when turned out are placed around the larger mold, or can be chopped coarsely and laid in a ring around the dish

title: Gelé-Sylte (Jellied Pork Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- pork head, prepared and cooked as in recipe No. 262
- broth from cooking the head
- bay leaves
- vinegar
- whole peppercorns
- 6 to 8 isinglass sheets
- 2 to 3 beaten egg whites
- beet vinegar or fruit coloring for red coloring

## Directions

1. After the pork head is treated and cooked as described in the preceding recipe, set it aside to cool before cutting it into slices
2. Place some of the broth in which the head was cooked over the fire to reduce it
3. When the broth is strong enough for aspic, take 1 L (1 pot) of broth and add 2 bay leaves, vinegar to taste, some whole peppercorns, 6 to 8 sheets of isinglass, and 2 to 3 beaten egg whites to clarify the aspic (see recipe No. 247)
4. When clarified, color half of the aspic red with beet vinegar or fruit coloring
5. Pour a thin layer of the red aspic into the bottom of the mold where the aspic is to be placed
6. While this sets, keep the remaining aspic only liquid near the heat
7. Lay a layer of meat slices and pour the clear aspic over it, then alternate red and clear layers; but the previous layer must always be set before the next is laid on top
8. If there is more aspic, it can be finely chopped at serving time and placed on the platter around the turned-out aspic
9. Nearest to the aspic, place a wreath of parsley or salad leaves

title: Gelée af sammenblandet Hindbær og Ribs (Mixed Raspberry and Currant Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice from 5 pounds of red currants
- Juice from 2 pots of raspberries
- Powdered sugar (equal weight to the combined juice)

## Directions

1. Filter the raspberry juice and cook it with an equal weight of powdered sugar as the juice weighs
2. Otherwise the treatment is the usual (see recipe 474)
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gelée med Frugt (Jelly with Fruit)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wine jelly (Recipe 424)
- Small, preserved fruits without any juice, for example yellow cherries and strawberries

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of a mold with wine jelly (Recipe 424) to a finger's height and set to cool.
2. When the jelly is stiff, lay small, preserved fruits, without any juice, for example yellow cherries and strawberries in small piles on top of it.
3. Pour 2 fingers of the same kind of jelly over, and when this layer is stiff, lay fruits again, and thus continue until the mold is full.
4. Set it in a cold place, and when the jelly is stiff and is to be served, turn it out on a dish and lay preserved fruits around it.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Genfer-Kage (Geneva Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams butter
- 125 grams sugar
- 4 eggs
- 125 grams flour
- Cream filling
- vanilla
- water glaze
- browned, chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat butter and sugar into foam, add eggs and flour
2. Divide this mixture into 2 bases and bake in a greased layer cake form
3. Put them together with cream flavored with vanilla
4. Coat the cake with water glaze and sprinkle with browned, chopped almonds

title: Genfer-Kage (Geneva Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 122 grams (1/4 pound) butter
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) sugar
- 4 eggs
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) flour
- Cream (see recipe 1120)
- Vanilla
- Water glaze
- Roasted, chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Cream together the butter and sugar until fluffy
2. Add the eggs and flour to the mixture
3. Divide the batter into 2 portions and bake in a greased layer cake form
4. Once baked, layer them together with cream flavored with vanilla
5. Brush the cake with water glaze and sprinkle with roasted, chopped almonds that have been dried in the oven

title: Genferkage (Geneva Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds butter
- 20 eggs
- 1.5 pounds dried, sifted, pounded white sugar
- 1.5 pounds finely pounded sweet almonds
- 1 pound finest fine flour
- Grated lemon zest
- Jam or marmalade, preferably cherry
- White sugar
- Egg white
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Wash 1.5 pounds butter in cold water and set aside by the heat so it can be stirred
2. Stir it well with 20 eggs and 1.5 pounds dried, sifted, pounded white sugar, 1.5 pounds finely pounded sweet almonds, 1 pound finest fine flour, and a little grated lemon zest
3. Form rounds from white paper, brush with butter, and spread the batter to the thickness of a thick pancake on them
4. Bake until quite pale brown, remove from the paper with great care using a knife, and trim the edges
5. Let each layer cool separately, then layer them together with jam between them, preferably cherry
6. Pound the trimmings from the edges and stir into thick currant jelly, and use this to coat the edge of the cake all around
7. Glaze the top with white sugar and egg white mixed with the strained juice of 1 lemon, then set to dry
8. Bake in a tart pan, and do not trim the edges until the cake has been baked approximately a day, as it is very brittle
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gentleman's Favorite
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 egg yolks
- half a cup butter
- 2 cups white sugar
- 2 cups flour
- 2 tablespoons baking powder
- 2 tablespoons water
- half a teaspoon salt
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 7 egg yolks first with half a cup butter, 2 cups white sugar, 2 cups flour, 2 tablespoons baking powder, 2 tablespoons water, and half a teaspoon salt
2. Finally add the whipped egg whites
3. Bake in pie pans in a hot oven
4. For the filling: stir together 1 egg, 1 cup sugar, 3 grated apples, and 1 lemon until it boils and becomes thick, then cool before placing between each cake layer
5. When served, sprinkle with white sugar

title: Ginger-Bread (Ginger Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams powdered sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 15 grams ground ginger
- 125 grams margarine
- 375 grams cake flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 250 milliliters semi-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Mix powdered sugar with eggs, ground ginger, and margarine
2. Add cake flour and baking powder dissolved in semi-skimmed milk
3. Bake for 1 hour in a greased form

title: Gingerbread (Ingefær Brød)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Teaspoons Soda
- 1 Dessertspoon Ginger
- 2 Cups Molasses
- 4 well-beaten Eggs
- 1 Cup melted Butter
- 1 Cup sour Milk with 1 Teaspoon Soda dissolved in it
- Flour

## Directions

1. Sift the soda and ginger into the molasses
2. Stir for a long time
3. Add the well-beaten eggs
4. Add the melted butter
5. Add the sour milk with dissolved soda
6. Mix all together
7. Add flour until the consistency is like pound cake
8. Bake in 2 cakes

title: Gjer (Yeast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint hops
- 2 gallons water
- 2 level teaspoons salt
- 0.5 pint best powdered sugar
- 0.5 pint wheat flour
- 3 pounds cooked and mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Monday morning steep 1 pint hops in 2 gallons water for half an hour
2. Strain into a crock and let cool until lukewarm
3. Add 2 level teaspoons salt and 0.5 pint best powdered sugar
4. Stir 0.5 pint wheat flour into some of the liquid, pour in and stir all together well
5. Wednesday morning add 3 pounds cooked and mashed potatoes, stir well and let stand until Thursday
6. Then strain and put into crocks with the lid loose for a day or two
7. Should be made two weeks before it is used, as it improves with standing
8. Store in a cool place and shake the crock so the yeast does not settle

title: Gjer - Alternative Method (Yeast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces hops
- 4 quarts water
- 0.5 pound brown sugar
- salt
- 1 pound sifted wheat flour
- 3 pounds potatoes

## Directions

1. Steep 2 ounces hops 0.25 hour in 4 quarts water
2. Strain and let stand until lukewarm
3. Dissolve 0.5 pound brown sugar and salt in it
4. Gradually stir this mixture into 1 pound sifted wheat flour until even
5. Pour into a crock or deep vessel and let stand four days in a warm place to ferment
6. On the third day, cook, shell and mash potatoes (about 3 pounds), stir into the yeast and let stand one more day
7. Next day strain and put into bottles which must be stored in a cold place

title: Gjær-Pandekager (Yeast Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pots lukewarm milk
- 1 Tablespoonful yeast
- 2 Pounds barley or wheat flour

## Directions

1. Spread the lukewarm milk with a tablespoonful of yeast in a bowl.
2. Pour in 2 pounds barley or wheat flour.
3. Work the dough well through until it forms bubbles.
4. Set the covered dough on a warm place to rise.
5. When it has risen to one and a half times its original size, it is ready for baking.
6. Bake the pancakes in browned fat on the pan over gentle heat and turn to make them sufficiently brown on both sides.
7. Grease the pan when you turn them or put a new pancake on.
8. Do not put too much dough on at once, as it rises during baking.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gjær-Æbleskiver (Yeast Apple Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints lukewarm milk
- 2 Tablespoonfuls yeast
- A little powdered sugar
- Cardamom
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Pint melted butter
- Fine wheat flour

## Directions

1. Stir 2 tablespoonfuls yeast into 2 pints lukewarm milk.
2. Add a little powdered sugar, cardamom, 4 eggs, 1 pint melted butter, and so much fine wheat flour that the mixture has the thickness of gruel.
3. Set the dough on a warm place to rise.
4. Bake the æbleskiver light brown in butter.
5. Serve with powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Glaser-Sukker (Glace Sugar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg white
- 3 ounces powdered and sifted white sugar
- juice of 1 lemon or half lemon
- a little rose water

## Directions

1. Whisk the egg white until it forms a foam
2. Stir in 3 ounces of very good, pounded and sifted white sugar
3. If the mixture does not become as thick as a thin porridge, add more sugar, as otherwise it will not be able to cover the cake properly
4. Stir in the juice of a lemon or half a lemon and a little rose water
5. To ensure the cake to be glazed is even, rub it with flour before coating it with the sugar
6. Place it in a lukewarm oven until the glaze is dry
7. The glace sugar can be given a red color with a little cherry juice or currant juice if desired

title: Glaserede Hvideroer (Glazed White Roots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Sugar
- Broth, soup, or water with Maggi bouillon cubes
- Peeled white roots

## Directions

1. Brown a slice of butter together with a little sugar
2. Add a few tablespoons of broth, soup, or water with Maggi bouillon cubes
3. Cook this together, then place the peeled and rinsed roots in it to cook tender until the sauce has reduced and formed a glaze around the roots

title: Glaserede Hvideroer (Glazed White Turnips)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A slice of butter
- A little sugar
- A few tablespoons of gravy, soup or water
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Peeled and sliced white turnips

## Directions

1. Brown a slice of butter together with a little sugar
2. Add a few tablespoons of gravy, soup or water with Maggi Bouillon cubes
3. Boil this together before adding the peeled and sliced turnips
4. Cook until tender and the sauce has reduced and formed a glaze around the turnips

title: Glaserede Kastanier (Glazed Chestnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Chestnuts
- Butter
- Sugar
- Stock or water

## Directions

1. Scald them, boil a little, and peel off the shell and the brown skin. Brown butter and sugar together on a frying pan, and add the chestnuts, which must be shaken regularly to avoid burning. As soon as they begin to become glazed, add a little stock to the pan, in which they are steamed tender, until the liquid has reduced and formed a glaze around them. Use as garnish around meat dishes.

title: Glaserede Løg (Glazed Onions)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small Spanish onions or red onions
- Butter
- Sugar
- Broth, gravy, or water

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the small Spanish onions or red onions to make them easier to peel.
2. Brown butter and sugar in a pot, then add the onions, which must be shaken regularly so they do not burn.
3. When the sugar has adhered to the onions, add a little broth, gravy, or water.
4. Cook them until tender with regular shaking.
5. When they are tender and a glaze has formed around them, they are ready to serve.
6. Glazed onions are used as a garnish around many meat dishes, to which they taste excellent.

title: Glaserede Løg (Glazed Onions)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small Spanish onions or red onions
- Butter
- Sugar
- Stock, gravy, or water

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over small Spanish onions or red onions to make them easier to peel. Brown butter and sugar in a pot, and add the onions, which must be shaken regularly so they do not burn. When the sugar has adhered to the onions, add a little stock, gravy or water, and boil them tender with regular shaking.

title: Glaseret Bayonne-Skinke (Glazed Bayonne Ham)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A good, meaty, fresh ham of 5-6 kg (10-12 lb)
- 1 kg (2 lb) salt
- 60 grams (12 quintal) saltpeter
- ½ kg (1 lb) powdered sugar
- A few cloves
- Peppercorns
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 small onions
- 2 carrots
- 1 lemon, peeled and cut
- ½ liter (½ pint) good broth or soup
- 1 bottle red wine
- Breadcrumbs
- Powdered sugar
- Sago flour

## Directions

1. Remove the rind from a good, meaty, fresh ham of 5-6 kg and cut off the shank.
2. Place the ham on a trough or in a basin and rub it with a mixture of 1 kg salt, 60 grams saltpeter and ½ kg powdered sugar.
3. Let it stand for a week, but rub it daily with the brine that collects gradually.
4. Before roasting, wash it well and place it on the roasting pan with a few cloves, peppercorns, 1 bay leaf, 2 small onions, 2 carrots, 1 peeled and cut lemon, and ½ liter good broth or soup.
5. A ham of this size requires 3½ hours roasting and must, after being placed in the hot oven, be basted for the first two hours with 1 bottle of red wine and then sprinkled well with a mixture of breadcrumbs and powdered sugar.
6. If it does not become sufficiently crispy, sprinkle with more sugar.
7. Strain the broth, skim it and thicken it with sago flour.
8. Serve the ham hot with various vegetables or with macaroni, chestnuts, or purees.

title: Glasseret Skinke (Glazed Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh ham
- water
- salt
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the fresh ham in salted water.
2. When tender, remove the skin.
3. Polish it, sprinkle with white sugar and place in a warm oven to brown.
4. When the sugar has melted and looks glossy, it is done.
5. At serving, wrap crinkled paper around the shank.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Glutenbrød (Gluten Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound gluten flour
- 4 whole eggs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 2 teaspoons vinegar
- 3.5 measures boiled, cooled water
- 1 pound fine rye flour

## Directions

1. Work 1 pound gluten flour, 4 whole eggs, 1 tablespoon butter, 1 teaspoon salt and 2 teaspoons vinegar together.
2. Stir in 3.5 measures boiled, cooled water, adding it little by little.
3. Finally work in 1 pound fine rye flour.
4. Knead well until the dough releases from the hands.
5. Form 4 small, oblong loaves and bake at good, even heat for approximately 2 hours.
6. When removed from the oven, cover them and let them cool.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gode Raad (Good Advice Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- ½ pound butter
- 4 eggs
- ½ pound powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mix 1 pound flour, ½ pound butter, 4 eggs, and ½ pound powdered sugar together into a stiff dough
2. Place a small piece in the center of the iron and bake
3. Grease the iron for each cake
4. This makes 24 cakes

title: Gooseberries (Stikkelsbær)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pot berries
- 1 Pd. Melis
- 1/2 Pægl water
- whole cinnamon
- vanilla
- cognac

## Directions

1. Clean the berries well
2. Boil the Melis to syrup with water
3. Add the berries along with some whole cinnamon and vanilla
4. Boil gently with a couple of boilings for 1/2 hour
5. When removed from heat, add a little cognac before distributing into jars or glasses
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Graa Ærter (Gray Peas Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds gray peas
- Cold water
- Bouillon, broth, or ham stock
- 1/4 pound butter
- 3 tablespoons wheat flour
- Crushed cloves
- A couple of bay leaves
- Vinegar
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the peas and soak overnight in cold water
2. Place peas over heat with enough cold water to stand a couple of inches above them
3. Cook 2 to 3 hours under a lid, skimming well but without stirring
4. When almost tender, place a cast-iron pan over heat with 1/4 pound butter
5. Brown the butter to a light brown color
6. Add 3 tablespoons wheat flour and let it brown dark brown with the butter
7. Meanwhile, the soup should be cooked, strained, and fat skimmed
8. Place the browned flour in a pot and thin with soup until it becomes a smooth sauce
9. Add crushed cloves, a couple of bay leaves, vinegar, and sugar to taste
10. Bring to a boil
11. Drain the cooked peas in a sieve
12. Place peas in the tureen and pour the sauce over them
13. If the soup was made with smoked ham, serve the ham at the same time after removing the rind
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Graa Ærter (Gray Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 S) peas
- 2 bay leaves
- 100 gr (20 Kvint) butter
- 50 gr (10 Kvint) flour
- smoked pork or smoked pork head broth
- ground pepper
- coloring
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the peas the day before and cook them together with 2 bay leaves in the same water in which they have stood.
2. Brown 100 gr (20 Kvint) butter, and bake 50 gr (10 Kvint) flour in it, which is diluted with the smoked broth, from which the fat must be well skimmed.
3. Add the peas and bring to a boil.
4. Season the soup with ground pepper and coloring.
5. Add vinegar and sugar to taste.
6. The pork is eaten with the soup.

title: Graham-Mush (Graham Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- graham flour
- water
- salt
- cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Sift graham flour slowly into boiling, slightly salted water, and stir briskly until somewhat thick
2. Serve with cream and sugar

title: Granade af Kødfarce (Meat Farce Grenade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat farce
- Butter
- Stewed green vegetables
- Cream

## Directions

1. Place farce in an oval dish, either of sheet metal or tinned copper, greased with butter.
2. Make a long hole in the middle so that there is only about a hand's breadth thickness of farce around all sides.
3. Fill this hole with stewed green vegetables that should not be overcooked.
4. Spread farce about as thick as a finger over this and brush with butter, then place it in the oven on a plate to cook through.
5. The oven should have heat like for wheat bread.
6. Of all kinds of green vegetables, shelled green peas and white or green cabbage are the best, because they are improved by the meat flavor and do not spoil it with their taste. The peas should then be stewed with a little cream.

title: Green Corn (Green Corn)
body:
## Ingredients

- Corn ears
- Salt
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. After husking, place them in boiling water with salt and boil for about 3 hours.
2. Serve with melted butter.

title: Green Plums, Reine Claude (Grønne Blommer)
body:
## Ingredients

- fully developed but unripe green plums
- powdered sugar equal in weight to the plums
- half a measure of brandy per pound of original fruit weight

## Directions

1. The plums must be fully developed but not ripe.
2. After wiping the plums, cast them into boiling water but remove them as soon as they float on top and place in a sieve to drain.
3. Cook a thick sugar syrup with powdered sugar equal in weight to the plums.
4. Place the plums in the syrup, then immediately remove the pot from heat and let the plums rest in the liquid until the next day.
5. The next day, remove the plums and reduce the syrup until it becomes even, then pour over the plums and let cool.
6. Repeat this process on the third day and again on the fourth day.
7. On the fourth day, place the plums in the jar for storage and cook the syrup until it is even and thick, then set aside to cool.
8. When cold, stir half a measure of brandy per pound of the fruit's original weight into the syrup, adding it by spoonfuls with constant stirring in the same direction.
9. Pour the syrup over the plums and store in the usual manner.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Greens
body:
## Ingredients

- beet greens
- chicory
- cowslips
- dandelion
- sorrel
- horseradish tops
- turnip greens
- salt
- soda
- cold water

## Directions

1. Thoroughly clean the greens in several changes of cold water, after removing roots, dry stems and similar
2. Cook them in salted water with a little soda added
3. When they are tender, pour off the boiling water and dry them in a sieve, pressing the water out with a spoon or similar

title: Grevinders Kage (Countess's Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine flour
- 4 pounds butter
- 0.5 pound sugar
- 1 egg
- Jam or rhubarb compote

## Directions

1. Knead 1 pound fine flour, 4 pounds butter, 1/2 pound sugar, and 1 egg well together and roll out into a fairly thick sheet, cut to fit the pan
2. Roll together the remaining dough, twist it, and lay it as a wreath on the edge of the bottom
3. Bake until pale brown, then fill the inside space with jam or rhubarb compote
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Grillerede Kyllinger (Grilled Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Butter
- Beaten egg yolks
- Crushed rusks

## Directions

1. Divide the chicken as mentioned and half-cook it, but only the best pieces; the rest should be saved for soup
2. Brown butter very lightly in a pot
3. Coat the pieces, which can be lukewarm but not hot, with beaten egg yolks, dip in crushed rusks, and fry in the pot

title: Grilleret Kalvebryst (Grilled Veal Breast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked veal breast with bones removed
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs, crushed
- Butter or claret for browning

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the cooked veal meat.
2. Brush the meat with egg, sprinkle with crushed breadcrumbs, and brown in a pan with butter or claret.
3. Serve with browned butter, compote, and cucumber salad.
4. Vegetables can be served as puree, stewed, or simply boiled au naturel.

title: Grilleret Kalvesteg (Grilled Veal Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal roast, sliced medium thickness
- butter
- eggs, beaten
- crushed biscuits

## Directions

1. Cut cold veal roast into moderately thick slices
2. Warm these in butter that is very light brown, then remove them from the pan and drain off the butter
3. Brown fresh butter and dip the pieces in beaten eggs, then in crushed biscuits
4. Brown them in the fresh butter, and when they are turned, the first butter can be used to help
5. Serve with browned butter and greens

title: Grilleret Kjöd (Breaded Fried Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal or lamb meat, preferably from the breast
- Eggs
- Bread
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Butter
- Water

## Directions

1. Dip veal and lamb meat first in beaten eggs, then in breadcrumbs mixed with salt, pepper and parsley, and fry quickly in a pan.
2. Alternatively, instead of eggs you can dip the meat in the butter and water melted on the pan, sprinkle it with bread and fry it.
3. Serve with vegetables.

title: Grilleret Lammebryst (Grilled Lamb Breast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked lamb meat
- Egg
- Bread
- Butter

## Directions

1. Brush the cooked meat with egg, sprinkle with bread and brown in butter.
2. Serve with compote, salad or stewed vegetables.
3. Serve the butter in which the meat was browned in a sauce bowl.

title: Grilleret Lammehoved (Grilled Lamb's Head)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb's head
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg
- Bread
- Parsley
- Butter, lard or Palmin

## Directions

1. Split the head and remove the brain. Wash it well in several changes of water, then soak it at least 1 hour in cold water.
2. Place it along with the tongue over the fire in salted water. It must cook until the skin can easily be pulled off both the head and the tongue.
3. Pick it clean carefully and remove the palate; cut out the eyes and ears, but the fat behind the eye must remain.
4. Split the tongue and dip it together with the head in egg and bread mixed with salt, pepper and chopped parsley.
5. Brown it in a frying pan in butter, lard or Palmin.

title: Grilleret Oksebryst (Grilled Beef Brisket)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover beef brisket
- Water or soup
- Salt
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Flour
- Parsley, chopped
- Soup or stock
- Claret or Palmin
- Chutney or cucumber sauce (No. 759 and 756)
- Cooked rice or other mixed vegetables

## Directions

1. Give the leftover beef brisket a reheat in water with salt or in soup
2. Brush with egg
3. Sprinkle with a mixture of crushed bread, a little flour and chopped parsley
4. Place the meat in the oven in the roasting pan or on a frying pan
5. Pour a little soup or stock on the pan so it does not burn, or brown it on the frying pan in claret or Palmin
