6. As soon as it is browned, serve it with chutney or cucumber sauce (see No. 759 and 756) and cooked rice or other mixed vegetables

title: Grisefødder (Pig's Feet)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's feet
- salt
- whole pepper
- bay leaves

## Directions

1. Scald the pig's feet, scrape them, clean them carefully and place them over the fire in cold water with salt, whole pepper and a couple of bay leaves
2. The feet must cook for at least 3 to 4 hours under a lid to be properly tender
3. Remove them, split them lengthwise, arrange them on a platter and pour the strained broth over them
4. Serve with spices as for aspic
5. If served in aspic, simply add isinglass as in recipe No. 263
6. They can also be cooked, boned and used as calf's head for mock turtle

title: Gron Salat til Steg, No. 1 (Green Salad for Roast, No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salad heads
- White sugar
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Vinegar
- Water

## Directions

1. Pick through salad heads to find no bad leaves on them; wash them well and cut into 7 or 8 parts.
2. Rub white sugar into hard-boiled egg yolks, and a little vinegar in water is added.
3. Add the salad and it must stand some time before it is used.

title: Gron Sangkaal (Green Savoy Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Savoy cabbage inner leaves
- Water
- Soda
- Bouillon
- Butter
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Chestnuts or browned potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook the innermost and finest leaves of savoy cabbage nearly tender in a little water with a little soda; discard this water, and add a little bouillon, some butter—half of this can be fat from freshly made soup—nutmeg and a little salt, with which it is cooked tender. Cooked chestnuts or in their absence browned potatoes can be placed on top of the cabbage.

title: Gronne Wrier (Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- Butter
- Water
- Sugar
- Salt
- Flour
- Parsley

## Directions

1. If the peas are cooked by steam, then when they are tender, place them on the dish, where they are stirred with a good piece of butter and a little salt. If you do not use steam, you can cook them with sugar and very little water until they are tender; then stir a good piece of butter into them, and arrange them immediately. You can also cook them with a little more water and add a roux of plenty of butter and a little flour together with chopped parsley, salt and a little sugar.

title: Grovt Bred (Coarse Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart hot water
- Graham flour
- 2 cents compressed yeast
- fine wheat or rye flour
- 1 small cup molasses or brown sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. To 1 quart hot water add as much Graham flour as necessary to mix it into a thick dough, plus 2 cents compressed yeast.
2. Let it rise for the usual time.
3. Stir in as much fine wheat or rye flour (as desired) as can be kneaded, along with 1 small cup molasses or brown sugar (if preferred) and a little salt.
4. When well kneaded, set to warm for a few hours.
5. Shape into loaves and bake with good heat.

title: Græskar (kogt) med maitre d'hotel Smør (Boiled Pumpkin with Maitre d'hôtel Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and hollowed pumpkin
- salt
- lightly salted boiling water
- parsley or lettuce for garnish
- maitre d'hôtel butter

## Directions

1. Cut the peeled and hollowed pumpkin into long strips like asparagus
2. Strew them with salt and let them stand for 1 hour if possible
3. Rinse off the salt
4. Tie them into bundles and place on a rack
5. Lower the rack into a pot of boiling, lightly salted water
6. Cook for about 1 quarter hour until tender
7. Arrange the pumpkin on a napkin on a hot plate
8. Garnish with a little parsley or lettuce
9. Serve with maitre d'hôtel butter

title: Græskar (Pumpkins)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mature pumpkins
- Well water
- Brine

## Directions

1. Peel the pumpkins, hollow them out and cut into strips of the thickness of cucumbers and the length of a finger
2. Place in well water for 24 hours
3. Remove and place in brine for 24 hours
4. Place between two boards with a light press for a few hours so the brine can drain out
5. Dry thoroughly and treat and preserve as with cucumbers

title: Græskar i brun Champignon-Sauce (Pumpkin in Brown Mushroom Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and hollowed pumpkin
- strong brown sauce or water with Maggi bouillon cubes and salt
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) fresh cleaned mushrooms for 6 persons
- Sherry
- lemon juice
- sugar
- paprika
- potato starch for thickening

## Directions

1. Cut the peeled and hollowed pumpkin into small, slightly elongated pieces
2. Cook until tender in a strong brown sauce or in water with Maggi bouillon cubes and salt
3. Season the sauce with Sherry, lemon juice, a small amount of sugar, and paprika
4. Bring the mushrooms to a good boil in the sauce
5. Thicken the sauce very slightly with potato starch
6. Serve as a vegetable side dish with fried fish

title: Græskar i Citron-Sauce (Pumpkin in Lemon Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled pumpkin
- water or thin meat broth with salt
- piece of lemon zest
- egg yolks
- lemon juice
- finely chopped parsley
- cold butter

## Directions

1. Cut the peeled pumpkin into cubes
2. Cook these in water with salt or preferably in thin meat broth
3. Drain the pumpkin through a sieve
4. Use the cooking liquid to make a white, smooth sauce
5. Bring a small piece of lemon zest to a boil in the sauce to flavor it
6. Thicken the sauce with a couple of egg yolks
7. Give it a sour taste with lemon juice
8. Add finely chopped parsley and a slice of cold butter to the sauce
9. Allow the butter to melt before pouring the sauce over the hot cooked pumpkin
10. Remove the lemon zest before serving
11. Serve as a vegetable side dish with fried fish

title: Græskar i Mælke- eller Fløde-Sauce (Pumpkin in Milk or Cream Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkin, cut into very small pieces
- salt
- water
- sauce ingredients as for stewed potatoes
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Let the pumpkin pieces stand strewn with salt for 1 hour
2. Rinse the pumpkin
3. Place it in boiling water
4. As soon as it is cooked, drain it
5. Turn it into a sauce as prepared for stewed potatoes
6. Add chopped parsley to the sauce
7. Serve with salted meat dishes

title: Græskar i Peberrods-Sauce (Pumpkin in Horseradish Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkin, cut up
- thin meat broth or water with Maggi bouillon cubes
- butter
- flour
- blanched grated horseradish
- vinegar or lemon juice
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the cut-up pumpkin in thin meat broth or water with Maggi bouillon cubes
2. Use this broth for the sauce
3. Make a white sauce as usual for boiled meat
4. Bring a little blanched grated horseradish to a boil in the sauce
5. Add grated horseradish, vinegar or lemon juice, sugar, and salt
6. Warm the pumpkin in the sauce
7. Serve as a vegetable side dish with boiled meat

title: Græskar med Citronskal (Pumpkin with Lemon Peel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) peeled pumpkin
- 250 gr (1/2 lb) powdered loaf sugar
- 1.5 dl (1/3 pint) vinegar
- 2 lemons

## Directions

1. After the pumpkin is prepared as described in the general pumpkin recipe, boil it until clear in a syrup made from the vinegar and sugar together with finely sliced peel of 2 lemons.
2. Pour the pumpkin onto a colander to later blend with the strongly reduced syrup before filling it into the jars.
3. Seal as soon as it has cooled.

title: Græskar med Vanille (Pumpkin with Vanilla)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) peeled pumpkin
- 1/4 dl (1/4 pint) vinegar
- 1.5 dl (1/4 pint) water
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) powdered loaf sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil the vinegar, water and sugar into a syrup; add the pumpkin, cut in pieces as previously described.
2. Boil it, skim the syrup, and remove the pumpkin when the pieces are clear.
3. Reduce the syrup together with sliced vanilla, and only when it is boiled quite smooth, add the pumpkin to then cool in crocks or jars, which are sealed when the preserve has cooled.
4. Calculate 1 vanilla pod for 3 kg (6 lb) of pumpkin.

title: Græskar med Vanille (Pumpkin with Vanilla)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 dl vinegar
- 1/4 dl water
- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/2 kg peeled pumpkin
- 1 vanilla pod (for every 3 kg pumpkin)

## Directions

1. Boil vinegar, water, and sugar together to make a syrup.
2. Add the pumpkin cut into pieces as previously explained.
3. Boil it. Skim the liquid and remove the pumpkin pieces when they are translucent.
4. Boil the liquid together with the chopped vanilla until it reaches the right consistency.
5. Add the pumpkin back into the boiling liquid.
6. Pour into jars or glasses and tie them up when the preserve has cooled.

title: Græskar, paneret (Breaded Pumpkin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled, hollowed, and cooked pumpkin
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- butter or Palmin
- parsley for garnish
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked pumpkin into slices
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Dip in egg and breadcrumbs
4. Fry on a pan in butter or Palmin
5. Garnish with parsley
6. Serve with melted butter

title: Græskar-Kompot (Squash Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram (2 pund) squash
- 375 milliliters (1/4 pægel) vinegar
- 500 grams (1 pund) powdered sugar
- 75 grams (15 kvint) whole ginger

## Directions

1. Peel the squash, hollow it out, and cut it like melon.
2. Cook a syrup from the vinegar and sugar.
3. Cook the squash together with whole ginger wrapped in a clean cloth until the squash is clear.
4. Remove the fruit and reduce the syrup until it is even, then pour it over.

title: Græskar-Marmelade (Squash Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 kg powdered sugar for 1 kg peeled squash
- 2 lemons
- 50 grams powdered ginger

## Directions

1. Cut the mature, hollowed, and peeled squash into small pieces and cook for 1/2 hour without any additions
2. Add the sugar, juice of 2 lemons, and powdered ginger
3. Cook the jam under constant watch for about 2 hours until ready

title: Græskar-Suppe (Squash Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg (3 lb) squash
- 4 L (4 pots) soup
- A bunch of herbs
- Yellow and white root vegetables
- 5-6 egg yolks
- Salt
- Ground white pepper
- Sliced cooked leeks, meat balls, or baked French bread cubes (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the ripe squash, remove seeds, and cut into small pieces.
2. Cook together with a bunch of herbs and some yellow and white root vegetables in some of the soup.
3. When vegetables are tender, remove and cut into pieces; place in tureen.
4. Remove the bunch of herbs and press well.
5. Pass the cooked squash through a coarse sieve by pressing with the remaining soup.
6. Bring to a boil.
7. Bind with 5-6 beaten egg yolks.
8. Season with salt and ground white pepper and arrange immediately.
9. In addition to the vegetables, add sliced cooked leeks, meat balls, or baked French bread cubes to the soup if desired.
10. Alternatively, the soup may be thickened with butter and flour baked together and diluted with the strained soup, in which case the eggs may be omitted.

title: Grøn Langkaal (Green Kale)
body:
## Ingredients

- Green kale
- Water
- Broth
- Butter
- Potatoes, browned

## Directions

1. Use only the innermost, fine, curled leaves. Rinse well in cold water and cook half-tender in a little water. Drain this water, pour cold water over the kale, and when drained in a colander, cook tender in a little broth and butter. Arrange with browned potatoes on top.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Grøn Salat til Steg, No. 1 (Green Salad for Roast, No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salad heads
- White sugar
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Vinegar
- Water

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves from the salad heads and wash them thoroughly; cut them into 7 to 8 pieces.
2. Rub white sugar into hard-boiled egg yolks, and add a little vinegar mixed with water.
3. Add the salad and let it stand for some time before using.

title: Grøn-Langkaal (Green Long Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Long cabbage leaves
- Water
- Salt
- Soda
- Butter
- Flour
- Cream or good milk
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves and rinse them well.
2. Cook them in water with salt and a small amount of soda.
3. When the cabbage is tender, drain it through a colander and press out the water well.
4. Chop it very finely.
5. Bake butter and a small amount of flour together and dilute with cream or good milk.
6. Add the chopped cabbage and bring to a boil.
7. Season with sugar and salt.
8. Finish with a piece of cold butter.
9. Serve in a ragout dish with a wreath of glazed chestnuts or browned potatoes arranged around the cabbage.

title: Grøn-Ærter, Stuvede (Stewed Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- shelled green peas
- water
- butter
- sugar
- flour
- parsley
- salt
- young carrots (optional)

## Directions

1. The shelled green peas, which should not be too large, are cooked tender in water with generous butter and a little sugar
2. Add the peas only when the water boils
3. When tender, thicken with a little flour, chopped parsley and salt
4. A good flavor and economy with sugar is gained by mixing the peas with some slices of young carrots
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Grøncerte-Suppe (Green Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat stock made from beef or mutton
- dried peas
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- nutmeg
- cayenne pepper
- young carrots (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook ordinary meat stock from beef or mutton and take as many dried peas as you judge will make the soup suitably thick; it should be like yellow peas that are somewhat thin
2. Since peas cook slowly in strong stock, especially when it is smooth, it is best to strain off some stock before the meat is completely cooked, mix it with water and let the peas cook longer in it
3. When the meat is cooked, strain off the second soup; dry the pot and place it empty on the fire; melt so much butter in it that you can smooth out an almost spoonful of flour in it, and when it is smoothed out, thin it first with water, then with stock
4. When all the soup has come in and boils, add fine parsley together with the peas and their soup, nutmeg and a little cayenne
5. If you wish to have young carrots, cook them whole with the peas
6. You can make this soup thinner with peas and with less butter and add meat balls and carrots

title: Grøngrte-Suppe uden Kjød (Vegetable Soup without Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and rinsed peas
- 4 quarts water per bushel of medium-sized peas
- a good and large bunch of herbs
- many carrot and parsley roots
- approximately 23 ounces flour
- 3 ounces butter
- salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- purslane or parsley

## Directions

1. Place the peeled and rinsed peas on the fire with 4 quarts water to a bushel of medium-sized peas
2. When they boil, add a good and large bunch of herbs and many carrot and parsley roots
3. When these are done, take them out and cut into smaller pieces, and press the bunch well
4. Mix approximately 23 ounces flour and 3 ounces butter into a roux, which is thinned with the soup, and cook it together with salt, 1 tablespoon sugar, purslane or parsley

title: Grønkaal (Kale)
body:
## Ingredients

- Grønkaal (kale)
- Saltvand (salt water)
- 3 Spiseskefulde Hvedemel (3 tablespoons wheat flour)
- 5 Potter Kjød- eller Saltmadssuppe (5 pots of meat or salt meat broth)
- Nogle pillede Løg (some peeled onions)
- Raaskrællede, kogte og ituskaarne Kartofler (raw peeled, cooked, and cut potatoes)
- Boller (meatballs), optional

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the kale with salt water and let it sit for a few hours so that any insects fall off.
2. Pick the good leaves from the stalk and rinse well in cold water.
3. If desired (especially in autumn when kale has a strong taste), blanch the leaves: bring to a boil with water, drain in a colander, and rinse with cold water until completely drained.
4. Finely chop the kale with 3 tablespoons of wheat flour.
5. Heat 5 pots of meat or salt meat broth.
6. When the broth boils, gradually thin the kale with a little of it until properly diluted.
7. Pour the kale into the boiling broth along with some peeled onions.
8. Cook for about 10 minutes.
9. Before serving, add raw peeled, cooked, and cut potatoes.
10. If desired, add meatballs as you would for meat soup.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Grønkaal-Suppe (Green Cabbage Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5-2 kg (3-4 lb) salted pork belly or brisket
- 4 heads green cabbage

## Directions

1. The soup for green cabbage is generally cooked in salted, striped pork belly or brisket.
2. While this cooks, remove the cabbage from the stems; rinse it well, squeeze out the water, then chop it very finely.
3. When the pork is cooked, skim the fat from the soup, and in this fat bake flour, which is thinned with the soup as in the previous recipes.
4. Alternatively, chop a couple of spoonfuls of flour together with the cabbage, which is added to the soup and cooked for several minutes.
5. The pork is served either cold or hot with the cabbage.

title: Grønne Blommer med Rom (Green Plums with Rum)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 lb) green plums
- 250 gr (1/2 lb) powdered loaf sugar
- white rum

## Directions

1. Dry the plums and cut off the outer part of the stems before placing them in glass jars or crocks.
2. Sprinkle them with sugar and pour white rum over them.
3. Seal the jars with a bladder in which several holes are pricked with a needle, then wrap the jars with straw.
4. Place them over heat in a pot with cold water and let them boil for 1 quarter hour.
5. Leave them standing in the water until they are approximately cold.

title: Grønne Blommer til Kompot (Green Plums for Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1.5 dl water for 0.5 kg fruit

## Directions

1. The not fully ripe, green plums can be treated as prunes and cooked like these either whole or split.

title: Grønne Tomater (Green Tomatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg (1 S) crushed powdered sugar
- 3/4 dl (1/4 Pægl) vinegar
- 1/2 kg (1 S) green tomatoes
- Vinegar and water in equal parts
- Vanilla bean or cinnamon stick and whole cloves

## Directions

1. Select small, oblong green tomatoes
2. Cook them in a mixture of equal parts vinegar and water until the skin can be removed
3. Boil sugar and vinegar together to make a syrup
4. Cook the tomatoes in this syrup for about 3 to 4 minutes, then pour the fruit and liquid into a crock
5. The following day, pour everything into the pot and give it one more boil, then remove the tomatoes with a slotted spoon and place in the crock
6. Let the syrup boil until very thick and pour it boiling over the tomatoes
7. The syrup can be cooked either with a vanilla bean for 2 1/2 to 3 kg (5 to 6 S) tomatoes or with a few pieces of whole cinnamon and 1/2 dozen whole cloves, according to taste
8. Seal immediately

title: Grønne Ærter (Preserved Green Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Green peas
- Salt

## Directions

1. Coarse peas are generally used for salting.
2. As they are shelled, place them in layers with salt in jars, and as mentioned for beans, place a piece of linen cloth on top, then a perforated wooden bottom, and finally a light press.
3. The brine should preferably cover the peas.
4. Rinse before cooking in water with a little sugar.
5. Suitable for example in Pea Purée (see the Cookbook).

title: Grønne Ærter 1 (Green Peas 1)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Good, carefully shelled peas
- Salt
- Natron (baking soda)
- Cold water
- 10 grams (2 quintals) salt to 1 liter (1 pot) water
- A small amount of powdered sugar (Melis)

## Directions

1. Rinse the shelled peas in cold water
2. Boil them for 3 minutes in boiling water with a little salt and natron
3. Drain the water from them and rinse the peas in cold water
4. Pack them in jars and cover with a cold brine made of 10 gr (2 quintals) salt to 1 l (1 pot) water with a small amount of powdered sugar
5. Close the jars with ring and clamp and boil in water for 1/2 hour
6. The peas must be boiled in the jars 2 times with 1 day in between; after each time they must be cooled in the water in which they were boiled, or covered with a woolen blanket

title: Grønne Ærter 2 (Green Peas 2)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- Natron (baking soda)
- A little salt
- Water where peas were cooked
- Cold water
- A small amount of powdered sugar (Melis)

## Directions

1. Shell the peas and boil them until almost tender in water with natron and a little salt
2. Drain the water from them and pour cold water over them
3. Fill the peas into jars
4. Take the water in which the peas were boiled, add a small amount of powdered sugar, and pour it over the peas
5. Close and boil the jars
6. Cool as usual
7. These peas must also be boiled several times as described in recipe 228

title: Grønne Ærter I (Green Peas I)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Good, carefully shelled peas
- Cold water
- Boiling water
- Salt
- Soda
- Cold brine

## Directions

1. Rinse good, carefully shelled peas in cold water and give them a 3 minute boiling in boiling water with a little salt and soda
2. Drain the water from them and rinse the peas in cold water
3. Pack in jars and pour over with a cold brine, 10 gr (2 Kvint) salt to 1 l (1 Pot) water

title: Grønne Ærter kogt paa Flasker (Green Peas Boiled in Bottles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Carefully shelled fresh peas
- Hay

## Directions

1. Put the peas in completely dry bottles
2. Wrap them with hay
3. Boil for approximately 3 quarters of an hour or place in a steam pot
4. Cork with new corks
5. Cool the bottles in the pot or under a blanket

title: Grønne Ærter konserverede i koldt Vand paa Flasker (Green Peas Preserved in Cold Water in Bottles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Shelled fresh peas
- Cold water

## Directions

1. Fill the peas into completely clean bottles
2. Cover with cold water
3. Cork with new corks
4. Store the bottles in sand in a cool place, preferably in a cellar that is not damp

title: Grønne Ærter med Salt (Green Peas with Salt)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine, good peas
- Fine salt

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the peas with a little fine salt, let stand until the next day and then fill into dry bottles, which are corked and placed in a dry spot
2. When they are to be used, cook them a little in plenty of water, which is poured off completely, and they are cooked tender in scant water, in which they are stewed

title: Grønt Smør (Green Cheese Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- grated green cheese

## Directions

1. When the butter is stirred smooth, add grated green cheese according to taste
2. Spread on hot toasted French bread or on crackers and serve at breakfast

title: Grøntsuppe (Green Vegetable Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Potter tynd Kjødsuppe (5 pots of thin meat broth)
- Et stort Hvidkaalshoved (a large head of white cabbage)
- Unge, rensede Supperødder (young, cleaned soup vegetables)
- Nogle Porrer (some leeks)
- En Suppevisk (a bunch of herbs)
- 2 Pægle unge, pillede Grønærter (2 pecks of young, shelled peas)
- 1 Pund ituskaarne Asparges (1 pound of cut-up asparagus)
- Lidt itupillet Blomkaal (some separated cauliflower)

## Directions

1. Bring 5 pots of thin meat broth to a boil.
2. Add strips of a large cabbage head along with young cleaned soup vegetables cut into strips, some leeks, and a bunch of herbs.
3. When the vegetables are half tender, add 2 pecks of young shelled peas, 1 pound of cut-up asparagus, and some separated cauliflower.
4. Cook until all the vegetables are tender.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Grønærte-Gratin (Green Pea Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Stuvede Ærter
- Hakket Persille
- Smør
- Mel
- Kogende Mælk
- Æg
- Salt
- Melis
- Eventuelt Rest Tunge, Skinke eller salt Kød

## Directions

1. Stuvede Ærter, hvori er rørt hakket Persille, lægges på Bunden af et Gratinfad.
2. Er der en Rest Tunge, Skinke eller salt Kød, kan den skæres småt eller hakkes groft og blandes i Ærterne.
3. Dejg som i Nr. 62 hældes over Ærterne.
4. Bages omkring 1 Time.

title: Grønærte-Suppe (Green Pea Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 handfuls peas
- 5 L (5 pots) soup
- Parsley
- Water
- Salt
- 100 grams (20 quintal) butter
- 50 grams (10 quintal) flour
- Small carrots
- Fine chopped parsley
- Small whole ripe tomatoes (optional)

## Directions

1. Shell the peas and cook tender with a bunch of parsley in salted water.
2. Reserve some whole peas for serving; strain the rest through a coarse sieve.
3. Brown 100 grams butter and 50 grams flour together and dilute with 5 L (5 pots) soup.
4. When boiling, add the pea purée and bring to a boil.
5. Cut small carrots that have been cooked in some of the soup and add to the soup with whole peas and finely chopped parsley.
6. If using coarser peas, sort during shelling and cook the finer ones separately in salted, sweetened water, adding whole to the tureen while the others are pressed through.
7. Season with salt and sugar.
8. Serve with toasted bread. Small whole ripe tomatoes may be added to the soup.

title: Grønærte-Suppe (Green Pea Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) dried green Dutch peas
- 4 to 5 pots good white meat broth
- 4 carrots
- 1 bunch

## Directions

1. Soak the peas the evening before.
2. Cook them in water and press through a sieve when they are tender.
3. Dilute the purée with 4 to 5 pots good white meat broth, in which 4 carrots and 1 bunch have been cooked.
4. Bring the soup to a boil.
5. Serve with the carved carrots or with small meat balls.

title: Grønærte-Suppe à la Reine (Green Pea Soup à la Reine)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 handfuls peas
- 5 L (5 pots) chicken stock
- 2 chickens
- 100 grams (20 quintal) butter
- 50 grams (10 quintal) flour
- Salt
- Sugar
- Fine chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare pea purée as in recipe 394.
2. Cook 2 chickens tender.
3. Pick meat from bones and cut into small, even pieces; keep warm until serving.
4. Brown 100 grams butter and 50 grams flour and dilute with chicken stock, adding the pea purée and bringing to a boil.
5. Season with salt, sugar, and finely chopped parsley.
6. Pour into tureen over the small cut chicken filets.

title: Gul Gelée (Yellow Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot Wine Jelly (Nr. 424)
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Set the Wine Jelly over heat
2. Whisk in the egg yolks, and while constantly whisking, bring to a boil
3. Remove from heat and continue whisking the jelly until cold
4. Pour into a glass bowl and set in a cold place until the jelly becomes stiff
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gulas (Goulash)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good quality beef, cut into cubes of nearly one inch
- Bacon, weighing one quarter of the beef weight
- Onions, weighing half the bacon weight
- Ground pepper
- Ground caraway
- Salt
- Broth
- Red wine

## Directions

1. Cut beef into small square cubes of nearly one inch.
2. Cut bacon into slightly smaller cubes.
3. Cut onions into pieces.
4. Place the bacon in a casserole and fry it.
5. When the bacon begins to brown and becomes crisp, add the onions.
6. When the onions are also brown and tender, add the beef.
7. After the beef has fried for 15 minutes, pour over enough broth and red wine to cover it.
8. Add ground pepper, ground caraway, and salt.
9. Boil the goulash well covered until the meat is tender and the broth is nearly evaporated.
10. Serve with mashed potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gulasch (Hungarian Goulash)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg veal, cut into 3-centimeter (1-inch) cubes, per person
- Butter or margarine
- Onions, chopped
- Flour
- Salt
- Pepper
- Stock, broth, or water
- Soya
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Tomato paste
- Paprika
- Potato puree
- Pickled cucumbers or pickles

## Directions

1. Heat a good portion of butter or margarine in a pot and brown it together with some chopped onions.
2. Add the meat, which has been tossed in flour mixed with salt and pepper.
3. Brown the meat.
4. Pour in enough stock, broth, or water so that it just reaches the level of the meat.
5. Add soya, Maggi bouillon cubes, several spoonfuls of tomato paste, and paprika.
6. Cook covered until the meat is tender. The sauce will usually be sufficiently thickened by this point.
7. Arrange the goulash with potato puree either placed in the middle of the dish or as a border around the meat.
8. The border can be decorated with chopped pickles or pickled cucumbers.

title: Gulaseh (Goulash)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover cooked meat or roast
- Onions, chopped
- Butter
- Flour
- Good stock or soup
- Paprika
- Salt
- Tomato puree
- Culinary color
- Potato puree

## Directions

1. Brown some chopped onions in butter
2. Brown flour in this mixture
3. Thin with good stock or soup
4. Season with paprika, salt, tomato puree and culinary color
5. Add the meat to the sauce and let it cook well
6. Arrange potato puree in a mound in the center of the dish
7. Place the goulash around this mound

title: Gule Ærter (Yellow Peas Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Approximately 1/2 pot or 1/4 pound peas per person
- Cold water
- Peeled small onions
- 8 pounds meat or salt meat
- 5 pots water
- Celery, parsley roots, whole peeled onions
- Peeled apple pieces
- Chopped thyme (optional)
- Toasted bread cubes
- Salt

## Directions

1. The evening before cooking, remove damaged peas and soak overnight in cold water
2. Place peas in a pot with enough cold water to stand about 4 inches above them
3. Add peeled small onions but no salt
4. Cook uncovered over moderate heat until peas begin to shed their shells, skimming but being careful not to remove the onions
5. When peas are soft enough to mash, stir with a flat spoon until they become like porridge
6. Place 8 pounds meat or salt meat over heat with 5 pots water
7. Boil, skim, and strain the broth as usual
8. Thin the peas with the broth until they reach the consistency of gruel, adding boiling water if needed
9. Bring to a good boil with constant stirring to prevent scorching
10. Serve as hot as possible with the meat
11. For strained yellow peas, press through a sieve and thin with broth until smooth, then cook in a pot with chopped celery, parsley roots, whole peeled onions, and peeled apple pieces
12. Cook over gentle heat with constant stirring until roots are tender
13. Serve as hot as possible with the meat and toasted bread cubes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gule Ærter (Yellow Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 S) peas
- 1/2 kg (3 S) lightly salted pork
- 1 bunch
- 2 celeries
- 5 leeks

## Directions

1. Soak the peas in lukewarm water and let them stand until the next day.
2. Cook them in 3 L (3 pots) water without salt.
3. Cook the pork separately together with a good bunch, 2 celeries and 5 leeks.
4. When the pork is tender, remove it and keep it warm, while the peas are pressed through a sieve with continued dilution with the broth, from which the fat has first been skimmed.
5. Cut the celeries and leeks into small pieces and add them to the peas, which must be served very hot.
6. The pork is eaten with the peas.

title: Gule Ørter (Yellow Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried yellow peas
- water
- a small piece of soda
- yellow carrot (optional)
- large onion
- parsley roots
- celery
- meat or sausage broth (optional)
- rolled sausage (optional)
- small peeled onions
- salt
- butter
- baked bread or meat balls

## Directions

1. Shell and rinse the peas and soak overnight in cold, soft water or water with a small piece of soda about the size of a large pea
2. The next day boil them in the same water, which should stand well above them when placed on the heat
3. Cooking a yellow carrot with them gives the peas a good color, but it can also be grated and added later
4. When they are skimmed and the clear shells that rise are removed, add a large bunch, whole parsley roots and celery, which are removed again when soft, rinsed, cut into pieces and placed in the terrine
5. When the peas are cooked soft, mash them and rub through a sieve with fresh or salt broth, if available, until the shells are removed
6. Since broth is not always on hand, one usually boils a small piece of meat or meat with the peas, and if desired, a rolled sausage, whose preparation will be explained later, and then the peas must be rubbed through with water
7. Set the finished peas back on the heat with the roots, some small peeled onions, and a little salt
8. At serving, stir a piece of butter into them
9. Serve either with baked bread or small meat balls, which are then boiled in the finished peas

title: Gulerod-Suppe (Carrot Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 large whole carrots
- Broth
- Rice grains

## Directions

1. Cook 8 large whole carrots tender in the broth, mash them and press them through a colander with the broth, which must be strained. Return to the fire with rice grains and cook.

title: Gulerod-Suppe (Carrot Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 carrots
- 3 onions
- 5 L (5 pots) broth
- a good whisk
- salt
- ground white pepper
- 100 grams (20 skein) butter
- 100 grams (20 skein) flour
- bread croutons (no. 500)

## Directions

1. After the carrots are scraped, rinsed and sliced together with the onions, place them over heat with 2 L (2 pots) of the broth, a good whisk, and salt and ground white pepper.
2. Cook them until tender so that they are easily rubbed through a coarse sieve.
3. Brown 100 grams (20 skein) butter and bake 100 grams (20 skein) flour, and thin with the sieved purée, which is brought to a boil.
4. Serve the soup with bread croutons (no. 500).

title: Gulerod-Suppe (Carrot Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 carrots
- 3 onions
- 5 L (5 pots) soup
- 2 L (2 pots) soup
- A good bunch of herbs
- Salt
- Ground white pepper
- 100 grams (20 quintal) butter
- 100 grams (20 quintal) flour
- Bread croutons

## Directions

1. Scrape, rinse, and slice the carrots along with the onions.
2. Place over heat with 2 L (2 pots) of the soup, a good bunch of herbs, salt, and ground white pepper.
3. Cook until tender enough to easily pass through a coarse sieve.
4. Brown 100 grams butter and 100 grams flour together and dilute with the strained purée.
5. Bring to a boil.
6. Serve with bread croutons.

title: Gulerod-Suppe (Carrot Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 large whole carrots
- meat stock
- 1/2 pint rice flour
- white root vegetables, a large bunch
- butter
- 2 tablespoons finely chopped carrots
- 1 tablespoon onion

## Directions

1. Cook 8 large whole carrots in the stock, mash them and press through a colander together with the stock, which should be strained, then place it back on the fire with 1/2 pint rice flour and cook until it has evened the soup
2. Serve with toasted bread or small meat balls
3. Alternatively, brown a piece of butter in the pot, add 2 tablespoons full of finely chopped carrots and 1 tablespoon onion and sauté well until light brown; then gradually add stock and let it cook evenly until the carrots can be pressed apart; then press it through a colander with more stock, finally through a sieve and give it a boil with cut white root vegetables and small meat balls

title: Gulerod-Suppe à la Stanley (Carrot Soup à la Stanley)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ingredients as for carrot soup (no. 398)
- shelled, cooked green peas
- small meat balls
- finely chopped parsley or chervil

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as the preceding recipe; only serve this soup with shelled, cooked green peas, small meat balls and finely chopped parsley or chervil.

title: Gulerods-Marmelade (Carrot Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/3 kg powdered sugar for 1/4 kg scraped carrots
- 4 lemons
- Peel of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots, rinse them, and cut into thin slices
2. Cook tender in very little water and press through a puree sieve or food mill
3. Cook sugar, juice of 4 lemons, and finely cut peel of 2 lemons together with the puree until the jam is sufficiently smooth
4. Fill into glasses, cover with paper, and seal with parchment paper

title: Gulerødder (Candied Carrots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- juice of 3 lemons
- zest of 1 lemon
- 1/2 kg carrots, cut into pieces
- herb water (1/4 liter)

## Directions

1. Scrape and rinse the carrots, weigh them and cut into thin slices.
2. Cut the slices into fine strips.
3. Cut the lemon zest very finely.
4. Cook the lemon zest under a lid together with the carrots in enough water to barely cover them for about 15 minutes.
5. Drain the carrots through a sieve.
6. Boil 1/2 kg sugar and 1/4 liter of herb water together with the juice of 3 lemons to a thin syrup.
7. Add the carrots and boil for 5-6 minutes.
8. Drain the carrots again through a sieve.
9. Boil the syrup until it is very smooth to avoid having to boil it multiple times.
10. Add the carrots directly into the boiling syrup and mix well.
11. Pour into glasses and tie them up as soon as the preserve is ready.

title: Gulerødder (Carrots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Lightly salted water
- Cold water
- Cold brine

## Directions

1. Use preferably carrots
2. Scrape them, rinse and cook in lightly salted water for 5 minutes
3. Take out the carrots, cool in cold water
4. Then place them in jars and pour cold brine over them
5. Close the jars hermetically with rubber ring and clamp, then boil for 30 to 35 minutes and cool
6. A little icing sugar can be added to the brine

title: Gulerødder i Sky-Sauce (Carrots in Gravy Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- raw scraped carrots
- butter
- sugar
- gravy or water with Maggi bouillon cubes
- salt
- coloring
- potato starch

## Directions

1. Cut the raw scraped carrots into not too thin slices
2. Braise them in a little browned butter and sugar on the bottom of a pot
3. Pour over them a little gravy or water with Maggi bouillon cubes
4. Add salt and coloring, but do not add more water than absolutely necessary
5. When the carrots are tender from gentle cooking, thicken the sauce with a small amount of potato starch
6. Season the vegetable with what it still needs of salt and sugar

title: Gulerødder å la maitre d'hotel (Carrots à la Maître d'hôtel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- butter
- flour
- vegetable cooking water
- egg yolks
- lemon juice
- chopped parsley
- cold butter
- Maggi seasoning

## Directions

1. Wash the carrots and cook them
2. Scrape and cut them preferably into slices with a fluted vegetable knife
3. Make a sauce of butter and flour, diluted with the vegetable cooking water
4. Thicken with egg yolks, 1 or 2 depending on portion size
5. Add lemon juice, chopped parsley, a piece of cold butter, and a few drops of Maggi seasoning
6. Serve with fried and boiled meat dishes

title: Gulerødder, glaserede (Glazed Carrots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked large carrots
- butter
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. If small carrots are not available, cut cooked large carrots into slices with a fluted vegetable knife
2. On a pan, brown a little butter
3. Add a small amount of sugar and salt
4. Toss the carrot slices in this mixture
5. Serve with boiled and fried meat dishes

title: Gulerødder, kogte (Boiled Carrots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- water
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Scrape and rinse the carrots
2. Place them in boiling salted water
3. Cook until tender
4. Serve with butter as for pumpkin

title: Gulerødder, stuvede (Stewed Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Meat broth
- Butter (3 ounces per plateful)
- Flour (4 ounces per plateful)
- Parsley, chopped
- Salt
- Sugar
- Young green peas (optional)

## Directions

1. Choose carrots with a nice red color and truncated roots (typically called Carrots). Clean and scrape the carrots, cut into small dice. For each plateful, use 3 ounces butter and 4 ounces flour. Stew in meat broth that is thickened with flour and butter. When tender, add some chopped parsley and salt and sugar to taste. When stewed together with young green peas, they are well suited to salt dishes in summer.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Gær-Vafler (Yeast Waffles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 Q) flour
- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) melted margarine
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 good tablespoon yeast
- lukewarm milk

## Directions

1. Mix into 1/2 kg (1 Q) flour, 1/4 kg (1/2 S) melted margarine, 6 egg yolks, and 1 good tablespoon of yeast so much lukewarm milk that it becomes a light batter like for apple pancakes; set to rise.

title: Gær-Vafler (Yeast Waffles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 Q) flour
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) melted margarine
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 good tablespoon yeast
- lukewarm milk
- spices as for Æbleskiver

## Directions

1. In flour, melted margarine, egg yolks and a good tablespoon of yeast, add so much lukewarm milk that it becomes a soft dough as for Æbleskiver; set this aside to rise.
2. Apply spices to taste as for Æbleskiver, and bake the waffles as the previous ones.
3. Keep them warm until serving and serve sprinkled with powdered sugar.
4. Instead of yeast, 2 small teaspoons of my baking powder can be used, but then they are baked immediately without being set to rise.
5. After use, the iron is dried and wrapped in paper, and paper is also placed between it.

title: Haarde Pandekager (Hard Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Qvint melted butter
- 12 Qvint sugar
- 24 Qvint flour
- 6 Eggs
- 30 Tablespoonfuls milk

## Directions

1. Stir the melted butter with the sugar.
2. Add the flour, eggs, and finally 30 tablespoonfuls of milk.
3. Place 1 tablespoonful of the batter on the pan for each pancake.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Haardkogte Æg i Karry-Sauce (Hard-Boiled Eggs in Curry Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Flour
- Meat broth or Maggi bouillon cubes
- Water
- Curry
- Cooked rice

## Directions

1. For this, boil the eggs for approximately 10 minutes. Remove them and pour cold water over them so the shell comes off better. Peel and arrange whole on a platter. While the eggs boil, make the curry sauce from butter and flour and dilute it with meat broth or Maggi bouillon cubes dissolved in water. Stir in curry to taste. Pour the sauce over the eggs, which can be garnished with cooked rice.

title: Haardkogte Æg med Sennep-Sauce (Hard-Boiled Eggs with Mustard Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Flour
- Meat broth or Maggi bouillon cubes
- Water
- Mustard
- Milk
- Fried potato slices
- Palmin

## Directions

1. Boil and arrange the eggs as in No. 119. Brown butter and a very little flour and dilute with meat broth or dissolved Maggi bouillon cubes; add mustard sauce to taste, and finally stir a little cold butter into the sauce, which is poured over the eggs. The sauce can be made with milk instead of meat broth; it is not as sharp and uses less butter. The dish can be garnished with breaded potato slices, fried in a pan in Palmin.

title: Hachis
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onion
- Anchovies
- Salt
- Pepper
- Curry or other spices
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Brown 2 ounces of butter (or more if the meat is lean).
2. Cook a grated onion in it first.
3. Then add 2 anchovies, scraped from the bones and chopped with a knife.
4. Season with a little salt, pepper or a little curry.
5. Add the juice of half a lemon.

title: Hachis (Hashed Meat Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces butter
- 1 onion, grated
- 2 anchovies, scraped from the bones and chopped
- salt
- pepper or a little curry
- juice of half a lemon
- 1 biscuit, crushed
- 1 teaspoon flour
- a little soy
- 1 gill bouillon
- strong gravy
- ½ pound finely chopped, cooked or fried meat
- approximately 1 gill white wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Brown 2 ounces butter (more if the meat is lean)
2. Cook the grated onion in it first, then add 2 anchovies scraped from the bones and chopped with a knife, a little salt, pepper or curry, juice of half a lemon, 1 crushed biscuit and 1 teaspoon flour
3. When it boils, add a little soy, 1 gill bouillon, strong gravy and ½ pound finely chopped, cooked or fried meat
4. Finally add approximately 1 gill white wine, though this may be omitted
5. Cook the whole for a good quarter hour
6. When serving, either place browned potatoes on top, or place slices of baked French bread on the dish and pour the hachis over
7. For 4 persons

title: Hachis af Dyrekød (Venison Hash)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted or cooked venison
- Butter
- Flour
- Game stock
- Color (food coloring)
- Red wine
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Chop or mince the roasted or cooked meat finely as for salad.
2. Turn it in a brown, smooth sauce made of butter, flour, game stock, color and red wine.
3. Place browned potatoes in a ring around the hash.

title: Haché (Hachis)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked beef
- Onions
- Ground pepper
- Salt
- Butter
- Wheat flour
- Anchovies
- Beef broth
- Egg yolk
- Potatoes
- Eggs
- Rum or cognac

## Directions

1. Chop the cooked beef finely with onions, ground pepper, and salt to taste.
2. For each pound of beef, brown half a quarter pound of butter with 2 grated onions.
3. Finely chop the beef of 2 anchovies.
4. Brown the mixture with a little salt, ground pepper, and 2 teaspoons of wheat flour until it leaves the spoon.
5. When it boils, add one gill of beef broth for each pound of meat, egg yolk, and the chopped beef.
6. The hachis should be the consistency of a thin porridge.
7. Cook for approximately a quarter hour.
8. Just before serving, add a little rum or cognac.
9. Serve garnished alternately with browned potatoes and hard-boiled eggs cut lengthwise.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Haché af Vildt (Minced Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover game roast
- Butter, 1/4 pound
- Onions, chopped
- Pepper
- Flour
- Stock, preferably from game
- Sherry, 1 glass
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover game roast into very small cubes.
2. Brown the butter and brown chopped onions in it with a little pepper.
3. Thicken with flour and thin with stock, preferably game stock, and a glass of sherry.
4. When the sauce is ready, strain it over the meat, which is arranged garnished with browned potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Haché-Rouletter (Meat Hash Roulettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- butter
- flour
- soup or bouillon
- mushrooms
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Treat exactly as No. 205, only use meat instead of fish. Serve with mushroom sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hakkebøf (Ground Beef)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg beef from the rib or middle cut
- Onion slices
- Butter or Palmin
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Gravy, broth or boiling water
- Maggi bouillon cubes

## Directions

1. Brown a good portion of onion slices and scrape off the pan
2. Cut the meat into smaller pieces and grind once through a meat grinder
3. Form into round pieces with a knife to make well-hacked small beef steaks
4. Sprinkle with salt and ground pepper and turn in flour
5. Fry on the pan in browned butter or Palmin; it should be red and juicy but not bloody
6. Deglaze the pan with gravy, broth or boiling water
7. Dry the pan and brown flour in a little browned butter or margarine, thin with the deglazed liquid to a smooth sauce
8. Season with Maggi bouillon cubes, salt and pepper

title: Hakkebøf (Minced Beef Patties)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg beef chuck or middle cut
- Onion slices
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Flour
- Brown butter or Palmin
- Gravy, broth or boiling water
- Brown butter or margarine
- Flour for sauce
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Food coloring or soya
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Brown a good portion of onion slices, then scrape them from the pan.
2. Cut the meat into smaller pieces and grind once through a meat grinder.
3. Form into round patties with a knife to create well-minced small beef pieces.
4. Sprinkle with salt and ground pepper and dredge in flour.
5. Fry the patties in brown butter or Palmin in a pan; they should be red and juicy but not bloody.
6. Deglaze the pan with gravy, broth or boiling water.
7. Dry the pan, and in a little brown butter or margarine, make a roux with flour, which is thinned with the deglazed liquid to make a smooth sauce.
8. Season the sauce with Maggi bouillon cubes, salt and food coloring or soya.
9. Let the onions simmer in the sauce, which is poured over the patties.
10. Serve with boiled potatoes.
11. Optionally, sinews and bones from the meat can be simmered in very little water, and this broth can be used for the sauce.

title: Halvsøde Agurker
body:
## Ingredients

- Agurker
- Salt
- Hvidt sukker
- Eddike

## Directions

1. Behandl dem ligesom de sure agurker, kun at man i eddiken, når den koger første gang, kommer hvidt sukker efter smag, således at eddiken bliver halv sur og halv sød

title: Hamborgsk Æggesøbe (Hamburg Egg Drink)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 L (2 Potter) pale ale
- 2 L (2 Potter) milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 Q) powdered sugar
- egg whites
- toasted rye bread cubes

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and ale separately and thicken like Nr. 450.
2. Beat 5 egg yolks in a tureen with 1/4 Kg (1/2 Q) powdered sugar, and pour the boiling ale and milk into it under constant whisking with a whisk.
3. The egg whites, which must be whipped to foam before serving, are added on top of the tureen.
4. Serve toasted rye bread cubes with it. Can also be thickened with Maizena.

title: Hamburger-Beefsteg (Hamburger Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Round beef
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Onion

## Directions

1. Use round beef, and in winter it is good if it has hung for several days.
2. Scrape the meat from the sinews, making sure to get everything while leaving only the clear sinews, which are not used.
3. Chop the meat together with the back of a knife into round, flat cakes.
4. Sprinkle salt and pepper on them and fry in well-browned butter; it is good to fry some chopped onion at the same time as the meat.
5. Serve with browned butter and potatoes.

title: Hambørger Kage (Hamburg Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1 pound finely ground powdered sugar
- ground cardamom
- ginger, ground
- lemon zest, grated
- potash (baking soda), 1 nut-sized piece or the value of 1 ore
- 1 pound wheat flour

## Directions

1. Beat 5 eggs in a bowl
2. Stir in 1 pound finely ground powdered sugar along with ground cardamom, ground ginger, grated lemon zest, and potash
3. Work in as much of the wheat flour as the mixture will take
4. Place the remaining flour on the work board, put the mixture on top, and work together with hands until the dough releases and shows eyes
5. Roll out the dough with a rolling pin, slightly thicker than for kleiner
6. Cut into small square cakes the size of a playing card
7. Place a blanched almond in the middle of each
8. Flour the baking pan before placing the cakes on it
9. Bake at good oven heat
10. If the stated amount of flour is not sufficient, more can be added
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hansk Wölekage (Danish Apple Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- butter
- rusk or coffee bread
- powdered sugar
- cinnamon
- currant or cherry juice or water

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the apples not too thin, removing the cores
2. Finely grate rusk or coffee bread
3. Melt a small piece of butter in a stone pot and turn it so butter coats all sides
4. Spread a fairly thick layer of bread in the pot, followed by small butter pieces spaced apart
5. Lay a layer of apple slices as close together as possible, sprinkle with powdered sugar according to the apples' tartness and cinnamon
6. Pour currant or cherry juice around, or water if not using juice
7. Begin again with a good layer of bread and continue alternating
8. The top layer must be bread, covered with butter all around and again drizzled with juice or water
9. Bake in a moderately warm oven until apples can be pierced without resistance
10. Can be made without juice by simply pouring water over when the pot is full, or with rye bread

title: Hare (Roasted Hare)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 unflayed hare weighing 5.5 kg (for 6 to 8 persons)
- Fine larding strips
- Butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Skin the hare as described for venison.
2. Cut off the head and forelegs, and place the hare on its back, then cut off the ribs down to the fillet.
3. On the inside of the back, break the spine in several places to facilitate carving.
4. Loosen all the membranes of the back, legs and forelegs with a sharp knife and remove them all at once without any meat following or being damaged.
5. Lard the hare on the back, legs and forelegs with fine larding strips, cover with butter or margarine and roast in a well-heated oven for about 1 hour and 15 minutes.
6. Serve with browned potatoes and red currant jelly or apple compote.
7. Carve and reassemble before serving.

title: Hare-Ragout (Hare Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 roasted hare or leftovers of one
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Truffles, mushrooms, olives, or chestnuts (optional)
- Butter pastry strips

## Directions

1. Cut up the roasted hare or the leftovers, and boil the bones in milk to make stock for the sauce.
2. Brown butter and flour and thin with the strained stock to make a smooth sauce in which to warm the meat.
3. The sauce can be garnished with truffles, mushrooms, olives or chestnuts and butter pastry strips.

title: Hareſteg (Roasted Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- One young hare
- Half an onion
- Three cloves
- Two bay leaves
- Some peppercorns
- A lump of fresh butter or the same amount of roasting fat
- Salt
- Cream

## Directions

1. Skin and clean a young hare, removing all the innards and cutting off the head and bones.
2. Remove the inner skin from the meat, lard the roast and place it in the roasting pan with half an onion, three cloves, two bay leaves, some peppercorns and a lump of fresh butter or the same amount of roasting fat.
3. Sprinkle with a little salt and roast slowly, with repeated basting, approximately three quarters of an hour or somewhat more.
4. Add cream to the meat gravy, let it come to a boil, strain it, and serve it with the roast.

title: Havre-Suppe (Oat Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts water
- 1/4 pound coarse oat grains
- sugar
- whole cinnamon stick
- lemon zest
- baked bread

## Directions

1. Place 2 quarts of water on heat
2. When it boils strongly, sprinkle 1/4 pound of coarse oat grains into it
3. When they are completely cooked, strain the soup through a fairly fine sieve so that no grains pass through, and set it back on heat
4. Add sugar to taste, a little whole cinnamon stick and lemon zest
5. When it has come to a boil, pour it into the terrine
6. Prepare with preserved juice and serve with baked bread

title: Havregrynsgrød (Oat Grits Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound good Scottish oat grits
- water and milk as per barley porridge

## Directions

1. Rinse 1 pound of good Scottish oat grits in cold water and treat as rice grains
2. Cook under constant stirring like barley grits porridge
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Havregrød (Oat Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Kg (1 S) grains
- about 4 L (4 Potter) water
- salt

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the grains into the boiling water with a little salt added and cook...

title: Havregrød (Oatmeal Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg oats
- approximately 4 L water
- salt

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the oats into the boiling water, add a little salt and cook covered over low heat for about half an hour.
2. The oats can also soak overnight and be cooked in the morning.
3. Serve with fruit juice or red wine and water, or with sweet, semi-skimmed or buttermilk.

title: Havregrød med Kakao (Oatmeal Porridge with Cocoa)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g oats
- 1 L water
- 2 teaspoons dry cocoa
- 2 to 3 teaspoons flour

## Directions

1. Cook the oats and water.
2. Stir in the cocoa and flour; the porridge comes to a boil.
3. Serve with sweet milk.

title: Havremels-Mush (Oatmeal Mush)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts water
- 1.5 cups best oatmeal
- salt
- cream or milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. In 2 quarts of boiling, slightly salted water, add 1.5 cups best oatmeal little by little; stir to prevent it from settling on the bottom
2. Should be cooked well for an hour or more
3. Serve with cream or milk and sugar

title: Havresuppe (Oat Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 measure oat grits
- 4.5 pots water
- sugar
- fruit juice
- preserves

## Directions

1. Wash the oat grits in cold water. This rinsing is especially important when the soup is to be used for the sick, as grits often contain considerable dirt.
2. Boil 4.5 pots of water, and when it boils, add the grits and cook over fairly strong heat without stirring until it becomes an even soup, which will take approximately 3 quarters of an hour.
3. Strain the soup: place a hair sieve over an earthenware dish, add some of the soup to the sieve, stir the thin part through with a spoon and rinse the remaining grits with a little boiling water.
4. Empty the grits from the sieve into a dish and repeat with a new portion until all the oat soup has been sieved, ensuring the soup becomes appropriately even during sieving and dilution.
5. Return the sieved soup to the fire and bring to a good boil.
6. When ready to serve, season with sugar, juice and preserves to taste and serve with baked wheat bread cut into small cubes.
7. For sick persons, prepare the oat soup with only sugar and possibly a few drops of lemon juice or wine, but not with fruit juice or preserves.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hel spekket Kalvelever (Whole Larded Calf's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's liver
- Bacon for larding
- Butter
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Crushed cloves
- Broth or gravy and water
- Flour
- Cream
- White wine
- Whole onions (optional)

## Directions

1. After the membranes are removed from the liver, soak it in several changes of cold water so the blood can drain out, then lard it with bacon.
2. Brown a piece of butter and turn the liver several times in it.
3. When it is light brown, add salt, ground pepper, and a couple of crushed cloves.
4. Add enough broth or gravy and water to have sufficient sauce.
5. When the liver is tender, remove it and strain the sauce if necessary, return it to heat and thicken with a little flour mixed in gravy, cream, and a little white wine.
6. Pour the sauce over the liver.
7. It gives good flavor to roast some whole onions with the liver and serve them around it.
8. Alternatively, the liver can be cut into fairly thick pieces and fried in a pan in enough butter and frying fat or lard so it can cook in it. When the liver is cooked through and laid on a platter, pour off the fat, add some cold butter on top, and when this is brown, stir in gradually a little cold water or gravy until the sauce is smooth, then pour it over the liver.

title: Hel, Stegt Kalvelever (Whole Roasted Veal Liver)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Good quality veal liver
- Fresh lard
- Butter
- Milk
- Culinary coloring
- Salt
- Flour
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Choose a good pale veal liver with a skin that bursts easily when pressed with a finger.
2. Soak in water for half an hour to draw out the blood, and remove the skin and veins as much as possible.
3. Lard the liver with fresh lard, brown it in butter in a pot, and add milk with culinary coloring and salt.
4. Roast gently for 1.5 hours, basting constantly.
5. Make a roux with butter and flour, and thin with the strained pan drippings until the sauce has the right consistency.
6. Add a little cold butter just before serving and pour a spoonful of sauce over the liver before it is served.
7. Serve with boiled potatoes.

title: Hele Appelsiner (Whole Oranges)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg powdered loaf sugar
- 3¾ dl water
- oranges

## Directions

1. Score the orange peel with a sharp knife in uniform broad strips, removing every other strip so the oranges have a striped appearance.
2. Pierce the oranges several times with a pointed sharp knife or with a larding needle and lay them in cold water for one day.
3. Remove from water and cook in fresh water under a lid until the peels are tender to pierce.
4. Remove the fruit and lay on a sieve, and only when it has drained does it go into the jar and is covered with the hot, well-skimmed syrup, which is made from ½ kg sugar and 3¾ dl water to 1/10 kg fruit.
5. Cover the jar and set aside until the next day.
6. Pour off the syrup, boil it, and pour it hot over the fruit, which is again covered well.
7. After 3 to 4 days, pour everything into the pot and the fruit is given a boil, after which it is removed and placed in the jar.
8. Skim the syrup and reduce it until it is smooth; then pour it hot over the oranges.
9. Seal the jar immediately.

title: Hele Ribs (Whole Red Currants)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/4 dl water
- 1/2 kg picked red currants

## Directions

1. Choose large ripe currants for this
2. Pick them carefully from the stems and only then weigh them
3. Cook sugar and water to a very thick syrup in which the currants get one boil
4. Remove them before they begin to burst and fill into glasses
5. Reduce the juice and pour it hot over them
6. Seal immediately

title: Helleflynder (kogt) (Boiled Halibut)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Halibut
- Salt
- Milk
- Water
- Carrot strips
- Parsley
- Cooked potatoes

## Directions

1. Scale and portion the halibut by the pound
2. Remove the dark skin as on plaice, scrape the white side well, wash the fish in several changes of cold water
3. The flayed side can be larded with fine carrot strips; turn it upwards during cooking
4. Place the fish in cold water with salt, or as many prefer, in half milk and half water, which makes it whiter
5. Garnish the fish with parsley at serving, place cooked potatoes on the sides
6. Cook for 6 minutes per ½ kg (1 lb) of fish

title: Henkogt Green Corn (Preserved Corn)
body:
## Ingredients

- Corn
- Salt
- Fat

## Directions

1. Remove the husks and cook the cob until no white juice flows out when the kernels are pricked
2. Remove the kernels and pack in stone jars as follows: at the bottom a layer of salt 1 inch high, then corn in a 2 inches high layer, and thus alternately until the jar is nearly full
3. The top layer of salt should be 1 inch high
4. Pour melted but not hot fat over the whole
5. Press a layer of thick white paper that fits in the jar on top, which is tied securely with paper or a bladder
6. Store in a cold place
7. Soak overnight before use
8. Should the top layer show mould, remove it
9. The rest will not be affected

title: Henkogte Blommer (Preserved Plums)
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Prick each plum with a needle so the skin does not burst
2. For each 3 quarts fruit prepare a sugar solution of 0.5 gill water and 0.5 pound sugar
3. When the water is well warmed and all sugar dissolved, add the plums
4. Bring slowly to a boil and cook very slowly for 5 minutes
5. Fill into jars as directed above

title: Henkogte Ferskener (Preserved Peaches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches, ripe but firm
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Peaches should be ripe but firm in the flesh; the large white ones are best
2. Peel them, cut in half and remove stones, place immediately in cold water
3. In an enamelled pot place fruit and sugar in layers (a large tablespoon of sugar or a little more to each quart of fruit)
4. Bring slowly to a boil and cook only 3 minutes or until all fruit is well heated through
5. Fill immediately into jars and screw shut

title: Henkogte Perer (Preserved Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pears
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. While 1 pint water with 0.5 pound sugar for each quart of fruit is heating, peel pears and place in cold water so they do not discolour
2. When the solution has come to a strong boil, add pears carefully so they do not fall apart; they cook until they can be easily pierced with a fork, whereupon they together with the solution, while it is still boiling, are filled into jars which are immediately screwed shut
3. Coarse and hard pears must first be cooked more in water and then placed hot in the heated solution, in which they cook 10 minutes before being filled into jars
4. Apples are treated the same way

title: Henkogte Tomater (Preserved Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes
- Water

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the fruit to loosen the skin, then peel them and remove all the juice you can without pressing them too hard
2. Then bring slowly to a boil in a pot and cook 10 minutes
3. Remove excess juice with a cup or ladle, and fill the tomatoes boiled into glass jars

title: Henningkager (Honey Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds syrup
- 2 pounds honey
- 1 pound butter
- 18 quintal potassium carbonate, dissolved in a cup of cold water
- 8 pounds flour
- spices to taste

## Directions

1. Give the syrup and honey a good boil. To this add the butter. Dissolve the potassium carbonate in a cup of cold water and measure out the flour in a dish. When the syrup has cooled enough that you can hold your hand in it, pour it onto the flour, add the dissolved potassium carbonate, and stir the whole together into a dough, which stands in a crock for 5-6 days in a cold place. Immediately before baking, a little spice can be added. The oven must be well heated. The whole is baked as one large cake of appropriate thickness, which is cut into oblong, square small cakes when it is taken from the oven. A scalded almond can be placed in the middle of each cake.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hermelinskage (Hermelin Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sweet cream
- 12 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons stiff currant or raspberry juice
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Whip 1 cup sweet cream to peaks, and add to it 12 whipped egg yolks, 3 tablespoons stiff currant or raspberry juice, the juice of 2 lemons, and 1 pound powdered sugar
2. Stir the whole well together
3. Finally add 6 whipped egg whites and fold in gently
4. Bake the cake in a pan at even heat, and when cold, cover with whipped cream with vanilla sugar and decorate with jam jelly
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Herring (Sild)
body:
## Ingredients

- Herring
- Flour
- Butter or clarified butter

## Directions

1. Herring can be used for soup, but they are mostly fried.
2. After the herring is cleaned, washed and dried, dip it in flour and fry in butter or clarified butter.
3. Often herring are fried in the following way: brown butter in a pan and then dip the herring in flour and place in the pan with the head of one to the tail of another as long as there is space, and they are packed so tightly together that they stick.

title: Hindbær (Raspberry Preserve)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g powdered sugar
- 1/4 dl water
- 1/2 kg raspberries

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water together.
2. Add the berries and bring to a boil.
3. Drain the berries through a sieve.
4. Boil the drained juice until it is smooth.
5. Pour the berries back into the juice and bring to a boil again.
6. Pour into glasses and tie them up as soon as the preserve has cooled.

title: Hindbær med Ribssaft (Raspberry Jam with Currant Juice, English Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/4 liter raw strained currant juice
- 1/2 kg raspberries

## Directions

1. Remove stems from the raspberries and place them in a pot.
2. Place over low heat and boil for 15 minutes, stirring and skimming constantly.
3. Add the currant juice and sugar.
4. Boil again for 1/2 hour.
5. Pour the preserve into jars and tie them up immediately.

title: Hindbær med Sprit (Raspberries in Spirits)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 dl spirits of wine
- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/2 kg raspberries

## Directions

1. Remove stems from the berries.
2. Layer them in glasses or jars alternately with the sugar.
3. Pour the specified amount of spirits over them and cover well.
4. For the next two days, gently turn the fruit around with a spoon, and tie up the glasses.

title: Hindbær og Ribs (Raspberry and Currant Jam, English Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg powdered sugar
- 1/2 kg raspberries
- 3/4 kg currants

## Directions

1. Remove stems from the raspberries and currants.
2. Layer them in the pot alternately with the sugar.
3. Place over low heat until the berries begin to release juice.
4. Skim well and boil for 3/4 hour.
5. Pour into jars and tie them up immediately.

title: Hindbær- og Jordbær-Is (Raspberry and Strawberry Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries and strawberries
- Powdered sugar
- 1 to 2 pints water
- 1 pound sugar
- Optional: lemon juice, a shot of rum, a beaten egg white

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the berries with powdered sugar and let them stand thus for half a day.
2. Boil a sugar syrup of 1 to 2 pints water and 1 pound sugar, which is poured boiling over the berries, which are first crushed into pieces.
3. When the mixture is cooled, strain it and freeze the juice.
4. You can, if you wish, add a little lemon juice, a shot of rum, and a beaten egg white.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hindbær- og Ribs-Gelé (Raspberry and Currant Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg raspberries (half)
- 1/2 kg red currants (half)
- 1/2 kg sugar (or corresponding weight to combined juices)
- 1 1/4 dl water

## Directions

1. Mix half raspberries and half red currants together and treat as above.
2. Cook as No. 93.
3. The berries are cooked to Russian marmalade, see No. 147.

title: Hindbær-Eddike (Raspberry Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter (1 pint) vinegar
- 1/4 kg (1/2 pound) berries
- 1/2 kg (1 pound) powdered sugar per liter of juice

## Directions

1. Place the berries in a crock and cover with vinegar
2. Tie the crock well and let stand for 3 to 4 days in a cold place
3. Pour the berries through a jelly cloth
4. Let the juice that runs through stand until the next day
5. Measure it and boil for 10 to 12 minutes with 1/2 kg powdered sugar per liter of juice
6. Skim carefully during cooking
7. Pour into bottles, cork and seal with plaster

title: Hindbær-Eddike (Raspberry Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pots of pressed juice
- 1 measure of wine vinegar
- 1.5 pounds of white sugar

## Directions

1. Pour the juice into a glass vessel, add the vinegar, and let the mixture stand for 2 days so impurities can settle to the bottom
2. Filter the clear liquid and add the white sugar, which is dissolved over a gentle fire
3. Give it one boil and skim it
4. When the juice is cold, fill it into bottles and cork them well
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hindbær-Gelé Nr. 1 (Raspberry Jelly No. 1)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg raspberries (wild or garden)
- 1/2 kg sugar (or corresponding weight to juice)
- 1 1/4 dl water

## Directions

1. Both wild and garden raspberries may be used.
2. Gently crush the berries with a spoon and pour into the jelly cloth.
3. When the juice has run through, weigh it, and cook a corresponding weight of sugar with water as described in No. 93.
4. Cook the juice for about fifteen minutes with the sugar and it must be well skimmed during this time.
5. Pour into the cups, which are tied up when the jelly begins to set.
6. The pulp may be cooked with sugar and made into marmalade.

title: Hindbær-Gelé Nr. 2 (Raspberry Jelly No. 2)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 parts raspberries
- 1 part white currants, picked over

## Directions

1. Use 3 parts raspberries and 1 part white currants, which are picked over.
2. Mix the berries together in a pitcher and place in a pot of boiling water, which must be kept at a boil until they release their juices well and fall together in the pitcher.
3. Pour into the jelly cloth, and the next day cook as No. 93.
4. This jelly obtains a beautiful clear color; the refuse is used for marmalade.

title: Hindbær-Kompot (Raspberry Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1.25 dl water for 0.5 kg fruit

## Directions

1. Cook sugar and water to a thin syrup.
2. Place large, whole, selected raspberries in the syrup.
3. Remove them with a slotted spoon as soon as they reach a boil and place them in sulfured jars.
4. Reduce the juice for 6 to 8 minutes and pour over the berries.
5. Place sticks and sulfur in the jars, light them, and seal the jars as quickly as possible.

title: Hindbær-Likør (Raspberry Liqueur)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 S) wild or garden raspberries
- 1 bottle white rum
- 1/2 kg (1 S) powdered sugar
- 1/4 l (1 pægl) water

## Directions

1. Pour 1 bottle of white rum over 1 kg (2 S) raspberries and let stand well covered for 8 days.
2. Boil 1/2 kg (1 S) powdered sugar and 1/4 l (1 pægl) water to make a syrup.
3. Pour the hot syrup into the strained rum.
4. Pour the liqueur into dry bottles, cork them, and seal with gypsum.

title: Hindbær-Marmelade (Raspberry Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe raspberries
- Powdered sugar (1/2 amount of berries)

## Directions

1. Press the ripe raspberries through a coarse sieve.
2. Put the pulp in a pot together with the sugar and cook down to an even mass.
3. Fill it hot into jars, cover with paper and tie up.

title: Hindbær-Sirup (Raspberry Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 pounds of filtered juice
- 4 pounds of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the juice and sugar and bring to a good boil
2. Filter the syrup
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hindbær-Suppe (Raspberry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg raspberries
- 0.5 kg currants
- 4.5 L water
- potato flour
- vanilla essence or vanilla tablets

## Directions

1. Cook raspberries and currants for half an hour in 4.5 L water
2. Strain and bring to boil again
3. Thicken with potato flour mixed with water
4. Add a few drops of vanilla essence or a couple of vanilla tablets

title: Hindbær-Suppe (Raspberry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart Raspberries
- 3 Pint Water
- Sugar
- 1 large Spoonful Corn-Starch

## Directions

1. Pick, clean and place the raspberries over the fire in 3 pints water.
2. This boils as long as until all juice is extracted from the berries, then strain it through a fine sieve and place it over the fire again with sugar according to how sour the berries are; it may well be brown sugar.
3. Reserve a little of the soup after it is strained, and stir a large spoonful of corn-starch into it.
4. When the soup is near boiling, stir this in, it boils up again and is then ready to serve.
5. Serve with rusks.

title: Hindbærkage (Raspberry Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 egg whites
- 36 quintals powdered sugar
- 0.5 pound raspberry marmalade

## Directions

1. Whip 24 egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Stir 36 quintals powdered sugar into 1/2 pound raspberry marmalade and pour into a cake pan
3. Add the egg whites one tablespoon at a time and stir carefully into the jam
4. Bake in a moderate oven for approximately 1.5 hours and serve with whipped cream over top
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hindbærsaft (Raspberry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-cleaned and dried raspberries
- 2 to 3 pounds of powdered sugar per pot of juice

## Directions

1. Mash the berries in a pot and wring them in a clean linen cloth
2. Place the sugar in the juice to dissolve overnight
3. Before putting it on to cook, skim it with the utmost care
4. Cook for half an hour, skim well and pour through a filter bag or piece of flannel
5. Fill it cold into bottles
6. Berries can be gathered over several days in a pot and should then be mashed gradually and stirred a couple of times daily
7. Otherwise treat as described above
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hindbærsaft (Raspberry Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Very ripe wild or garden raspberries
- 1/2 kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar per 1 liter (1 pot) juice

## Directions

1. Very ripe berries can either be wrung out in a cloth or mashed with a spoon, then poured into a jelly bag or fine sieve.
2. Measure the juice that runs through and place on heat together with the sugar.
3. Boil and skim for 8 to 10 minutes, pour hot into bottles and tie immediately.
4. The refuse left behind is boiled in water, and the juice is used in the household for fruit pudding or fruit soup, or served cold with porridge instead of beer.

title: Hjortekjølle (Venison Haunch)
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison haunch
- Lard
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Crushed cloves
- Butter
- Water
- Wheat flour
- Bouillon
- Red wine
- Cream
- Roasted potatoes
- Redcurrant jelly

## Directions

1. Beat the haunch well, rinse and dry with a clean linen cloth.
2. Lard first on the underside and then in the middle of the thigh's upper side in the form of a star or rose.
3. Pour vinegar over the haunch and sprinkle with salt mixed with finely crushed cloves. Cover with butter and place buttered white paper over it.
4. Pour a little water over it and roast with regular basting for approximately 3 to 4 hours until tender.
5. Remove the paper. Brown the sauce with flour, thin with good bouillon, and add a little red wine and cream, then strain.
6. Serve with roasted potatoes and redcurrant jelly.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hjortetaksalts-Kage (Deer Antler Salt Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarter-pounds butter
- 7 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 1 pound flour
- 1/4 pound sweet almonds, cut into narrow strips
- 12 quintals bitter almonds, finely ground
- a little grated lemon zest
- 7 egg whites, beaten
- 8 ore deer antler salt

## Directions

1. Wash and clarify 3 quarter-pounds butter, then beat well with 7 egg yolks and 1 pound powdered sugar.
2. Beat in 1 pound flour, 1/4 pound sweet almonds cut into narrow strips, 12 quintals finely ground bitter almonds and a little grated lemon zest.
