3. Finally add the 7 beaten egg whites and 8 ore deer antler salt.
4. Fill the whole mixture into a greased form and bake hard.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hollandaise Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce butter
- 1 ounce flour
- 1 gill boiling water
- 3 egg yolks
- a saltspoonful dry mustard
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 3 tablespoons oil
- salt

## Directions

1. Set 1 ounce butter over the fire
2. Sprinkle 1 ounce flour in it until it bubbles
3. Gradually add 1 gill boiling water
4. When it boils, remove it from the fire
5. Add 3 egg yolks, one at a time, 1 saltspoonful dry mustard, 1 tablespoon vinegar, 3 tablespoons oil gradually drop by drop, stirring constantly, until it is smooth
6. For boiled fish

title: Hollandsk Sauce A (Dutch Sauce A)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon flour
- butter
- water
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Bake 1 tablespoon flour with a little butter and thin with water.
2. Finish with vinegar and salt and stir in butter, but must not boil. For 10 portions: half a pound butter and half a tablespoon flour.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hollandsk Sauce A (Dutch Sauce A)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon flour
- a little butter
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- butter for finishing

## Directions

1. Bake 1 tablespoon flour with a little butter and dilute with water.
2. Mix in vinegar and salt and stir in butter, but do not allow to boil.
3. For 19 portions use 1 pound butter and half a tablespoon flour.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hollandsk Sauce B (Dutch Sauce B)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 well beaten egg yolks
- 25 quintals butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- cold water
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Set a pudding mold in a pot of boiling water.
2. Add 4 well beaten egg yolks to it.
3. Stir in 25 quintals butter which has first been stirred to a salve, and 1 tablespoon flour.
4. Pour in several spoonfuls of cold water and then gently boiling water under constant stirring so the sauce becomes smooth.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hollandsk Sauce B (Dutch Sauce B)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 well-beaten egg yolks
- 25 quint butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- cold water
- boiling water
- 1 slice lemon
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Set a pudding mold in a pot of boiling water.
2. Add 4 well-beaten egg yolks to the mold.
3. Stir in 25 quint butter that has been stirred to a paste, and 1 tablespoon flour.
4. Pour in several tablespoons of cold water and then slowly boiling water with constant stirring, so the sauce bubbles up somewhat.
5. Add a slice of lemon, 2 tablespoons vinegar and a little sugar.
6. Keep the pot over heat and stir the sauce until smooth, but do not allow it to boil.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Holstenisk Saltlage (Holstein Brine)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram kitchen salt
- 10 grams saltpeter
- 125 grams powdered sugar
- 4 liters boiling water

## Directions

1. Dissolve the salt, saltpeter and powdered sugar in 4 liters of boiling water
2. Place the meat to be salted in the brine when it is completely cold
3. After 14 days it is ready to be cooked

title: Holstenisk Saltlage (Holstein Salt Brine)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Kilo (2 lb) Kitchen salt
- 10 Gr (2 Dram) Saltpeter
- 125 Gr (1/4 lb) Powdered sugar
- 4 L (4 Pots) Boiling water

## Directions

1. Dissolve the kitchen salt, saltpeter, and powdered sugar in 4 liters of boiling water
2. Place the meat to be salted in the brine when it is completely cold
3. After 14 days it is ready to be cooked without needing to be desalted
4. This mild brine is excellent for salting tongues, beef brisket, salt meat, rolled sausages, and hams
5. Veal and lamb meat require only a few days of salting
6. Hams must lie in it for at least 3 weeks

title: Hominy-Grød, kogt i Mælk (Hominy Porridge Cooked in Milk)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg hominy grains
- approximately 5 L milk
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- beer, fruit juice and water, or red wine

## Directions

1. If cooking the grains in milk, soak them as described above, drain the water, and add the grains to the boiling milk; they require the same cooking time.
2. Season with salt.
3. Serve with butter, sugar, cinnamon and either beer, fruit juice and water or red wine.
4. The corn grains produce a beautiful white porridge.

title: Hominy-Vandgrød (Hominy Water Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg hominy grains
- 4 L water
- salt
- butter
- sugar
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. For ½ kg grains, use 2 L cold or lukewarm water; let them stand until the next day.
2. Pour everything into an enameled pot with approximately 2 L boiling water.
3. Cook the porridge for 1 hour over the heat and then place it in a hay box, where it stands for about 4 hours.
4. All grain types must be well cooked.
5. Salt the porridge and serve with butter, sugar and sweet milk.
6. Hominy is American corn grains, very nourishing and cheap.

title: Hons med Cottin (Chicken with Cottin)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound rice grains
- 3 pints milk
- Handful of finely chopped sweet almonds
- 2 ounces butter
- Nutmeg flower
- 1 teaspoon white sugar
- 4 eggs
- Chicken meat
- Sauce with thickening and mustard, cayenne or curry
- Beaten eggs
- Crushed rusks
- 3 to 4 tablespoons clarified butter

## Directions

1. Cook the rice grains in the milk to make a porridge that should not be overcooked, and cook a handful of finely chopped sweet almonds in it
2. Stir in 2 ounces of butter, nutmeg flower and 1 teaspoon white sugar
3. When cold, add 4 eggs
4. When the chicken meat is half-cooked and cold, place it in a dish as for pâté
5. Pour the cold sauce, which is made with only a thickening and with mustard, cayenne or curry, over it
6. Spread the cottin over 2 finger-widths thick and make a hole in the middle for steam
7. Coat with beaten eggs and sprinkle with crushed rusks
8. The oven should not be hotter than for wheat bread; when it has set until slightly browned on top, take it from the oven and coat with 3 to 4 tablespoons of clarified butter
9. When the meat is presumed to be tender, the cottin is also roasted

title: Honse-Frikashee (Chicken Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen
- Water
- Salt
- 12 peppercorns
- Carrot
- Onion
- 2 cloves
- Herb bouquet or dried vegetables
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolk
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the hen and chop it up; first take off the legs and wings
2. Split the breast and back, and chop these into suitable pieces
3. Place over heat in boiling water that must come just over them
4. Skim as often as foam rises, then add a tablespoon of salt, a dozen peppercorns, a carrot, an onion, a couple of cloves, and a bunch or bouquet of dried vegetables
5. Cook gently until the hen is tender, about one hour, then remove it and keep it warm while the sauce is made
6. Strain the soup and return it without vegetables to heat; stir together a lump of butter and a lump of flour over the heat
7. Gradually stir in 0.5 pint of the soup, adding a beaten egg yolk and stirring until the sauce is smooth
8. Add salt and pepper, and serve on a warm dish

title: Hornfisk (Boiled) (Boiled Garfish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Hornfisk (Garfish)
- Salt
- Kartofler (Potatoes)
- Sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the head and tail from the garfish.
2. Clean and cook as Mackerel (No. 551).
3. Serve with boiled potatoes and sauce as described for mackerel.

title: Hornfisk (Fried) (Fried Garfish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Hornfisk (Garfish)
- Salt
- Mel (Flour)
- Æg (Egg)
- Brød (Bread)
- Smør or Palmin (Butter or Margarine)
- brunt Smør (Brown butter)
- Citron or Eddike (Lemon or Vinegar)
- Kartofler (Potatoes)
- Gemyse (Vegetables)

## Directions

1. Split the fish along the belly and remove the bone.
2. Partition into smaller pieces.
3. Rinse the pieces and sprinkle with salt.
4. Dip in flour, then in egg, then in bread.
5. Fry in butter or Palmin.
6. Serve with brown butter, lemon or vinegar, and potatoes.
7. Vegetables can be served alongside.

title: Hornfisk (Garfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hornfisk (garfish)
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Whole peppercorns
- Parsley sauce
- Vinegar (for sauce)
- Roe

## Directions

1. Remove the head and discard it
2. Salt the fish before cooking
3. Cut the fish into pieces 3 inches in length
4. Cook in water with salt, vinegar, and whole peppercorns
5. Arrange with parsley sauce containing a little vinegar
6. Cook the roe with the fish
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hornfisk, Stegt som Kyllinger (Garfish Fried Like Chicken)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Hornfisk (Garfish)
- Persille (Parsley)
- Smør (Butter)
- Sky or Suppe (Stock or Soup)
- Sagomel (Sago flour)
- Maggis-Bouillon-Tærninger (Maggi Bouillon cubes)
- Kartofler (Potatoes)
- grøn Salat (Green salad)

## Directions

1. Split the fish along the belly and remove the bone.
2. Divide into 3 or 4 pieces.
3. Fill with parsley and a little butter.
4. Sew the belly together.
5. Brown the garfish in butter.
6. Add either stock or soup.
7. Cook briefly as the fish cooks quickly and falls apart easily.
8. Skim the stock and thicken with stirred sago flour.
9. Season with Maggi bouillon cubes.
10. Remove the thread before serving.
11. Serve with boiled or browned potatoes along with green salad.
12. This dish is delicious served either cold or warm.

title: Hoved-Salat (Head Lettuce Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 heads of lettuce
- 4 spoonfuls oil
- 2 spoonfuls vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Pour the oil, vinegar, salt and pepper mixture over the rinsed and prepared salad.
2. Garnish with hard-boiled eggs, either cut into quarters or sliced.

title: Hoved-Salat (Lettuce Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Five lettuce heads
- Three egg yolks
- Two tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cream
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the outer coarse leaves from the lettuce heads
2. Rinse well and place in cloth, shaking them until approximately dry
3. Mix 3 egg yolks well with 2 tablespoons powdered sugar
4. Pour in 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cream and vinegar to taste
5. Cut the lettuce into portions, place in a bowl, and pour the sauce over

title: Hovedsalat med Fløde (Lettuce Salad with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head fresh lettuce
- thick sour cream
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves from the head of lettuce
2. Rinse it in several changes of cold water and place in a colander to drain
3. Cut into 4 pieces, remove the stalk, and place the leaves in thick sour cream mixed with vinegar and sugar to taste
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hovedsalat med Olie (Lettuce Salad with Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head lettuce
- fine cooking oil
- 2 hard-boiled egg yolks
- wine vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the lettuce head as described in the cream salad recipe
2. Pour some spoonfuls of fine cooking oil over it and gently toss with a fork and spoon
3. Work 2 hard-boiled egg yolks well with 2 to 3 spoonfuls of wine vinegar, pepper, and salt, and a little powdered sugar if desired, all to taste
4. Pour this sauce over the salad
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hummer i Fiskerand (Lobster in Fish Border)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish forcemeat
- Cooked Lobster
- Butter
- 1 Tablespoon Wheat Flour
- 1 Pail strong Broth
- 1 Pail Cream
- Small cooked Fish balls
- Cooked Asparagus pieces one inch in length

## Directions

1. Fill a greased ring mold with fish forcemeat and place it in a pan with enough water to reach halfway up the mold.
2. Cover with a piece of paper greased with butter and cook for about a quarter hour.
3. Turn the mold onto a serving dish and let it stand for a few minutes before lifting off the fish border.
4. Prepare the lobster stewing: Pick the cooked lobster and cut the meat into small pieces.
5. Melt a piece of butter and stir in one tablespoon of wheat flour.
6. Add one pail of strong broth and one pail of cream, and cook together.
7. Pour this sauce over small cooked fish balls and lobster meat to warm it.
8. Add small pieces of cooked asparagus about one inch in length to the mixture.
9. Fill the fish border with the lobster mixture and serve as hot as possible.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hummer i Majonaise (Lobster in Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold, cooked lobster tail and claws
- Mayonnaise sauce
- Green salad leaves

## Directions

1. Serve the tail and claws of cold, cooked lobster covered with proper mayonnaise sauce
2. Garnish the dish with fine, green salad leaves
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hummer Salat (Lobster Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large lobster
- 2 tablespoons oil
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt
- Cooked picked fish or fish balls (optional)

## Directions

1. Pick the lobster as previously shown
2. Make the sauce from 2 tablespoons of oil, stirred with 1 tablespoon vinegar, pepper and salt
3. Stir the picked lobster in this
4. You can add cooked and picked fish or cut fish balls, but then a little more sauce must be made

title: Hummer- og Krebse-Sauce (Lobster and Crayfish Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh cooked lobster
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock
- Parsley
- Lemon juice
- White pepper
- Salt
- Lobster and crayfish extract (alternative method)

## Directions

1. Shell the meat from a fresh, cooked lobster and pound the shells in 100 gr (20 Kvint) of butter
2. Put the whole in a casserole together with a whisk of parsley and cover with 1 L (1 Pot) of stock
3. After it has boiled a few minutes, strain off the stock
4. Skim the butter and use it to bake the flour for the sauce, which is thinned with the strained stock
5. Season with lemon juice, white pepper, and salt
6. Cut the lobster meat into smaller pieces and add to the sauce
7. Alternatively, instead of pounding the shells, simply add a little of the excellent lobster and crayfish extract available in Danish wine and preserved foods factories
8. Dissolve the extract in the hot sauce, which it gives a beautiful color and excellent lobster flavor
9. Good for lobster soup made from thin clear stock with fish balls
10. Asparagus can be added to lobster sauce and soup

title: Hummer- og Krebsesauce (Lobster and Crayfish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster or crayfish shells
- butter
- water
- flour
- bouillon
- salt
- lobster or crayfish meat

## Directions

1. After the lobsters or crayfish are cooked, pound the shells fine with butter, set to boil over steady heat and dilute with boiling water.
2. Let them boil until all the butter has risen to the surface, then skim off, strain and set to boil again with flour.
3. Dilute with bouillon and add a little salt.
4. Finally add the crayfish meat or lobster meat cut into suitable pieces.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hummer-Budding (Lobster Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- fish forcemeat
- meat from 2 cooked lobsters, coarsely chopped
- lobster shells
- cooked asparagus (optional)

## Directions

1. This pudding can be prepared from fish forcemeat, into which the coarsely chopped, cooked meat from 2 lobsters is stirred.
2. The shells are crushed and cooked down for sauce (see Lobster Sauce).
3. Cooked asparagus can be added to the sauce.

title: Hummer-Gratin 1 (Lobster Gratin 1)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 stor, kogt Hummer
- 50 Gr (10 Kvint) Mel
- 50 Gr (10 Kvint) Margarine
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) Mælk
- Hakket Persille
- Paprika
- Salt
- 2 Æggeblommer
- Stødt Brød
- Smeltet Smør

## Directions

1. Af en stor, kogt Hummer udtages Kødet, som skæres i små Stykker.
2. 50 Gr (10 Kvint) Mel bages i 50 Gr. (10 Kvint) Margarine og fortyndes med godt 1/4 L (1 Pægl) Mælk.
3. Når Saucen har kogt tilsættes den lidt hakket Persille, Paprika og Salt; den lieres med 2 Æggeblommer, og heri kommer Hummerkødet.
4. Denne Gratin kan enten bages på Fad, i Gratinskaller eller i Hummerskallerne, som da må bevares hele.
5. I alle Tilfælde bestros den med stødt Brød og dryppes med lidt smeltet Smør.
6. Den sættes i Ovnen for at få Farve og anrettes på Serviet, pyntet med Persille.

title: Hummer-Gratin 2 (Lobster Gratin 2)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 stor Hummer
- 4 Æg
- 8 å 10 Skefulde Mælk
- 1 Teskefuld Kartoffelmel
- Salt

## Directions

1. Æggeblommerne røres godt med Melet og Mælken; heri kommer Salt, småtskaaret Hummerkød og til sidst de piskede Hvider.
2. Den bages i Ovnen omkring 1 Kvarter, til den er gulbrun, og må straks anrettes.
3. Der serveres Hummer-Sauce til.

title: Hummer-Salat (Lobster Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Preserved lobster
- Mayonnaise made with 2 egg yolks, 1/4 liter oil, 4 spoonfuls tomato puree, salt, ground white pepper, mustard and tarragon vinegar
- Fresh salad leaves
- Cooked, flaked cod
- 125 grams cooked, diced macaroni
- Lobster meat
- Watercress
- Toasted French bread slices
- Caviar

## Directions

1. Place a wreath of fresh salad leaves on a platter.
2. In the center place finely cut lobster meat, a flat plate of cooked, flaked cod, and 125 grams of cooked, diced macaroni.
3. Pour the mayonnaise over.
4. Some of the lobster meat can be placed on top of the salad and sprinkled with watercress.
5. Toasted French bread slices topped with caviar can be arranged around the salad leaves.

title: Hummer-Salat Nr. 1 (Lobster Salad No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lobsters
- 4 heads fresh green lettuce
- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons melted butter
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- Cayenne pepper
- Salt
- 2 teaspoons sugar
- 2 cups vinegar

## Directions

1. Pick lobster meat finely
2. Cut fresh green lettuce finely; layer with lobster meat in a fairly deep dish
3. Grind hard-boiled egg yolks and mix with 3 tablespoons melted butter, 1 teaspoon mustard, cayenne pepper and salt to taste, 2 teaspoons sugar, and 2 cups vinegar
4. Heat the mixture over the stove and pour over the salad while warm

title: Hummer-Salat Nr. 2 (Lobster Salad No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster with its greenness
- Cream or fine salad oil
- Juice from inside the lobster
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Lobster meat
- Lettuce leaves, small and curled
- Watercress
- Lobster roe

## Directions

1. Grind the green part of the lobster in a little cream or fine salad oil
2. Add the juice that flows from the lobster when opened (collected carefully) and a little vinegar and pepper
3. Cut the lobster meat into small pieces and arrange between small curled lettuce leaves in the sauce
4. Garnish with watercress and some of the finest and lightest lettuce leaves
5. Decorate with the internal red firm roe, cut into figures and arranged among the greens; if roe is scarce, place it in the center and corners of the salad dish

title: Hummer-Suppe (Lobster Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large female lobsters with roe
- 4.5 L soup stock
- 175 gr butter
- 100 gr flour
- ground white pepper
- Madeira wine
- 1/2 kg asparagus
- small fish balls
- Butter dough slices or Simels

## Directions

1. Rinse the lobsters and place them in boiling water
2. When the legs come off easily, remove the lobsters
3. Cool them, split them open and remove the meat from the tail and claws in pieces as large as possible
4. Cut the meat into small, uniform pieces and place in a tureen
5. Pound all remaining parts of the lobsters - shells, bones and roe - in a mortar together with 175 gr butter
6. Pour this mixture into 4 L spare soup together with a good whisk and cook for 10 minutes so that the red lobster butter can collect on top
7. Skim off the red butter, then bake 100 gr flour in the soup and thin with the lobster soup
8. Add ground white pepper and Madeira
9. Add 1/2 kg asparagus, the lobster meat and small fish balls to the soup
10. Serve with Butter dough slices or Simels and add the asparagus water to the soup

title: Hummer-Suppe (Lobster Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large lobster or 2 small lobsters
- 1 quart milk
- 1 pint water
- 1 pound butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- salt
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Remove all meat from the lobster bones and chop finely
2. Warm 1 quart milk together with 1 pint water
3. Add the lobster meat to the milk and water along with 1 pound butter mixed with 1 tablespoon flour
4. Season with salt and red pepper to taste
5. Boil for 10 minutes

title: Hummersalat (Lobster Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Diced, cooked lobster meat (about as much as is usually in a tin)
- 2 pints cream or sweet milk
- 7 egg yolks
- 25 pound cooked, cold cod, free of bones and skin
- Capers worth 16 øre
- 3 celery stalks, diced
- Vinegar and sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Mix diced, cooked lobster meat with a crème made from 2 pints cream or sweet milk and 7 egg yolks
2. Blend in 25 pound cooked, cold cod (free of bones and skin), capers worth 16 øre, 3 diced celery stalks, and vinegar and sugar to taste
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvedebrod (Wheat Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pounds sifted flour
- 1 quart lukewarm water
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 0.5 gill yeast or 2 cents compressed yeast
- additional lukewarm water as needed

## Directions

1. Put 7 pounds sifted flour in a deep vessel
2. Make a hole in the center
3. Add 1 quart lukewarm water, 1 tablespoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, and 0.5 gill yeast or 2 cents compressed yeast dissolved in a little lukewarm water
4. Have more lukewarm water ready and use as much as needed to make a fairly soft dough
5. Knead thoroughly with both hands; the softer the dough, the better the bread, as long as it releases from the hands and vessel
6. When even and good, sprinkle a little flour on it, lay a folded cloth over the vessel, and let it stand four or five hours in a warm place to rise
7. If the dough was stirred in the evening, it can stand overnight if the room is warm
8. When risen enough, turn out onto a floured breadboard and knead for about fifteen minutes, using enough flour so it does not stick to the board or hands
9. Put it back in the bread pan and let it rise fully again, which happens faster than the first time
10. Divide the dough into 4 breads, put in baking pans, let stand fifteen minutes, then bake in a hot oven
11. The recipe makes about 8 pounds of bread; divided into 4 pieces, bake for one hour
12. Test with a splint or straw; if it comes out clean, the bread is done
13. It should also have the proper color

title: Hvedebrød (Wheat Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds fine wheat flour
- 6 cups sweet, lukewarm milk
- a little salt
- 2 tablespoons thick yeast (or 20 grams pressed yeast)

## Directions

1. Knead the wheat flour with the lukewarm milk, a little salt, and yeast until the dough releases from the hands.
2. Let it rise well covered for 3 or 4 hours.
3. If baking in the morning, the dough can be laid out in the evening and left overnight in cold water to rise in a moderately warm place.
4. Knead the dough again and shape into 3 or 4 loaves, which should rise a little more.
5. Brush with egg white, water, or beer and place in a well-heated oven that is not so hot that the crust burns.
6. Since the heat is strongest at the back of the oven, it may be right to turn the pan once during baking, which takes 1 hour.
7. If the loaves should be smooth, make one or two cuts lengthwise or crosswise; the deeper the cut, the thicker the crust becomes.
8. The crust will be thinner by beginning baking with slightly less heat; for this purpose, you can leave the oven door slightly ajar.
9. When the loaves are taken out, it is good to cover them slightly so the cooling proceeds more slowly.
10. The milk used can also be skimmed, but it must in no way be sour, nor must the yeast be bitter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvedekiks (Wheat Crackers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 36 grams butter
- 18 grams light powdered sugar
- 2 eggs
- 4 to 5 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. Knead 1 pound flour, 36 grams butter, 18 grams light powdered sugar, and 2 eggs well with 4 to 5 tablespoons water.
2. Roll out and bake as described for rye crackers.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvid Benne-Salat (White Bean Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram white beans
- Water
- 2 spoonfuls oil
- 4 spoonfuls tomato puree
- 1 spoonful chopped parsley
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the white beans overnight.
2. Cook them tender in water.
3. Mix together the oil, tomato puree, chopped parsley, salt and pepper and pour over the beans.

title: Hvid Biskop (White Bishop)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds white sugar
- 2 fresh oranges
- 1 bottle Mosel wine
- 1 bottle white Port wine
- 1 bottle Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Dip 2 pounds white sugar in cold water and place in a tureen or punch bowl with 2 fresh cut oranges
2. Cover with 1 bottle Mosel wine
3. Let it stand for 2 hours
4. Strain it and add 1 bottle white Port wine and 1 bottle Rhine wine
5. Cool the mixture strongly in ice
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvid Fiskesauce (White Fish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon cornstarch
- A good piece of butter
- Strong fish broth
- 2-3 egg yolks
- A couple spoonfuls of Madeira or other wine
- A little nutmeg
- Salt

## Directions

1. Knead cornstarch into a good piece of butter
2. Place this in a large cup of sieved strong fish broth and bring to a boil
3. Whip 2-3 egg yolks and stir into the sauce while it is removed from the heat
4. When it is about to boil again, add a couple spoonfuls of Madeira or other wine
5. Whip well until it is poured out
6. Add a little nutmeg and salt if needed

title: Hvid Havre-Suppe (White Oat Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 190 g oat groats
- 4½ L water
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 egg white
- approximately ¼ kg powdered sugar
- ¼ L good sherry
- ¼ L rhubarb juice
- Whole cinnamon
- Lemon peel

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the grains into the boiling water and cook ½ to 1 hour.
2. Strain the soup and pour into a serving bowl in which 2 egg yolks and 1 egg white have been stirred with approximately ¼ kg powdered sugar.
3. Into this thickened soup pour ¼ L good sherry and ¼ L rhubarb juice.
4. Whole cinnamon and lemon peel can be cooked in the soup or rather together with the grains.
5. This can be served with compote of fresh, dried or preserved fruit.

title: Hvid Kastanie-Suppe (White Chestnut Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- white spare broth
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 L (1 mug) raw cream
- fillets of cooked or roasted chicken or squab

## Directions

1. The difference is only that white spare broth is used for this, and that it is bound with 4 egg yolks stirred in 1/4 L (1 mug) raw cream.
2. Fillets of cooked or roasted chicken or squab can be placed in the soup.

title: Hvid Sago-Suppe (White Sago Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- good 1/4 L (good 1 Pægl) medium-fine sago grains
- 4 1/2 L (4 1/2 Pot) water
- lemon peel
- whole cinnamon
- raisins
- 7 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- 1/2 Kg (1 S) powdered sugar
- 3 3/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) sherry
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) rhubarb juice or white wine

## Directions

1. When the water boils, add lemon peel, whole cinnamon and raisins together with the grains under constant stirring and cook until the grains are clear.
2. In a tureen, beat 7 egg yolks and 3 egg whites with 1/2 Kg (1 S) powdered sugar until white and foaming.
3. Pour the boiling soup into the egg mixture while stirring well.
4. Season the soup with 3 3/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) sherry and 1/4 L (1 Pægl) rhubarb juice or white wine.

title: Hvid Sago-Suppe (White Sago Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- good ¼ L medium-fine sago grains
- 4½ L water
- Lemon peel
- Cinnamon
- Raisins
- 7 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- ½ kg powdered sugar

## Directions

1. When the water boils, add lemon peel, whole cinnamon and raisins together with the grains while stirring constantly and cook until the grains are clear.
2. Stir 7 egg yolks and 3 egg whites in the serving bowl with ½ kg powdered sugar until it is white and foamy; then pour the hot soup into it while stirring.

title: Hvid Sagosuppe (White Sago Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound raisins
- 2 lemons
- several sticks cinnamon
- 1 pint cleaned small sago grains
- 3.5 pots boiling water
- 5 egg yolks
- 5 spoonfuls powdered sugar
- sugar to taste
- 1/2 bottle old white wine

## Directions

1. Pick and wash the raisins.
2. Peel the lemons; put the yellow peel together with several sticks of cinnamon, the raisins, and 1 pint of cleaned small sago grains (see recipe 36) in a pot with 3.5 pots of boiling water and cook for just over half an hour under a lid, until the grains are tender.
3. Beat 5 egg yolks with 5 spoonfuls of powdered sugar, then whisk this mixture into the soup.
4. Just before serving, add the lemon slices (with pits removed), sugar to taste, and half a bottle of old white wine.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvid Sauce (White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart Bouillon
- 1 Tablespoon Wheat Flour
- Butter
- Nutmeg
- Cayenne
- white Roots
- small Meat balls
- preserved Oysters, Capers or Anchovies

## Directions

1. For 1 Quart Bouillon take 1 Tablespoon good Wheat Flour, which is stirred out in a piece of Butter.
2. This must first be melted in a Pot, and when it begins to boil, put the Flour in and rub it out.
3. It must absolutely not become brown, and it is gradually thinned with the Broth.
4. Add Nutmeg, Cayenne, white Roots, small Meat balls, and otherwise what you have of preserved Oysters, Capers or Anchovies.
5. If it is for Chickens, Turkeys or Calf's Breast, it is also good to whisk a Spoon of Cream into the Sauce when it is to be served; but for Geese and Ducks it does not suit.
6. Have the Sauce a little sharper with Cayenne for these, and do not use Oysters.
7. If you want to make the White Sauce for Geese sour and sweet, then do not make it so sharp, and use only white Roots and Apples.
8. Some use sliced Almonds and Currants for this.

title: Hvid Sauce til Høns, Kalve- og Lammekjød (White Sauce for Chicken, Veal and Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint Cream
- Pepper, preferably white
- Shell of half a small Lemon
- 2 to 3 Ounces Butter
- 1 Tablespoon Flour
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Boil 1 Pint Cream very gently with a little crushed Pepper, preferably white, and the Shell of half a small Lemon.
2. When it has boiled a little, add a Thickening of 2 to 3 Ounces Butter (depending on how thick the Cream is), stirred with 1 Tablespoon Flour.
3. Stir constantly until it has become thick, and then thin it with Bouillon until the Sauce has obtained a suitable Thickness.
4. When serving, press the Juice of half a Lemon into it.
5. Good Milk may be used instead of Cream, but then use more Butter.
6. Instead of Lemon Shell and Juice, cook so much strong Broth or good Soy in the Sauce that it gets a reddish Colour.
7. For 8 to 10 Persons.

title: Hvid Soya af Agurker (White Soy from Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- flesh, seeds and juice from hollowed cucumbers
- Salt
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the flesh, seeds and juice hollowed from cucumbers on the stove with a little salt and cook for 1/2 hour with constant stirring so nothing sticks, which would give a burnt or musty taste; if this happens the whole must be discarded
2. Press through a sieve
3. Return the juice to the stove with one gill vinegar per pint of juice and cook until it is smooth, about 1/2 hour
4. Place in small bottles or jars and bind well the next day
5. This soy gives a pleasant flavor in all kinds of white sauces, fricassees and the like, and must be added early so it cooks a bit with the sauce

title: Hvid sødt Sauce (White Sweet Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- old Wine mixed with Water and Sugar
- 1 Pint
- Juice and grated Shell of half a Lemon
- 1 Teaspoon Corn Starch
- 3 well-beaten Egg yolks
- cold Wine

## Directions

1. For this take old Wine mixed with Water and Sugar.
2. For 1 Pint of this use Juice and grated Shell of half a Lemon; thicken it with 1 Teaspoon Corn Starch, and when it is to be served, add 3 well-beaten Egg yolks, thinned with a little cold Wine.
3. It is used warm, preferably for Bread Pudding and Potato Pudding, but can also be used for others, if you wish.

title: Hvid Vin-Gelé (White Wine Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) chopped powdered sugar
- 1/4 L (1 pint) water
- 1 bottle Rhenish wine or Graves (with a little Madeira or Sherry optionally added)
- 12 sheets isinglass
- 1 1/4 dl (1/2 pint) water
- fresh grapes (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the chopped powdered sugar with 1/4 L (1 pint) water to a syrup.
2. Pour in 1 bottle Rhenish wine or Graves, but the wine must not boil.
3. Melt 12 sheets of isinglass in 1 1/4 dl (1/2 pint) water without boiling and stir into the jelly.
4. Pour the jelly through a clean cloth into a mold moistened with water and strewn with sugar.
5. Fresh grapes can be layered in the mold: pour a little jelly into the bottom, and when it is set, lay some grapes, then jelly again and more grapes, until the mold is full.
6. While the mold is being filled, keep the remaining jelly near heat so it does not set.
7. The jelly can be arranged in small mounds on pineapple slices, decorated with grapes and foam peaks, and garnished with petits fours.
8. Prepared this way, it is not necessary to clarify the jelly.
9. At serving, turn the jelly out and serve with cream sauce.

title: Hvid Vin-Kréme (White Wine Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints French white wine
- 1 pint water
- 16 beaten egg yolks
- 1 pound white sugar
- 8 beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Made like Citron-Kréme (No. 273), but without lemons.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvid-Vin-Sauce (White Wine Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 bottle Rhine wine or Graves
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) powdered sugar
- 5 egg yolks (whites carefully separated)

## Directions

1. Pour 1/2 bottle Rhine wine or Graves into a fairly large saucepan with 125 grams powdered sugar and 5 egg yolks, with the whites carefully separated.
2. Set the sauce over heat and whisk strongly with a whisk until it is about to boil.
3. Then lift it quickly off and serve immediately.

title: Hvide Pebernødder (White Pepper Nuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 625 gr (1.25 lb) powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon cardamom
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 4 teaspoons chopped lemon zest
- 3 teaspoons hartshorn salt, dissolved in a little rose water
- 20 drops lemon oil
- 1 Kg (2 lb) flour

## Directions

1. Mix the eggs with the powdered sugar, cardamom, ginger, chopped lemon zest, hartshorn salt dissolved in rose water, and lemon oil
2. Add the flour
3. Roll the dough into thin sticks and cut very small
4. Place on a buttered baking sheet, not too close together
5. Bake until very crisp. This yields approximately 2.5 Kg (5 lb) of nuts

title: Hvide Polser (White Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound scalded rice grains
- 2 quarts milk
- ½ pound butter
- ½ pound powdered white sugar
- 8 eggs
- ½ pound scalded, coarsely ground almonds
- peel of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- salt
- 1 pound scalded raisins

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound scalded rice grains almost completely soft in 2 quarts milk
2. Pour it into a bowl and stir in ½ pound butter, ½ pound powdered white sugar, 8 eggs, ½ pound scalded, coarsely ground almonds, the peel of 1 lemon finely chopped, a little salt and 1 pound scalded raisins
3. Stir well
4. Fill the intestines about half full with the mixture and cook for ½ hour
5. Since they are usually made at the same time as the black sausages, the white ones should be cooked first in the water
6. If they are to be warmed on another day, both kinds of sausages can be warmed together
7. Serve with the same sauce as the black ones

title: Hvide Pølser (White Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pounds scalded rice groats
- 3 Pots sweet milk
- 20 Quintals melted butter
- 10 beaten eggs
- 0.5 Pound washed raisins without seeds
- 0.5 Pound finely chopped lard
- 12 Quintals sweet finely ground almonds
- 5 Quintals bitter finely ground almonds
- 18 Quintals ground icing sugar
- Ground nutmeg
- Intestines
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. 2 Pounds of scalded rice groats are cooked half-soft into a stiff porridge in 3 pots of sweet milk.
2. When the porridge is somewhat cooled, add 20 Quintals of melted butter, 10 beaten eggs, 0.5 Pound of washed raisins without seeds, 0.5 Pound of finely chopped lard, 12 Quintals of sweet and 5 Quintals of bitter, finely ground almonds, 18 Quintals of ground icing sugar and a little ground nutmeg.
3. Stir everything well and stuff into cleaned intestines that have first lain for one hour in soak in lukewarm milk.
4. They are then cooked in water with a little salt in it.
5. The sausages should not be stuffed too firmly; the intestines should only be three-quarters full.
6. They are arranged whole, either boiled or roasted with melted butter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvideroer (Stewed Parsnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsnips
- Water
- Butter
- Flour
- Cream or milk
- Parsley
- Sugar
- Salt
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Peel, cut into pieces, cook tender by steam or in water and drain; melt butter, in which flour is mixed, cream or milk, which is cooked to a smooth sauce, into which the roots are placed together with chopped parsley, a small piece of sugar and finally salt. You can also cook the roots with a little bouillon and a little water. When they are tender, make a roux of butter and flour and chopped parsley.

title: Hvidkaal (White Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- White cabbage
- Butter
- Nutmeg
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Milk
- Cream

## Directions

1. Prepare like cauliflower, cut into 4 parts for cooking, and when they are to be stewed, cut them again into smaller pieces and keep warm while the sauce is made. A good piece of butter is stirred with a little nutmeg over heat and served in a sauce bowl or poured over the cabbage when it is arranged. You can also stir flour into melted butter, approximately 1 ounce flour in 2 ounces butter, spread it with bouillon and give the cabbage a brief cooking in this together with salt and nutmeg. You can stew the cabbage in milk, then melt a little butter, in which flour is stirred out, cream or milk, and it is cooked evenly. The cabbage then gets a brief cooking in this with a piece of sugar and finally salt. In everything where there is milk, salt is added last and only after it is taken off the heat.

title: Hvidkaal, stuvet og brunet (Stewed and Browned White Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- White cabbage, pointed cabbage, or savoy cabbage
- Butter
- Sugar
- Broth
- Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare cooked white cabbage, pointed cabbage, or savoy cabbage the same way as cauliflower. Generally it is browned by cutting the head into pieces, which are placed in browned butter with sugar and cooked tender under a lid over even heat. Salt and broth are added to the sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvidkaal-Salat - Snittet (Cabbage Salad - Sliced)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small white cabbage head
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Oil

## Directions

1. Slice the white cabbage into thread-fine strips without cutting any of the stem or the coarse ribs in the leaves.
2. Sprinkle with salt, place in a press for 1 hour, just as one treats cucumber salad.
3. Prepare as this with vinegar and pepper.
4. Add oil to taste.

title: Hvidkaal-Salat med Mayonnaise - Hakket (Chopped Cabbage Salad with Mayonnaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small, raw white cabbage head
- Mayonnaise made with mustard
- Cooked carrots
- Green head lettuce

## Directions

1. Chop a small, raw white cabbage head fairly fine and turn this chopped cabbage in mayonnaise made with mustard.
2. Decorate the salad with coarsely chopped cooked carrots and a little sliced green head lettuce.

title: Hvidkaal-Suppe (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat stock
- 20 whole peppercorns
- root vegetables
- 1 large cabbage head, cut into 4 parts
- 4 to 5 tablespoons oat flour or wheat flour

## Directions

1. When the stock is skimmed, add approximately 20 whole peppercorns, the root vegetables and a large cabbage head that has been cut into 4 parts
2. When both are done, take them out; if you wish the soup to be smooth, add 4 to 5 tablespoons of stirred oat flour or wheat flour to the soup, with which it cooks until the meat is tender and the soup is smooth
3. After this is strained, place it back on the fire together with the root vegetables and cabbage, which are cut into smaller pieces and give it a good boil
4. It is eaten with toasted bread

title: Hvidkaal-Suppe (White Cabbage Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 L (5 pots) stock
- 1 large head white cabbage
- 1 teaspoon caraway
- 20 peppercorns
- salt
- flour
- butter
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cooked mostly the same as green cabbage soup on salted pork.
2. Divide 1 head white cabbage into 4 parts, which are scalded in boiling water.
3. Cut away the stem and chop the cabbage finely.
4. A couple of spoonfuls of flour can be chopped into the cabbage as directed for green cabbage, or it can be baked as with other thickened soups.
5. Cook the cabbage for a quarter hour in the stock together with 1 teaspoon caraway and 20 peppercorns.
6. Peeled potatoes cooked in the stock can be served with it; the pork is served alongside.

title: Hvidkaalsuppe paa Svineryg (White Cabbage Soup with Pork Back)
body:
## Ingredients

- Svineryg (pork back) or other pork or salt meat, 8 til 10 Pd.
- Vand (water)
- Et fast Hvidkaalshoved (a firm head of white cabbage)
- Gulerødder (carrots)
- Persillerødder (parsley roots)
- En Selleri (celery)
- 20 hele Peberkorn (20 whole peppercorns)
- En lille Spiseskefuld stødt Kommen (a small tablespoon of ground caraway)
- Raaskrællede, kogte og ituskaarne Kartofler (raw peeled, cooked, and cut potatoes)
- Salt

## Directions

1. Break the pork back at the joints before cooking.
2. Place 8 to 10 pounds of pork in a pot with enough water to cover it well.
3. Remove the outer leaves and tough stalk from the cabbage head; cut the rest into fine strips.
4. When the broth has boiled and been skimmed, add the cabbage strips along with carrots, parsley roots, and celery.
5. Place 20 whole peppercorns and a small tablespoon of ground caraway in a small tied linen bag and add to the soup.
6. Remove the bag before serving.
7. When the cabbage and vegetables are tender, remove them and cut the vegetables in pieces.
8. Strain the broth and bring to a boil again.
9. Pour into a tureen with the cabbage, vegetables, and raw peeled, cooked, and cut potatoes.
10. Sprinkle the pork back with a little salt and serve alongside the soup.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvidling (Whiting)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Hvidling (Whiting)
- Salt
- smeltet Smør (Melted butter)
- hakket Persille (Chopped parsley)
- kogte Kartofler (Boiled potatoes)

## Directions

1. Clean the whiting as you would cod.
2. Place whole in boiling salted water.
3. Cook for only a few minutes.
4. Serve with melted butter and chopped parsley along with boiled potatoes.

title: Hvidling à l'Anglaise (Whiting à l'Anglaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Hvidling (Whiting)
- Maître d'hôtel-filer
- Tartar-Smør (Tartar butter)

## Directions

1. Prepare the whiting fillet as Torske-Filet No. 567.
2. Serve with maître d'hôtel fillet and tartar butter (No. 745-746).

title: Hvidlingen (Whiting)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hvidlingen (whiting)
- Water
- Parsley
- Butter, melted

## Directions

1. Does not keep well; must be eaten fresh immediately
2. Place in boiling water
3. Cook and serve with parsley and melted butter or sour butter sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hvidroe-Salat (White Turnip Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked turnips
- False mayonnaise
- Watercress
- Spinach juice

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked turnips into strips and turn them in false mayonnaise and garnish with watercress.
2. Alternatively, color the mayonnaise green with spinach juice.

title: Hyben (Rose Hip Preserve)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/4 dl vinegar
- 3/4 dl rose water
- 1/2 kg rose hips

## Directions

1. Select beautiful large red garden rose hips that are firm in flesh and dry carefully with a cloth.
2. Keep the stems on but remove the sepals.
3. Cut a slit lengthwise into each fruit with a knife and remove the small seeds and hairs through the slit.
4. Boil sugar, rose water, and vinegar together to make a syrup and skim.
5. Add the fruit and boil until tender.
6. Pour everything through a sieve and boil the liquid again over heat until well reduced.
7. Mix the fruit with the syrup and pour into glasses or jars and tie them up when the preserve is done.
8. When serving, pour a little rose water over it for a fine effect.

title: Hyben med Rom (Rose Hip Preserve with Rum)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/4 liter Jamaica rum
- 1 kg rose hips

## Directions

1. Prepare the rose hips according to the previous instructions.
2. Place them in boiling water and boil until tender, then immediately strain through a sieve.
3. Melt the sugar in 1/4 liter of the water in which the rose hips were boiled and pour the warm syrup over them.
4. Over the next two days, drain the syrup and reboil it, pouring it over the fruit only after it has cooled.
5. On the fourth day, drain the syrup again and boil it down to a very thick syrup.
6. Add the rose hips without boiling them again.
7. After partially cooling, pour the rum over the preserve.
8. Pour into glasses and tie them up.

title: Hyben-Suppe (Rose Hip Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 575 g dried rose hips
- 4½ L water
- Lemon peel
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak the rinsed and dried fruit the evening before it is to be cooked.
2. Use the water in which it has stood for cooking.
3. Cook the soup, strain it, thicken with sago flour, sweeten and serve with baked bread.
4. Cook lemon peel and cinnamon together with the berries.
5. If using fresh rose hips, slit the fruit lengthwise and remove the small nuts and hairs through the slit.
6. Cook as other fruit soup and serve with raisins added.

title: Hyldebær-Suppe (Elderberry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg elderberries
- 4.5 L water
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- rhubarb juice, currant juice, or lemon juice
- vinegar
- rhubarb stalks

## Directions

1. Remove elderberries from stems, rinse and cook for half an hour in water with lemon peel and cinnamon
2. Strain the soup
3. Add acidity with rhubarb juice, currant juice, lemon juice, or vinegar
4. Can cook rhubarb stalks together with berries in autumn

title: Hyldebærsaft (Elder Berry Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Elder berries
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) farina per 1 liter (1 pot) juice

## Directions

1. Pick the berries and put them in a pot; place over very low heat until they release their juice well, then strain through a fine sieve.
2. Measure the juice and boil for a quarter hour with the sugar; skim it and pour hot into bottles, which are tied immediately with parchment paper.
3. The elder berries are then boiled with water, with a little vinegar added, and the juice, after being strained, can be poured into dry bottles, corked, and gradually used in the household for elder berry soup.

title: Hyldebærsaft (Elderberry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Elderberries
- 3 pounds of powdered sugar per pot of juice
- A few pieces of cinnamon
- Cognac

## Directions

1. Strip and place the berries over the fire, preferably in a clean earthenware pot
2. When they begin to burst, take them out, set them aside to cool and wring them in a clean linen cloth
3. Filter the juice if necessary and place it over the fire with the sugar and cinnamon
4. Cook with regular skimming until after about a quarter of an hour it begins to appear clear, then remove it from the fire and add a little Cognac
5. When the juice has cooled, fill it into bottles, whose upper part is filled again with Cognac
6. Cork the bottles well
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Høns eller Kyllinger à la Marengo (Chicken Marengo)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 chickens
- 1 L (1 pot) stock, gravy, or water
- salt
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- 5-6 tablespoons tomato purée
- preserved or fresh mushrooms
- paprika
- 1¼ dl (1/10 pægl) Madeira or Sherry
- butter
- sago flour
- puff pastry strips or border

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the chickens and cut them into 6 pieces each.
2. Brown the pieces in a slice of butter in a pot.
3. Pour over with 1 L (1 pot) stock, gravy, or water, seasoned with salt and Maggi bouillon cubes.
4. Stir in 5-6 tablespoons of tomato purée and some preserved or fresh mushrooms, paprika, 1¼ dl (1/10 pægl) Madeira or Sherry, and coloring to give the sauce a beautiful brown color.
5. Cook the chickens gently in this sauce.
6. Thicken the sauce with sago flour.
7. Pour the sauce over the chickens when serving and garnish with puff pastry strips or border.

title: Høns eller Kyllinger à la Marengo (Chicken à la Marengo)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4.5 chickens
- butter
- 1 liter of spare broth, gravy, or water
- salt
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- 5 to 6 tablespoons tomato puree
- mushrooms, canned or fresh
- paprika
- 1.25 deciliters Madeira or sherry
- color for the sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the chickens and cut them into 6 pieces.
2. Brown them in a slice of butter and cover with 1 liter of spare broth, gravy, or water mixed with salt and Maggi bouillon cubes.
3. Stir in 5 to 6 tablespoons of tomato puree and some canned or fresh mushrooms, paprika, 1.25 deciliters of Madeira or sherry, and color to give the sauce a beautiful brown color.
4. Cook the chickens until tender in this mixture.

title: Høns i Gelée (Chicken in Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pints broth
- 4-5 quintal dissolved isinglass
- vinegar
- pepper
- 2 egg whites
- cooked chicken

## Directions

1. To the broth add the dissolved isinglass and vinegar and pepper to taste
2. Add 2 egg whites which have been whisked until white and stiff
3. Filter the whole and pour over cooked chicken cut into appropriate pieces
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Høns med Østers (Chicken with Oysters)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken or capon
- oysters
- water
- salt
- lemon juice
- stock
- flour
- butter
- egg yolks
- white wine

## Directions

1. Select an excellent, fat, and good chicken or capon. After cleaning and removing the skeleton, fill it with oysters and sew it closed along the back.
2. Place it on a rack in the pot to be steamed tender.
3. Boil the beard from the oysters in water with salt and lemon juice.
4. Mix it with a little stock made from the skeleton and neck, and add the oyster water from the shells.
5. Strain this stock and pour it over the chicken, which must not boil hard.
6. Within 1½ hours the meat will certainly be steamed tender, provided it is a young chicken.
7. For the sauce, make a beurre manié with flour and butter, dilute with the stock from the chicken, and bind with 2-3 egg yolks, seasoning with lemon juice and white wine.
8. Oysters can be added to the sauce, placing them in the hot sauce without bringing to a boil.
9. Remove the thread before serving the chicken.

title: Høns à la royale (Chicken à la Royale)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- good, smooth ragout of calf sweetbreads and mushrooms

## Directions

1. Remove the skeleton from the chickens and fill them with a good, smooth ragout of calf sweetbreads and mushrooms.
2. Sew them closed tightly.
3. The chickens can either be boiled or roasted; if roasted, lard the breast with fine, fresh strips of fat.
4. For roasted chicken, make a brown mushroom sauce from the gravy. For boiled chicken, make a white mushroom sauce from the stock.
5. Serve the chickens with puff pastry strips or border.

title: Høns-Salat Nr. 1 (Chicken Salad No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken, cooked until meat separates easily from bones
- Green lettuce
- 1 tablespoon salad oil
- 1 teaspoon vinegar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt and pepper
- 1 raw egg yolk
- 1/2 teaspoon each of salt, dry mustard, and white pepper
- Juice of 1/2 lemon
- Cayenne pepper to taste
- 3 gills salad oil
- Juice of 1/2 lemon
- 4 tablespoons vinegar
- Olives
- Capers
- Hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cook chicken until meat separates easily from bones
2. Remove all meat from bones, free from skin and fat, and cut into pieces about 3/8 inch or smaller
3. Wash green lettuce, break into small pieces, and dry on a cloth
4. Mix meat and lettuce in a deep dish and season with 1 tablespoon salad oil, 1 teaspoon vinegar, and 1/2 teaspoon salt and pepper
5. Prepare a mayonnaise as follows: blend 1 raw egg yolk with 1/2 teaspoon each of salt, dry mustard, and white pepper, juice of 1/2 lemon, and cayenne pepper to taste
6. Mix well with a whisk, then add 3 gills salad oil and juice of 1/2 lemon drop by drop while stirring constantly
7. When two-thirds of the oil is incorporated, begin adding vinegar, totaling 4 tablespoons
8. Cover the salad with the mayonnaise and decorate with olives, capers, and hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters

title: Høns-Salat Nr. 2 (Chicken Salad No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken, cut into very small pieces
- Celery, cut into same-sized pieces
- Equal parts chicken and celery
- Hard-boiled eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon mustard per egg
- Salt
- 1 tablespoon oil per egg
- 1 wine glass vinegar per egg

## Directions

1. Cut chicken meat from bones into very small pieces, almost like peas
2. Cut celery the same way
3. Use equal parts chicken and celery
4. Grind hard-boiled egg yolks finely; to each egg add 1/2 teaspoon mustard, same amount salt, 1 tablespoon oil, and 1 wine glass vinegar
5. Mix chicken and celery well in a deep dish
6. Pour the sauce (to which a little cream has been added at the end) over them
7. Do not pour the sauce over until the salad is to be served

title: Høns-Salat Nr. 3 (Chicken Salad No. 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- Same quantity of celery as chicken
- 3 or 4 eggs
- 1 tablespoon prepared mustard
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon cold butter
- 2/3 cup vinegar
- 5 tablespoons salad oil
- 2/3 cup cold cream

## Directions

1. Cut cooked chicken meat and celery into small pieces
2. Beat the whites and yolks of eggs separately
3. Whisk oil slowly into the yolks
4. Mix all ingredients (except cream) in a casserole and place over heat
5. Cook until fairly thick
6. When cool again, whisk the cream and pour over the salad just before serving

title: Hønse-Gelee (Chicken Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken, about 2 pounds
- 2 quarts cold water
- salt and light seasoning, if the doctor permits

## Directions

1. Pluck the chicken and remove all the fat; cut it open and clean carefully so as to avoid breaking the gall and entrails
2. Cut up the meat and break the bones into pieces
3. Place in a casserole with 2 quarts of cold water, bring slowly to a boil and skim until clear
4. Cover with a lid and simmer gently until the chicken has become a collapsed mass, about 2 to 4 hours
5. Strain through a flannel cloth until clear and return to the fire to boil gently until it has become a complete jelly
6. Test by placing a spoonful of jelly on a saucer; if it is stiff when cool, it is ready
7. Season lightly if the doctor permits and pour into cups; serve cold or slightly warmed

title: Hønse-Gratin (Chicken Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Høns
- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) Ærter (henkogte)
- Smør
- Mel
- Ærtevandet
- Hønsekødsuppe
- 2 å 3 Æggeblommer
- 4 Æg
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) Mælk
- Blomkål

## Directions

1. Når Hønsene er kogt, skæres Kødet i passende Stykker og arrangeres på et lidt dybt Fad, som kan tåle at udsættes for Varmen i Ovnen.
2. Af Smør Mel, Ærtevandet og Hønsekødsuppen laves en godt Frikassé-Sauce, som kan forbedres med 2 å 3 Æggeblommer.
3. 4 Æg piskes med 1/4 L (1 Pægl) Mælk og hældes over Stuvningen, som sættes i Ovnen og bages 1 Kvarter, eller til Æggemassen er bagt.
4. Ved Anretningen kan der omkring Kanten af Fadet lægges en Krans af Ærterne, og med Mellemrum kan stikkes små Buketter af Blomkål deri.

title: Hønse-Karry (Chicken Curry - East Indian Style)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- a good slice of butter
- 5 tablespoons chopped onions
- 1 tablespoon curry
- 1½ liters boiling water
- salt
- rice flour for thickening
- water for thickening
- mashed potatoes
- rice for serving
- dry curry or browned onions or chutney for garnish

## Directions

1. Brown a good slice of butter in a pot.
2. Sauté the chopped onions and curry in the butter.
3. Add the raw chickens cut into 4 to 6 pieces.
4. Pour in 1½ liters of boiling water and add salt.
5. Cook the chickens until tender.
6. Thicken the sauce with rice flour mixed in water and allow to boil well.
7. Serve with both mashed potatoes and rice.
8. Place the mashed potatoes in a mound in the center of a platter, arrange the chicken in curry around it, and place rice in a ring around the outside.
9. Sprinkle with dry curry or garnish with browned onions or chutney.

title: Hønse-Salat (Chicken Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover chicken meat
- Cooked celery
- Chicken carcasses
- Watercress for garnish
- Hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Pick the meat from the bones and cut it very fine together with a cooked celery
2. Boil the carcasses to make the sauce, prepared exactly as for Fish Salad (No. 278)
3. Garnish the salad with watercress and slices of hard-boiled eggs

title: Hønsekjød-Suppe No. 1 (Chicken Soup No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- water
- white root vegetables
- bread or flour balls
- parsley
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. It is made like other meat stock; however, take only 1/4 water over the portion of soup you want if the chickens are young; if they are old and fatty, nearly double of what you want to keep must be taken
2. Cut up the chickens so that the legs and wings are taken off; next cut the backbone from the breast; cut it over in 3 parts, the breast is split, and each half is divided in two; legs, offal and outer wings are taken along
3. On chicken soup only white root vegetables are used, and bread or flour balls, parsley and a little nutmeg

title: Hønsekjød-Suppe No. 2 (Chicken Soup No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hen
- white root vegetables
- beef
- water
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 pint white wine (optional)
- nutmeg (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook a hen with white root vegetables and beef in so much water that it stands over it
2. When it is done, take it out and cut the meat from the bones in small pieces; the bones are cut up and cook somewhat longer in the soup
3. Strain this and place it back on the fire with the cooked white root vegetables and chicken meat to get a little boil
4. Then pour it into the tureen in which 4 egg yolks have been beaten
5. It is good to have 1/2 pint white wine and a little nutmeg in the eggs
6. Eaten with toasted bread

title: Hønsekjødsuppe (Chicken Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound hen per portion
- 3 pots water
- veal shank (optional)
- salt
- soup vegetables
- dumplings or bread dumplings

## Directions

1. If you do not wish to use the cooked hen as a second course, this soup can be made with an old bird.
2. When the hen is slaughtered, soak it for one hour in cold water, then scald, pluck, clean and place it over heat with 3 pots water.
3. If you also have a veal shank to put in the pot, the soup will be much stronger.
4. When the soup boils, skim it well, add a little salt and soup vegetables, then continue cooking under even heat and under a covered lid until the vegetables are tender.
5. Remove these, cut them into pieces and place them in the tureen, while the soup continues to cook until the meat is tender.
6. Remove it, strain the soup and give it a good boil with the dumplings, and when the dumplings are tender, pour the soup into the tureen.
7. You can use small, fine dumplings or bread dumplings.
8. If the hen is old, it must preferably be chopped up before cooking, and all strength extracted from it, so the meat is not worth much.
9. Young hens (or turkeys) are instead cooked whole and can be served as a second course with cream horseradish.
10. To prevent the liver from cooking apart, place it during cooking inside the carcass with the cleaned giblets and heart.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hønsekød-Suppe (Chicken Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 hens or young roosters
- 2½ kg (5 lbs) veal shank
- 1 celery
- 4 carrots
- 1 good bunch of herbs

## Directions

1. Place the hens and veal shank in cold water with salt, equal in amount to the poultry.
2. Once the scum has been removed, add 1 celery, 4 carrots, and a good bunch of herbs.
3. Cook until the hens are tender.
4. Strain the soup through a fine sieve, leaving only a little liquid in the pot to keep the hens steaming hot until serving.
5. Skim the fat well from the soup, and only when it has been returned to the fire and comes to a boil again, pour it into the tureen.
6. Serve with small cut filets of meat, meatballs, vegetables, and similar garnishes.

title: Hønsekødfars (Chicken Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken breast meat
- 125 g cold butter or margarine

## Directions

1. From chickens, which need not be young for this, take only the breast meat, which is chopped and pounded with 125 g cold butter or margarine per 1/2 kg meat.
2. Otherwise stir and handle it as for beef and veal forcemeat.
3. The remainder of the chickens can be used for soup, chicken salad and various fillings or fricassée.

title: Hønsekødfars (Chicken Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken breast meat
- 125 grams cold butter or margarine per 1/2 kg meat

## Directions

1. Take only the breast meat from chicken
2. Chop and pound the meat with cold butter or margarine (125 grams per 1/2 kg meat)
3. Treat and handle as for beef and veal forcemeat
4. The remaining parts of the chicken can be used for soup, chicken salad, various fillings, or fricassée

title: Hønsepostej (Chicken Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry dough
- fat hens
- veal forcemeat
- mushroom stew

## Directions

1. Line a smooth mold with puff pastry dough.
2. Cook fat hens and cut into suitable pieces.
3. Mix in veal forcemeat that has been cooked, cooled and cut into suitable pieces.
4. Dip each piece in a good mushroom stew.
5. In the mold, layer alternately a layer of forcemeat and a layer of chicken, so that the top layer becomes chicken.
6. Over this place a layer of puff pastry dough.
7. Bake the pie for one hour in a hot oven.
8. When it is to be served, loosen it with a knife at the sides of the mold and carefully turn it onto the dish.
9. Serve with mushroom sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Hønsesalat (Chicken Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon fine salad oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Mustard
- Vinegar
- Broth from the chicken
- 1 pint Madeira
- Fine, green salad leaves for garnish

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken until very tender, then let it cool
2. Remove the bones and cut the meat into small cubes
3. Place 6 hard-boiled egg yolks, 1 tablespoon fine salad oil, salt, pepper, mustard, vinegar and broth from the chicken over heat, stirring until it begins to boil
4. Remove from heat, strain through a sieve, and add 1 pint Madeira
5. Place the meat in the sauce and cover it well until it is cold and ready to be served
6. Garnish with fine, green salad leaves
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Høvlspaaner (Wood Shavings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 12 ounces bitter almonds
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 egg white
- 1 egg
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- oblat wafer slices

## Directions

1. Grind sweet and bitter almonds with 1 egg yolk and 1 egg white until it becomes a tough dough
2. Stir 1 egg well with 1 pound powdered sugar and mix together with the almond dough
3. Spread the dough to the thickness of a straw on oblong slices of oblat
4. Bake the cakes light brown at slow heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Indbagt Faarekjöd (Breaded Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton meat
- Butter
- Flour
- Eggs
- White sauce
- White root vegetables
- Asparagus (optional)

## Directions

1. Chop the mutton as for fricassee and cook it, but not too thoroughly.
2. Clean the pieces of outer skin and remove any loose bones.
3. Brown them lightly in butter.
4. Prepare a dumpling dough without sugar and with one or two more eggs.
5. Spread this dough around all sides of the pieces.
6. Brown them again very lightly in butter.
7. Serve with white sauce and white root vegetables; asparagus is also suitable.

title: Indbagt Kaal (Fried Cauliflower)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cauliflower
- batter for cauliflower gratin (Nr. 64)
- milk
- breadcrumbs
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. After the cauliflower is cooked, separate it into small florets
2. Dip them in a batter prepared as for cauliflower gratin Nr. 64, thinning the batter with a little milk if needed
3. Place the dipped florets on a flat dish without touching each other
4. When the batter has cooled, dip them in ground breadcrumbs
5. Fry them light brown in fat
6. They can be used as a garnish around meat dishes or served as a first or middle course with butter

title: Indbagt Kjöd (Breaded Fried Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat or roast
- Flour
- Eggs
- Butter
- Milk
- Sugar
- Salt
- Clarified butter
- Apple sauce, gooseberry sauce, rhubarb stalks, or other vegetables

## Directions

1. Slice the meat or roast into very thin slices.
2. Make a pancake batter from one pound flour, 3 beaten eggs, two ounces melted butter, one pint milk, a little sugar and salt.
3. Dip the meat slices in the batter and fry on the pan in clarified butter.
4. Serve with apple sauce, gooseberry sauce, rhubarb stalks or other vegetables.
5. This portion of batter is sufficient for 8 persons.

title: Indbagt Kjød (Meat in Pancake Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat or roast, sliced very thin
- 1 pound flour
- 3 beaten eggs
- 2 ounces melted butter
- 1 pint milk
- a little sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the meat or roast into very thin slices and dip them in a pancake batter made from 1 pound flour, 3 beaten eggs, 2 ounces melted butter, 1 pint milk, a little sugar and salt
2. Fry the slices on the griddle in clarified butter
3. Serve with vegetables such as turnip, gooseberry, rhubarb stalks or other sorts of vegetables
4. This amount of batter is sufficient for 8 persons

title: Indbagt Lammebryst (Breaded Lamb Breast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb meat
- Batter (Nr. 560)
- Palmin or lard
- Butter
- Vegetables
- Salad

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into smaller pieces and cook them, then dip them in a batter (see Nr. 560) which must completely cover the meat.
2. Fry in Palmin until it has a nice, light brown color.
3. Breaded meat is eaten either with browned butter and garnished with vegetables or with a piquant sauce and green salad.

title: Indbagt Makrel (Battered Mackerel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mackerel
- Pancake batter

## Directions

1. Clean the mackerel and cut from the bones
2. Cut into smaller pieces
3. Dip the pieces in pancake batter
4. Cook in clarified butter as explained in recipe 560
5. Arrange and serve with sauce as for battered plaice

title: Indbagt Rødspætte (Plaice in Batter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- plaice fillets
- salt
- flour
- eggs
- margarine
- sugar
- milk
- butter
- lard or Palmin
- lemon
- lettuce
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut out fillets and dry after standing sprinkled with salt
2. Mix batter: 1/4 kg flour, 3 eggs, 50 gr melted margarine, salt, slight sugar and 2½ dl milk stirred together to a thick pancake batter
3. Take fish with a fork and dip into batter so it completely covers it
4. Place directly into hot lard or Palmin
5. Cook quickly until batter has a nice light brown color
6. Serve with stirred butter, chutney or tarragon butter, or with mayonnaise or maître d'hôtel butter
7. Place stirred butter in a bowl in center of platter, arrange fish around it decorated with lemon, green salad or parsley

title: Indbagte Høns à la Villeroi (Chicken Coated à la Villeroi)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens, cooked
- batter (as in recipe 560)
- clarified butter or Palmin for frying
- cooked rice
- Chutney sauce (recipe 759) or Macaroni and Tomato sauce (recipe 790)

## Directions

1. Cook and portion the chickens as for breaded chicken.
2. Dip them in batter.
3. Fry light brown in clarified butter or Palmin.
4. Serve with cooked rice and Chutney sauce, or with macaroni and Tomato sauce.

title: Indbagte Kyllingbryster (Baked Chicken Breasts)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Meat forcemeat
- Puff pastry
- Broth
- Thickening for white sauce
- Stuffed celery (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the head with neck, bones and the outermost wing from the chicken
2. Divide it into 4 parts, first down the middle of the breast bone and then across
3. Both these and simpler pieces are cooked; but the best ones are removed before they are too tender
4. When cold, coat each piece with meat forcemeat and wrap puff pastry around it so the outside is smooth and the seam is on the underside
5. Roast on plates in the baking oven which should be as hot as for wheat bread; but be prepared to cover them with paper as puff pastry should never brown
6. Prepare sauce from the broth as white sauce usually is; sometimes stuffed celery is used for this dish; then no root vegetables are used in the sauce itself

title: Indbagte Æbler i Butterdejg (Apples Baked in Pastry)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 large fine apples
- 375 gr (3/4 S) puff pastry
- preserved fruits (strawberry, plum, or prune)
- blanched almond
- egg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, remove the blossom and stem, and hollow out the core cavity so the apples remain whole.
2. Fill the cavity with various fruits such as a large preserved strawberry, plum, or prune (with the pit removed and a blanched almond inserted in its place), or leave empty.
3. Cover each apple completely with a thin layer of puff pastry, sealing it at the bottom near the stem, which should be turned downward on the baking sheet.
4. Brush the apples with beaten egg and sprinkle with a good layer of sugar.
5. Place on the baking sheet in a well-heated oven and bake for approximately 3/4 hour so the apples become very tender.
6. Serve very hot, placed on a napkin on a plate.
7. Whipped cream can be served separately.

title: Indisk Hummer-Suppe (Indian Lobster Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lobsters
- 4 L spare soup
- 125 gr butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 2 teaspoons curry
- 1/4 L raw cream

## Directions

1. Cook the lobsters, pick out the meat and pound the shells and roe with 125 gr butter
2. Boil these in the specified quantity of soup
3. Skim off the lobster butter
4. Thicken the soup with 6 beaten egg yolks in which 2 teaspoons curry and 1/4 L raw cream have been stirred
5. Do not let the soup boil after the eggs are added
6. Finally, whisk the lobster butter into the soup and pour it over the lobster meat in the tureen
7. Serve immediately with boiled rice served separately

title: Indisk Pickles (Indian Pickles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 l (5 pots) vinegar
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) ground ginger
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) mustard (Colman's Mustard)
- 60 gr (12 drams) whole black pepper
- 50 gr (10 drams) long pepper
- 10 gr (2 drams) Cayenne pepper
- 6 cloves garlic
- A little Indian saffron to give it a suitable yellow color, or 2 tablespoons curry
- Small carrots (scraped)
- Small cucumbers (dried)
- Sugar peas (ribboned and cut into smaller pieces)
- String beans (ribboned and cut into smaller pieces)
- Pearl beans (ribboned and cut into smaller pieces)
- Cauliflower (divided into small florets)
- Pearl onions (peeled)
- Shallots (peeled)
- Red onions (peeled)
- Celery (peeled and cut into pieces)
- Nasturtium seeds
- A medium-sized white cabbage head
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place the spices in a larger jar and pour the boiling vinegar over them.
2. Cover the jar and let it stand for the next day to be tied shut.
3. As the vegetables mentioned in the previous recipe appear during the course of the summer, rinse them in a little cold vinegar and dry them in a cloth before placing them in the jar.
4. For the cabbage, cut a medium-sized white cabbage head into strips and sprinkle this cabbage together with the chopped cauliflower with salt and let it stand in a dish for a couple of days; then pour off the brine and press the cabbage dry in a cloth before filling it into the jar.
5. When you have collected what you want for the pickles jar, pour off the vinegar and mix the vegetables together well as they are placed in smaller glasses or jars.
6. Give the vinegar a boil and pour it boiling over the vegetables in the glasses, which, as soon as the vinegar is cold, are tied shut either with bladders, parchment paper, or, in the case of the smaller glasses, corked and sealed.
7. There must be plenty of vinegar over the vegetables; if, after it has boiled, there is not enough, some more must be boiled, which is added spices in proportion to the amount of vinegar.
8. This pickles can keep for years, but must stand for 1 year before being used; otherwise the boiling of the vinegar must be repeated a couple of times to soften the vegetables.

title: Ingeferol (Ginger Ale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 13 pounds sugar
- 13 ounces ginger
- 23 ounces cream of tartar
- 1 lemon, sliced and seeded
- 7 quarts boiling water
- 0.5 gill yeast

## Directions

1. Place the sugar and spices in a stone crock, pour the boiling water over, stir, cover well and let stand for 24 hours in a cool place. Then open the crock, add the yeast and stir well, let stand for another 24 hours. Then pour into bottles, cork and seal with wax. Can be served after 3 days.

title: Ingefær-Budding (Ginger Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) icing sugar
- 1 L (1 Pot) boiling half-skimmed or sweet milk
- 10 leaves isinglass
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water
- 1 L (1 Pot) cream
- finely chopped ginger
- cream foam with ginger juice and liqueur

## Directions

1. Stir 10 egg yolks with 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) icing sugar
2. Whisk this mixture into 1 L (1 Pot) boiling half-skimmed or sweet milk
3. Cook to custard consistency
4. Dissolve 10 leaves of isinglass in 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water and stir into the custard
5. When the custard is partially cooled, stir in the foam from 1 L (1 Pot) cream along with finely chopped ginger
6. Pour the pudding into a wetted mold sprinkled with sugar
7. Serve with cream foam flavored with ginger juice and a little liqueur of some kind

title: Ingefær-Kager (Ginger Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams (1/4 lb) margarine
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) flour
- 375 grams (1/4 lb) icing sugar
- 3 eggs
- 30 grams (6 quintal) ground ginger
- 1 teaspoon baking soda, dissolved in 2 tablespoons milk
- candied orange peel

## Directions

1. Crumble together margarine, flour and icing sugar
2. Mix in the 3 eggs along with ground ginger and baking soda dissolved in milk
3. Roll the cakes thinly and cut out with a glass or mold
4. Top with a small piece of candied orange peel
5. Place on buttered sheets and bake in a well-heated oven

title: Ingefær-Sauce (Ginger Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter (1 pint) water
- 1 tablespoon potato starch
- 1/4 liter (1 pint) Graves wine
- Finely chopped preserved Chinese ginger with some of its syrup, to taste
- Preserved pumpkin with ginger (optional)

## Directions

1. When the water boils, smooth it with 1 tablespoon mixed potato starch.
2. Pour in 1/4 liter Graves wine and finely chopped preserved Chinese ginger with some of its syrup, to taste.
3. Add diced preserved pumpkin with ginger if desired.
4. A cheaper sauce can be made by using rhubarb juice instead of wine.

title: Irsk Stuvning (Irish Stew)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- fat, butter or margarine
- 1 fairly large cabbage head
- potatoes
- 1.5 kg meat (lamb breast, beef brisket, or lightly salted striped pork)
- ground pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. On the bottom of a pot place a little fat, butter or margarine.
2. A fairly large cabbage head is cut very coarsely, and the bottom of the pot is covered with some of this cabbage.
3. Thereon is laid a layer of raw, peeled potatoes cut into 2-4 pieces.
4. 1.5 kg meat is cut into smaller pieces; this can be either lamb breast, fat beef brisket or lightly salted striped pork cut into slices.
5. The meat is laid over the potatoes, sprinkled with ground pepper, and for fresh meat also with salt.
6. The meat is covered again with potatoes, and on top cabbage is laid.
7. Melted butter is poured over, the amount depending on the fattiness of the meat.
8. The lid is placed on the pot and must close well, since the dish must be softened by steam.
9. The pot, which must be shaken regularly during cooking, which lasts 3 hours, is placed over low heat to prevent the cabbage from burning on.
10. Meat and cabbage are arranged together on a dish.

title: Is-Punch à la Romaine (Punch Ice à la Romaine)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) powdered sugar
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) water
- Thinly pared and sliced zest of 1 orange
- Juice of 2 oranges
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 1/4 bottle good aged rum or arrack
- 1 bottle white wine or champagne
- 100 gr (20 quintals) powdered sugar
- Foam from 4 to 5 stiffly beaten egg whites
- Or alternatively foam from 1/2 L (1/2 pot) cream

## Directions

1. Cook the powdered sugar with the water to a thin syrup.
2. When cold, add the thinly pared and sliced orange zest along with the juice of 2 oranges and 2 lemons.
3. After 15 minutes pass, strain the whole mixture and pour it into the freezing container.
4. Pack this in a mixture of finely chopped ice and Spanish salt; treat as otherwise done when freezing ice.
5. Into the frozen mass work in 1/4 bottle of good aged rum or arrack along with 1 bottle of white wine or champagne.
6. Sprinkle 100 gr powdered sugar into the foam of 4 to 5 stiffly beaten egg whites, stir with a light hand until well blended, and stir this egg white mixture into the punch shortly before serving.
