7. Alternatively cream foam can be used instead of the egg white mixture.
8. Serve this thick punch preferably in flat champagne glasses; pour into glass decanters.

title: Israeli Kager (Israeli Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 10 eggs
- 1 pound wheat flour
- sliced almonds
- washed currants
- Ceylon cinnamon, ground

## Directions

1. Melt the butter, clarify it, and stir into a smooth paste with 1 pound powdered sugar
2. Add 10 eggs, 2 at a time, and stir well for 1 hour
3. Work in 1 pound wheat flour
4. Place the dough in a flat mold to the thickness of a two-crown coin
5. Sprinkle with sliced almonds, a few washed currants, and ground Ceylon cinnamon
6. Bake light brown
7. As soon as the cake is taken from the oven, cut it into oblong square pieces
8. Return them to the oven to dry at low heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Italiensk Kød-Salat (Italian Meat Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked, finely chopped macaroni
- beets
- carrots
- potatoes
- canned asparagus
- salt herring
- cooked smoked ham
- veal
- lost mayonnaise (see recipe 244)
- mustard
- capers
- pepper
- tarragon vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Use equal parts of each ingredient: cooked finely chopped macaroni, beets, carrots, potatoes, canned asparagus, salt herring, cooked smoked ham, and veal
2. Toss in lost mayonnaise seasoned with mustard, capers, pepper, tarragon vinegar, and oil to taste

title: Italiensk Salat (Italian Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal amounts of beets, potatoes, carrots, celery, apples, and pickles or aïoli
- False mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cut equal amounts of beets, potatoes, carrots, celery, apples, and pickles or aïoli into small cubes and turn in false mayonnaise.
2. This salad can be arranged in a puff pastry ring and served as a vegetable accompaniment to a roast.

title: Italiensk Salat (Italian Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal parts beets
- potatoes
- carrots
- celery
- apples
- canned asparagus or pickles

## Directions

1. Cut the beets, potatoes, carrots, celery, apples, and canned asparagus or pickles into small dice
2. Toss in lost mayonnaise
3. This salad can be arranged in a puff pastry ring and used as a vegetable side dish for roasts

title: Italiensk Salat (Italian Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint green peas
- small bundle of asparagus
- 1 pint string beans
- a couple of cents carrots
- parsnip roots
- a very small cauliflower head
- 1 large beet
- 1 tablespoon salad oil
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Shell 1 pint of green peas
2. Use the heads of a small bundle of asparagus
3. Cut 1 pint of string beans in different forms
4. Clean a couple of cents of carrots and parsnip roots and cut them into pieces about an inch in size
5. Cut a very small cauliflower head into small florets
6. Cook all these things and then place them in cold water to preserve their color
7. Wash and cook 1 large beet without breaking the skin or cutting off the top and root
8. When tender, peel it and cut it like the carrots
9. Arrange all the vegetables on a plate and prepare with 1 tablespoon salad oil, 1 tablespoon vinegar, 1 teaspoon salt and a little pepper

title: Italiensk Suppe med Makaroni (Italian Pasta Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pund Makaroni
- Salt
- 6 store Rødløg
- 6 Gulerødder
- rigeligt Smør
- 3 Spiseskefulde Mel
- 5 Potter god Suppe
- reven Parmesan Ost

## Directions

1. Knækkes Makaronien itu og lægges med lidt Salt i rigeligt kogende Vand, og koges et Øjeblik, men inden de endnu ere halvmøre, ophældes de i en Sigte og afkøles ved Overhældning med koldt Vand, hvorpaa de skæres i mindre Stykker paa et Par Tommers Længde.
2. Rengjøres 6 store Rødløg og 6 Gulerødder, skrælles og skæres i Skiver.
3. Brunes Løgene og Gulerodderne i rigeligt Smør.
4. Naar det er godt brunt, kommes 3 Spiseskefulde Mel deri, og det Hele omrøres godt, indtil Alt er rigtig brunt.
5. Spædes med 5 Potter god Suppe og koges godt sammen dermed.
6. Hældes det gjennem en Sigte, sættes atter over Ilden tilligemed de ituskaarne Makaroni og koger under Laag ved sagte Ild med disse, indtil de ere møre.
7. Skulde der ved Suppens Henstaaen danne sig en Hinde paa den, maa den aftages.
8. Suppen serveres med reven Parmesan Ost til.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Italiensk Suppe med Makaroni (Italian Pasta Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound macaroni
- Salt
- Water
- 6 large onions
- 6 carrots
- Plenty of butter
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 5 pots good broth

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into pieces and place with a little salt in plenty of boiling water
2. Cook just a moment, and before they are half tender, drain in a sieve
3. Cool by pouring cold water over them
4. Cut the macaroni into smaller pieces about a couple of inches long
5. Clean, peel, and cut 6 large onions and 6 carrots into slices
6. Brown them in plenty of butter
7. When well browned, add 3 tablespoons flour and stir well until everything is properly browned
8. Thin with 5 pots good broth
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Iødekager (Deer Antler Salt Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound fine flour
- 2 pounds butter
- 4 pounds sugar
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons deer antler salt dissolved in a little cold water
- egg yolk for brushing
- chopped almonds
- coarse powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Knead together ½ pound fine flour, 2 pounds butter, 4 pounds sugar, 3 eggs, and 2 tablespoons deer antler salt dissolved in a little cold water
2. Roll out on the baking board
3. Cut into small round cakes using a glass
4. Brush with egg yolk
5. Sprinkle with chopped almonds mixed with coarse powdered sugar
6. Bake at good heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Jeg kan ikke lade være (I Cannot Resist)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 4 pounds powdered, sifted sugar
- Juice of 2 large or 3 small lemons
- Egg whites
- 6 quintals cold, dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Stir 10 egg yolks well with 4 pounds powdered, sifted sugar
2. Stir in the juice of 2 large or 3 small lemons
3. Add the stiffly whipped egg whites and 6 quintals cold, dissolved isinglass
4. Let it stand for an hour and serve with whipped cream over and small biscuits on the side
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Jelly Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce Sugar
- a couple of Teaspoonfuls Currant Jelly
- 1 Pint boiling Water
- 2 Teaspoonfuls Corn Starch
- 1/2 Cup cold Water

## Directions

1. Melt 1 Ounce Sugar and a couple of Teaspoonfuls of Currant Jelly over the Fire in 1 Pint boiling Water.
2. Stir in 2 Teaspoonfuls Corn Starch dissolved in 1/2 Cup cold Water.
3. As soon as it boils up, it is done.

title: Jelly Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce sugar
- a couple of tablespoons currant jelly
- 1 pint boiling water
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1/2 cup cold water

## Directions

1. Melt 1 ounce sugar and a couple of tablespoons of currant jelly over heat in 1 pint boiling water
2. Stir in 2 tablespoons cornstarch dissolved in 1/2 cup cold water
3. As soon as it boils up, it is ready

title: Jelsy Cake (Jelly Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups sugar
- half a cup butter
- half a cup sweet milk
- 2.5 cups flour
- 3 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Stir 1.5 cups sugar with half a cup butter, half a cup sweet milk
2. Whip 3 eggs separately and stir the whites in last
3. Sift 2.5 cups flour and 2 teaspoons baking powder into the mixture
4. Bake in pie or jelly pans in the usual way
5. For the filling: cook together 1 cup sugar, 1 egg, grated lemon zest, 1 tablespoon water, and 1 teaspoon flour, then cool and place in thick layers between the cakes as usual

title: Jordber-Is (Strawberry Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart ripe berries
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1 pound sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. Dust 1 quart of ripe berries with 1/2 pound of sugar.
2. Let them stand for 3 hours, then crush them and press through a thin cloth.
3. Add 1 pound of sugar, and when it is dissolved, beat in the cream little by little.
4. Then freeze as quickly as possible.

title: Jordber-Suppe (Strawberry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart Strawberries
- 3 Pint Water
- Sugar
- Bread (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as Raspberry Soup, but many prefer to leave the berries whole, in which case no bread is used.

title: Jordberlikør (Strawberry Liqueur)
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 pounds strawberries
- 23 quarts sugar solution (approximately 0.5 pound sugar to scarcely 3 quarts water)
- 13 pints fine spirits

## Directions

1. Let the strawberries stand in the sugar solution for 2 days, press through a sieve, mix with the spirits and filter.

title: Jordbær med Vin (Strawberries in Wine)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g powdered sugar
- 1/2 kg strawberries
- Sherry or Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Choose large fleshy strawberries, preferably light-colored pineapple strawberries, pick them in dry weather and remove stems.
2. Place them immediately into completely dry glasses with wide necks.
3. Sprinkle sugar between the berries.
4. Tap the glasses on the table so the fruit settles tightly together.
5. Pour as much Sherry or Madeira into the filled glasses as there is room for.
6. Tie up immediately.

title: Jordbær og Hindbær Kompot (Strawberry and Raspberry Preserve)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries and raspberries
- 125 grams powdered sugar

## Directions

1. The strawberries and raspberries can either be laid in layers in the jars with 125 grams powdered sugar, or the sugar can be melted in a small amount of water and poured boiling over the fruit well shaken together in the jars.
2. If the sugar is dissolved in a small amount of boiling water, the same amount of fruit will fit in the jars.
3. Cook for 15 minutes.

title: Jordbær, Hindber og Frugter (Strawberries, Raspberries, and Fruits)
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries, raspberries, or apples, pears, and all kinds of fruit
- Water
- Sugar
- Corn-starch

## Directions

1. Boil them in very little water with sugar and a small amount of cornstarch thickening.
2. Peel apples and pears and cut them into pieces; remove the cores.
3. For cherries, add a few whole cloves and use no thickening.

title: Jordbær- eller Hindbær-Fromage (Strawberry or Raspberry Fromage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilo (1/2 S) vanilla chocolate
- 12 sheets isinglass
- 5 teaspoons cacao
- 175 gr (35 quintal) powdered sugar
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) water
- 3/4 L (3 pægle) cream
- 1/4 L (1 pægl) cream for decoration

## Directions

1. Melt the vanilla chocolate, isinglass, cacao and powdered sugar in water
2. When almost cold but not yet set, stir in whipped cream
3. Decorate at serving with whipped cream
4. Prepare the same day it is to be used

title: Jordbær- eller Hindbær-Fromage (Strawberry or Raspberry Fromage) for 12 Persons
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 L (1 pot) whipped cream
- strawberry or raspberry juice to taste, or candied berries
- 10 sheets isinglass
- 1 1/4 dl (1/2 pægl) water
- small macaroons
- whipped cream for decoration

## Directions

1. Stir strawberry or raspberry juice or candied berries into the whipped cream to taste
2. Dissolve isinglass in water and stir into the cream
3. Serve with small macaroons and decorate with whipped cream

title: Jordbær-Gelé (Strawberry Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg strawberries
- 625 gr sugar
- 1 1/4 dl water

## Directions

1. Pick over the strawberries and treat them just like raspberries No. 98.
2. Used like the preceding varieties for Russian marmalade.

title: Jordbær-Gelé (Strawberry Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 S) fresh strawberries
- 1/2 kg (1 S) powdered sugar
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) water
- 10 sheets isinglass per liter (pot) of juice
- juice of 1 lemon (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the strawberries, powdered sugar, and water together until the berries are cooked.
2. Pass them through a jelly bag or a dense cloth.
3. For each liter (pot) of juice, take 10 sheets of isinglass, dissolved in a little of the warm juice without allowing it to boil.
4. Stir it into the remaining juice, which is poured into a moistened and sugar-strewn ring mold.
5. Place on a cold spot or on ice until the next day.
6. At serving, turn the ring out onto a plate, and place a dollop of whipped cream in the center.
7. The jelly ring can be decorated around the outside with fresh, beautiful strawberries that have been standing for an hour, strewn with sugar.

title: Jordbær-Is (Strawberry Ice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) powdered sugar
- 3 3/4 dl (1 1/2 cup) water
- 1/4 L (1/4 pot) strawberry juice or 1 kg (2 lb) fresh strawberries
- Juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Cook the powdered sugar to a syrup with the water and skim.
2. When it is cold, stir in the strawberry juice or fresh strawberries grated through a sieve, along with the juice of 2 lemons.
3. Pour the mixture through a fine sieve directly into the freezing container.

title: Jordbær-Kage (Strawberry Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough, rolled thin according to the size of the pan
- Fresh strawberries
- Powdered sugar (Melis)
- Whipped cream

## Directions

1. Roll out a layer of butter dough thinly and bake it in a very hot oven
2. Once the cake is cold, place a layer of fresh strawberries on top, which have been well dusted with powdered sugar
3. Cover the cake with whipped cream

title: Jordbær-Koldskaal (Strawberry Cold Fruit Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 to 2 Kg (3 to 4 lb) Strawberries
- Powdered Sugar
- nearly 1 L (1 Pot) Water per 1/2 Kg (Pound) of Berries
- a little white Wine or Sherry

## Directions

1. Select some of the most beautiful berries and sprinkle with powdered sugar, which must stand with them for a couple of hours
2. Pass the rest through a fine sieve with nearly 1 L (1 Pot) water for each 1/2 Kg (Pound) of berries
3. Sweeten the cold soup and add a little white wine or sherry to taste
4. It is served with small Macaroons or Rusks and must be very cold
5. Either the tureen can be placed in an ice chest, or a small piece of ice is placed in it
6. Just before serving, the whole berries are placed in the tureen
7. Raspberries can be treated in a similar manner

title: Jordbær-Kompot (Strawberry Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1.25 dl water for 0.5 kg fruit

## Directions

1. Choose preferably the very large, firm and fleshy berries.
2. Treat them as raspberries. Pineapple strawberries are particularly beautiful for preserving.

title: Jordbær-Kompot (Strawberry Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams (1/2 pund) powdered sugar
- 375 milliliters (1/2 pægel) water
- 500 grams (1 pund) strawberries
- Sago flour

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar and water to a syrup and add the strawberries.
2. Remove them from the syrup before they cook.
3. Thicken the syrup with sago flour and pour it over the berries.

title: Jordbær-Likør (Strawberry Liqueur)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 S) wild or garden strawberries
- 1 bottle white rum
- 1/2 kg (1 S) powdered sugar
- 1/4 l (1 pægl) water

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as Raspberry Liqueur.

title: Jordbær-Marmelade (Strawberry Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Very ripe strawberries
- Powdered sugar (1/4 amount of berries)

## Directions

1. Hull the very ripe strawberries and cook them with the sugar until it is an even, fully cooked pulp.
2. Fill into jars and tie up as with the other jams.

title: Jordbær-Suppe (Strawberry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg strawberries
- 4.5 L water
- potato flour
- sugar
- wine

## Directions

1. Cook strawberries in water
2. Strain and bring to boil again
3. Sweeten and thicken with 2 tablespoons potato flour mixed with water
4. Season with wine to taste

title: Jordbærkage (Strawberry Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 egg whites
- 1 cup preserved fresh strawberries
- 14 dessert spoonfuls powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Whip 14 egg whites
2. Stir together 1 cup preserved fresh strawberries and 14 dessert spoonfuls powdered sugar and place in a cake pan
3. Gently fold in the egg whites
4. Immediately place the pan in a warm oven or tart pan
5. Bake for half an hour and serve immediately, as it will fall otherwise
6. Arrange with whipped cream over top
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Jordbærlikør (Strawberry Liqueur)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 pounds strawberries
- 2 bottles good rum or cognac
- 1½ pounds white sugar
- ½ pound white sugar or crushed rock candy dissolved in 15 pails rum and filtered

## Directions

1. Let strawberries, rum or cognac, and 1½ pounds sugar stand to steep for 5 to 6 days
2. Strain and add ½ pound white sugar or crushed rock candy, which has been dissolved in 15 pails rum and filtered
3. Pour the liqueur into bottles
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Jordbærsaft (Strawberry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pots of strawberries
- 3 pounds of currant juice
- 4 pots of water
- 0.5 pound of powdered sugar per pound of juice

## Directions

1. Add the currant juice and water to the strawberries and cook for half an hour, then press through a sieve
2. Cook the berries that remain with 2.5 measures of water for half an hour and press through the sieve again
3. Filter the juice, weigh it and cook for half an hour with 0.5 pound of powdered sugar for each pound of juice
4. This juice becomes very stiff
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Jordbærsaft (Strawberry Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries
- 575 gr (1/4 lb) sugar per 1 liter (1 pot) juice

## Directions

1. See Raspberry Syrup No. 181.

title: Jordskokke-Gratin (Jerusalem Artichoke Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Jordskokker
- Smør
- Mel
- Kogende Mælk
- Æg
- Salt
- Melis

## Directions

1. De parterede, ikke helt mørkоgte, Jordskokker lægges i Gratin-Dejg, som til Asparges-Gratin Nr. 62.
2. Rørt Smør.

title: Jordskokke-Suppe (Jerusalem Artichoke Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg (3 S) Jerusalem artichokes
- 4 L (4 pots) broth
- 60 grams (12 skein) flour
- 75 grams (15 skein) butter
- 4 to 5 egg yolks
- salt
- ground white pepper
- Sherry
- baked French bread cubes
- cooked, cut Jerusalem artichokes

## Directions

1. Peel the Jerusalem artichokes, rinse them, cook them in water or broth, and rub them through a sieve.
2. Bake 60 grams (12 skein) flour in 75 grams (15 skein) butter, thin this with the Jerusalem artichoke purée, which is brought to a boil.
3. Bind it with 4 to 5 egg yolks, season with salt, ground white pepper and Sherry.
4. Serve with baked French bread cubes. Cooked, cut Jerusalem artichokes can be served in the soup.

title: Jordskokker (Jerusalem Artichokes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- water
- vinegar
- skimmed milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel the Jerusalem artichokes and place them gradually in cold water with a little vinegar
2. Cook until tender in skimmed milk
3. Serve on a napkin on a plate
4. Serve with melted butter

title: Jordskokker med hakkede Trøfler (Jerusalem Artichokes with Chopped Truffles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- chopped truffles
- butter
- chopped truffles or mushrooms

## Directions

1. Prepare Jerusalem artichokes as in recipe Nr. 613
2. Cook until tender
3. Arrange on a dish
4. Sprinkle with chopped truffles
5. Serve with butter mixed with chopped truffles or mushrooms

title: Jordskokker med Vin (Jerusalem Artichokes with Wine)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- small Jerusalem artichokes
- butter
- flour
- water from cooking the artichokes
- egg yolks
- salt
- sugar
- Maggi seasoning
- wine

## Directions

1. Peel small Jerusalem artichokes
2. Cook them
3. Make a white, smooth sauce of butter and flour
4. Dilute the sauce with the water in which the artichokes were cooked
5. Thicken this sauce with 1 to 2 egg yolks
6. Add salt, sugar, Maggi seasoning and wine to taste

title: Jordskokker og Ærter (Jerusalem Artichokes and Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked Jerusalem artichokes
- cream or hollandaise sauce
- cooked green peas

## Directions

1. Stew the cooked Jerusalem artichokes in a cream or hollandaise sauce
2. Arrange on a dish
3. Garnish with a wreath of cooked green peas

title: Jordskokker, indbagte (Fried Jerusalem Artichokes in Batter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams Pillsbury flour preferably
- 3 eggs
- 50 grams melted butter or margarine
- 250 milliliters milk
- salt
- sugar
- peeled and cooked Jerusalem artichokes
- Palmin for frying
- parsley
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Mix flour with 3 eggs, melted butter or margarine, milk, and a small amount of salt and sugar
2. Dip the peeled, cooked Jerusalem artichokes in this batter
3. Take them one by one on a fork and place them directly from the batter into hot Palmin
4. Cook until light brown
5. Place these battered Jerusalem artichokes briefly on paper before arranging on a hot plate with a napkin
6. Parsley, cooked in the Palmin, can be sprinkled over the dish
7. Serve with melted butter

title: Jule-Kage (Christmas Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 whole eggs
- 200 Gr (40 Kvint) powdered sugar
- 2 small teaspoons cardamom
- 1 small teaspoon cinnamon
- 100 Gr (20 Kvint) yeast
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) lukewarm, sweet milk
- 1 Kg (2 S) good flour
- a little salt
- 400 Gr (80 Kvint) cold butter or margarine
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) scalded and pitted raisins
- 150 Gr (30 Kvint) finely chopped candied citrus peel
- melted butter for brushing

## Directions

1. Stir eggs with powdered sugar, cardamom and cinnamon
2. Dissolve yeast in lukewarm sweet milk and add to the eggs together with flour and salt
3. Knead the dough together, place on a floured baking board and roll out with a rolling pin on a sheet
4. Distribute 400 Gr (80 Kvint) cold butter or margarine in small lumps on the dough
5. Fold the dough together, roll out and fold together 3 times
6. The last time, cover the rolled-out sheet with 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) scalded and pitted raisins and 150 Gr (30 Kvint) finely chopped candied citrus peel
7. Roll it up as a very long sausage, roll this as a snail, place on a cold, floured sheet and let rise
8. When it has risen and become half again larger, brush over with melted butter and bake at good heat for 1 hour
9. Can also be baked in a form

title: Julekage (Christmas Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 20 grams white sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 pound fine wheat flour
- 1/2 pound potato flour
- 1/4 pound washed raisins
- 15 grams finely cut citron peel
- a little cardamom
- 1 tablespoon yeast

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and stir with the white sugar and 3 whole eggs.
2. Add 1 cup sweet milk, 1 pound fine wheat flour, 1/2 pound potato flour, 1/4 pound washed raisins, 15 grams finely cut citron peel, a little cardamom, and 1 tablespoon yeast.
3. Stir well and knead with the hands until the dough releases and begins to rise.
4. Let it rise until well leavened, then shape as a Christmas cake.
5. Long strips of citron peel can be placed on top.
6. Brush with egg white and bake for 1 hour at good heat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Julekage (Christmas Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ounces butter (3 tablespoons)
- salt
- 2 tablespoons white sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 1 pint fresh milk
- 13 pounds flour
- 2 cents yeast dissolved in a little milk
- half a pound raisins
- cardamom
- sugar

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and hold back most of the salt.
2. Stir it with two tablespoons white sugar and 3 whole eggs.
3. Add 1 pint fresh milk.
4. Add 13 pounds flour, 2 cents yeast dissolved in a little milk, half a pound raisins, cardamom and sugar.
5. When it becomes too hard to stir, knead it with the hands until the dough begins to rise and releases from the board.
6. Set it aside until it has risen, then place it in a mold or shape it into a Christmas cake without kneading further.
7. Brush with sugar or spread with butter or egg white and bake in a good hour at good heat.

title: Julienne Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Salt
- Water
- Fat

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes, cut into thin slices and place in well-salted cold water for at least one hour.
2. Wash them well in several changes of water and dry in a clean cloth.
3. Fry golden brown in hot fat, then place on gray paper to drain of fat and serve hot.

title: Julienne- eller Urte-Suppe (Julienne or Vegetable Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L (4 pots) clear soup or boiling water with Maggis Bouillon cubes
- 4 carrots
- 2 turnips
- 3 leeks
- 1 celery
- 1 head of lettuce
- 50 g (10 drams) butter
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon chopped chervil
- Toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cut the carrots, turnips, leeks, celery, and lettuce into approximately 2 centimeter (1 inch) long strips, as fine as knitting needles
2. Melt 50 g (10 drams) butter in a pot and sauté the vegetables with 1 teaspoon sugar
3. Pour the soup over the vegetables and cook them completely tender
4. Skim all the fat from the soup carefully
5. A tablespoon of chopped chervil can be added and brought to a boil
6. Serve with toasted bread

title: Julienne-Royal
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L (4 pots) clear soup or boiling water with Maggis Bouillon cubes
- 4 carrots
- 2 turnips
- 3 leeks
- 1 celery
- 1 head of lettuce
- 50 g (10 drams) butter
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- Egg-royal (recipe No. 507)

## Directions

1. Prepare the soup as for the Julienne or Vegetable Soup
2. Serve with Egg-royal (recipe No. 507)

title: Julisuppe à la Julienne (Julienne Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young carrots
- Celery
- Leeks
- Parsley roots
- Corn salad
- 5 pots good clear bouillon
- Toasted French bread slices

## Directions

1. Clean the carrots, celery, leeks, parsley roots, and corn salad
2. Cut into thin strips the thickness of a straw and a couple of inches long
3. Cook covered over gentle heat with 5 pots good clear bouillon
4. Serve with toasted French bread slices
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Jærper med Champignons i Vol au vent (Capercaillie with Mushrooms in Vol-au-vent)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Capercaillie
- Game stock, soup, or water with meat extract
- Flour
- Butter
- Sauce
- Fresh or preserved mushrooms

## Directions

1. Baste the capercaillie with game stock, soup, or water with meat extract
2. When tender, strain the gravy and use it with the baked flour and butter to make the sauce
3. Add fresh or preserved mushrooms to the sauce

title: Jærper med Champignons i Vol au vent (Grouse with Mushrooms in Vol-au-vent)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Grouse
- Gravy, soup or water with meat extract
- Baked flour and butter
- Fresh or preserved mushrooms
- 1 glass Sherry
- Vol-au-vent (see No. 33)

## Directions

1. Drip the grouse with gravy, soup or water with meat extract.
2. When tender, strain the gravy and combine with the baked flour and butter to make the sauce.
3. Add some fresh or preserved mushrooms to the sauce and bring to a boil.
4. Finally add 1 glass Sherry.
5. Split the breast of the grouse and place in a large vol-au-vent.
6. Pour some of the sauce over; the remainder can be served in a sauce boat.

title: Jødekager (Jewish Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 625 grams (1 1/4 lb) Pillsbury flour
- 375 grams (3/4 lb) margarine
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) icing sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking soda, dissolved in a small amount of water

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Roll out thinly and cut out with a glass
3. Place fairly close together on the sheet
4. Brush with egg
5. Sprinkle with a mixture of sugar and cinnamon and with chopped or blanched, scalded almonds
6. Bake light in a not too hot oven
7. Very crisp Jewish cakes can be made with my baking flour

title: Jødekager med Chokolade-Glasur (Jewish Cakes with Chocolate Glaze)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 625 grams (1 1/4 lb) Pillsbury flour
- 375 grams (3/4 lb) margarine
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) icing sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking soda, dissolved in a small amount of water
- chocolate glaze

## Directions

1. Bake the cakes from the dough described above
2. When slightly cooled, spread with chocolate glaze
3. Then dry in the oven
4. Bake at not too strong heat

title: Kaal-Dolma (Cabbage Dolma)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg prepared forcemeat (serves 12 people)
- 1 white cabbage head
- twine
- butter
- broth or water
- salt

## Directions

1. Separate the cabbage leaves and place them briefly in boiling water to soften and make them pliable
2. Remove the coarsest part of the stems from the leaves
3. Place a spoonful of forcemeat in each leaf
4. Roll the cabbage carefully around the forcemeat to form long rolls and bind with twine
5. Place the dolmas side by side in a pot with browned butter
6. When browned, pour a little broth or salted water over them
7. Cook under a closed lid for at least 2 hours
8. When tender, strain the gravy and thicken it into sauce with a nice brown color
9. Pour the sauce over the dolmas when serving
10. Alternative: use a Bavarian sausage instead of forcemeat, or place a cooked leek on a piece of white cabbage leaf, wrap the leaf around it, and place another leaf on top, then bind, brown, and cook as for cabbage dolma with forcemeat

title: Kaalrabi (Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- Kohlrabi
- Bouillon
- Fresh butter
- A little sugar
- Salt
- Flour
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Remove all hard parts, cut the kohlrabi into half-moon shaped pieces and clean them
2. Place over heat with bouillon, fresh butter, a little sugar, and salt, and cook gently
3. Thicken the stock with flour and chopped parsley and bring to a boil
4. Serve with veal or lamb cutlets

title: Kaalrabi Suppe (Kohlrabi Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Kaalrabi (kohlrabi)
- Bouillon
- Fresh butter
- Sugar
- Salt
- Flour
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Remove all hard parts
2. Cut kohlrabi into crescent-shaped pieces and clean them
3. Place over heat with bouillon, fresh butter, a little sugar and salt
4. Cook until tender
5. Thicken soup with flour and chopped parsley
6. Add a dash of seasoning
7. Serve with veal or lamb cutlets

title: Kaffe-Budding (Coffee Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 L (3 Pægle) very strong coffee
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) vanilla chocolate
- 16 leaves isinglass
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) cream, whipped to stiff foam
- 1/4 L (3 Pægle) half-skimmed or sweet milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 150 Gr (30 Kvint) icing sugar
- 2 teaspoons wheat flour or powdered starch
- vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Dissolve the vanilla chocolate and 16 leaves of isinglass in the very strong coffee
2. Allow to cool and begin to set
3. Stir in 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) cream, whipped to stiff foam
4. Pour into a wetted and coated mold
5. The pudding can be prepared the day before
6. For the cream sauce: whip 5 egg yolks with 150 Gr (30 Kvint) icing sugar and 2 teaspoons wheat flour or powdered starch
7. Heat 1/4 L (3 Pægle) half-skimmed or sweet milk and whisk in the egg mixture
8. Keep on low heat and stir regularly until almost cold
9. Add vanilla essence
10. Serve the pudding with this cream sauce

title: Kaffe-Creme (Coffee Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- strong extract of good coffee
- 1 quart fresh cream
- 5 ounces sugar
- 10 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Brew or strain a strong extract of good coffee.
2. Whisk 1 quart fresh cream with 5 ounces sugar.
3. Beat 10 egg yolks first a little alone, then with the cream.
4. Place in an enameled pot along with 1/2 pint of coffee extract (or adjust to taste).
5. Stir constantly until it reaches a boil and likewise when poured into a dish, until it becomes cold.
6. Serve in small cups with cake.

title: Kaffe-Créme (Coffee Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- vanilla cream as Nr. 1060
- 3 1/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) very strong coffee extract
- 14 sheets of isinglass, dissolved in the extract
- whipped cream for decoration

## Directions

1. Stir the very strong coffee extract into the vanilla cream as Nr. 1060, before the whipped cream is added.
2. Dissolve the isinglass in the extract.
3. Decorate the cream with whipped cream foam.

title: Kaffe-Fromage (Coffee Fromage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 L (1 pægl) very strong coffee
- 12 sheets isinglass
- 125 gr (1/8 S) powdered sugar
- 3/4 L (3 pægle) cream
- whipped cream for decoration

## Directions

1. Melt isinglass with powdered sugar in very strong coffee
2. When cooled, stir in whipped cream
3. Decorate with whipped cream

title: Kaffebrød (Coffee Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds wheat flour
- 0.75 pound butter
- 3 tablespoons cream
- 3 tablespoons rum
- 3 tablespoons white crushed sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Stir together 1.5 pounds wheat flour, 0.75 pound butter, 3 tablespoons cream, 3 tablespoons rum, 3 tablespoons white crushed sugar, 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks into a firm dough.
2. Roll out the dough, sprinkle with sugar and bake at even heat.
3. When the cake is baked, remove it from the oven and cut it, while warm, into oblong pieces.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kager paa Traad (Cakes on String)
body:
## Ingredients

- Raisins (parboiled)
- blanched Almonds
- Apples (in small pieces)
- Batter for liquid Æbleskiver

## Directions

1. On good thread thread alternately Raisins (which are first parboiled slightly), blanched Almonds, and small pieces of Apple
2. When about 1 Yard is threaded full and tight, but so that there is a piece of thread outside at each end for holding, dip it in Batter prepared according to the recipe for liquid Æbleskiver
3. Lay in boiling clarified fat to fry until light brown
4. Pull the thread out before the cake is cold

title: Kakao-Suppe med Maizena (Cocoa Soup with Cornstarch)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 liters semi-skimmed milk
- 125 grams cocoa
- 3 level teaspoons cornstarch
- vanilla essence
- sugar
- rusks

## Directions

1. Bring the milk to a boil
2. Mix the cocoa in some of the hot milk
3. Mix 3 level teaspoons cornstarch in a little cold milk and blend into the cocoa
4. Stir the cocoa mixture into the milk
5. Bring to a boil
6. Add a little vanilla essence
7. Sweeten to taste
8. Serve with rusks

title: Kakao-Suppe med Maizena (Cocoa Soup with Maizena)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 L (3 Pots) semi-skimmed Milk
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) Cocoa
- 3 heaping Teaspoonfuls Maizena
- a little cold Milk
- Vanilla Essence
- Sugar
- 2 1/10 Dl (1 Pægl) Cream

## Directions

1. Bring the milk to a boil
2. In some of this hot milk, stir the cocoa
3. In the cocoa, blend 3 heaping teaspoonfuls of Maizena stirred in a little cold milk; stir this into the milk, which then receives another boil
4. Add a little Vanilla Essence
5. Sweeten the soup and serve with Rusks
6. 2 1/10 Dl (1 Pægl) Cream can be whipped into foam and served on top or placed in the Tureen

title: Kalkun i Gelee (Turkey in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Water
- Salt
- Gelatine
- Beets

## Directions

1. Cook the turkey whole until nearly done
2. Cut off the legs and wings, removing the outermost joints which are not used in the aspic
3. Cleave the breast lengthwise and cut each half into 2 or 3 pieces according to size
4. Do not include the back, but cook it and the bones longer in the stock
5. The good pieces should only cook until properly done
6. The aspic should be white and is made stiff with gelatine, using 1 ounce to each quart of soup when the meat is cooked in barely sufficient water
7. Test by straining a couple of spoonfuls
8. Decorate the aspic with beets

title: Kalkun med Trøffel (Turkey with Truffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- young turkey
- veal meat from the leg
- kidney fat
- beef marrow
- truffles
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- cognac

## Directions

1. Clean a young, fat turkey as usual and remove the innards.
2. Pat dry but do not wash.
3. Finely chop 4 pounds of veal meat from the leg with 3 pounds of kidney fat, 4 pounds of beef marrow, and a small glass of truffles, setting aside some of the largest truffle pieces.
4. Work the stuffing together with 3 eggs, salt, pepper, and a glass of good cognac.
5. While the turkey is still warm, fill the neck skin with the stuffing and sew it closed.
6. Fill the inside cavity with the remaining truffles and any leftover stuffing, being careful not to overfill as the stuffing will expand.
7. Let the turkey rest for a couple of days to absorb the truffle flavor.
8. Roast over gentle heat and arrange whole with truffle sauce.
9. Mushrooms may be substituted for truffles if desired.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalkun å la diable (Deviled Turkey)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover turkey or turkey meat
- pan drippings from roasting
- broth cooked from the bones
- sauce à la diable (recipe 760)
- Maggis bouillon cubes
- coloring

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from the bones.
2. Warm it in a sauce à la diable made from the pan drippings from the roast together with the broth cooked on the bones from which the meat was first removed.
3. The sauce can be improved with Maggis bouillon cubes and colored with food coloring.

title: Kalkun-Suppe (Turkey Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey, half a year old
- Veal shank

## Directions

1. The turkey should be treated and served as chicken
2. Cook together with a veal shank as described in recipe No. 934 for boiled turkey

title: Kalkunsteg paa engelsk Maade (Roasted Turkey, English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- medisterpølse sausage
- bread crumbs
- egg

## Directions

1. If using a male turkey, after cleaning, make a small cut in the leg joint without cutting through it.
2. Push the leg bone through the crack on the back of a door, and while one person presses the door together on the bone, another person pulls off the lower leg so that the hard sinews in the thigh come out with it.
3. Fill the turkey with medisterpølse sausage stuffing mixed with bread crumbs and a beaten egg.
4. Roast as usual.
5. Serve with small medisterpølse sausages.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalkunſteg (Roasted Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- One turkey
- Cold water
- Hot water
- Several slices of wheat bread
- Milk
- Cold butter
- Salt
- Ground ginger
- A couple of cloves
- Three eggs

## Directions

1. Wash the turkey in cold water, then pour boiling water over it, let it lie for a couple of minutes and place it again in cold water.
2. If desired, you can stuff turkeys.
3. To fill the space where the crop has been, prepare a farce of several slices of wheat bread soaked in milk and then covered and left to stand for one hour.
4. Work a piece of cold butter until creamy, then stir in the bread after it has been crushed with a spoon, salt, a little ground ginger, a couple of cloves and three eggs.
5. When well mixed, you can add the crop, liver and heart, which are finely chopped, fill it in the skin and sew it, but the farce must not press down into the turkey, so a piece of wheat bread must be placed in the internal hole below the neck.
6. The oven must be well heated before the turkey is set in, and it must have an even heat, but not too strong, otherwise it will become too dark brown.
7. It cannot be roasted in a pot when it is stuffed.
8. Set it in with water and a little salt, with which it is gradually basted; later butter is placed on it, with which it is then basted.
9. A turkey requires two to three hours of roasting according to its size.

title: Kalkunſteg à l'Anglaise (Turkey Roasted in the English Manner)
body:
## Ingredients

- One turkey, slaughtered a couple of days before use
- Finely grated breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Two eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Sage
- Marjoram
- Water
- Flour
- Egg white
- Gooseberry sauce

## Directions

1. Slaughter a couple of days before use and hang it up by the legs.
2. Prepare in the usual manner.
3. Fill with finely grated breadcrumbs that are soaked with butter and two eggs and seasoned with salt, pepper, parsley, sage and marjoram.
4. Sew it up and roast in a roasting pan, preferably on a rack in the same; drip constantly with butter, pepper, salt and water and turn it.
5. A couple of minutes before it is done, glaze it with the white of an egg.
6. Take it out.
7. Most of the fat is poured off, and into it are put the chopped giblets with the water in which they were cooked, which is thickened with flour rubbed with butter; everything is stirred in the roasting pan and is given a good boil before it is served.
8. Gooseberry sauce is served with it.

title: Kallun-Suppe (Lamb Tripe Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 times lamb tripe
- 1/2 bushel shelled peas
- 6 L (6 pots) water
- salt
- 100 g (20 skein) butter or vegetable margarine
- 100 g (20 skein) flour
- 20 new carrots, cut in pieces

## Directions

1. Kallun soup is a summer dish which requires fresh vegetables to achieve the right flavor.
2. Cleaning tripe is work that must be done with great care and requires considerable time.
3. Split the bones before putting them in salted water.
4. Scrape the tripe at least 3-4 times, pouring boiling water over it in between.
5. Scrape and clean the fat intestines on the outside, then split and clean inside.
6. Cut these together with the tripe into small, square pieces.
7. Place the bones and cut tripe and fat intestines in a pot in 6 L (6 pots) salted water.
8. When the soup boils, skim well before adding vegetables, then simmer gently for about 3 hours under a closed lid or until the tripe is nearly tender; then add the shelled peas and about 20 cut new carrots and cook until tender.
9. In a pot, bake 100 g (20 skein) butter or vegetable margarine and 100 g (20 skein) flour and thin with some of the soup until it becomes a thick liaison, which is poured into the tripe soup.
10. Season with salt and ground pepper.

title: Kallunsuppe (Lamb Offal Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Kallun, Fedetarme og Fødder af 5 Lam (tripe, mesentery, and feet from 5 lambs)
- 5 til 6 Potter Vand (water)
- Salt
- En Suppevisk (a bunch of herbs)
- Smaa, fine Supperødder (small soup vegetables)
- Gulerødder (carrots)
- Grønærter (peas)
- Lidt afvadskede Rosiner (raisins, rinsed)
- Et godt Fjerdingspund Ris (a good quarter pound of rice)
- Finthakket Kruspersille (finely chopped parsley)

## Directions

1. Clean the tripe, mesentery, and feet from 5 lambs with great care.
2. Scrape the tripe several times, placing it in cold salted water between each scraping.
3. Clean the mesentery first on the outside, then cut it open and clean the inside.
4. When the feet are cleaned, split the bones.
5. Cut the tripe into small cubes and place with the feet in 5 to 6 pots of water.
6. Bring to a boil and skim; add the mesentery (also cut into small cubes), salt, and a bunch of herbs.
7. Cook until the tripe is tender.
8. Add small soup vegetables, especially carrots, shelled peas, and rinsed raisins, along with rice prepared as described in recipe No. 6.
9. When the vegetables are tender, serve as hot as possible with finely chopped parsley.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalve i Karri (Veal Curry)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast or other meat-rich cuts
- Butter
- Onion
- Wheat flour
- Curry powder
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Chop the meat into small pieces and wash it in several changes of water.
2. Melt a large spoonful of butter in a pot over heat; when melted but not browned, add a small grated onion and cook for a couple of minutes.
3. Stir in two large spoonfuls of wheat flour and curry powder to taste, along with salt (use sparingly, about half a teaspoon, as more can be added later).
4. Cook this together and gradually add boiling water until it becomes a thick sauce.
5. Add the meat and gradually add more boiling water as needed, keeping the sauce fairly thick.
6. Cook covered as much as possible, but skim thoroughly.
7. The dish is ready when the meat is tender, which takes about an hour for a couple of pounds of meat.
8. Serve immediately with rice; if the sauce has become lumpy, remove the meat first and pour the sauce over it.

title: Kalve- eller Lammesteg med Røræg (Veal or Lamb Roast with Scrambled Eggs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal or lamb roast
- Stock from the roast
- Butter
- Flour
- Scrambled eggs
- Chives, chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the roast into cubes or slices
2. Warm these in a brown stock sauce made from the stock of the roast
3. Arrange the warmed roast on a hot, flat dish
4. Garnish with a ring of scrambled eggs
5. Sprinkle with chopped chives

title: Kalve-Filet (Veal Fillet)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder
- fat from the shoulder
- salt
- pepper
- twine
- butter
- carrots
- celery root
- onions
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Cut 2 or 3 fillet pieces from a veal shoulder, place each on a board
2. Place some of the fat from the shoulder on each fillet, sprinkle with salt and pepper, roll together and tie with twine
3. Place the fillets in a casserole with butter that has been lightly browned, together with 2 carrots, a celery root and a couple of onions
4. Chop the shoulder bones, brown them in butter and salt, and thin with bouillon
5. Use this stock to baste the fillets during cooking
6. When done and a light golden color, remove the twine and slice the fillet, but arrange it in its original form for serving
7. Thin the stock in the casserole with bouillon and thicken the sauce with flour, strain and serve with the fillet alongside browned potatoes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalve-Fricandeau (Veal Fricandeau)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg inner loin of veal
- salt
- pepper
- brown butter
- stock or cream

## Directions

1. Cut the inner loin into smaller pieces and beat lightly with a meat mallet and lard it. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, place with the larded side down against the pan, and fry in brown butter.
2. Within about 10 minutes they are as a rule fried. The gravy that has stuck to the pan is boiled off with stock or cream until it becomes smooth.
3. The sauce can also be made from butter and flour, which is thinned with the gravy. Serve with currant jelly, compote, salad, or vegetables.
4. Alternatively: prepare beforehand a sauce of butter and flour, browned well, thin this with stock, gravy, or boiling water and Maggi bouillon cubes, add coloring, and let the meat, previously browned, simmer in it. Before serving, some mushrooms are given a boil in the sauce; this is prepared with salt, sugar (very slightly) and wine.

title: Kalve-Frikassee (Veal Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal (same cuts as used for curry)
- Butter
- Flour
- Water or stock
- Leek and celery leaves
- Whole celery and parsley roots
- Nutmeg
- Lemon juice
- Egg yolk
- White wine (optional)
- Green peas (optional)
- Oysters (optional)
- Asparagus (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces in the same manner as for curry.
2. Melt a piece of butter over heat and stir in enough flour for thickening, then gradually add water or stock until it becomes a thick sauce.
3. Add the meat with salt and add water until it just covers the meat.
4. Add a bundle of leek and celery leaves, but remove it before it falls apart.
5. Add whole celery and parsley roots and simmer until the meat is tender, then remove the meat and roots, cutting the roots into pieces.
6. Strain the sauce and return it to the pot with a little nutmeg and lemon juice.
7. When simmering, bind the sauce with an egg yolk mixed with white wine if desired.
8. Pour the sauce over the meat, which should be kept warm on a platter.
9. If using green peas, omit the egg and nutmeg, adding only a little chopped parsley just before serving.
10. For oysters: remove the beards and cook them in a little stock, then strain and add to the fricassee; add the oysters to the sauce after binding with eggs, without cooking them further.
11. For asparagus: omit the roots, trim the asparagus, cut into short pieces, and cook more in the sauce.

title: Kalve-Frikassé (Veal Fricassée)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast or brisket
- Salt
- Water
- Bouquet garni
- Peppercorns
- Onions
- Root vegetables for soup
- Flour
- Butter
- Asparagus (optional)

## Directions

1. Use breast or brisket, chop into smaller pieces, rinse well and set to cook with cold water and a little salt and a bouquet garni.
2. When it boils, skim the soup several times; add some peppercorns and a couple of onions.
3. When the meat is cooked, remove it and strain the soup and reduce it with root vegetables and thicken with a tablespoon of flour mixed with butter until the thickness is appropriate.
4. Place the meat in the soup and warm it.
5. If you have asparagus, you can add cooked pieces about two inches long to the fricassée along with a little of the water in which they were cooked.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalve-Kallops med Persille (Veal Kallops with Parsley)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- good veal
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- stock or gravy
- cold butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut good veal into small pieces, beat them lightly, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and brown on both sides in approximately 8 to 10 minutes.
2. In the butter in which it is fried, add flour, which is thinned with stock or gravy, and boil well together.
3. Just before serving add a piece of cold butter and a tablespoon of chopped parsley. Pour the sauce over the meat.
4. Serve with potatoes. Kallops can be made from pork tenderloin.

title: Kalve-Karbonade (Veal Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder
- egg yolks and whites
- crushed zwieback
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- stock

## Directions

1. Cut the shoulder as close to the back as possible, cut the ribs into finger-length pieces, and divide the meat with a sharp knife so each piece has a bone
2. Remove skin and sinews
3. Scrape the meat from the thinnest part of the bone to make it clean, but ensure the meat is not removed from the thick part of the bone
4. Beat the meat lightly with a mallet, chop it with the back of a knife and shape it into a rounded form
5. Brush each piece with beaten egg yolks and whites using a feather, then coat both sides with crushed zwieback mixed with pepper and salt
6. Fry the chops in a pan with lightly browned butter over high heat, turning during cooking
7. In the remaining browned butter in the pan, add butter and stock, cooking until it begins to separate, then serve as sauce with the chops alongside boiled, peeled potatoes
8. Alternatively, chops can be made from other veal meat mixed with an equal amount of fat pork or bacon, chopped together, and formed into chops without bone
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalve-Kotelet (Veal Cutlet)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cutlets from shoulder
- egg
- crushed zwieback
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- stock

## Directions

1. Cut the cutlets from the shoulder as described previously, chop with the back of a knife and shape
2. Dip them in melted butter, coat lightly with crushed zwieback, salt and pepper
3. Fry over high heat in a pan with lightly browned butter, turning frequently until they are firm and a light golden brown
4. If the butter in the pan diminishes and begins to steam, add more butter
5. Deglaze the remaining butter with a little stock and serve as sauce with the cutlets alongside boiled, peeled potatoes or stewed mushrooms
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalve-Mørbrad (Veal Tenderloin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal tenderloin
- Lard
- Butter
- Broth
- Madeira
- Sago flour
- Vegetables for garnish

## Directions

1. Remove the inner fillet before the back is larded and roasted.
2. Beat the tenderloin lightly and remove the skin.
3. Lard and brown the meat, then roast for just under half an hour, basting regularly with pan drippings.
4. Make a good Madeira sauce from the pan drippings of the fillet, thickened with a little sago flour and flavored with Madeira.
5. Garnish the tenderloin with vegetables.

title: Kalve-Ragout (Veal Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions
- Butter
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Stock (Sky)
- Pepper
- Salt
- Rock sugar
- Pickled cucumber or gherkin strips
- Cooked veal
- Tarragon vinegar (optional)
- Pickled onions or mushrooms (optional)

## Directions

1. Slice onions, fry them in butter and brown them golden with a couple of tablespoons of flour.
2. Thin with bouillon and stock, adding a little pepper, salt, a piece of rock sugar and some strips of pickled cucumber or gherkin, and cook the whole until it becomes a smooth sauce.
3. Place slices of cooked meat in this and warm until the whole is boiling hot.
4. A little tarragon vinegar, pickled onions or mushrooms improve the flavor.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalve-Snitter (Veal Schnitzels)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- inner loin of veal
- butter
- good gravy or stock
- spinach, sorrel, peas, asparagus, or other vegetables

## Directions

1. Cut from the inner loin into small pieces and give a blow with the meat mallet, trim, and lard.
2. Brown the schnitzels in butter in a pot and pour over them a little good gravy or stock, but no more than is needed to braise them tender.
3. Baste them frequently with the gravy, which gradually must fry away until it becomes a good, smooth, and strong sauce, which is strained and served with the meat.
4. Arrange the schnitzels in a line lengthwise on the dish, which is decorated at each end with a bouquet of salad or parsley.
5. Spinach, sorrel, peas, asparagus, or other vegetables can be served with this.

title: Kalvebrissel i Franskbrød (Calf Sweetbread in French Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small French bread rolls, crust scraped off
- Stewed sweetbreads
- Egg white
- Milk
- Bread crumbs
- Butter or Palmin
- Sauce from the stewing of sweetbreads
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut a lid from small French bread rolls from which the crust has been scraped, and hollow them out.
2. Fill with stewed sweetbreads while they are still hot.
3. Glue the lid on with egg white.
4. Dip the breads in milk and the scraped bread crumbs.
5. Fry in butter or Palmin.
6. Serve with sauce made at the same time as the sauce for the stewing, thinned and bound with a couple of egg yolks.

title: Kalvebrissel, paneret (Breaded Calf Sweetbread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf sweetbreads, cleaned and boiled
- Egg
- Bread crumbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter or lard
- Stewed vegetables

## Directions

1. After the sweetbreads are cleaned and boiled, cut them into slices.
2. Dip the slices in egg and bread crumbs mixed with salt and pepper.
3. Fry in butter or lard and arrange as a garnish around stewed vegetables.

title: Kalvebrissel-Ragout (Calf Sweetbread Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf sweetbreads
- Butter
- Sweetbread cooking liquid
- Coloring
- Paprika
- Sherry
- Tomato paste
- Sago flour
- Small meatballs of veal
- Olives or mushrooms
- Puff pastry

## Directions

1. Half-cook the sweetbreads, split them, and brown in butter.
2. Pour some of the sweetbread cooking liquid over them.
3. Add coloring, paprika, sherry, and tomato paste to taste.
4. Thicken the sauce with sago flour.
5. Arrange small veal meatballs alongside the sweetbreads and pour the sauce over.
6. The sauce can be further improved with either olives or mushrooms.
7. Serve with puff pastry, either baked as a lid, border, or slices.

title: Kalvehjerte (Calf Heart)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- One large, fat calf heart (serves 2-3 people)
- Parsley
- Butter
- Water
- Coloring
- Salt
- Flour
- Maggi bouillon cubes

## Directions

1. Open the heart by cutting it lengthwise and remove the blood vessels and tendons, then wash it thoroughly.
2. Stuff the heart like a chicken with parsley and butter.
3. Sew the heart together, brown it in butter, and add a little water along with coloring and salt.
4. Roast for about 3 hours.
5. Brown butter and flour together, then thin with the cooking liquid to make a sauce.
6. Strengthen the sauce with Maggi bouillon cubes if needed.
7. Cut the heart in half and cover with the sauce.
8. Serve with compote, currant jelly, salad, or browned potatoes.

title: Kalvehoved à la Maître d'Hôtel (Calf's Head in Maître d'Hôtel Style)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's head
- Salt
- Bouillon
- Butter
- Flour
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Mushrooms
- White pepper
- Sugar
- Egg yolks
- Parsley

## Directions

1. After the calf's head has been soaked, cleaned and cooked in very little water with salt and good bouillon, remove it from the bones and cut it into cubes when it is cold.
2. Brown butter and flour together and dilute with the bouillon to make a good, white, smooth sauce, and if necessary add Maggi Bouillon cubes.
3. Add mushrooms, white pepper, salt and a little sugar to the sauce, and lastly add the meat to warm it.
4. Just before serving, thicken the sauce with 3 to 4 egg yolks.
5. Serve in a puff pastry border or with slices and sprinkle with finely chopped parsley.

title: Kalvekjod a l'Anglaise (Veal a l'Anglaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Veal
- Water
- Flour
- Cream

## Directions

1. Put a large tablespoon of butter over the heat in a pot; when it begins to brown, add two pounds veal, which can be simple meat, shoulder or neck, and brown it
2. Then pour water over it so it stands just above the meat; stew as much as necessary
3. When the meat is tender, stir a few tablespoons of flour mixed in good cream and thicken the broth with it
4. It is an inexpensive and well-flavored dish

title: Kalvekjodsuppe (Veal Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal
- Water
- Bones from mutton or preferably beef

## Directions

1. Veal becomes tender much faster than beef, so less water is needed for it. This broth and lamb broth have a weak flavor in themselves, so it is good if you have bones from mutton or preferably beef to cook with them.

title: Kalvekjød i Karri (Veal Curry)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal
- Salt
- Water
- Onion
- Melted butter
- Flour
- Curry
- Rice

## Directions

1. Chop and cut the meat into small pieces, rinse in several batches of cold water and blanch by placing in boiling water, immediately remove and pour cold water over it.
2. Cook grated onion in just under 10 minutes in melted butter, then add flour, curry and salt, cook the whole together and thin it with boiling water until it becomes as smooth as thin gruel.
3. Place the meat in this and cook covered until tender, thinning occasionally with boiling water.
4. To clear the sauce, strain it after the meat has been removed.
5. Serve rice cooked in water (see recipe 6) alongside.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalvekødfars (Veal Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal or shank meat
- Cold margarine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Grated onion
- Chopped mushrooms or truffles (optional)

## Directions

1. Use either cut meat or shank meat, treating it the same as for beef forcemeat.
2. Instead of tallow, use the same quantity of cold margarine, which is chopped together with the meat.
3. Use salt, pepper and grated onion as seasonings; chopped mushrooms or truffles also have an excellent effect, for example for small balls for soup, for pasties etc.

title: Kalvelever Stegt i Skiver (Fried Veal Liver in Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal liver
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into slices, season, dip in beaten egg and grated breadcrumbs, and fry in butter.
2. Alternatively, dip in flour and fry in butter.
3. Serve with slices of fried bacon and applesauce that is not too sweet.

title: Kalvelever stegt som Bøf (Calf Liver Fried as Steak)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf liver
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Butter or Palmin
- Onion slices
- Stock, broth, or water
- Coloring
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Boiled potatoes
- Salt pork slices

## Directions

1. After the liver is drained and the membrane removed, cut it into very thin slices.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, dip in flour, and fry on a pan in browned butter or Palmin.
3. In a good portion of butter, brown slices of onion and bake a spoonful of flour in it.
4. Thin the flour with stock, broth, or water, adding coloring, Maggi bouillon cubes, and salt.
5. Once the sauce has boiled, pour it over the liver.
6. Serve with boiled potatoes.
7. Many people fry slices of salt pork alongside the liver, which improves the flavor of both. Serve both together in the sauce.

title: Kalvenyre-Ragout (Veal Kidney Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted or boiled kidneys, finely chopped
- Other finely chopped meat (optional)
- Browned butter
- Flour
- Stock or soup
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Paprika
- Wine
- Coloring
- Mushrooms or a little of the pickling liquid they are preserved in

## Directions

1. Cut roasted or boiled kidneys very finely as for salad
2. Mix in other finely chopped meat if desired
3. Make a fairly thick sauce from browned butter, flour, stock or soup, Maggi bouillon cubes, paprika, wine, and coloring
4. Add the chopped kidney meat to the sauce along with mushrooms or a little of their pickling liquid
5. Bring to a boil
6. Serve on slices of French bread fried in butter, or fill into rolls, Roseljer (No. 27), or croustades (No. 1), etc.
7. Can also be served on breaded potatoes
8. Use the same preparation for lamb kidneys

title: Kalveost (Veal Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- Simple veal
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Since one often receives several calves at once in the country than one has use for, one can prepare veal cheese from the simpler meat, which can later be used as a topping for open-faced sandwiches.
2. The meat is boiled and treated as aspic in gelée, pressed firmly in a bowl and pressed by means of a lid with a heavy object on top.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kalveryg à la Jardiniére (Veal Back à la Jardinière)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 to 6 kg veal back, trimmed like venison back
- Butter or claret
- Thin cream, broth, or water
- Salt
- Fresh or preserved vegetables
- Flowers, salad, or parsley for decoration

## Directions

1. Cut the ribs so they are the same length and not visible beyond the fillet.
2. Remove the skin and lard the back in tight strips, then blanch.
3. Place butter or claret on a roasting pan in a hot oven to brown, then lay the back with the larded side up.
4. Sprinkle with salt and cover with thin cream, broth, or water, basting frequently during roasting, which takes about one hour.
5. When carving, loosen the fillet on both sides of the spine and place on a carving board to slice crosswise into smaller pieces, then reassemble on the back to restore its original shape.
6. Decorate with a decorative skewer through a bouquet of flowers, salad, or parsley, and garnish along the sides with fresh or preserved vegetables.

title: Kalvesteg (Roast Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal roast
- Butter
- Flour
- Wheat flour
- Cream
- Sugar

## Directions

1. The roast becomes whiter and thicker by blanching.
2. If you are willing to do this, it is good to lard the veal roast.
3. Roast in a shorter time than beef roast and should not, in our opinion, be red inside.
4. Stir cream and a piece of sugar into the sauce.
5. It is good to let some butter and flour, in which a couple of teaspoons of wheat flour have been worked, come to a boil in a roasting pan and then pour it over the roast after it has been browned in butter; baste it often with this.

title: Kalvesteg (Roasted Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal roast
- Butter
- Flour
- Cream
- Sugar

## Directions

1. The roast becomes whiter and thicker by blanching. If you wish to take on that trouble, it is good to lard the veal roast.
2. It is roasted in a shorter time than beef roast and should, in our opinion, not be red inside.
3. In the sauce stir cream and a piece of sugar.
4. It is good to let some butter and flour, in which a couple of teaspoonfuls of wheat flour have been rubbed, come to a boil in a roasting pan, and then pour it over the roast after it has been browned in butter; with this it is basted often.

title: Kalvesteg (Veal Roast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal leg
- Water

## Directions

1. The veal leg is used for this roast. A typical weight for a leg is about 10 kg, and it can be roasted whole or divided.
2. The piece with the shank is the most advantageous, as it includes the large, tender piece of meat that is excellent both for the main course and later for use as cold cuts.
3. Veal can be stored for a few days in buttermilk or skim milk, which must be renewed every day or every other day, though it is best to prepare it without this preservation method.
4. When ready to use, rinse the meat well, pour boiling water over it, remove the skins, and lard it.

title: Kalvesteg med Rislaag (Veal Roast with Rice Layer)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover veal roast
- Stock from the roast
- Curry
- Paprika
- Cooked rice

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover veal roast into cubes or slices and place in the bottom of a deep dish that can be placed in the oven
2. Make a good smooth sauce from the stock of the roast
3. Season the sauce with curry and paprika
4. Pour some of the sauce over the meat
5. Lay a thick layer of cooked rice (prepared as for curry) over this
6. Pour the remaining strongly spiced sauce over the rice
7. Place the dish in the oven until the ragout is heated through

title: Kalvesteg, opvarmet (Reheated Veal Roast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal roast
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock from the veal roast
- Raw cream
- Browned potatoes
- Lettuce or cucumber salad
- Pickled vegetables
- Preserved fruit compote

## Directions

1. Make a good brown sauce from butter, flour and stock from the veal roast
2. Stir a little raw cream into this sauce
3. Warm the cut meat in this sauce
4. Place the warmed meat on a flat dish
5. Garnish with browned potatoes
6. Serve with lettuce or cucumber salad, pickled vegetables, preserves and fruit compote

title: Kan ikke la'  være (Can't Help Myself)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 1/2 kg (5/8 S) powdered sugar
- juice and zest of 3 lemons
- 15 sheets isinglass
- 1 1/4 dl (1/2 pægl) boiling water
- egg whites, whipped stiff
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) cream for whipped topping

## Directions

1. Stir egg yolks white with powdered sugar
2. Add lemon juice and zest to the mixture along with isinglass dissolved in boiling water
3. Fold in stiffly whipped egg whites immediately after adding isinglass
4. Pour into glass bowls at once
5. Can be prepared one hour before serving or the day before
6. Whip cream to stiff peaks and use for decoration

title: Kanelkager (Cinnamon Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 whole eggs
- 1 pound light sugar
- 1 pound flour
- 1 ounce ground cinnamon
- 1 small teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat 6 whole eggs, one at a time, with 1 pound light sugar until white.
2. Stir in 1 pound flour, 1 ounce ground cinnamon, and 1 small teaspoon baking powder.
3. Place in small lumps on a cold pan that has first been buttered or greased with a spatula, and bake with good heat.
4. Makes approximately 80 cakes.

title: Kanelstænger (Cinnamon Sticks)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 0.5 pound crushed white sugar
- 3 quints crushed Ceylon cinnamon
- 1 egg
- 0.75 pound wheat flour

## Directions

1. Soften 1 pound butter and work together with 0.5 pound crushed white sugar, 3 quints crushed Ceylon cinnamon, 1 egg and 0.75 pound wheat flour until the dough releases from the hands.
2. Roll out the dough in flour on a baking board, cut into oblong sticks and bake for half an hour at good, even heat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kaninsteg (Roasted Rabbit)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit
- Larding strips
- Butter or margarine
- Parsley

## Directions

1. The meat is light but fairly dry, so it is recommended to lard and baste it well.
2. Skin, clean, cut up and roast the rabbit as you would hare.
3. Serve as hare or as lamb roast, larded with parsley.

title: Kapers i kogt Eddike (Capers in Cooked Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Seeds or berries
- Brine
- Raw vinegar

## Directions

1. Pour the aforementioned brine over the seeds, where they stand until the next day.
2. Then pour away the brine and pour raw vinegar over them.
3. The day after, pour fresh vinegar over the seeds and tie the jar shut.

title: Kapers i raa Eddike (Capers in Raw Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Seeds or berries
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) salt
- 1 l (1 pot) boiling water
- Vinegar, as much as needed to cover the seeds
- Several whole white peppercorns

## Directions

1. Place the seeds or berries in a brine of 125 gr (1/4 lb) salt to 1 l (1 pot) boiling water.
2. The next day, drain them in a colander and let them stand so the brine drains well.
3. Bring as much vinegar as is necessary to cover the seeds to a boil together with some whole white peppercorns.
4. Place the seeds directly into the boiling vinegar and then pour into jars or glasses, which are covered with a cloth and tied shut.

title: Kapers-Sauce (Caper Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls capers
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) even gravy sauce
- Wine
- A little paprika
- Salt
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Add 2 tablespoonfuls of capers to 1/2 L of even gravy sauce
2. Prepare with wine, a little paprika, salt and sugar to taste

title: Kapers-Sauce (Caper Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Meat or fish stock
- Capers
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- White pepper
- Sugar
- Wine

## Directions

1. Bake butter and flour and thin with meat or fish stock to a white, smooth sauce
2. Add 2 tablespoons of capers
3. Bind with 2 egg yolks per 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) of sauce
4. Season to taste with salt, ground white pepper, sugar, and a little wine

title: Kapers-Sauce (Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- veal bouillon or cream
- capers
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Bake butter and flour together and thin with veal bouillon or cream.
2. Add capers.
3. Thicken the sauce with a couple of egg yolks.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Karamel-Budding (Caramel Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) icing sugar
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) water
- 12 egg yolks
- 75 Gr (15 Kvint) icing sugar
- 1 L (1 Pot) boiling cream
- vanilla essence
- 6 egg whites
- cream foam
- cognac

## Directions

1. Make the caramel the day before: cook 1/2 Kg (1 S) icing sugar with 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) water until dry, stirring constantly until it becomes liquid again and a beautiful brown color (do not let it become too dark or it will taste burnt)
2. Pour some of the caramel sugar into the dry mold and turn it well until a layer of sugar has set on both the sides and bottom
3. For the pudding: stir 12 egg yolks with 75 Gr (15 Kvint) icing sugar and whisk well with a whisk while stirring 1 L (1 Pot) boiling cream into the mixture and adding vanilla essence
4. Stir regularly in the custard until it is cold, then fold in 6 whipped egg whites
5. Pour the pudding into the prepared mold and cook for approximately 2 hours until completely set
6. Keep the pudding in the mold until the next day
7. Serve on a not too flat platter with the caramel that comes with the pudding
8. For the remaining caramel sugar: dilute with water and stir with a little cream foam and a glass of cognac the next day, then serve with the pudding

title: Karamel-Budding (Caramel Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart sweet cream
- 15 egg yolks
- Two large heaped spoonfuls sugar per portion
- A little vanilla
- Grated lemon zest
- Beaten egg whites
- 4 ounces white sugar
- A little water

## Directions

1. Whisk the cream and egg yolks separately, each with two large heaped spoonfuls of sugar
2. Then whisk together with a little vanilla and grated lemon zest
3. Finally add a couple beaten egg whites
4. Meanwhile, melt 4 ounces white sugar in a little water and stir over the fire until light brown
5. Pour this into the dry pudding mold and turn it around until it covers all sides
6. Pour the batter into the mold and cook for a little hour
7. Serve cold with white sweet sauce
8. Turn out of the mold when serving

title: Karamel-Creme (Caramel Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 Q) powdered sugar
- 3 tablespoons water
- 1 L (1 pot) skimmed milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 125 gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar
- 10 sheets isinglass
- 1/4 L (1 pægl) cream for whipped topping
- vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Heat powdered sugar with water in a pot, stirring until it becomes dark caramel
2. Add skimmed milk and bring to a boil
3. Whisk egg yolks with powdered sugar and whisk into the boiling milk, then remove from heat immediately
4. Pour into a dish to cool and add isinglass, stirring until melted
5. Keep the cream moving gently while it begins to set to prevent a skin from forming
6. Pour into glass bowls and refrigerate until the next day
7. Decorate with whipped cream mixed with vanilla extract

title: Karamel-Is (Caramel Ice Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar
- 1 L (1 Pot) cream as for vanilla ice cream Nr. 1068
- 8 eggs
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar to caramel sugar (see Caramel Pudding, Nr. 1037).
2. Stir this into 1 L (1 Pot) cream as for vanilla ice cream Nr. 1068.
3. Stir the eggs with only 125 Gr (1/4 S) sugar, as the ice cream would otherwise be too sweet.

title: Karamelbudding (Caramel Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- 10 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 15 quintals finely ground melis sugar
- 1 quarter-pound white sugar

## Directions

1. Heat 1 pot sweet cream.
2. Whip together 10 egg yolks and 6 egg whites with 15 quintals finely ground melis sugar.
3. When the cream is hot, remove from heat and add the eggs while stirring constantly.
4. In a separate pan, dip 1 quarter-pound white sugar in water and bring to a boil until it becomes dry and again liquid.
5. Pour this caramel into a mold, turning and tilting it to coat all sides, ensuring no dry spots remain.
6. If any caramel remains, pour it into the cream.
7. Pour the cream into the caramel-lined mold, place the mold in water, and boil for one hour.
8. Prepare this pudding the day before, turn it out of the mold onto a dish, and serve cold.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kardinal (Cardinal)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pounds sugar
- 6-7 oranges
- 1 bottle Rhine wine
- 1 bottle Champagne

## Directions

1. Dip the sugar in water and place it in a bowl together with the juice of 4 oranges and some of the peel. Add the Rhine wine and Champagne gradually. Peel the remaining oranges and add them. Set on ice until ready to serve.

title: Karot-Salat (Carrot Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- small carrots
- lettuce leaves
- oil
- vinegar or lemon juice
- pepper
- parsley
- cooked green peas

## Directions

1. Scrape small carrots or cook them first, and rub off the skin as you would with hot cooked beets
2. Cut them into slices with a fluted vegetable knife and arrange them on a bed of lettuce leaves
3. Pour a sauce of oil, vinegar or lemon juice, and pepper over them and sprinkle chopped parsley on top, or arrange cooked green peas in a ring on top of the salad

title: Karotter (Pickled Carrots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small carrots
- Whole white pepper
- Spanish pepper husks
- Pickling vinegar

## Directions

1. Scrape the small carrots, rinse them, and half-cook them in water.
2. Drain them in a colander and rinse them with cold water.
3. Dry the roots in a cloth and place them in glasses or jars together with the spices, which consist of some whole white pepper and a few Spanish pepper husks.
4. Pour over as much good pickling vinegar as is necessary to cover the vegetables.
5. Let them stand until the next day, then pour off the vinegar, give it a boil, and immediately pour it over the roots, after which the jar is tied shut with parchment paper.
6. Carrots can be peeled with a fluted herb knife and thereby get a beautiful appearance.

title: Karpen (Carp)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Wine vinegar
- Salt
- Water
- Horseradish cream

## Directions

1. Scrape the fish along the scales, but leave the scales and head on
2. Remove the innards
3. Pour wine vinegar over both sides and sprinkle with a little salt
4. Let stand to turn blue until ready to cook
5. Cook in water with a little salt and some of the vinegar used to pour over the fish
6. Serve with horseradish cream
7. To remove any muddy taste, place some bread crumbs in the cooking water
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Karper, kogt i Rødvin (Carp Cooked in Red Wine)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour (1 good spoonful)
- Red wine (½ bottle)
- Thin broth (enough to make sufficient sauce to cover the fish)
- Bay leaves
- Whole peppercorns
- Onions (a couple) or a small bunch of soup vegetables
- Carp (prepared as in the previous recipe)
- Roe or milk from the carp
- Parsley
- Lemon slices
- Sago flour (if needed for thickening)

## Directions

1. Brown a slice of butter and cook a good spoonful of flour in it.
2. Gradually add ½ bottle of red wine and then enough thin broth to make sufficient sauce to cover the fish.
3. Add bay leaves, whole peppercorns, and onions or a small bunch of soup vegetables for seasoning.
4. Place the prepared carp in the sauce with the scale side up, and cook gently along with the roe or milk until tender.
5. If necessary, thicken the sauce further with a little sago flour mixed with liquid.
6. Strain the sauce and serve in a sauce boat.
7. Garnish the fish with parsley and lemon slices.

title: Karri af Faarekjod (Curry of Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton
- Curry seasonings

## Directions

1. Prepare curry of mutton exactly as for calf curry.

title: Karry-Sauce (Curry Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onion
- Flour
- Meat or fish stock
- Curry powder
- Rice

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and sweat a grated onion in it before adding the flour
2. Bake the flour and thin with meat or fish stock, depending on what the sauce is to be used for
