3. When the sauce has boiled, add curry powder stirred in to taste
4. Serve cooked rice with curry
5. If the curry sauce is to be used for fish dishes, the onion is usually omitted

title: Karry-Suppe (Curry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L (4 pots) thin vegetable stock
- 1 celery
- 4 carrots
- 2 medium onions
- 125 g (1/4 lb) butter
- 1 teaspoon curry
- 125 g (1/4 lb) flour
- 1/4 L (1 pint) rice
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook 1 celery and 4 carrots until tender in the stock, then peel, cut in pieces, and place in the tureen.
2. Grate 2 medium onions and sauté in 125 g (1/4 lb) butter together with 1 teaspoon curry.
3. Add 125 g (1/4 lb) flour and thin with the stock.
4. When it has boiled, it is ready, but more curry may be added to taste if desired, first stirred in water or stock.
5. 1/4 L (1 pint) rice, cooked in ample water with salt, can either be placed in the tureen or served separately.

title: Karse (Watercress)
body:
## Ingredients

- watercress
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse the watercress, pick it, and prepare with vinegar and sugar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Karse-Salat (Watercress Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A couple of egg yolks
- oil
- vinegar
- a little wine
- salt
- pepper
- watercress

## Directions

1. Mix egg yolks with oil, vinegar, a little wine, salt and pepper
2. Clean the watercress well and pour the sauce over it

title: Kartoffel Salat (Potato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal parts potatoes and beets
- Mustard
- Oil
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Raw egg yolk
- Pepper
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Celery, raw apples, cooked egg whites and mushrooms (optional)
- Onion (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut equal amounts of potatoes and beets into thin slices, place on the salad dish and pour over with a sauce made from a little stirred mustard, stirred with oil or with hard-boiled egg yolks, which are first mashed with one raw egg yolk, pepper, salt and vinegar, half of it from the beets
2. You can add celery, raw apples, cooked egg whites and mushrooms; it is then best to cut everything small and stir it in the sauce
3. If you prefer onion, these must first be cooked and chopped

title: Kartoffel-Budding med Skarp Sauce (Potato Pudding with Sharp Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds potatoes
- 0.5 pound butter
- 15 eggs
- Onion
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. When the potatoes are cooked, not overcooked, peel them and grate them with a light hand so no lumps remain.
2. Bake them in butter; when baked, stir in 15 eggs and plenty of onion, pepper, and salt.
3. Boil for 3 hours and serve with strong brown sauce.

title: Kartoffel-Kager (Potato Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover cooked potatoes
- Eggs
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Milk
- Butter

## Directions

1. Use potatoes left over from the previous day; grate them and mix with a couple of eggs, a little salt and nutmeg, and, if necessary to even them out, a little milk.
2. Fry them in browned butter either in flat cakes or like patties.
3. They should be beautiful, light brown and eaten with all kinds of preserves or cooked fruits.

title: Kartoffel-Mos (Mashed Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Large mealy potatoes
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- White pepper, ground
- Milk

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and boil them tender in salted water.
2. Drain the water.
3. Mash the potatoes with a spoon or potato masher.
4. If lumps remain, press them through a sieve or coarse strainer.
5. Place the mash over heat with a piece of butter, salt, ground white pepper, and enough milk to achieve the proper consistency.
6. When arranged, decorate with a fluted butter knife if desired.

title: Kartoffel-Mos (Potato Purée)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled, cooked potatoes
- Eggs (approximately 1 per 1/2 kg potatoes)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter (a small amount)

## Directions

1. Mash the cooked potatoes thoroughly
2. Press them through a coarse sieve
3. Mix in eggs (ca. 1 to each 1/2 kg potatoes), salt, pepper and a small amount of butter
4. Using a pastry bag, pipe the mixture into roses, small mounds, wreaths or other figures on a cold buttered baking sheet
5. Bake until light brown at gentle heat

title: Kartoffel-Mos med Bagt, Revet Brød (Mashed Potatoes with Baked Bread Crumbs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Prepared warm mashed potatoes
- Grated French bread
- Butter

## Directions

1. Arrange warm prepared mashed potatoes.
2. Cover completely or stripe with grated French bread, fried light brown in butter in a pan.

title: Kartoffel-Mos med Brunede Løg (Mashed Potatoes with Browned Onions)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mashed potatoes
- Onions
- Butter

## Directions

1. Pour the mash into a serving dish.
2. Top with crispy browned onions, fried in butter as for beef.

title: Kartoffel-Mos med Kogte Svesker (Mashed Potatoes with Cooked Prunes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Warm prepared mashed potatoes
- Prunes, cooked and pitted

## Directions

1. Arrange the warm prepared mash in layers with cooked, warm prunes without pits.
2. Serve with warm or cold roasted pork.

title: Kartoffel-Mos med kogte Svesker (Mashed Potatoes with Stewed Prunes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mashed potatoes
- Cooked prunes, pitted

## Directions

1. Arrange the warm, prepared mashed potatoes in layers with cooked, warm prunes without pits. Serve with warm or cold fried pork.

title: Kartoffel-Mos med Tomat-Puré (Mashed Potatoes with Tomato Purée)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mashed potatoes
- Tomato purée
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Mix the mashed potatoes with tomato purée instead of milk.
2. Add salt and pepper as the vegetable requires.
3. Serve with fried fish dishes.

title: Kartoffel-Mos med Æbler (Mashed Potatoes with Apples)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Apples

## Directions

1. Either cut some pieces of apples and stew them together with the peeled potatoes for the mash and press through a sieve with them, or soften small pieces of peeled, good apples in the warm, prepared mash. Serve with roast pork, cutlets and similar dishes.

title: Kartoffel-Mos, Bagt i Ovn (Baked Potato Mash)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mashed potatoes (for 12 people)
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) butter
- 3 egg yolks
- Salt
- Ground white pepper
- Breadcrumbs
- Pickled cucumbers, optional

## Directions

1. Stir 125 grams of butter, 3 egg yolks, salt and ground white pepper into the mash.
2. Butter a baking dish and sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
3. Fill the dish with the mash.
4. Sprinkle breadcrumbs on top again.
5. Place the dish in a warm oven until the mash has a beautiful light brown color.
6. Arrange in the dish with a napkin around it.
7. Chopped pickled cucumbers may be arranged in a star shape on top.
8. Serve with meat dishes.

title: Kartoffel-Rouletter (Potato Roulettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked, cold potatoes
- 2 eggs
- 4 spoonfuls butter
- a little sweet cream
- salt
- pepper
- fine sifted bread crumbs
- egg whites
- clarified butter for frying

## Directions

1. Grate cooked cold potatoes and mix with 2 eggs, 4 spoonfuls butter, a little sweet cream, salt, and pepper. Mix well together, and form the mixture into oblong or round roulettes, which are rolled in fine sifted bread crumbs and set aside for half an hour. Then dip them in egg whites and roll in bread crumbs, and thus they are breaded twice. Fry them in clarified butter, 5 to 6 pieces at a time, until they become beautifully golden brown. Take them out with a slotted spoon and place on a sieve so the butter can drip off. Use them as garnish for roasted meat and similar dishes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartoffel-Salat (Potato Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A little flour
- butter
- soup stock
- a couple of egg yolks
- oil
- pickled shallots
- potato slices
- watercress, chervil, or parsley

## Directions

1. Cook a little flour and butter together, thin with soup stock to make a thin white sauce, thickened with a couple of egg yolks
2. Stir the sauce until cold, then add 2 tablespoons of oil, pickled shallot vinegar to taste, and some finely chopped pickled shallots
3. Toss the potato slices in the sauce
4. At serving, sprinkle the salad with watercress, finely chopped chervil, or parsley
5. Serve cold with hot fried fish and roasts

title: Kartoffel-Salat Alternative (Potato Salad with Tomato)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- tomato purée
- salt
- ground white pepper
- mustard
- parsley
- grated onion
- small cooked, cold, peeled chestnut potatoes
- watercress

## Directions

1. Make a sauce from tomato purée, salt, ground white pepper, mustard, chopped parsley and a little grated onion
2. Toss small cooked, cold, peeled chestnut potatoes in the sauce
3. Sprinkle watercress over the salad

title: Kartoffel-Salat Nr. 1 (Potato Salad No. 1)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Onions
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes the day before, peel and cut them into thin slices
2. Cut a couple of onions likewise into thin slices and cook tender in a little water in the bottom of the pan
3. Add a good piece of butter, salt, pepper, and vinegar
4. When the butter is melted and the sauce sufficiently seasoned, turn the potatoes gently into it without breaking
5. Serve warm with meat

title: Kartoffel-Salat Nr. 2 (Potato Salad No. 2)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) boiling stock
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) cold butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- One tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- Tarragon vinegar or juice of one lemon
- Egg yolks
- Thin potato slices

## Directions

1. Stir the cold butter, salt, pepper, finely chopped parsley, and tarragon vinegar or lemon juice into the boiling stock
2. Thicken the sauce with 2-3 egg yolks
3. Turn thin slices of potato into it
4. Serve warm with veal and lamb roasts, veal cutlets, etc.

title: Kartoffel-Sne (Potato Snow)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Very large, floury potatoes
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Boil very large, floury potatoes in salted water. As soon as they are tender, drain the water and keep the pot warm while peeling the potatoes and then pressing them through a purée sieve or grating over a very hot serving dish. Serve the potato snow as quickly as possible, and it must not be touched after it has been pressed through the sieve or grated on the grater, as handling it will cause it to lose its light appearance. Serve with spiced meat dishes such as breaded meat, goulash, boneless birds, etc.

title: Kartoffel-Suppe (Potato Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 S) peeled, floury potatoes
- 4 L (4 pots) spare broth
- 1 whisk
- 4 carrots
- 75 grams (15 skein) butter
- 50 grams (10 skein) flour
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 L (1 mug) raw cream
- ground white pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes tender in water with salt.
2. Drain the water, and rub the potatoes through a sieve with 4 L (4 pots) of broth in which 1 whisk and 4 carrots have been cooked.
3. Bake 75 grams (15 skein) butter and 50 grams (10 skein) flour together and thin with the potato soup, which is bound with 4 egg yolks beaten with 1/4 L (1 mug) raw cream, but without bringing it to a boil afterwards.
4. Season the soup with ground white pepper and salt.
5. The soup can be served with meat balls cooked in the soup, cut carrots cooked in the soup, cut Bavarian sausages cooked in the soup, small cut leeks sautéed in butter, or onion cut in cubes and sautéed in butter.
6. Serve with toasted bread.

title: Kartoffel-Suppe (Potato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- cold water
- carrot and white root vegetables
- a large bunch of herbs
- small whole onions
- salt
- ground black pepper or cayenne pepper
- meat or bacon stock (optional)
- fresh bacon in small cubes (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes raw, cut them into pieces and place them on the fire with so much cold water that it stands well over them
2. When they have cooked some, add carrot and white root vegetables and a large bunch of herbs; take this out when it is done and press it well
3. Cook some small whole onions in it together with salt and a little ground black pepper or cayenne pepper
4. You can add a little less water at first and later dilute the soup with stock from salt meat or bacon
5. You can also cut fresh bacon in small cubes, which is cooked with the potatoes and eaten in the soup

title: Kartoffel-Suppe (Potato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Cold water
- Yellow and white root vegetables
- A bundle of herbs
- Whole small onions
- Salt
- Ground black pepper or Cayenne pepper
- Salted meat or meat broth (optional)
- Fresh meat, diced (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes raw, cut them into pieces and put them on the fire with enough cold water that it roughly stands over them.
2. When they have cooked somewhat, add yellow and white root vegetables and a bundle of herbs; this is removed when soft and pressed well.
3. Cook several whole small onions in this with salt and a little ground black pepper or Cayenne pepper.
4. You can add less water at first and later thin the soup with broth from salted meat or meat.
5. If desired, fresh meat can be cut into small dice, which are cooked with the potatoes and served in the soup.

title: Kartoffelboller (Potato Balls)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 80 grams (16 dram) butter or margarine
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) grated, cooked, cold potatoes
- 25 grams (5 dram) potato flour
- 3 eggs
- Salt
- Sugar
- Zest of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Melt the butter or margarine
2. Bake the grated cooked cold potatoes in the butter
3. Sprinkle with potato flour
4. Bake until the dough releases from the pot and spoon
5. Lift the pot from the heat and stir in 3 eggs one at a time
6. Add a little salt, sugar, and grated lemon zest
7. Cook in salted water

title: Kartoffelboller (Potato Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound boiled potatoes
- 3 ounces flour
- 2 whole eggs
- Sugar
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel and grate the potatoes and stir in the flour and the rest.
2. Mix well together and place on the soup.

title: Kartoffelbrei (Mashed Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Broth or milk
- Butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. Peel potatoes raw, cut them into slices and place on the fire with just enough broth or milk so it comes level with the potatoes.
2. Cook until completely cooked through, strain and press through a sieve.
3. Cook the mash again briefly with butter, salt, and if it is too thick, with more broth or milk.
4. Cooked potatoes can be grated; they then do not need to be pressed through the sieve.
5. Instead of broth, water can be used, but then more butter must be used.

title: Kartoffelbudding (Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cold cooked potatoes
- a quarter pound butter
- 3/4 cup cream
- half a pound ground sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 18 quintal sweet almonds
- 9 quintal bitter almonds
- a little grated lemon zest
- cardamom
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Peel 1 pound cold cooked potatoes and grate or pound very finely, then bake in a quarter pound butter. Then thin and stir with 3/4 cup cream. Then stir for about a quarter hour first with half a pound ground sugar, then with 10 egg yolks, 18 quintal sweet almonds and 9 quintal bitter almonds, a little grated lemon zest, cardamom, and finally with the beaten egg whites. Boil the pudding in a water bath for 2 hours and serve with sweet fruit sauce with a little red wine in it or with melted butter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartoffelkage (Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds cooked, peeled, cold potatoes
- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 5 quintals finely pounded bitter almonds
- 12 egg yolks
- 35 quintals powdered sugar
- Zest of 1 lemon
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Egg whites

## Directions

1. Grate 1.5 pounds cooked, peeled, cold potatoes and stir, always in the same direction, with 1 pound sweet and 5 quintals finely pounded bitter almonds, 12 egg yolks, 35 quintals powdered sugar, the grated zest of 1 lemon, and the juice of 2 lemons
2. When this mixture is well stirred together, gradually stir in the whipped egg whites just before placing the cake in a buttered form
3. Immediately place in the oven
4. Bake for one hour and should be a fine pale brown
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartoffelkage med Æblekompot (Potato Cake with Apple Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- 2 pounds powdered sugar
- zest and juice of 1 lemon
- 1 pound grated cooked cold potatoes
- 18 quintals sweet almonds, finely ground
- 12 quintals bitter almonds, finely ground
- 12 egg whites, beaten to stiff foam
- apple compote
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat 12 egg yolks for 3 quarter-hours with 2 pounds powdered sugar, and add the strained juice and grated zest of 1 lemon, 1 pound grated cooked cold potatoes, 18 quintals sweet and 12 quintals bitter finely ground almonds.
2. When beaten for a quarter-hour more, fold in the 12 egg whites beaten to stiff foam and mix carefully.
3. In a greased tin form with loose bottom, lay a layer of the dough and on top a layer of apple compote and lastly again a layer of dough.
4. Bake slowly at even heat for 1 hour.
5. The apple compote should be cooked with a little cinnamon, and the apples should be whole and not cooked too soft.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartoffelmels-Budding (Potato Starch Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 grams potato starch
- 100 grams melted margarine
- 0.75 liter boiling milk
- 10 egg yolks
- 125 grams powdered sugar
- egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. 175 grams of potato starch is baked in 100 grams of melted margarine and diluted with 0.75 liter of boiling milk.
2. While the batter is warm, it is stirred with 10 egg yolks, 125 grams of powdered sugar, and finally with the beaten egg whites.
3. It is cooked for 2.5 hours and served with red wine or fruit sauce.
4. The batter can have essences added as indicated for other puddings.

title: Kartoffelmels-Omelet (Potato Starch Omelet)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon potato starch
- 5 tablespoons milk
- egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks well and stir in the potato starch, milk, and finally the beaten egg whites.
2. Melt a little butter on a pan and pour in the eggs.
3. As soon as the omelet is baked on one side, cover it with jam and fold together.

title: Kartoffelmels-Opløb (Potato Flour Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 drams powdered sugar
- 18 drams butter
- 1 measure thin cream
- 18 drams potato flour
- 9 egg yolks
- 9 egg whites
- jam

## Directions

1. Heat 12 drams powdered sugar, 18 drams butter and 1 measure thin cream in a pan and boil together
2. Mix 18 drams potato flour with 1 measure thin cream and bake with the first mixture in the same way as making buns, until the dough leaves the pan
3. Remove from heat and stir in 9 egg yolks one by one
4. When cold, stir in the 9 egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks
5. Butter a dish and lay a layer of jam at the bottom
6. Pour the dough over the jam and bake the omelet in the dish for 1 hour
7. Bake slowly, especially at the beginning
8. Serve warm, sprinkled with powdered sugar with a napkin around the dish
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartoffelmelsboller (Potato Starch Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound melted butter
- 1/4 pound potato starch (or half potato starch and half wheat flour)
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Stir the potato starch (or flour mixture) into the melted butter and bake as described for Melboller
2. Stir in 6 eggs, one egg at a time
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartoffelmelsgrød (Potato Starch Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarter pounds potato starch
- 4 pots sweet milk
- a quarter pound butter
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Stir three quarter pounds of potato starch little by little with 4 pots of sweet milk
2. Place over the fire together with a quarter pound of butter
3. Cook while stirring continuously
4. When thoroughly cooked, add salt
5. Serve immediately, as it otherwise becomes stringy
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartoffelmelsgrød (Potato Starch Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Three-quarter pound potato starch
- 4 pots sweet milk
- One-quarter pound butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. Mix the potato starch gradually with 4 pots of sweet milk and place over heat together with one-quarter pound of butter.
2. Cook with continuous stirring.
3. When fully cooked, add salt and serve immediately, as it becomes long and stringy if allowed to stand.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartoffelmos-Rand (Potato Purée Border)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Potato purée (as in Nr. 333)
- Egg
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Pipe the purée onto a dish that can be exposed to heat in the oven
2. Bake the beautifully piped purée until light yellow
3. Alternatively, the purée can be shaped on the dish with 2 spoons, brushed with egg and sprinkled with breadcrumbs, then baked until light yellow
4. If baking the purée in a ring form, butter the form and sprinkle with breadcrumbs
5. Mix the potato purée with egg, a very little butter, milk, pepper and salt
6. Bake in the ring form in the oven for approximately 1/2 hour
7. Turn out and fill with a meat dish or ragout

title: Kartoffelsalat (Potato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Unpeeled potatoes
- Salt
- Water
- Vinegar
- Salad oil or melted butter
- Chopped shallot
- Pepper
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook unpeeled potatoes in salted water and drain
2. Cut the potatoes into thin slices
3. Pour some vinegar over them and add a little salad oil or melted butter, chopped shallot, salt, pepper and sugar to taste
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartofler (Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place them on the heat in as much water as it stands over them, salt is added and they cook continuously until they are tender, then drain the water, salt is sprinkled on and the pot is shaken well so the salt gets around, thereafter place the pot for half a dozen minutes at the back of the oven without a lid, so they can steam off and become dry. It is best that they are thereafter eaten immediately, but if you are obliged to wait, then as they are peeled, they can be placed in hot water with plenty of salt in; they are covered and placed at the back of the oven and in this way they can be kept good for a couple of hours. If they are very large potatoes, then you can peel them raw and either cook them whole or cut them in half; as you peel them they must be placed in cold water so they do not become dark. They are cooked and dried off like the previous ones.

title: Kartofler à la Lyonnaise (Potatoes Lyonnaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onions
- Potatoes, boiled the previous day
- Parsley, chopped
- Salt
- White pepper, ground
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Place thin slices of onion in a pan with plenty of butter and brown them lightly.
2. Slice the potatoes, which must not be mealy, and brown them in the pan until they acquire a brownish-yellow color.
3. Before serving, stir in chopped parsley, salt, ground white pepper, and vinegar to taste.
4. Serve as hot as possible with roasted meat, cutlets, etc.

title: Kartofler à la Maître d'Hôtel (Potatoes Maître d'Hôtel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- About 12 large potatoes
- 1/4 liter (1 pint) soup or Maggi bouillon cube dissolved in boiling water
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- Salt
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) butter
- 2-3 egg yolks
- Juice of 1 lemon, or tarragon or ordinary vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare the soup or bouillon with water, chopped parsley, salt and butter.
2. Bring to a boil and bind with 2-3 egg yolks.
3. Add the juice of 1 lemon or tarragon or ordinary vinegar to taste.
4. Slice firm, cold potatoes into thin slices.
5. Warm them gently in the sauce.
6. Serve with open-faced sandwiches or as a vegetable alongside fried fish and meat.

title: Kartofler, Engelske (English Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Large potatoes
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Scoop small, round balls from large potatoes using a potato scoop.
2. Boil them tender in salted water.
3. Drain the water and steam them dry.
4. Layer a good slice of butter rolled in finely chopped parsley between them.
5. Swing the potatoes several times in the pan without breaking them.
6. Once the butter is melted, quickly arrange them in a warm serving dish.
7. Serve with cooked fish dishes.

title: Kartofler, raaskrællede, brunede og stuvede (Potatoes, Raw-Peeled, Browned and Stewed)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Salt
- Butter
- Powdered sugar
- Fat or oil
- Milk
- Flour
- Water

## Directions

1. For raw-peeled potatoes: Peel potatoes raw before cooking to make them more mealy and remove the unpleasant taste of sprouted potatoes. Let raw-peeled potatoes stand a day in cold water, then boil in a pot without a lid. Start them in cold salted water. When they begin to boil, skim the water, and when almost tender, drain the water and add a piece of butter to the pot. Shake the potatoes and cook tender. For browned potatoes: Choose small round potatoes, cook and peel them, then brown in lightly browned butter. When potatoes are brown, add powdered sugar. Potatoes can also be browned in slices: Peel raw potatoes and cut into very thin slices. Place in very hot but not burning fat in a pan. The fat should be a couple of inches high in the pan, and stir the potatoes so they do not stick together. Do not add too many at once for the same reason. When potatoes have taken on a nice brown color, remove and sprinkle with a little salt. This method is used much in France, where potatoes are baked in fine oil instead of fat. For stewed potatoes: Cook potatoes first, peel and cut into suitable pieces. Make a sauce of melted butter, flour and milk, and when smooth, give the potatoes one boil. For mashed potatoes: add a little melted butter, milk or broth to these potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kartofler, stegte (Roasted Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Very large, uniform potatoes
- Kitchen salt
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Brush very large, uniform potatoes clean in lukewarm water, dry in a coarse cloth and place on a baking sheet, covered with a layer of kitchen salt. Bake for 1 hour in a hot oven, turning them occasionally. Arrange them as hot as possible in a napkin on a dish. Serve with cold or softened butter. Serve with salt and pepper. Eat with a spoon from the skin.

title: Kartofler, svenske (Swedish Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion slices
- Butter
- Cooked, firm potatoes
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Brown onion slices in butter on a frying pan and turn and heat slices of cooked, firm potatoes in it. They should not be fried hard, but should really only be heated in plenty of butter. Season them with salt, if the butter is not salty enough, and sprinkle with ground pepper. With this potato vegetable, for example cutlets or fried meatballs can be garnished.

title: Karudsen (Crucian Carp)
body:
## Ingredients

- Karudsen (crucian carp)
- Scales (optional)
- Cream and horseradish or thyme sauce

## Directions

1. Use only when the fish has reasonable size
2. Remove the innards but keep the head on
3. Cook whole either with scales on or scrape the scales off
4. Serve with cream and horseradish or thyme sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kassebrod (Cassé Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1 pound butter
- 3 tablespoons cream
- 3 tablespoons rum
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- sugar

## Directions

1. Stir together flour, butter, cream, rum, 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks into a stiff dough.
2. Roll out and place on a baking sheet.
3. Sprinkle generously with sugar and bake at even heat.
4. When baked enough, cut it into oblong pieces.

title: Kastanie-Sauce (Chestnut Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A few cooked chestnuts
- Madeira sauce

## Directions

1. Chop a few cooked chestnuts and blend them into the Madeira sauce

title: Kastanier (Preserved Chestnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/2 kg chestnuts
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the brown shell from large selected genuine chestnuts.
2. Place them for a few minutes in boiling water and then in cold water so that the inner thin seed coat can be removed more easily.
3. Place the peeled chestnuts in boiling water with lemon juice and boil until almost tender.
4. Remove them with a slotted spoon or place on a sieve.
5. Melt 1/2 kg sugar in 1 1/4 dl of the water in which the chestnuts were boiled and add lemon juice to taste.
6. Bring the chestnuts to a boil in this syrup and immediately pour into a jar and set aside until the next day.
7. The next day, drain the syrup, bring to a boil and pour boiling over the chestnuts.
8. On the third day, place the syrup back on the heat to reduce until smooth.
9. Add the chestnuts to the boiling syrup and pour into jars and tie them up while the preserve is still hot.

title: Kathrine-Blommekage (Katherine Plum Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound Katherine plums
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 stick cinnamon
- Lemon zest
- Sweet almonds, blanched and peeled
- 3 eggs
- 65 quintals flour
- Half pound sugar
- 12 quintals butter
- 12 egg yolks
- Half a pound powdered icing sugar
- 3 cups thick sweet cream
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 12 egg whites, whipped to peaks
- Whipped cream with vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Wash and cook 1 pound Katherine plums with 1 pound sugar, a stick of cinnamon, and a little lemon zest.
2. When tender, remove the pits and replace each with a blanched and peeled sweet almond.
3. Knead together 3 eggs, 65 quintals flour, half pound sugar, and 12 quintals butter into a dough.
4. Roll the dough thin and line a pan with a removable bottom so the pan is completely covered.
5. Dry the dough either in a tart pan or in an oven where something has just been baked.
6. Whisk 12 egg yolks well together with half a pound powdered icing sugar.
7. Then whisk in 3 cups thick sweet cream, the juice of 2 lemons, the grated zest of 1 lemon, and the 12 whipped egg whites.
8. Lay the plums on the dough in the lined form and pour the mixture over them.
9. Bake the cake in a tart pan with heat from above and much heat from below for one hour.
10. When serving, cover with whipped cream mixed with vanilla sugar.
11. The cake will sink somewhat in the center.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kejsersuppe (Emperor Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pund magert Oxekjød
- et halvt Pund Kalvekjød
- et Fjerdingspund Skinke
- et Fjerdingspund Oxetalg
- et Par Skiver Flæsk
- nogle Gulerødder
- en Selleri
- 10 Løg
- lidt Peber og Salt
- et Par Spiseskefulde Mel
- 4 Potter god Bouillon
- lidt Kajennepeber og Portvin

## Directions

1. Skæres 1 Pund magert Oxekjød, et halvt Pund Kalvekjød og et Fjerdingspund Skinke i smalle Smaastykker og lægges i en Gryde tilligemed et Fjerdingspund Oxetalg, et Par Skiver Flæsk, nogle Gulerødder, en Selleri og 10 Løg, som ere skaarne i Skiver, samt lidt Peber og Salt.
2. Sættes det over Ilden og det Hele røres, indtil det begynder at blive brunligt i Bunden.
3. Kommes et Par Spiseskefulde Mel deri, omrøres det godt og opspædes det samtidig med 4 Potter god Bouillon.
4. Efter at være kogt sies Suppen og tilsættes lidt Kajennepeber og Portvin.
5. Tilsættes fine Kjødboller.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kiks med Oste-Smør (Crackers with Cheese Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams (40 quintals) Gorgonzola cheese
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) butter

## Directions

1. Place the cheese in a bowl, set it over boiling water, and as soon as the cheese is soft and easily stirred, blend it after cooling into the stirred butter
2. Give this a slight green color and place in a pastry bag as soon as it is sufficiently stiffened to pipe
3. Set off in small stars or roses over the crackers
4. Arrange on parchment paper
5. Use not very thin crackers

title: Kirsebær- og Ribs-Gelé (Cherry and Currant Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal quantities of cherries and currants
- 565 gr sugar
- 1 1/4 dl water

## Directions

1. Use equal quantities of cherries and currants.
2. Remove the pits from the cherries, which are pounded in the mortar, and pick over the currants before pressing them.
3. Mix the cherries and currant juice now in the jelly cloth, where it remains until the next day; then weigh the juice and cook into jelly.
4. See its preparation in No. 93.

title: Kirsebær-Gelé (Cherry Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg cherries (sour cooking cherries or a mixture of sour and sweet)
- 625 gr sugar
- 1 1/4 dl water

## Directions

1. Either sour cooking cherries may be used, or a mixture of sour and sweet berries.
2. Pick the cherries from their stems and pound in a mortar so the pits are crushed.
3. Pour the mass into the jelly cloth, where it remains overnight.
4. The next day, weigh it and cook into jelly as mentioned in No. 93.
5. The refuse from this as well as from the following jelly must be pressed through a coarse sieve before it can be used for marmalade.

title: Kirsebær-Is (Cherry Ice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 lb) pitted cherries
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) powdered sugar
- 3 3/4 dl (1 1/2 cup) water

## Directions

1. Make the ice from sweet cherries, remove the pits and crush them.
2. Crush the kernels and cook them with the syrup made from the powdered sugar and water.
3. Crush about 1 kg (2 lb) pitted cherries before working them through a sieve.
4. Strain and freeze.

title: Kirsebær-Koldskaal (Cherry Cold Fruit Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Kg (2 1/2 S) Cherries
- 3/4 Kg (1 1/2 lb) Powdered Sugar
- 2 1/2 L (2 1/2 Pot) Water
- 1 whole Cinnamon stick
- Peel of 1/2 Lemon
- 1/2 Bottle Sherry

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from the cherries, which are given a boil with the sugar and poured into the tureen
2. Crush the pits in a mortar and place over the fire with 2 1/2 L (2 1/2 Pot) water, 1 whole cinnamon stick and peel of 1/2 lemon
3. This must boil for a few minutes and is then poured through a sieve into the tureen over the berries
4. Place the cold soup on ice and finish just before serving with 1/2 Bottle Sherry and, if necessary, with more sugar or water to taste
5. Serve with Vanilla Pastries

title: Kirsebær-Kompot (Cherry Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams (1 pund) cherries
- 250 grams (1/2 pund) sugar
- 375 milliliters (1/2 pægel) water
- Sago flour

## Directions

1. The berries can be cooked with or without pits.
2. Cook the berries in the sugar and water syrup, but not so long that they become tough.
3. Thicken the syrup with sago flour.

title: Kirsebær-Kompot Gule spanske (Spanish Yellow Cherry Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1.5 dl water for 0.5 kg fruit

## Directions

1. Cut off half of the stems.
2. Cook sugar and water to a thin syrup in which the cherries boil for about 5 minutes.
3. Fill them into sulfured jars.
4. Boil the juice for 8 to 10 minutes before pouring it over the berries.
5. The stones can, if desired, be removed from the cherries before preserving.
6. Seal as previously instructed.

title: Kirsebær-Likør (Cherry Liqueur)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kg (8 S) culinary cherries
- 4 l (4 pots) white rum
- 1 1/2 kg (3 S) ground icing sugar
- 1/4 l (3 pægles) water

## Directions

1. Pit and crush 4 kg (8 S) culinary cherries in a wooden or stone mortar.
2. Transfer the crushed cherries to a jug or jar and pour 4 l (4 pots) white rum over them, well corked or covered.
3. Over the course of a month, shake or stir regularly.
4. Strain the rum through a clear piece of cloth.
5. Boil 1 1/2 kg (3 S) ground icing sugar with 1/4 l (3 pægles) water to make a syrup and blend with the cherry rum.
6. Pour into bottles, cork them, and seal with gypsum.
7. Pour 2 l (2 pots) rum over the remaining solids and store in a well-corked jug for the next year. The rum will have extracted the remaining juice from the berries and should be measured and used the following year together with fresh rum to pour over freshly crushed berries.

title: Kirsebær-Suppe (Cherry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg cherries
- 4.5 L water
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- potato flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pound 1 kg of the cherries in a mortar until the pits are crushed
2. Heat in water and cook at least half an hour with whole cinnamon and lemon peel
3. Mix 2 tablespoons potato flour with water and thicken the soup when it boils
4. Remove pits from remaining cherries and cook in very little water with sugar
5. Mix with the strained soup and sweeten

title: Kirsebær-Suppe (Cherry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg dried cherries
- 4½ L water

## Directions

1. Crush the berries so that the pits are broken.
2. Prepare as Cherry Soup from fresh cherries, No. 425.

title: Kirsebær-Suppe (Cherry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound Cherries
- 1 Quart Water
- 1 Pint Water (second cooking)
- whole Cinnamon
- Cloves
- Sugar
- Bread dumplings or baked Bread

## Directions

1. Pit and cook the cherries with 1 quart water, strain, cook again with 1 pint and pour into the first cooked soup.
2. To make the soup clear, lay a thin cloth in the sieve and pour the soup through it, after which place it on the fire with whole cinnamon, cloves and sugar to bring to a good boil.
3. Serve with bread dumplings, which are added when the soup boils, or with baked bread.
4. A few of the berries can be removed and cooked in the strained soup.
5. If you want the soup smooth, stir a heaping spoonful of corn-starch dissolved in water or in some of the soup while cold, diluted with the hot and stirred in when the soup boils.
6. The starch must only boil a few minutes to not become stringy.

title: Kirsebærlikør (Cherry Liqueur)
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 pounds crushed preserved cherries
- 6½ pounds white sugar
- 40 quarts bitter almonds
- 3 quarts cinnamon
- 5 pails rum

## Directions

1. Let the crushed cherries, white sugar, bitter almonds, cinnamon, and rum stand well covered for one week
2. Strain and pour into bottles
3. Pour the sediment with 1 pail of boiling water and set aside to steep
4. Strain this as well; do not mix this liqueur with the first and do not store it long
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kirsebærsaft (Cherry Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 S) berries per batch
- 1/4 liter (1 Pægl) water per 1/2 kg berries

## Directions

1. Crush the berries in a wooden or stone mortar and place them in the pot together with the water.
2. Boil for a good quarter of an hour.
3. Sift the juice, pour into dry bottles, cork them, bind with sailcloth, wrap with hay, and place in the pot to boil for 5 minutes.
4. Plaster the bottles the day after.

title: Kirsebærsaft (Cherry Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 liter (1 pint) water
- 1/2 kg (1 pound) berries

## Directions

1. Crush the berries in a wooden or stone mortar
2. Place them in a pot together with the water and boil for a good quarter hour
3. Strain the juice, pour into dry bottles, and cork them
4. Bind with twine, wrap with hay, and place in a kettle to boil for 5 minutes
5. Seal the bottles with plaster the next day

title: Kirsebærsaft (Cherry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good, fully ripe preserved cherries
- 3 pounds of powdered sugar per pound of juice

## Directions

1. Crush the cherries in a mortar so that the pits are also crushed
2. Fill the berries into a pot and let them stand for a day
3. Filter the juice and cook it with 3 pounds of powdered sugar for each pound of juice for 1 hour, skimming it regularly
4. When it is cold, pour it into bottles and cork them
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kirsebærsaft (Cherry Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar or farina per 1 liter (1 pot) juice

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the berries, which are then crushed in a wooden or stone mortar; then press the juice through a fine sieve or pour through a jelly bag.
2. Measure the juice and boil with the sugar for 10 minutes.
3. Skim well during boiling, fill into dry bottles and tie immediately.
4. The refuse is boiled in water, and the juice is used in the household.

title: Kirsebærsuppe (Cherry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds black preserved cherries
- 3.5 pots water
- several sticks cinnamon
- a couple of lemon slices
- a little clove
- a little potato flour
- small bread balls
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. From 2 pounds of black preserved cherries, select a plateful of the best ones.
2. Crush the remainder so that the pits break apart; use more or fewer pits according to how much you want the kernel flavor to come through.
3. Put them over the fire with 3.5 pots of water, or enough so the water just covers the berries, and cook them until all the strength is extracted, which will take about a quarter hour.
4. Strain them, and put the strained soup back over the fire together with the cherries set aside the first time, several sticks of cinnamon, a couple of lemon slices without pits, and a little clove, and cook it; then thicken it with a little potato flour and top with small bread balls.
5. Before serving, sweeten to taste with sugar.
6. Alternatively, instead of bread balls, you can serve it with small rusks or baked wheat bread cubes.
7. Dried cherries can also be used for this soup.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kjernemelks-Suppe (Buttermilk Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- 2 large tablespoons corn starch
- currants
- whole cinnamon
- sliced almonds
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the corn starch in the milk while it is cold
2. Place it on the fire with currants, whole cinnamon, sliced almonds and sugar
3. Whisk it with a whisk or fork until it comes to a boil, which need only be very brief
4. It will curdle during cooking but will come together again

title: Kjoedpolse (Meat Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pounds beef
- 4 pounds chopped suet
- Salt
- Seasonings
- Strong pepper
- Ginger
- 1/2 quart milk

## Directions

1. Cut and mince the beef finely.
2. Cut the suet into fine cubes, season with ground pepper and a little ginger.
3. Mix everything well and work for a good half hour.
4. Add the milk (which should be boiled the day before) gradually while working the mixture for another half hour.
5. Season with salt to taste.
6. Stuff the sausage fairly firmly and salt it in a tub.

title: Kjoedpolse med Kartofler (Meat Sausage with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds finely minced meat
- 2.5 pounds bacon in fine cubes
- 5 pounds finely minced raw potatoes
- Salt
- Strong pepper
- Seasonings
- Oil

## Directions

1. Mince the meat finely like dough and the potatoes like grains.
2. Mix well and work in the bacon, pepper, salt, and oil.
3. Do not stuff the sausage firmly.
4. Salt and boil as in the previous recipe.

title: Kjærnemælkssuppe (Buttermilk Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pots buttermilk
- One-third pound rice flour
- A little lemon peel
- Whole cinnamon
- A little chopped almonds
- Half a pot rinsed raisins
- Sugar to taste
- Cold sweet cream for serving

## Directions

1. Mix a couple of pots of buttermilk with one-third pound of rice flour and place over heat together with the rest of the buttermilk, a little lemon peel, whole cinnamon, a little chopped almonds, and half a pot of rinsed raisins.
2. Stir continuously during cooking.
3. When ready, add sugar to taste.
4. At serving, cold sweet cream can also be added.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kjødbudding (Meat Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. beef, chopped
- 4.5 lbs. kidney fat
- a little marrow
- salt
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 2 to 3 eggs
- a considerable amount of onion
- a little sweet milk
- grated nutmeg
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Scrape the kidney fat and marrow free of sinews and chop very finely with a little salt.
2. Add 4 tablespoons flour, 2 to 3 eggs, and a considerable amount of onion, and chop together until quite fine and cohesive.
3. Stir well with a little sweet milk and a little grated nutmeg.
4. Grease a pudding mold with butter, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, and put the mixture in it to cook for 2 hours.
5. Serve with a strong sauce of meat broth with a little Madeira or caper sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kjødbudding (Meat Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef, chopped, 2 pounds
- Beef kidney fat (Nyretalg), 4.5 pounds
- Marrow, a little, scraped free of sinews
- Salt
- Fine flour (Flormel), 4 tablespoons
- Eggs, 2 to 3
- Onion, fairly abundant
- Sweet milk, a little
- Nutmeg, grated, a little
- Bread, crushed, for coating the mold
- Butter for the mold

## Directions

1. Scrape the beef and kidney fat, along with a little marrow, free of sinews and chop very finely with a little salt.
2. Then add 4 tablespoons of fine flour, 2 to 3 eggs, and fairly abundant onion, chopping it all together until it is quite fine and cohesive.
3. After this, stir it well with a little sweet milk and a little grated nutmeg.
4. Butter a pudding mold, sprinkle it with crushed bread, put the mixture in it, and cook for 2 hours.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kjødpølser (Meat Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- Scrape meat
- Kidney fat
- Fat
- Salt
- Ground nutmeg
- Ground white pepper
- Onion
- Cloves
- Ginger
- Intestines

## Directions

1. The filling can be made from various kinds of meat but must be softer than the filling for meatballs and should stand for one day.
2. From the waste of the scrape meat, cook a soup with onion, cloves and a piece of ginger.
3. Knead the scrape meat with finely chopped kidney fat and fat along with salt, ground nutmeg and white ground pepper until it becomes a tough mass.
4. Dilute and knead it with the lukewarm soup, from which the fat has been skimmed.
5. When the filling is ready, it should be quite smooth, after which it is filled into intestines and stuffed fairly loosely.
6. During cooking, the sausages must be pricked with a needle so they do not burst.
7. The sausages can be preserved for some time if they are simmered in strong soup with melted fat on top.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kjødsalat (Meat Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Diced cooked chicken meat
- Celery root
- Finely diced ham or ox tongue
- 10 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1 pint good, sweet cream
- 16 anchovies
- 1 small shallot
- 1 good teaspoon strong mustard
- Capers
- French vinegar

## Directions

1. Combine diced chicken meat, celery root and finely diced ham or ox tongue
2. Mash 10 hard-boiled egg yolks and mix with 1 pint good, sweet cream
3. Remove bones from 16 anchovies and finely chop them together with 1 small shallot, 1 good teaspoon strong mustard, capers and French vinegar to taste
4. Mix all together and strain through a sieve
5. Place the meat in the sauce and let it sit for a couple of hours before serving
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kjødsuppe med Boller (Meat Soup with Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 pounds soup meat
- 4 pounds simpler beef, such as shank
- 12 pots cold water
- salt
- 1 soup bunch
- cleaned whole soup vegetables
- 10 whole peppercorns
- a small piece of ginger
- a small piece of candy sugar
- dumplings

## Directions

1. For 10 pounds of soup meat, take 4 pounds of simpler beef, such as shank; after washing, chop it into pieces and place it over heat with 12 pots of cold water.
2. When this soup has been brought to boil over slow heat, skim it, and add a little salt, then boil the soup evenly for one hour.
3. Only then add the proper soup meat together with a soup bunch and some cleaned whole soup vegetables, 10 whole peppercorns and a small piece of ginger, then cook the soup evenly again until the vegetables are tender.
4. Remove these vegetables, cut them up and place them in the tureen.
5. When the meat is tender, remove it and keep it warm until serving.
6. Strain the soup and let it rest quietly for a quarter hour, then skim off the fat.
7. Now pour the soup carefully back into the pot so that no sediment comes with it.
8. Add a small piece of candy to the soup and, if necessary, salt it.
9. Add the dumplings, give it a good boil, and when the dumplings are tender, pour it into the tureen over the vegetables.
10. The portions mentioned here will be sufficient for 12 persons.
11. The soup meat is sprinkled with a little salt and can be served as a second course with horseradish sauce.
12. If soup is left over, the vegetables must be strained out, as it will easily turn sour during storage.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kjørvelsuppe (Chervil Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 til 4 Haandfulde Kjørvel (chervil)
- 5 Potter Bouillon (beef or lamb broth)
- Et Fjerdingspund Smør (butter)
- Rengjorte Asparges (cleaned asparagus)
- Nogle smaa kogte Gulerødder (small cooked carrots)
- Forlorne Æg (poached eggs)
- Ristede Skiver af Franskbrød (toasted French bread slices)

## Directions

1. Rinse and finely chop 3 to 4 handfuls of chervil.
2. Heat 5 pots of bouillon (made from veal or lamb meat) and skim it.
3. Melt a quarter pound of butter in a saucepan and brown it for about a quarter hour.
4. Gradually thin the roux with the bouillon.
5. Cut cleaned asparagus into one-inch pieces, boil in water, and add to the soup along with some of the cooking water.
6. Add small cooked carrots and the chopped chervil to the soup.
7. Bring the soup to a boil and serve with poached eggs and toasted slices of French bread.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kjørvelsuppe (Chervil Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 handfuls chervil
- 5 pots bouillon made from veal or lamb meat
- 1/4 pound butter

## Directions

1. Clean and finely chop 3 to 4 handfuls of chervil.
2. Place 5 pots of bouillon, which can be made from veal or lamb meat, over heat and skim it.
3. Place 1/4 pound butter in a casserole and bake for approximately a quarter hour, then gradually thin the roux.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Klap Torske-Suppe (Clear Cod Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 L (2 pots) fish broth
- 2 L (2 pots) clear meat broth
- 4 to 5 carrots
- 1 celery
- 4 leeks
- a good bunch
- finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Mix the broths together and cook with 4 to 5 carrots, 1 celery, 4 leeks and a good bunch.
2. When the vegetables are tender, remove them, cut them up and add them to the tureen, over which the broth is poured.
3. Finely chopped parsley can be added to this.

title: Kleiner (Fried Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Egg Yolks
- 2 whole Eggs
- half a Pound white Sugar or dried and crushed Powdered Sugar
- 2 Tablespoons Cream
- 1 Tablespoon Butter (clarified)
- 1 Pound Flour

## Directions

1. Whip 6 Egg Yolks, 2 whole Eggs, and half a Pound white Sugar or dried and crushed Powdered Sugar well and stir with 2 Tablespoons Cream and 1 Tablespoon clarified Butter
2. Add as much of 1 Pound Flour as you can incorporate; work the rest in on the board until the batter releases from the board and hands
3. Roll out very thin with Flour on the outside and cut into oblong pieces, best with a pastry wheel, so the cakes become notched at the edge
4. Cut a slit in the middle of each piece and push one end of the cake through it, so that it becomes twisted in the middle
5. Fry very light

title: Klejner (Danish Fried Pastry Strips)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites
- 4 pounds powdered sugar
- 7 quintal butter
- zest of 1 lemon
- 1 pound flour

## Directions

1. Mix together egg yolks, egg whites, powdered sugar, butter, and lemon zest with a wooden spoon
2. When well mixed, stir in the flour until a smooth dough forms
3. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a five-øre coin
4. Cut strips 2 fingers wide and 4 inches long using a pastry wheel
5. Cut a slit in the center of each strip and pull one end through
6. Fry in melted butter or fat until golden brown
7. Turn during frying, remove and drain on paper so the butter runs off
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Klejner (Fried Pastry Twists)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites
- 4 pounds powdered sugar
- 7 quintal butter
- zest of one lemon, grated

## Directions

1. Stir together the egg yolks, egg whites, powdered sugar, butter, and grated lemon zest with a wooden spoon, and when it is well stirred...
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Klejner 1 (Fried Pastry Twists 1)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 150 grams (30 quintal) icing sugar
- 150 grams (30 quintal) lukewarm margarine
- ground cardamom
- zest of one lemon, chopped or grated
- 3 tablespoons raw cream
- approximately 1/2 kg (1 lb) flour
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) Palmin for frying

## Directions

1. Mix the eggs well with icing sugar
2. Add lukewarm margarine, cardamom and lemon zest
3. When this is mixed into a tough dough, add 3 tablespoons raw cream and approximately 1/2 kg flour
4. Roll the dough very thinly
5. Cut with a cake cutter into long cakes
6. Make a slit in the middle of each cake with a cake cutter and draw one end of the cake through the slit, tying it into a knot in the middle
7. Fry this portion in 1/4 kg Palmin in a frying pan

title: Klejner 2 (Fried Pastry Twists 2)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) icing sugar
- 125 grams (1/4 lb) lukewarm margarine
- a couple tablespoons cream
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) flour
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) Palmin for frying

## Directions

1. Mix eggs with icing sugar and lukewarm margarine
2. Add a couple tablespoons cream and 1/2 kg flour, mix with hands
3. Roll out, form and fry as described above

title: Klidkager (Bran Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds coarse rye bran
- a few spoonfuls gluten flour (if available)
- 1 pound butter
- 4 eggs
- 2 teaspoons sodium bicarbonate
- 5 quints pressed yeast
- a handful salt
- a handful caraway seeds
- 5.5 measures boiled, cooled water
- 0.5 measure vinegar

## Directions

1. The evening before the cakes are to be baked, work together 4 pounds coarse rye bran, a few spoonfuls gluten flour if available, 1 pound butter, 4 eggs, 2 teaspoons sodium bicarbonate, 5 quints pressed yeast, a handful salt, a handful caraway seeds, 5.5 measures boiled, cooled water and 0.5 measure vinegar into a dough.
2. Before baking, press the dough out on a pan with the hand to the thickness of a biscuit, roll thin and smooth with a bottle and cut into round or square small cakes before the pan is placed in the oven.
3. When the dough has dried a little on the pan, loosen all the cakes with a thin knife and later turn them so they can also dry on the side facing the pan.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Klipfisk (Dried Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- Klipfisk (dried cod)
- Water
- Soda
- Salt
- Boiled peeled potatoes
- Mustard sauce or melted butter
- Hard-boiled eggs, chopped

## Directions

1. The day before use, trim the fins and cut the fish into suitable pieces
2. Soak in cold water with a small piece of soda for 16 to 24 hours
3. The next day, scrape the scales without removing the skin
4. Place in fresh cold water again
5. Cook slowly for about 1 hour, placing in fresh cold water before cooking
6. When cooked, remove and arrange with skin side up
7. Serve with boiled peeled potatoes and mustard sauce or melted butter with chopped hard-boiled eggs
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Klipfisk (Dried Salted Cod)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Klipfisk (Dried salted cod)
- Natron
- lunkent Vand (Lukewarm water)
- Smør (Butter)
- haardkogte Æg (Hard-boiled eggs)
- Persille (Parsley)
- smeltet Smør (Melted butter)
- Sennep-Sauce (Mustard sauce)
- kogte Kartofler (Boiled potatoes)

## Directions

1. Cut the dried salted cod in half.
2. Soak for 1 day in lukewarm water with a small amount of sodium bicarbonate.
3. If very salty, change the water several times.
4. Before cooking, scrape the grey skin well and remove the fins.
5. Place in cold water over heat and cook for about half an hour, skumming well.
6. Arrange on a platter with skin side up, garnished with sliced hard-boiled eggs and decorated with parsley.
7. Serve with melted butter and mustard sauce (No. 789) along with boiled potatoes.
8. The eggs can also be chopped and served separately with the fish.

title: Klipfisk (Salt Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt cod
- Soda
- Cold water
- Salt
- Butter
- Mustard
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut off the fins from the fish, which is cut into pieces and placed in water in the evening before it is to be used.
2. In the water is placed a small piece of soda, which is completely harmless and causes the fish to be cooked faster and whiter in appearance.
3. The next day scrape it on both sides so the scales, not the skin, come off, and give it fresh water, in which it stands until it is to be cooked; then place it on the heat in cold and plenty of water without salt, skim and cook until it is tender, usually 2 or 3 hours.
4. It must not remain in the water after it is cooked, but place it immediately on the plate with the skin upward.
5. Serve with butter and mustard, and hard-boiled chopped eggs, which are added to the sauce at the table, and boiled potatoes.

title: Klipfisk-Budding (Dried Cod Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried salted cod
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 rusks
- Flour
- Milk
- 0.5 pound melted butter
- Salt
- 1 cup cleaned currants
- Egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Soak the dried cod in water the evening before or at least in the morning.
2. Boil it and chop it fine; for each deep plate full of chopped fish use 8 egg yolks.
3. Soak 8 rusks in flour, let stand covered for some time, and then rub them together with the egg yolks and enough milk to make a consistency like ordinary pancake batter.
4. Add 0.5 pound of melted butter (reserve the salt), 1 cup of well-cleaned currants, then the fish, and lastly the stiffly beaten egg whites.
5. Immediately pour into the buttered form and boil for 2 hours.

title: Klipfiske-Budding (Salt Cod Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.25 kilogram flour
- 0.25 kilogram margarine
- 375 grams finely chopped, cooked salt cod
- just under 0.75 liter boiling milk
- 10 egg yolks
- salt
- ground white pepper
- 125 grams plucked and scalded raisins
- egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. 0.25 kilogram of flour is baked in 0.25 kilogram of margarine together with 375 grams of finely chopped, cooked salt cod and diluted with just under 0.75 liter of boiling milk.
2. When the batter is slightly cooled, it is stirred with 10 egg yolks, salt, ground white pepper, 125 grams of plucked and scalded raisins, and finally with the beaten egg whites.
3. The pudding is eaten with melted or creamed butter.
4. Salt cod pudding can also be made as bread pudding, with the fish stirred into it.

title: Klipfiske-Gratin (Dried Fish Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Kogt Klipfisk
- Smør
- Mel
- Kogende Mælk
- Æg
- Salt
- Melis

## Directions

1. Se Opskrift på Fiske-Gratin Nr. 67.
2. Den kogte Klipfisk hakkes og røres i Dejgen.
3. Smeltet eller rørt Smør. Tomat-Sauce kan også passe hertil.

title: Klipfiskebudding (Salt Cod Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 37 Quintal Flour
- 37 Quintal Butter
- 3 Pails sweet Milk
- 153 Egg yolks
- Three-Quarter Pound cooked, boned and chopped Salt Cod
- Nutmeg to taste
- Salt and Pepper to taste
- Cleaned Raisins to taste

## Directions

1. Bake and stir together the flour, butter and sweet milk with 153 egg yolks, one at a time.
2. Add three-quarter pound of cooked, boned and chopped salt cod.
3. Finally add the beaten egg whites.
4. Add nutmeg, salt, pepper and raisins to taste.
5. Cook the pudding for a couple of hours in a well-greased mold.
6. Serve with melted butter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Klipfiſk Budding (Salt Cod Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt cod, soaked and cooked
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 tablespoons melted butter, dissolved in flour
- Milk
- 1/2 pound melted butter
- 1 cup cleaned currants
- Egg whites, stiffly beaten
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the salt cod the evening before use, or at least in the morning
2. Cook the cod and chop it finely
3. For a deep plate full of chopped fish, use 8 egg yolks
4. Dissolve 8 tablespoons in flour, let it sit covered for some time, then mix with the egg yolks and enough milk to achieve a consistency like ordinary pancake batter
5. Add 1/2 pound melted butter (salt held back)
6. Add 1 cup of well-cleaned currants
7. Add the fish and finally the stiffly beaten egg whites
8. Pour immediately into a buttered mold and cook for 2 hours
9. Alternatively, bake in the oven in an earthenware dish, ready in 3 quarters of an hour
10. Serve with melted or stirred butter with pepper

title: Klipfiſk Budding med Kartofler (Salt Cod Pudding with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cooked, chopped salt cod
- 3 tablespoons cooked, grated potatoes
- 2 tablespoons butter
- A little ground nutmeg
- 3 whole eggs
- 1 gill cream
- A couple spoonfuls chopped raisins
- A small teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix the salt cod and potatoes thoroughly with butter and nutmeg
2. Stir in 3 whole eggs, cream, and a couple spoonfuls of chopped raisins
3. Finally add a small teaspoon of baking powder
4. Pour the batter into a buttered earthenware dish and place immediately in the oven
5. Serve with stirred or melted butter

title: Kniplingskage (Lace Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg whites
- 12 egg yolks
- 1 pail good cream
- a little cardamom
- 3 quarter-pounds powdered sugar
- 1 pound flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Whisk the egg whites into a foam
2. Add the egg yolks together with the cream, a little cardamom, powdered sugar, and flour
3. Bring butter to boiling in a small saucepan
4. Pour a spoonful of the batter through a funnel held high over the butter, moving it back and forth in circles
5. Fry the cake and when golden brown, turn it carefully
6. Remove and drain the butter off
7. Arrange on a plate
8. Sprinkle with powdered sugar before serving
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Knudekaal (Kohlrabi)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Kohlrabi
- Water
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Peel the kohlrabi thickly, removing all the woody parts as these are inedible, indigestible, and cannot be made tender.
2. Cut lengthwise like asparagus.
3. Tie in bundles and place over heat in boiling water with salt.
4. The kohlrabi must boil for at least 1 hour.
5. Arrange and serve like asparagus with stirred butter.

title: Knudekaal, revet (Grated Kohlrabi)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked kohlrabi
- Milk or cream sauce
- Salt
- Sugar
- Butter

## Directions

1. Peel the whole cooked kohlrabi thickly and grate it.
2. Toss gently in a good milk or cream sauce that has been seasoned with salt, a little sugar, and a small slice of cold butter before the kohlrabi is stirred in.

title: Knudekaal, stuvet (Stewed Kohlrabi)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Kohlrabi
- Milk
- Butter
- Flour
- Parsley
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut peeled kohlrabi into elongated strips.
2. Stew on the same manner as asparagus.
3. Add a little finely chopped parsley to the milk sauce.
4. Serve mainly with salted meat dishes.

title: Knudekaal-Suppe (Kale Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg (3 S) kale
- 4 L (4 pots) broth
- 4 egg yolks
- cooked, cut kale

## Directions

1. The cut kale is cooked so tender that it is easily rubbed through a sieve (at least 1.5 hours), poured back into the pot, brought to a boil and bound with 4 egg yolks.
2. Cooked, cut kale can be placed in the tureen.

title: Knudekaals-Gratin (Kohlrabi Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Knudekaalen
- Smør
- Mel
- Kogende Mælk
- Æg
- Salt
- Melis

## Directions

1. Knudekaalen må være mørkоgt og skaaret i mindre Stykker som Asparges, når den uden Væde ved lægges i Gratin-Dejgen (se Nr. 62).
2. Rørt eller smeltet Smør til.

title: Knækbrød (Crisp Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg flour
- 1/4 kg margarine
- 1 good teaspoon baking powder
- 4 to 5 tablespoons water
- 3 eggs
- a little salt
- a small amount of sugar
- a handful of caraway seed

## Directions

1. Crumble the margarine and baking powder into the flour
2. Mix in the water, eggs, salt, sugar, and caraway seed
3. When the dough releases from the hands, roll it out very thinly
4. Prick with a fork
5. Bake for a quarter hour
6. The bread can be baked in one large sheet and broken into smaller pieces when baked, or cut into small squares before baking
7. Serve spread with butter

title: Kogt Aborre (Boiled Perch)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Perch
- Salt
- Parsley
- Boiled potatoes
- Melted butter
- Grated horseradish
- Parsley sauce

## Directions

1. Scrape off the scales using a grater, as they adhere firmly
2. Clean the fish and cut off the fins, and after rinsing well, sprinkle it with salt until it is cooked
3. Place in cold water with salt and a bunch of parsley
4. Cook until the fins begin to loosen
5. Serve with boiled potatoes and melted butter with grated horseradish or parsley sauce made from the cooking liquid

title: Kogt Faare- eller Lamme-Kjöd med Capersauce (Boiled Mutton or Lamb with Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt
- A lamb shank with clear, white and hard fat
- Milk
- Flour for thickening
- Butter
- Salt and pepper
- Capers

## Directions

1. Put a handful of salt in a roomy pot with boiling water.
2. Choose a lamb shank with clear, white and hard fat, wash it and rub it thoroughly with salt.
3. Boil for three hours or more, according to the size of the shank.
4. Make a sauce from a pint of boiling milk, thickened with flour; add butter, salt, pepper and 2 spoonfuls of capers, and serve the meat with it.

title: Kogt Farseret Hvidkaal (Boiled Stuffed White Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 very large firm cabbage head
- 2.5 kg prepared forcemeat (serves 12 people)
- salt
- water
- melted butter or sauce made from butter, flour, and broth

## Directions

1. Remove the outer loose leaves from a good firm cabbage head
2. Cut a lid around the stem large enough to hollow out the head
3. Fill the forcemeat inside, leaving room for it to rise
4. Place the lid on and cover with a few of the removed leaves
5. Bind the head with twine and place in a pot with plenty of boiling salted water
6. Boil for 3 to 3.5 hours
7. When serving, remove the lid and place the head with forcemeat facing down on the platter
8. Serve with melted butter or sauce made from butter, flour, and the broth
9. The hollowed cabbage can be cooked with the head and arranged around it, or prepared together with cabbage broth into a brown cabbage soup with the addition of Maggi bouillon cubes

title: Kogt farseret Hvidkaal (Cooked Stuffed White Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 very large cabbage head
- 2 1/2 kg (3 lb) prepared ground meat
- Sailcloth
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the outer loose leaves from a good firm cabbage head
2. Cut out a lid around the stalk large enough to hollow out the head
3. Fill with ground meat, taking care to leave room for it to rise
4. Place the lid on and cover with a couple of the removed leaves
5. Tie the head with sailcloth
6. Place in a pot with abundant boiling salted water and cook for 3 to 3 1/2 hours
7. Remove the lid at serving time

title: Kogt fersk Sild 1 (Boiled Fresh Herring 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 to 8 herrings
- 8 ounces salt

## Directions

1. Place 3 quarts water over the heat with the salt. When it boils, add the herrings and simmer for a quarter hour, then let them drain.
2. They should be served and eaten immediately, otherwise they become heavy and difficult to digest.

title: Kogt fersk Sild 2 (Boiled Fresh Herring 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Herring
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolks
- Cream
- Vinegar
- Mustard
- Salt
- Allspice
- Bay leaves

## Directions

1. Clean the herring, rinse, drain and lay tightly in a casserole with the above spices except mustard. Add a little water and a spoonful of vinegar, and cook the herring covered.
2. Brown butter and flour, whisk in a little vinegar and mustard. Smooth the sauce with egg yolk and cream and serve with the herring.

title: Kogt Fisk med Östersæuce (Boiled Fish with Oyster Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds cod
- salt
- peppercorns
- 1 pint oysters

## Directions

1. Clean 2 pounds cod and boil according to directions in cold water
2. As soon as the fish is done, lift the pot from the fire and set it back
3. Meanwhile set 1 pint oysters over the fire to cook in their own sauce
4. When the oysters are cooked, strain them

title: Kogt Fugl med Østers-Sauce (Boiled Fowl with Oyster Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fowl
- Boiling water
- One quart of oysters
- One ounce butter
- One ounce flour
- Oyster sauce
- Broth from the fowl
- Salt
- White pepper
- Grated nutmeg
- Finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the fowl but do not stuff.
2. Place in enough boiling water to cover the fowl.
3. Simmer carefully until the fowl is cooked.
4. When nearly done, prepare the oyster sauce: bring one quart of oysters to boil in their own liquid.
5. Strain the oysters; reserve the liquid and wash the oysters in cold water.
6. Mix one ounce of butter and one ounce of flour together over heat until they form a white paste, being careful not to brown.
7. Stir in enough of the oyster sauce and enough broth from the fowl to make the sauce the consistency of melted butter.
8. Season with salt, white pepper, and grated nutmeg.
9. Add the oysters and pour over the fowl.
10. Sprinkle finely chopped parsley over all.
11. This dish can also be made with hens and chickens.

title: Kogt Gedde (Boiled Pike)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ to 3 kg pike
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Green salad or parsley
- Boiled potatoes
- Melted butter
- Grated horseradish
- Cream and grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Clean the pike by removing the gills and entrails, and rinse well inside
2. The scales should remain on the fish; rub them well with vinegar and salt
3. When cooking, tie the head and tail together with a piece of twine passed through with a needle, so the fish lies curved when served
4. Place in cold water and cook until the fins begin to loosen
5. Serve on a platter with a fish rack and a folded napkin underneath
6. Remove the band and place either green salad or parsley in the mouth of the fish
7. Serve with boiled potatoes and sauce of melted butter and grated horseradish, or cream and grated horseradish

title: Kogt Hummer (Boiled Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Set a pot over the fire with plenty of water and salt.
2. When the water is boiling, add the live lobsters with their claws tied together, after rinsing them in cold water.
3. Boil over strong heat until the legs can be removed. Do not cook longer or the meat will become stringy.
4. Skim the water during cooking.
5. Remove the pot from the fire and let the lobsters cool until half-cold.
6. Remove the lobsters and crack the back and tail lengthwise and the claws crosswise.
7. Serve cold with chopped parsley, pepper, vinegar and fine oil, along with fine green salad leaves.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kogt Hummer (Boiled Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster
- Cold water

## Directions

1. Place the lobster in cold water and bring to a boil.
2. The lobster suffers less this way as it dies as soon as the water becomes warm, and its meat will be more tender.
3. Cook for 15 to 30 minutes depending on its size; very large ones may need an hour.
4. When cooked, remove it and split lengthwise from head to tail.
5. Remove the stomach (which follows right after the head) and the dark or bluish vein that runs from near the head to the end of the tail.

title: Kogt Kalvekød i Ragout (Cooked Veal in Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- White mushroom sauce (see Nr. 775)
- Egg yolks
- White wine or sherry
- Cooked veal, cut into pieces
- Puff pastry strips

## Directions

1. Make a white mushroom sauce bound with egg yolks and seasoned with a little white wine or sherry
2. Add the cut cooked meat
3. Garnish with puff pastry strips when serving
4. Tomato sauce (see Nr. 790) and rice border can be used instead of mushrooms

title: Kogt Laks (Boiled Salmon)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg salmon per 3 persons
- Salt
- Cold water
- Whole peppercorns
- Bay leaves

## Directions

1. Before cleaning, place the salmon in cold water. Scrape it with a knife from the head toward the tail without loosening the scales.
2. Make a small crosswise cut at the vent fin nearest the tail, and remove the entrails from the opening near the head without opening the belly.
3. Rinse the fish thoroughly in several changes of cold water and clean the inside well.
4. Sprinkle with salt and let stand for a couple of hours before cooking.
5. The salmon is best when cooked whole, but may also be cut into round slices about an inch thick, which are reassembled into the original form when served. Alternatively, the fish may be split while raw by following the backbone from the tail through the head with a sharp knife for a beautiful presentation on two platters.
6. For cooking time, allow 5 to 6 minutes per inch of thickness.
7. If cooking whole without a fish kettle, use a fish rack or lay a coarse cloth in the bottom of the pot large enough to hang over the sides. Place a plate on the cloth and lay the fish on it for easy removal when plated.
8. For whole fish, place in enough cold water to just cover. For cut fish, place in boiling water with vinegar (2½ dl or 1 pint per 5 to 6 liters of water), whole peppercorns, bay leaves, and salt.
9. Keep the cooking at an even temperature, skim well during cooking, and let the fish rest in the water for a few minutes before serving.
10. Serve on a warm platter lined with a fish rack and a folded napkin. Garnish the head and tail with parsley and lemon slices or boiled crayfish. The platter may be further garnished with croutons filled with a ragout of shrimp, crayfish, lobster, or oysters, or the ragout may be served in gratinée shells arranged around the edge of the platter.

title: Kogt Lammebryst (Boiled Lamb Breast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ Kg (5 S) lamb breast
- Salt
- Broth bundle

## Directions

1. From a lamb forequarter, remove the brisket, cut off the chops, and break and remove the breastbones along with the brisket in smaller pieces.
2. Wash the pieces well and place them over heat in cold water with salt and a broth bundle.
3. Skim well and cook tender in the course of about an hour.
4. Boiled lamb meat, especially the leg, is excellent served with cream horseradish sauce (see Nr. 798).

title: Kogt Løg-Sauce (Cooked Onion Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1 raw egg yolk
- 1 tablespoon oil
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chopped parsley
- Mustard (stirred smooth)
- Cooked, chopped, or raw grated onions
- Strong broth

## Directions

1. Press the hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve
2. Stir with a raw egg yolk
3. Add oil, then vinegar
4. Season with salt, pepper, and chopped parsley
5. Add stirred smooth mustard
6. Add cooked, chopped, or raw grated onions
7. Add strong broth until the sauce reaches the desired consistency

title: Kogt Makrel (Boiled Mackerel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mackerel
- Salt
- Bay leaves
- Peppercorns
- Parsley
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Ensure the mackerel is firm and stiff when purchasing
2. Clean as you would salmon
3. Keep the head on
4. Boil the mackerel whole in cold water with salt, bay leaves and whole peppercorns for a quarter of an hour
5. Remove from water immediately once cooked
6. Arrange on a platter
7. Garnish with parsley and cooked potatoes

title: Kogt Oxetunge (Boiled Beef Tongue - alternate method)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- salt
- water
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. Rinse the tongue well and boil it in salted water
2. Skim the stock frequently during cooking
3. When fully cooked, remove and allow to cool
4. Strain the stock and remove the skin from the tongue, cutting away the lower part
5. Return to heat and bring briefly to a boil
6. Serve either whole or sliced with sharp brown sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kogt Oxetunge (Boiled Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. After rinsing well, boil the tongue in salted water
2. Skim the stock frequently
3. When the tongue is fully cooked, remove it and set aside to cool
4. Strain the stock, and remove the skin from the tongue and cut away the lower part
5. Return to heat and bring to a boil once more
6. Serve either whole or sliced with sharp brown sauce
7. Salted beef tongue is treated similarly but served with cooked vegetables without sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kogt Oxetunge (Boiled Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fresh beef tongue
- 1/3 ounce salt
- Pepper
- 2 cloves
- 4 ounces onion

## Directions

1. Soak the tongue in water for one hour.
2. Boil for 3 hours with 3 quarts of water and the seasonings.
3. When well cooked, remove the skin.
4. Serve with tomato sauce.

title: Kogt Oxetunge med Oliven (Boiled Beef Tongue with Olives)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- olives
- strained stock from cooking the tongue
- bouillon or gravy

## Directions

1. After cooking the tongue as described in the previous recipe, cut it into slices
2. Peel the olives and cut them into long strips
3. Insert these strips into one side of each tongue slice
4. Lay the slices close together with the olive-studded side upward and pour over some of the strained stock in which the tongue was cooked
5. Cook until done over gentle heat
6. Prepare an olive sauce (see recipe 252) and arrange the tongue slices in a ring around the edge of the dish, pouring the sauce in the center
7. When the olives are peeled, boil the interior in bouillon or gravy, strain and add to the olive sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land
