title: Kogt Pighvarre (Boiled Turbot)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Turbot
- Salt
- Bay leaves
- Parsley or green salad
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean by removing entrails through an incision near the head, keeping the head on
2. Brush in salt water to clean the dark bumpy skin on top
3. Scrape the white underside until clear and free of slime
4. Polish the fins
5. Wash in several changes of cold water
6. Sprinkle with salt
7. Cook in a fish kettle on a rack or on a platter, starting in cold salted water
8. Once it comes to a boil, reduce heat
9. After about half an hour of cooking, when the fins begin to loosen, the fish is done
10. Arrange on a platter with a porcelain rack under the folded napkin
11. Garnish at the head and tail with a bouquet of parsley or green salad
12. Place small boiled potatoes on the sides

title: Kogt Sild (Boiled Herring)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- laurel leaves
- whole pepper
- boiled potatoes
- parsley sauce or caper sauce or melted butter sauce with vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean herring by removing gills and entrails
2. Cut off head and some of tail fin
3. Scrape well to remove scales
4. Keep roe and milt in the herring
5. Rinse well and sprinkle with salt
6. Place over heat in boiling water with vinegar, salt, laurel leaves and whole pepper
7. Serve with boiled potatoes and parsley, caper, or melted butter sauce with vinegar

title: Kogt Skinke (Boiled Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked or green-salted ham
- water
- salt
- ground hardtack
- green cabbage

## Directions

1. Soak the smoked or green-salted ham 24 hours in cold water, or if very salty, in lukewarm water.
2. After scraping and rinsing well, boil in abundant water for 2.5 to 5 hours depending on its size.
3. When tender, remove it; take off the skin except at the shank where a small strip is kept.
4. Sprinkle the ham with ground hardtack and place in a hot oven for a quarter hour.
5. After the meal, cover the remaining part of the ham with the removed skin.
6. Serve with green cabbage.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kogt Torsk (Boiled Cod)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cod (1/4 kg per person)
- salt
- vinegar
- parsley

## Directions

1. Large cod should be cooked whole and placed in cold water with salt and a little vinegar to make the flesh firmer, while smaller cod are cut in half and placed in boiling water.
2. When the cod is slaughtered, make a cut at the nape and one at the tail, then hold it under running water, letting the water stream rinse it well, which causes the muscles to contract and the fish to become firmer.
3. Open the belly, remove the innards, and cook the liver, roe, or milt together with the fish. Remove the gall bladder carefully.
4. Scrape the fish from the tail upward, clean it well inside, and after sufficient rinsing, make 2 or 3 cuts on each side of the skin so it does not burst during cooking.
5. Let it stand with salt for a couple of hours before cooking, which must take place without a lid, otherwise the fish becomes loose and falls apart.
6. When plating, place a fish rack on the platter and over it a folded napkin, upon which the cod is laid.
7. Garnish at the head and tail with liver, roe, or sliced milt, between which parsley is inserted.
8. Place boiled potatoes on the sides of the cod.

title: Kogt, Farseret Kalvebryst (Boiled Stuffed Veal Breast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 kg veal breast
- Forcemeat of veal or pork
- Salt
- Parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Remove the bones with a sharp knife.
2. Spread a layer of forcemeat (which should not be beaten too fine) over the meat.
3. Roll the breast tightly together and sew it closed to prevent the forcemeat from escaping during cooking.
4. Place in boiling salted water and cook for 1.5 to 2 hours.
5. Remove the thread and slice the meat, garnishing with parsley.
6. Use the cooking liquid partly for sauce and partly for a light soup that does not require much strength.

title: Kogte Høns med Ris, Karri eller Champignons (Boiled Hens with Rice, Curry, or Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- hen
- water
- salt
- celery root
- parsley roots
- flour
- butter
- rice
- curry

## Directions

1. Cut the hen into pieces and wash in lukewarm water.
2. Boil until tender in water and salt.
3. Make a sauce from the strained cooking broth with celery root and parsley roots.
4. Thicken the sauce with flour and butter.
5. Clean half a pound of rice and cook slowly over steady heat with constant stirring.
6. Place the chicken in the sauce and arrange with a ring of cooked rice around the edge of the platter.
7. If desired, add a little curry to the sauce.
8. Alternatively, arrange the boiled hen in a mushroom sauce (see recipe 240).
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kogte Jordartiſkokker (Boiled Jerusalem Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- Salt
- Water
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Clean the artichokes thoroughly in water and peel them
2. Shape them like small pears and cook in water with a little salt
3. Drain on a sieve and arrange with melted butter over them

title: Kogte Karper (Boiled Carp)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ to 2 kg carp per portion
- Salt
- Whole peppercorns
- Bay leaves
- Parsley
- Vinegar (3¾ dl or 1½ pint per 2 to 3 carp)
- Water

## Directions

1. Clean the carp, scraping the scales lightly and leaving them on. The fish may be cooked whole or split, with bones remaining on one half.
2. After thorough rinsing, sprinkle the carp with salt and let stand for one hour.
3. Place in a casserole and pour over boiling vinegar (3¾ dl or 1½ pint per 2 to 3 carp). Turn the fish regularly and let stand for a good quarter hour.
4. Add whole peppercorns, a couple of bay leaves, a bunch of parsley, and enough water to cook the fish.
5. Bring to a rapid boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer for 3 quarters of an hour to 1 hour, depending on the size of the fish.
6. Serve with cold cream and horseradish sauce, melted butter, and boiled potatoes. Garnish with boiled crayfish, lemon slices, and parsley.

title: Kogte Kartofler (Boiled Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Water
- Salt
- Vinegar (1 teaspoon per 6 potatoes for winter potatoes)

## Directions

1. For early new potatoes: wash well and dry in a rough cloth. Place over heat in hot but not boiling salted water. They require very little cooking.
2. For winter potatoes: peel and leave in cold water for several hours before cooking. Place in an enameled pot in cold water with salt and vinegar, which will make them white and attractive.
3. Once cooked, drain the water and place over low heat, shaking them dry until water is completely evaporated.
4. If cooked ahead of serving, drain water and immediately cover with a cloth to keep warm.
5. Serve with onion sauce (recipe 796) and with matjes herring, salt herring, spiced herring, or smoked ham.

title: Kogte Krebs (Boiled Crayfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Wash the crayfish and cook them like lobster in boiling salted water.
2. If cooking a large quantity at once, heat an iron implement (such as fire tongs) until red-hot and stir it in the boiling water as soon as the crayfish are added, to prevent the temperature from dropping too much.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kogte Kronartiſkokker (Boiled Globe Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Globe artichokes
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the lower leaves as they cannot be eaten and clip the remaining ones half off
2. Place the artichokes inverted in a pot and add just enough boiling water to reach one-third of the way up
3. Cover with a lid and place over moderate strong heat so sufficient steam develops to cook the artichokes better and preserve their flavor
4. When done, arrange quickly with fresh butter or another suitable sauce

title: Kogte Radiser (Cooked Radishes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Long English radishes
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the radishes, and remove the outermost coarse leaves
2. Tie them in bundles like sweet asparagus and place in boiling salted water
3. They require only a few minutes of cooking
4. Arrange on a napkin with the tops toward the center of the platter
5. Since these lose their fresh appearance during cooking, garnish the dish with a bouquet of parsley
6. Serve creamed butter with this

title: Kogte Radiſer (Boiled Radishes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young radishes of the common sort
- Salted water
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Tie young radishes together in small bundles
2. Boil for 20 minutes in salted water
3. Serve like asparagus with melted butter, often arranged on toasted bread slices

title: Kogte Ris (Cooked Rice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice (1 1/4 L or about 1 pint per 10 persons)
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice first in cold and then in warm water
2. Place it in abundant boiling salted water
3. When it is so tender that it can easily be pressed apart, drain the water from it
4. Fill the pot with cold water and stir the rice until it is completely separated
5. Drain the water and cover with a lid
6. Keep at heat until serving
7. Alternatively, after cooking, spread the rice on a colander and cover with cold water, then place in a warm oven, stirring regularly with a fork until it is separated and all water has evaporated

title: Kogte Rødspætter (Boiled Plaice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Plaice
- Salt
- Bay leaves
- Boiled potatoes
- Butter
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Purchase the plaice preferably alive
2. Cut off the head and remove entrails through the neck opening
3. Cut off the side and tail fins
4. Loosen the dark skin at the head and pull it off with a quick motion
5. Scrape the white skin side well to remove slime
6. Choose larger plaice for boiling
7. After cleaning, rinse well in several changes of cold water
8. Sprinkle with salt and let stand until cooking time
9. The fish can either be cut lengthwise along the backbone and divided in two, or filleted crosswise into several pieces
10. Bring to boil in salted water with some bay leaves
11. Cook until done in a few minutes
12. Arrange on a napkin and garnish with small boiled potatoes
13. Serve with melted butter and finely chopped parsley

title: Kogte Rødspætter (Boiled Plaice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- plaice
- salt
- laurel leaves
- water
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Choose larger plaice, clean and rinse well several times in cold water
2. Sprinkle with salt and let stand until cooking
3. Cut the fish lengthwise along the backbone into 2 parts, or cut crosswise into several pieces
4. Place over heat in boiling water with salt and some laurel leaves
5. Cook for several minutes until done
6. Arrange on serviette and garnish with small boiled potatoes

title: Kogte Selleriſtiſke (Boiled Celery Stalks)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 white, fresh celery plants
- Salt
- Pepper
- Carrot (2 Ounces)
- Butter (2 Ounces)
- Flour (½ Ounce)
- Bouillon (1 Quart)

## Directions

1. Remove the green leaves from the stalks, cut the stalks to a length of 4 inches
2. Boil for 10 minutes in water, place in cold water, clean well, rinse in several changes of water, and set to drain
3. Tie the stalks together in groups of two or three, cook with ½ Pint bouillon, ½ Pint water, carrot, spices, and a little butter
4. Cook very gently for 2 hours covered
5. Drain and arrange
6. Make a roux with butter and flour, thin with the remaining bouillon, which is reduced to 3 spoonfuls, strain, and pour over the stalks
7. Serve as a vegetable or as an independent course

title: Kogte Skorzoner-Rødder med rørt Smør (Cooked Salsify Roots with Melted Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare and clean the roots as described in recipe 710
2. Arrange the cooked whole roots on a napkin on a plate
3. Garnish with salad or parsley
4. Serve with melted butter

title: Kogte Svesker (Cooked Prunes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large prunes
- Water
- Lemon juice
- Lemon peel
- Sugar
- Butter

## Directions

1. Select the largest and best prunes.
2. Cook them in barely enough water until soft; then add lemon juice and peel, sugar and a very little butter.
3. The stones can be removed when the prunes are cooked.
4. You can be satisfied with adding only sugar.

title: Kokosnod Jelly Cake (Coconut Jelly Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups sugar
- half a cup butter
- half a cup milk
- 1 egg
- yolks of 4 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Stir 1.5 cups sugar with half a cup butter, half a cup milk, 1 egg, and the yolks of 4 eggs well
2. Sift 2 cups flour and 2 teaspoons baking powder into it
3. Bake into five cakes
4. For the filling: cook together 1 cup powdered sugar and 2 tablespoons water, remove from heat and stir in a stiffly whipped egg white, then combine with desiccated coconut (soaked for half an hour in warm milk)
5. Spread a good layer between the cakes and over the top

title: Kold Appelsin-Budding (Cold Orange Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound powdered sugar
- rind of 2 oranges and 2 lemons
- juice of 6 oranges and 2 large lemons
- 1 bottle white wine
- 12 kvint isinglass
- 6 egg yolks
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. To 1 pound powdered sugar add the grated rind of 2 oranges and 2 lemons, also the juice of 6 oranges and 2 large lemons, 1 bottle white wine, 12 kvint isinglass and 6 egg yolks.
2. Stir everything well together and place over the fire, bringing to a boil while stirring constantly.
3. Pour the mixture into a mold and let stand for a few hours in a cold place.
4. The pudding is served with vanilla cream.
5. When the pudding is taken from the fire, one can also carefully fold in 2 pægle sweet cream that has been whipped to stiff peaks.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Appelsinret (Cold Orange Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- zest of 2 oranges
- zest of 1 lemon
- juice of 4 oranges
- juice of 1 lemon
- 14 egg yolks
- 9 quintals cold dissolved isinglass
- 14 stiffly beaten egg whites
- raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 pound sugar and the grated zest of 2 oranges and 1 lemon for 1 hour with the juice of 4 oranges, juice of 1 lemon, 14 egg yolks in which 9 quintals cold dissolved isinglass has been mixed, and finally with 14 stiffly beaten egg whites.
2. Serve with raspberry juice.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Citron-Budding (Cold Lemon Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 30 kvint powdered sugar
- rind of 2 lemons
- juice of 2 lemons
- 2 pægle white French wine
- 6 kvint dissolved isinglass
- 2 pægle sweet cream

## Directions

1. Stir 10 egg yolks, 30 kvint powdered sugar and the grated rind of 2 lemons with the juice of 2 lemons until it becomes white.
2. Add 2 pægle white French wine and 6 kvint dissolved isinglass.
3. After stirring this well, carefully fold in 2 pægle sweet cream, whipped to stiff peaks.
4. Fill the mixture into a mold and chill for about an hour in ice and salt.
5. Turn out onto a platter and garnish with small oblong sugar breads.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Frugtpunsch (Cold Fruit Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds white sugar
- 1 pot water
- ½ pound fruit juice from crushed cherries, raspberries, strawberries or currants
- 1 pot boiling water
- juice of 2 lemons and yellow peel of 1 lemon
- 1 bottle red wine (or Mosel wine)
- 2 measures arrack

## Directions

1. Boil 2 pounds white sugar with 1 pot water to a syrup and skim
2. Add ½ pound fruit juice from crushed cherries, raspberries, strawberries or currants as desired
3. Add 1 pot boiling water, juice of 2 lemons and yellow peel of 1 lemon, 1 bottle red wine (or Mosel wine) and 2 measures arrack
4. Filter and let it stand well covered until it is completely cold
5. Drink cold
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Hummer-Postej (Cold Lobster Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry dough
- fish forcemeat
- cooked lobster
- lobster butter

## Directions

1. Line a mold with puff pastry dough.
2. In the bottom place fish forcemeat with pieces of cooked lobster pressed into it.
3. Over this place forcemeat mixed with lobster butter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Hummer-Postej (Cold Lobster Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough
- Fish forcemeat
- Cooked lobster pieces
- Lobster butter
- Lobster sauce

## Directions

1. Line a mold with butter dough
2. Place fish forcemeat in the bottom and press pieces of cooked lobster into it
3. Alternate layers of forcemeat mixed with lobster butter and lobster meat until the mold is nearly full
4. Top with a layer of butter dough, rolled out and pressed firmly at the edges
5. Place in a warm oven and bake for 1.5 hours
6. When the pâté is cold, turn it out onto a serving dish
7. Slice and serve with hot lobster sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Lever-Postej (Cold Liver Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal liver
- Salt
- Water
- Pepper
- Pork fat from the loin, finely chopped, 3/4 pound per pound of liver
- Broth from cooking the liver
- Sweet cream
- Butter

## Directions

1. Wash and cook the veal liver
2. Cool it thoroughly
3. Grate it finely
4. For each pound of grated liver, take 3/4 pound of finely chopped pork fat from the loin
5. Work everything together well with pepper and salt
6. Thin with some of the broth in which the liver was cooked and with sweet cream until it becomes smooth and spongy
7. Place in a buttered mold
8. Bake for 1 hour in a warm oven
9. Cool thoroughly
10. When the pâté is to be served, dip the mold in boiling water to loosen it
11. Serve as an accompaniment to open-faced sandwiches
12. Optional: add finely chopped onion to the liver mixture, or line the mold with very thin bacon slices
13. Note: Pork liver or game liver can be used instead of veal liver
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Mandelbudding (Cold Almond Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pægle sweet milk
- 20 kvint sweet almonds, skinned and finely ground
- 10 kvint bitter almonds, skinned and finely ground
- 30 kvint wheat starch
- 10 egg yolks
- ¾ pound white sugar
- 1 tablespoon rum

## Directions

1. Place 5 pægle sweet milk over the fire, and when the milk boils, add 20 kvint sweet almonds and 10 kvint bitter almonds, skinned and finely ground, and half-cook them.
2. Mix 30 kvint wheat starch, 10 egg yolks, ¾ pound white sugar and 1 tablespoon rum in 1 pægle cold milk.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Mandelhudding (Cold Almond Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pails sweet milk
- 20 quintals sweet almonds, blanched and finely ground
- 10 quintals bitter almonds, blanched and finely ground
- 30 quintals wheat starch
- 10 egg yolks
- 3/4 pound white sugar
- 1 tablespoon rum
- whipped egg whites

## Directions

1. Heat 5 pails sweet milk over the fire, and when the milk boils, add 20 quintals sweet and 10 quintals bitter blanched and finely ground almonds, which are half-cooked.
2. Mix 30 quintals wheat starch, 10 egg yolks, 3/4 pound white sugar, and 1 tablespoon rum in 1 pail cold sweet milk.
3. Stir this mixture into the boiling milk and let it cook for approximately 5 minutes.
4. Remove from heat, and when it stops boiling, stir in the whipped egg whites.
5. Serve the pudding cold with fruit sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Peberrod-Sauce (Cold Horseradish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Thick sour cream
- White powdered sugar
- Vinegar
- Grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Whip thick sour cream
2. Add white powdered sugar and vinegar according to the sourness of the cream
3. Add grated horseradish until it becomes sufficiently sharp
4. This sauce is used for cold fish and pike; some even use it for warm fish

title: Kold Postej af Vildt (Cold Game Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Butter dough
- Thin bacon slices
- Game bird, plucked and cleaned
- Breast meat from game bird, finely chopped
- Pork fatback, finely chopped, equal amount to breast meat
- 2 onions
- Pepper
- 4 whole eggs
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- Thyme, crushed
- 3 cups white beer
- Pitted olives
- Bouillon, good and strong
- Root vegetables

## Directions

1. Grease a casserole with butter and line with butter dough
2. Line the bottom and sides with thin bacon slices
3. Pluck and clean the game bird thoroughly
4. Scrape all meat from the breast and chop very finely with an equal amount of pork fatback, 2 onions, pepper, 4 whole eggs, 2 tablespoons vinegar, a little crushed thyme, and 3 cups white beer
5. Mix everything together well
6. Layer the forcemeat mixture in the bottom, then a layer of game, then forcemeat again, then a layer of pitted olives, and continue alternating until the mold is nearly full
7. Top with a layer of butter dough, pressed firmly at the sides
8. Place in a warm oven and bake for 30 minutes
9. Remove and cut an opening in the center
10. Fill this opening with good, strong hot bouillon
11. Return to the oven and bake for another 2 hours, occasionally pouring hot bouillon into the opening
12. Remove from oven and let cool until half-cold
13. Place a light press on top and leave until the next day
14. Place the casserole in hot water to loosen the pâté and turn it out onto a serving dish
15. Serve with robust vegetables
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Vanille-Budding (Cold Vanilla Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- 4½ pounds white sugar
- 6 kvint isinglass
- a couple of teaspoons powdered vanilla
- 12 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pot sweet cream, 4½ pounds white sugar and 6 kvint isinglass until the sugar is dissolved.
2. Stir in a couple of teaspoons powdered vanilla.
3. Pour this mixture into 12 egg yolks, which have first been stirred until white.
4. When nearly cold, pour into a mold and preferably let stand until the next day.
5. Serve with sweet fruit sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kold Æblesuppe for Syge (Cold Apple Soup for the Sick)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 medium, juicy apples
- 2 pots water
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the apples and cut them into four pieces
2. Boil them with 2 pots of water until they are very soft
3. Strain the juice from the pulp
4. Sweeten with powdered sugar
5. Serve cold
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Koldskaal (Cold Fruit Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Bottle red wine
- 1 Quart cold Water
- Juice and peel of 1 Lemon, sliced
- White sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the red wine with 1 quart cold water, the juice and sliced peel of 1 lemon, and white sugar to taste.
2. When served, place a piece of ice in the tureen.
3. Serve with rusks.
4. This can also be used as a refreshing summer drink and can be made with ale.

title: Koldskaal af Øl (Beer Cold Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Bottles White Beer
- Juice and Peel of 1 Lemon
- whole Cinnamon
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) cold Water
- Sugar
- Sherry or white Wine
- Cognac
- 500 Gr (1 lb) Rye Rusks

## Directions

1. Put the beer in the tureen together with juice and peel of 1 lemon, whole cinnamon and 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) cold water
2. Add sugar, sherry or white wine and cognac to taste
3. Cover the cold soup and let it stand thus for a couple of hours in a cold place
4. Shortly before serving, 250 Gr (1/2 Q) rye rusks are placed in the tureen, while the rest is served on the side

title: Koldskaal af Øl (Beer Cold Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint sour cream
- 3 bottles light beer or double beer (not bitter)
- 1 whole bottle Bavarian beer
- a little ice-cold water
- a little finely chopped lemon peel
- rye rusks or wheat rusks

## Directions

1. Whip 1 pint of sour cream in a tureen with 3 bottles of light beer or double beer (that is not bitter) and 1 whole bottle of Bavarian beer and a little ice-cold water.
2. A little finely chopped lemon peel gives a good flavor.
3. Serve with rye rusks or wheat rusks cut into large cubes and added to the cold soup just before serving.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Koldskål af Øl (Beer Cold Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint sour cream
- 3 bottles light beer or double beer (not bitter)
- 1 bottle Bavarian beer
- a little ice-cold water
- a little finely chopped lemon peel
- rye bread cubes or wheat bread cubes

## Directions

1. Whisk 1 pint of sour cream in a bowl with 3 bottles of light beer or double beer (not bitter) and 1 bottle of Bavarian beer along with a little ice-cold water
2. Add a little finely chopped lemon peel for good flavor
3. Just before serving, add rye bread cubes or wheat bread cubes cut into large pieces to the cold soup
4. Serve immediately
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kombudding A (Kummel Pudding A)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound finely ground melis sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 1 stick vanilla, chopped
- 1 pot boiling cream
- 7 quintals dissolved isinglass
- 5 bottles white rum
- 12 whipped egg whites
- 1 pail cream, whipped to foam

## Directions

1. Whip 1/2 pound finely ground melis sugar together with 12 egg yolks for about a quarter hour and place over heat with 1 chopped stick vanilla.
2. Whip in 1 pot boiling cream along with 7 quintals dissolved isinglass.
3. When it has come to a boil, remove from heat and continue stirring, adding 5 bottles white rum.
4. When the cream is almost cold, add the 12 whipped egg whites and 1 pail cream whipped to foam.
5. Pour the pudding into glass dishes and let it stand in a cold place for at least one day before using.
6. Serve with sauce made from fruit juice.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kommenskringler (Caraway Ring Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine flour
- 6 quints butter
- 6 quints yeast
- 1 measure milk
- caraway seeds
- coarse salt (optional)

## Directions

1. Work 1 pound fine flour, 6 quints butter, 6 quints yeast and 1 measure milk together well and let rise for a moment.
2. Form the dough into fairly large rings and sprinkle with caraway seeds.
3. Let them rise again for a short time and place in boiling water.
4. When they float up, remove them, place on a pan and put in a hot oven to bake light brown.
5. Before baking, you can also sprinkle some coarse salt on them.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kommenskringler (Caraway Seed Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 g margarine
- 2/4 kg flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon caraway seed
- 30 g yeast, dissolved in 3 1/4 dl lukewarm milk

## Directions

1. Crumble the margarine into the flour
2. Add salt, caraway seed, and yeast dissolved in milk
3. Mix the dough well and form into rings
4. Let rise, then cook, brush, and bake as Fastekringler

title: Kompot af Abrikoser (Apricot Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots
- water
- white sugar
- white wine

## Directions

1. Boil the apricots in water
2. Remove them and strip off the skin and split them
3. Remove the stones and place the apricot pieces in a sauce of white sugar boiled with a little water
4. Crack some of the stones, remove the kernels, scald them and remove the outer skin, then place them in the compote
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kompot af Blommer (Plum Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- white sugar
- water
- white wine
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Treat as for apricot compote, but add a little lemon peel to the sauce when it is boiled
2. Use white wine for half the liquid instead of water
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kompot af Kirsebær (Cherry Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- white sugar
- water
- white wine
- lemon peel
- potato starch

## Directions

1. Treat as for plum compote
2. To make the sauce slightly smoother, a teaspoon of potato starch dissolved in water may be added, and the berries gently shaken with it
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kompot af Pærer (Pear Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- white wine
- water
- lemon peel
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and split them lengthwise so a piece of the split stalk remains with each half
2. Cut out the core and scrape the stalk a little with a penknife or replace it with a piece of cinnamon
3. Set a saucepan over heat with half white wine and half water, lemon peel, and white sugar
4. When this has boiled a little, place the pear pieces in it and cook until tender, which if the pears are hard may sometimes take over two hours
5. Gray pears are best for compote
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Korende-Kager (engelske) (Caraway Cakes - English)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 grams (3/4 lb) Pillsbury flour
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) margarine
- 100 grams (20 quintal) icing sugar

## Directions

1. Mix together flour, margarine and icing sugar
2. Roll out and cut with a cake cutter into long cakes
3. Brush with egg
4. Sprinkle with icing sugar and caraway seeds
5. Bake in a well-heated oven

title: Korum (Danish Curdled Milk Dessert)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- nearly 1/2 L (1/10 Pot) sweet Milk per person
- 1/2 Dl (3/10 Pægl) sweet Cream per person
- Grated Rye Bread
- Sugar
- crushed Almonds
- Jam, if desired

## Directions

1. Set the milk up in a vessel, and when it has become thick, skim off the top cream layer and whip it together with 1/2 Dl (3/10 Pægl) sweet cream for each person
2. Pour the milk into the tureen and whip it with the whisk
3. Stir in grated rye bread, sugar, crushed almonds and, if desired, a little jam together with the whipped cream

title: Koteletter af Helleflynder (Halibut Cutlets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Halibut meat
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg
- Bread
- Butter or Palmin
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thick slices lengthwise with the grain of the meat, without bone and skin
2. Beat the pieces with a meat mallet
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, dip in egg and bread
4. Fry on the pan in butter or ample Palmin
5. Arrange standing upright in a point in the middle of the platter, covered with a napkin and garnished with lemon
6. Serve with browned butter and cooked potatoes or with various kinds of compound butter

title: Koteletter af kogt Kalvebryst (Cutlets of Cooked Veal Breast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked veal breast
- Melted butter
- Breadcrumbs
- Pepper
- Salt
- Egg
- Claret
- Mixed vegetables
- Salad or parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked breast into pieces with 1 or 2 bones
2. Dip them in melted butter and turn in crushed bread mixed with pepper and salt
3. Then dip in egg and again in bread
4. Cook the cutlets light brown in claret
5. Place mixed vegetables in the center of the dish
6. Arrange the cutlets around this, decorated with salad or parsley

title: Kraase-Ragout (Goose Giblet Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 to 5 portions of goose giblets
- a good slice of butter
- 1 pair of grated onions
- flour for coating
- a pair of whole carrots
- paprika
- salt
- a little curry
- color
- boiling water as needed
- sherry
- potato starch for thickening
- puff pastry strips, cooked apple halves, or glazed chestnuts for garnish

## Directions

1. Chop the giblets, clean and blanch them as described for giblet soup.
2. Brown a good slice of butter in a pot together with grated onions.
3. Coat the giblets in flour and place them in the pot.
4. Braise well together with whole carrots, paprika, salt, a little curry, and color.
5. Pour in as much boiling water as needed for the sauce.
6. Cook the giblets until tender under a lid for at least 2 hours.
7. Remove the carrots and skim the fat from the gravy.
8. Finish the sauce with sherry and thicken further with potato starch mixed in water.
9. Serve very hot, garnished with puff pastry strips, cooked apple halves, or glazed chestnuts.

title: Kraase-Suppe (Goose Giblet Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 times goose giblets
- apples
- prunes
- 100 g (20 skein) butter or vegetable margarine
- 100 g (20 skein) flour
- vinegar
- sugar
- Maggi bouillon cubes

## Directions

1. After the giblets are cleaned, split the heads, cut off the beaks, and remove the brains and eyes.
2. Chop the wings into 2 pieces and the neck with skin into 3 pieces.
3. Remove the windpipe and gullet, and clean the heart of blood.
4. Split the giblets, clean, and scald together with the feet (claws removed) and remove the outer skin, as well as the thick membrane on the inside of the giblets.
5. When sufficiently cleaned and washed, place in cold water with salt so it reaches the level of the giblets.
6. When boiling, skim the soup, then add whisks and vegetables as mentioned for goose soup.
7. As soon as the giblets are tender, which takes 2-3 hours, strain most of the soup away, leaving just a little in the pot to keep the giblets warm.
8. Bake 100 g (20 skein) flour in 100 g (20 skein) butter or vegetable margarine and thin with the strained soup and the water in which the apples and prunes were cooked.
9. Season with vinegar and sugar to taste and can be mixed with Maggi bouillon cubes.
10. Pour the boiling soup into the tureen over rolls, apples, prunes, and the cut vegetables.
11. The giblets can be served either in the soup or separately; garnish the platter with parsley.

title: Kraasesuppe og Andesuppe (Goose and Duck Giblet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Kraasemaden af 2 Gæs eller 4 Ænder (giblets from 2 geese or 4 ducks)
- Hovederne (heads)
- Tungerne (tongues)
- Halsen (neck)
- De yderste Led af Vingerne (the outer joints of the wings)

## Directions

1. Use giblets from 2 geese or 4 ducks.
2. Clean the giblets carefully, remove the thick inside membrane by scraping, and cut into 2 or 4 pieces.
3. Split the heads and clean them along with the tongues.
4. Clean the neck and the outer joints of the wings.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kraasesuppe og Andesuppe (Goose and Duck Offal Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Offal from 2 geese or 4 ducks
- 5 pots cold water
- Salt
- Soup vegetables and roots
- Peeled apple pieces
- Washed prunes
- A small piece of ginger
- Sugar
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the offal thoroughly, removing the thick inner membrane and cutting into 2 or 4 pieces
2. Clean the heads by splitting and removing the tongues, neck, and outermost wing joints, carefully plucking out fine feathers
3. Scald the feet and remove the outer skin
4. Clean the liver and heart
5. Place all cleaned and rinsed offal in a pot with 5 pots cold water and a little salt
6. Bring to boil and skim
7. Add soup vegetables and roots
8. Simmer until the offal is tender
9. Remove the offal and keep warm for serving with the soup
10. Remove the roots, cut into pieces, and place in the tureen
11. Strain the soup and return to heat for a boil with peeled apple pieces, some washed prunes, and a small piece of ginger
12. When the apples are tender, remove from heat and season with sugar, vinegar, and salt to taste
13. For duck soup, old ducks may also be used; one duck is usually sufficient for 6 portions
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kraaſe-Suppe (Offal Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- offal
- bunch of herbs
- root vegetables
- flour
- vinegar
- apples
- sugar
- bread or flour balls

## Directions

1. Clean the offal, cut it into pieces, wash it in several changes of cold water so it stands well covered; cook bunch of herbs and root vegetables with it; when these and the offal are done, take them out
2. Strain the soup, place it on the fire, and when it boils again and is skimmed, add a roux of very little flour stirred in vinegar and chopped apples and sugar
3. When the apples are cooked enough, take them out so there is room to add bread or flour balls; pour the soup over the offal
4. You can also serve these on a plate and thus offer them at table

title: Kraaſepostei (Goose Giblet Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- For 8 persons: Pastry dough
- 2 cleaned goose giblets
- 2 carrots
- 1 celery root
- 2 onions with 2 cloves in each
- Parsley
- Thyme
- a few peppercorns
- Salt
- Bouillon or water
- Soy sauce
- Farce of ox meat

## Directions

1. Simmer the giblets for 5 minutes in water with a little salt, rinse, drain on a sieve, place in a casserole with the vegetables and seasonings, moisten with sufficient bouillon or water and a little soy sauce and stew very gently over a weak heat 1.5 hours or longer or shorter according to the age of the geese.
2. When the giblets are cooked, drain them; cut them together with the vegetables into pieces of a couple of inches length. The liver is cooked less. Arrange in a pâté mold that has been lined with farce.
3. Reduce the bouillon in which the giblets were cooked to a suitably thick sauce, from which the fat is skimmed well. Pour the sauce into the mold, cover the mold with pastry dough and bake. Serve.

title: Krabber (Crabs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized crabs
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Ground white pepper
- Cayenne pepper
- Mustard
- Lemon slices
- Parsley or salad leaves

## Directions

1. Medium-sized crabs are best, and the crab should, like the lobster, be heavy when it is full of meat.
2. Cook the crab as you would cook lobster.
3. When cold, split them and pull the meat out.
4. The shell, that is the back shell, is used to arrange the meat in.
5. Cut the meat into small pieces and lay it on one side, while the soft mass in the crab, stirred together with oil, vinegar and salt and with a little ground white pepper and cayenne pepper or with a little mixed mustard, is laid in a stripe on the opposite side.
6. Arrange the shells, garnished with the claws, lemon slices and parsley or salad leaves on a platter, covered with a napkin.
7. The crab meat can also be served in mayonnaise, seasoned with English fish sauce, tarragon vinegar and a little mixed mustard, or the mayonnaise is laid in stripes over the meat, arranged in salad leaves, with which the shell is covered.

title: Krabber bagt i Gratinskaller (Crabs Baked in Shells with Gratin Topping)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Crab meat
- Fairly smooth, clarified hollandaise sauce
- Salt
- Tarragon vinegar or lemon juice
- Ground white pepper
- Breadcrumbs
- Parmesan cheese, grated
- Melted butter
- Parsley or salad leaves

## Directions

1. Prepare a fairly smooth, clarified hollandaise sauce, seasoned with salt, tarragon vinegar or lemon juice and with ground white pepper.
2. Turn the meat in this sauce, fill it into the shells, sprinkle with breadcrumbs and brown the surface in the oven.
3. The breadcrumbs can be mixed with grated Parmesan cheese and drizzled with a little melted butter.
4. Serve directly from the oven on a platter, covered with a napkin and decorated with parsley or salad leaves.

title: Kramsfugle (Thrush)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrushes
- Bacon slices
- Butter
- Cream
- Maggis bouillon cubes
- Food coloring
- Cold butter
- Heart, gizzard, and liver

## Directions

1. Pluck the birds and remove the entrails.
2. Wrap the breast with thin slices of bacon.
3. Brown in butter and braise in a pot for a quarter hour, basting with cream during cooking.
4. The sauce should reduce well and can have Maggis bouillon cubes and coloring added to achieve a light brown color.
5. At the end, stir cold butter into the sauce.
6. The heart, gizzard, and liver can be made into a forcemeat (see Snæpper, No. 979), which can either be stuffed into the birds or prepared as woodcock bread.

title: Kramsfugle-Postei (Partridge Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pâté dough or Morde dough
- 6 Partridges
- Meat forcemeat
- Bacon
- Meat stock
- Butter

## Directions

1. Clean the birds thoroughly and remove the heads and wings
2. Roast the birds
3. Line a mold with dough
4. Layer alternately the birds and meat forcemeat in the mold
5. First lay a layer of bacon slices, which are removed before serving
6. Bake for 2 hours over heat
7. Serve warm with sauce made from strong meat stock

title: Kramsfugle-Postei (Woodcock Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- For 4 persons: Pastry dough or fine pastry dough
- 6 woodcocks
- Meat farce
- Pork fat (lard)
- Gravy

## Directions

1. Clean the birds thoroughly, remove the head and wings, then roast the birds.
2. Line a mold with dough.

title: Krandskage (Wreath Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds sweet almonds
- 2 ounces bitter almonds
- 1.5 pounds powdered sugar
- zest of 1 lemon
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Fold together the sweet and bitter almonds
2. Toast and pound them
3. Stir the almonds with 1.5 pounds powdered sugar, lemon zest, and egg whites that have been whipped to stiff peaks
4. Form rings from the dough, each one smaller than the last so they can be stacked together when baked
5. Bake at an even temperature until light golden
6. If making small wreaths, this yields approximately 40 pieces

title: Kransekage (Wreath Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound blanched sweet almonds
- 5 pounds sweet almonds
- 10 quintals bitter almonds
- 4 egg whites
- 2 pounds powdered sugar
- Zest of 1 lemon
- Caramel
- Decorative sugar
- Sugar figurine

## Directions

1. Cut 1 pound blanched sweet almonds into small cubes
2. Pound 5 pounds sweet and 10 quintals bitter almonds finely in a mortar after drying them slightly in the oven overnight
3. Whip 4 egg whites to stiff peaks, then whip with 2 pounds powdered sugar, the zest of 1 lemon, and finally the almonds
4. Form wreaths from this batter using a pastry bag and bake at gentle oven heat either on a wax-buttered plate or on wafers
5. Join the wreaths together using caramel and decorate the whole cake with decorative sugar and a sugar figurine on top
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Krebs (Freshwater Crayfish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Freshwater crayfish
- Salt
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Dill

## Directions

1. Freshwater crayfish are caught in fresh water.
2. Cook them as you would cook lobster, but for at most 5 minutes.
3. Crayfish are used very much for decoration in various dishes.
4. For eating crayfish, serve with oil, vinegar and pepper, and arrange them in a pyramid, decorated with parsley and dill.

title: Krebse-Budding (Crayfish Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 to 60 crayfish
- sauce ingredients

## Directions

1. Prepare as for Lobster Pudding.
2. Some crayfish tails are added to the sauce.

title: Krebse-Sauce (Crayfish Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock
- Crayfish meat
- Lemon juice
- White pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Make as lobster sauce
2. Place the meat of crayfish tails in the finished sauce

title: Krebse-Suppe (Crayfish Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 crayfish
- soup stock
- asparagus
- small fish balls
- wine
- parsley
- ground white pepper
- Butter dough slices or Simels

## Directions

1. Follow the same procedure as for Lobster Soup (Nr. 376)
2. When the crayfish are cooked and cold, pick out the meat from the tails and remove the black vein
3. Place the crayfish tails in the soup together with asparagus and fish balls
4. Season with wine, chopped parsley and ground white pepper
5. Serve as Lobster Soup with Butter dough slices or Simels

title: Krebse-Suppe (Crayfish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 crayfish of medium size
- Butter
- Meat broth
- Rice flour
- White root vegetables or asparagus
- Bread dumplings or fish dumplings

## Directions

1. Boil the crayfish as usual.
2. Pick out the meat from the tails, heads and claws; crush the shells and all the small bones finely with a little butter, place on the fire and blanch well, but not too long, so as to avoid a bad taste, and moisten slightly with meat broth, which does not need to be strong, until it stands over this.
3. When it has cooked a little, strain it, place it back on the fire with the same amount of broth, and after a good boiling strain it again into the dark portion.
4. Let the soup stand awhile so that you can skim off the red butter; in this stir in approximately two ounces of rice flour, which is added to the crayfish soup when this, mixed with the remaining meat broth, boils again.
5. Use only white root vegetables or asparagus.
6. It is eaten either with bread dumplings, into which the chopped crayfish claws and heads are stirred, or with fish dumplings.
7. The shells of the crayfish heads are filled with some of the dumpling dough and cooked in the soup.
8. After the dark vein is removed from the tails, these whole tails are placed on the finished soup, but must not be cooked with it.

title: Krebsesalat (Crayfish Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish meat
- Cooked potatoes
- Beets
- Cucumbers
- Onion
- Sugar
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Mustard
- Crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Mix crayfish meat with cooked potatoes, beets, cucumbers and onion, all cut into small cubes, and with sugar, vinegar, salt, pepper and mustard to taste
2. Line a cone on the inside with crayfish tails so they face toward the cone
3. Fill it with all the chopped ingredients
4. When serving, turn the cone over onto the dish and garnish it with green salad leaves
5. The salad may also be prepared as in recipe 451
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Krebsesuppe eller Hummersuppe (Crayfish or Lobster Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 medium crayfish
- salt
- water
- 1/4 pound butter
- wheat flour (1 tablespoon)
- crayfish or lobster bouillon
- good bouillon
- fish forcemeat
- 4 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons good sweet cream
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the crayfish and place in boiling salted water. Cook for approximately a quarter hour until done.
2. Remove and let cool, then shell them. Set aside the tails (removing the intestine) and some of the largest claws. Clean the 20 to 24 finest heads well and place in cold water.
3. Finely pound all remaining debris and shells with 1/4 pound of butter.
4. Place in a saucepan and sauté with constant stirring so it does not burn.
5. Dilute with the crayfish bouillon so it stands well above the shells and cook until the red butter floats on top. Skim this during cooking as it appears.
6. Place the crayfish butter in a saucepan with 1 tablespoon of wheat flour and cook together, then dilute with the strained crayfish bouillon and good bouillon until it makes between 4 and 5 pots total.
7. Fill the cleaned empty head shells with fish forcemeat, boil in salted water, clean them and add to the soup along with the tails and large claws. Small fish balls may also be added.
8. In the tureen, beat 4 egg yolks with 6 tablespoons of good sweet cream, then carefully add the soup a little at a time under continuous beating.
9. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
10. For lobster soup, prepare in the same manner but also add 1 pound of asparagus cut into pieces about one inch long, along with a little of the asparagus water, which is brought to a boil with the soup.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kringler til The og Kaffe (Kringler for Tea and Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint milk
- 2 cents yeast
- fine wheat flour
- 1/2 cup melted butter
- 1 cup raisins
- candied orange peel, chopped
- lemon zest
- sugar
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Warm 1 pint milk and set with 2 cents yeast dissolved in a little of the milk plus as much fine wheat flour to make dough of the usual thickness and evenness.
2. Set in a warm place, and when well risen (which takes 2 to 3 hours), stir in 1/2 cup melted butter, 1 cup raisins, a generous amount of chopped candied orange peel, lemon zest, and a handful of sugar and 3 eggs.
3. Stir in flour until the dough is stiff enough to knead but not more.
4. When kneaded so it no longer sticks to the fingers, divide into three parts, roll out long and thin, and braid the dough in an ordinary braid that is long enough to fit around inside a bread pan.
5. Set to rise again, and when risen, brush with egg.

title: Krokant-Kringler (Brittle Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound wheat flour
- ¼ pound powdered sugar
- ¾ pound rinsed butter
- 1 egg
- lemon zest, grated
- egg yolk
- coarsely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Mix together wheat flour, powdered sugar, rinsed butter, egg, and grated lemon zest
2. Roll into small rings
3. Brush with egg yolk
4. Sprinkle with coarsely ground sugar
5. Bake light brown
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kroketter af Kalvebrissel (Croquettes of Calf Sweetbreads)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pair calf sweetbreads
- 1/2 kg calf meat
- butter
- flour
- stock from the sweetbreads
- 2 egg yolks
- 4 leaves dissolved isinglass
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Brown butter and flour and thin with the stock from the sweetbreads to a thick sauce.
2. Bind this with 2 egg yolks and add 4 leaves dissolved isinglass, salt, pepper, and a few drops of lemon juice.
3. Last, add the coarsely chopped sweetbreads and the cooked, chopped calf meat.
4. Form the cold stew and otherwise treat as Roulettes (Nr. 16).
5. These croquettes are placed on the platter as garnish around braised vegetables.

title: Krumkager (Crispy Wafers)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound butter
- ½ pound powdered sugar
- ¾ pound wheat flour
- 3 egg whites, whipped to stiff peaks

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and remove the salt
2. Stir well
3. Stir in ½ pound powdered sugar
4. Stir in ¾ pound wheat flour
5. Finally fold in 3 egg whites whipped to stiff peaks
6. Bake the cakes light brown
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Krumkager (Rolled Wafers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- ½ pound sugar
- ½ pound butter, clarified
- 4 eggs
- approximately 1½ pint milk
- ⅓ gill cream, whipped to foam
- a little jam

## Directions

1. Mix 1 pound flour, ½ pound sugar, ½ pound butter (clarified), 4 eggs, and approximately 1½ pint milk well together and bake
2. The iron does not need to be greased for each cake
3. When the cake is taken from the iron, roll it like a cone
4. Whip ⅓ gill cream to foam; put 1 spoonful in each cake and on top a small teaspoonful jam
5. The foam must be put in the cakes just before they are to be used, otherwise they become soft; you can also place jam on a dish, put the foam on it and serve it together with the cakes
6. This makes approximately 70 cakes

title: Krustader (Crusted Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 ounces fine flour
- 6 ounces clarified butter
- 1 egg
- 2 cups white beer
- 1 cup water

## Directions

1. Mix flour, clarified butter, egg, white beer, and water to form a smooth batter of the consistency of pancake batter
2. Heat the croustade iron well in boiling butter or clarified fat
3. Dip the iron in the batter
4. Place it back in the hot butter or fat and fry until the croustade becomes a beautiful light brown
5. Remove from the iron carefully with a thin knife at the edge of the iron
6. Place upside down on a plate until needed (preferably the same day)
7. If croustades must be kept, cover them so they do not lose their crispness
8. Fill with similar filling as used for small pâtés
9. Allow 8 to 10 per serving
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Krustader (Fried Pastry Shells)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg flour
- 1/4 L cold water
- 1 1/4 dL egg white
- 2 teaspoons powdered sugar
- 4 eggs
- Clarified butter or Palmin for frying

## Directions

1. Mix flour, cold water, egg white, powdered sugar, and eggs into a thin pancake batter
2. Heat clarified butter or Palmin in a pot with a krustader iron
3. Do not heat the Palmin so strongly that it steams
4. Pour batter into as many small cups as there are forms on the iron
5. Dip the iron into the batter, not quite to the rim
6. Keep in the hot fat and fry until the shells are light brown
7. Loosen with a fork or larding needle and place upside down on paper to drain the fat
8. Pour the remaining fat through a sieve
9. The shells can be stored in metal boxes for a long time, but must be placed in a warm oven before filling is added

title: Krustader (Pastry Shells)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg flour
- 1/4 L cold water
- 1 1/4 dl egg white
- 2 teaspoonfuls powdered sugar
- 4 eggs
- good lard or Palmin for frying

## Directions

1. Stir together the flour, cold water, egg white, powdered sugar, and eggs into a pancake batter
2. Fry approximately 80 shells in good lard or Palmin

title: Krustader med Asparges og Fiskeboller (Fried Pastry Shells with Asparagus and Fish Balls)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauce as in recipe 2
- Small fish balls
- Asparagus water
- Gravy
- Lobster and crab extract (optional)

## Directions

1. Make sauce as in recipe 2
2. Cook small fish balls in asparagus water
3. Add gravy to the sauce
4. Lobster and crab extract may be added to the white, smooth sauce; egg yolks can then be omitted
5. See fish farce recipe 205-206 for preparation of fish balls

title: Krustader med Asparges og Rejer (Fried Pastry Shells with Asparagus and Shrimp)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter shrimp, cooked and shelled
- 1/2 kg asparagus, peeled
- Salt
- Flour
- Butter
- Asparagus broth
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook and shell the shrimp
2. Peel the asparagus and cut into 2 centimeter pieces
3. Cook the asparagus tender in salted water
4. Make a roux with flour and butter
5. Dilute with asparagus broth and thicken with 2 egg yolks
6. Add the asparagus and shrimp to the sauce

title: Krustader med Hachis (Fried Pastry Shells with Chopped Meat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- 1 onion, chopped
- Flour
- Stock
- Tomato purée
- Paprika
- Pickled vegetables or spiced vinegar
- Maggi bouillon cube
- Sugar
- Salt
- Wine
- Cognac
- Coloring
- Chopped cooked or fried meat

## Directions

1. Brown butter, chopped onion, and flour
2. Dilute with stock and tomato purée to a thin consistency
3. Add paprika, chopped pickled vegetables or spiced vinegar, Maggi bouillon cube, sugar, salt, wine, a little cognac, and coloring so the hachis becomes a beautiful brown color
4. Add the chopped meat (cooked or fried) last
5. Fill into warm krustader and arrange as usual

title: Krustader med Høns og Champignons (Fried Pastry Shells with Chicken and Mushrooms)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked chicken or leftovers from chicken dishes
- Sauce as in recipe 6
- Mushrooms (optional)
- Lobster and crab extract (optional, as a cheaper alternative)

## Directions

1. Use cooked chicken or leftover chicken dishes
2. Prepare sauce as in recipe 6
3. Turkey can be used in the same way
4. Lobster and crab extract can be used instead of mushrooms as it is cheaper

title: Krustader med Kalvebrissel (Fried Pastry Shells with Calf Sweetbreads)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pair calf sweetbreads
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth from sweetbreads
- 2 egg yolks
- Pepper
- Lemon juice
- Gravy or sherry
- Mushrooms (optional)

## Directions

1. Soak sweetbreads in water for one hour
2. Rinse carefully
3. Pour boiling water over them (blanch) and place on heat with cold water, only as much as is needed for the sauce
4. Cook until firm, then remove
5. Remove the membranes and cut into small cubes
6. Make a roux with butter and flour
7. Dilute with the strained broth to a thick sauce
8. Thicken (liaise) with 2 well-beaten egg yolks without bringing to a boil
9. Add salt, ground pepper, a little lemon juice, gravy or sherry, and if desired, mushrooms
10. Add the sweetbreads last
11. Serve krustader as quickly as possible after filling
12. Arrange on a plate with a folded napkin underneath

title: Krustader med Muslinger (Fried Pastry Shells with Mussels)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh mussels
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Mussel broth
- Wine
- Lemon juice
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Examine each mussel to ensure it is fresh and good - it should be closed; if it has opened, discard it and do not put it in the pot with the others
2. Clean mussels with a brush
3. Place on heat with a little water and salt on the bottom of the pot, covered with a tight-fitting lid
4. As soon as they open, they are cooked
5. Remove from shells and remove the beard and black intestine
6. Strain the broth and use for the sauce
7. Prepare sauce and add wine, lemon juice, and egg yolks in the same way as in recipe 13

title: Krustader med Skildpadde-Ragout (Fried Pastry Shells with Turtle Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 split calf head with tongue, brain, and eyes removed
- Water
- Salt
- Margarine or fat (soup fat)
- 1 slice smoked bacon or bacon fat
- 4 onions
- 4 carrots

## Directions

1. Soak the split calf head in cold water for a few hours to draw out the blood
2. Clean and blanch it and cook the day before
3. The tongue can either be used for the turtle ragout or salted and used as a cold cut
4. Place on heat in cold water with a little salt, skim well and simmer until it loosens from the bones
5. Remove the meat and place on a flat plate with a light press on top
6. When cold, cut into cubes of about 2 1/2 centimeters
7. Brown margarine or fat (soup fat) and a slice of smoked bacon or just bacon fat
8. Add 4 onions and 4 carrots

title: Krustader med Torsk (Crust Cases with Cod)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cod
- flour
- butter
- fish stock
- a little Graves wine
- mushrooms or tomato paste or lobster and crayfish extract (optional)
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pick the cooked cod into small pieces and free it from skin and bones.
2. Brown flour and butter together, thin it with fish stock and add a little Graves wine.
3. The sauce can be made either with mushrooms, tomato paste, or lobster and crayfish extract; wine alone can also be sufficient.
4. Last, add the fish and turn it carefully in the sauce.
5. If mushrooms are used, place one on top in each crust case and bind the sauce with egg yolks.

title: Krustader med Vildt-Ragout (Crust Cases with Game Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover game of all kinds, both animals and birds
- butter
- flour
- stock or water or milk
- meat extract or Maggi seasoning
- salt
- truffles or olives or mushrooms
- currant jelly or a little red wine

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into small cubes.
2. Make the sauce of browned butter and flour, thinned with stock.
3. If you do not have stock left over, crush the bones and cook them in stock, water, or milk.
4. Add meat extract or Maggi seasoning and salt to the sauce so it becomes sufficiently strong.
5. The sauce can be seasoned with various things, such as finely chopped truffles, peeled olives, or mushrooms.
6. Currant jelly or a little red wine stirred into the sauce gives a good flavor.

title: Krustader med Ærter, Tunge eller Skinke (Crust Cases with Peas, Tongue, or Ham)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- green peas
- water
- sugar
- butter
- cooked smoked ham or beef tongue (leftovers)

## Directions

1. Steam the green peas tender in very little water, with a little sugar added.
2. Drain them on a sieve, then toss them in butter.
3. If you have leftovers of cooked smoked ham or beef tongue, cut it very fine and stir it into the peas.
4. Fill the peas into the crust cases and arrange according to previous instructions.
5. The peas can also be braised.

title: Krustader med Østers (Crust Cases with Oysters)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 to 2 oysters per crust case
- oyster liquor from the shells
- oyster beards
- fish stock
- lemon juice
- bouillon or water with Maggi bouillon cubes
- butter
- flour
- Graves wine
- ground white pepper
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Open the oysters; pour the water in the shells into a saucepan together with the removed beards and give it a boil in fish stock with a few drops of lemon juice, in bouillon, or water with Maggi bouillon cubes added.
2. Strain the stock and use it for the sauce, which is made of butter and flour and thinned to an even sauce.
3. Add 1 glass of Graves wine, ground white pepper, and any liquid that may still be with the oysters; bind it with a couple of egg yolks.
4. Pour boiling water over the oysters and place them in the warm sauce.
5. Arrange as soon as possible.

title: Krustader à la Macédoine (Fried Pastry Shells with Mixed Vegetables)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh vegetables: peas, carrots, cauliflower, beans, and asparagus
- Sugar
- Butter
- Flour
- Meat broth or vegetable water
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Salt
- 2 egg yolks
- Raw cream
- Smoked salmon (for garnish)
- Smoked goose breast (for garnish)
- Small sausages (for garnish)
- Parsley (for garnish)

## Directions

1. Cook whole vegetables in water with a little sugar
2. Make a sauce with butter and flour, diluted with meat broth or vegetable water with Maggi bouillon cubes and salt
3. Thicken with 2 well-beaten egg yolks mixed with a few spoonfuls of raw cream
4. Separate cauliflower into small pieces and cut other vegetables with a Chartreuse knife
5. Carefully turn vegetables in the sauce so they remain whole
6. Fill as hot as possible into the krustader
7. Garnish the plate with different things: thinly sliced smoked salmon rolled together and arranged in a ring around the krustader, thinly sliced smoked goose breast, or very small sausages
8. Decorate everything with parsley

title: Krydder-Eddike (Spiced Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece horseradish the length of a finger
- 1 garlic clove
- 1 dessertspoon whole white pepper
- 1 whole yellow mustard seed
- 1 teaspoon ground ginger
- a few fresh or dried bay leaves
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1 liter vinegar

## Directions

1. Scrape the horseradish and cut into smaller pieces.
2. Place the horseradish, garlic, pepper, mustard seed, ginger, bay leaves, salt, sugar, and vinegar together in a jar.
3. After about 10 days, strain the vinegar and use it in salad, hachis, ragout, and similar dishes.
4. Fresh vinegar can be poured over the spices for reuse.

title: Kræmmerhuse (Cone-Shaped Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg whites
- 1 pound finely powdered sugar
- 5 pounds flour
- 10 egg yolks
- whipped cream with vanilla sugar
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Whip the egg whites until stiff and stir in the finely powdered sugar, then the flour and finally the egg yolks, 2 at a time. Grease and butter the baking sheet carefully; with a spoon place the cakes on it, smooth well with a knife and bake light golden at moderate heat. Remove them from the sheet and form into cone shapes, which are left in a dry place. When they are to be served, fill them with whipped cream with vanilla sugar, and place a little currant jelly on each cone. On the dish place them with the points inward and the openings outward.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kræmmerhuse (Cream-Filled Cone Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg whites
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) Margarine
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) Powdered sugar (Melis)
- 140 Gr (28 Quintal) Flour (Flormel)
- 8 beaten egg whites
- Whipped cream
- Preserved strawberries, cherries, raspberry jam, currant or gooseberry jelly

## Directions

1. Melt the margarine; while hot, stir in the powdered sugar and flour, and finally 8 beaten egg whites.
2. The baking sheet must be warm; brush it well and place the dough on it with a teaspoon, with large intervals.
3. Spread the dough with a spoon into elongated cakes and bake quickly in a well-heated oven; they should have a beautiful golden-brown color.
4. When the cakes are finished but still in the oven, loosen them with a knife and twist into the form of a cone, which must be done very quickly, as the cakes otherwise break and cannot be bent once cooled.
5. Store in a tin box and fill with whipped cream just before serving, as they otherwise become soft. Preserved strawberries, cherry jam, raspberry jam, or currant or gooseberry jelly may be served alongside.

title: Kréme til Rødgrød og Frugtgrød (Cream for Red Fruit Compote and Fruit Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet milk
- vanilla
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon salep
- egg whites
- 1 tablespoon powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pot of sweet milk with a little vanilla, which is soon removed to avoid giving too strong a taste.
2. Whip 2 egg yolks and stir them in.
3. Dissolve 1 tablespoon of salep and whip it into the milk.
4. When the milk is almost cold, whip the egg whites together with 1 tablespoon of powdered sugar into foam and whip this into the milk.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kuller (Saithe)
body:
## Ingredients

- Saithe
- Water
- Salt
- Whole peppercorns
- Liver and roe
- Mustard sauce or sour butter sauce

## Directions

1. Scrape and clean the fish, cut into pieces
2. Cook the liver and roe with the fish
3. Place over heat in boiling water with a little salt and whole peppercorns
4. Cook over brisk heat
5. Serve with mustard sauce or sour butter sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kv eder (Quinces)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- One pound quinces (prepared)
- One pound sugar, white or brown
- One pint water

## Directions

1. Place the quinces in boiling water and cook until they begin to soften.
2. Remove, peel, and cut according to size into 4 or 8 pieces.
3. Remove the seed cavities and all the hard matter surrounding them; weigh the pieces.
4. For 1 pound quinces, take 1 pound sugar, white or brown, and place on heat with one pint water.
5. When the first scum is removed, add the quinces and skim well.
6. Cook evenly for one hour or longer, if needed, until they are completely tender and the syrup is even.
7. Pour into a vessel, spread cloth over, and after a day, cover with paper dipped in brandy and tie.

title: Kv eder Nr. 2 (Quinces No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces with peelings
- One pound peels
- One pint water
- One pound quinces (prepared)
- One pound white or brown sugar

## Directions

1. Dry the quinces, remove the peelings, and weigh them.
2. For 1 pound peelings, take 1 pint water; cook very evenly for one hour, then strain.
3. Cut the quinces into 4 to 8 pieces, remove the seed cavities and all hard matter, and weigh.
4. For 1 pound quinces, take 1 pound white or brown sugar and place in the water that was strained from the peelings.
5. When melted, add the quinces and cook covered over a good, even heat, not flame, stirring regularly until tender.
6. This may take a couple of hours.
7. By this even cooking under closed lid, the quinces will turn red; if not, a little cherry juice may be added.
8. Pour into a vessel and tie as usual.

title: Kvæde-Gelé (Quince Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg quinces
- Approximately 1/2 liter water per 1/2 kg fruit (or enough to cover level with the fruit in the pot)
- 1/2 kg sugar (or corresponding weight to juice)
- 1 1/4 dl water

## Directions

1. Dry the quinces and quarter or divide into 4 to 6 pieces, cutting away the blossom end.
2. Place them in the pot and cover with approximately 1/2 liter water per 1/2 kg fruit or with enough so it stands level with the quinces in the pot.
3. They must be completely cooked through before they are poured into the cloth.
4. When the juice has run through the next day, cook as instructed in No. 93.

title: Kvæde-Gelé (Quince Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg quince
- 625 gr sugar
- 0.5 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice

## Directions

1. Wipe the quinces and cut them into 4 to 6 pieces, removing the blossom end.
2. Place them in a pot and cover with approximately 0.5 l water for each 0.5 kg fruit, or with enough water so it reaches the level of the quinces in the pot.
3. They must be completely cooked before pouring into the jelly cloth.
4. When the juice has run through the following day, cook the jelly like all other varieties. (See description in No. 93.)

title: Kvæde-Kompot (Quince Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) Quince
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) powdered Melis sugar
- 2 1/2 Pint (1/2 Pægel) water from cooking the quince

## Directions

1. Cook the quinces until almost tender in water
2. Peel and cut into 4 pieces
3. Mix the powdered Melis sugar with 2 1/2 Pint (1/2 Pægel) of the water in which they were cooked and boil together
4. Cook the quince pieces in this syrup until completely tender
5. Remove the fruit and place in a serving bowl
6. Either boil the juice a bit longer or thicken slightly with a little sago flour and pour over the fruit

title: Kvæde-Marmelade (Quince Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe quinces
- Powdered sugar (575 gr to 1/2 kg cooked pulp)
- Water

## Directions

1. Wipe the ripe quinces dry and cut them into 4 to 6 pieces.
2. Put these in a pot with enough water so they can just float in it, and cook until completely soft.
3. Towards the end, stir regularly so that the pulp does not burn on the bottom.
4. Press the quinces through a coarse sieve, then weigh the strained pulp and put it back in the pot together with the sugar.
5. Cook the pulp with stirring, and the jam should, when it is finished cooking, be like a thick, concentrated mass.
6. Fill it into jars, cover with paper and tie up.

title: Kvæde-Suppe (Quince Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 kg quinces
- water
- sugar
- sago flour
- white wine or bottled rhubarb juice

## Directions

1. Wipe quinces, cut off blossom and stem, cut into smaller pieces
2. Rinse and cook until tender in water so they easily pass through a sieve
3. Bring the strained fruit soup to boil, sweeten and thicken with sago flour mixed with water
4. Season with white wine or a little bottled rhubarb juice

title: Kvædegelée (Quince Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pear quinces
- Kernels from the fruit
- Water
- Powdered sugar (equal weight to the strained juice)
- Cognac

## Directions

1. Scald and peel the pear quinces, then cut them into pieces
2. Cook the pieces together with the kernels in a little water until they are completely soft and can be mashed into a pulp
3. Pour them into a sieve so the juice can run off
4. Filter the juice through a jelly bag and weigh it
5. Cook the juice with an equal weight of powdered sugar until the jelly appears clear and thick
6. Skim it and pour it into jelly cups that have been previously moistened with Cognac
7. Treat it as described in recipe 474
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Kvæder med Cognac (Quinces with Cognac)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pear quinces
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Water
- Lemon peel
- Cinnamon
- Cognac

## Directions

1. Peel the pear quinces, remove the cores, and cut the fruit into 8 pieces, which are gradually placed in cold water.
2. Cook the quinces until partially tender in water; remove them, place on a sieve, and only when the water has well drained, pierce each piece with a thin strip of lemon peel and a small piece of cinnamon.
3. Weigh the fruit and cook the sugar to an even syrup with the measured amount of the water in which the quinces were cooked.
4. Pour the hot syrup over the fruit.
5. After a couple of days have passed, pour off the liquid, which has become considerably thinner, and cook it until it is again very even; then pour it into the jar over the quinces.
6. Finally pour in enough Cognac to cover the fruit. Can be sealed immediately.

title: Kvæder og Æbler (Quinces and Apples)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- Cooking apples
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. After the quinces are peeled, cut into pieces, and cores removed, weigh the fruit before placing it over the fire in enough boiling water to cover the fruit.
2. Cook the quinces until they begin to soften, but not until tender; then remove them on a colander.
3. Add the sugar to the water in which the fruit was cooked, and cook it to a syrup, in which the quinces are cooked until tender, but the pieces must remain whole.
4. After they are removed, some firm, peeled, cut cooking apples can be cooked tender in the liquid.
5. Both types are distributed in the jars. The apples will take on both the appearance and taste of the quinces.
6. If the liquid is not sufficiently even, it must be reduced a little more before pouring hot over the fruit.
7. Seal the jars when the preserve is finished.

title: Kvædesaft (Quince Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- 575 gr (1/4 lb) powdered sugar per 1 liter (1 pot) juice

## Directions

1. Dry the quinces and cut into very small pieces, cutting away the core.
2. Put them in a pot and pour on enough water so it is level with the fruit.
3. Boil the quinces until completely soft, then strain through a jelly cloth and let hang for several hours.
4. Measure the drained juice and boil with the sugar for a quarter hour.
5. Skim well during boiling; pour hot into dry bottles, which are tied with parchment paper as soon as they are filled.
6. The quince pulp left in the cloth is boiled in water and used for soup or similar dishes.
7. Quince syrup can be made into an excellent dessert by adding isinglass and filling into molds.

title: Kyllingepostei (Chicken Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pastry dough
- Butter
- a few slices of veal
- 2 fat chickens, cooked
- Boiled ham
- Parsley
- Egg

## Directions

1. Grease a mold with butter; layer in it first the slices of meat, then the chickens, cut as for fricassée, and between them the ham and parsley.
2. Cover the mold with dough, which is brushed with egg.
3. Bake in a hot oven for 1 hour and serve on the same mold.

title: Kyllinger med Cottin (Chickens with Cottin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickens
- Cottin mixture
- Chicken broth

## Directions

1. Chickens can be prepared like hen with a layer of cottin; or you can divide them so that the breast is cut out completely by itself, and when it is cooked, it is coated with cottin and roasted on plates in the oven until the cottin is roasted
2. The rest of the chicken is then used only for broth for the sauce
3. However, this is very wasteful, and one should at least take the legs with it, placing them with the thick end against the narrow end, and with cottin over
4. Chickens can otherwise be used for fricassee, curry and with white sauce like hen

title: Kyllingesteg (Roasted Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Parsley finely chopped
- Cold fresh butter
- Brown butter for sauce

## Directions

1. Scald the chicken in hot water so the feathers come off easily; place them immediately in lukewarm water when they are opened
2. To remove the small pointed breast bone that gives the chicken a lean appearance when roasted, insert your hand into the chicken with the thumb on the outside of the breast; press on the breast bone and a small triangle of it will give way immediately and turn inward when removed
3. Turn the head back under one wing
4. Place the giblets and heart under the other wing; then insert a pin through one wing and fasten it
5. Press the legs back into the skin and turn the bone back from the knee joints; insert a pin through these as well so they stay in this position
6. Fill with finely chopped parsley mixed with cold fresh butter
7. Roast best in a pot because it has no fat, so it can easily become too dry in a roasting oven if not constantly basted
8. For sauce use only brown butter
9. Can be roasted in about one hour

title: Kys (Chocolate-Topped Cream Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ingredients for water cakes (Nr. 1117)
- Vanilla cream (Nr. 1060)
- Chocolate glaze

## Directions

1. Bake as Water cakes (Nr. 1117), open and fill with Vanilla cream (Nr. 1060).
2. Brush the cakes with Chocolate glaze.

title: Kærligheds-Kranse (Love Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 375 grams (1/4 pound) flour
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) margarine
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) powdered sugar
- Vanilla essence
- Egg for dipping
- Sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Crumble the hard-boiled egg yolks with the flour
2. Knead in the margarine, powdered sugar, and vanilla essence
3. Roll the dough into a thin rod and form small rings
4. Dip them in egg and sugar
5. Place them not too close together on the baking sheet

title: Kærnemælk-Suppe (Buttermilk Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4.5 liters buttermilk
- 2.5 deciliters rice flour
- citrus peel
- whole cinnamon
- scalded raisins or currants
- 3 egg yolks
- sugar
- crushed almonds (optional)
- whipped cream and strawberry or raspberry jam (optional)

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with some of the buttermilk in the bottom of the pot
2. Add the remaining cold buttermilk with citrus peel, cinnamon, and scalded raisins or currants
3. Place over heat and stir continuously from the start, as the mixture will separate if not stirred constantly
4. Beat 3 egg yolks with sugar in a tureen
5. Pour the soup into the egg mixture
6. Serve immediately
7. Optional: add finely crushed almonds
8. Optional: serve separately with whipped cream and strawberry or raspberry jam, omitting the raisins and currants

title: Kærnemælk-Suppe med Maizena (Buttermilk Soup with Cornstarch)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- buttermilk
- cornstarch
- powdered sugar
- egg yolks
- vanilla essence

## Directions

1. For each liter of buttermilk, use one good tablespoon of cornstarch
2. Mix the cornstarch in some of the cold milk
3. Pour under stirring into the remaining boiling milk
4. Bring to a boil
5. Stir in a tureen where 1 egg yolk per liter of milk has been mixed with powdered sugar
6. Add a little vanilla essence
7. Optional: the soup can be served cold as a dessert after a meat course, with whipped cream folded in, and strawberry or raspberry jam and small macaroons served alongside

title: Kærnemælks-Grød med Maizena (Buttermilk Porridge with Maizena)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L buttermilk
- 1½ packages maizena
- 4 egg yolks
- ¼ kg powdered sugar
- 4 egg whites
- salt
- orange essence or vanilla essence
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix 4 L buttermilk with 1½ packages maizena and bring to a boil while stirring.
2. Let the porridge boil for a few minutes.
3. Remove the pot from heat.
4. Beat 4 egg yolks with ¼ kg powdered sugar and then blend with the 4 beaten egg whites; stir this into the boiling porridge.
5. Season with salt and a little orange essence or vanilla essence.
6. Serve immediately with butter, sugar and cinnamon.

title: Kød med glaserede Løg (Meat with Glazed Onions)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cut meat
- Brown stock sauce
- Glazed onions (see No. 671)
- Potato croquettes (see No. 14)

## Directions

1. Warm the cut meat in a brown stock sauce
2. Place on a hot, flat dish
3. Garnish with glazed onions (see No. 671) and potato croquettes (see No. 14)
