title: Kød med Æbler (Meat with Apples)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Sliced cooked or roasted meat
- Butter
- Apples, peeled, cored, and sliced
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Brown the sliced meat in butter in a pan
2. Arrange on a flat serving dish
3. Add more butter to the pan
4. Fry slices of peeled and cored apples
5. If the apples are very sour, sprinkle with a little sugar while frying
6. Place the apples in the center of the dish
7. Serve browned butter with the meat

title: Kød-Boller i brun Sauce, garneret med overskaarne Æg (Meatballs in Brown Sauce, Garnished with Sliced Eggs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Sinews and trimmings from meat used for forcemeat
- salt
- water
- browned butter
- 1 large grated onion
- paprika
- flour
- stock from the meat
- wine
- food coloring
- tomato purée
- Maggi bouillon cubes

## Directions

1. Boil the sinews and other trimmings from the meat in salted water
2. When strained and brought to a boil again, form oval-shaped balls with a spoon
3. To make good balls, ensure they do not boil longer than necessary, which depends on the size of the balls, as the forcemeat otherwise easily loses its juiciness and becomes hard and dry
4. Make the sauce from browned butter, add a large grated onion, paprika and flour; thin with the stock and add wine, food coloring and a little tomato purée
5. Pour the sauce over the balls
6. Since the sauce for the balls should be strong and piquant, use Maggi bouillon cubes to improve it

title: Kød-Boller i Selleri-Sauce (Meatballs in Celery Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- water or stock
- meatballs made from forcemeat
- butter
- flour
- strained stock
- 2 egg yolks
- ground white pepper
- 1 glass sherry

## Directions

1. Peel the celery, rinse well and cut into small cubes
2. Boil the celery cubes in water or stock
3. When the celery is cooked, remove it
4. Boil the meatballs in the stock
5. For the sauce, brown butter and flour, thin with the strained stock and bind at the end with a couple of egg yolks; add ground white pepper and a glass of sherry
6. Serve with puff pastry strips

title: Kød-Budding med Asparges (Meat Pudding with Asparagus)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cold butter
- prepared forcemeat
- cooked asparagus

## Directions

1. Butter a pudding mold well with cold butter
2. Layer the prepared forcemeat alternately with cooked asparagus
3. The top and bottom layer must be forcemeat, which can be made of beef, veal or pork
4. Place the pudding mold in boiling water in a pot over even heat, keeping it at a boil
5. As the water reduces, continually replenish with boiling water; but ensure the water does not rise too high so that it seeps into the pudding
6. The pudding can be cooked without coals on the lid
7. It can also be cooked in the oven in a pudding or cake mold by placing it in boiling water in a roasting pan
8. The pudding can cook for 2 hours, but if placed in a good oven, it needs only 1 hour
9. Serve with asparagus sauce

title: Kød-Budding med Blomkaal (Meat Pudding with Cauliflower)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared forcemeat
- half-cooked cauliflower
- butter

## Directions

1. Treat as for meat pudding with asparagus, layering half-cooked cauliflower in bouquets with the forcemeat
2. When the pudding is turned out, it can be further decorated with a wreath of cooked cauliflower
3. Serve with melted butter

title: Kød-Budding med Makaroni (Meat Pudding with Macaroni)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked macaroni
- melted butter
- prepared forcemeat

## Directions

1. Pour melted butter over cooked macaroni and layer with forcemeat as for meat pudding with asparagus
2. Serve with lemon sauce or chutney sauce

title: Kød-Frikadeller (Meat Patties)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat, firmer than for meatballs
- Flour or breadcrumbs
- Clarified butter

## Directions

1. Form the ground meat mixture with a spoon and shape in flour or breadcrumbs, or place directly on the pan and smooth with the spoon
2. Fry the patties in clarified butter over low heat so they do not brown too much before cooking through
3. Serve with browned butter

title: Kød-Jærpe (Meat Jerpe)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg pork
- 375 g fresh bacon
- ginger
- pepper
- salt
- 6-8 boiled potatoes, grated
- 2-3 eggs
- cream or good milk
- soup made from bones and trimmings

## Directions

1. Mince the meat very finely together with the bacon.
2. Season with ginger, pepper, and salt.
3. Knead the mixture by hand or with a spoon.
4. Add 6-8 grated boiled potatoes and 2-3 eggs.
5. Add enough cream or good milk to achieve a proper consistency for forming into a fillet shape.
6. Place in a roasting pan, give it an oblong shape, and roast in a hot oven for one hour.
7. Baste frequently with soup made from bones and trimmings.
8. Make sauce from browned butter and flour thinned with gravy.
9. Slice the roast and garnish with warm potato salad.

title: Kød-Kage med kogte Porrer (Meat Cake with Cooked Leeks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Palmin or margarine
- Ground meat
- Cooked leeks of even length
- Browned butter
- Cooked or browned potatoes

## Directions

1. Melt the palmin or margarine on a frying pan
2. Spread a layer of ground meat over the pan
3. Before the meat is cooked on the underside, arrange a star of evenly long cooked leeks on top with their root ends pointing toward the edge
4. Turn the cake onto a lid, fry on the other side
5. Serve with browned butter and cooked or browned potatoes

title: Kød-Rand med Gemyse (Meat Loaf with Vegetables)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) ground meat for the loaf
- Butter
- Vegetables, either stewed or sautéed in butter
- Mushrooms, sautéed in butter
- Spicy brown gravy sauce

## Directions

1. Butter a loaf mold well and fill it with the ground meat
2. Place the mold upside down in a pot of boiling water, ensuring the water does not come over the top, or place it in the oven in a roasting pan with water
3. Cook for 20 to 25 minutes
4. Arrange vegetables in the center of the turned-out loaf, or fill with sautéed mushrooms
5. Serve with spicy brown gravy sauce

title: Kød-Rand med glaserede Løg (Meat Loaf with Glazed Onions)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat
- Butter
- Small glazed onions
- Cooked macaroni
- Sharp brown sauce
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Turtle sauce

## Directions

1. Butter a loaf mold and fill it about halfway with ground meat
2. Press small glazed onions into the meat
3. Cook the loaf, turn it out, and arrange it
4. Fill with cooked macaroni and serve with sharp brown sauce
5. Alternatively, arrange hard-boiled eggs the same way and serve with turtle sauce
6. Chestnuts may be used instead of macaroni

title: Kød-Rand med Porrer (Meat Loaf with Leeks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Ground meat
- Leeks cut to the height of the mold
- Water and salt
- Stirred or melted butter or brown spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Butter a loaf mold and fill it about halfway with ground meat
2. Cut leeks to the height of the mold and give them a good boil in salted water
3. Insert the leeks root-end down with 2.5 centimeters (1 inch) spacing
4. Cook the loaf as usual
5. Serve with stirred or melted butter or a brown spicy sauce

title: Kødbudding (Meat Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat farce
- Butter
- Green vegetables
- Browned butter
- White sauce
- Root vegetables
- Asparagus tips
- Oysters (optional)

## Directions

1. The farce for pudding should be softer than for meatballs.
2. Grease the mold with butter; there should be at least 3 inches lacking to make it full so the pudding can rise during cooking.
3. It needs about an hour of cooking.
4. Serve it either with green vegetables and browned butter sauce, or also use white sauce with white root vegetables, asparagus tips and, if desired, oysters.

title: Kødbudding med Makkaroni og Skinke (Meat Pudding with Macaroni and Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound macaroni
- Thin bouillon
- Smoked ham
- Meat farce
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cook 0.5 pound macaroni in just enough thin bouillon to barely cover it until tender, but not overcooked.
2. Cook smoked ham well and it should not be too salty.
3. When cold, thickly line a pudding mold with meat farce.
4. Layer small slices of the lean part of the ham and the macaroni with their sauce, then meat farce over.
5. You can either stir a little rinsed butter into the macaroni while hot, or leave a little of the fat on the ham pieces as desired.
6. It must cook for at least 3 hours.

title: Kødfarce med Vildt (Meat Farce with Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridge backs and wings
- Partridge thighs
- Partridge breast
- Raw meat farce
- Sharp brown sauce

## Directions

1. Take the backs and wings from the partridges, and from the thighs cut off the lower joint. Divide the breast into 4 pieces.
2. When they are well roasted and cold again, place raw meat farce in a sheet metal dish, then place a layer of the roasted birds, then again meat farce.
3. Roast it in a roasting oven until the farce is roasted.
4. Serve with sharp brown sauce.
5. The birds can also be stuffed inside with meat farce.

title: Kødpølser (Meat Sausages)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg (3.3 lbs) meat sausage
- Water
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the fresh or lightly salted sausages in water for approximately 10 minutes.
2. Serve decorated with parsley and eat with stewed vegetables and various types of cabbage, Russian peas, etc.
3. The water in which they are cooked should not be discarded but used for soup.

title: Kørvel-Suppe (Chervil Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 L (5 pots) stock
- 5-6 tablespoons chopped chervil
- 125 g (1/4 lb) butter
- 125 g (1/4 lb) flour

## Directions

1. Bake 125 g (1/4 lb) butter and 125 g (1/4 lb) flour together and thin with the stock.
2. When it boils, add the shelled and finely chopped chervil, which is given just one boil.
3. New carrots and asparagus, cooked in some of the stock, can be added, then mixed with the remaining soup.

title: Labskews (Labskaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover roast, ham, and soup meat
- Boiled potatoes
- Onions
- Butter
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Peppercorns
- Bay leaves
- Worcestershire sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut leftover meat into cubes.
2. Cut boiled potatoes into pieces.
3. Peel and chop onions.
4. Mix together equal amounts of meat cubes, potato pieces, and chopped onions.
5. Brown the onions in butter in a casserole.
6. Add the meat and fry for approximately 5 minutes.
7. Add the potatoes and a little bouillon with salt, whole peppercorns, and a few bay leaves.
8. Cook everything together until it forms an even mass.
9. Before serving, stir in a lump of butter.
10. A little Worcestershire sauce can be added for a sharper taste.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Lady Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- ½ pound butter
- 1 pound flour
- whites of 16 eggs
- 1½ teaspoons soda
- 1½ teaspoons cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Stir the butter and sugar together first
2. Then stir in the egg whites and finally the flour, sifted twice, with soda and cream of tartar

title: Lady's-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams fine sugar
- 12 whole eggs
- 500 grams margarine
- 65 grams finely chopped or ground sweet almonds
- 35 grams finely chopped or ground bitter almonds
- 500 grams Pillsbury flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix fine sugar, eggs, margarine, and almonds for at least 15 minutes
2. Stir in Pillsbury flour and baking powder
3. Pour batter into a greased form and bake at good heat for about 1 hour

title: Lag-Kage (Layer Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 124 grams (37 quintals) margarine
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) powdered sugar
- 8 eggs
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) flour
- 5 grams (1 quintal) deer salt
- A few drops of lemon oil
- Butter for greasing paper
- Cream (see recipe 1120)
- Jam, if desired
- Water glaze
- Piped glaze and jelly for decoration

## Directions

1. Heat the margarine in the bowl where the dough will be mixed
2. Stir in the powdered sugar and eggs, and finally add the flour, deer salt, and a few drops of lemon oil
3. Spread the dough on 4 sheets of buttered and cut paper according to the size of the pan, and place on baking sheets
4. Bake the cakes quite pale, and as soon as they are done, remove the paper
5. Stack them on top of each other with cream between the layers and jam if desired
6. Trim the edges of the cake, glaze with water glaze, dry slightly in the oven, and decorate with piped glaze and jelly

title: Lag-Omelet (Layered Omelet)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g (1/4 S) margarine
- 125 g (1/4 S) Pillsbury flour
- 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) boiling milk
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites
- jam
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Bake the margarine and flour together and dilute with 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) of boiling milk.
2. When the batter has cooled, stir it with 6 egg yolks and finally the beaten egg whites.
3. Bake the batter in 3 thick pancakes with a layer of jam between them. Sprinkle the top with powdered sugar.

title: Lag-Omelet (Layered Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds butter
- 0.5 pound flour
- 1 pint milk
- Preserved fruit or jam
- White sugar

## Directions

1. Bake butter and flour until it leaves the spoon
2. While still over heat, stir in milk
3. Finally fold in beaten egg whites
4. Grease pan with cold butter
5. Divide batter into 3 parts and bake as 3 thick pancakes
6. Spread jam between each layer
7. Sprinkle white sugar on top
8. Serves about 8 persons
9. Serve warm with powdered sugar

title: Lage (Brine)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 quarts water
- 3 pounds coarse salt
- 0.5 pound powdered sugar
- 3 ounces saltpetre

## Directions

1. Place water over heat and add saltpetre first, as it dissolves most easily
2. Then add the rest and bring the whole to a boil, then skim and pour out
3. When cold, all kinds of meat and flesh can be preserved in it and do not need to be leached before cooking

title: Lage-Agurker (Brined Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- soft water
- dill
- pepper
- horseradish
- cherry leaves and vine leaves
- salt
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the cucumbers in clean water, changing it every other hour.
2. After 6 hours, remove them and place them in a jar layered with dill, pepper, horseradish, and cherry and vine leaves.
3. Preferably place the leaves between the cucumbers so they do not touch each other.
4. Cook a brine of soft water with 30 parts salt and 1 gill wine vinegar added for each pot.
5. When it is cold, pour it over the cucumbers.
6. Immediately place a lid with a weight on them, then tie them and place them in a dry cellar.
7. They can also be stored in a wooden vessel, preferably made of oak.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Lage-Agurker (Pickled Cucumbers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized, long cucumbers without seeds
- Dill
- Bay leaves
- Grape leaves
- Unripe, green grapes (if available)
- Vinegar (1/4 liter per liter of water)
- Water
- Kitchen salt

## Directions

1. Choose medium-sized, long cucumbers without seeds rather than small, thick ones.
2. Rinse in lukewarm water and dry thoroughly.
3. Line the bottom of a barrel or jar with dill, bay leaves, grape leaves, and if available, unripe green grapes.
4. Layer cucumbers on top, continuing in layers.
5. Make a brine of 1/4 liter vinegar to each liter of water, bring to a boil, and dissolve enough kitchen salt to make a very strong brine.
6. Only when the brine is completely cold, pour it over the cucumbers, which should be covered by it.
7. Spread a cloth over the barrel, place a perforated wooden bottom fitting the size on top, and lastly a light press to push the cucumbers down.
8. For the first couple of weeks, place the barrel in a warm location until fermentation begins, then move to a cooler place.
9. The cucumbers are ready to eat only when they become clear.

title: Lagkage (Layer Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 0.5 pound fine Hungarian flour
- Zest of 1 large lemon
- Juice of 1 large lemon
- Egg whites
- White wine cream
- Sugar glaze

## Directions

1. Stir 1 pound butter to a salve and add 1 pound powdered sugar, stirring for a good half hour until white
2. Stir for half an hour with 8 egg yolks until quite light
3. Add 1/2 pound fine Hungarian flour and the grated yellow zest and strained juice of 1 large lemon
4. Finally add the egg whites after whipping to stiff peaks
5. Roll out this batter and bake into 8 sheets, either in 8 special forms or in 8 paper forms; in both cases butter the forms and the oven should be very hot
6. When the layers are baked a fine pale brown, remove from the oven and set to cool
7. The given amount is for 2 cakes, as each uses 4 layers, between which white wine cream is placed (vanilla cream easily becomes sour)
8. Decorate on top with sugar glaze
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Laks i Karry-Sauce (Salmon in Curry Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover cooked salmon
- Butter
- Flour
- Curry
- Fish stock
- Cooked rice
- Dry curry

## Directions

1. Cut leftover cooked salmon into smaller pieces
2. Warm the pieces in a curry sauce made from butter, flour, curry and diluted with fish stock
3. Arrange a border of loose cooked rice around the edge of the dish
4. Sprinkle the rice with a little dry curry

title: Lamme-Frikassé (Lamb Fricassée)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb meat
- Water
- Salt
- Bouquet garni
- Peppercorns
- Onions
- Flour
- Butter
- Root vegetables for soup
- Green peas
- Cabbage (optional)

## Directions

1. Make as Calf's Fricassée (recipe 140); but to it is usually added a little fresh, cooked and chopped root vegetables such as carrots and parsley roots, as well as shelled peas.
2. The meat can also be prepared as Cabbage Fricassée. Cut the meat into pieces, and each piece is smeared with a little butter, then laid in layers in a pot.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Lamme-Frikassé med Blomkål (Lamb Fricassée with Cauliflower)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb forequarter
- cooked cauliflower
- butter
- flour
- salt
- parsley
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Around the edge of the dish place a wreath of cooked cauliflower divided into small florets.
2. Chopped parsley can be stirred into the sauce.
3. It does not need to be thickened, but tastes best if it is.

title: Lamme-Frikassé med Fars (Lamb Fricassée with Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb fricassée
- fish or veal forcemeat
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- parsley

## Directions

1. When the fricassée is prepared, pour it onto a flat dish and cover with a good layer of fish or veal forcemeat, which is brushed with egg and sprinkled with breadcrumbs.
2. Bake in the oven at high heat until the forcemeat is light brown and cooked through, and garnish afterwards with parsley.

title: Lamme-Frikassé med Grønærter (Lamb Fricassée with Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb forequarter
- cooked peas
- butter
- flour
- salt
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Prepare as the preceding recipe, with the difference that cooked peas are added to the sauce instead of shrimp and asparagus.

title: Lamme-Frikassé med Rejer og Asparges (Lamb Fricassée with Shrimp and Asparagus)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Kg (6 S) lamb forequarter
- 1 Kg (2 S) asparagus
- 1 L (1 Pot) unpeeled shrimp
- Salt
- Broth bundle

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into suitably small pieces, wash in cold water and blanch it.
2. Place it over heat in cold water with salt and blanch it and cook with a good broth bundle.
3. When it is tender, strain off some of the broth, the rest stays with the meat so it can be kept warm.
4. Peel the asparagus and cut it into about 3 centimeter (1 inch) long pieces.

title: Lamme-Frikassé med Rejer og Asparges (Lamb Fricassée with Shrimp and Asparagus)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg lamb forequarter
- 1 kg asparagus
- 1 liter unpeeled shrimp
- salt
- butter
- flour
- 3-4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into appropriately small pieces, rinse in cold water and blanch.
2. Place over heat in cold water with salt, skim and cook with a good soup whisk; when tender, some of the broth is drained off, the rest remains with the meat so it can be kept warm.
3. Peel the asparagus and cut into approximately 3 centimeter long pieces, which are cooked in water with salt.
4. Brown butter and flour and thin with broth mixed with asparagus water into a smooth sauce, which is thickened with 3-4 egg yolks.
5. At serving, arrange the asparagus and the cooked, peeled shrimp between the meat, and pour the sauce over.

title: Lamme-Koteletter (Lamb Chops)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lamb chops per person
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Often 2 bones are taken so that the pieces do not become too small; during preparation one is cut away.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and fry like veal chops, but not as long.
3. Arrange stewed spinach in the center of the dish and place the chops around it on edge.
4. Practically all vegetables go well with this.
5. They can also be served with gooseberry compote and cucumber salad or head lettuce, prepared with cream or with oil, vinegar, and pepper.

title: Lamme-Koteletter (Lamb Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb chops with bone
- Ground pepper
- Fine salt
- Butter
- Bread crumbs
- Green peas, spinach or potato purée

## Directions

1. Cut the chop piece from the breast, and each chop must have a bone.
2. Give the chops a blow with a meat mallet; sprinkle with ground pepper and fine salt, dip in butter and turn in grated bread, then fry them golden brown in butter.
3. Arrange them in a ring on the platter and serve with green peas, spinach or potato purée alongside.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Lamme-Koteletter med Asparges og Rejer (Lamb Chops with Asparagus and Shrimp)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg asparagus
- 1 L unpeeled shrimp
- lamb chops

## Directions

1. Arrange the chops around the stewed shrimp and asparagus.

title: Lamme-Koteletter med Hønse-Ragout (Lamb Chops with Chicken Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- good chicken ragout
- lamb chops

## Directions

1. Place a good chicken ragout in the center of the dish and arrange the chops around it. (Chops of pork tenderloin with macaroni bones can be substituted).

title: Lamme-Lever (Lamb's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb's liver, cut into fairly thick pieces
- Flour or breadcrumbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter or good frying fat
- Gravy
- Cream
- Butter for sauce
- Vegetables: turnip greens, gooseberry sauce or rhubarb compote

## Directions

1. Treat as calf's liver and cut into fairly thick pieces.
2. Lay them spread out so they are not too wet, then turn them in flour or breadcrumbs mixed with pepper and salt.
3. Fry on a pan in butter or good frying fat.
4. Make the sauce as for calf's liver but without onions.
5. You can cut the fat from raw smoked bacon in large cubes, fry it along with the liver and place it on the platter with it.
6. This requires less butter and fat for frying the liver.
7. Serve with vegetables, preferably with turnip greens, gooseberry sauce or rhubarb compote.

title: Lamme-Steg (Roast Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half of a slaughtered lamb
- Salt
- Butter
- Water
- Parsley
- White writing paper
- Flour
- Potatoes
- Salad

## Directions

1. While lambs are small, it does not matter if you choose meat from a young ewe or wether lamb; but later the latter often acquire a less pleasant woolly taste, so the former are to be preferred.
2. For roasting, choose the loin with attached back.
3. Remove the shank, break the bones and back, then beat the roast lightly with a meat mallet, rub with salt and lard with fine parsley.
4. Place it in a warm oven in a pan with salt, butter and a little water.
5. Lay a piece of white writing paper, dipped in butter or water, over the roast.
6. When the roast has cooked somewhat and the heat is less strong, remove the paper and baste it with the sauce.
7. Roasting takes 1 to 2 hours depending on the size of the roast, and you must be careful that it does not burn.
8. Skim the fat from the sauce, thicken it with a little flour, strain and serve to the roast together with potatoes and salad.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Lammebryst i Grønnærter (Lamb Breast with Green Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ to 3 Kg (5 to 6 S) lamb meat
- ½ Skp. peas
- Salt
- Butter
- Sugar
- Small boiled carrots for garnish

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into smaller pieces as for fricassée.
2. After washing them, scald them in boiling water and place them in a pot.
3. Pour 1 L (1 Pot) boiling water over the meat with a little salt, and only when the broth is completely skimmed, add the peas.
4. Cook the meat with the peas tender under a lid and over gentle heat, but it must be constantly watched so the water does not evaporate and the meat and peas burn on.
5. If the liquid shrinks away, pour a little more broth or water into the pot.
6. The meat must be stirred to become tender, so that the top pieces come to the bottom of the pot.
7. When it is nearly done, add 125 Gr (¼ S) butter along with salt and sugar, if the peas are not sufficiently sweet in themselves.
8. Serve meat and peas together on a platter, and the dish can be garnished with small boiled carrots.

title: Lammebryst å l'anglaise (Lamb Breast à l'anglaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb breast with brisket
- Egg
- Bread
- Pepper
- Salt
- Palmin or lard
- Broth
- Mushrooms
- Parsley
- Puff pastry slices

## Directions

1. When the breast with brisket is cooked tender, brush it with egg, sprinkle with bread, pepper and salt, and brown it on a pan in abundant Palmin or lard.
2. Make a brown mushroom sauce from the broth (see Nr. 758), which is served separately.
3. Cut the meat and garnish with parsley and mushrooms, and place puff pastry slices around it.

title: Lammebryst å la Marengo (Lamb Breast à la Marengo)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ to 3 Kg (5 to 6 S) lamb forequarter
- Butter
- Salt
- Paprika
- 1 L (1 Pot) broth, soup or water
- 1¼ Dl (½ Pægl) Madeira
- 6 to 8 tablespoons tomato purée
- Coloring
- Fresh or preserved mushrooms
- Sago flour
- Puff pastry slices for garnish

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces as for fricassée and wash well in cold water.
2. On a roasting pan, brown the meat in a good slice of butter, then place in a pot, sprinkle with salt and paprika and pour over 1 L (1 Pot) broth, soup or water, 1¼ Dl (½ Pægl) Madeira, 6 to 8 tablespoons tomato purée and coloring so the broth gets a nice color.
3. Cook the meat in this until tender; then add fresh or preserved mushrooms and give it a good boil in the sauce, which is thickened with sago flour.
4. Serve the meat in the sauce and garnish with puff pastry slices.

title: Lammekjodsuppe (Lamb Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb meat
- Water
- Bones from mutton or preferably beef

## Directions

1. Prepare similarly to veal broth, as lamb also has a weak flavor and benefits from the addition of mutton or beef bones.

title: Lammelever (Lamb Liver)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb liver (1 liver serves 3-4 people)
- Flour
- Egg
- Bread crumbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter or lard
- Browned butter
- Boiled new potatoes
- Gooseberry compote, cucumber salad, or lettuce salad

## Directions

1. Soak the liver in water for ½ hour so the blood drains out well.
2. Remove the membrane and cut the liver into thin slices.
3. Dip in flour, then in egg and bread crumbs mixed with salt and pepper.
4. Fry until crispy and brown in butter or lard.
5. Serve with browned butter and newly boiled potatoes, along with either gooseberry compote, cucumber salad, or lettuce salad. Most vegetables are suitable as accompaniments.

title: Lammelever stegt som Bøf (Lamb Liver Fried as Steak)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb liver, cut into slices
- Flour
- Salt
- Pepper
- Palmin or butter

## Directions

1. Dip the cut liver slices in flour mixed with salt and pepper.
2. Fry in Palmin or butter.
3. Make sauce as for calf liver.

title: Lammeryg (Lamb Loin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb loin from a large lamb
- Bacon or lard for larding (optional)
- Parsley for larding (optional)
- Butter, margarine, or Palmin
- Salt
- Gravy, soup, or water

## Directions

1. From a large lamb, remove the neck, front ribs, legs, and enough of the ribs so that the loin has an even and attractive shape.
2. The lamb loin can be larded with bacon and roasted like veal loin (Nr. 839) or larded with parsley and roasted like lamb roast.
3. Alternatively, it can simply be covered with small pieces of butter, margarine, or Palmin and sprinkled with salt and placed in a well-heated oven.
4. After 10 minutes, pour gravy, soup, or water over it, with which it is basted during roasting, which lasts about one hour.
5. When serving, loosen the meat along the center of the loin and cut it from the bones, then cut into smaller pieces which are reassembled on the loin.
6. Garnish with cooked vegetables and decorate with salad or parsley.

title: Lammeryg à la Russe (Lamb Loin à la Russe)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb loin
- Butter or fat
- Parsley for garnish
- Potato croquettes (Nr. 14)
- Reduced gravy from roasting
- Sago flour (optional for thickening)
- Maitre d'hôtel or chutney butter

## Directions

1. Prepare a lamb loin covered with butter or fat and roast it as described in the previous recipe.
2. When ready to serve, carve the meat as indicated above and decorate with parsley and garnish with a ring of potato croquettes.
3. Serve the reduced gravy from the roasting as sauce, which, if necessary, can be thickened with a little sago flour.
4. Along with the gravy, serve maitre d'hôtel or chutney butter, which is stirred into the sauce.

title: Lammesteg (Roast Lamb)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.5 kg lamb hindquarter with kidney piece
- parsley
- butter
- salt
- water
- flour
- cream

## Directions

1. The hindquarter with kidney piece is generally what is used for roast lamb, and to have the proper form, it should be cut so that 3 of the ribs follow with the roast.
2. These ribs are given a break on the inside with an axe, so the roast can lie well on the dish.
3. The shank is cut off, and the back is broken on the underside at appropriate intervals to ease carving, but not so deeply that the back comes apart.
4. With a pointed knife, holes are pierced along the back and leg, into which parsley is inserted together with a little butter; some of the parsley must be visible over the meat.
5. On the roasting pan, which is placed in the hot oven, a little meat gravy or butter is laid before the roast is placed on it.
6. The roast is sprinkled with salt and must stand thus for about 10 minutes; then boiling water is poured on the pan, and the roast is basted well.
7. Usually one counts 1.5 hours of roasting for a lamb roast of 2.5 kg.
8. For the sauce, butter is browned, which is baked with flour, thinned with the strained drippings with the fat skimmed off.
9. The sauce must not be too dark, and after it has boiled well, a little raw cream and a slice of cold butter can be added, but this is not necessary.
10. Roast lamb can be served garnished with a bouquet of parsley, salad or flowers, pierced with a skewer, which is fastened in the shank joint at the tail part.

title: Lammesteg med Fyldning (Lamb Roast with Stuffing)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb shoulder
- Parsley, picked and rinsed
- Butter
- Bread dough (Nr. 492)
- Ground almonds (optional)
- Currants (optional)
- Cardamom (optional)

## Directions

1. If the shoulder remains attached to the roast, fill it either with parsley that has been picked and rinsed with a piece of butter added, or with a bread dough which may be seasoned to taste with ground almonds, currants, and a small amount of cardamom.
2. Open the shoulder on one side enough to get your hand between the membranes to loosen the outer layer.
3. If there is a hole in the membrane, it must be carefully sewn up before filling is inserted.
4. Sew tightly before the dough, as if too much is filled, the membrane will easily burst.
5. Sprinkle the roast with parsley and roast as described.
6. When carving, ensure that some of the stuffing is cut and served along with the meat.

title: Lammesteg med Fyldning (Roast Lamb with Stuffing)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb hindquarter
- parsley
- butter
- breadcrumb dough (Nr. 492)
- almonds (optional)
- currants (optional)
- cardamom (optional)

## Directions

1. When the flap remains with the roast, it is filled either with parsley that has been picked and rinsed, and into which a piece of butter has been added, or with a breadcrumb dough (see Nr. 492), which can optionally be mixed with ground almonds, currants and a small amount of cardamom.
2. The flap is opened on one side as needed.

title: Langen (Ling)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ling fish
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Palmin (cooking fat)
- Browned butter
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Split the fish which is bought in flayed condition
2. Remove the bone and cut out the two long, meaty fillets into smaller pieces
3. Pound the very thick pieces with a meat mallet
4. Sprinkle the fish generously with salt and let it stand for half an hour
5. Rinse and dry the fish
6. Sprinkle with pepper
7. Dip it in egg and breadcrumbs
8. Fry it thoroughly in Palmin
9. Serve with browned butter and lemon

title: Larded Veal Cutlets
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Thin bacon strips
- Butter
- Brown gravy
- Vegetables, asparagus, or peas
- Parsley or salad

## Directions

1. After the cutlets are formed, lard them with very fine bacon strips.
2. Brown them on the pan in butter and drip frequently with a little good brown gravy to obtain a nice brown glaze.
3. Place vegetables, asparagus, or peas in the center of the platter.
4. Arrange the cutlets around them, garnished with parsley or salad.

title: Lax og Laxeørred (Salmon and Sea Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon or sea trout
- Cold water
- Salt
- Bay leaves (a pair)
- Peppercorns
- Parsley
- Boiled potatoes, freshly peeled
- Sour butter sauce or caper sauce

## Directions

1. Rinse well with cold water
2. Scrape along the scales from head toward tail with a knife that is not too sharp
3. Rinse the fish again
4. Remove the innards and bile very carefully
5. For a large fish, lay it on the kitchen table with back outward along the table's edge, head to the left
6. Cut the tail at the small hole visible on the belly
7. With a large sharp knife that is rounded like a common butcher's knife, cut the fish open along the back so the knife follows along the backbone; also cut through the head
8. Remove the innards, being especially careful the bile bladder does not break
9. The roe can be used for false caviar
10. Rub the whole salmon inside with coarse salt and rinse if it is to be cooked
11. Cut into pieces and place in boiling water with salt, a pair of bay leaves, and peppercorns
12. Cook until tender over fairly brisk heat, then remove the pot from heat and let stand without cooking on the stovetop for about 10 minutes
13. Arrange on a platter with skin side up and can be garnished with parsley and freshly peeled boiled potatoes
14. Serve with sour butter sauce or caper sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Lemon Pie No. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- Pie dough
- Zest of 2 lemons
- 1 grated apple
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 small lump melted butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 1 small cup hot water
- Beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Line the pie form with ordinary pie dough.
2. Grate together the zest of 2 lemons with a grated apple.
3. Stir together 2 egg yolks with 1 cup sugar, a small lump of melted butter, 2 tablespoons flour, and the juice of 2 lemons.
4. Stir in 1 small cup of hot water and beat the whole mixture well.
5. Pour the mixture onto the dough.
6. Bake as you would an egg pie: bake, brush with beaten egg whites, and set in the oven again.

title: Lemon Pie No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- Pie dough
- Zest of 1 lemon
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 1 cup sugar
- Yolk of 3 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 1 tablespoon corn starch
- Beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix well together the grated zest and juice of 1 lemon with 1 cup sugar and the yolk of 3 eggs.
2. Stir well.
3. In 2 cups flour, stir 1 tablespoon corn starch; bring to the heat and let it just come to a boil.
4. Stir it into the other ingredients and pour into a pie form lined with good dough.
5. When baked, brush the egg whites on, beaten with sugar.

title: Lemon Pie No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- Pie dough
- 6 eggs, fewer than 2 whites
- 2 cups white sugar
- A little salt
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 tablespoons corn starch
- Zest of 2 large lemons
- Juice of 2 large lemons
- 2 egg whites for topping

## Directions

1. Treat the same as the previous pies.
2. Stir together 6 eggs (fewer than 2 whites) with 2 cups white sugar and a little salt.
3. In 1 cup sweet milk, stir 2 tablespoons corn starch, then add the grated zest and juice of 2 large lemons.
4. Use the 2 whites for the topping.

title: Lemon Pie No. 4
body:
## Ingredients

- Pie dough
- 1 tablespoon corn starch
- A little cold water
- 1 cup boiling water
- 7 tablespoons sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- Zest of 2 lemons
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Egg whites for topping

## Directions

1. Treat the same as the previous pies.
2. Dissolve 1 tablespoon corn starch in a little cold water.
3. Add 1 cup boiling water and bring to a boil.
4. Remove from heat and stir in 7 tablespoons sugar, 4 egg yolks, grated zest and juice of 2 lemons.
5. Use the whites for the topping.

title: Lemon Pie Nr. 1 (Lemon Pie No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- pie dough
- zest of 2 lemons
- 1 peeled apple
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup sugar
- a small piece of melted butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- juice of 2 lemons
- a small cup of hot water
- egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. Line the mold with ordinary pie dough
2. Grate the zest of 2 lemons together with a peeled apple
3. Stir 2 egg yolks with 1 cup sugar, a small piece of melted butter, 2 tablespoons flour and the juice of 2 lemons
4. Stir in a small cup of hot water and when all is well mixed, pour onto the dough
5. Treat entirely as an egg pie: bake, brush with beaten egg whites and place in the oven again

title: Lemon-Omelet (Lemon Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 0.5 cup white sugar
- Juice of one large or two small lemons
- Grated peel of one lemon
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir egg yolks white with sugar
2. Stir in lemon juice
3. Stir in grated lemon peel
4. Fold in beaten egg whites, stirring strongly to blend well with batter
5. Bake for half an hour
6. Serves 3 to 4 persons

title: Leverbred (Liver Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Homemade liver pâté
- French bread slices without crust
- Egg, beaten
- Bread crumbs
- Lard or butter

## Directions

1. Spread a good layer of the homemade liver pâté over French bread slices without crust.
2. Dip the spread side in beaten egg and then in bread crumbs.
3. On a frying pan with boiling lard or preferably a little butter, place the bread slices with the bread crumb side down.
4. Fry until light brown and turn for a moment.

title: Leverbred (Liver Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Homemade liver pâté (see No. 259)
- Slices of French bread without crust
- Beaten egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or Claret
- Lemon
- Pickled cucumber (optional)

## Directions

1. Spread the homemade liver pâté in a good layer over slices of French bread without crust
2. Dip the spread side in beaten egg and then in breadcrumbs
3. Place the bread slices on a frying pan with boiling Claret or preferably a little butter, breadcrumb side down
4. Fry until light brown, turn briefly onto the bread side
5. Arrange on a warm platter
6. Top with a piece of lemon from which the juice is pressed as they are eaten
7. A thin slice of pickled cucumber can replace the lemon

title: Leverpostej (Liver Pâté) (Made as Strasbourg-style Goose Liver Pâté)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg (3 lb) fresh pork fat
- 1 medium liver, about 1.5 kg (3 lb)
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) margarine
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) flour
- Milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon ground black pepper
- 1 teaspoon ground cloves
- Salt
- 1 small onion, grated
- Egg whites (whipped)

## Directions

1. Mince the pork fat very finely.
2. Make a roux by cooking the margarine and flour together, then thin it with milk to create a thick batter or soft gruel.
3. Add the minced pork fat to the batter.
4. Place the pot over very low heat to prevent burning and let the mixture cook very gently until the pork fat is rendered.
5. While the mixture cooks, prepare the liver.
6. Select the lightest livers, which are also the fattest, with a fragile membrane that breaks easily with finger pressure.
7. Wash the liver thoroughly and remove the membrane.
8. Scrape the liver so that no veins or connective tissue are included.
9. Mince the liver finely and place on a coarse, inverted sieve.
10. Gradually press the warm mixture through the sieve with the liver.
11. In the strained liver mixture, stir in 2 egg yolks, 1 teaspoon ground black pepper, 1 teaspoon ground cloves, salt to taste, 1 grated small onion, and the whipped egg whites.
12. Fill molds (not completely full) and place on a baking sheet in a moderately warm oven.
13. Avoid high heat as it will form a hard crust on top.
14. Cover the molds with paper during baking.
15. Bake in a water bath for 1 hour.
16. If a layer of liquid fat forms on top of the hot pâté, do not pour it off as it will be reabsorbed.
17. Let rest until the next day.
18. Place the mold over a steaming kettle or pass it several times over a weak gas flame to loosen it from the sides.
19. Turn the pâté out of the mold.
20. In a gelatin mold, pour good, clear aspic mixed with isinglass.
21. When set, turn it over onto the liver pâté.
22. Decorate with parsley, pickled shallots, small onions, pickles, slices of cucumber, or other garnishes.

title: Leverpostej i Sky (Liver Pâté in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover pâté
- Good, clear aspic
- Isinglass
- Hard-boiled eggs (optional)
- Truffles (optional)
- Lemon slices
- Parsley
- Aspic buddings (for garnish)

## Directions

1. Use small molds or small coffee cups with the bottom lined with good, clear aspic mixed with isinglass.
2. Once set, place a small piece of liver pâté in the mold.
3. Pour lukewarm aspic over it so as not to melt the aspic on the bottom.
4. Optionally, place a slice of hard-boiled egg or truffle on the set aspic before adding the pâté.
5. A ring-shaped mold can also be filled with liver pâté in aspic.
6. Turn out onto a dish and decorate the center with lemon slices arranged in a star pattern.
7. Place parsley between the slices.
8. A pudding mold can also be used, garnished with small aspic buddings.

title: Leverpostej med Høns, Duer og Harer (Liver Pâté with Chicken, Pigeon and Hare)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- leftovers of cooked or roasted meat (chicken, pigeon, or hare)
- liver pâté as in recipe No. 259

## Directions

1. Cut the leftovers of cooked or roasted meat into small fillets
2. Distribute the fillets in the pâté before it is placed in the oven for baking
3. Prepare the liver pâté as in recipe No. 259

title: Leverpölse (Liver Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pork liver of 2 pounds
- 2 pounds offal (heart, etc.)
- 2 pounds panade (soaked and seasoned bread)
- 1/2 pound meat without sinews and skin
- 1 pint sweet cream
- Salt
- Nutmeg and white pepper in equal quantities, ground and mixed together

## Directions

1. After all sinews and membranes are removed, cut the liver and offal into square pieces of one inch size.
2. Melt the offal in a frying pan over gentle heat; mix salt and seasonings with the liver; stir everything together for 3 minutes over the heat and then set aside to cool.
3. Place the meat first 30 minutes in boiling water, then in cold water and cut into small cubes.
4. Pound the liver and offal, press through a steel sieve, then pound in a mortar into a fine mass together with the panade.
5. Add the cream and eggs little by little.
6. Season carefully with salt and spices; finally mix the meat cubes into the whole.
7. Fill the finished farce either into sausage casings and treat in the usual manner, after which it is eaten cold; or it can also be placed in a large, round or oblong mold that has been previously buttered and cooked evenly for 2 hours, after which the farce is set aside to cool and the mold is turned. Cut into slices and eaten cold.

title: Leverpølse (Liver Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- One good pork liver
- Water
- Salt
- Pork fat
- Fine salt
- Ground white pepper
- Shallot onions
- Fat
- Sweet cream or cold bacon soup
- Intestines

## Directions

1. Place a good pork liver in water and let it stand for one day so that the blood is completely drawn out.
2. Cook it then in some water with salt until it is tender, then take it up, set it aside to become cold, and remove the skin.
3. Then grate the liver finely, making sure that there are no small hard pieces in between.
4. From pork fat remove all sinews and skins, then scrape the fat so it becomes like butter.
5. Mix equal weight of this and of the grated liver well together with finely ground salt and white pepper.
6. Some shallot onions are cooked down in a little fat, and this is strained into the mass.
7. Stir with sweet cream or in the absence thereof with cold bacon soup until it has reached an appropriate thickness, then stuff firmly into cleaned intestines.
8. Tie these and sprinkle with salt, after which they stand 1 day.
9. They then get a brief cooking of a few minutes in warm water, are taken up and stirred.
10. Eaten cold with open-faced sandwiches.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Likør-Budding (Liqueur Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Liter (1/2 Pot) cream
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) small macaroons
- 65 Gr (13 Kvint) blanched sweet almonds, sliced into strips
- candied pumpkin with ginger or Chinese ginger
- a little strawberry jam
- liqueur or 1 wine glass cognac
- 5 leaves isinglass
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) water
- cream foam, half colored pink with a few drops of fruit coloring and flavored with vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Whip 1/2 Liter (1/2 Pot) cream to foam
2. Stir in 125 Gr (1/4 S) small macaroons, 65 Gr (13 Kvint) blanched sweet almonds sliced into strips, candied pumpkin with ginger or Chinese ginger, and a little strawberry jam
3. Flavor the pudding with liqueur, or if not available, add 1 wine glass of cognac
4. Dissolve 5 leaves of isinglass in 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) water and stir into the pudding
5. Pour into a mold and decorate with cream foam, half of which is colored pink with a few drops of fruit coloring and flavored with vanilla essence

title: Likør-Creme (Liqueur Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- vanilla cream as Nr. 1060
- Maraschino, Noyau, Cacao, Chartreuse, Orange, or Strawberry liqueur to taste
- whipped cream for decoration
- food coloring (pink, green, or yellow)

## Directions

1. Stir either Maraschino, Noyau, Cacao, Chartreuse, Orange, or Strawberry liqueur to taste into the vanilla cream (see Nr. 1060).
2. Fill the cream into larger or smaller molds, turn them out, and decorate with small peaks of whipped cream colored pink, green, or yellow with food coloring.

title: Limonade (Lemonade)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pitcher water
- 1 pound fine sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 drop citron oil
- 2 measures Graves

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pitcher water and 1 pound fine sugar together
2. Press the juice of 1 lemon through a sieve into it
3. Add 1 drop citron oil and 2 measures Graves
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Limonadesukker (Lemonade Sugar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 quintals ground fine sugar
- ½ quintals citric acid
- ½ drops citron oil
- 25 drops vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Mix 30 quintals ground fine sugar well with ½ quintals citric acid
2. Drop ½ drops citron oil and 25 drops vanilla essence onto this mixture
3. Preserve the mixture in a glass jar with glass stopper
4. Add to cold, fresh water in appropriate quantity when you want to make lemonade
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Lincoln Budding (Lincoln Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams cornstarch
- 0.25 liter cold milk
- 0.5 liter milk, brought to boil
- 75 grams cold margarine
- 8 egg yolks
- 125 grams powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 50 grams finely cut orange peel
- zest of 0.5 lemon, grated
- egg whites, beaten
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. 250 grams of cornstarch is stirred into 0.25 liter of cold milk.
2. Just over 0.5 liter of milk is brought to a boil, the mixture is quickly stirred in, and cooked for a couple of minutes.
3. The pot is lifted from the heat, while 75 grams of cold margarine is stirred into the mass; this is poured into a dish in which 8 egg yolks have been stirred with 125 grams of powdered sugar, 1 teaspoonful of salt, 50 grams of finely cut orange peel, and grated zest of 0.5 lemon.
4. The beaten egg whites are stirred in last, and the batter is immediately poured into a mold buttered and sprinkled with breadcrumbs.
5. Bake 0.25 hour in the oven and serve in the mold.
6. Serve with fruit sauce or fruit compote.

title: Lingonberries (Tyttebær)
body:
## Ingredients

- Berries
- 1.5 Pd. Melis per 1 Pd. berries
- whole cinnamon

## Directions

1. Clean the berries by placing them on a coarse cloth and rolling them down to remove stems and fuzz
2. Place in cold water
3. Pound the Melis and place layered in the pot with the berries along with some pieces of whole cinnamon
4. Boil for 1/2 hour, skim frequently, and preserve in the usual manner
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Linse-Kringler (Lentil Twists)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram flour
- 375 grams (3/4 pound) margarine
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) powdered sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- Glaze made from 1/2 kilogram (1 pound) flour mixed with 4 tablespoons water and a few drops of vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Knead together the flour, margarine, powdered sugar, and egg yolks
2. Roll out into small, thin twists
3. Place fairly close together on the baking sheet in a not-too-hot oven
4. Bake until very pale
5. Once the twists are cold, brush them with glaze using a knife and place in a barely warm oven to dry
6. The oven door must be left open for the glaze to become shiny, but if the twists are left in too long, the glaze will crack

title: Linse-Makroner (Lentil Macaroons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- thin rolled puff pastry
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) blanched, peeled, finely chopped almonds
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar
- 6 raw egg whites
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Line the molds with thinly rolled puff pastry
2. Mix together the almond paste of blanched, peeled, finely chopped almonds with powdered sugar, 6 raw egg whites and 1 teaspoon baking powder
3. Place with a teaspoon in the molds
4. Bake the cakes until light brown at even heat

title: Linse-Makroner (Lentil Macaroons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Kg (1/2 lb) blanched, peeled, finely chopped almonds
- 1/4 Kg (1.5 lb) powdered sugar
- 6 raw egg whites
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- thin rolled butter dough for lining the molds

## Directions

1. Line the molds with thin rolled butter dough
2. Mix the almond paste made from the blanched, peeled, finely chopped almonds with the powdered sugar, raw egg whites, and baking powder
3. Place the mixture in the molds with a teaspoon
4. Bake the cakes light brown at even heat

title: Linser (Cream-Filled Tartlets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) Pillsbury flour
- 375 Gr (3/4 S) Margarine
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) Powdered sugar (Melis)
- 4 egg yolks
- 3 egg yolks for filling cream
- 3 tablespoons powdered sugar (Melis)
- 1 tablespoon flour (Flormel)
- Vanilla essence
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) half-cream

## Directions

1. Knead together the flour, margarine, powdered sugar, and 4 egg yolks until a dough forms; let it rest for half an hour.
2. Roll out thinly and line small buttered baking forms.
3. Place 1 teaspoon cream in each form; arrange these closely together on the kitchen counter and cover with a large sheet of dough, thinly rolled out.
4. Trim the edges of the forms and place in the oven.
5. Bake for approximately half an hour. They can be prepared early in the day and baked later.
6. For the filling cream: beat 3 egg yolks with 3 tablespoons powdered sugar, 1 tablespoon flour, and vanilla essence. When 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) half-cream is added, continue as directed.
7. This portion yields 40 cakes.

title: Lobscouse (Stewed Beef with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cooked beef
- 1 pound lean bacon
- 1 knap ounce butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 onion
- Parsley
- 2 pounds potatoes
- 1 quart broth

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into 2-inch cubes.
2. Cut the bacon into 2-inch slices and brown lightly with the butter.
3. Add the broth.
4. Layer in raw sliced potatoes, onion, parsley, and seasonings.
5. Simmer gently for 15 minutes.
6. Add the meat and simmer gently for 10 minutes.
7. Remove the onion and seasonings.
8. Ensure the potatoes are fully cooked and adjust seasoning before serving.

title: Løg-Koteletter med brunede Kartofler (Onion Cutlets with Browned Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion cutlets
- Browned potatoes
- Parsley
- Brown butter
- Gravy sauce

## Directions

1. Lay the cutlets in strips in the middle of the hot dish, garnish them on the sides with browned potatoes and decorate the dish with parsley. Serve with browned butter or a good gravy sauce.

title: Løg-Koteletter med Kartoffel-Mos (Onion Cutlets with Mashed Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Prepared onion cutlets
- Mashed potatoes
- Brown butter

## Directions

1. Place a portion of mashed potatoes on top of each hot, breaded, fried onion slice. Serve browned butter separately.

title: Løg-Koteletter med Ris- og Tomat-Sauce (Onion Cutlets with Rice and Tomato Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked rice
- Onion cutlets
- Tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Arrange cooked, warm rice as for curry in a portion in the middle of the dish and garnish with the warm onion cutlets. Serve with tomato sauce.

title: Løg-Koteletter med rørt Smør (Onion Cutlets with Softened Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried onion cutlets
- Softened butter
- Parsley

## Directions

1. On each of the fried cutlets place a small ball of softened butter with finely chopped parsley in it. Make these small balls with a teaspoon dipped in hot water, and serve the dish before the butter melts.

title: Løg-Koteletter med Spejlæg (Onion Cutlets with Fried Eggs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Large Spanish onions
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Brown butter

## Directions

1. Peel large Spanish onions and cut into not too thin slices, which are dipped in egg and ground bread and fried light brown and tender in butter on a pan. Arrange the cutlets on a hot dish, and a round fried egg is placed on each. Browned butter can be poured on the dish or served in a sauce boat.

title: Løg-Sauce (Onion Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Three medium onions
- Water
- A good slice of butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Thin cream or sweet milk
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut the onions into thin slices or chop them and cook them tender in a little water on the bottom of the pot
2. Add a good slice of butter and bake 1 tablespoon flour in it, which is diluted with thin cream or sweet milk to a smooth sauce
3. After it has boiled, salt it and it should preferably be served immediately
4. This sauce is eaten with cured herring and cured ham

title: Løgsauce (Onion Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- large onions
- salt
- water
- butter
- sugar
- milk or cream
- flour
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil large onions in salt and water, chop or slice them, and put them in melted butter with a little more salt and 1 tablespoon sugar.
2. Cook while stirring and thinning with milk or cream.
3. If desired, the sauce can be thickened with a little flour and a little ground pepper added.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Maccaroni
body:
## Ingredients

- Maccaroni
- Bouillon
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese
- Salt

## Directions

1. Break the maccaroni into fairly long pieces and cook in bouillon, which does not need to be very strong.
2. There should not be more broth than one can imagine will be suitable as it will cook down.
3. When one takes one out with the edge of a fork and it thereby breaks in the middle, they are done.
4. Then stir a little cold butter into them until the butter is melted.
5. Serve immediately and eat with grated sharp cheese and, if one wishes, melted butter.

title: Maccaroni paa Neapolitansk (Neapolitan Maccaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- Maccaroni
- Water or thin bouillon
- Finely chopped ham
- Meat
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Let maccaroni cook in water or thin bouillon until almost completely cooked.
2. Then drain the water so only a little remains with which they can stand and soak up.
3. In another pot cook finely chopped and drained ham and meat in thin bouillon until well cooked.
4. Remove it from the sauce, then layer everything in a deep baking dish as follows: first a layer of maccaroni, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese, then a thin layer of ham, then maccaroni with cheese and then meat and ham, continuing thus as long as you have something; but the last must be cheese.
5. Place the dish in a warm baking oven for about 10 minutes, that is, until it is well heated and has taken on a golden brown color on top.
6. Serve hot.

title: Madeira-Sauce (Madeira Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Gravy
- Paprika
- Salt
- Madeira
- Maggi bouillon cubes

## Directions

1. Brown the butter and bake flour in it, thin with good gravy and season as in the Madeira Gravy with paprika, salt, Madeira and Maggi bouillon cubes

title: Madeira-Sky (Madeira Gravy)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) gravy sauce
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Sago flour
- Paprika
- Salt
- Madeira
- Mushrooms, truffles, olives, or chopped chestnuts (for variations)

## Directions

1. Prepare a very strong, clear, brown gravy, which can be further improved with Maggi bouillon cubes if necessary
2. Thicken with a little stirred sago flour and season with paprika, salt and Madeira
3. This gravy can be varied with different additions, after which it takes its name: Madeira Gravy with Mushrooms, Truffles, Olives, or Chestnuts
4. These additions are brought to a boil in the gravy
5. Can be served with veal and beef filet, with cutlets, and with roasted and boiled poultry

title: Madeirabudding (Madeira Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 quintals isinglass dissolved in boiling water
- 1 stick vanilla
- 4 pounds white sugar
- 1 bottle Madeira
- 12 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Dissolve 12 quintals isinglass in boiling water and set aside to cool.
2. Add the cooled isinglass along with 1 stick vanilla, 4 pounds white sugar, 1 bottle Madeira, and 12 egg yolks (first whipped with a little of the wine) to the heat.
3. Bring to a boil while stirring constantly, then pour out.
4. When cold, stir in 1 pot cream whipped to foam.
5. Pour the whole into a porcelain mold greased with salad oil and let it stand until the pudding is stiff.
6. Serve with raspberry juice or mixed fruit juice.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mais-Suppe (Corn Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- milk
- corn flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Let half water and half milk boil
2. Stir in enough corn flour to achieve a suitable thickness
3. Let it cook for a short time while stirring
4. Add salt when serving

title: Maitre d'hotel-Sauce (Maitre d'Hotel Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Meat or fish stock
- Chervil
- Parsley
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Lemon juice
- Cold butter

## Directions

1. In 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) of white, smooth sauce made with meat or fish stock, add 1 tablespoon of finely chopped chervil and 1 tablespoon of parsley
2. Bind with 2 egg yolks
3. Stir in salt, lemon juice, and a slice of cold butter
4. This sauce suits fish, poultry, veal, and lamb

title: Maitre d'hôtel Smør (Maître d'hôtel Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kg (½ pound) stirred butter
- 2 tablespoons finely chopped parsley
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- White pepper

## Directions

1. To ¼ kg stirred butter, add 2 tablespoons finely chopped parsley, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, and a little white pepper.
2. This butter is used for French beef as well as for other meat and vegetable dishes.
3. A teaspoon is dipped in lukewarm water, and with it small butter balls are formed and placed on the beef just as it is served.

title: Maizena Kokos-Kager (Maizena Coconut Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 grams margarine
- 200 grams powdered sugar
- 125 grams maizena
- 125 grams cake flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 1/4 deciliters sweet milk
- 3 egg whites
- 125 grams cake flour with powdered sugar
- 125 grams coconut flour
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Melt the margarine and stir in the powdered sugar, maizena, cake flour, baking powder, and sweet milk
2. Fold in the 3 beaten egg whites
3. Grease small molds with margarine and place 1 teaspoon of batter in each
4. Mix the 2 beaten egg whites with the cake flour with powdered sugar and coconut flour
5. Distribute this mixture on top of the cakes using a teaspoon
6. Bake until light brown. Makes approximately 35 cakes

title: Maizena Mandel-Vælling (Cornstarch Almond Gruel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 liters sweet milk
- 200 grams cornstarch
- 20 bitter almonds
- 5 eggs
- 0.25 kg powdered sugar
- salt
- vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Bring 5 liters of sweet milk to a boil
2. Mix 6 tablespoons (200 grams) cornstarch in 1 liter cold milk
3. Stir 20 blanched, scalded, grated bitter almonds and the cornstarch mixture into the boiling milk
4. Bring to a boil, season lightly with salt, and pour into a tureen
5. In the tureen, beat 5 egg yolks with 0.25 kg powdered sugar
6. Fold the whipped egg whites gently into the hot gruel with the rice
7. Serve with sugar and cinnamon, or with raspberry jam

title: Maizena-Fromage (Maizena Fromage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 625 gr (1 1/4 S) cold maizena porridge
- ground almonds or vanilla extract to taste
- 2 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 3/4 L (1 pægl) cream
- apple or apricot compote
- 125 gr (1/4 S) small macaroons
- 1/4 L (1 pægl) cream for whipped topping, colored pale pink

## Directions

1. Stir ground almonds or vanilla extract and powdered sugar into the cold maizena porridge
2. Fold in whipped cream
3. Spread on a flat dish covered with apple or apricot compote
4. Top with small macaroons
5. Spray whipped cream tinted pale pink over the fromage

title: Maizena-Kiks (Maizena Biscuits)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram maizena
- 1/2 kilogram cake flour
- 1/4 kilogram cold margarine
- 100 grams powdered sugar
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- vanilla essence
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 liter milk

## Directions

1. Mix the maizena, cake flour, cold margarine, powdered sugar, baking powder, and vanilla essence together on a pastry board until the margarine is completely worked into the flour
2. Stir in the eggs and milk with a spoon and mix the dough together
3. Roll out as thin as possible, as the biscuits rise considerably during baking
4. Prick the dough and cut out with a tin can, placing very close together on a cold greased baking sheet
5. Bake light brown or golden. Makes approximately 150 excellent round tea biscuits, suitable for butter and cheese or marmalade

title: Maizenagrød (Maizena Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 package maizena
- 2½ L sweet or semi-skimmed milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon powdered sugar
- ¼ L cold milk
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 3 egg whites
- almond, lemon, vanilla or orange essence
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- fruit juice and water or beer

## Directions

1. Bring 2 L milk to a boil.
2. Make a liaison by beating 3 egg yolks with 1 tablespoon powdered sugar, mix in the flour and ¼ L cold milk, and stir this into the boiling milk.
3. Let it come to a good boil, then stir in 50 g butter or margarine.
4. After removing from heat, stir in the 3 beaten egg whites.
5. A little almond, lemon, vanilla or orange essence can be stirred in.
6. Serve with butter, sugar and cinnamon, fruit juice and water or beer.
7. Cold maizena porridge can be served as a pudding with cold fruit sauce or compote.

title: Major
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 wine glass Banko
- 1 tablespoon vanilla ice cream
- 2 pounds fine sugar
- 1 pot boiling water
- 3 measures arrack (Api-api)
- 4 measures Sherry
- vanilla

## Directions

1. In a wine glass of Banko, place 1 tablespoon vanilla ice cream and drink the Banko thus
2. To make Banko: dissolve 2 pounds fine sugar in 1 pot boiling water
3. When it has cooled, add 3 measures arrack (Api-api), 4 measures Sherry and a little vanilla
4. Filter it
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Majs og Tomater (Corn and Tomatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal amount of cooked fresh corn kernels cut from the cob, or salted corn soaked as described
- Fresh scalded tomatoes, cut into pieces and with the skin removed
- Salt
- Pepper
- A little sugar
- Butter
- Tomato puree (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook equal parts of fresh corn kernels (or soaked salted corn) and fresh tomatoes together gently for about half an hour.
2. Season with salt, pepper and a little sugar.
3. Cook gently for another quarter hour.
4. Stir in a piece of butter, bring to a boil and serve.
5. Tomato puree may be used instead of fresh tomatoes if these are not available at reasonable prices.

title: Majs, friske, kogte og stuvede i Mælk (Fresh Cooked Corn Stewed in Milk)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Kernels from cooked corn cobs
- Cold sweet milk
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the kernels off the cooked cob.
2. Cover them with a little cold sweet milk and let them cook over a low fire until they thicken.
3. Stir in cold butter, salt and pepper.
4. Salted corn is treated in the same manner.

title: Majs-Suppe (Corn Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg (1 pound) can of canned Sweet Corn
- 4 L (4 pots) spare broth
- 50 grams (10 skein) butter
- 50 grams (10 skein) flour
- 3 to 4 egg yolks
- salt
- ground white pepper
- wine
- tomato purée
- toasted bread
- fresh corn cobs (optional)

## Directions

1. Bake 50 grams (10 skein) butter and 50 grams (10 skein) flour together and thin with the broth, pour the contents of the can into it, and let the thinned soup simmer for 5 minutes before sieving.
2. Pour it into the pot, bring it to a boil, bind it with 3 to 4 egg yolks, season with salt, ground white pepper, wine and tomato purée to taste.
3. Serve with toasted bread. Can be made from fresh corn cobs.

title: Majsflage-Grød (Corn Flake Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg corn flakes
- 2½ L milk
- 20 bitter almonds, grated and blanched
- salt
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- fruit juice and water

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the corn flakes into the boiling, sweet milk together with 20 grated blanched bitter almonds.
2. The porridge cooks for 10 minutes, is salted and served with butter, sugar, cinnamon and fruit juice and water.

title: Makaroni med Parmesanost (Macaroni with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg macaroni
- Salt
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into short pieces and place in plenty of boiling salted water or in milk, cooking until tender but not overcooked.
2. Drain through a colander and transfer to a serving dish.
3. Pour melted butter over the macaroni.
4. Serve grated Parmesan cheese separately.

title: Makaroni med Østers (Macaroni with Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Salt
- Butter
- Grated cheese
- Oysters

## Directions

1. Boil the macaroni in salted water and strain through a sieve.
2. Butter a deep dish and layer macaroni alternately with grated cheese and oysters.
3. The top layer should be macaroni with small pieces of butter on top.
4. Bake until brown.

title: Makaroni å l'anglaise (Macaroni à l'Anglaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter good rich gravy
- A little sago flour
- 2 truffles, sliced
- 1/4 kg cooked macaroni, broken into pieces
- Cooked salted tongue, cut out

## Directions

1. In good rich gravy thickened with a little sago flour, add 2 truffles cut into slices and bring to a boil in the gravy, which gains an excellent taste from this.
2. Warm the cooked macaroni pieces in the sauce and serve in a border.
3. Around this can be arranged cooked salted tongue cut into pieces.

title: Makaroni å la Florentine (Macaroni à la Florentine)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg cooked macaroni, broken into pieces
- 100 grams butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- About 1/2 liter broth or water with Maggi bouillon cube
- Ground white pepper
- Salt
- Cayenne pepper
- Mustard, stirred
- 1/4 kg grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Make a white sauce with butter, flour and about half a liter of broth or water with a Maggi bouillon cube.
2. When the sauce has boiled, season it with ground white pepper, salt, cayenne pepper and stirred mustard.
3. Add the grated Parmesan cheese and bring back to a boil.
4. Finally warm the cooked macaroni in the sauce.
5. Serve as a vegetable with meat dishes.

title: Makaroni- og Tomat-Salat med Kastanier (Macaroni and Tomato Salad with Chestnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- tomato purée
- paprika
- sugar
- salt
- a little wine
- cooked finely chopped macaroni
- cooked chestnuts
- cooked salted beef tongue
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Brown butter and flour together, thin with tomato purée to make an even sauce, seasoned with paprika, sugar, salt and a little wine
2. Mix the cooked finely chopped macaroni with the sauce and arrange the salad on a platter
3. Garnish with lettuce leaves and arrange a ring of cooked chestnuts around it
4. Slices of cooked salted beef tongue can be placed around it

title: Makaroni-Gratin med Skinke (Macaroni and Ham Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) Makaroni
- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) mager, kogt, røget Skinke
- 100 Gr (20 Kvint) Smør
- 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) Fløde, Mælk eller Suppe
- Stødt Peber

## Directions

1. Makaronierne brækkes itu, koges og hældes på Dørslaget.
2. I Kaserollen kommer 100 Gr (20 Kvint) Smør, som smeltes, og heri røres 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) Fløde, Mælk eller Suppe, stødt Peber og til sidst den hakkede Skinke og Makaronierne.
3. Rester af kogt Skinke kan finde Anvendelse her.
4. Ophedes godt i Ovnen.

title: Makaroni-Salat (Macaroni Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams macaroni
- celery
- lost mayonnaise (see recipe 244)
- tarragon vinegar
- mustard
- capers
- lean ham

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni and cut it small together with celery
2. In lost mayonnaise, stir in tarragon vinegar, mustard and 2 tablespoons of capers
3. Toss the macaroni and celery in the sauce
4. On the salad you can place strips of finely chopped lean ham, or the salad can be arranged on a platter with slices of cooked ham around it

title: Makaroni-Salat (Macaroni Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.25 pound macaroni
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Cream
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Horseradish

## Directions

1. Cook 0.25 pound macaroni tender in water with salt
2. Mix a lump of butter and 0.5 pint cream with vinegar, sugar and grated horseradish to taste
3. Add the macaroni (drained on a colander) to this sauce
4. Serve this salad cold
5. To serve warm, break 0.5 pound macaroni into fairly large pieces and cook slowly for about 3 hours in water with a little salt, butter and onion
6. Heat 1 pint sweet cream over heat with 30 quint grated Parmesan cheese, a little nutmeg and a little white pepper
7. Add the macaroni (drained on a colander) and steam for 20 minutes, then serve immediately
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Makaroni-Salat med Flødeskum og Peberrod (Macaroni Salad with Cream Foam and Horseradish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 Gr (1/4 S) macaroni
- Milk or water
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cream
- Salt
- Sugar
- Vinegar
- Grated horseradish
- Watercress for garnish
- Cooked celery (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni tender in milk or water
2. Drain the cooked macaroni through a colander, rinse with cold water, and cut into 2-3 centimeter (1 inch) long pieces
3. Whip 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cream to stiff foam
4. Mix salt, sugar, vinegar, and grated horseradish into the foam
5. Turn the macaroni into it
6. Garnish the salad with watercress and prepare it just before serving
7. Cooked, finely cut celery can be used instead of macaroni

title: Makaroni-Suppe à l'Italienne (Macaroni Soup Italian Style)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 gr thin macaroni
- 5 L soup stock
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/4 liter raw cream
- 75 gr grated Parmesan cheese
- ground white pepper
- Madeira or Graves wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the thin macaroni until tender in half of the soup for approximately 1/2 hour
2. Drain the macaroni and once cooled, cut it into very small pieces
3. Mix the soup in which the macaroni was cooked with the remaining soup and return the macaroni to it
4. As soon as it boils, lift the pot from the heat
5. Beat 6 egg yolks with 1/4 liter raw cream, 75 gr grated Parmesan cheese and a little ground white pepper
6. Add this mixture to the soup while stirring vigorously
7. Set the pot back over the heat for a couple of minutes while continuing to stir, but do not let it boil
8. Season with Madeira or Graves wine if desired
9. Serve immediately

title: Makrel (Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mackerel
- Water
- Salt
- Whole peppercorns
- Sour butter sauce
- Boiled potatoes, freshly peeled

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the fish
2. Place in boiling water with salt and whole peppercorns to cook
3. When cooked, serve with sour butter sauce and freshly peeled boiled potatoes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Makrel (Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mackerel
- salt
- whole peppercorns
- sour butter sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the mackerel, rinse it, and place it in boiling water with salt and several whole peppercorns
2. Cook until done
3. Serve with sour butter sauce and freshly peeled boiled potatoes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Makroner (Macaroons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) blanched almonds
- 7 egg whites
- 3/4 kg (1 1/2 lb) powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Grind the blanched almonds very fine
2. Mix together well the egg whites, powdered sugar and baking powder
3. Place on a cold, buttered sheet
4. Bake at low heat

title: Makroner (Macaroons)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 15 quintal bitter almonds
- 1 pound fine white sugar or Demerara sugar
- peel of 1 lemon, finely grated
- 4 beaten egg whites
- oblate wafers or buttered baking sheet

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds and grate them finely on a sharp, fine grater
2. Chop the larger pieces finely
3. Mix in 1 pound fine white sugar and the finely grated lemon peel
4. Stir in the 4 beaten egg whites
5. Place the macaroons on oblate wafers or on a buttered sheet
6. Bake for a fairly long time at weak oven heat
7. When removed from the oven, press them together slightly with your hand
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Makronnødder (Macaroon Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 quintal sweet almonds
- 10 quintal bitter almonds
- 4 egg whites
- 1 pound flour
- 1 quintal ground cardamom

## Directions

1. Scald the almonds and pound them fine with the egg whites, flour, and ground cardamom. If necessary, knead a little more egg white into it. Roll the dough in long strips and cut pieces of the size of a nut, place on a buttered sheet and bake at moderate heat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mandel- eller Nødde-Créme (Almond or Nut Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 Gr (1/4 S) scalded almonds or nut kernels
- 1 L (1 Pot) milk
- egg yolks and isinglass as in vanilla cream Nr. 1060
- macaroons
- whipped cream for decoration

## Directions

1. Pound the scalded almonds or nut kernels very finely and place in 1 L (1 Pot) milk.
2. When it boils, add egg yolks and isinglass exactly as in vanilla cream Nr. 1060.
3. Serve with macaroons and decorate with whipped cream.

title: Mandel-Budding (Almond Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 150 Gr (30 Kvint) icing sugar
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) finely ground sweet almonds
- 25 Gr (5 Kvint) bitter almonds
- 8 leaves isinglass
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water
- 1 L (1 Pot) cream
- cold fruit sauce

## Directions

1. Stir 5 egg yolks with 150 Gr (30 Kvint) icing sugar
2. Add 125 Gr (1/4 S) finely ground sweet almonds and 25 Gr (5 Kvint) bitter almonds
3. Dissolve 8 leaves of isinglass in 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water and stir into the egg mixture along with the foam from 1 L (1 Pot) cream
4. Make the pudding a few hours before serving in a glass bowl
5. Serve with cold fruit sauce

title: Mandelbudding (Almond Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound sweet almonds
- 6 kvint bitter almonds
- 36 kvint powdered sugar
- a couple of tablespoons water
- 1 pægle melted butter
- ¾ pægle finely ground wheat bread
- 8 beaten eggs
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Scald the sweet and bitter almonds, remove the skins and pound them fine in a mortar.
2. When nearly fine, add 36 kvint powdered sugar and a couple of tablespoons water, and pound again until the whole becomes like a stiff dough.
3. Take it out and stir in 1 pægle melted butter, ¾ pægle finely ground wheat bread and 8 beaten eggs.
