4. Beat 5 egg whites to stiff peaks and fold into the mixture.
5. Butter a mold and sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
6. Pour the mixture in and place the mold in boiling water.
7. Cook the pudding for 2 hours.
8. Turn out onto a platter and serve with powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mandelgrød (Almond Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound almonds, of which approximately 6 ounces are bitter
- A little lemon peel
- 4 pots sweet milk
- 1 pound rice flour
- One-quarter pound vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Scald and chop the almonds with a little lemon peel.
2. Place 3 pots of the sweet milk in an enameled pot smeared with cold butter at the bottom and cook over gentle heat.
3. Mix 1 pound of rice flour with the remaining 2 pots of cold milk.
4. When the milk in the pot begins to boil, add the mixed flour and almonds.
5. Stir the porridge continuously until fully cooked.
6. Stir in one-quarter pound of white vanilla sugar.
7. Pour into a large bowl to cool.
8. Serve cold with mixed fruit juice or diluted wine with sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mandelkage (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 12 bitter almonds
- 62 quintal finely ground powdered sugar, sifted
- zest of 1 lemon
- 10 tablespoons melted butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 10 tablespoons sweet cream
- 12 quintal finely grated bread
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Scald the almonds, dry them, and pound them finely
2. Work in the powdered sugar and lemon zest
3. Stir in the melted butter until cool, with 5 egg yolks and 10 tablespoons sweet cream
4. Combine both mixtures and stir together for about an hour
5. Finally add the finely grated bread and the 5 egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
6. Pour the entire mixture into a buttered form and bake 1.5 hours at medium heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mandelkage (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 12 pieces bitter almonds
- 62 quintals powdered, sifted sugar
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 10 tablespoons melted butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 10 tablespoons sweet cream
- 12 quintals finely grated bread
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Blanch 1 pound sweet and 12 pieces bitter almonds, dry and pound finely
2. Work into this 62 quintals powdered, sifted sugar and the grated yellow zest of 1 lemon
3. Stir 10 tablespoons melted butter into this until cold, then add 5 egg yolks and 10 tablespoons sweet cream
4. Stir both mixtures together for approximately an hour
5. Finally add 12 quintals finely grated bread and the 5 egg whites whipped to stiff peaks
6. Pour the whole mixture into a buttered form and bake for 1.5 hours at moderate heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mandelkager (Almond Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg whites
- 3 pounds finely ground powdered sugar
- ½ pound sweet almonds
- 1 lemon, finely grated peel
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Whip egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Stir in 3 pounds finely ground powdered sugar
3. Add ½ pound sweet almonds, half finely ground and half sliced
4. Add the finely grated peel of 1 lemon
5. Grease a warm baking sheet with butter
6. When cool, spread the batter very thinly over the sheet
7. Insert into the oven and bake
8. When removed, cut immediately while still warm into small cakes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mandelmælk (Almond Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 12 quintals bitter almonds
- 5 pound powdered sugar
- 1 can boiling water

## Directions

1. Scald the sweet and bitter almonds and pound them very fine together with the powdered sugar.
2. Pour 1 can of boiling water over the mixture.
3. Let it stand covered for a couple of hours.
4. Strain it, cool it, and drink it cold.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mandelmælk (Almond Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 12 quintals bitter almonds
- ½ pound fine sugar
- 1 pitcher boiling water

## Directions

1. Scald 1 pound sweet almonds and 12 quintals bitter almonds and grind them very finely with ½ pound fine sugar
2. Pour 1 pitcher boiling water over it
3. Let it stand well covered for a couple of hours, then strain it, cool and drink cold
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mandelspaan (Almond Wafers)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound blanched almonds
- 6 egg whites
- 4 pounds finely ground powdered sugar
- 4 pounds fine flour
- sliced almonds for topping

## Directions

1. Finely pound the blanched almonds with 6 egg whites
2. Add 4 pounds finely ground powdered sugar and 4 pounds fine flour
3. Spread this batter on oblate wafers
4. Sprinkle with sliced almonds
5. Bake in a moderately warm oven
6. When removed, curl them over a cake roll or over a bottle
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mandeltærte (Almond Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet almonds
- 24 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 12 egg whites
- Butter
- Paper
- Fine powdered sugar
- Water
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound 1 pound sweet almonds
2. Stir 24 egg yolks with 1 pound powdered sugar until white and foaming, then fold in the pounded almonds carefully
3. Whip 12 egg whites to peaks and fold carefully into the aforementioned mixture
4. Butter a paper and use it to line the form, then pour in the mixture
5. Bake the tart for 1 hour in a moderate oven
6. When somewhat cooled, turn out of the form and decorate on top with sugar glaze made from fine powdered sugar moistened with a little cold water and mixed with a few drops of lemon juice, then dry in the oven
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Maraschino- og Madeira-Kréme (Maraschino and Madeira Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints sweet cream
- quarter pound powdered sugar
- 5 quarters dissolved isinglass
- half pint Maraschino liqueur or Madeira
- preserved plums for garnish

## Directions

1. Whip 2 pints of sweet cream to stiff foam with a quarter pound of powdered sugar, and into this mix 5 quarters of dissolved isinglass and half pint of Maraschino liqueur or Madeira.
2. The cream is poured into tall glass bowls and garnished with preserved plums.
3. Biscuits are served with it.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Maraschino- og Punsch-Is (Maraschino and Punch Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints water
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 1 pound white powdered sugar
- Juice of 5 lemons
- 1 glass maraschino or 1 pint arrack

## Directions

1. Boil 2 pints water, the zest of 1 lemon, and 1 pound white powdered sugar to a syrup and skim well, then cool the syrup.
2. Add the juice of 5 lemons and strain the whole mixture several times to become clear.
3. Then freeze it, and when it is half-frozen, stir in either a glass of maraschino or a pint of arrack, then freeze it thoroughly.
4. Maraschino ice is served garnished with small meringues, arrack ice is served in small ice glasses.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Maraschinobudding (Maraschino Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- 1/2 pound white sugar
- 6 quintals isinglass dissolved in boiling water
- 2 pails thick cream, whipped to stiff foam
- Maraschino liqueur
- biscuits
- candied fruits

## Directions

1. Heat 1 pot sweet cream with 1/2 pound white sugar and pour out.
2. Dissolve 6 quintals isinglass in boiling water and add it to the cream.
3. Whip 2 pails thick cream to stiff foam.
4. When the cooked cream is approximately cold, add enough Maraschino to give the cream a strong flavor of it, and then add the whipped cream.
5. Let the whole stand for a couple of hours in ice in a jelly mold, then turn it out and garnish with biscuits and candied fruits.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Maraschinobudding (Maraschino Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- half a pound white sugar
- 6 quintals isinglass
- 2 pints thick cream
- Maraschino

## Directions

1. Boil the sweet cream with half a pound white sugar and strain it.
2. Dissolve 6 quintals isinglass in boiling water and add to the cream.
3. Whip 2 pints thick cream to stiff peaks.
4. When the boiled cream is approximately cold, add enough Maraschino to give the cream a strong flavor of it.
5. Add the whipped cream.
6. Let the whole stand for a couple of hours in ice in a gelatin mold.
7. Turn it out and garnish with biscuits and preserved fruits.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Maraskino-Gelé (Maraschino Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- wine, isinglass, sugar (same quantities as in White Wine Jelly, Nr. 1102)
- 1/4 L (1 pint) maraschino liqueur

## Directions

1. Prepare using the same method as Wine Jelly, Nr. 1102, adding 1/4 L (1 pint) maraschino liqueur.
2. Serve as Wine Jelly.

title: Marble Cake (Marmor-Kage)
body:
## Ingredients

- White part: 1 cup white powdered sugar, ½ cup butter, ½ cup sweet milk, whites of 4 eggs, 2¾ cups flour, 2 heaped teaspoons baking powder
- Dark part: 1 cup brown sugar, ½ cup molasses, ½ cup sour milk, 2¾ cups flour, 1 level teaspoon soda dissolved in the milk, 4 egg yolks, ½ cup butter, ½ teaspoon cinnamon, allspice, cloves

## Directions

1. For the white part: stir the butter and sugar together until cream, beat the whites stiff and add last along with ½ cup of the flour in which the baking powder is sifted. Any kind of drops can be added.
2. For the dark part: stir ½ cup butter together with the sugar; add ½ teaspoon cinnamon, allspice and cloves.
3. Either kind can be used by itself and will make good cakes, or they can be mixed together so they represent marble.

title: Marcipan (Marzipan)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg good sweet almonds
- 25 gr bitter almonds
- 375 gr flour sugar
- 375 gr flour sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls rose water or rum

## Directions

1. Blanch the sweet and bitter almonds, remove the skin, rinse and dry in a cloth.
2. Pass this mass twice through an enameled meat grinder and then 2 more times with the flour sugar.
3. Now knead the almond paste with the flour sugar and rose water or rum.
4. Can be stored in parchment in a cool place.
5. Form figures from this fresh marzipan and decorate with a brush using confectioner's colors, which can be bought in small portions at the grocer's.
6. Place the finished marzipan on dishes and set in a cool place for a couple of days.
7. The marzipan should not be baked.

title: Marenger (Meringue)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg whites
- 1 pound finely sifted powdered sugar
- butter for greasing the pan
- flour for dusting
- coarse sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Whip the egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Slowly stir in 1 pound finely sifted powdered sugar while continuing to whip
3. Grease a baking sheet with butter and dust with flour
4. Place small cakes on the sheet using a silver spoon
5. Sprinkle with coarse sugar and dry them in a very weak oven heat until they release from the pan
6. Note: Blanched sweet almonds cut into long thin strips (40 quintal) can be added to the mixture, but then use only 75 quintal sugar. This mixture can also be used to make a ring cake.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Marenger (Meringues)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 Egg whites
- 1 Pound fine sifted powdered sugar
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Beat 10 egg whites to stiff foam.
2. Slowly stir in 1 pound fine sifted powdered sugar while constantly beating.
3. Grease a clean baking sheet with butter and flour it.
4. With a silver spoon, place the small cakes on the sheet.
5. Sprinkle with caster sugar.
6. Dry before putting them in a very gentle oven heat, where they stay until they release from the sheet.
7. This mass can also be used to make small cakes filled with vanilla ice cream.
8. To the foregoing mixture you can also add 40 qvint scalded, long, fine strips cut sweet almonds, but then use only 75 qvint sugar.
9. From the latter mentioned mixture you can also make Kransekage.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Marengs (Meringues)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 lb) powdered flour sugar
- a small amount of cream of tartar, just the amount on the tip of a teaspoon

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites to very stiff peaks
2. Carefully fold in the powdered flour sugar and a small amount of cream of tartar
3. Place on buttered baking sheets with a serving spoon or pipe onto the sheets
4. Dry for a couple of hours in a warm oven
5. Store in a tin box in a dry place

title: Marie Høns (Marie Hens)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- ½ pound powdered sugar
- 3 pounds wheat flour
- 1 good tablespoon water
- chopped almonds
- coarsely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Mix butter, powdered sugar, and wheat flour together until it crumbles
2. Add a good tablespoon of water
3. Roll the dough very thin
4. Cut it into small cakes
5. Sprinkle with chopped almonds and coarsely ground sugar
6. Bake at even heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Marinerede Sild (Marinated Herring)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cured or spiced herring
- Milk
- Powdered sugar
- Ground white pepper
- Onions
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. If using cured herring, soak them in milk overnight; spiced herring generally need not be soaked
2. Skin and clean the herring, cut off the head and tail
3. Split the herring from the back and remove the bone and entrails
4. Cut the herring halves crosswise into small pieces, lay them together again and turn them with the skin side upward when arranging
5. Sprinkle the herring with a little powdered sugar and ground white pepper and cover with thin onion slices
6. Pour vinegar over them a few hours before serving

title: Marineret fersk Sild (Marinated Fresh Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- Herring
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Lemon peel
- Pepper
- Bay leaf
- Cloves
- Thyme

## Directions

1. Clean the herring, rinse, dry and brush with oil, season, roast on both sides.
2. Layer in a glass jar with the spices and pour over with vinegar that has previously been brought to a boil and cooled.

title: Marineret Sild (Marinated Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- Herring
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Lemon peel
- Pepper
- Bay leaf
- Cloves
- Thyme

## Directions

1. Clean the herring, rinse, and dry it.
2. Brush with oil and season.
3. Roast on both sides.
4. Layer in a glass jar with the seasonings.
5. Pour over vinegar that has been brought to a boil and cooled.
6. Seal well and store in a cool place.

title: Marineret Torsk (Marinated Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- One cod weighing 6 pounds
- Butter
- Grated bread
- Shallots
- Chopped parsley
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into 4 to 5 pieces, remove the bones. Marinate the meat with melted butter, lemon juice, parsley and shallots.
2. Lay with marinade above and below, sprinkle thickly with grated bread, pour over with warm butter and roast in the oven.
3. Serve with sauce made of white wine, smoothed with egg yolks and seasoned to taste.

title: Marineret Vildsvineryg (Marinated Wild Boar Back)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar back
- 2 bottles red wine
- 2 onions
- 1 celery
- 2 carrots
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 lemon
- Whole peppercorns
- Salt
- Juniper berries
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Cut the back as you would venison back and remove the skin.
2. Place it in a marinade for 2 to 3 days made of 2 bottles red wine, 2 onions, 1 celery, 2 carrots, 2 bay leaves, 1 lemon (all cut up), some whole peppercorns, a little salt and juniper berries.
3. Remove the roast from the marinade, cover with butter, baste with the marinade, and roast at least 3 hours in a well-heated oven.
4. Strain the stock and thicken browned butter and flour with it to make the sauce.

title: Marmelade af Hindbær (Raspberry Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of raspberries
- 3/4 pound of powdered sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place the sugar over the fire with a little of the juice from the berries
2. When the sugar is melted, pour in the berries
3. Cook for nearly three-quarters of an hour, then pour the berries onto a colander and cook the juice for 1 hour with a little cinnamon
4. Then return the berries together with the kernels, but pour it out immediately before it begins to boil
5. Distribute the marmalade into pots in the usual manner
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Marmelade af Kirsebær (Cherry Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Black preserved cherries with pits removed
- 5 pounds of sugar per 1 pound of cherries
- Kernels from some of the broken pits

## Directions

1. Pit the cherries
2. Break some of the pits, remove the kernels and cook them with the berries
3. Otherwise the treatment is as described in recipe 480, but using only 5 pounds of sugar per 1 pound of cherries
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Marmor-Budding (Marble Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle white wine
- 12 ounces white sugar
- Juice of 3 lemons and zest of 2
- 14 ounces gelatine
- 20 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook white wine with white sugar, lemon juice and zest, and gelatine
2. As soon as the gelatine is dissolved, remove from heat and strain if necessary
3. Mix with 20 egg yolks, first beaten white

title: Marmor-Budding (Marble Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Bottle white wine
- 12 Ounces white sugar
- Juice of 3 lemons and zest of 2 lemons
- 1.5 Ounces gelatine or isinglass
- 20 Egg yolks
- 3 Teaspoons vanilla
- 0.5 Pint Madeira wine
- 1 Pint sweet cream, whipped to stiff peaks

## Directions

1. Cook the white wine with the white sugar, lemon juice, and zest and gelatine until dissolved
2. Remove from heat and strain if necessary
3. Mix with the egg yolks, which have been beaten white
4. Return to heat and stir constantly until it becomes thick
5. Add the vanilla and Madeira wine
6. Cool, stirring occasionally, and when nearly cold, fold in the whipped cream
7. Pour into the mold

title: Marmorbudding (Marble Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle white French wine (Graves)
- 3 quarter-pounds white sugar
- juice of 3 lemons
- rind of 2 lemons
- 8 quintals dissolved isinglass
- 16 egg yolks
- 1 pail Madeira or Sherry
- 2 pails sweet cream, whipped to stiff foam

## Directions

1. Boil 1 bottle white French wine with 3 quarter-pounds white sugar, the juice of 3 lemons, and the rind of 2 lemons, along with 8 quintals dissolved isinglass.
2. When the sugar is dissolved, strain through a sieve.
3. Whip 16 egg yolks until white and add them to this mixture.
4. Place the whole over heat again and bring to a boil while stirring constantly.
5. Add 1 pail Madeira or Sherry.
6. When almost cold, stir in 2 pails sweet cream whipped to stiff foam.
7. Pour the mixture into a wet mold and let it stand for several hours in a cold place.
8. Serve cold with cream sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Marts-Sne (March Snow)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart sweet cream
- 6 Ounces fine sugar
- Zest and juice of 2 lemons
- 1.5 Ounces gelatine, dissolved in warm water

## Directions

1. Whip the sweet cream well with the fine sugar, lemon zest, and lemon juice
2. Add the gelatine dissolved in warm water
3. Do not cook, but allow to set

title: Marv-Budding (Bone Marrow Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- bread pudding batter ingredients (with 50 grams less margarine)
- 0.25 kilogram beef bone marrow

## Directions

1. The batter is made as for bread pudding, except that about 50 grams of margarine is saved.
2. 0.25 kilogram of beef bone marrow is well cleaned of bone splinters and distributed in small pieces in layers in the pudding.
3. Red wine or fruit sauce is served with it.

title: Marv-Budding (Marrow Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ingredients as for bread pudding
- 1/2 pound marrow, cut into fairly large pieces

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter the same way as for bread pudding, but with only 4 ounces butter
2. When poured into the mold, distribute 1/2 pound marrow, cut into fairly large pieces, throughout
3. Serve with red wine sauce or another sweet sauce

title: Marvben (Marrow Bones)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Marrow bones
- Bouillon or boiled water
- Maggi Bouillon cube
- Linen cloth
- Rye bread
- Kitchen salt
- Horseradish
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Order the marrow bones from the butcher in advance.
2. Scrape the bones well on the outside and place them in cold water for 10 minutes.
3. Bind the ends with washed small linen cloths.
4. Place the bones in a pot and cover with bouillon or boiled water, adding one Maggi Bouillon cube for each 1/4 liter (1 pint).
5. Bring to a boil, then place the pot over low heat and simmer gently for at least one hour; it must not boil hard.
6. When serving, remove the linen cloths and place the bones on a hot plate, wrap with a warm napkin, and serve as quickly as possible.
7. Serve with hot toasted rye bread, kitchen salt, grated horseradish, or chopped parsley.

title: Marvbudding (Marrow Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound beef marrow
- 2 old 10-øre French breads, crusts scraped off
- sweet milk or thin cream
- ½ pound butter
- 15 egg yolks
- finely chopped marrow
- a little sugar
- salt
- cardamom
- ½ pound raisins
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Cut the beef marrow into pieces and place in cold water, then on a colander to drain, then on a clean linen cloth.
2. Soak the two French breads (with crusts removed) in sweet milk or thin cream, press them out and mix with ½ pound butter.
3. Pour the whole mixture into a dish to cool.
4. Stir in 15 egg yolks, one at a time, the finely chopped marrow, a little sugar, salt, cardamom, and ½ pound raisins.
5. Fold in the egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks.
6. Cook for 2½ hours and serve with sweet fruit sauce with red wine, or with melted butter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Marvbudding (Marrow Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound ox marrow
- cold water
- two old 10-ore French breads
- sweet milk or thin cream
- half a pound butter

## Directions

1. Cut half a pound ox marrow into pieces, place in cold water and then on a colander so the water can drip off, then place the marrow on a clean linen cloth. Two old 10-ore French breads, from which the crust has been scraped off, are soaked in sweet milk or thin cream, pressed out and baked with half a pound butter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mayonnaise (2 Egg Yolks, 1/4 Liter Good Salad Oil or Genuine Olive Oil)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Salad oil or olive oil
- Lemon juice or tarragon vinegar
- Water
- Ground white pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Pour the egg yolks, well separated from the whites, into a large bowl and stir with a wooden spoon, adding a tiny amount of salt.
2. Stir the yolks constantly in the same direction until they become a silky light yellow mass.
3. Only then begin to add the oil drop by drop under constant stirring, occasionally adding a few drops of lemon juice or tarragon vinegar.
4. If the mayonnaise becomes too thick, thin it with a little water, which often also helps to bring the mayonnaise together if it shows a tendency to separate.
5. If it does separate, take a fresh egg yolk with a little salt and stir well; gradually stir in the failed mayonnaise.
6. Season with ground white pepper, vinegar, and salt to taste.
7. The mayonnaise can be colored red with ground lobster roe and green by adding a little juice from ground spinach, squeezed out in a cloth.
8. In winter it can be made the day before; in summer it should preferably be made the same day and kept on ice until needed.
9. Duck eggs can be used.

title: Mayonnaise-Sauce (Mayonnaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 raw egg yolks
- 1.5 tablespoons stirred smooth mustard
- Best salad oil
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- A little Cayenne
- Half a teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Whip together 2 raw egg yolks (no white may be included) and stirred smooth mustard
2. Add the best salad oil drop by drop while stirring constantly
3. Continue until the bowl or cup can be turned over without spilling
4. Add vinegar, a little Cayenne, and half a teaspoon salt
5. Stir strongly until it becomes lightly colored
6. This is used for lobster, green salad, or fish

title: Mazarinkager (Mazarin Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- raspberry, strawberry, or cherry marmalade
- 6 egg whites
- ¾ pound finely ground powdered sugar
- vanilla, ground
- finely chopped almonds
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Line small molds with butter dough
2. Place a little fruit marmalade in each mold
3. Whip 6 egg whites to stiff peaks
4. Fold in ¾ pound finely ground powdered sugar mixed with ground vanilla
5. Fill the molds with the foam and smooth with a knife
6. Sprinkle with finely chopped almonds mixed with powdered sugar
7. Bake in a medium-warm oven
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Maître d'Hôtel Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce butter
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- 0.25 teaspoon pepper

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 ounce butter, 1 tablespoon chopped parsley, 1 teaspoon lemon juice and 0.25 teaspoon pepper
2. Mix together cold and spread on what you wish to use it for
3. It is excellent for any kind of fried fish

title: Medicer-Polser (Meatier Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- small pieces of lean and fat meat
- salt
- crushed allspice
- cloves
- pepper
- bouillon cooked on meat stock
- pork lard or meat, cut into cubes
- narrow pork intestines
- warm water

## Directions

1. Chop small pieces of lean and fat meat until it releases from the board and chopping knife
2. Salt it and stir it with crushed allspice, cloves, pepper and bouillon cooked on meat stock (which should be lukewarm) or water
3. The mixture should be quite soft and stirred well with pork lard or meat cut into cubes
4. Do not stuff the intestines too tightly
5. Place the intestines in a trough with warm water while filling
6. Use narrow pork intestines
7. Dry them well, place them on the roasting pan in boiling water and prick with a needle so they do not burst
8. When they have cooked a little on both sides, remove them, drain the water from the pan into a bowl and pour it gradually over the sausages during roasting
9. Wipe the pan well and brown 1 spoonful of butter on it
10. Brown the sausages in it and fry them until they are completely fried through
11. Serve with stewed potatoes, red cabbage or green cabbage

title: Medisterpølse (Meatier Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bacon side
- Rinds and waste
- Salt
- Water
- Cream or cream and soup
- Fine, dry salt
- Ground white pepper
- Ground nutmeg
- Pig intestines

## Directions

1. The bacon side is best; chop it very finely.
2. Set the rinds and waste to cook with some salt and water, and when this soup is strained and cooled, knead the filling with it so it becomes tough and supple.
3. Even better than soup alone is half soup and half cream.
4. Add fine, dry salt, ground white pepper and nutmeg, after which the whole is filled into cleaned pig intestines.
5. The intestines should only be stuffed loosely, otherwise they will burst during roasting.
6. These sausages can also be smoked.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Medisterpølser (Medister Sausages)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg (3.3 lbs) medister sausage
- Butter

## Directions

1. Place the sausages in a pot or on a frying pan and pour hot water over them.
2. Allow them to boil for approximately 5 minutes.
3. Remove them and dry the pan.
4. Brown the sausages in a good amount of butter.
5. Pour the butter in which they were browned into a sauce bowl.
6. Serve with stewed potatoes or a vegetable stew of various types of cabbage.

title: Mel-Budding (Flour Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 tablespoons flour
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 3.5 gills milk
- 8 egg yolks
- A handful sugar
- A little cardamom
- A little salt
- Egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Brown the flour well in the butter; heat the milk and pour it in gradually, little by little.
2. When this is well baked, remove from heat and cool it.
3. Stir the egg yolks, sugar, cardamom, and a little salt together very well for some time; lastly add the beaten egg whites.
4. Boil for 2 hours and serve with fruit or wine sauce.
5. Cannot be baked.

title: Melboller (Flour Balls)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams (1/4 pound) margarine
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) Pillsbury flour
- 2.5 deciliters (1 good pail) boiling water
- 5 egg yolks
- Salt
- Sugar
- Cardamom
- Egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. Melt the margarine and bake the flour in it
2. Add the boiling water gradually
3. When the dough has cooled, stir in 5 egg yolks, a little salt, sugar, and cardamom
4. Finally fold in the beaten egg whites
5. Immediately drop spoonfuls into boiling salted water
6. Cook only very gently for a moment
7. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain
8. Place in a tureen
9. The balls can be cooked several hours ahead and reheated just before serving by giving them a quick boil in the soup or water

title: Melboller (Flour Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- approximately 1/4 pound wheat flour
- 1 pail boiling water
- 4 eggs
- sugar to taste
- salt to taste
- nutmeg or cardamom to taste
- currants, rinsed and dried (optional)

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and stir in enough wheat flour to make it stiff
2. Bake while gradually adding the boiling water until the dough no longer sticks to the pan or spoon
3. Place the dough over coals and bake for another half hour while stirring
4. Remove from heat to cool slightly
5. Stir in the eggs one at a time until the dough becomes smooth and white
6. Add sugar, salt, nutmeg or cardamom to taste, and if desired, some rinsed and washed currants
7. Place the balls on the soup and cook for at most 10-15 minutes over steady heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Melboller (Meal Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 4 ounces butter
- 7 ounces wheat flour
- 1 ounce sugar
- 5 bitter almonds
- 3 pints milk

## Directions

1. Bring the milk to a boil. Add the sugar, butter and crushed almonds.
2. Whip the porridge until it leaves the casserole, cool and then work in the eggs.
3. Dip a spoon in water; use it to take balls from the dough and place them in boiling water.
4. Serve in boiled milk.

title: Melle-Fiuler
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- baking powder
- salt
- fat
- milk
- melted fat

## Directions

1. Make the dough the same as for biscuits and cut it the same way.
2. Brush the top of each square with melted fat.

title: Melon (Preserved Melon)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe melon, peeled and sliced
- Powdered loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Layer the ripe, peeled, and sliced melon with sugar and set aside until the next day.
2. Pour the liquid that has formed into a pot, bring to a boil, and skim before pouring hot over the melon slices.
3. Repeat this for the 2 following days.
4. The melon must not be cooked.

title: Melon med Cognac (Melon with Cognac)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Melon, peeled and cored
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Cognac

## Directions

1. Layer the peeled and cored melon with sugar in a jar, and pour the Cognac over it.
2. Seal immediately.

title: Melon med Ingefær (Melon with Ginger)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Melon, peeled and cut into finger-thick pieces
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Ground ginger
- Ground white pepper
- Cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Briefly place the peeled melon cut into finger-thick pieces in boiling water for a boil.
2. Pour it onto a colander and cover with cold water.
3. Layer the melon pieces in a jar with a mixture of sugar, ginger, pepper, and a very slight amount of cayenne pepper, and leave covered until the next day.
4. Pour off the liquid, cook and skim it, then place the melon in it for a single minute, remove onto a colander, and pour it from the jar, but only after the liquid has well drained.
5. Reduce this liquid until it is even, and pour it hot over the fruit, which is then sealed.

title: Melon med Rhinskvin (Melon with Rhine Wine)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Unripe netted melon, peeled
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Rhine wine
- Lemon peel

## Directions

1. Peel an unripe netted melon, remove the seeds, and cut the melon into smaller pieces, either with an ordinary knife or with a fluted herb knife.
2. Layer the melon pieces in a jar together with finely cut lemon peel (peel of 1 lemon to 1.5 kg melon).
3. Cook the sugar with the Rhine wine to a thin syrup, and pour it hot over the fruit.
4. The next day, pour off the liquid and reduce it to a very even syrup, which is again poured hot over the fruit.
5. Should the fruit after this treatment be too hard, it can simply be brought to a boil in the syrup.
6. Seal the jar as soon as the preserve is finished.

title: Melon med Rom (Melon with Rum)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Melon, peeled and cut into pieces
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Water
- White rum

## Directions

1. Cook sugar and water to a syrup, then add the rum.
2. Bring the peeled and cut melon to a boil in this.
3. Remove the fruit onto a colander, and reduce the liquid until it is very even to avoid boiling the next day.
4. Pour the liquid hot over the melon.
5. Seal the jar when the preserve has cooled.

title: Melon-Koldskaal (Melon Cold Fruit Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Kg (3 S) Melon
- 1/4 Kg (1 1/2 S) Powdered Sugar
- 2 Lemons

## Directions

1. Peel the ripe melon, scrape out the pits and cut the fruit into small pieces, sprinkle with 1/4 Kg (1 1/2 S) powdered sugar and press the juice of 2 lemons over them
2. Leave this for 1 hour and finish it with wine as in Nr. 486

title: Melon-Kompot (Melon Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ripe melon
- 1/2 Kg (1 S) powdered sugar
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægel) white rum
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the melon into slices about 1 good centimeter (1/2 inch) thick
2. Cut the slices into pieces of 5 centimeters (2 inches) length
3. Give the melon pieces a brief boiling in water
4. Cook the powdered sugar to syrup with the white rum and lemon juice
5. Cook the melon in this syrup
6. Remove the melon when cooked and place in the serving bowl
7. If necessary, boil the syrup a bit longer before pouring it over the melon

title: Melonskal (Candied Melon Rind)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 gr powdered sugar
- 1/2 l vinegar
- Melon rind

## Directions

1. Peel the melon very thinly and cut the rind into elongated pieces.
2. Boil sugar and vinegar to a syrup and skim before adding the melon rind to cook until tender.
3. Cook a few whole cloves, a couple pieces of cinnamon, and 1 teaspoon whole white pepper together with the rind.
4. Pour into jars and seal. Serve with roasted meat.

title: Melonskal (Melon Rind Preserve)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Melon rind, cut into oblong pieces
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Vinegar
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- White peppercorns

## Directions

1. Peel the melon very thinly, and cut the rind into oblong pieces.
2. Cook sugar and vinegar to a syrup and skim before adding the melon rind to cook until tender.
3. Cook some whole cloves, a few pieces of cinnamon, and 1 teaspoon of whole white pepper together with the rind.
4. Cool and seal. Serve.

title: Mi-careme-Suppe (Mid-Lenten Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds flounder
- 1 carrot
- 1 parsnip
- 1 onion
- Bundle of greens
- 1.25 quarts cold water
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 0.5 dozen lettuce leaves
- Slices of cold boiled beet
- 1 quart milk
- 2 ounces butter
- Flour
- White pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare the flounder as for fillet.
2. Chop up a carrot, a parsnip and an onion; prepare a bundle of greens as previously shown.
3. Place these vegetables on the fire in 1.25 quarts of cold water together with the fish and one teaspoon of salt, and bring the whole to a boil.
4. Gently remove the filet, place it between two plates to cool, and let the soup cook gently.
5. Cut half a dozen lettuce leaves into small rounds with an instrument such as a core remover, and plunge them for a minute in boiling, well-salted water, then dry them and place in cold water until needed.
6. Cut some slices of cold boiled beet in the same manner as the lettuce, and treat the filet in the same way; set all this aside until it is needed.
7. Rub the remainder of the filet through a strainer with a spoon.
8. Place 1 quart of milk on the fire to boil; place 2 ounces of butter on the fire beside it, and stir into it the same amount of flour.
9. Now thin this little by little with the boiling milk and with 1 quart of the water in which the vegetables have been cooked; season the soup with a little white pepper and grated nutmeg, and, if needed, with a little more salt.
10. Stir in the sieved fish and thicken the soup slowly with a whipped egg yolk.
11. When the soup is ready for serving, add the fish, lettuce and beets, and serve it immediately, so that the strongly contrasting colors do not lose their appearance.

title: Mignon-Salat (Mignon Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- tomatoes
- lettuce leaves
- mayonnaise
- celery leaf stalks

## Directions

1. Rinse a head of celery in cold water and cut it very small
2. Remove the skin from 250 grams of tomatoes and cut them into dice
3. Mix the celery and tomato together and arrange in small piles on lettuce leaves laid on a platter
4. On each pile place a little real mayonnaise, which should not completely cover the vegetables, and stick the top of the leaf stalks in the piles of salad for garnish
5. Keep in a cool place until ready to serve

title: Mimis-Kager (Mimi's Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 egg yolks
- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon hartshorn salt
- either lemon, almond, or vanilla essence
- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) flour
- the beaten egg whites
- blanched almonds, split

## Directions

1. Mix the egg yolks well with the powdered sugar and hartshorn salt
2. Add either lemon, almond, or vanilla essence, as well as the flour and finally the beaten egg whites
3. Place the cakes on buttered baking sheets with a teaspoon, with generous spacing between them
4. Top each with a split almond
5. Bake very light

title: Mince Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- Pie dough
- Mince pie filling

## Directions

1. Line the form with good dough.
2. Put in a thick layer of filling.
3. Lay dough over as a lid.
4. Prick holes in the lid and bake in a hot oven; they should be quite light brown, but not hard.

title: Mince Pie Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds fresh beef
- 1 pound suet
- 5 pounds apples
- 2 pounds raisins
- 2 pounds currants
- 1 pound citron
- 2 tablespoons ground cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon grated nutmeg
- 1 tablespoon cloves
- 1 tablespoon allspice
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 3 pounds brown sugar
- 1 quart wine
- Broth from the beef

## Directions

1. Cook 6 pounds fresh beef tender in barely covered water.
2. When cold, chop it fine.
3. Chop 1 pound suet fine.
4. Peel and chop 5 pounds apples.
5. Remove stones from 2 pounds raisins.
6. Mix together the beef, suet, apples, raisins, 2 pounds currants, 1 pound citron, 2 tablespoons ground cinnamon, 1 tablespoon grated nutmeg, 1 tablespoon cloves, 1 tablespoon allspice, 1 tablespoon salt, 3 pounds brown sugar, 1 quart wine, and the broth in which the meat was cooked.
7. Put in a stone jar with a double layer of paper over it.
8. It must be made at least a day before it is to be used.
9. When the pies are prepared, add a little more wine and a little more chopped apple to what you take for the pies.
10. This kind of pie is used especially for Christmas and other holidays and is therefore made in large batches; it will also keep a long time and is eaten both hot and cold.

title: Mince Pie Filling No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds raisins
- 1 pound currants
- 1 pound suet
- 2.5 pounds brown sugar
- 0.5 pound citron
- 2 tablespoons ground cinnamon
- 3 lemons
- 1 nutmeg
- Wine
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Mix together 2 pounds raisins, 1 pound currants, 1 pound suet, 2.5 pounds brown sugar, 0.5 pound citron, 2 tablespoons ground cinnamon, the grated zest and juice of 3 lemons, 1 grated nutmeg, wine, and cloves to taste.

title: Mince Pie Filling No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints peeled and chopped apples
- 1 pint cooked chopped meat
- 0.5 pint butter or chopped suet
- 1 pint molasses
- 0.5 pint water
- 1.5 tablespoons allspice
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 0.5 teaspoon cloves
- 2.5 large tablespoons vinegar
- 3 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix together 3 pints peeled and chopped apples, 1 pint cooked chopped meat, 0.5 pint butter or chopped suet, 1 pint molasses, 0.5 pint water, 1.5 tablespoons allspice, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon cloves, 2.5 large tablespoons vinegar, and 3 nutmeg.

title: Mirabeller (Mirabelle Plums)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 3/4 dl water
- 3/4 dl cognac
- 1/2 kg mirabelle plums

## Directions

1. These small yellow plums must not be completely ripe.
2. Pour boiling water over them, then cold water.
3. Boil sugar and water to a thin syrup and add the mirabelles, giving them just one boil.
4. Remove the plums and place in a jar, and cook down the syrup before pouring it hot over the fruit.
5. Cover the preserve immediately.
6. After 2 days, pour off the syrup to cook down further, and add 1/4 dl cognac as soon as the pot is removed from heat, then immediately pour the syrup over the mirabelles and seal.
7. Mirabelles are excellent for making into compote.

title: Miroton (Miroton)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cut beef roast
- Stock from the roast
- Potato puree (No. 651)
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Pickled cucumbers, chopped

## Directions

1. Place the cut beef roast on a flat dish that can be exposed to heat in the oven
2. Pour a good beef sauce made from the stock of the roast over the meat
3. Cover the meat with a layer of potato puree (No. 651), raised in the middle
4. Brush with egg
5. Sprinkle with bread
6. Place in the oven until it has a brownish color
7. Chopped, pickled cucumbers can be placed as decoration on top of the puree

title: Mixed Pickles
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 l (5 pots) vinegar, one of which should preferably be tarragon vinegar
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) ground ginger
- 250 gr (1/2 lb) English mustard (Colman's Mustard)
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) salt
- 25 gr (5 drams) ground white pepper
- 10 gr (2 drams) ground Cayenne pepper
- A little Indian saffron to give it a beautiful yellowish color or 2 tablespoons curry
- Small carrots (scraped)
- Small cucumbers (dried)
- Sugar peas (ribboned and cut into smaller pieces)
- String beans (ribboned and cut into smaller pieces)
- Pearl beans (ribboned and cut into smaller pieces)
- Cauliflower (divided into small florets)
- Pearl onions (peeled)
- Shallots (peeled)
- Red onions (peeled)
- Celery (peeled and cut into pieces)
- Nasturtium seeds

## Directions

1. Mix all these spices into some of the vinegar before adding the remaining vinegar.
2. Cover the jar with a lid or tie it shut with parchment paper and let it stand for 3 to 4 weeks, but it must be stirred regularly in the meantime.
3. Dry the fresh vegetables in a cloth and they must be completely dry before being placed in the vinegar, gradually as they appear during the course of the summer.
4. While the pickles are being prepared, stir frequently, and only when all the vegetables have been collected and laid down, distribute them in smaller jars or glasses, which are tied shut and stored.
5. As they are stored well, the raw vegetables become sufficiently soft.

title: Mixed Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- Green beans
- Cauliflower
- Sour cucumbers
- Carrots
- Onions
- Green melon
- 20 peppercorns
- Spanish pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Give all the vegetables one boiling, and let them drain carefully
2. Layer them together with the pepper in square glass bottles with wide necks
3. Pour the vinegar over and cork the bottles tightly

title: Modne Stikkelsbær med Cognac (Ripe Gooseberries with Cognac)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 gr powdered sugar
- 1/4 dl red currant juice
- 1/2 kg picked berries
- Cognac

## Directions

1. From ripe thin-skinned gooseberries cut off the flower and stem
2. Pour sugar and water in the pot and when melted add the berries to cook for 1/4 hour
3. Remove the gooseberries with slotted spoon and place on a colander then cook the juice again and skim until thick
4. Add 1 glass of Cognac per 1/2 kg of berries
5. Put the berries in jar and pour the hot juice over them
6. Seal when the preserve is done
7. Instead of Cognac the gooseberries can be cooked with a few pieces of whole cinnamon and a few whole cloves

title: Modne Stikkelsbær med Ribssaft (Ripe Gooseberries with Red Currant Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 gr powdered sugar
- 1/4 dl red currant juice
- 1/2 kg picked berries

## Directions

1. Treat the gooseberries as per the previous instructions
2. The only difference is that the sugar is cooked with red currant juice instead of water and the Cognac is omitted

title: Modne Tomater med Citron (Ripe Tomatoes with Lemon)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 2 lemons
- 1/2 kg ripe tomatoes

## Directions

1. Tomatoes are available until late autumn but as soon as night frost begins they stop
2. Ripe but not overripe tomatoes are dried well pricked with a needle and placed in jar in layers with the powdered sugar
3. The next day the juice is poured off and cooked together with the juice of 2 lemons then poured hot over the tomatoes
4. The two following days the cooking of the juice is repeated and both times it is poured hot over
5. To 2 1/2 kg tomatoes dissolve 1/2 teaspoon salicylic acid in a little Cognac and pour this solution over the glasses or jars which are sealed as soon as possible
6. The salicylic acid must not be omitted with tomatoes as it greatly helps preserve them

title: Mohrenkopf (Moor's Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 pounds sweet almonds
- 15 pounds powdered sugar
- 1.5 pints water

## Directions

1. Scald the sweet almonds and chop them somewhat fine and place them in a pan together with the powdered sugar and water. Cook it with constant stirring until the sugar has melted and thereafter become dry and finally the whole has taken on a beautiful light brown color. Then pour another good half pint of water on it and let it cook again until it is like a good, thick porridge. Then pour it into a tin form that must be dipped in cold water, or it will stick to it. Quickly press the sugar firmly in the form with a silver spoon dipped in cold water and let it then stand for 5-6 hours until it is cold, then pour it out of the form.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Morbær (Mulberries)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 gr powdered sugar
- 1/2 kg mulberries

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the ripe mulberries with sugar and let sit until the next day.
2. Pour everything into the pot and boil for only 4 to 5 minutes.
3. Remove the mulberries with a slotted spoon and boil and skim the syrup for at least 10 minutes.
4. Pour the boiling syrup over the berries and seal the jar.
5. After a few days, check it, and if the syrup is too thin, pour it off, bring to a boil, and pour immediately back over the berries.

title: Morbærsaft (Mulberry Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mulberries
- 1/3 kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar per 1 liter (1 pot) juice

## Directions

1. Press the berries with a spoon and pour into a sieve or jelly cloth.
2. Measure the juice that runs through, boil with the sugar and skim for 10 minutes.
3. Pour hot into bottles which are tied immediately.

title: Mordeig (Fine Pastry Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sifted flour
- barely 1/2 pound butter
- 1/4 ounce sugar (or slightly less)
- 2 eggs
- barely 1 gill cream
- 1/2 ounce salt

## Directions

1. Place the flour on the table and make a depression as usual; place in it the sugar, salt, butter in small pieces, eggs and cream; work together well and moisten with a little cream that has been held back.
2. This dough should be loose, so if it has become too stiff, add even more cream.
3. Before use, it should rest 1 to 2 hours in a cool place.

title: Muffins
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram Pillsbury flour
- 4 teaspoons baking powder
- 375 grams margarine
- 125 grams powdered sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 liter half-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Mix the Pillsbury flour and baking powder together
2. Add the margarine, powdered sugar, eggs, and half-skimmed milk
3. Knead the batter well and roll out to a 1/4 inch thick sheet
4. Cut out round cakes and place close together on a baking sheet
5. Bake immediately in a fairly warm oven for approximately 20 minutes
6. While warm, split the cakes, spread with butter, and fold together
7. Serve immediately or reheat in the oven for a few minutes before serving. Makes 30 muffins

title: Mulberries (Morbær)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pd. mulberries
- 2 Pd. Melis

## Directions

1. Boil the Melis to a very thick syrup
2. Place the berries in a shallow dish and pour the sugar syrup over them
3. Cover and let stand for 2 days
4. Pour off the syrup and boil it
5. When slightly cooled, pour the syrup back over the berries and let stand until the next day
6. Give the syrup a final boiling, then add the berries and boil gently together
7. When cooled, pour into a jar and seal in the usual manner
8. After 8 days, check if the syrup needs to be poured off and boiled again
9. When cooled, distribute into smaller jars
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Multebær (Cloudberries)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/2 kg cloudberries
- Cognac

## Directions

1. These Norwegian berries are picked and sprinkled with sugar, then left until the next day.
2. Pour everything into the pot and bring just to a boil.
3. Remove the berries with a slotted spoon and place in a jar.
4. Cook the syrup until it is smooth, then add 1 sherry glass of cognac for each 1/2 kg fruit.
5. Pour the boiling syrup over the berries and seal once the preserve has cooled.

title: Multebær-Suppe (Cloudberry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cloudberries
- 5.5 L water
- raisins
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- potato flour or sago flour
- white wine
- whipped cream
- small macarons
- almonds

## Directions

1. Cook berries in water and strain
2. Bring soup to boil again with raisins, whole cinnamon and lemon peel
3. Thicken with potato flour or sago flour mixed with water
4. Season with a little white wine
5. Serve with whipped cream and small macarons or coarsely chopped or lengthwise-cut almonds

title: Muslinge-Salat (Mussel Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked mussels
- Pickled fish or small fish balls
- Mustard seed vinegar
- Tarragon vinegar
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as in No. 8, adding mustard seed and tarragon vinegar
2. Place the cooked mussels in the sauce along with some flaked fish or finely cut fish balls
3. Lay some mussels and lemon slices around it

title: Muslinger (Mussels)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- butter
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mussels are only available in winter. Cook them as instructed in Nr. 8 and can be used like oysters in many dishes.
2. Cooked mussels should be brought steaming to the table; they are arranged unopened in a deep dish, covered with a napkin.
3. Serve with melted butter, vinegar, and pepper.

title: Muslinger (Mussels)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels
- Melted butter
- Vinegar
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook mussels as directed in No. 8
2. Bring cooked mussels steaming to the table
3. Arrange unopened in a deep dish, covered with a napkin
4. Serve with melted butter, vinegar and pepper

title: Muslinger (Mussels)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels
- Salt
- Onions
- Nutmeg
- Peppercorns
- Bay leaves
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Rinse the mussels in several changes of cold water and remove the black tuft.
2. Place them in a pot over the fire without water, but with a little salt and some onions between them.
3. Cook covered with their own liquid and shake occasionally.
4. When cooked and all opened, serve hot in the shells with stirred butter, ground pepper, vinegar and lemon.
5. To preserve them for use in sauces or cold dishes: Remove the mussels from the shells and cut off the beard and black intestine.
6. Cook the trimmings along with the juice from the mussels with nutmeg, peppercorns, some bay leaves and a little vinegar.
7. Strain and pour over the mussels, which are meanwhile placed in a crock or glass.
8. For longer storage, seal the glass with butter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Muslingesauce (Mussel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- butter
- flour
- white wine
- lemon juice
- soup or broth

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as Oyster Sauce, but do not use the beards, only the liquor and the mussels without beards.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Muslingskaller (Mussel Shell Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 325 grams flour
- 190 grams margarine
- 190 grams powdered sugar
- 75 grams crushed almonds
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mix flour, margarine, powdered sugar, crushed almonds, and egg into a dough
2. Roll out the dough very thinly
3. Line small mussel shell molds (baking forms) with the dough
4. Bake the cakes light brown in the oven
5. Serve as cornucopias

title: Muslingskaller (Shell-Shaped Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 62 quintal flour
- 36 quintal light powdered sugar
- 37 quintal butter
- a handful of scalded, finely chopped almonds
- 1 egg
- whipped cream with vanilla sugar
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Knead the flour, sugar, butter, almonds, and egg until the dough releases from the dish; then press the dough into small shell molds that have been buttered beforehand. Bake them in a moderately warm oven and serve cold, filled with whipped cream with vanilla sugar and a little currant jelly on top.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mælkepunsch af Arrak (Milk Punch with Arrack)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ pounds white sugar
- 2 pots boiled water
- 12 measures arrack
- juice of 12 lemons and peel of 5 lemons
- 1 pot boiling sweet milk with a half vanilla pod boiled in it

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1½ pounds white sugar in 2 pots boiled water
2. Add 12 measures arrack (white rum can be used instead) and juice of 12 lemons and peel of 5 lemons
3. Pour in 1 pot boiling sweet milk in which a half vanilla pod has been boiled
4. Let the punch stand until the next day, then filter and pour into bottles, which are corked
5. Drink cold
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mælkepunsch af Rom (Milk Punch with Rum)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds white sugar
- 3 pots boiling water
- 3 bottles (9 measures) best white rum
- 1 grated nutmeg
- 1 vanilla pod
- juice of 10 lemons and thinly pared yellow peel of lemons
- 1½ pots boiling sweet milk

## Directions

1. Dissolve 3 pounds white sugar in 3 pots boiling water
2. Add 3 bottles (9 measures) best white rum, 1 grated nutmeg, 1 vanilla pod, juice of 10 lemons and thinly pared yellow peel
3. Add 1½ pots boiling sweet milk
4. Let the mixture stand, covered, until the next day
5. Filter it a couple of times and pour into bottles, which are corked
6. Drink cold
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Mørdeig til Kjødpostejer (Short Crust Dough for Meat Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. butter
- 5 eggs
- 1 pint sweet cream
- flour

## Directions

1. Wash the butter free of salt and stir until quite white with 5 eggs and 1 pint sweet cream.
2. When well mixed, add enough flour to make a suitably thick dough.
3. Set on a cold place to stiffen.
4. This mixture can be used for all kinds of meat pies.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Napoleon-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams margarine
- 300 grams fine sugar
- 7 egg yolks
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- a little almond essence
- 250 milliliters semi-skimmed milk
- 625 grams Pillsbury flour
- egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Mix margarine with fine sugar
2. Add egg yolks, baking powder, almond essence, and semi-skimmed milk
3. Stir in Pillsbury flour
4. Fold in whipped egg whites last
5. Grease form well and line with paper
6. Pour batter in, sprinkle sugar and chopped almonds on top, and bake in a moderately warm oven for about 1 hour

title: Napoleonssuppe (Napoleon Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 til 20 Rødløg
- et Fjerdingspund Smør
- 1 Haandfuld Hvede- eller Sagomel
- omtrent 4 Potter brun Sky
- 25 smaa pillede Rødløg
- nogle i fine Skiver skaarne Persillerødder
- bagte Terninger af Franskbrød

## Directions

1. Pilles 10 til 20 Rødløg, skæres i Skiver og kommes i et Fjerdingspund smeltet Smør for at svede, medens de jævnlig røres med en Træske.
2. Røres forsigtig, af det ikke sætter sig paa Bunden, 1 Haandfuld Hvede- eller Sagomel deri.
3. Fortyndes det med omtrent 4 Potter brun Sky, indtil det bliver som tynd Havresuppe.
4. Det koges et Kvarterstid og rives derefter gjennem en Sigte.
5. Hældes Suppen paany i Gryden tilligemed 25 smaa pillede Rødløg og nogle i fine Skiver skaarne Persillerødder.
6. Suppen koges derefter, indtil Løgene ere møre, og anrettes med bagte Terninger af Franskbrød.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Nedlægning af Sild til Kryddersild (Preparation of Spiced Herring)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 barrel of herring
- 1/2 kg (1 pound) kitchen salt
- 1 L (1 pot) vinegar
- 1 L (1 pot) water
- 1 kg (2 pounds) Lüneburg salt
- 1/4 kg (1/2 pound) powdered sugar
- 15 grams (3 quintals) Spanish hops
- 20 bay leaves
- 25 grams (5 quintals) cloves
- 25 grams (5 quintals) allspice
- 50 grams (10 quintals) pepper
- 15 grams (3 quintals) saltpeter

## Directions

1. Use herring from autumn if possible
2. Between 1 barrel of herring (which must not be cleaned beforehand), sprinkle 1/2 kg (1 pound) of kitchen salt and let it stand for 12 hours under a light press
3. Take each herring from the brine, scrape off the scales, lay them again and cover with 1 L (1 pot) of vinegar mixed with 1 L (1 pot) of water
4. Let stand for another 24 hours
5. Take up the herrings and let the vinegar drain well before laying them again with the spices
6. For 1 barrel of herring, combine 1 kg (2 pounds) of Lüneburg salt, 1/4 kg (1/2 pound) of powdered sugar, 15 grams (3 quintals) of Spanish hops, 20 bay leaves, and of ground spices: 25 grams (5 quintals) of cloves, 25 grams (5 quintals) of allspice, 50 grams (10 quintals) of pepper and 15 grams (3 quintals) of saltpeter
7. Lay the herrings with the back downward in a butter barrel

title: Nedlægning af Sild til Spegesild (Preserved Herring for Curing)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh, newly caught autumn herring
- Kitchen salt
- 1.5 kg (3 S) Lüneburg salt
- A wooden barrel or butter churn, well-cleaned and sealed

## Directions

1. Lay fresh, newly caught autumn herring without cleaning them in a barrel and sprinkle with kitchen salt
2. Leave them for a couple of hours and stir regularly with a stick so that the salt reaches between the herring properly and the blood is drawn out of them
3. Take them out and drain them, then lay them again in a well-cleaned and sealed butter churn
4. Lay the herring with the back downward and pack them in tight layers, between which is sprinkled at least 1.5 kg (3 S) Lüneburg salt
5. The top layer must be salt, and over it lay the loose wooden bottom with a good weight on it so that the brine can stand over them
6. The herring are usable after 4 to 6 weeks

title: Nesselrode Budding (Nesselrode Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- 8 egg yolks
- 1/2 stick vanilla
- 1/2 pound white sugar
- 3 pails Madeira
- 6 quintals dissolved isinglass
- 1 pail sweet cream, whipped to stiff foam
- finely chopped candied citron
- cleaned raisins without pits
- candied strawberries without juice

## Directions

1. Heat 1 pot sweet cream with 8 egg yolks, 1/2 stick vanilla, and 1/2 pound white sugar, stirring well while bringing to a boil.
2. Pour out when done and while still warm, add 3 pails Madeira and 6 quintals dissolved isinglass.
3. Before it cools completely, stir in 1 pail sweet cream whipped to stiff foam, as well as a handful of finely chopped candied citron, an equal amount of cleaned raisins without pits, and some candied strawberries without juice.
4. In a mold, layer candied strawberries at the bottom, then a layer of the mixture, then again a layer of jam, and so forth until the mold is full.
5. Let the pudding stand for several hours in a cold place or freeze in an ice box.
6. Quickly dip the mold in hot water, turn it out onto a dish, and serve the pudding with raspberry jelly on top.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Norsk Budding (Norwegian Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces rice flour
- 5 ounces butter
- 5 ounces white sugar
- 1.5 pints thin cream
- 18 egg yolks
- 4 ounces sweet, finely ground almonds
- 6 to 8 bitter almonds
- 2 ounces finely cut citron
- 1 stick ground vanilla
- 2 ounces currants
- A little chopped lemon peel
- Egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Cream the rice flour, butter, and white sugar together well and gradually add the thin cream.
2. Pour this mixture and stir in 18 egg yolks, then add the sweet and bitter almonds blended together, the citron, vanilla, currants, and chopped lemon peel.
3. Finally fold in the beaten egg whites.
4. Boil for 2.5 hours.
5. Serve with sweet sauce; serves 14 persons.

title: Norsk Fiskefars (Norwegian Fish Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg scraped fish
- 100 grams cold butter
- 2 tablespoons flour, pounded
- ground white pepper
- milk

## Directions

1. In Norway, pollack is much used for forcemeat
2. Split and clean the fish, remove the bone
3. Scrape very carefully (do not chop) and pound in a mortar until it is like a very fine dough, finally pound it with salt to make it even more tough and cohesive
4. Stir the cold butter until it is white
5. Add 2 tablespoons pounded flour, ground white pepper and a little milk until it becomes a soft porridge
6. When well stirred together, work in the scraped fish and stir the forcemeat with quite small amounts of milk at a time until it reaches proper firmness, which is tested by cooking a test ball
7. This makes an excellent forcemeat

title: Norsk Frimurerpunsch (Norwegian Freemason's Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot water
- 1 pound white top sugar
- 1 bottle good Cognac
- 1 bottle white French wine (Sauternes)
- 1 bottle Sherry

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pot water with 1 pound white top sugar and skim well
2. Let it cool
3. When cold, filter it and pour into a tureen with 1 bottle good Cognac, 1 bottle white French wine (Sauternes) and 1 bottle Sherry
4. Stir and pour into bottles, which are corked
5. Drink the punch cold, mixed with seltzer water
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Norsk Pund-Kage (Norwegian Pound Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1 pound sugar
- 10 eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks and sugar together
2. Then add the butter, the beaten whites and flour last
3. Usually baked in smaller pans and glazed

title: Norsk Øllebrød (Norwegian Ale Bread Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 L (2 Potter) pale ale
- 2 L (2 Potter) milk
- a good tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. Stir a good tablespoon of flour in some of the cold milk, which is stirred in when the milk boils.
2. The ale is cooked separately, sweetened and poured simultaneously with the milk into a tureen, while whisking vigorously with a whisk.
3. It is better thickened with Maizena.
4. Serve rye bread cubes baked in butter and sugar with it.

title: Norsk Øllebrød (Norwegian Beer Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot light beer
- 2 pots milk
- 2 tablespoons wheat flour
- sugar to taste
- baked rye bread in cubes

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pot of light beer
2. Boil 2 pots of milk with 2 tablespoons of wheat flour stirred in
3. Blend both parts together
4. Add sugar to taste
5. Bring to a boil once more and immediately remove from heat to prevent it from separating
6. Serve with baked rye bread in cubes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Norsk Øllebrød (Norwegian Beer Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot white beer
- 2 pots milk
- 2 spoonfuls (10 drams) wheat flour
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pot of white beer and likewise 2 pots of milk in which 2 spoonfuls (10 drams) of wheat flour have been stirred.
2. Mix both parts together, add sugar to taste, give it a boil, and take it off the fire immediately so that it does not separate.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Norske Tvebakker (Norwegian Twice-Baked Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons white sugar
- half a pound flour
- cardamom
- rind of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Whip 1 pint cream to foam.
2. When 4 egg yolks are stirred white, stir 3 tablespoons white sugar into them, together with half a pound flour.
3. Finally add the whipped egg whites, as well as a little cardamom and grated rind of half a lemon.
4. Bake best in paper forms in a good warm oven.

title: Norske Æbleskiver (Norwegian Apple Pancake Balls / Monks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 tb) fine flour
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) melted margarine
- 1 L (1 Pot) sweet milk
- 8 egg yolks
- cardamom
- grated lemon zest
- sugar

## Directions

1. In fine flour, stir 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) melted margarine, 1 L (1 Pot) sweet milk, 8 egg yolks, cardamom, grated lemon zest and sugar to taste.
2. Beat the egg whites and add them last.
3. Bake as soon as the dough is mixed and can, as previously mentioned, be filled with apples, or the fruit is served separately.

title: Nougats-Is (Nougat Ice Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 Gr (30 Kvint) almonds
- 1 L (1 Pot) cream or milk
- nougat as prepared in Nr. 1062

## Directions

1. See preparation of nougat, Nr. 1062.
2. Stir the crushed nougat into vanilla cream made with 1 L (1 Pot) cream.
3. Set aside for freezing.

title: Nyre-Steg af Kalv (Roasted Veal Kidney)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half the back with kidney from veal without leg piece
- Bacon
- Butter
- Egg
- Grated breadcrumbs
- 1 carrot
- Onion
- Capers
- Parsley
- Lemon peel
- Cream
- 1/2 pint water
- Seasonings
- Anchovies (if available)

## Directions

1. Remove the kidney, backbone ribs, and ribs.
2. Make a forcemeat from veal, bacon, egg, anchovies, grated breadcrumbs, onion, parsley, lemon, cream, and seasonings.
3. Spread this forcemeat on the inside of the meat piece.
4. Roll it together, bind with twine, brown with butter, and add water.
5. Add the carrot.
6. Roast for 1 to 2 hours, basting frequently with the sauce.
7. When done, remove the binding.
8. Skim the fat from the sauce, add cream, and add 2 spoonfuls of capers.
9. Serve.

title: Nyre-Suppe (Kidney Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 calf's kidneys
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) Flour
- 4 L (4 Pots) brown spare soup
- 125 Gr (1/4 Q) Macaroni
- Water

## Directions

1. Rinse the kidneys well and cut them into small cubes
2. Brown and steam them partially tender in 125 Gr (1/4 S) butter with a little salt and pepper
3. Brown 125 Gr (1/4 S) butter and 125 Gr (1/4 S) flour and dilute with 4 L (4 Pots) brown spare soup
4. When it boils, add the kidneys together with their juices and bring to a boil
5. Cook 125 Gr (1/4 Q) macaroni in salted water, cut into small rings and place in the tureen
6. Serve with salt sticks

title: Okse-Filet (Beef Fillet)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg beef fillet (calculated as ¼ kg per person)
- Speck (larding fat), in thin strips
- Butter for browning
- Gravy or stock
- Flour
- Tomato purée or Maggi bouillon cubes, if needed
- French bread or household bread for the base
- Egg white
- Vegetables for garnish

## Directions

1. Remove the sinews and fat from the fillet before larding it.
2. Lard the fillet thickly in neat rows with fine, fresh speck strips.
3. Brown the fillet in butter on a roasting pan or on the stovetop, without piercing it as this causes it to lose juice.
4. When brown on both sides, place it in a roasting pan in a hot oven, pour some gravy over it, and roast with frequent basting for half an hour.
5. When the fillet is roasted, strain off the gravy and make a strong, smooth gravy sauce as for roast beef.
6. If the gravy is not strong enough, improve it with tomato purée or Maggi bouillon cubes.
7. For presentation, take a slice of French bread or household bread, cut off the upper crust, and attach it to the center of an elongated platter with a mixture of flour and egg white.
8. On this base, arrange the roasted fillet, sliced thinly and overlapped together.
9. Surround the base with vegetables and arrange herb salads in shells or bird's nests around the presentation.

title: Okse-Filet med Champignons (Beef Filet with Mushrooms)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef filet, roasted
- Brown mushroom sauce (see recipe 758)
- Stuffed tomatoes (see recipe 236)
- Parsley for garnish
- Mushroom
- Salad
- Tomato
- Whole mushrooms

## Directions

1. Serve the roasted filet with a brown mushroom sauce made from the gravy
2. Garnish the dish with stuffed tomatoes interspersed with parsley
3. A skewer can be passed through a mushroom, salad and a tomato, and used to garnish the filet
4. Some whole mushrooms can be placed on top of the meat

title: Okse-Filet med farserede Agurker (Beef Filet with Stuffed Cucumbers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef filet, roasted
- Stuffed cucumbers (see recipe 218)
- Fresh tomato slices
- Salad or parsley for garnish
- Gravy from the filet
- Gravy from the cucumbers

## Directions

1. Around the roasted filet, arrange slices of stuffed cucumbers alternately with slices of fresh tomatoes
2. Garnish the dish with salad or parsley
3. Make a smooth brown sauce from the gravy of the filet and the gravy from the cucumbers

title: Okse-Filet med farserede Agurker (Beef Fillet with Stuffed Cucumbers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted beef fillet
- Stuffed cucumbers
- Fresh tomatoes
- Salad or parsley for garnish
- Gravy from the fillet
- Gravy from the cucumbers

## Directions

1. Around the roasted fillet, arrange slices of stuffed cucumbers alternating with slices of fresh tomatoes.
2. Make a smooth, brown gravy sauce from the gravy of the fillet and the gravy from the cucumbers.
3. Garnish the dish with salad or parsley.

title: Okse-Filet med glaserede Løg (Beef Filet with Glazed Onions)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted beef filet
- Glazed onions (see recipe 671)
- Browned potatoes (see recipe 647)

## Directions

1. Garnish the roasted beef filet with glazed onions and browned potatoes

title: Okse-Filet med Grønsager (Beef Filet with Vegetables)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef filet
- Butter
- Flour
- Cooking gravy
- Vegetables of the season, either preserved or fresh
- Butter for piping

## Directions

1. Lard, brown and roast the filet as in recipe 814
2. Make the sauce from browned butter and flour, thinned with the strained cooking gravy to a smooth sauce of appropriate consistency
3. Serve vegetables either preserved or whatever the season brings, and with these garnish the base on which the filet is laid
4. Serve the sauce in a sauce bowl
5. Small tartlets can be filled with creamed butter for the vegetables and placed around them
6. Pass the butter through a piping bag

title: Okse-Filet med Madeira-Sky (Beef Filet with Madeira Gravy)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Good soup or gravy
- Madeira wine
- Beef filet
- Sago flour

## Directions

1. To baste the filet you must have a good soup or gravy which gives a beautiful brown color, is well reduced and prepared with Madeira to taste
2. With this gravy baste the browned filet and roast it in the gravy
3. When the filet is nearly done, strain the gravy, skim off fat and thicken if necessary with sago flour
4. Add more Madeira to taste

title: Okse-Filet med Oliven (Beef Filet with Olives)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted beef filet
- Good olive sauce (see recipe 767)
- Olives for garnish
- French potatoes (recipe 661)

## Directions

1. Serve the roasted filet with a good olive sauce
2. Garnish the filet with olives and serve with French potatoes

title: Okse-Filet med skrabet Peberrod eller Peberrods-Parfait (Beef Filet with Scraped Horseradish or Horseradish Parfait)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted beef filet
- Scraped horseradish
- Watercress
- Browned potatoes
- ½ L whipped cream
- 60 grams grated horseradish
- Brown gravy with Madeira

## Directions

1. On the roasted filet place a strip of scraped horseradish, interspersed with small bunches of watercress
2. Place a similar garnish around it and outside this again browned potatoes
3. Or instead of the scraped horseradish, make a horseradish parfait from ½ L whipped cream, prepared with 60 grams grated horseradish
4. Freeze this parfait in approximately 4 hours and garnish the roasted filet with it
5. Place the ice either in gratin shells or in hollowed-out tomatoes; it can also be arranged on salad leaves
6. Serve with a brown gravy sauce with Madeira

title: Okse-Filet med Trøfler (Beef Filet with Truffles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted beef filet
- Gravy from the filet
- Sago flour
- Truffles
- Madeira wine
- Roasted or glazed chestnuts (see recipes 663-664)

## Directions

1. When the filet is roasted, pour off the gravy; strain it, skim it and thicken with sago flour stirred smooth
2. Cut truffles into slices and braise briefly in Madeira, then place in the gravy and bring to a boil
3. Reserve a few slices and place on top of the filet
4. Garnish with roasted or glazed chestnuts

title: Oksebryst med Tomat-Sauce og Makaroni (Beef Brisket with Tomato Sauce and Macaroni)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover beef
- Tomato Sauce (see Nr. 790)
- Cooked macaroni
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Heat leftover beef in tomato sauce
2. Serve with cooked macaroni sprinkled with Parmesan cheese

title: Oksehale-Ragout (Oxtail Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Oxtail
- Madeira
- Paprika
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Tomato purée
- Egg

## Directions

1. Prepare as for soup (see Nr. 389), but use significantly less liquid for the ragout, as the sauce should be more robust.
2. The sauce should have a fine color and is made with good Madeira, paprika, Maggi Bouillon cubes and tomato purée.
3. Arrange the ragout on a flat, warm platter and garnish with egg.

title: Oksehale-Suppe (Oxtail Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oxtails
- 5 L (5 Pots) spare soup
- Butter or Claret
- 1 pair of onions, diced
- 4 whole carrots
- Paprika
- Sherry
- Tomato Purée
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Sago flour
- Water
- Kulør (caramel coloring)
- Sliced eggs or hard-boiled egg yolks

## Directions

1. Order the oxtails from the butcher
2. Cut them into pieces joint by joint, wash in cold water and scald in boiling water
3. Brown butter or claret in a pot, and in it fry 1 pair of diced onions, 4 whole carrots and a little paprika
4. Brown the tails together with the carrots
5. Cook them tender in 5 L (5 Pots) thin, brown spare soup or water with salt for at least 3 hours or until the meat easily separates from the bones
6. Remove them
7. Strain the soup, skim and season with Sherry, a little Tomato Purée and Maggi Bouillon cubes if needed
8. Thicken with sago flour mixed in water
9. Put the tails back in the soup to keep warm until serving and serve in the soup, which is given a nice brown color with kulør
10. For Oxtail Soup serve sliced eggs or hard-boiled egg yolks, 1 in each plate

title: Oksekød med Peberrod-Sauce og Sveske-Kompot (Beef with Horseradish Sauce and Prune Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Salt
- Horseradish Sauce (see Nr. 784)
- Cooked prunes

## Directions

1. Heat the meat first in soup or water with salt
2. Cut the meat into pieces
3. Place on a hot serving dish
4. Pour a good horseradish sauce over it
5. The dish can be garnished with a wreath of cooked prunes, or these can be served separately

title: Oksekød med Peberrod-Sauce og Sveske-Kompot (Beef with Horseradish Sauce and Prune Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Soup or water
- Salt
- Horseradish sauce (No. 784)
- Cooked prunes

## Directions

1. Warm the meat first in soup or water with salt
2. Cut the meat
3. Place on a hot dish
4. Pour a good horseradish sauce over it (see No. 784)
5. The dish can be decorated with a ring of cooked prunes, or these can be served separately

title: Oksekød med Sauce å la diable (Beef with Devil Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cut beef
- Sauce à la diable (No. 760)
- Potato puree (No. 336)

## Directions

1. Warm the cut meat in sauce à la diable (see No. 760)
2. Serve in a border formed by potato puree (No. 336)

title: Oksekødfars (Beef Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg scraped beef
- 125 g tallow or fresh fat pork
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 2 to 3 eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1 small onion, grated
- 1/4 to 1 liter milk

## Directions

1. Cut the meat and if you do not have a meat grinder, pound it with a meat hammer to remove sinews more easily.
2. Chop 1/4 kg scraped beef very finely with 125 g tallow or fresh fat pork.
3. Work in 3 tablespoons flour, 2 to 3 eggs, salt, pepper and 1 small grated onion.
4. Add milk very gradually, stirring constantly in the same direction, as the forcemeat will easily separate otherwise. For this portion use 1/4 to 1 liter milk, but this depends on how long the forcemeat is being worked.
5. If you have a meat grinder, it is much easier to prepare the forcemeat.
6. If you want the forcemeat even finer, after stirring, press it through a coarse sieve.
7. When ready, cook a test ball to ensure the forcemeat is sufficiently soft.

title: Oksesteg (Roast Beef)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef (Thick steak, tenderloin, or thin steak)
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. When purchasing, allow 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) per person.
2. Beat the meat well with a meat mallet before rinsing.
3. Place it in the roasting pan and put it in the hot oven for a few minutes before pouring boiling water with some salt into the pan.
4. This way the pores contract, and the meat becomes juicier.
