5. When placing the meat in the pan, turn the tenderloin upward, i.e., with the outer fillet (the most meaty side) down toward the pan.
6. It must be basted frequently during roasting, which must proceed very evenly.
7. A beef roast (thick steak) of 5 to 6 kilograms (10 to 12 pounds) needs 3 hours of roasting and is still juicy and red in the meat without being bloody.
8. Half an hour before serving, skim most of the gravy from the meat, and remove the fat, while pouring a little more water onto the pan to prevent the meat from browning too much.

title: Oksesteg (Roast Beef)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef (thick roast) of 5 to 6 kg
- Salt
- Water
- Butter
- Flour
- Half white beer and half water for the gravy

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water with salt into the oven pan before placing the meat.
2. Place the roast in the pan with the outer fillet side facing down towards the pan.
3. Roast for 3 hours, basting frequently and evenly.
4. Half an hour before serving, remove most of the gravy from the roast and skim off the fat, then pour a little water back into the pan to prevent the roast from burning while it remains in the oven.
5. For the sauce, brown butter and bake flour in it, then thin with the gravy to make a fairly thin sauce. Let it boil for about 10 minutes before serving.
6. The sauce should have a nice brown color.
7. Arrange the roast on a hot platter with the tender side facing up, as it lay in the oven.
8. Serve with boiled potatoes, peas, cucumbers, pickles, preserves, or compote.
9. Optionally, rub the roast with garlic or place a couple of onions on the pan to roast together, and roast some fresh or salted tomatoes with the roast.

title: Oksesteg i Sky (Beef Roast in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of beef roast
- Good aspic (from roasting or made fresh)
- Roasting bone
- Onions
- Black pepper, whole
- Lemon juice or tarragon vinegar
- Isinglass (12 leaves per 1 liter)
- Horseradish
- Fresh tomato slices or pickled cucumbers
- Salad leaves

## Directions

1. Arrange slices of beef roast on a flat dish.
2. Prepare aspic from the leftover aspic from roasting or cook it on the roasting bone with a couple of onions, whole black pepper, lemon juice or tarragon vinegar, and isinglass.
3. Once it has boiled, strain the aspic and clarify it with egg whites if necessary.
4. Pour the aspic over the beef.
5. When the aspic has set, scrape horseradish into thin shavings with a knife and streak the dish with the scraped horseradish.
6. Arrange slices of fresh tomatoes or pickled cucumbers and salad leaves around the edge.

title: Oksesteg som Vildt (Roast Beef as Game)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of inner thigh, well beaten and larded
- Half vinegar and half water
- Butter or claret
- Milk
- Flour
- Couleur (coloring)
- Browned potatoes for serving
- Redcurrant jelly for serving

## Directions

1. The evening before use, place the meat in a mixture of half vinegar and half water.
2. Brown butter or claret in a pot and add the meat, browning it on all sides.
3. Pour milk over the meat and roast it covered for about 3 hours.
4. Strain the gravy from the roast and thicken it with browned butter and flour mixed together to make a sauce.
5. Color the sauce with couleur.
6. Pour a couple of spoonfuls of sauce over the roast and serve with browned potatoes and redcurrant jelly.

title: Oksesteg, indbagt (Beef Roast, Battered)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef roast
- Thick pancake batter (No. 560)
- Palmin
- Mixed vegetables
- Preserved fruit compote
- Salad
- Browned butter

## Directions

1. Cut the beef roast into slices
2. Dip them in a thick pancake batter (see No. 560) which must completely cover the meat
3. As soon as it is dipped, place it immediately on the frying pan in hot Palmin
4. Remove when it is brown on both sides
5. Serve with mixed vegetables, preserved fruit compote and salad
6. Browned butter is served alongside
7. Cooked beef can be battered in the same manner

title: Oliven-Sauce (Olive Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Olives
- Madeira
- Even, brown gravy sauce

## Directions

1. Peel olives from the pits in a spiral and twist them back into their original form
2. Bring them to a boil in a little Madeira
3. Add the Madeira and olives to an even, brown gravy sauce and bring to a boil again

title: Oliven-Sauce (Olive Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- brown sauce
- red wine
- salt
- sugar
- lemon juice
- olives
- Madeira

## Directions

1. Reduce brown sauce with red wine.
2. Add a little salt, sugar, and lemon juice.
3. Add several spoonfuls of olives with the pits removed.
4. Bring the whole to a boil.
5. A glass of Madeira can be added.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ollebrod (Bread Drink)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread, preferably rye bread
- Ale or Beer and Lager in equal parts with Water
- Brown sugar
- whole Cinnamon
- Lemon peel
- Cream

## Directions

1. In the morning, soak as much bread as desired, preferably rye bread, in cold water to soften; if using fresh bread, soaking is not necessary.
2. If homemade ale is available, use it exclusively, but for beer or lager, take equal parts water.
3. Place the bread over the fire with ale and water, and let it cook until well blended.
4. Stir occasionally, and do not use too much bread as it may easily burn on the bottom.
5. When cooked, strain it and place it over the fire again with brown sugar, whole cinnamon and lemon peel, and after it has come to a boil, it is ready to serve.
6. Cream is offered at the table with this dish.

title: Omelet Arranged on Tomatoes (Omelet, anrettet paa Tomater)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Raw, ripe tomatoes
- Salt
- Pepper
- Eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Split raw, ripe, dried tomatoes and place them on a round platter.
2. Sprinkle them with salt and pepper and place the platter in the warm oven for about ten minutes.
3. Bake the omelet on one side and turn it over onto the platter so that the tomatoes are covered by it, with the baked side facing upward.
4. Serve with open-faced sandwiches or with creamed butter.

title: Omelet med Asparges og Rejer (Omelet with Asparagus and Shrimp)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 10 tablespoons milk or cream
- salt
- egg whites, stiffly beaten
- butter
- Asparagus and shrimp stew (see Nr. 2)

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks well
2. Stir in 10 tablespoons milk or cream and a small amount of salt
3. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Place a good slice of butter on the pan and when it boils, pour in the eggs
5. With a fork or knife, lightly prick the eggs and constantly shake the pan so the omelet does not burn or form a hard crust
6. Bake the omelet only on one side
7. When it is partly set but still loosely baked, place the asparagus and shrimp stew in a stripe down the middle
8. Fold the omelet together from both sides so it takes an elongated form
9. Place on a warm plated and serve immediately

title: Omelet med Champignons (Omelet with Mushrooms)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 10 tablespoons milk or cream
- salt
- egg whites, stiffly beaten
- butter
- mushrooms prepared according to Nr. 66
- whole mushroom pieces for garnish

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks well
2. Stir in 10 tablespoons milk or cream and a small amount of salt
3. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Place a good slice of butter on the pan and when it boils, pour in the eggs
5. With a fork or knife, lightly prick the eggs and constantly shake the pan so the omelet does not burn or form a hard crust
6. Bake the omelet only on one side
7. When it is partly set but still loosely baked, lay the prepared mushrooms on half of the omelet and fold the other half over
8. Garnish one side of the plate with a few whole mushroom pieces
9. Serve immediately

title: Omelet med Fisk (Omelet with Fish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 10 tablespoons milk or cream
- salt
- egg whites, stiffly beaten
- butter
- leftover cooked or fried fish
- Palmin for heating
- tomato sauce, mushroom sauce, hollandaise sauce, lobster sauce, crab sauce, or caper sauce
- Tartar butter, Maitre d'hotel butter, tomato butter, or watercress butter
- smoked fish such as herring, hornfish, or mackerel
- salt cod

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks well
2. Stir in 10 tablespoons milk or cream and a small amount of salt
3. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Place a good slice of butter on the pan and when it boils, pour in the eggs
5. With a fork or knife, lightly prick the eggs and constantly shake the pan so the omelet does not burn or form a hard crust
6. Bake the omelet only on one side
7. For cooked fish: pick the flesh from bones in small pieces and stew in tomato, mushroom, hollandaise, lobster, crab, or caper sauce, then place on the omelet
8. For fried fish: heat leftover in boiling Palmin, pick from bones and place in the center of the omelet with Tartar, Maitre d'hotel, tomato, or watercress butter
9. For smoked fish such as herring, hornfish, or mackerel: chop and place in a stripe on the omelet, then fold. Serve as smørrebrød, as hot as possible
10. For salt cod: chop finely and stir into the egg mixture before pouring on the pan. Omit salt from the omelet recipe
11. Serve immediately

title: Omelet med Gulerødder (Omelet with Carrots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 10 tablespoons milk or cream
- salt
- egg whites, stiffly beaten
- butter
- cooked whole warm carrots
- parsley, chopped
- cooked kale, optional

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks well
2. Stir in 10 tablespoons milk or cream and a small amount of salt
3. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Place a good slice of butter on the pan and when it boils, pour in the eggs
5. With a fork or knife, lightly prick the eggs and constantly shake the pan so the omelet does not burn or form a hard crust
6. Bake the omelet only on one side
7. Slice the cooked warm carrots with a fluted knife and toss in butter
8. Lay the carrot slices over the middle of the omelet, sprinkle with chopped parsley, and fold together
9. Alternatively, cooked kale can be warmed, cut, and placed in the omelet in the same manner
10. Serve with stirred butter on the side

title: Omelet med Hachis og Ragoulter (Omelet with Hachis and Ragouts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 10 tablespoons milk or cream
- salt
- egg whites, stiffly beaten
- butter
- well-prepared hachis
- turtle ragout, forlorn or authentic
- kidney ragout
- calf's sweetbread ragout
- leftover goulash

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks well
2. Stir in 10 tablespoons milk or cream and a small amount of salt
3. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Place a good slice of butter on the pan and when it boils, pour in the eggs
5. With a fork or knife, lightly prick the eggs and constantly shake the pan so the omelet does not burn or form a hard crust
6. Bake the omelet only on one side
7. Place a well-prepared hachis in the middle of the omelet, ensuring it is not too thin
8. Alternatively, place warmed leftover turtle, kidney ragout, calf's sweetbread ragout, or goulash in the omelet
9. Do not serve with additional sauce
10. Fold together and serve immediately

title: Omelet med røget Laks (Omelet with Smoked Salmon)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 10 tablespoons milk or cream
- salt
- egg whites, stiffly beaten
- butter
- smoked salmon, cut into very small pieces
- cooked smoked ham, optional
- watercress

## Directions

1. Cut the smoked salmon into very small pieces and stir into the eggs
2. Beat egg yolks well with the salmon pieces
3. Stir in 10 tablespoons milk or cream and a small amount of salt
4. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
5. Place a good slice of butter on the pan and when it boils, pour in the eggs
6. With a fork or knife, lightly prick the eggs and constantly shake the pan so the omelet does not burn or form a hard crust
7. Bake and fold together
8. Alternatively, cooked smoked ham can be chopped coarsely or cut small and used in the same manner
9. Sprinkle watercress over the omelet
10. Serve immediately

title: Omelet med Syltetøi (Omelet with Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- ingredients for simple omelet
- 2 tablespoons jam

## Directions

1. Make an omelet as for Simple Omelet
2. Place 2 tablespoons jam in the center and finish as with the other

title: Omelet med Syltetøj (Omelet with Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound potato flour
- 1/4 pound flour
- 1/2 pound butter
- 6 measures sweet, boiling milk
- 20 egg yolks
- 1 cup powdered sugar
- 20 egg whites
- vanilla
- cardamom
- jam

## Directions

1. Bake 1/4 pound potato flour and 1/4 pound flour with 1/2 pound butter
2. Thin with 6 measures sweet, boiling milk
3. Stir in 20 egg yolks, first beaten white with 1 cup powdered sugar
4. Then stir in the 20 egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks, and a little vanilla and cardamom
5. Line a dish with jam at the bottom
6. Pour the mixture over the jam
7. Bake in the oven at good heat for 1 hour until light brown
8. Serve with a napkin around the dish
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Omelet som Pandekage (Omelet as Pancake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 4 teaspoons powdered sugar
- 4 teaspoons potato flour
- 4 tablespoons cream
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg yolks well with 4 teaspoons powdered sugar, 4 teaspoons potato flour and 4 tablespoons cream
2. Finally beat in the 4 egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks
3. Bake over slow heat
4. Serve with powdered sugar and jam or jelly on top
5. This portion makes 2 omelets; beat the egg whites fresh for each omelet, just before baking
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Omelet with Beets (Omelet med Majpoer)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked beets
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Butter
- Leek

## Directions

1. Cooked warm beets can either be cut into small cubes and stirred into the eggs, or they can be cut into slices, dipped in egg and bread, and fried in butter.
2. Place a strip of this over the omelet, which is then closed together.
3. Coarsely cut leek goes well with the beets and is stirred into the egg mixture.

title: Omelet with Browned Onions (Omelet med brmuede Løg)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion slices
- Butter
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Brown a good portion of onion slices in butter in the pan.
2. These can either be stirred into the eggs or placed inside the omelet after it is baked.
3. Hold back some of the onion slices and sprinkle them over the omelet.
4. Serve with open-faced sandwiches.

title: Omelet with Leeks (Omelet med Porrer)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leeks
- Eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. After the lower white part of the leeks is cut away for sweet leeks, some of the firm yellow-green part above can be cooked and placed in the baked omelet, which is then folded together.
2. Sweet leeks look more beautiful in this and taste even better.
3. Serve with creamed butter.

title: Omelet with Oysters (Omelet med Østers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Oysters

## Directions

1. Bake the omelet as previously instructed.
2. In the middle place a stew of oysters and fold the omelet over it.
3. If there is more filling than the omelet can hold, place it outside of it.

title: Omelet with Smoked Salmon (Omelet med røget Laks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked salmon
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the smoked salmon into very small pieces and stir into the eggs.
2. Bake the omelet and fold together.

title: Omelet with Spinach (Omelet med Spinat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Spinach

## Directions

1. Place a thick layer of braised spinach over the omelet.
2. It is served either whole on a platter, or the spinach is placed in the middle of the omelet, which is then folded together.

title: Omelet with Tomatoes (Omelet med Tomater)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Large, ripe tomatoes
- Salt
- Pepper
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut large ripe tomatoes into slices and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
2. Dip them in egg and bread and fry in plenty of butter in the pan.
3. Pour the egg mixture over the tomatoes.
4. This omelet must be baked on both sides.

title: Omelette Soufflée (Soufflé Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- White sugar, one tablespoon per egg
- Wheat flour, half a tablespoon per egg
- Butter
- Bread crumbs
- Preserved fruit or jam

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks first
2. Beat with sugar
3. Add flour
4. Finally add beaten egg whites
5. Grease paper forms well with butter and sprinkle with bread crumbs
6. Forms should be about three fingers high with two fingers of batter
7. If baking in oven and top heat is too strong, cover with paper to prevent over-browning
8. Serve cold, stacked with jam between layers
9. Can be decorated with whipped cream and jam on top

title: Omeletter à la jardiniére (Omelets à la Jardiniére)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs or similar amount
- butter
- various cooked vegetables
- artichoke hearts or cardoons
- head lettuce for garnish

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks well
2. Stir in milk or cream and salt
3. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Divide the egg mixture into 3 omelets
5. As each omelet is completed and cooked, arrange it on a warm platter
6. Belege each omelet with different vegetables before folding
7. As each omelet is finished, folded, and topped with vegetables, place side by side on the warm platter and keep in the oven until all are ready to serve
8. In addition to the usual vegetables, artichoke hearts or cardoons can be used
9. Place a stripe of cooked vegetables on top of each omelet to indicate its contents
10. Garnish the ends of the platter with head lettuce
11. Serve immediately

title: One Egg Cake (1 Ægs Kage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Cups Sugar
- 1½ Cups Butter
- 1 Egg
- 1 Cup Milk
- 1⅔ Cups Flour
- 1⅔ Teaspoons Baking Powder
- Chocolate

## Directions

1. Mix together the sugar and butter
2. Add the egg
3. Add the milk
4. Mix in the flour and baking powder
5. Bake mostly in thin cakes
6. Layer with chocolate between and on top

title: Oplagt Mælk (Curdled Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pots milk, preferably sweet
- 1 pot sour cream
- Rennet (4 drops, obtained from the apothecary)

## Directions

1. Mix 1 pot of sour cream into 5 pots of milk in an enameled pot, cover with a tight-fitting lid, and place in a lukewarm oven so the milk becomes warm (15 degrees), but not hot, as it would then become grainy.
2. The pot stands this way overnight (14 to 16 hours).
3. Then remove the lid, place a thin, clean linen cloth, dipped in cold water and wrung out, over a colander.
4. Place the curdled milk in the cloth and leave for 5 to 6 hours so the whey can drain completely.
5. Shortly before serving, turn the milk from the cloth onto a dish and serve with whipped cream on top, and sweet cream and powdered sugar on the side.
6. Alternatively, reduce the amount of sour cream and take a corresponding amount of buttermilk instead. The faster the milk curdles, the better. For this reason, 4 drops of rennet (obtained from the apothecary) can be added; the milk is warmed to 15 degrees, left to stand, and treated as previously mentioned.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Oplagt Mælk (Strained Curdled Milk)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 L (1 Pot) sweet Milk per person
- 1 1/4 Dl (3/10 Pægl) boiling Water per Liter (Pot) of Milk
- Cream
- Sweet Cream
- fresh Strawberries or Raspberries
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Pour the milk into a vessel, and to make it curdle more quickly, pour 1 1/4 Dl (3/10 Pægl) boiling water into each Liter (Pot) of milk
2. Usually about 1.5 hours is required for this
3. When it is thick, skim the cream off first, and take slices of the milk with a spoon and place in a sieve, in which a piece of clear cloth is spread
4. Thus leave the sieve for 4 to 5 hours, so that the whey can drain well; thereafter turn the strained curdled milk from the cloth over onto a dish
5. Whip the skimmed cream and pour it over
6. Add sweet cream, fresh strawberries or raspberries and sugar

title: Orange-Omelet (Orange Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 orange
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons powdered sugar
- Butter

## Directions

1. Press juice from orange and grate the yellow peel
2. Beat egg yolks white and add peel and juice with powdered sugar
3. Beat egg whites until stiff foam
4. Heat pan over heat and add a piece of butter about the size of a small walnut
5. Pour in omelet with folded whites and fry, constantly lifting from bottom with fork and gathering in center
6. When ready, push to one side of pan, hold hot plate to it and slide omelet over quickly
7. Sprinkle with fine sugar and serve hot

title: Orange-Omelet (Orange Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- juice from one orange
- zest of one orange
- yolks from 3 eggs
- zest and juice from orange
- two tablespoons powdered sugar
- egg whites
- a piece of butter the size of a small walnut

## Directions

1. Press the juice from an orange and grate the yellow zest
2. Beat the yolks of 3 eggs white and add the zest and juice along with two tablespoons powdered sugar
3. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks
4. Heat a pan over the fire and when hot, add a piece of butter the size of a small walnut
5. Pour the omelet batter into the pan and fry, constantly lifting from the bottom with a fork and heaping toward the center
6. When it seems done, slide it to one side of the pan, hold a warm plate against it and quickly slide the omelet onto it
7. Sprinkle with fine sugar and serve hot

title: Ore-Omelet (Beef Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds round beef
- 6 eggs
- Five or six soda crackers
- Butter
- Chopped celery
- Black pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Scrape and mince 4 pounds round beef finely, beat 6 eggs, crush five or six soda crackers finely, add a little butter, a little chopped celery, pepper and salt, mix all this together
2. Form two small loaves, roll them in crackers and bake one hour
3. When cold, slice them into pieces

title: Orehalejuppe (Oxtail Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ox tails cleaned of all waste
- 1 carrot
- 3 parsnips
- 1 celery
- 1 leek
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Bay leaves
- 12 whole peppercorns
- 1 cup flour
- 3 ounces butter
- 1 tablespoon salt

## Directions

1. Cut the oxtails into joints and wash thoroughly in cold water
2. Place butter on the bottom of a casserole and add the oxtail pieces with the vegetables, seasonings and ½ pint water
3. Place over strong heat, stir now and then until the bottom of the casserole is covered with a thick, brown sauce or jus
4. Add the flour and blend well
5. Fill the casserole with 1 gallon water, season with salt and stir now and then until it comes to a boil
6. Move the casserole to a gentle heat, skim carefully, and the soup cooks until the oxtail pieces are so tender that the meat easily slips from the bones
7. Remove the pieces and place in the soup tureen
8. Strain the soup over them through a fine sieve
9. Add a celery that has been previously cooked in bouillon and cut into pieces
10. The soup is ready to serve. If you omit the flour, the soup will be clear, and many prefer it this way

title: Oste-Kiks (Cheese Crackers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams (1/4 pound) grated cheese
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) cold butter
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) Pillsbury flour
- 2 egg yolks
- a small amount of paprika

## Directions

1. Rub butter and flour together with the cheese
2. Knead the egg yolks into it
3. Roll out the dough very thinly
4. Cut into squares with a pastry wheel
5. Prick and bake light brown
6. Serve hot
7. They can be stored in a tin box but must be refreshed in the oven before serving
8. The dough can be rolled into small sticks; they are baked light brown and likewise served with coffee or at dinner

title: Oste-Linser (Cheese Lentils)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) Pillsbury flour
- 190 grams (38 quintals) cold butter or margarine
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) grated cheese
- 2 eggs
- 2 beaten egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 50 grams (10 quintals) grated cheese
- 1/4 liter (1 pint) sweet milk

## Directions

1. Knead all ingredients together into a dough and let stand 1/2 hour
2. Roll out and line small lentil molds with it
3. In each mold place 1 teaspoon of cream made from 2 beaten egg yolks, 1 tablespoon flour and 50 grams (10 quintals) grated cheese stirred together
4. Bring 1/4 liter (1 pint) sweet milk to a boil, pour into the eggs, whisk over heat until smooth, then cool before placing in the lentils
5. Cover the cream with dough and bake the lentils 1/2 hour
6. Arrange hot for dinner
7. Serve them on cut parchment paper on a platter

title: Oste-Sandwiches (Cheese Sandwiches)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- rye bread
- butter
- a tasty, rich cheese

## Directions

1. Cut not too thin slices of rye bread and spread with butter
2. Layer with a tasty, rich cheese
3. Place on a baking sheet after cutting into the desired shape
4. Bake in the oven until the bread is toasted
5. Arrange quickly on a napkin on a platter and serve with tea or coffee

title: Osters Salat (Oyster Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 large oysters
- 3 pounds chestnuts
- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 2 raw egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons oil
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fish balls or cooked fish (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the beard from 25 large oysters and cut into 4 pieces each
2. Boil 3 pounds of chestnuts, shell them and cut according to their size into 2 or 4 pieces
3. Make the sauce from 3 hard-boiled egg yolks, which are mashed and stirred with 2 raw ones, then with 2 tablespoons of oil, 1 tablespoon vinegar, salt and a little pepper
4. Stir the chestnuts in this sauce together with the cut oysters together with the sauce that comes with them
5. You can either cut 3 to 4 fish balls into small pieces or pick cooked fish into pieces and stir into the sauce, of which a little more must then be made

title: Ostindisk Karry (East Indian Curry)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg veal shoulder or neck, cut into 1-inch cubes
- 125 g butter
- 1 dessert plate finely chopped onions
- Salt
- 1 tablespoon curry
- 3/4 liter water
- Potato puree (see recipe 650)

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into small 1-inch cubes and coat in flour.
2. Heat 125 g butter in a pot and sauté the finely chopped onions.
3. Add the meat along with salt, 1 tablespoon curry, and 3/4 liter water.
4. Cook the meat gently under a lid for approximately 1.5 hours, or until very tender.
5. Arrange potato puree in a mound in the center of a flat dish and surround with the curry, or alternatively place the meat in the center and arrange the puree around it as a border, which can be placed in the oven and baked before serving.
6. In East India, potato puree and rice are served simultaneously with this dish.

title: Othello-Kage (Othello Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough from recipe 1120
- Cream from recipe 1120
- Chocolate glaze

## Directions

1. Bake two layers using the same dough and quantity as in recipe 1120
2. Place cream between the layers
3. Cover the cake with chocolate glaze

title: Oxefilet paa engelsk Maade (Beef Tenderloin English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tenderloin
- Bacon or pork fat for larding
- Fine salt
- Ground pepper
- Butter
- Bouillon
- Fresh mushrooms, coarsely chopped (optional)
- Wheat flour (optional)
- Cream (optional)
- Madeira wine (optional)

## Directions

1. After the tenderloin is prepared and larded as mentioned in the previous recipe, beat it lightly with a meat mallet.
2. Rub it with fine salt and ground pepper.
3. Heat abundant butter in a pan and place the fillet in it, frying uncovered at strong heat with constant dripping.
4. Thin the sauce with bouillon and arrange it with the roast.
5. Optionally, when the roast is removed, add some coarsely chopped fresh mushrooms to the sauce and thicken it with a little wheat flour, a tablespoonful of cream, and bouillon.
6. If desired, add a little Madeira to the sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Oxefilet à l'espagnole (Beef Tenderloin Spanish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tenderloin
- Fat from the surface of the tenderloin
- Sinew from the side of the tenderloin
- Skin of the tenderloin
- Bacon or pork fat for larding
- Butter
- Sugar
- Thin gravy or stock
- Onions, approximately 30-40

## Directions

1. Cut off the fat that sits on the surface of the tenderloin as well as the sinewed string at the side.
2. Remove the skin and lard the fillet with bacon.
3. Roast it as a beef roast, but proportionally not for as long.
4. Brown 30-40 onions with butter and sugar in a pan and thin them with thin stock.
5. Drip the fillet alternately with this sauce and with the juice that runs from the fillet itself.
6. Serve with the onions on top.
7. Cook together the sauce from the roast and the sauce from the onions and serve with it.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Oxehalesuppe (Oxtail Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Pd. Oxehale eller omtrent 3 Stk.
- Et Fjerdingspund Smør
- 5 Potter tynde Sky
- nogle Peberkorn
- lidt Salt
- et Løg
- en Suppevisk
- To Theskefulde Hvedemel
- Skiver af kogte Supperødder
- en Flaske Portvin

## Directions

1. Renses og skæres Oxehalen itu efter Leddene, dog plejer man ikke at medtage de 3 yderste Led.
2. Afvadskes Stykkerne derefter godt i flere Sæt Vand.
3. Brunes Et Fjerdingspund Smør.
4. Kommes Stykkerne deri og brunes smukt lysebrune.
5. Spædes derefter med 5 Potter tynde Sky og koges med nogle Peberkorn, lidt Salt, et Løg og en Suppevisk under Laag og ved sagte Ild, indtil Kjødet er gjennemmørt og med Lethed løsnes fra Benene.
6. Båges To Theskefulde Hvedemel sammen med Fedtet, der skummes af Suppen.
7. Heri spædes Suppen, indtil den bliver passende jævn.
8. Skæres Skiver af kogte Supperødder ud med Formjern og kommes i Terrinen tilligemed en Flaske Portvin.
9. Herover hældes Suppen.
10. Ved Anretningen lægges et Par Stykker Oxehale i hver Tallerken.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Oxehoved (Ox Head Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat from a cooked ox head
- Ox lung
- Ox heart
- Ox liver
- Water
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Apples
- Ginger
- Black pepper
- Cloves
- Whole allspice
- Flour
- Butter

## Directions

1. Wash the ox parts well and soak them several hours in water to draw out the blood
2. Boil them in salted water and skim carefully
3. After sufficient cooking, remove all unusable parts; mince the lung and heart, and grate the liver to more easily remove the sinews
4. Strain the broth in which they were cooked and bring the minced meat to a boil in this broth along with the head meat cut into cubes
5. Place in crocks and seal when cold
6. When ready to use, add vinegar and sugar along with sliced apples, if you have them, some pieces of ginger, whole pepper and cloves, and whole allspice and thicken with flour mixed in butter

title: Oxehovedjuppe (Ox Head Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ox head
- Butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 4 carrots
- 6 sliced parsnips
- 2 celeries
- 4 cloves
- 1 teaspoon mace
- strong pepper
- 1 tablespoon salt
- Soya
- Port wine

## Directions

1. Clean the head thoroughly and soak in water for 10 hours
2. Crush the bones and place the head in the pot with the meat side down
3. Simmer gently for 1 hour with 13 quarts water
4. Add nearly a gallon of water and add the salt
5. Cook a few minutes, skim well, and add vegetables and seasonings
6. When the meat is tender, remove the head
7. Clean the meat and cut into large cubes
8. Return the bones to the pot and cook the soup until about 3 quarts remain
9. Strain the soup and add butter, flour, 1 tablespoon soya and 1 glass port wine
10. Add the meat to the soup
11. Cook for 15 minutes and it is ready to serve

title: Oxekjodsuppe (Beef Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds beef
- 6 quarts water
- 1 carrot
- 1 onion
- Salt
- Celery leaves
- Parsley
- Celery root
- Parsnip root

## Directions

1. Rinse the meat in several changes of water, but do not let it soak. Place the meat in cold water with a carrot, onion, and a little salt over the fire. Skim when the broth first comes to a boil. Make a bouquet of celery leaves, leeks, and parsley. When the fat has been removed from the broth, which happens about an hour before the meat is tender, add the bouquet along with carrots, celery, and parsley roots that have been scraped and washed but not cut into pieces. The roots should be cooked in the broth at the same time as the meat; both should become tender at approximately the same time. Remove both the meat and roots, clean the roots, cut them into pieces, and place them in the serving dish to receive the broth. Remove the bouquet before it falls apart. Use slow cooking with steady, even heat for the best broth; wood fire cannot be used well. The lid must close well and should only be removed when the broth needs skimming. Add water for thinning only with boiling water if necessary. When everything is removed, strain the broth through a fine sieve. Let it stand briefly to settle, remove the fat, and pour it back into the pot carefully so the sediment does not come along. When the broth comes to a boil again, it will throw off a little more scum, and this should also be removed if you want it perfectly clear. After this, add the dumplings, though it is best to cook these in water or thin broth separately, as the broth becomes less clear and diminishes somewhat by cooking them in it. When serving the broth, you can add a piece of white sugar, which gives it a pleasant flavor. This portion will require approximately 2-3 hours of cooking.

title: Oxesteg (Beef Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef, preferably from a young, well-fed animal
- Salt
- Butter, the best quality fresh butter
- Bouillon, ale, or water (optional, used sparingly)
- Butcher's paper, buttered

## Directions

1. Select a piece of meat from a young, well-fed animal, recognizable by its beautiful red color (not dark red), fine fat threads throughout, and white and clear bone. Prefer the tenderloin, though the thick roast, brisket, or leg and neck can also be used if the animal was young and fat.
2. Allow the meat to hang for several days, according to the season, before roasting.
3. Calculate about half a pound of roast per person, preferring a larger piece to a smaller one.
4. Heat the oven appropriately according to the size of the piece to be roasted, so that the pores in the meat contract and it retains its juices.
5. Scatter salt in the pan to prevent the roast from sticking.
6. Place the roast in the pan with the best side up, which should also be uppermost when serving. Never turn the roast during roasting.
7. If the meat is very fatty, it will soon melt enough fat to provide sufficient liquid; otherwise, add a little butter to the pan.
8. Only if there is danger that the butter or fat will become too brown, pour a little bouillon, ale, or water under the roast, but only sparingly, as much liquid produces a poor roast that seems boiled.
9. After the roast has been in the oven for half an hour, drip it with the sauce from the pan, or pour new butter over it.
10. Continue to drip the roast from time to time, but do not keep the oven door open too long.
11. If the roast threatens to become too brown, place a piece of buttered butcher's paper over it, lifting it each time the roast is to be dripped.
12. Roast mainly at brisk heat in the beginning; towards the end, the heat may be reduced somewhat.
13. When done, the roast should be red inside but not bloody, and the juice should flow out when cut. A 12-pound roast requires approximately 2½ hours, and one generally allows a quarter hour more for each additional 2 pounds.
14. When the roast is removed from the oven and placed on the serving dish, pour all the juice that has run into the pan into a pot along with the scrapings from the pan.
15. Boil it with thin soup or gravy and water, strain it, skim the fat, brown butter very lightly, stir a little flour into it, thin it with the roasting gravy, and give it all a quick boil.
16. Serve the roast with this sauce, along with raw-peeled, boiled, or browned potatoes, and according to the season with salad, cucumbers, aspics, etc.
17. Provide very hot plates for this and all roasts.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Oxesteg (Roast Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef roast, approximately ten pounds
- Hot water
- Salt

## Directions

1. A beef roast of approximately ten pounds requires three hours of roasting, sometimes more, sometimes a little less.
2. There may be some red inside, but it should not be bloody.

title: Oxesteg på Amerikansk (Roast Beef American Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib section
- String
- Small onion
- Half carrot
- Half turnip
- Three parsley stems
- One thyme stem
- Three bay leaves
- Salt
- Half pint hot water
- Flour (optional)

## Directions

1. Have the butcher prepare the rib section, removing the bones and holding the piece in place with strong string tied around it.
2. Place the roast on a roasting pan on a bed of vegetables: one small onion, half a carrot, half a turnip, three parsley stems, one thyme stem, and three bay leaves.
3. Do not add water to the pan.
4. Do not add salt until the surface has contracted.
5. If making sauce, remove the vegetables and add half a pint of hot water to the roasting pan.
6. Boil rapidly for a few minutes with the pan juices until the sauce is thick enough, then salt to taste.
7. Some thicken the sauce with one teaspoon of flour mixed with two tablespoons of cold water before stirring it into the sauce.

title: Palace-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams margarine
- 250 grams fine sugar
- 4 eggs
- 125 grams currants
- 50 grams finely chopped preserved orange peel
- a little orange essence
- 250 grams Pillsbury flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 250 milliliters milk

## Directions

1. Mix margarine, fine sugar, and eggs well
2. Add currants, finely chopped preserved orange peel, and orange essence
3. Stir in Pillsbury flour, baking powder, and milk
4. Bake for 1 hour 15 minutes
5. Grease the form

title: Pandekager (Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a Pound Wheat Flour
- sweet Milk as needed
- a small piece melted Butter
- 3 Eggs
- a small handful Sugar
- a very little Cinnamon
- grated Lemon Peel

## Directions

1. The pan must be hot before beginning to bake, and a little Butter is added for the first cakes, later not
2. Sift half a Pound Wheat Flour and stir in as much sweet Milk that the batter becomes a little thicker than it should be
3. Add a small piece melted Butter, 3 Eggs (one at a time), a small handful Sugar, and a very little Cinnamon and grated Lemon Peel
4. Pour the batter thin on the pan and when the underside is baked enough, turn the cake, which is best done by loosening it all around the edge with a Knife, taking it at two places at the edge and turning quickly, after which it is baked finished on the other side
5. To make the cakes hard, they must be baked slowly over coals, not flames, which give too strong a fire, causing the cakes to become dark and not good; these should not be placed warm one on top of another
6. If you desire the cakes soft, a little Butter is added to the pan for each cake, they are baked faster and placed warm one on top of another
7. If the batter becomes too thick during baking, it can be thinned with a little Milk
8. The first cakes are rarely whole, but when several are baked on the pan, it goes better

title: Pandekager med Bagepulver eller Gær (Pancakes with Baking Powder or Yeast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) flour
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- cardamom
- salt
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) milk
- egg whites
- butter or Palmin

## Directions

1. From 1/2 Kg (1 S) flour, approximately 15 pancakes are made.
2. Stir 5 egg yolks with 3 tablespoons sugar, a little cardamom, salt and 2 teaspoons baking powder.
3. Mix in 1/2 Kg (1 S) flour along with 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) milk and lastly the beaten egg whites.
4. Bake the dough immediately in thick cakes with butter on the pan for each pancake.
5. Granulated sugar and compote or jam are served with it.
6. Thick pancakes must be baked slowly and turned on a lid.
7. If yeast is used, take 50 Gr (10 Kvint). Set the dough to rise before baking.

title: Pandekager Nr. 1 (Pancakes Nr. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a Pound Wheat Flour
- sweet Milk as needed
- a small piece melted Butter
- 3 Eggs
- a small handful Sugar
- a very little Cinnamon
- grated Lemon Peel

## Directions

1. Sift half a Pound Wheat Flour and stir in as much sweet Milk that the batter becomes a little thicker than it should be
2. Add a small piece melted Butter, 3 Eggs (one at a time), a small handful Sugar, and a very little Cinnamon and grated Lemon Peel

title: Pandekager Nr. 2 (Pancakes Nr. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Egg Yolks
- 3 Tablespoons white Sugar
- 12 Tablespoons Flour
- 6 small Tablespoons melted Butter
- a little grated Lemon Peel
- crushed Cardamom
- 1.5 Pints Milk or thin Cream
- Egg Whites (whipped)

## Directions

1. Whip 8 Egg Yolks well with 3 Tablespoons white Sugar
2. Stir in 12 Tablespoons Flour, 6 small Tablespoons melted Butter, a little grated Lemon Peel, crushed Cardamom, 1.5 Pints Milk or thin Cream, and finally the whipped Egg Whites

title: Panerede Artiskokbunde i Mellereuret (Breaded Artichoke Hearts in Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Canned artichoke hearts
- Hollandaise sauce
- Breadcrumbs
- Egg
- Clarified butter
- Salad leaves or parsley
- Lemon slices
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Open the cans and empty the artichoke hearts into a saucepan so they can be warmed in the liquid they are in.
2. Dip the warm hearts in a good, warm hollandaise sauce and lay them on a flat platter to cool.
3. Then dip them in finely ground bread, then in egg, and again in bread and cook in clarified butter until they get a nice brown color.
4. Serve on a warm platter with a nicely folded napkin, decorated with salad or parsley and lemon slices.
5. Serve with melted butter.

title: Panerede eller indbagte Skorzoner-Rødder (Breaded or Battered Salsify Roots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Scraped, cut, and cooked salsify roots
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Pancake batter, optional
- Clarified butter or Palmin for frying

## Directions

1. Drain the cooked roots on a colander and when the water is well drained, pour them onto a coarse cloth to remove the remaining moisture
2. Dip in egg and breadcrumbs, or alternatively in pancake batter
3. Fry in clarified butter or Palmin until beautifully light brown
4. Use as garnish around a meat course, or serve with melted butter as a side dish

title: Panerede Høns (Breaded Chicken)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens, cooked
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- pepper
- salt
- clarified butter for frying
- vegetables such as cauliflower, peas, asparagus, sea kale, beans, carrots, or fresh mushrooms
- melted butter
- parsley or lettuce for garnish
- brown butter for sauce

## Directions

1. Split the cooked chickens lengthwise and cut each half into 2 or 3 pieces depending on size.
2. Dip the meat in egg, then in a mixture of breadcrumbs, pepper, and salt.
3. Brown in a pan in plenty of clarified butter.
4. Arrange vegetables in the center of the platter, cooked tender in salted water and served with melted butter.
5. Arrange the chicken pieces around the vegetables, garnished with parsley or lettuce.
6. Serve with brown butter as sauce.

title: Panerede Muslinger (Breaded Mussels)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels
- Egg
- Bread crumbs
- Clarified butter
- Parsley
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Bread the mussels and fry them in clarified butter as for recipe 237.
2. Garnish with parsley and lemon.

title: Panerede Svinenyrer (Breaded Pork Kidneys)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Soaked and well-dried kidneys
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg
- Bread
- Claret or Palmin
- Warm potato salad

## Directions

1. Cut the soaked, well-dried kidneys into slices across the width
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Dip in egg and bread
4. Fry in Claret or Palmin
5. Arrange around warm potato salad in the middle of a platter

title: Panerede Østers (Breaded Oysters)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Egg
- Bread crumbs
- Butter or Palmin
- Lemon
- Oyster water
- Oyster beard

## Directions

1. Clean the oysters and remove them from their shells.
2. Dip them in egg and bread crumbs.
3. Brown them in butter or Palmin.
4. Serve with lemon.
5. Cook the oyster water and beard to make a sauce and bottle it, well corked.
6. Arrange on a napkin, garnished with lemon, toasted bread, and butter.

title: Panerede Østers (Breaded Oysters)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter or Palmin
- lemon
- oyster water

## Directions

1. Clean the oysters and remove from shells.
2. Dip in beaten egg and bread crumbs.
3. Brown in butter or Palmin.
4. Serve with lemon.
5. Cook the oyster water and beards to make sauce, which can be preserved in well-stoppered bottles.
6. Arrange on napkin garnished with lemon and toasted bread.

title: Paneret Flæsk (Breaded Pork)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Striped, slightly salted pork
- Egg
- Bread
- Clarified butter (Klaret)
- Stewed vegetables

## Directions

1. Cut striped but only slightly salted pork into slices and remove the rind.
2. Beat the slices with a meat mallet, dip them in egg and then in bread and fry on the pan in clarified butter.
3. Serve with stewed vegetables.
4. If the pork is saltier than desired, after cutting it, soak it in milk for a few hours, press it in a cloth and bread it.

title: Paneret Kalvebrisskel (Breaded Veal Sweetbread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 veal sweetbreads
- 2 to 3 ounces butter
- 2 eggs
- Grated breadcrumbs
- 1/2 pint cream
- Toasted breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbreads in water 1 to 2 hours, then in boiling water for a few minutes.
2. Remove, cool, and cut into 1-inch thick slices.
3. Dip in egg and grated breadcrumbs.
4. Place on a tray, lightly butter, place in a warm oven for a quarter hour.
5. Arrange on slices of toasted breadcrumbs with cream around it.

title: Paneret Oksebryst (Breaded Beef Brisket)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover cooked beef brisket
- Pepper
- Salt
- Egg white
- Breadcrumbs
- Flour
- Parsley, chopped
- Claret
- Mixed vegetables
- Brown stock sauce or browned butter

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover cooked beef brisket into slices
2. Sprinkle with pepper and salt
3. Dip first in egg white and then in crushed bread mixed with a little flour and chopped parsley
4. Fry the meat light brown in claret on the pan
5. Serve with mixed vegetables and brown stock sauce or browned butter

title: Paneret Oxetunge (Breaded Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 beef tongue (prepared as Kogt Oxetunge)
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the beef tongue as in Kogt Oxetunge.
2. Bread it by dipping in beaten egg and grated breadcrumbs.
3. Place in an oven to brown.
4. Serve with tomato sauce.

title: Paradise Apples (Paradisæbler)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- 1 Pd. sugar per 1 Pd. apples

## Directions

1. Wipe the apples and cut off half the stem
2. Carefully give them a brief boil in water
3. Place on a colander to drain
4. Boil and skim the sugar, then cook the apples in the syrup
5. The next day pour off the syrup, give it a boil, and pour it back over the apples
6. Repeat this process 3 times over 3 days
7. On the final day the apples should also have a gentle boiling together with the syrup
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Paradisæbler (Candied Paradise Apples)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 3/4 dl water
- 1/2 kg paradise apples

## Directions

1. Cut off half of the stems from the paradise apples.
2. Place over heat in cold water and bring to a boil.
3. Remove and place in cold water.
4. Boil sugar and water to a syrup in which the apples are cooked until tender enough to pierce easily with a knitting needle.
5. Remove the apples and place in a jar, and cook the syrup until smooth.
6. Pour the boiling syrup over them and seal while the apples are still hot.

title: Parfait (Foam Ice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 L (1 pot) cream or foam
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar
- Optional: finely chopped pineapple, nuts, chocolate or cocoa, nougat, cold caramel, ginger, coffee extract, almonds, or fruit puree

## Directions

1. Whip the cream until stiff frozen.
2. Blend this foam into the well-stirred egg yolks and sugar.
3. Rinse an ordinary pudding or ice mold with cold water and dust with powdered sugar.
4. Fill with the foam, cover with a piece of paper before placing the lid firmly on.
5. Pack the mold in the ice mixture as described in recipe 1067.
6. Do not stir this ice like cream ice; spread a woolen blanket over the packed ice and let it stand on its own.
7. Only check the ice mixture and pack ice firmly around it or renew it if necessary.
8. Allow 6 hours.
9. If adding peach puree, stir 1/2 kg (1 lb) peach puree into the foam.
10. For striped parfait, layer 2 to 3 types of foam, for example with cocoa, raspberry juice with a few drops of red fruit coloring, and vanilla added to the uncolored white foam.
11. Dip the mold briefly in hot water when turning the ice out of it.
12. Parfait can be garnished with whipped cream, white or colored.

title: Parfait (Skum-Is) (Foam Ice Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 L (1 Pot) cream or foam
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make stiffly whipped cream, and blend this foam into the well-stirred egg yolks and sugar.
2. Rinse an ordinary pudding or ice cream mold.

title: Paris-Buns
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kilogram Pillsbury flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 125 grams margarine
- 125 grams powdered sugar
- 2 eggs
- 3 3/4 deciliters half-skimmed milk
- sugar
- chopped candied citrus peel

## Directions

1. Mix together the Pillsbury flour, baking powder, margarine, and powdered sugar
2. Add the eggs and half-skimmed milk
3. Knead the batter well and shape into small, flat buns
4. Brush with egg and sprinkle with sugar and chopped candied citrus peel
5. Bake for approximately 1 quarter of an hour

title: Parmesan-Croutons
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 65 grams (13 dram) wheat flour
- 65 grams (13 dram) grated Parmesan cheese
- 40 grams (8 dram) cold butter
- Cayenne pepper
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mix flour and grated Parmesan cheese together
2. Work a little cayenne pepper into the cold butter and add to the flour mixture
3. Bring together with 1 egg
4. Roll out very thinly and cut into pieces the size of a crown coin
5. Place on a buttered baking sheet
6. Bake until golden brown
7. Arrange cooled on a platter lined with parchment paper

title: Parsley Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped parsley
- water
- butter
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Set chopped parsley on the fire with a little water
2. When it boils, remove the pot and stir in the butter until it is melted
3. The sauce becomes smooth from this
4. To save on butter, use less and mix a little flour in it, and it will then have a boiling in the sauce

title: Pastinakrødder, stuvede (Stewed Parsnips)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsnips, scraped and cooked
- Milk
- Broth or the water in which they were cooked
- Egg yolks
- Butter
- Parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the scraped, cooked roots and stew them either in a milk sauce, in broth, or in the water in which they were cooked.
2. Thicken the sauce with a couple of egg yolks.
3. In the milk sauce, stir in a piece of butter rolled in finely chopped parsley.

title: Pastinakrødder, stuvede (Stewed Parsnips)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Scraped, cooked parsnips
- Milk sauce, soup, or water from cooking the parsnips
- Egg yolks
- Butter
- Finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the scraped, cooked parsnips and stew them either in a milk sauce, in soup, or in the water in which they were cooked
2. Thicken this sauce with a couple of egg yolks
3. In the milk sauce, stir in a piece of butter rolled in finely chopped parsley

title: Pearl Beans (Perlebønner)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- small, fine beans
- salt
- water
- butter

## Directions

1. Rub the beans, rinse them, and place in boiling salted water
2. Cook until tender
3. If larger, cut them once
4. Serve whole with stirred butter, or stew as string beans

title: Pearl Beans à l'Anglaise (Perlebønner å l'Anglaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked pearl beans
- cold butter

## Directions

1. Drain the cooked beans thoroughly
2. Pour them very hot into a serving dish
3. Place a slice of cold butter on top of the beans

title: Pears (Perer)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pears
- 1 pound white sugar per pound of pears
- 1/2 pint water
- Cinnamon stick
- Lemon peel or finely ground and sifted ginger at 1 tablespoon per pound of pears
- Lemon juice (if pears are very sweet)
- 1/4 gill vinegar per pound of pears (if for serving with roast)

## Directions

1. If unripe, put the pears in boiling water and cook slightly until they begin to soften
2. Remove, peel, and cut into 4 pieces, removing all the core
3. Weigh the pears
4. Cook sugar into a syrup with 1/2 pint water
5. Add the pears and cook until tender
6. Pour out and cover with a cloth
7. After several days, pour the pears into a coarse sieve so the juice can drain, then place in a pot
8. Cook the juice with a piece of cinnamon and lemon peel or ginger, skim, and pour cold over the pears
9. The next day cover with paper dipped in brandy and seal
10. If pears are ripe, do not cook them first in water but peel, cut into pieces or leave whole if small, and cook longer in the syrup
11. If for serving with roast, add 1/4 gill vinegar per pound of pears

title: Pears Cooked in a Pot (Perer kogte i Krukke)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe pears
- 1 pound crushed white sugar per pound of pears
- Nearly 3 pints brandy

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, remove the blossom and trim the stem
2. If large, cut them in half and remove the core and stem
3. Weigh the pears
4. Layer them in a pot with 1 pound crushed white sugar per pound of fruit
5. Pour nearly 3 pints of brandy over
6. Place a cover or lid that fits well at the sides over the pot
7. Seal around the edge with rye flour and water made into a thick dough so no steam can escape
8. Place the pot in an oven that is not as hot as needed for roasting
9. Leave until juice begins to come out, then remove the pot and check if the seal has loosened and put it back
10. Repeat for 1 day until the pears are done
11. Keep the pot sealed until needed; later tie it well, if possible with a bladder, so the brandy does not lose its strength
12. Peaches may be preserved the same way, as well as raspberries and strawberries

title: Peas in Omelet (Ærter i Omelet)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar peas
- Peas
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Thin, fine, ribbed and cut cooked sugar peas or shelled and cooked peas are arranged in the omelet.
2. Serve with creamed butter.

title: Pebernødder A (Spice Nuts A)
body:
## Ingredients

- 13 pounds syrup
- 1 pound flour
- 1 pound clarified butter
- 12 quintal sweet almonds
- 3 quintal bitter almonds
- 1 quintal cardamom
- 6 quintal potassium carbonate, stirred in lukewarm milk

## Directions

1. Warm the syrup and mix it with the flour, clarified butter, sweet and bitter almonds, cardamom, and potassium carbonate stirred in lukewarm milk. Knead the whole together and let it stand for 12 hours in a warm place. Then knead it again, roll it out, and form it into small balls, which are baked at good heat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Pebernødder B (Spice Nuts B)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1 pound dried, light powdered sugar
- 4 whole eggs
- ground ginger, cinnamon, and cloves to taste
- up to 1 pound butter (optional)

## Directions

1. Knead the flour, powdered sugar, eggs, and spices well together and roll out and form into spice nuts of appropriate size. Bake the spice nuts light golden brown. They can be made more palatable by adding up to 1 pound butter; but it is not necessary.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Peberrod-Sauce (Horseradish Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Meat stock
- Horseradish root
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. In a white, smooth sauce made from butter and flour and thinned with meat stock, add cleaned and blanched horseradish root, vinegar, sugar, salt, and grated horseradish
2. Bring the sauce just to a boil

title: Peberrod-Sauce (Horseradish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- grated horseradish
- flour
- cream or milk
- currants

## Directions

1. Melt butter over heat.
2. Before it boils, add the grated horseradish and stir until it boils.
3. It can also be thickened with half as much flour as butter, and with cream or milk.
4. Sometimes it is made sweet and sour, and a little cleaned currants are cooked in it.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Peberrod-Smør (Horseradish Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kg (½ pound) stirred butter
- 3 to 4 tablespoons grated horseradish

## Directions

1. To ¼ kg stirred butter, use 3 to 4 tablespoons grated horseradish, which is mixed together with the butter.
2. Used for beef, cutlets, and other fried meat dishes.

title: Perch (Aborrer)
body:
## Ingredients

- Perch
- Butter
- Parsley
- Egg sauce or perch fricassée: good beef broth, celery, parsley roots, stock, salt, butter, flour, nutmeg, oysters, wine or lemon juice, egg yolk

## Directions

1. Scales are scraped off.
2. Perch are eaten boiled with butter and parsley or with egg sauce.
3. For fricassée: boil a good beef broth, celery and parsley roots in water and a little stock.
4. Once done, remove them and add the fish with salt, cooking until done.
5. Place the fish on a platter along with the roots, cut into pieces.
6. Strain the soup, place on heat with a roux of butter and flour and a little nutmeg and pour over the fish.
7. For oysters version, use no roots but a little wine or lemon juice added to the sauce, which is thickened with egg yolk.

title: Perlegryn-Suppe (Pearl Barley Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ L pearl barley
- 5 L water
- Lemon peel
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak the grains in 2 L water the evening before they are to be used.
2. Bring 3 L water to a boil, add the grains together with lemon peel and cinnamon and cook for 3 hours.

title: Perlegryns-Grød (Pearl Barley Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ L pearl barley
- 4½ L milk
- salt
- sweet beer

## Directions

1. Soak the pearl barley overnight.
2. The water poured over them must be warm and is drained before the grains are poured into 4½ L boiling milk.
3. When the porridge boils again, cover the lid and it can stand over very low heat without stirring for about 3 hours, or place it in a hay box.
4. Salt the porridge and serve with sweet beer.

title: Perlegrynssuppe (Pearl Barley Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 measure pearl barley
- 4 pots water
- cinnamon stick
- cloves
- prunes
- fruit juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the pearl barley in cold water the day before use.
2. Boil 4 pots of water and add the drained grains along with a piece of cinnamon and some cloves.
3. Cook covered over even heat.
4. When the grains are almost tender, add rinsed prunes to the soup.
5. When ready to serve, season with fruit juice and sugar to taste.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Perlegrynsvælling (Pearl Barley Gruel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint pearl barley
- 4 pots milk
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Rinse the pearl barley and soak it in cold water the day before use
2. Boil 4 pots of milk
3. When the milk boils vigorously, sprinkle the grains into it
4. Cook the gruel over even heat under a lid for approximately 3 hours until the grains are tender, stirring occasionally
5. Add salt when done
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Perleløg (Pearl Onions)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small onions
- 1 l (1 pot) vinegar
- 2.5 gr (1/4 oz) sugar to 1 kg (2 lb) onions

## Directions

1. Place the small onions in boiling water and after boiling for 5 minutes, remove them and cover with cold water.
2. Peel them and boil them for 5 minutes in the vinegar which has had the sugar added to it.
3. Pearl onions can be pickled without spices or as in recipe No. 232 and boiled together in the vinegar.
4. After the onions are poured into the glasses together with the vinegar, tie them shut with parchment paper.

title: Perleløg (Pearl Onions)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small onions
- Cold water
- Cold brine
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Scald the onions, peel and pour cold water over them
2. Place in jars and fill with the mentioned cold brine with a small sugar addition
3. Cook for a good half hour and cool

title: Persille (Preserved Parsley)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley leaves
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves from the stems.
2. Pack the parsley tightly in a jar in layers with salt.
3. Place a piece of cloth on top, and finally a light press.
4. Before use, rinse the parsley in cold water.
5. After a couple of days, the brine that has formed can be poured off and boiled, then poured cold over the parsley, which is bound the next day.

title: Persille Sauce (Parsley Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chopped parsley
- A little water
- Butter
- Flour (optional)

## Directions

1. Place chopped parsley on the heat with a little water
2. When it boils, remove the pot from heat
3. Stir in butter until melted and the sauce becomes smooth
4. If you wish to economize on butter, use less butter and knead a little flour into it, then bring to a boil in the sauce

title: Persille, kogt i Klaret (Parsley Boiled in Clarified Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh parsley
- Clarified butter or Palmin

## Directions

1. Remove the coarse stems from the parsley
2. Rinse the parsley in water and allow it to drain well
3. Place it in hot clarified butter or Palmin and cook until crisp
4. Use the cooked parsley to garnish roulettes, croquettes, etc.

title: Persille-Sauce (Parsley Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Thin cream or sweet milk
- Chopped parsley
- Salt
- Insignificant amount of sugar
- A little cold butter

## Directions

1. Make a roux with butter and flour and dilute with thin cream or sweet milk to a properly smooth sauce, into which is stirred chopped parsley, salt, insignificant sugar and a little cold butter

title: Persille-Sauce (Parsley Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- water
- butter

## Directions

1. Bring finely chopped parsley to a boil in water.
2. Stir in butter until it is melted.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Persille-Sauce til Fisk (Parsley Sauce for Fish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Fish stock
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Bake butter and flour and thin with fish stock and add chopped parsley
2. Or else serve melted butter with the fish and serve the chopped parsley separately

title: Pickled Beans (Bønner)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- haricots verts
- dill
- tarragon leaves
- fresh bay leaves
- raw vinegar
- powdered sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Remove the seams from the back and belly of the beans
2. Dry the beans and layer them in jars with dill, tarragon leaves and some fresh bay leaves
3. Pour raw vinegar over them, seasoned with a little powdered sugar to taste
4. The next day, drain the vinegar, bring it to a boil, and once cooled, pour it over the beans
5. Bind the jar
6. Serve with roasted meat

title: Pickled Cauliflower (Blomkål)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cauliflower, divided into small florets
- 0.5 l vinegar
- 3 pods Spanish pepper
- 1 tablespoon whole white pepper

## Directions

1. Divide the cauliflower into small florets, rinse and give it a good boil in salted water
2. Drain the cauliflower in a sieve
3. Boil the vinegar with 3 pods of Spanish pepper and 1 tablespoon whole white pepper
4. Place the cauliflower in a jar, pour the boiling vinegar over it and cover with a cloth
5. The next day, bind the jar with parchment paper

title: Pickled Mushrooms (Champignons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg mushrooms
- 0.25 l vinegar
- 0.25 l water
- a small amount of salt

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms as described in recipe 217
2. Place them in boiling vinegar and water with a small amount of salt
3. Boil the mushrooms for only 5 minutes and fill them into jars, where the boiling vinegar must completely cover them
4. Bind immediately with parchment paper

title: Pickled Mushrooms with Lemon Juice (Champignons med Citronsaft)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg mushrooms
- juice of approximately 6 lemons

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms and cook according to the instructions for pickled mushrooms, with the difference that these are cooked in the pressed and strained lemon juice
2. There must be enough juice to cover the mushrooms after they are filled into the jars

title: Pickled Pumpkin (Græskar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkin
- salt
- spices as for pickled cucumbers (recipe 232)
- raw vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel the pumpkin and cut it into long pieces of the same shape and thickness as pickled cucumbers
2. Scrape out the seeds with a silver spoon
3. Sprinkle the pumpkin with salt and let it stand until the next day
4. Remove it and dry it well before layering it with spices in the jar
5. Use the same spices as for pickled cucumbers (recipe 232)
6. Pour raw vinegar over the pumpkin and bind the jar with parchment paper
7. The vinegar can be boiled as for pickled cucumbers, but the pumpkin often becomes soft over the winter

title: Pickled White Cabbage (Hvidkaal)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 medium heads of white cabbage
- 2 l vinegar
- 0.5 kg salt
- 2 l boiling water
- 20 peeled shallots
- 75 grams East Indian curry
- 50 grams cayenne pepper
- 25 grams ground ginger
- powdered sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage heads into fine strips and place in cold salt brine consisting of 0.5 kg salt to 2 liters boiling water for 4 to 5 days
2. Drain the cabbage in a sieve and press out the brine well before placing the cabbage in the pot together with the vinegar
3. Add 20 peeled shallots, 75 grams East Indian curry, 50 grams cayenne pepper, 25 grams ground ginger and powdered sugar to taste
4. Cook all of this with regular stirring for 1.5 hours, then fill into jars and bind
5. This cabbage is very piquant; it is served with meat, similar to how pickled cucumbers and gherkins are otherwise used

title: Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- small thumb-length cucumbers
- very small onions
- small peeled carrots
- small green peas
- fine pieces of cauliflower
- a little saltpeter
- salt water
- good wine vinegar
- scraped horseradish cut into smaller pieces
- Spanish pepper
- black pepper
- yellow mustard
- mustard seed

## Directions

1. Place the cucumbers, onions, carrots, peas, and cauliflower along with a little saltpeter in a salt brine and let stand for 1 week
2. Place on a sieve so the brine can drain off
3. Let sit for 6 hours in fresh water
4. Place in the sieve again, then on a clean, dry linen cloth to further remove the water
5. Remove the cauliflower and carrots; place the remaining items over heat with enough good wine vinegar to cover well
6. Add scraped horseradish cut into smaller pieces, a little Spanish pepper, black pepper, and yellow mustard to the pot
7. Boil until the cucumbers have a beautiful green color
8. Cook the carrots and cauliflower separately in a salt brine
9. Arrange everything in layers in a jar and cover with the brine
10. Cover the jar with a serviette and place a plate or similar over it so the vapors remain until everything has cooled
11. When cooled, cover with a fine linen cloth and place a small bag filled with mustard seed on top, then bind the jar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Pighvarre (Turbot)
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot
- coarse salt
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the turbot by making a cross-wise incision below the gill cover on the white side, and remove the entrails and gills through it
2. Rub it well with coarse salt, and brush the scraped skin with a stiff brush, while the white side is brushed and scraped until all slime is removed
3. Clip off the fins
4. Wash the fish again in several changes of cold water, then sprinkle it with salt and let it stand for half an hour
5. Tie a strong string around the backbone below the head and make an incision in the meat for the string
6. Place it over the heat in cold water, which is quickly brought to a boil; the water must contain a good handful of salt
7. When the water begins to boil, skim it, reduce the heat, and cook the fish over gentle heat for about an hour, until the meat easily loosens from the bones
8. Take it up and lay it so the water can run off, then arrange it on a platter with a napkin
9. Turn the white side upward
10. Serve with peeled boiled potatoes and sour butter sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Pike (Gjedder)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Butter
- Horseradish
- Browned butter
- Capers

## Directions

1. Pike are good except in March and are fattest in February.
2. They are best boiled whole with the scales left on, brushed with a little vinegar and sprinkled with salt.
3. The tail is considered the best and fattest piece.
4. Serve with butter and horseradish.
5. They can be boiled in fricassée like perch, but then the scales must be scraped off.
6. A large pike can be roasted whole, the skin is removed, it is stuffed, roasted in the oven and basted frequently.
7. Serve with browned butter in which capers are added.

title: Pike in Salt Water (Gjedder i salt Vand)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike of 3-4 pounds
- Salt water
- Finely chopped parsley
- Melted butter
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Scrape the pike clean, clean and dress it; cut off the fins and stick the tail between the teeth in the mouth so the fish forms a ring.
2. Place the necessary salt water over heat; when it boils, put in the pike, let it come to a boil once and then let it simmer gently for a quarter hour under a lid.
3. Carefully lift it out, let the water drain off, place on a serving platter and sprinkle with finely chopped parsley.
4. Melted butter and boiled potatoes are served with it.

title: Pillau (Pilaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen
- One pound rice
- One quarter pound good fat (clarified) or butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. Boil the hen until there is a good quart of broth.
2. Remove the meat from the bones and cut into small pieces.
3. Cook one pound of rice in the reserved quart of broth with one quarter pound of good fat or butter.
4. Add salt to taste.
5. When the rice is cooked (it should remain whole, not broken apart), add the meat and warm through.
6. Stir everything together and place in a round mold, turning out onto a serving dish so it resembles a pudding.

title: Pillsbury-Kiks, 1 (Pillsbury Biscuits, 1 - Square)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram Pillsbury flour
- 125 grams potato flour
- 125 grams powdered sugar
- 125 grams margarine
- 3 teaspoons hartshorn salt, dissolved in 1/4 liter cold water

## Directions

1. Mix the Pillsbury flour, potato flour, powdered sugar, margarine, and dissolved hartshorn salt together
2. Let the dough rest for a quarter of an hour
3. Roll out the biscuits very thin

title: Pillsbury-Kiks, 1 (Square Crackers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg Pillsbury flour
- 125 g potato starch
- 125 g icing sugar
- 125 g margarine
- 3 teaspoons deer salt, dissolved in 1/4 L cold water

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well together
2. Let the dough rest for a quarter hour
3. Roll out the crackers very thinly
4. Prick them well throughout
5. Cut with a pastry wheel
6. Bake until pale golden

title: Pillsbury-Kiks, 2 (Square Crackers)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg Pillsbury flour
- 150 g cold margarine
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 2 teaspoons deer salt
- just over 1/4 L cold water

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together into a dough
2. Let the dough rest 10 minutes in a cold place
3. Roll out very thinly
4. Prick well
5. Cut out and bake as previously instructed
6. The crackers should be pale golden

title: Piroger (Piroshki)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound cooked beef, preferably
- Onion
- 2 tablespoons flour
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Broth or light gravy
- Anchovy, finely chopped, optional
- Wine, optional
- 1 pound flour
- 3/4 pound butter
- 4 tablespoons thick cream
- 3 eggs
- 1 shot of rum

## Directions

1. Chop the cooked beef finely with a little onion and 2 tablespoons flour
2. Brown a piece of butter and add the chopped meat along with salt, pepper, and broth or light gravy
3. Cook together until it forms a thick hash
4. Optionally add finely chopped anchovy and some wine
5. Make a dough from 1 pound flour and 3/4 pound butter, worked together well
6. Stir in 4 tablespoons thick cream, 3 eggs, and a shot of rum with a spoon
7. Work it until it releases from the hands
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Piroger (Russian Meat Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Three quarters pound cooked beef, preferably ox beef
- a little onion
- 2 tablespoons flour
- a piece of butter
- salt
- pepper
- gravy or soup
- a little chopped anchovy fish
- some wine
- 1 pound flour
- 3/4 pound butter
- 4 tablespoons thick cream
- 3 eggs
- a splash of rum
- egg yolk
- flour for dusting

## Directions

1. Chop the cooked beef with a little onion and 2 tablespoons flour. Brown a piece of butter and add the chopped beef along with salt, pepper, and gravy or soup, cooking it together into a thick hash. A little chopped anchovy fish and some wine may be added if desired. Make a dough from 1 pound flour and 3/4 pound butter, kneaded well together. Stir 4 tablespoons thick cream, 3 eggs, and a splash of rum into the dough with a spoon. Knead until it releases from the hands, then roll out very thinly, using flour outside the weight. Place a spoonful of hash on a piece of dough and fold it over to form a half-moon shape, cutting the edges with a small cutter. Brush the outside with egg yolk, bake at a brisk heat in the oven or in a baking pan for about a quarter hour. Serve hot with a strong gravy sauce. This amount is for 4 pieces or 10-12 portions.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Piroger (Russian Pastry Pockets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Shortcrust Dough
- Filling

## Directions

1. Make using shortcrust dough (see Tartelettes).
2. The preparation is the same as for Rissoller, except that the Piroger are shaped like half-moons.
3. Serve with or without sauce.

title: Pisket Flødeskum (Whipped Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- thick, sweet cream
- white sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Only thick, sweet cream can be whipped into foam. The cream must be well chilled and held in a spacious container, and the whipping must take place in a cool room.
2. If you cannot get the cream to foam, try adding a few drops of lemon juice or vinegar.
3. White sugar and vanilla are whipped in according to taste.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Pistacie-Creme (Pistachio Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Gr (20 Kvint) green almonds (pistachios)
- a little sugar
- 1 L (1 Pot) milk
- egg yolks and isinglass as in vanilla cream Nr. 1060
- whipped cream for decoration
- macaroons

## Directions

1. Pound the green almonds (pistachios) very finely with a little sugar and place in 1 L (1 Pot) milk.
2. When it boils, strain it through a sieve onto the stirred egg yolks.
3. Stiffen and treat the cream as vanilla cream Nr. 1060.
4. Serve with whipped cream and macaroons.

title: Pistacie-Is (Pistachio Ice Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Gr (20 Kvint) pistachios (green almonds)
- 1 L (1 Pot) cream or milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Pound the pistachios (green almonds) and place them in the cream.
2. Bring to a boil, then strain out the pistachios.
3. Cook the cream to cream with 8 egg yolks, stirred with 125 Gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar.
4. When the cream is cold, pour it into the freezer.

title: Pitted Cherries (Marmalade)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg pitted cherries
- 375 gr powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries and pit them using a cherry pitter, which can be purchased at kitchen supply stores.
2. Pour boiling water over the pits and rub the flesh that adheres to them well, then strain off the juice.
3. Spread a cloth on the table, pour the pits onto it, rub them well in it, then place them on a baking sheet in a warm oven or on a frying pan over low heat to dry completely.
4. When crushed with a meat mallet, place them either between paper or in a cloth, and remove only the whole kernels.
5. Pour the juice that has collected from the cherries into a pot and melt the sugar in it before adding the fruit to be boiled quite evenly and simultaneously skimmed well for at most 10 minutes.
6. Remove the cherries with a slotted spoon or place them on a colander, and when the juice has drained well, pour them into a jar.
7. Boil the juice until it is very thick, and give the kernels a final boil in it, then pour it over the cherries.
8. If there is plenty of juice, some of it can be poured into dry bottles and sealed with parchment.

title: Pitted Cherries Preserved in Spirits
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg pitted cherries
- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 3/4 dl brandy

## Directions

1. Pit the cherries and treat the kernels as described above.
2. Sprinkle the sugar over them as they are laid in the jar.
3. Finally pour the spirits over them and cover the jar well.
4. For the first 4 to 5 days stir once daily so that the sugar can mix with the spirits and juice, then seal it airtight.

title: Plain Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Cup cornmeal, sifted
- 1 Cup wheat flour
- 1 Cup Sugar
- 1 Teaspoon Salt
- 1 Egg
