- a piece of Butter the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Stir the sugar into the butter first
2. Dissolve the soda in the milk
3. Mix all ingredients together

title: Pleskener
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- wheat flour, equal weight to eggs
- sugar, equal weight to eggs

## Directions

1. Stir eggs, sugar, and wheat flour (of equal weights) well together in a bowl
2. Set small cakes on a greased baking pan using a tablespoon
3. Bake at even heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Pletter (Pancake Batter Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pints sweet cream
- 3 Eggs
- A little powdered sugar
- Fine wheat flour
- 5 Pints melted butter

## Directions

1. Beat 2 pints sweet cream in a bowl with 3 eggs and a little powdered sugar.
2. Beat in so much fine wheat flour that the batter becomes suitably thick.
3. Stir in 5 pints melted butter.
4. Set the pan over the heat to become really warm through.
5. Grease with butter, but only for the first cake.
6. Serve as hot as possible with preserves.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Plukfisk (Flaked Fish Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked fish (cod, pike, or salt fish preferred)
- butter
- wheat flour
- milk
- white sugar
- salt
- mustard (if using salt fish)

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from the bones of the cooked fish in small pieces
2. For each pound of fish, take half a quarter pound of butter and 9 quintal of wheat flour, baked together with the butter and thinned with 2 pails of milk
3. Add a little white sugar and salt, then the fish, and give it a boil
4. If making plukfisk from salt fish, a little mustard can also be added to the sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Plukfisk (Flaked Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover cooked fish
- Butter
- Flour
- Malt
- Ground nutmeg or pepper
- Sugar
- Salt
- Egg yolk
- French bread

## Directions

1. Plukfisk is usually made when you have leftover fish from the day before.
2. Pick the meat from the bones.
3. Melt a good piece of butter, stir in flour; gradually stir in malt until it has a suitable thickness, then add the fish and ground nutmeg or pepper, cook a little, finally add a small piece of sugar and salt.
4. Before the salt is added, you can bind the fish with an egg yolk, which gives it a good flavor.
5. Then place slices of French bread, which have first been baked in butter on a plate, and pour the fish over.
6. You can also place the warm plukfisk in a deep plate, sprinkle a layer of ground bread a finger's thickness over it, pour melted butter over it and place it in the oven where it is baked light brown.
7. To prevent the bread from sinking into the fish, it must be fairly thick and is therefore best made by binding it with two egg yolks.
8. You can of course also use freshly cooked fish. Cooked salt cod can also be used, but it must be chopped fine and requires much butter.
9. If you do not want to use bread for the salt fish, small diced carrots and pepper can be added.

title: Plukfisk (Shredded Dried Cod)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Smør (Butter)
- Mel (Flour)
- Mælk (Milk)
- Salt
- Sennep (Mustard)
- Fisk (Shredded fish from klipfisk)
- stødt Brød (Crushed bread)
- smeltet Smør (Melted butter)

## Directions

1. Bake butter and flour together and dilute with good milk to make an even sauce.
2. Season the sauce with salt and stirred mustard or the remaining mustard water from the dried cod.
3. Stir in the shredded fish and pour the stew onto a platter.
4. Sprinkle with a good layer of crushed bread and pour a little melted butter over it.
5. Place the platter in the oven and bake the stew until the bread is brown.

title: Plukkekød (Shredded Meat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions
- Fat from a roasted pork roast
- Cooked pork shank, shredded
- Flour
- Pepper
- Salt
- Mustard
- Coloring
- Cooked potatoes, small diced
- Meat, shredded

## Directions

1. Brown onions in a pan with the fat from a roasted pork roast and the shredded cooked pork shank
2. Mix in flour
3. Dilute to make a smooth sauce
4. Season with pepper, salt, and a little mustard
5. Give it color
6. Add small diced cooked potatoes
7. Let the shredded meat cook through in the sauce

title: Plum-Budding (Plum Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram flour
- 0.25 kilogram chopped beef kidney fat
- 125 grams beef bone marrow, cut into smaller pieces
- 0.25 kilogram raisins
- 125 grams currants, plucked and scalded
- 125 grams finely cut citron peel
- 0.25 kilogram powdered or caster sugar
- 1.25 deciliters rum
- 4 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful soda, dissolved in 1.25 deciliters milk
- margarine

## Directions

1. Into 0.5 kilogram of flour, stir 0.25 kilogram of chopped beef kidney fat, 125 grams of beef bone marrow cut into smaller pieces, 0.25 kilogram of raisins, 125 grams of currants, both plucked and scalded, 125 grams of finely cut citron peel, 0.25 kilogram of powdered or caster sugar, 1.25 deciliters of rum, 4 eggs, and 1 teaspoonful of soda dissolved in 1.25 deciliters of milk.
2. The batter can be placed in a cloth that is buttered in the middle and sprinkled with flour.
3. The cloth is then gathered and tied with a ribbon.
4. It is cooked in the cloth in a pot with plenty of water for about 3 hours.
5. Plum pudding can, like any other pudding, be cooked in a buttered and floured mold.
6. At serving, a hole is cut in the top; into it are placed some pieces of sugar, which are covered with rum, just as the sides of the pudding are covered.
7. It is lit and must be brought to the table burning.
8. Serve with fruit sauce.

title: Plum-Budding Nr. 2 (Plum Pudding No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- A small half loaf of wheat bread, 1/2 pound by weight
- Milk
- 4 tablespoons sifted flour
- 3 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 3 gills old rum
- 10 eggs
- 4 ounces beef marrow, removed and chopped
- 2 ounces finely chopped beef suet
- A handful good raisins
- A small handful cleaned currants
- Half a teaspoon coarse salt

## Directions

1. Remove the crust from the wheat bread, grate it on a grater
2. Soak in boiling milk until well soaked through
3. When cold, grind it out and mix with sifted flour, powdered sugar, and old rum
4. Add eggs two at a time, beef marrow, beef suet, raisins, currants, and coarse salt
5. Cook for 2.5 hours and serve mostly with red sauce
6. Pour a couple spoonfuls of rum over the pudding and set it alight when serving

title: Plum-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams cake flour
- 375 grams powdered sugar
- 250 grams melted margarine
- 125 grams currants
- 35 grams finely chopped preserved orange peel
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon cardamom
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 250 milliliters semi-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Mix cake flour, powdered sugar, melted margarine, currants, finely chopped preserved orange peel, eggs, salt, cardamom, cinnamon, and baking powder
2. Add semi-skimmed milk
3. Pour into a greased form and bake for 1 hour

title: Plumbudding (Plum Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 French breads (4-øre pieces)
- rum
- sweet milk
- a small handful of flour
- 4 pounds finely chopped kidney fat
- 4 pounds beef marrow
- 19 pounds cleaned raisins without pits
- 1 pound washed currants
- a little mace and nutmeg
- 1 tablespoon powdered sugar
- a little salt
- 14 whole eggs
- 13 pægle sweet cream
- 1 pægle good rum
- sugar for glazing

## Directions

1. Remove the crusts from 8 French breads.
2. Cut the breads into slices, dip them in rum, then soak in sweet milk until soaked through.
3. Take them out and add a small handful of flour, 4 pounds finely chopped kidney fat, 4 pounds beef marrow, 19 pounds cleaned raisins without pits, 1 pound washed currants, a little mace and nutmeg, 1 tablespoon powdered sugar, and a little salt.
4. Beat 14 whole eggs with 13 pægle sweet cream and 1 pægle good rum and add to the mixture, beating well together.
5. Pour the entire mixture into a sugar-glazed mold.
6. Place in boiling water and cook for approximately 4 hours.
7. Turn out onto a serving platter.
8. Pour 1 pægle warm rum over it and set it alight, so the pudding is served flaming.
9. Serve with Chantilly sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Plums No. 1 (Blommer År. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fully ripe plums
- 1/2 gill vinegar per pound of plums
- 1/4 pound powdered sugar per pound of plums

## Directions

1. Dry the plums, prick them with a sharp stick or needle and place in a pot
2. Cover with 1/2 gill vinegar per pound of plums and let stand until the next day
3. Drain the vinegar over 1/4 pound powdered sugar for each pound of plums
4. Put on the fire and when just melted, add the plums and turn a couple of times

title: Pocherede eller farlorne Æg (Poached Eggs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Vinegar
- Eggs

## Directions

1. A frying pan, which must be well filled with boiling water with a tablespoon of vinegar added, is used for this. The eggs are broken in one at a time and carefully so that the yolks remain whole. When the whites are set, the eggs are done. If you wish the yolks to be visible and lie uncovered by the whites, boil the eggs in less water. Take them now out of the water with a slotted spoon, and clean the loose and uneven edges. Poached eggs can be boiled in an egg poacher. The eggs are broken into the cups after the pan is placed in the frying pan, approximately filled with boiling water.

title: Pomeransskal (Candied Orange Peel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg lump sugar
- 1/2 kg orange peel

## Directions

1. If a large number of lemons or oranges are used in the household, the peels can be preserved and used in baking where candied orange peel or other citrus peel is otherwise used.
2. Remove the peels in 2 halves and soak in cold water for 3 to 4 days, changing the water daily.
3. Cook the peels tender in water under a lid, then remove and place on a strainer.
4. Pour the sugar over the peels.
5. After 3 to 4 days, pour off the syrup and cook until very smooth, then pour back over the peels.
6. 3 days later, pour everything into the pot and boil for 5 to 6 minutes.
7. Remove the peels and place in a jar, then bring the syrup to a boil before pouring over. Seal.

title: Poor Knights (French Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- French bread
- milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter
- fruit jelly

## Directions

1. Dip slices of French bread in milk
2. Cover them for an hour
3. Roll in sugar and cinnamon and fry in butter on both sides
4. Then spread with a good layer of fruit jelly and arrange

title: Pork Shoulder Roasted as Game (Svinekam, stegt som Vildt)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ kg (5 lbs) pork shoulder, with all fat trimmed
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- 1 bottle ordinary, good red wine
- Milk
- Flour
- Cream
- Butter
- Browned potatoes
- Lingonberries or lingonberry jelly

## Directions

1. The shoulder can be browned and roasted in milk in a pot. Another method is to sprinkle the pork shoulder, with all fat removed, with a little salt and ground pepper, and pour over it 1 bottle of ordinary good red wine.
2. Place the meat side down, and leave it in this way until the next day; then place it in the pan, which is set in a well-heated oven, and pour the marinade in which it has lain over it. It must be basted well with it.
3. Make the sauce from flour baked in some of the fat skimmed from the strained broth, which is used to thin it. Finally give it a boil with a little raw cream, and stir in cold butter.
4. Serve with browned potatoes and either lingonberries or lingonberry jelly.

title: Pork Tenderloin as Mock Chicken
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork tenderloin
- Parsley
- Cold butter
- Liquid for braising
- Flour
- Food coloring
- Cream
- Browned potatoes
- Cucumber or tomato salad

## Directions

1. Slit the pork tenderloin lengthwise and place parsley and a little cold butter inside as for chicken.
2. Bind or sew the meat lightly together.
3. Brown it, pour liquid over it and roast until tender, then remove the strings.
4. Make the sauce from butter, flour, and the strained gravy.
5. When serving, a piece of cold butter or a little cream can be added to the sauce, and it is given a nice brown color with food coloring.
6. Serve with browned potatoes and cucumber or tomato salad.

title: Pork Tenderloin Filled with Apples and Prunes
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork tenderloin
- Peeled and halved apples
- Prunes
- Boiling water
- Brown gravy sauce
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut a slit in the pork tenderloin.
2. Fill it with peeled halved apples and prunes; these must first be placed in boiling water so that the pits can be pressed out better, if pitted prunes are not used.
3. Serve with brown gravy sauce and potatoes.

title: Pork Tenderloin Roasted as Game
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork tenderloin
- Milk
- Food coloring
- Butter
- Flour
- Browned potatoes
- Redcurrant or lingonberry jelly or apple sauce

## Directions

1. Lard the pork tenderloin, brown it and drip milk mixed with food coloring over it.
2. Make the sauce from butter, flour, and the strained gravy.
3. Cut the meat into suitable pieces and arrange them together on the platter.
4. Coat with sauce and garnish with browned potatoes.
5. Serve with redcurrant or lingonberry jelly or apple sauce.

title: Porre-Salat (Leek Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- leeks
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut pieces of leeks 2 to 3 centimeters long and cook them until almost tender in water with salt
2. Drain them in a colander so the water can run off, before tossing them in a mixture of oil, vinegar, pepper and salt

title: Porre-Salat med Mayonnaise (Leek Salad with Mayonnaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- leeks
- salt
- real mayonnaise
- mustard
- raw cream or whipped cream
- tomatoes
- watercress

## Directions

1. Cut the lower white piece of the leeks into 1.5 centimeter long pieces and cook them in lightly salted water, but not so long that they lose their firm appearance and become stringy
2. Real mayonnaise made with mustard can have a little raw cream or whipped cream added to it, and arrange the leeks on a platter and cover with it
3. Cut 2-3 tomatoes in slices, with which the salad is lined
4. On each tomato slice place mayonnaise in very small pearls, and place watercress in the center

title: Porrer, kogt i Smør (Leeks Cooked in Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned leeks
- Butter
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned leeks into smaller pieces and give them a good boil in salted water
2. Drain the water from them
3. Place a good slice of butter in the pot
4. Steam the leeks in this under a lid and over gentle heat
5. Arrange them with no other addition than salt; this is a tasty vegetable

title: Porrer, stuvede (Stewed Leeks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leeks
- Butter
- Flour
- Leek water or beef broth
- Eggs
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean the leeks and cut them into thumb-length pieces, which are cooked in salted water
2. Make a smooth sauce from butter and flour, diluted with leek water or beef broth
3. Thicken this sauce with 1 to 2 eggs depending on the size of the portions
4. Warm the cooked leeks in this sauce
5. This is a good vegetable to serve with boiled soup meat

title: Portugiserkage (Portuguese Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter, as much as 8 eggs weigh
- powdered sugar, as much as 7 eggs weigh
- 8 egg yolks
- flour, as much as 6 eggs weigh
- 8 egg whites, beaten to stiff foam

## Directions

1. Beat as much washed and slightly lukewarm butter as 8 eggs weigh well with as much powdered sugar as 7 eggs weigh, then with 8 egg yolks, so with as much flour as 6 eggs weigh, and finally carefully with the 8 egg whites beaten to stiff foam.
2. Bake in a greased form for 1 hour at good heat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Portulakstilke (Purslane Stems)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Purslane stems
- water
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the stems into smaller pieces and pour boiling water over them.
2. Place them in a strong brine.
3. Let the stems stand until the next day.
4. Boil them in as much vinegar as needed to cover them.
5. Strain the stems and pack them in jars.
6. Heat the vinegar and pour it over them.
7. Seal the jars.

title: Posteideig til større Posteiier (Pastry Dough for Large Pâtés)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound wheat flour
- 3 pounds butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon cognac
- 1 gill sand sugar (if the butter is not salted, 1/2 ounce salt)

## Directions

1. Place the flour on the table.
2. Mix the butter into it in small pieces; make a depression in the center, into which pour the water, 2 egg yolks, 1 egg white, the cognac (and the salt, if necessary).
3. Mix everything well together, fold in thirds and roll out, which is repeated 4 times.

title: Postej af Oksesteg (Pâté of Beef Roast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry (No. 22)
- Slices of beef roast
- Warm stock sauce

## Directions

1. Line a form with a thin layer of puff pastry (see No. 22) that must be large enough to extend over the edge of the form
2. Lay slices of beef roast in the form
3. Pour a little warm stock sauce over them
4. Lay a layer of puff pastry on top
5. Fold up the hanging pastry over the lid
6. The lid can be decorated with figures cut out of pastry
7. Brush the whole with egg white
8. Place in the oven until the puff pastry is baked
9. Serve the remaining sauce in a sauce bowl

title: Postej med Agerhøns, Edderfugle, Vildænder, Jærper eller Snæpper (Game Bird Pâté)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Game birds (partridge, eider duck, wild duck, capercaillie, or snipe)
- Milk
- Short pastry
- Farce
- Butter
- Flour
- Game sauce
- Apple compote, redcurrant jelly, or apple sauce

## Directions

1. Lard and roast the birds in milk
2. Cut the meat from the breast of each bird
3. Cook the bones in milk for the sauce
4. Line the mold with short pastry and farce
5. Use the same method as for the preceding pâtés
6. Serve with game sauce, apple compote, redcurrant jelly, or apple sauce

title: Postej med Duer (Squab Pâté with 6 to 8 Squabs, for 12 Persons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs, roasted beforehand
- Short pastry
- Farce of veal or pork
- Butter
- Flour
- Gravy from the squabs
- Pearl onions, pickled

## Directions

1. Line the mold with short pastry and veal or pork farce
2. Cut the roasted squabs into 4 pieces and remove the breastbone, or use the breast alone
3. Place the meat in the center of the mold
4. Pour a good brown sauce made from butter, flour, and squab gravy over the meat
5. Add pickled pearl onions to the sauce
6. Layer with farce and short pastry
7. Serve the remaining sauce with the pâté

title: Postej med Dypesteg (Game Pâté)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Game farce (or beef, veal, or pork as substitute)
- Leftover game meat
- Short pastry
- Butter
- Flour
- Game sauce
- Olives, chestnuts, or truffles (optional)

## Directions

1. Line the mold as described for the fish pâté recipe
2. Use leftover game meat in the same manner as for Hare Pâté
3. Layer the meat with farce in the mold
4. Between the meat layers, place olives, chestnuts, or truffles if desired
5. Cover with short pastry and bake

title: Postej med Fiske-Filet (Pâté with Fish Fillets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 plaice, at least 625 grams (1.25 pounds) each
- Short pastry
- Fish farce
- Butter
- Flour
- Fish stock
- Graves (fish roe)
- Champignons
- Capers
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Line a mold with short pastry (see Nr. 21) and fish farce (as in Nr. 35).
2. Skin and clean plaice weighing at least 625 grams (1.25 pounds) each.
3. Remove the fillets from the bones; from each plaice you will get 4 fillets.
4. Roll each fillet individually like a snail and place them tightly together in the mold.
5. Pour over a sauce made from butter and flour, thinned with the stock cooked from the fish bones.
6. The sauce must be quite smooth and seasoned with a little graves and champignons or capers.
7. For the sauce poured over the fish in the mold, eggs are not necessary; however, thin the rest and bind it with 3 to 4 egg yolks before serving.

title: Postej med Haresteg (Hare Pâté)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare meat, cooked leftovers
- Short pastry or butter pastry
- Farce of game, beef, veal, or pork
- Milk
- Butter
- Flour
- Sauce or gravy from the roast
- Maggi Bouillon cubes (optional)
- Cream (optional)
- Egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Line the mold with short pastry or butter pastry and farce
2. Pick the hare meat from the bones and cook the bones in milk
3. Blend this milk gravy with any remaining gravy or sauce from the roast
4. Brown butter and flour and dilute with the gravy to make a good game sauce of light brown color
5. Improve the sauce if necessary with Maggi Bouillon cubes or cream
6. Stir a small piece of cold butter into the sauce at the end
7. Place the meat in the center of the mold and pour some sauce over it
8. Layer with farce, then cover with short pastry brushed with egg
9. Decorate with pastry figures cut out with a pastry wheel
10. Bake for 1 hour in good heat
11. Serve the remaining sauce with the pâté
12. Accompany with redcurrant jelly, lingonberry jam, or apple sauce

title: Postej med Høns og Hummer (Pie with Hens and Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- lobsters
- flour
- bouillon
- lobster butter
- cooked hens
- butter
- chicken bouillon
- white wine
- sugar
- salt
- lemon zest
- egg yolks
- fish forcemeat
- salt water
- puff pastry dough

## Directions

1. Break the lobsters and pound the shells, from which cook lobster butter, strain and set to cook with flour and dilute with bouillon; the sauce should be fairly thick.
2. Cut the lobster meat into pieces and place in it.
3. Cut cooked hens into pieces; make a sauce of butter and flour and dilute with chicken bouillon, to which add a little white wine, sugar, salt, grated lemon zest and 4 egg yolks, with which to bind the sauce.
4. Place the meat in the sauce and set to cool.
5. Cook small balls of fish forcemeat in salt and water, which are likewise set to cool.
6. Line a mold with puff pastry dough on the bottom and sides.
7. Over this place a layer of fish forcemeat, then a layer of the chicken fricassee, then a layer of the balls, then of lobster and so on until the mold is full.
8. Cover this with a layer of fish forcemeat and finally with a lid of puff pastry dough.
9. Place in a hot oven, bake for a good hour and serve with lobster sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Postej med Høns og Tomat-Sauce (Pâté with Chicken and Tomato Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) scraped fish for farce
- 3 chickens
- 1/2 kg (1 pound) flour for short pastry
- Butter
- Flour
- Chicken stock
- Tomato purée
- Madeira or Sherry
- Egg

## Directions

1. The pâté must be baked in a mold, either porcelain or a common metal form with a firm base.
2. For a metal mold, use short pastry for lining, as the pâté would otherwise be exposed to too strong heat.
3. Make the short pastry from 1/2 kg (1 pound) flour (see Nr. 21) and roll it out fairly thin.
4. Line the buttered mold with the pastry; it must be ample enough to hang 2 to 3 centimeters (1 inch) over the edge of the mold.
5. Lay a layer of fish farce (see Nr. 205), approximately 2 to 3 centimeters (1 inch) thick, over the short pastry on both the bottom and sides.
6. Boil 3 chickens until tender and remove the meat from the bones, placing it in the center of the mold.
7. Make a sauce from butter and flour, thinned with the chicken stock, add tomato purée to taste and Madeira or Sherry.
8. Pour enough sauce into the mold to roughly cover the meat.
9. Place a layer of farce on top and finally a layer of short pastry.
10. Fold the pastry hanging over the mold up onto the lid so it closes well.
11. Brush the lid with egg and bake the pâté for about one hour in a well-heated oven.
12. At serving, place the mold on a dish on which a folded napkin has been laid.
13. Conceal the mold either by fastening a napkin around it or using an embroidered band.
14. Prepare the sauce for the pâté at the same time you make the interior sauce to pour over the meat; thin it and add more tomato purée, Maggi's bouillon cubes, and wine if needed.
15. Small fish balls may be added to the sauce boat.
16. Champignons may be used instead of tomatoes.

title: Postej med Kalvesteg (Pie with Veal Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover veal roast
- old French bread
- sweet milk
- eggs
- mushrooms
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- sweet cream
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from leftover veal roast into cubes, which are mixed with old French bread, a little sweet milk, some eggs, mushrooms, a little vinegar, salt, pepper and 1 pint sweet cream.
2. After everything is well mixed, put it in a mold that has been greased with butter and sprinkled with breadcrumbs.
3. Place the mold in a hot oven for an hour.
4. Serve with remoulade sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Postej med Kramsfugle (Thrush Pâté)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrushes
- Game farce
- Butter
- Short pastry
- Stock or gravy
- Maggi Bouillon cubes (optional)
- Sago flour
- Apple compote or apple sauce

## Directions

1. Cut open the thrushes along the back and remove bones except the backbone
2. Fill each bird with game farce and add a small ball of farce
3. Sew together along the back, brown in butter, and roast until tender in the oven or pot with a little good gravy or stock for basting
4. Line a mold with short pastry and farce
5. Remove the threads from the birds and layer them in the mold with a thin layer of farce between each
6. Top with farce and then short pastry decorated with figures cut with a pastry wheel
7. Brush the pâté with egg and bake for 1 hour in a hot oven
8. Make a strong gravy sauce from the bird gravy, improved with Maggi Bouillon cubes if needed, and thickened with sago flour
9. Serve with apple compote or apple sauce

title: Postej med Muslinger (Mussel Pâté)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels
- Short pastry
- Fish farce
- Butter
- Flour
- Fish stock
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare the mussels as in recipe 8
2. Handle in the same manner as Oyster Pâté
3. Line mold with pastry and farce
4. Fill with prepared mussels
5. Make sauce from butter, flour, and fish stock
6. Bake for about one hour

title: Postej med Rødspætter (Fish Pâté with Plaice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Plaice, at least 625 grams per piece
- Short pastry (pâte brisée)
- Fish farce
- Butter
- Flour
- Fish stock made from fish bones
- Tarragon
- Mushrooms or capers
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Line a mold with short pastry and fish farce
2. Clean and fillet plaice of at least 625 grams each, yielding 4 fillets per fish
3. Roll each fillet into a spiral and arrange tightly in the mold
4. Make a sauce from butter, flour, and fish stock made from the bones
5. Season the sauce with tarragon and add mushrooms or capers
6. Pour some of this sauce over the fish in the mold
7. Thin the remaining sauce and bind with 3 to 4 egg yolks before serving
8. Bake for about one hour

title: Postej med Østers (Oyster Pâté)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Short pastry
- Fish farce
- Butter
- Flour
- Fish stock made from fish bones
- Oyster beards
- Oyster water
- Lemon juice
- Tarragon
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Line the mold as for the preceding pâtés
2. Prepare the oysters and sauce as in recipe 13
3. Fill the pâté with oysters
4. Make a sauce from butter, flour, fish stock made from fish bones from the farce, oyster beards, oyster water, and a few drops of lemon juice
5. Add tarragon to the sauce
6. Thin the remaining sauce and bind with eggs
7. Oysters may be placed in the sauce boat if first scalded with boiling water
8. Bake for about one hour

title: Potato Cake (Kartoffelkage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound grated potatoes
- 10 Egg yolks
- 1½ Cups powdered Sugar
- 1 Teaspoon Baking Powder
- Sweet almonds (for 5 cents)
- Bitter almonds (for 1 cent), finely ground
- Juice of 2 Lemons
- Zest of 1 Lemon, grated
- 10 beaten Egg whites

## Directions

1. Boil and peel the potatoes the day before grating
2. Grate them with a fine grater, using only what falls behind the grater to avoid lumps
3. Beat the egg yolks well with 1½ cups powdered sugar
4. Mix in the baking powder
5. Mix in the potatoes
6. Add the sweet and bitter almonds
7. Add the lemon juice and grated lemon zest
8. Stir and beat well in a bowl
9. Gently fold in the beaten egg whites
10. Bake immediately with moderate heat, especially at first until it rises, then with stronger heat, so it is done in one hour's time
11. Serves 10 people

title: Potato Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart potatoes
- Egg yolks
- Butter
- Salt
- White pepper
- Nutmeg
- Breadcrumbs
- Egg
- Water
- Fat or melted butter

## Directions

1. Peel and cook the potatoes in the usual way.
2. Press them through a fine sieve and stir with 2 egg yolks, a little butter, salt, a little white pepper and nutmeg.
3. Spread a thick layer of breadcrumbs on the table and beat one egg with a spoonful of water.
4. Dip the hands in cold water and form the dough like corks, which are dipped in the egg and rolled in breadcrumbs.
5. Fry golden brown in fat or melted butter and serve around roasted chicken or other meat.

title: Potkåse (Potted Cheese)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram (2 pounds) inexpensive dairy cheese
- 1 liter (1 pot) semi-skimmed milk
- 1/4 liter (1 pint) simple cognac
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the cheese into smaller pieces and place in a crock
2. Pour 1 liter (1 pot) semi-skimmed milk over the cheese and add 1/4 liter (1 pint) simple cognac
3. Stir the potted cheese at intervals of several days and ensure it does not become dry; if it does, pour more milk on top
4. After 6 to 8 weeks you can try to press it through a coarse sieve, and what is not yet dissolved is poured back into the crock
5. The contents can be increased over the year with what remains of cheese scraps and regularly topped up with milk and occasionally with 1 glass of cognac
6. When the potted cheese has been pressed through the sieve, season it with a little salt and sugar and, if it is too thin, let it stand a few days

title: Preserved Lemons as Lemmons (Citroner som Lemoner)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- lemons
- salt brine
- 0.5 dozen whole cloves per liter of vinegar
- 1 tablespoon whole white pepper per liter of vinegar
- 1 tablespoon ground ginger per liter of vinegar
- 2 tablespoons whole yellow mustard per liter of vinegar
- 2 pods Spanish pepper per liter of vinegar
- a little sliced horseradish per liter of vinegar
- 2 cloves garlic per liter of vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the lemons in a strong salt brine for 6 days
2. Place them in a pot with boiling water and boil for 1 quarter hour
3. Remove them and place on a cloth until completely dry, then place them in the jar
4. Measure the water used for the brine to determine exactly how much vinegar is needed to completely cover the lemons
5. For each liter of vinegar, take 0.5 dozen whole cloves, 1 tablespoon whole white pepper, 1 tablespoon ground ginger, 2 tablespoons whole yellow mustard, 2 pods Spanish pepper, a little sliced horseradish and 2 cloves of garlic
6. Boil the vinegar with the spices for 5 minutes, then pour it boiling over the lemons
7. Bind the jar when they are cold
8. Preferably store for 1 season before using in sauce or serving as pickles

title: Presse-Sylte (Pressed Pork Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- one good meaty pork head
- 1/2 to 1 kg (1 to 2 pounds) lean pork shoulder or tenderloin
- salt
- whole pepper
- 2 handfuls salt
- bay leaves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Split the pork head and clean it; remove the brains, eyes, and tongue
2. Clean the ears carefully or cut them off entirely if preferred
3. Cut away the snout cartilage to better drain the blood, and clean the snout
4. Soak the head in water overnight, then rinse it several times in cold water
5. Scald it in boiling water and it is then ready for cooking
6. Place it over the fire in cold water with salt and whole pepper; skim the broth well when it boils
7. Cook 1/2 to 1 kg of lean pork shoulder or tenderloin together with the head; this will be distributed in the aspic
8. When the head is cooked so much that the meat loosens from the bones, remove it
9. Spread a cloth wrung out in boiling water in a mold or deep dish
10. Cut the rind from the head and use it to line the mold
11. Cut the remaining meat into smaller pieces, distribute the fat and lean meat, and sprinkle salt and ground pepper between layers
12. Bind the cloth tightly together with a band and place a lid with weight on top; the lid should not be larger than to fit down into the mold, as the aspic shrinks greatly during pressing
13. The next day, remove the cloth and place the aspic in a cold, mild brine made of 2 handfuls salt, some bay leaves, whole pepper and a little vinegar, and pour 2 L (2 pots) of boiling water over it
14. When serving, cut the aspic into slices and eat with vinegar, pepper, mustard and beets
15. The tongue can be boiled and added to the aspic, or else salted as a condiment
16. The head broth can be used for pea soup, kale soup, or brown cabbage soup, or boiled together with herbs and marrow bones and used in the household as economy broth

title: Pressed Corned Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- Boiled corned beef
- Dry mustard

## Directions

1. After the meat is boiled, or when you have enough left over from the midday meal, it is chopped up while still warm, not excessively fine, but so that fat and lean are well mixed together; a little dry mustard is stirred in to give it flavor.
2. It is then placed in a pan that can give it form, and pressed together by a strong weight on top of a lid that must be able to slide down into the pan, touching its sides.
3. The next day it can be sliced into well-held together slices.

title: Presseylte (Pressed Head Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork head or mixed meat with some fat
- Salt
- Cloves
- Ground pepper
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Use pork head, which should be flayed, cleaned and cooked as for Sylte
2. Remove bones, innards and all unusable parts and remove most of the fat from the jelly
3. Place one piece of jelly in a bowl of suitable size
4. Cut the innards into strips or cubes along with the fat cut from the jelly and mix with ears and snout, cut into thin pieces
5. Place over heat with salt, cloves, ground pepper and a cup of bouillon
6. Stir well and as soon as it begins to boil, remove from heat and place immediately in the jelly
7. Place the other piece of jelly over and then again a bottom that fits well in the bowl
8. For the first hour place a light weight on top, later a heavier one, but not so heavily that fat presses out the sides
9. The next day remove from bowl and place in brine, or it can be cut into slices and placed in aspic

title: Prince of Wales-Fromage (Prince of Wales Fromage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 375 gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar
- 12 sheets isinglass
- 1 1/4 dl (1/2 pægl) water
- 1 1/4 dl (1/2 pægl) white rum
- juice of 1 lemon
- stiffly whipped egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir egg yolks well with powdered sugar
2. Dissolve isinglass in water, add white rum and lemon juice
3. Stir this mixture into the eggs
4. Fold in stiffly whipped egg whites
5. Serve with fruit sauce

title: Prindseſſe-Budding (Princess Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart thick, sour cream
- 1 good cup sugar
- 5 tablespoons butter
- 6 ounces flour
- 20 egg yolks
- Cardamom
- Lemon peel
- A little chopped almonds
- 20 egg whites

## Directions

1. Whip the cream slightly and bring to a boil with the sugar.
2. Melt the butter without clarifying it; when it begins to boil, brown 6 ounces of flour in it.
3. Gradually add the boiling cream to this mixture.
4. When almost cold, add 20 egg yolks which have been whipped slightly, cardamom, lemon peel, and a little chopped almonds.
5. Beat the 20 egg whites stiff and fold them in just before pouring into the form.
6. Boil for about 2 hours; serves 20 persons.
7. It can be made in half size. Serve with red sauce.
8. 16 eggs can be used, but then it serves only 16 persons.

title: Prins Picklers Is (Prince Pickler's Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet raspberry juice
- 1 pot thick, sweet cream
- 3 pounds very small macaroons the size of a ten-øre coin

## Directions

1. Blend 1 pot sweet raspberry juice with 1 pot thick, sweet cream that has been beaten until foam.
2. When this is well mixed, add 3 pounds very small macaroons the size of a ten-øre coin and blend well with the rest.
3. Freeze it.
4. Serve the ice whole on the serving dish, garnished with macaroons.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Prinsesse-Budding (Princess Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g (20 quintal) Pillsbury flour
- 100 g (20 quintal) margarine
- 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) boiling milk
- 8 egg yolks
- sugar to taste
- a little salt
- cardamom to taste
- egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Bake the flour in the margarine and dilute with 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) of boiling milk.
2. When cooled, stir in the egg yolks, sugar, a little salt, and cardamom to taste, and finally the beaten egg whites.
3. Boil the pudding for 2 hours and serve with fruit or cream sauce.

title: Prinsesse-Budding (Princess Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 grams Pillsbury flour
- 100 grams margarine
- 0.5 liter boiling milk
- 8 egg yolks
- sugar
- salt
- cardamom
- egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. 100 grams of Pillsbury flour is baked in 100 grams of margarine and diluted with 0.5 liter of boiling milk.
2. Then 8 egg yolks, sugar, a little salt, and cardamom to taste, and the beaten egg whites are stirred in.
3. The pudding is cooked for 2 hours and served with fruit or cream sauce.

title: Prinsesse-Budding (Princess Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 36 quintal butter
- 36 quintal flour
- 36 quintal ground sugar
- 1 pot cooked sweet milk
- 20 egg yolks
- 20 egg whites
- salt
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Bake 36 quintal butter, 36 quintal flour, and 36 quintal ground sugar together and thin with 1 pot cooked sweet milk. When it has cooled, stir in 20 egg yolks, 2 at a time, and lastly stir in the 20 egg whites, beaten to a foam, gently. Then stir the mixture continuously for half an hour, pour into a pudding mold that is buttered and sprinkled with grated bread, and boil for 2 to 2.5 hours. You can also add a little salt and a little vanilla to the pudding, which is served with sweet fruit sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Prinsessetærte (Princess Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound clarified butter
- 0.5 pound powdered sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 40 quintal potato flour
- 30 quintal flour
- zest of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. When the butter is cooled, stir it well with the egg yolks, one by one, then with the sugar and lemon zest
2. Stir in the flour, and finally fold in the 6 egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
3. Bake 6 to 7 round, flat cakes as for layer cake either in the oven or in a cake pan on paper dipped in melted butter
4. When done, remove carefully from the paper as they are very brittle, and trim immediately
5. Between the layers place a mixture made of 1 pound powdered sugar, the zest and juice of 1 lemon, and 4 cups good white rum
6. When the cake is assembled, glaze it in the usual manner
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Printaniére-Suppe (Spring Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 carrots
- 2 turnips
- 1 celery
- 3 leeks
- 75 g (15 drams) butter
- 4 L (4 pots) soup
- Cooked peas or asparagus
- Water in which peas or asparagus were cooked

## Directions

1. Cut the carrots, turnips, celery, and leeks into small dice and brown them lightly in 75 g (15 drams) butter
2. Pour the soup over the vegetables and cook until tender
3. Skim the soup
4. Just before serving, add cooked peas or asparagus to the tureen along with the water in which they were cooked

title: Prune Plums (Svedskeblommer)
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- 1/2 pound powdered sugar per pound of fruit
- just over 1 measure vinegar per pound of fruit
- cinnamon sticks
- cloves

## Directions

1. Wipe the plums, remove the stems, and prick the fruit with a needle.
2. For each pound of fruit, use 1/2 pound powdered sugar and just over 1 measure vinegar.
3. Heat the vinegar and sugar over the fire, bring to a boil, skim, and pour hot over the plums.
4. Let sit until the next day.
5. The next day, place everything over the fire and cook with some cinnamon sticks and some cloves at even heat for half an hour.
6. Skim the plums well during cooking, then pour them with the syrup into the preserving jar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Pudding Diplomatique (Diplomatique Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound raisins
- 1 good lemon
- 1/2 pound currants
- A prepared sponge cake
- 9 eggs
- 1 pint milk
- 3 pounds sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Rinse the raisins and remove the pits
2. Slice the lemon into rounds
3. Wash and clean the currants
4. Prepare a sponge cake beforehand
5. Slice the cake and layer it with the fruit in a buttered earthenware mold
6. Make a custard: whisk 9 eggs with 1 pint milk, 3 pounds sugar, and 1 teaspoon vanilla
7. Pour this custard over the pudding and distribute 1 tablespoon butter in small pieces on top
8. Fill the baking pan with enough boiling water to come two-thirds up the mold
9. Place the mold in the water and cook gently for 1 hour
10. Serve usually with fruit sauce

title: Pumpemickel (Pumpemickel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 medium eggs
- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) powdered sugar
- 25 gr (5 quintals) hartshorn salt
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) crushed almonds
- 1 Kg (2 lb) flour

## Directions

1. Mix the eggs well with the powdered sugar, hartshorn salt, and crushed almonds
2. Work the flour in
3. Roll the dough and cut into strips approximately 2 finger-widths wide and the length of the baking sheet
4. Brush with egg and bake at good heat
5. Cut into smaller pieces after baking, while still warm

title: Pund-Kage (Pound Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound butter (softened first)
- ½ pound sugar
- 12 eggs
- 20 drops vanilla
- 1 pound flour

## Directions

1. Line the pan with buttered paper
2. Put ½ pound softened butter, ½ pound sugar and 12 eggs in a bowl and beat until cream
3. Add 20 drops vanilla
4. Finally fold in 1 pound flour, sifted as usual
5. Bake in a moderately warm oven in half an hour

title: Punsch (Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar
- Water
- Essence
- Lemon juice
- Rum, Arrack, or other spirits

## Directions

1. Dissolve sugar in boiling water and add the essence. A little lemon juice improves the flavor.

title: Punsch-Kréme (Punch Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints French white wine
- 1 pint rum, cognac, or arrack
- 1 pint water
- 16 beaten egg yolks
- 1 pound white sugar
- zest of 3 lemons
- juice of 3 lemons
- 8 beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Made like Citron-Kréme (No. 273); but instead of 1 pint wine, take 1 pint rum, cognac, arrack, or similar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Punsch-Tærte (Punch Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- zest and juice of 2 lemons
- 20 egg whites, beaten
- 1 pound potato flour
- 16 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 1 pint good Arack or Rum
- juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Beat 20 egg yolks for 3 quarter-hours with 1 pound powdered sugar.
2. Add the grated zest and strained juice of 2 lemons.
3. Stir in the 20 beaten egg whites and finally 1 pound potato flour.
4. Bake 7 thin cakes as for layer cake in greased paper forms.
5. Make the cream as follows: beat 16 egg yolks well with 1 pound powdered sugar, and when well beaten, add 1 pint good Arack or Rum and the strained juice of 2 lemons.
6. Heat the cream over the fire and cook with constant stirring until the whole is well cooked.
7. Set aside to cool so that it becomes cold before spreading between the layers of the cake.
8. Stack the cakes on top of each other with cream between.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Pære- og Æble-Marmelade (Pear and Apple Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe fruit, half of each
- water

## Directions

1. Peel the ripe fruit, half of each, and cut away the core house and blossom.
2. Place the fruit in the pot, whose bottom is covered with water, and reduce the jam to a firm mass.
3. Alternatively, dry the fruit before cutting it into 4 pieces, from which the core house is cut away.
4. Cook until well reduced, then pass through a coarse sieve and pour back into the pot to reduce to jam.
5. Pour out and seal as previously explained.

title: Pære-Gelé (Pear Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.625 kg pear
- 625 gr sugar
- 1.25 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice

## Directions

1. Treat as Quince Jelly, No. 104.

title: Pære-Kompot (Pear Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1.5 dl water for 0.5 kg fruit

## Directions

1. Peel not fully ripe gray pears or bergamot pears, remove the blossom end and scrape the stem.
2. During peeling, place the pears in water with a little vinegar added.
3. In a thin syrup made from the sugar and water, boil the pears until a knitting needle can be easily inserted through them.
4. Remove them and place in sulfured jars.
5. Boil the juice for 6 to 8 minutes before pouring it hot over the fruit.
6. The pears can also be cut in 2 or 4 parts and the core removed, but the fruit is most beautiful when boiled whole.

title: Pære-Marmelade (Pear Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pears
- Powdered sugar (1 1/2 kg to 2/3 kg pear pulp)
- Water
- Vinegar
- Lemon zest (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the pears.
2. Remove the blossom end and seed cavity, and place the pears in cold water with a little vinegar as they are peeled.
3. Remove the pears from the water, put them in a pot and set over heat with enough water so they can just float in it.
4. Cook the pears until soft enough to easily pass through a coarse sieve.
5. Weigh the pulp, pour it into the pot and cook it down together with the sugar with regular stirring, until the jam is cooked to a porridge consistency.
6. Fill it into jars, cover with paper and tie up.
7. To this portion, the zest of 1 lemon can be added, which is cooked at the same time as the sugar.

title: Pære-Sirup (Pear Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter (1 pint) water
- 7.5 kg (15 pounds) pears

## Directions

1. Use ripe or even overripe pears that are otherwise unusable
2. Do not peel them, but cut them in half
3. Remove the blossom and stem
4. Rinse the fruit and place in a pot with the specified amount of liquid
5. Boil the pears until completely tender so the juice can be pressed out using a fruit press or through a sieve
6. Pour the juice into the pot and boil until it is thick as syrup and hangs in threads from the spoon, at which point it has changed color and become dark
7. Pour the syrup into crocks and can be preserved for years
8. Tie with parchment paper

title: Pære-Sirup (Pear Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe or overripe pears
- Water (amount as specified)

## Directions

1. Do not peel the pears, but cut them through and remove the flower and stem
2. Rinse the fruit and place it in a pot with the specified amount of water
3. Cook the pears until completely soft so that the juice can be pressed out using a fruit press or through a sieve
4. Pour the juice into the pot and cook until it is thick as syrup and hangs in threads from the spoon, at which point it will have darkened in color
5. Pour the syrup into jars and seal with parchment paper; it can be preserved for years

title: Pære-Suppe (Pear Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 2.5 kg pears
- 4.5 L water
- Graves wine, rhubarb juice, or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook like apple soup
2. Add acidity with Graves wine, rhubarb juice, or lemon juice as pears often need some sourness

title: Pærer (Dried Pears)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe grey pears, fig pears, bergamots, or common cooking pears
- Water
- Sugar (for inferior varieties)

## Directions

1. Peel the pears preferably with a nickel-plated fruit knife, dipping it regularly in water
2. Keep the pears whole, cutting off only the blossom end while preferably leaving the stem
3. Cook them tender in water until they can be easily pierced with a straw
4. Remove the pears from the water as soon as they are cooked and place them on a sieve to drain before placing them on the trays with the stem facing upward
5. The thermometer should show 50°R at this point
6. When the pears are so dry that they are somewhat tough on the outside and can withstand pressing without bursting, place them briefly under a press or use a pear clamp consisting of 2 boards connected with a hinge
7. After the fruit is partially pressed flat, place them back in the oven and continue drying until no more juice can be pressed from them
8. Pack the completely cold, dried pears tightly in boxes lined with parchment paper

title: Pærer i Tyttebærsaft (Pears in Lingonberry Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 gr powder or Demerara sugar
- 1 l lingonberry juice
- peeled whole or halved pears

## Directions

1. When picking lingonberries for preserving, separate the less good ones, rinse them and put them in a pot with enough hot water to cover them
2. Cook for at least 1/4 hour and strain through a fine sieve or through a juice cloth without pressing so the juice is clear
3. Measure the juice and give it a boil with the specified amount of sugar
4. Add the peeled whole or halved pears to cook until tender
5. Remove pears and place in jar while the juice cooks again until thick
6. Pour the hot juice over the fruit in the jar and seal immediately
7. The lingonberries can be cooked again with more water and the juice strained off for use in fruit soup

title: Pærer med Citron (Pears with Lemon)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 gr powdered sugar
- 3/4 dl pear water
- 1 lemon
- 1/2 kg peeled pears

## Directions

1. Select unripe cooking pears or Bergamots as for compote.
2. The pears can be preserved whole or cut into 2 to 4 pieces with the core removed.
3. If preserved whole, cut off the blossom; scrape the stem that remains.
4. Cook the pears in water until tender enough to pierce easily with a knitting needle.
5. For each 1/2 kg sugar, use 3/4 dl of the water in which the pears were cooked, along with the juice of 1 lemon, and boil to a thin syrup in which the pears are cooked for at least 5 minutes together with half the lemon peel.
6. Remove the pears and place in a jar, removing the lemon peel.
7. Cook the syrup until smooth, then pour it over the pears in the jar, which is sealed immediately.
8. The water in which the fruit was cooked can be used with other juices for fruit soup.

title: Pærer med Eddike (Pears with Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 575 gr powdered sugar
- 3/4 dl vinegar
- 1/4 dl pear water
- 1/2 kg peeled pears

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and either halve them or cook whole as in recipe 70.
2. Boil sugar, vinegar, and water to a thin syrup in which the fruit is cooked for a few minutes, then removed and placed in a jar.
3. Cook the syrup until smooth before pouring it boiling over the pears.
4. Seal the preserve once it has cooled.

title: Pærer med Ingefær (Pears with Ginger)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 gr whole ginger
- 375 gr powdered sugar
- 3/4 dl vinegar
- 1/4 dl water
- 1/2 kg peeled pears

## Directions

1. Soak the ginger overnight
2. Scrape and scald the ginger and cook it together with sugar, vinegar and water to a thin syrup
3. Cook the peeled pears in this syrup until tender
4. Remove pears with a slotted spoon and place in jar
5. Pour the syrup over them unless it is not sufficiently thick and needs further reduction
6. The ginger can either remain in the jar with the pears or be removed
7. Seal the jar as soon as the fruit is poured into it

title: Pærer med Rom eller Cognac (Pears with Rum or Cognac)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 gr powdered sugar
- 3/4 dl pear water
- 1/2 kg peeled pears
- Rum or cognac

## Directions

1. Cook the whole peeled pears partially tender in water.
2. Boil sugar and water to a smooth syrup in which the pears are cooked completely tender, then remove and place in a jar.
3. The syrup is cooked until very smooth, then flavored to taste with rum or cognac, but without bringing to a boil after.
4. Pour the syrup over the pears so they are completely covered. Seal as soon as possible.

title: Quince Jelly (Kvædegelée)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pear quinces
- Melis, equal weight to the strained juice

## Directions

1. Scald and peel the quinces
2. Cut into pieces and boil along with the cores in a little water until completely soft and can be mashed into a pulp
3. Pour into a sieve so the juice runs off
4. Filter the juice through a jelly bag and weigh it
5. Boil the juice with an equal weight of Melis until the jelly appears clear and thick
6. Skim and pour into jelly cups that have been previously moistened with cognac
7. Treat further as noted for raspberry jelly
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Quinces (Kvæder)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pear quinces
- 1 Pd. Melis per 1 Pd. quinces

## Directions

1. Boil the quinces in water
2. Place in a colander, peel, and cut into 2 or 4 pieces lengthwise
3. Dissolve the Melis in water and boil to a smooth syrup
4. Boil the quinces in this syrup until tender
5. Remove the quinces and place in preserve jars
6. Continue boiling the syrup with constant skimming until smooth, then pour over the quinces
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Quinces with Cognac
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg pear quinces, peeled
- 375 gr powdered sugar
- 1/4 l water

## Directions

1. Peel the pear quinces, cut away the cores, and cut the fruit into 8 pieces, which are placed in cold water as needed.
2. Boil the quinces partly soft in water, remove them, place on a sieve, and only when the water has drained well, pierce each piece with a thinly cut strip.

title: Quinces with Ginger
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg quinces, peeled
- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/4 l water
- 75 gr whole ginger

## Directions

1. Both apple and pear quinces are found; however, the latter are considered to be unconditionally the best.
2. They should be beautifully yellow and preferably have been exposed to frost before picking.
3. Place the quinces in boiling water and boil until they begin to soften, then remove them, cool, cut into 4 to 8 pieces, and cut away all the hard core.
4. Weigh the fruit now.
5. Boil sugar and water to a syrup, in which the quinces are boiled completely soft.
6. Remove the fruit with a slotted spoon and place it in a jar, then reduce the juice until it is thick, together with the ginger that has been previously scalded and scraped, which is removed before the boiling juice is poured over the fruit, which is then poured out and the glass sealed.

title: Quinces with Vinegar
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg quinces, peeled
- 375 gr powdered sugar
- 1/4 l vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces and cut them into 8 pieces, and cut away the core.
2. Place them over the fire together with sugar and vinegar and boil soft, while at the same time skimming.
3. Remove the quinces and further reduce the juice before pouring it boiling over the fruit.
4. Seal as soon as the preserve has cooled.

title: Raa Saft med Sprit (Raw Juice with Spirit)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) powdered sugar
- 3/4 deciliter (3/4 pint) wine spirit
- 1/2 kg (1 pound) picked berries

## Directions

1. Pick and weigh the berries and pour into a large crock
2. Layer the berries with the sugar and pour the spirit over at the end
3. Cover well and let stand for 4 to 5 days, stirring gently once daily so the sugar dissolves properly
4. Pour through a sieve or fairly open jelly cloth until the juice has only partly run off so the berries do not become too dry
5. Pour the juice into dry bottles, cork and seal with plaster
6. Fill the berries into crocks or jars and tie with parchment paper

title: Raa Saft med Vinstensyre (Raw Juice with Tartaric Acid)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 to 20 grams tartaric acid
- 2 liters (2 pints) cold water
- 7.5 kg (15 pounds) berries
- 1/2 kg (1 pound) powdered sugar per liter of juice

## Directions

1. Dissolve the tartaric acid in the cold water
2. Pour over the fruit, which stands undisturbed for 24 hours
3. Pour through a jelly cloth
4. Measure the juice that runs through
5. For each liter of juice, take 1/2 kg powdered sugar, which is stirred into the cold juice until completely dissolved
6. Pour the juice immediately into bottles, cork and seal with plaster

title: Raager, stegt som Kyllinger (Young Crows Roasted Like Chickens)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Young flying-capable crows
- Parsley
- Cold butter
- New boiled potatoes
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. In early summer, flying-capable young crows taste excellent.
2. Either flay or pluck them, stuff with parsley in which a little cold butter has been placed, and roast like chickens (No. 940).
3. Split them and garnish with newly boiled potatoes sprinkled with chopped parsley.
4. Cucumber or head lettuce and compote are suitable accompaniments.
5. Crows are very inexpensive.

title: Raager, stegt som Vildt (Crows Roasted as Game)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Crows
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Potatoes
- Rhubarb compote or currant jelly
- Lettuce or parsley

## Directions

1. Flay them, brown them, and roast as game (see Doves as Game, No. 914).
2. When serving, split them, garnish with lettuce or parsley, and serve with browned potatoes and rhubarb compote or currant jelly.

title: Rabarber-Gelé (Rhubarb Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg rhubarb stalks
- 625 gr sugar
- 1.25 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice

## Directions

1. Wipe the stalks and cut them into smaller pieces, which are cooked through over very low heat, after which the mass is poured into the jelly cloth.
2. The strained juice is weighed and cooked into jelly. (See No. 93.)

title: Rabarber-Grød (Rhubarb Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 to 8 rhubarb stalks
- 2 1/2 L (2 1/2 pots) water
- 30 g (6 quintal) potato flour per liter (pot) of juice
- 30 g (6 quintal) sago flour per liter (pot) of juice
- sugar to taste
- vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Cut the stalks into smaller pieces, rinse them, and cook them over the fire in 2 1/2 L (2 1/2 pots) water.
2. Strain the juice and measure it before pouring it back into the pot to be brought to the boil and sweetened.
3. For each liter (pot) of juice, take 30 g (6 quintal) potato flour and 30 g (6 quintal) sago flour, stirred in a little of the juice, and as soon as it boils, stir the thickening in.
4. Remove the pot from the fire as soon as the compote has boiled, otherwise it becomes stringy.
5. Stir vanilla essence into the finished compote.
6. If it is to be poured into glass bowls, these must first be rinsed in lukewarm water and the compote must be slightly cooled.
7. It is sprinkled with powdered sugar so that it does not form a skin.
8. Serve with cream or compote.

title: Rabarber-Likør (Rhubarb Liqueur)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 S) well-dried rhubarb stalks
- 1/2 l (1/2 pot) wine spirits
- 1/2 kg (1 S) powdered sugar
- 1/2 l (1/2 pot) water
- 1/4 stick vanilla
- Red fruit coloring or wine coloring (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut 1 kg (2 S) well-dried rhubarb stalks very finely or crush in a wooden or stone mortar and place in a jar.
2. Pour 1/2 l (1/2 pot) wine spirits over them. Tie the jar with parchment paper and let stand for one week.
3. Pour the entire contents into a jelly bag to obtain completely clear liquid; if not completely clear, filter through a piece of sterilized cotton placed in a funnel.
4. Boil 1/2 kg (1 S) powdered sugar with 1/2 l (1/2 pot) water and 1/4 stick vanilla.
5. Remove the pan from the heat before pouring in the strained liquid.
6. The liqueur is now ready to be poured into bottles. This portion yields 2 bottles of liqueur.
7. Color slightly with a few drops of red fruit coloring or wine coloring if desired.

title: Rabarber-Marmelade (Rhubarb Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Rhubarb stalks, peeled and cut very small
- Powdered sugar (1 1/2 kg to 1/2 kg chopped stalks)

## Directions

1. Strip the outer skin from the stalks, which are then cut into very small pieces, much smaller than for compote; rinse them and put them in the pot together with the sugar.
2. The cooking must take place over low heat and with constant stirring to prevent the stalks from burning on the bottom of the pot.
3. Cook the stalks for about 1/2 hour, fill into jars, which are covered with paper and tied up.
4. This jam can in many cases be used instead of apples, especially in baking and the like.

title: Rabarbersaft (Rhubarb Syrup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Rhubarb stalks
- 125 gr (1/4 lb) powdered sugar per 1 liter (1 pot) juice
- 2 small teaspoons salicylic acid
- Water

## Directions

1. This syrup is quite excellent and also the cheapest available. Since the stalks contain much acidity, they can be boiled 3 times, and yet the third batch of syrup is good and long-lasting. At the end of May or beginning of June is when the stalks are most juicy.
2. First Boiling: Cut 50 good thick stalks into 8 centimeter (1/2 quarter) long pieces and rinse well. Cover the bottom of the preserving pot with 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) of boiling water to prevent the stalks from burning. Boil them completely under regular stirring and over very low heat. Pour the mass into a stretched jelly cloth until the juice has partially run off after 1/2 hour.
3. Second Boiling: Put the rhubarb mass back in the pot together with 2 liters (2 pots) of boiling water and boil for 10 minutes, then strain in the cloth again until the juice has almost completely run off.
4. Third Boiling: This time add 1.5 liters (1.5 pots) of boiling water, and boil the mass again for 10 minutes before straining in the cloth, so that the last bit of juice can run off.
5. Now you can, if you wish, mix all 3 batches of juice and boil them together, but the first drained juice is decidedly the strongest and should be preserved separately. Only 125 gr (1/4 lb) sugar is added to each liter (pot) of juice, which is boiled and skimmed for 10 minutes.
6. Dissolve 2 small teaspoons of salicylic acid in some of the warm juice and distribute throughout the entire batch.
7. The salicylic acid must not be omitted. Pour the juice hot into bottles which are tied immediately with parchment paper.
8. Rhubarb syrup is a refreshing summer drink and can be made as a good dessert at little expense. Even after the stalks have been boiled 3 times, water can be added again and they can boil for 10 minutes before straining in the cloth and hanging until the next day. The juice that runs through can be made into rhubarb pudding and if necessary mixed with a few drops of red fruit coloring.
9. From ordinary good large stalks, about 14 bottles of good syrup can be obtained from this amount.

title: Rabarberstilke (Rhubarb Stalks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/4 dl water
- 1/2 kg peeled rhubarb stalks

## Directions

1. From the well-dried stalks remove the skin and cut the stalks into pieces the length of a thumb
2. Cook sugar and water to a thin syrup and cook the stalks in it until soft but completely whole
3. If too many stalks are put in the pot at once it is difficult to prevent them from breaking up
4. Remove with slotted spoon and place in jar
5. Cook the syrup afterward until very thick then pour it hot over them
6. Seal the jar while the preserve is boiling hot
7. 1/2 teaspoon salicylic acid to 3 kg stalks is stirred into the warm juice

title: Radiser (Radishes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Radishes
- Salt
- Butter
- Mayonnaise (Nr. 753)

## Directions

1. Remove the outermost coarse leaves, leaving only the heart leaves
2. Scrape the radish well around these leaves, rinse, and serve with salt
3. Alternatively, arrange them on a larger plate, in the center of which place a smaller dish with creamed butter or Mayonnaise (Nr. 753), which is eaten with the radishes

title: Radiser, stuvede (Stewed Radishes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Radishes
- Water
- Salt
- Beef broth or Maggi bouillon cubes
- Butter
- Flour
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves from the radishes and cook them for a few minutes in salted water, or in thin beef broth or water with Maggi bouillon cubes
2. Make a sauce from butter, flour, and the broth
3. Thicken it and prepare it with salt and finely chopped parsley
4. Serve with roasted meat dishes

title: Ragout (Meat Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 1 onion, grated
- anchovies, cut into small pieces
- pepper
- flour
- gravy
- bouillon
- currant juice, sweetened
- meat or roast
- oysters, stuffed lemons or pearl onions (optional)
- meat or fish forcemeat (optional)
- egg yolk
- water

## Directions

1. Brown a little butter and cook the grated onion in it with small pieces of anchovies, pepper and flour
2. Thin it with gravy, bouillon and a little sweetened currant juice
3. Chop or cut the roast or meat into pieces and add it to the sauce
4. Remove it as soon as it is well heated through
5. When the sauce boils again, strain it through a sieve over the meat
6. Vary by adding oysters, stuffed lemons or pearl onions to the sauce
7. Meat or fish balls may also be cooked in this ragout, or a layer of forcemeat may be spread over the hot ragout when it is placed on the serving dish; brush the forcemeat with egg yolk mixed with a little water
8. Set the dish in the oven and bake for about half an hour

title: Ragout (Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Gravy or soup
- Pepper
- Allspice
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Coloring
- Meat, sliced
- Cooked potatoes or potato croquettes (see Nr. 14)

## Directions

1. Make a brown, smooth gravy sauce from gravy or soup
2. Add pepper, allspice, and a little vinegar
3. Add sugar and coloring to taste to give it a nice color
4. Heat the sliced meat in the sauce
5. Serve with cooked potatoes or potato croquettes

title: Ragout (Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Brown, smooth stock sauce made from stock or soup
- Pepper
- Pickled vegetables
- Vinegar
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Make a brown, smooth stock sauce from stock or soup
2. Add pepper, pickled vegetables and a little vinegar, sugar to taste and culinary color to give it the desired appearance

title: Ragout af Kalvesteg med Kartoffel-Mos (Ragout of Veal Roast with Mashed Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions
- Butter
- Flour
- Gravy, soup, or water
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Pepper
- Salt
- Sugar
- Coloring
- Gherkins, pickles, lemon sauce, pearl onions, or chutney
- Veal roast, sliced
- Potato border (Nr. 336)

## Directions

1. Brown onions in butter
2. Brown flour in the mixture
3. Dilute with gravy, soup, or water and Maggi bouillon cubes
4. Add pepper, salt, a little sugar, and coloring to the sauce
5. Season with gherkins, pickles, lemon sauce, pearl onions, or chutney
6. Place slices of veal roast in the sauce to warm through
7. Serve garnished with potato border

title: Ragout af Kalvesteg, Brissel og Fiskefars (Ragout of Veal Roast, Sweetbreads, and Fish Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Gravy from veal roast
- Wine
- Mushrooms
- Veal roast, carved
- Slices of cooked or roasted sweetbreads
- Fish balls
- Puff pastry strips

## Directions

1. Make a brown gravy sauce from butter, flour, and the gravy from the veal roast
2. Season with wine and mushrooms
3. Heat the carved meat in the sauce together with slices of cooked or roasted sweetbreads and fish balls
4. Garnish the ragout with puff pastry strips

title: Ragout af kogt eller stegt Kød med Kød- eller Fiskefars (Ragout of Cooked or Roasted Meat with Meat or Fish Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onions, finely chopped
- Anchovies, chopped
- Flour
- Gravy or soup
- Sherry
- Paprika
- Pickled shallots
- Cooked or roasted meat
- Meat or fish forcemeat
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Salt

## Directions

1. Brown butter and sauté finely chopped onions in it
2. If using anchovies, chop a few pieces and sauté with the onions
3. Brown flour in the mixture
4. Dilute with gravy or soup to the desired consistency
5. Season the sauce with sherry, paprika, and pickled shallots
6. Give the meat a quick boil in the sauce
7. Pour onto a flat serving dish
8. Cover the ragout with a layer of meat or fish forcemeat
9. Brush with egg, sprinkle with breadcrumbs and salt
10. Bake in a hot oven until the forcemeat is cooked, which takes about 1/2 hour

title: Ragout af kogt Kalvekød med Asparges (Ragout of Cooked Veal with Asparagus)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Soup
- Asparagus water
- Egg yolks
- Parsley, chopped
- Asparagus
- Cooked veal, cut into pieces
- Puff pastry border, strips, or bread croutons

## Directions

1. Brown butter and flour
2. Dilute with soup and some of the asparagus water to make a smooth sauce
3. Bind the sauce with a couple of egg yolks
4. Add chopped parsley
5. Add the asparagus and cut meat, which can be either sliced or cubed
6. Garnish the ragout with puff pastry border, strips, or bread croutons

title: Ragout af Oksekød (Beef Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds round beef
- 2 ounces flour
- Butter
- 1 carrot
- 3 small onions
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 0.5 teaspoon pepper
- Boiling water
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut 2 pounds round beef into square pieces, 2 inches on each side, and brown in butter.
2. Stir in 2 ounces flour and brown it.
3. Cut 1 carrot into pieces about 1 inch in size and peel 3 small onions, and make a bouquet garni.
4. Add these ingredients to the meat along with 1 tablespoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon pepper and boiling water until it covers the meat.
5. Simmer until the bouquet garni is soft, then remove it.
6. Taste and adjust salt if needed.
7. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon chopped parsley over it, or arrange some slices of fried bread around the dish when serving.

title: Ragout af Oxekjod (Beef Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds round beef
- Butter
- 2 ounces flour
- 1 carrot
- 3 small onions
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon pepper
- Boiling water
- Parsley

## Directions

1. 2 pounds round beef is cut into square pieces, two inches on each side, and browned in butter.
2. Two ounces of flour is stirred in and browned.
3. One carrot is cut into pieces about an inch in size, three small onions are peeled, and a bouquet garni is made.
4. These ingredients are added to the meat together with a tablespoon of salt, 1/2 teaspoon of pepper and boiling water enough that it stands over the meat.
5. It is boiled until the bouquet garni is tender, then this is removed, you taste if there is enough salt in it, and add what is missing.
6. A teaspoonful of chopped parsley can be sprinkled over it, or some slices of fried bread are placed on top.

title: Ragout af Oxekjød (Beef Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of leftover boiled beef or roasted beef
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth
- Egg yolk
- Capers
- Lemon capers
- Pickled onions
- Soy sauce

## Directions

1. Brown butter with half as much flour.
2. Thin with broth to make a sauce of the right consistency.
3. Add egg yolk, capers, lemon capers, pickled onions, and soy sauce to taste.
4. Place the beef slices in the sauce and bring to a boil together.
5. Do not boil longer than until the meat is heated through, otherwise the beef becomes tough.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ragout af Vildt (Game Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover roasted or boiled game
- Butter, 1/4 pound
- Onions, finely chopped
- Wheat flour, 2 tablespoons
- Wine, 1 pægel
- Vinegar, 1 wine glass
- Stock
- Sugar, a small piece
- Soup made from simple game meat (if using previously cooked meat)

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in a casserole.
2. Brown the finely chopped onions in it until tender.
3. Add 2 tablespoons of wheat flour, or as much as the butter will take.
4. Brown the whole strongly to a yellow color and thin it with a pægel of wine, a wine glass of vinegar, stock, a small piece of sugar, and if using simpler meat that was first boiled, the soup made from it.
5. After straining the sauce, give the meat a little boil in it and arrange it in the sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ragout med Kød- eller Fiskeboller og Snitter (Ragout with Meat or Fish Balls and Strips)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Ragout (prepared as above)
- Meat or fish balls
- Puff pastry strips

## Directions

1. Arrange the ragout on a flat serving dish
2. Instead of covering the entire dish with forcemeat, place a wreath of meat or fish balls around it
3. Place puff pastry strips around the outside of the balls
4. This method avoids using the oven and creates an attractive presentation

title: Ramequins (Cheese Ramequins)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) butter dough
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) grated Parmesan or half Swiss cheese

## Directions

1. Roll out the dough thinly
2. Sprinkle with cheese; thus roll out, sprinkle and layer it 3 to 4 times
3. Ramequins can be cut in any shape desired, as half-moons, round or elongated
4. Place on a baking sheet, brush with egg and bake a few minutes in a hot oven until light brown
5. Arrange in a pyramid on a napkin as hot as possible
6. Serve with soup or instead of cheese course

title: Raspberries (Hindber)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound good raspberries
- 1 pound white sugar or good powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Pit the raspberries and sprinkle with 1 pound white sugar or powdered sugar
2. The next day put the whole thing on the fire and cook 10 to 12 minutes with a couple of boils, skimming
3. Pour through a coarse sieve so the juice can drain
4. Cook and skim the juice 8 to 10 minutes
5. When taken off the fire, add the berries to blend with the juice
6. Pour into a pot

title: Raspberry Jelly (Hindbærgelée)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful, fully ripe raspberries
- Melis, equal weight to the berries

## Directions

1. Extract the juice from the raspberries using a linen cloth
2. Let stand overnight for sediment to settle
3. The next day strain the clear juice (but not the sediment) through a jelly bag and weigh it
4. Pour into an enameled pot with an equal weight of Melis
5. Boil and skim until it passes the jelly test, determined by dripping a few drops of hot jelly onto a cold tin plate; if they quickly become stiff, the jelly is sufficiently boiled
6. Pour into small jelly cups or glasses that are widest at the top so the jelly can later be turned out onto a glass plate like a small pudding when serving
7. Let the jelly stand until stiff, then cover with a piece of wax paper dipped in cognac
8. Bind over as with other preserves using a bladder or parchment paper
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Raspberry Juice (Hindbeersaft)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good raspberries
- White sugar at 2 pounds per quart of juice

## Directions

1. Leave the raspberries for 24 hours
2. Strain the juice, measure it, and pass through a sieve lined with thin cloth
3. Let it stand for another 24 hours
4. Put the juice in the kettle with any sediment and scum that has collected on top and 2 pounds white sugar per quart of juice
5. Cook well but only 5 minutes, and skim well
6. The next day bottle and cork the juice

title: Ravigote-Smør (Ravigote Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kg (½ pound) butter
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped chervil
- 2 tablespoons parsley
- 1 tablespoon watercress
- 1 tablespoon leek

## Directions

1. ¼ kg butter is mixed with 1 tablespoon finely chopped chervil, 2 tablespoons parsley, 1 tablespoon watercress, and 1 tablespoon leek.

title: Raw Cherry Juice in Bottles (Raa Kirsebærjaft paa Flasker)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good, freshly picked berries
- Brandy

## Directions

1. Crush the berries and strain through a cloth
2. Place in completely dry bottles
3. Pour brandy over so it stands about two inches above the juice
4. Cork and seal the bottles immediately

title: Red Currants (Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- 1.5 Pd. Melis per 1 Pd. currants

## Directions

1. Strip the currants from the stems
2. Boil the Melis into syrup in the usual manner
3. Add the berries and simmer until clear
4. Remove them with a slotted spoon and place in a deep dish
5. Boil the syrup until thick and pour over the berries
6. Shake them until they begin to swell
7. Skim well and preserve in the usual manner
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Reine-Claude-Gelé (Greengage Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg green plums
- 625 gr sugar
- 1.25 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice

## Directions

1. Wipe the green plums and place them in a pot set over very low heat until the fruit is cooked through, then pour into the jelly cloth.
2. When the juice has dripped through the next day, weigh and cook it into jelly. (See Sugar treatment in No. 93.)

title: Reje- og Asparges-Gratin (Shrimp and Asparagus Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 L (1 Pot) Rejer
- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) Asparges

## Directions

1. Rejer og Asparges tillaves som i Nr. 2.
2. Bages i Gratinskaller.

title: Reje-Sauce (Shrimp Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked, shelled shrimp
- Shrimp shells
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock

## Directions

1. Shell the cooked shrimp and pound the shells and boil them as explained in No. 778

title: Reje-Suppe (Shrimp Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L spare soup
- 2 L shrimp
- shrimp butter from shells and roe
- 100 gr flour
- Madeira wine
- ground white pepper
- asparagus
- Butter slices or Simels

## Directions

1. Cook and pick the shrimp
2. Pound the shells and roe and cook as instructed for Lobster Soup
