3. Thicken with 100 gr flour baked in the shrimp butter
4. Season with Madeira and ground white pepper
5. Serve with the picked shrimp and asparagus which has been cooked in some of the spare soup
6. Serve Butter slices or Simels with it

title: Rejers (Shrimp)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Live shrimp
- Salt
- A little sugar
- A live ember or red-hot iron bar

## Directions

1. The shrimp must be alive; rinse them in cold water before putting them into boiling water with salt and a little sugar.
2. While they cook, stir in the pot with a live ember on an iron rod to deepen the shrimp's color.
3. Cook for about 5 minutes, remove with a slotted spoon and hold under running water until they are cooled; then toss them in salt and remove.
4. In this way the meat becomes firm.
5. The shrimp are easy to shell, and they keep fresh longer.
6. They can be shelled and eaten with vinegar and pepper like lobster.
7. Otherwise, as mentioned with lobster, they can find use in many dishes.

title: Remolade-Sauce (Remoulade Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-5 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1 spoonful anchovies made into anchovy butter
- 1 cup chopped herbs such as celery, leek and a little thyme
- 3 apples
- 4 spoonfuls fine culinary oil
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- 3 teaspoons mustard
- capers

## Directions

1. Rub the hard-boiled egg yolks fine in a bowl with a spoonful of anchovies made into anchovy butter, chopped herbs such as celery, leek and a little thyme, 3 apples grated on a grater, 4 spoonfuls fine culinary oil and 2 tablespoons vinegar.
2. Mix well together and let stand for 2 hours to draw, then strain through a sieve.
3. Add 3 teaspoons mustard and some capers.
4. Chill on ice until serving.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Remoulade (Remoulade Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Forlorn Mayonnaise
- 1 tablespoon parsley
- 1 tablespoon capers
- 1 tablespoon pickled shallots
- Tarragon or other vinegar
- Pepper
- 1¼ dl (½ pint) whipped cream

## Directions

1. To the forlorn mayonnaise, stir in parsley, capers, and pickled shallots—1 tablespoon of each kind, all finely chopped.
2. The sauce can be given tarragon or other vinegar and pepper to taste and 1¼ dl whipped cream.
3. It is placed on ice or in a cold place until it is to be served.

title: Rensdyrkød (Reindeer)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Reindeer back
- Reindeer legs
- Lard

## Directions

1. The back and legs are used.
2. They are larded and roasted as [recipe continues on next page].

title: Rhabarber- og Stikkelsbærsuppe (Rhubarb and Gooseberry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds peeled rhubarb stalks, or 11 pots unripe gooseberries
- 3 pots water
- a couple of sticks cinnamon
- a little lemon peel
- sugar to taste
- 2 spoonfuls rice flour
- a little cold water
- a little grated lemon peel
- a little white wine (optional)
- small rusks or baked wheat bread cubes

## Directions

1. Put the peeled, cut-up rhubarb stalks or the picked gooseberries over the fire with the water, a couple of sticks of cinnamon, and a little lemon peel in an enameled pot.
2. Cook them until they are completely tender, then strain the soup, add sugar to taste, and put it back over the fire to come to a boil.
3. Thicken the soup with 2 spoonfuls of rice flour mixed with cold water, and add a little grated lemon peel, after which it is ready.
4. If you like, you can also add a little white wine when serving.
5. Serve small rusks or baked wheat bread cubes with the soup.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rhabarber-Gelé (Rhubarb Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g (1/4 S) powdered sugar
- 15 sheets isinglass, rinsed in cold water
- 1/4 L (1 pint) boiling water
- 1/4 L (3 pints) cold, preserved rhubarb juice
- 1/4 L (1 pint) sherry
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) cream
- 1 tablespoon powdered sugar
- a few drops of vanilla essence
- stewed fruit (optional)

## Directions

1. Melt the powdered sugar and isinglass in 1/4 L (1 pint) boiling water.
2. Pour this into 1/4 L (3 pints) cold, preserved rhubarb juice together with 1/4 L (1 pint) sherry.
3. Fill into 2 moistened and sugar-strewn ring molds.
4. When the jelly is stiff and ready to serve, turn it out and decorate with foam made from 1/2 L (1/2 pot) cream, to which 1 tablespoon powdered sugar and a few drops of vanilla essence are added.
5. The jelly can be garnished with stewed fruits.
6. If the jelly is made from fresh stalks, use 125 g (1/4 S) more sugar for each liter (pot) of juice.

title: Rhabarber-Grød (Rhubarb Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb stalks
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare this the same way as gooseberry sauce
2. When desired for roasted meat, do not strain it, and do not thicken it either, but only cook it in very little water and sufficient sugar
3. In all cases, strip the stalks by taking hold with a knife at the top of the red end and then drawing off the skin, and cut the stalks into small pieces

title: Rhabarber-Kompot (Rhubarb Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) rhubarb stalks
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) powdered Melis sugar
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægel) water

## Directions

1. Use stalks of medium size
2. Remove the outer skin and cut the stalks into thumb-length pieces
3. Rinse the cut stalks
4. Make a syrup by cooking the powdered Melis sugar with the water
5. Cook the rhubarb in this syrup
6. Remove the pieces gradually and place in the serving bowl
7. Either boil the juice down to a smooth syrup or thicken slightly with a little sago flour and pour over the fruit

title: Rhabarber-stilke (Rhubarb Stalks)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rhubarb stalks
- Water
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Corn starch

## Directions

1. Peel them carefully as for porridge, cut into small pieces and place for a couple of hours in cold water.
2. Then place them on the fire in fresh water and if the stalks are very sour there should be enough that half can be drained off when the stalks have cooked until they begin to fall apart.
3. Then rub them out with sugar and a little cinnamon.
4. A thickening can be added of an insignificant amount of corn starch, dissolved in a little cold water, but this can also be omitted if one simply does not have too much water.

title: Rhabarber-Suppe (Rhubarb Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 to 6 large rhubarb stalks
- 4.5 to 5 L water
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- sugar
- potato flour
- egg yolks
- sago grains

## Directions

1. Cut rhubarb stalks in pieces, rinse and cook in water with lemon peel and a little cinnamon until cooked through
2. Strain and bring to boil again
3. Thicken with potato flour mixed with water
4. For improved flavor, beat sugar with 3 egg yolks and pour into soup while stirring constantly
5. Can also cook sago grains in the soup by beating sugar with 3 egg yolks

title: Rhabarbergrød (Rhubarb Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb stalks
- sugar to taste
- sago flour
- cold sweet cream

## Directions

1. Peel the rhubarb stalks, cut into pieces and cook to extract juice in the same manner as described for Stikkelsbærgrød
2. Treat 3 pots of juice in the same manner and proportions as described for gooseberries
3. Serve with cold sweet cream
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ribbenssteg (Rib Roast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib section with most fat removed
- Peeled apples
- Scalded, pitted prunes

## Directions

1. The rib section is used for this dish, with most of the fat removed.
2. Break the bones in the middle and lay a layer of peeled, cut apples and scalded, pitted prunes on them.
3. Bend the bones toward each other and sew them together.
4. The rib roast can be roasted either in the oven or in a pot, but in the latter case must be browned before adding water or broth.
5. The flour for the sauce can be baked in some of the fat from the broth; it must cook well and have a nice brown color.
6. When serving, pull out the thread and serve the roast with red cabbage or potatoes and beets.

title: Ribbenssteg (Roasted Pork Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork ribs
- salt
- ground pepper
- apples
- prunes
- bouillon
- flour
- boiled peeled potatoes
- red cabbage

## Directions

1. Break the ribs, rub with salt and ground pepper and fill with peeled, chopped apples and prunes.
2. Sew the roast together.
3. Roast at gentle heat, and if necessary thin the sauce with a little bouillon.
4. Brown the sauce with flour and strain it.
5. Remove the thread at serving.
6. Serve with boiled, peeled potatoes and red cabbage.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ribbensſteg (Roasted Rib of Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib of pork
- Pepper
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Roast on a spit for one hour.
2. Crack it crosswise over the bones in two places.
3. Rub it with pepper and salt.
4. Add only as much water from the beginning as can drip in and go over to baste it.
5. Many use only the fat drippings for sauce, prepared at table with mustard and vinegar.

title: Ribs uden Kærner (Red Currants without Seeds)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg soft sugar
- 2 1/2 dl water
- 1/2 kg picked red currants without seeds

## Directions

1. Pick large ripe currants carefully from the stems so they remain whole
2. Remove the seeds with a piping bag
3. Cook sugar, water and the juice from the berries to a very thick syrup in which the fruit gets one boil
4. Remove the berries on a colander and after the juice has drained well pour them into a jar while the juice is further reduced and poured hot over them
5. Seal as soon as possible

title: Ribs-Gelé af hvide Ribs (White Currant Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- White currants
- A little red currants

## Directions

1. Prepare as Red Currant Jelly No. 109. If a little red currant is added, the jelly will have a beautiful pale pink color.

title: Ribs-Gelé af pillede Ribs (Red Currant Jelly from Stripped Currants)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg red currants
- 625 gr sugar
- 1.25 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice
- Salicylic acid as needed

## Directions

1. Pick the currants from their stems and place them in a jar, which is set in a pot of boiling water, keeping it at a boil until the berries begin to burst and release their juice well.
2. Pour into the jelly cloth and leave thus until the following day.
3. Sugar and water are cooked as No. 93. The currant juice must cook for at most 8 minutes and be well skimmed at the same time.
4. Pour into jars and can be sealed after several hours have passed.
5. The currants remaining in the cloth, along with the juice still adhering to them, can be placed over heat and cooked for a quarter hour with sugar according to taste.
6. This can be either pounded sugar or good powdered sugar.
7. After the pot is removed from heat, dissolve 1 small teaspoonful of salicylic acid in some of the warm juice for every 3 to 4 kg currants and then stir throughout the pot.
8. Seal the jar immediately.

title: Ribs-Gelé af upillede Ribs (Red Currant Jelly from Unpicked Currants)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg red currants
- 625 gr sugar
- 1.25 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice

## Directions

1. For this jelly only the green leaves are removed from the berries, after which the currants are placed in a pot set over low heat.
2. Stir regularly until the currants begin to burst, then remove the pot from heat and crush the berries with a spoon before pouring into the cloth.
3. What has run through the next day is weighed and treated as the preceding varieties of jelly. (See Cooking of sugar for jelly No. 93.)
4. The currant juice must likewise cook for only 8 minutes, and is skimmed during cooking.
5. What remains in the cloth is boiled in water and used for fruit soup and the like.

title: Ribsgelée (Currant Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- Powdered sugar (2 pounds per pot of juice)
- Water

## Directions

1. Place the currants in pots in a warm oven so they burst and the juice can run off, making it clearer than by the usual pressing method
2. Pour the juice through a jelly bag
3. Dip the sugar briefly in water, place it over a gentle fire and dissolve it with regular stirring
4. When it is melted, place it over stronger fire and cook until it resembles an even pulp
5. Remove the pot from the fire and pour in the juice
6. Cook the whole together for 10 minutes, after which the jelly is ready to be poured into containers
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ribssaft (Currant Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- 4 kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar or Melis per 1 liter (1 Pot) juice
- 1 small teaspoon salicylic acid per 4 liters (4 Pots) juice

## Directions

1. Remove only the green leaves from the currants; the berries need not be stemmed.
2. Wring the berries in a cloth, but to do this more easily, place them in the preserving pot set over low heat until the berries burst.
3. Measure the juice and place it in the pot together with the sugar and boil for a quarter of an hour.
4. Stir 1 small teaspoon of salicylic acid per 4 liters (4 Pots) juice into the warm juice.
5. Pour the juice into bottles and bind them immediately with parchment paper.

title: Ribssaft (Currant Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds of pressed juice
- 5 pounds of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Place the sugar in the juice to dissolve 12 hours before cooking
2. Then put the juice on to cook for half an hour
3. Stir constantly and skim the foam carefully
4. When it is cooked and clear, filter it and fill it cold into warm bottles, which are immediately corked
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ribsvin (Currant Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 pounds currants
- 25 pounds good, light powdered sugar
- Alternatively, 4 pounds of the currants may be replaced with 4 pounds red raspberries
- 13½ pails water

## Directions

1. Place the sugar in a clean bucket
2. The currants, which are not stripped from their stems, are wrung out in a clean linen cloth and the juice is poured over the sugar, stirred well, and left until the next day
3. Pour 13½ pails water over the wrung-out berries, which likewise stands until the next day, then strain through a sieve and press well
4. Pour the juice into the barrel along with the water from the wrung-out berries, and if this is not sufficient to fill the barrel, fill it with water
5. To make room for the juice to ferment, draw off 2 bottles of juice from the barrel and set aside loosely corked
6. Shake the barrel vigorously to promote mixing and place it in a cellar or other cool place
7. Seal it only loosely and leave it thus for 6 weeks to ferment
8. When fermentation has taken place, add the 2 bottles of juice previously removed, which have also fermented, then seal the barrel well and seal with clay
9. It then rests completely until around February, when the wine can be drawn off and corked well
10. To serve warm, heat the wine over the fire with 1 crushed cardamom per pail, a little whole cinnamon, a couple of cloves and sugar to taste; when the wine is about to boil, light it briefly and then remove from the fire
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rice Porridge Cake (Risengrynskage)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ Pound rice grains, ground
- 3 Pints Milk
- 4 Ounces Butter (4 Tablespoons, not heaped)
- 9 Egg yolks
- ½ Pound sweet almonds, ground
- a few Bitter almonds, ground
- Zest of Lemon, grated
- Sugar to taste
- Salt to taste
- beaten Egg whites

## Directions

1. Grind the rice grains and cook to stiff porridge in 3 pints milk
2. Stir in the warm porridge the butter
3. Add the egg yolks
4. Add the sweet and bitter almonds
5. Add the grated lemon zest
6. Add sugar and salt to taste
7. Lastly fold in the beaten egg whites
8. Pour into greased and floured form
9. Sprinkle with sugar
10. Bake for one hour
11. Serves 10 people

title: Ris med Tomat-Puré (Rice with Tomato Purée)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/6 kg (1 Skp) rinsed and scalded rice
- 1 carrot
- 1 onion
- 1 L (1 Pot) white vegetable broth
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) tomato purée
- Salt

## Directions

1. Pour 1 L white vegetable broth over the 1/6 kg rinsed and scalded rice, together with 1 carrot and 1 onion
2. Set over gentle heat to steam tender under a lid for approximately 1 hour
3. If there is still liquid remaining after this time, remove the lid and cook further until it has evaporated
4. Remove the carrot and onion, and stir 1/4 L tomato purée into the rice
5. Serve this vegetable with meat and fish dishes

title: Ris à l'amande (Rice with Almonds)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 grams (15 quintals) rice
- ½ liter (½ pot) half-skimmed milk
- 100 grams (20 quintals) coarsely chopped or ground sweet almonds
- 15 sheets isinglass
- ¼ dl (¼ pægel) sherry
- vanilla essence
- 3 to 4 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1 liter (1 pot) heavy cream

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in half-skimmed milk.
2. Stir in the coarsely chopped almonds along with isinglass dissolved in sherry, vanilla essence, and powdered sugar.
3. When the pudding has cooled somewhat, stir in whipped cream made from the heavy cream.
4. Pour the rice pudding into the glass bowl in which it is to be served.
5. Place in a cold location for a few hours before eating.
6. Cold sauce made from cherry jam is particularly good with this.

title: Ris à la Pompadour (Rice Pompadour)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kilo (½ pound) rice
- 1 liter (1 pot) half-skimmed milk
- 125 grams (¼ pound) powdered sugar
- vanilla essence
- orange essence
- 12 sheets isinglass
- 1¼ dl (½ pægel) boiling milk
- 10 stiffly beaten egg whites
- powdered sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in half-skimmed milk until soft.
2. Stir in powdered sugar along with vanilla and orange essence to taste and isinglass dissolved in boiling milk.
3. When the pudding is almost cold but not set, stir in the stiffly beaten egg whites.
4. Pour the rice into a wetted mold sprinkled with powdered sugar.
5. Place the pudding in a cold location and serve with chocolate sauce (see no. 803).

title: Ris- og Tomat-Suppe (Rice and Tomato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint tomatoes
- 2 quarts good, clear broth, free from fat
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 0.5 teaspoon pepper
- 4 ounces rice

## Directions

1. Boil the tomatoes until soft and press them through a sieve with a spoon.
2. Stir them into 2 quarts of good, clear broth, free from fat; season with one teaspoon of salt and 0.5 teaspoon of pepper.
3. Add 4 ounces of rice, well washed in cold water, and now cook the soup slowly for half an hour before it is served.

title: Ris-Forme til Kødsuppe (Rice Mold for Beef Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.5 deciliters (11/10 pails) rice
- 0.5 liter (0.5 pot) boiling water
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) blanched and scalded raisins
- Butter
- Sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Scald the rice and place it over heat in boiling water
2. After the rice has cooked for 15 minutes, add the blanched and scalded raisins
3. Cook until the rice is tender but not completely cooked
4. During cooking, gradually add boiling water as needed
5. Stir in a good portion of butter, a little sugar, and salt
6. Rinse a mold or bowl with water
7. Pour the rice mixture into it to be turned out onto a platter later and served with the soup
8. The mixture can also be formed into small bowls, allowing one for each person

title: Ris-Karamel (Rice Caramel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kilo (½ pound) rice
- 1 liter (1 pot) half-skimmed milk
- 75 grams (15 quintals) margarine
- vanilla essence
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons powdered sugar
- the stiffly beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the rice tender in half-skimmed milk and stir in the margarine and vanilla essence.
2. Stir the egg yolks with powdered sugar and add the cooled pudding along with the beaten egg whites.
3. Prepare the pudding mold as for Caramel Pudding, no. 1037, and cook the pudding for about an hour until it has set, or place in a warm oven.
4. Serve cold the next day and eat with caramel syrup.
5. The caramel can be mixed with a little rum or sherry and a little whipped cream.

title: Ris-Rand (Rice Border)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram rice
- 2 liters milk
- 125 grams butter
- 4 eggs
- Salt
- Breadcrumbs
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Cook the rice until approximately tender in 2 liters of milk
2. While the porridge is still warm, stir in the butter, eggs and salt
3. Brush a flat dish with a broad rim with egg white
4. Form the border on it and sprinkle with breadcrumbs
5. Bake for approximately 1/4 hour at good top heat
6. The dough can also be filled into a ring form that is buttered and sprinkled with breadcrumbs
7. Bake in the oven and turn out onto a dish
8. Fill and serve immediately

title: Ris-Sokkel (Rice Pedestal)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram rice
- 2 liters water
- Lemon slices
- Parsley
- Salad
- Red peppers (pimientos)
- Tomatoes
- Mayonnaise
- Fish forcemeat or cooked rice (optional for filling)

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in 2 liters of water
2. Work the porridge well with a spoon in the pot in which it is cooked, frequently dipping the spoon in cold water
3. Arrange the porridge either in the form of long or round pedestals or simply cover the dish with it
4. Around the edge, decorate with lemon slices and parsley or salad
5. Hollowed tomatoes can be filled with mayonnaise to garnish a fried fish dish
6. Red, ripe, fresh peppers can be used as follows: dry them and cut them to an even height, remove the stems and inner seeds, these cases can now be filled with mayonnaise and placed around fried fish or vegetables
7. If filled with fish forcemeat or cooked rice, bake them in the oven
8. Tomatoes can be used in the same way as peppers

title: Ris-Soufflé (Rice Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound rice grains
- 3 cups sweet, boiling cream
- 1 pound white sugar
- Zest of 1 lemon
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Scald the rice grains in several changes of water and pour into 3 cups of sweet, boiling cream with 1 pound white sugar.
2. Cook gently for approximately 2 hours until the rice is completely tender.
3. Press through a coarse sieve.
4. Add the grated lemon zest and juice of 2 lemons.
5. Whip the egg whites to stiff peaks and gently fold in.
6. Pour the mixture into a deep cake pan, sprinkle with crushed white sugar, and place in a moderately warm oven.
7. When it has risen, it is done. Serve either without sauce or with raspberry sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ris-Vandgrød (Rice Water Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg rice, rinsed and blanched
- 4 L water
- apples, blanched and cut, or rhubarb treated the same way, or blanched raisins
- butter
- salt
- butter
- sugar
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Stir the rinsed and blanched rice into the water when it boils, and after 1 hour apples (blanched and cut), rhubarb (treated the same way), or blanched raisins can be cooked in it.
2. Stir a little butter and salt into the porridge.
3. Serve with butter, sugar and sweet milk.

title: Risengrod med Melk (Rice Porridge with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds rice grains
- water
- milk

## Directions

1. Pick through the grains, rinse them in cold water and blanch them twice in boiling water
2. The safest method is then to let them stand for a short time in boiling milk; for most often

title: Risengryn-Suppe (Rice Grain Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ L grains
- 4½ L water

## Directions

1. This soup is cooked and prepared as Barley Soup.
2. It tolerates 3 hours of cooking, and if it should still not be sufficiently thickened, a little potato flour stirred in can be added, which will also prevent the grains from settling to the bottom.

title: Risengrynskage (Rice Grain Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound best Carolina rice
- 2 pots sweet milk
- 1 pound melted butter
- 10 egg yolks
- 9 quintal bitter pounded almonds
- 25 quintal powdered sugar
- zest of 1 lemon
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Clean the rice (see recipe 6) and cook it with 2 pots sweet milk into a soft porridge
2. When cold, stir in 1 pound melted butter, 10 egg yolks one by one, 9 quintal bitter pounded almonds, 25 quintal powdered sugar, and lemon zest
3. Finally fold in the 10 egg whites beaten to peaks
4. Place the whole in a buttered form and bake 0.75 hour in a moderately warm oven
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Risengrynssuppe (Rice Grits Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 measure rice grits
- cold water
- warm water

## Directions

1. Wash the rice grits in cold water, place in a colander, pour warm water over them, then cold water.
2. Cook covered and prepare as Barley Soup (No. 32).
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Risengröd med Melk (Rice Porridge with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds grain
- 2.5 quarts milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter
- sweet cream or milk

## Directions

1. Pick through 3 pounds of grain, rinse in cold water and scald twice in boiling water
2. Let the grain stand for a short time in boiling milk, as the grains are often sour, which causes the porridge to separate; the boiling milk extracts the sourness and gives the porridge a whiter appearance
3. Do not use this milk for the porridge
4. For 3 pounds grain take 2.5 quarts milk
5. Shake the grain in the boiling milk and cook without stirring according to directions until soft, usually about an hour
6. Add salt
7. Serve with sugar, cinnamon, and butter, along with sweet cream or milk

title: Risengröd med Vand (Rice Porridge with Water)
body:
## Ingredients

- grain
- 2 quarts water (proportionally less than milk)
- butter
- currants (optional)

## Directions

1. Use proportionally less water than milk would be used for the milk version; measure about 2 quarts water to the same amount of grain
2. When cooked, stir in a spoonful of butter
3. Add currants if desired

title: Risengrød og Risenvælling (Rice Porridge and Rice Gruel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound rice grains
- 4 pots milk
- powdered sugar
- cinnamon
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean 1 pound of rice grains
2. Grease the bottom of a pot with butter to prevent sticking
3. Add 4 pots of milk and bring to the fire
4. When the milk boils, sprinkle the drained rice grains into it from a sieve
5. Cook until the grains are tender, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking
6. Serve sprinkled with powdered sugar and cinnamon and with butter on the side
7. For gruel, use half the amount of rice grains
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Risengrøds-Rouletter (Rice Pudding Roulettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned rice grains
- boiling water
- strong bouillon
- butter
- flour
- pepper

## Directions

1. Rinse cleaned rice grains in several batches of boiling water and then place to cook in strong bouillon until they begin to burst. Bake butter and flour together, and add the grains along with a little pepper. Stir the mixture during a small boil and set aside to cool. Form roulettes from this, which are treated in the manner mentioned in No. 203. Rice roulettes are used as garnish for boiled chicken and turkey.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Risengrødsklatter (Rice Pudding Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Plate rice pudding
- A couple of tablespoonfuls potato flour
- 3 Whole eggs
- A little raisins
- Crushed almonds

## Directions

1. Stir the rice pudding with a couple of tablespoonfuls potato flour, 3 whole eggs, a little raisins, and crushed almonds.
2. Bake small, flat cakes or cakes in butter on the pancake pan.
3. Serve with powdered sugar over.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Risengrøns-Omelet (Rice Grits Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 35 drams rice grits
- 1 pot sweet milk
- 1.5 measures cream
- vanilla
- 35 drams butter
- 8 egg yolks
- sugar
- salt
- 7 egg whites

## Directions

1. Clean rice grits well and cook almost tender with 1 pot sweet milk
2. Stir 1.5 measures cream with a vanilla stick cooked in it and 35 drams butter into the warm grits
3. When cooled somewhat, add 8 beaten egg yolks with sugar and salt to taste
4. Finally stir in the 7 beaten egg whites
5. Bake the omelet for 2 hours
6. Serve with sprinkled powdered sugar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Risflage-Grød (Rice Flake Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 L milk
- 375 g rice flakes
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stir 375 g rice flakes into the boiling, sweet or semi-skimmed milk.
2. The porridge cooks for 20 minutes, is salted and served.
3. Serve with butter, sugar and cinnamon.

title: Risgrynsklatter (Rice Pudding Fritters)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover rice pudding from dinner
- 2 eggs or just egg whites
- currants, raisins, ground almonds, lemon essence or grated lemon zest
- flour

## Directions

1. Rice pudding fritters are made from the leftover rice pudding from dinner.
2. In a portion for 6 people, add 2 eggs, or just the whites, to the pudding along with either currants, raisins, ground almonds, lemon essence or grated lemon zest.
3. Stir in a couple of spoonfuls of flour or in any case enough so the dough can hold together during baking.
4. It must not sit after it is stirred, as it will become too soft and require more flour to be added.
5. Bake them as cream puffs and serve hot, sprinkled with sugar.
6. Jam can be served with them.

title: Risgrød (Rice Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 625 g rice
- 5½ L semi-skimmed milk
- 3 bottles wheat beer
- salt

## Directions

1. When the milk boils, stir in the grains after they have been rinsed in cold water and then blanched in boiling water.
2. Only stir the porridge until it boils; then put on the lid and place the pot over very low heat so it just maintains a boil.
3. A quarter hour before serving, check the porridge; if it is too stiff, thin it with boiling milk, stirring it in gently along with salt.
4. The porridge cooks for 1½ hours.
5. Leftover porridge can be used for ale porridge, rice grain fritters or a cold rice dessert with fruit.

title: Riskager (Rice Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound Caroline rice
- sweet cream
- ¼ pound butter
- 4 eggs
- 12 quintal finely ground powdered sugar
- fine grated bread for dusting

## Directions

1. Clean the ½ pound Caroline rice as previously mentioned (No. 6)
2. Cook it soft in sweet cream until it becomes like a thick porridge, then pour into a dish
3. Stir ¼ pound butter white, adding 4 eggs one at a time
4. Add 12 quintal finely ground powdered sugar
5. When well stirred, mix with the cooked rice and stir well
6. Grease small forms with butter and sprinkle with fine grated bread
7. Fill with the batter
8. Bake in a moderately warm oven
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rismels Grød (Rice Flour Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound rice flour
- 2 quarts milk
- sliced almonds (worth 5 cents), finely sliced
- salt
- sugar
- butter
- cinnamon
- vanilla (if served cold)

## Directions

1. Mix the flour in some of the cold milk, adding it little by little to prevent lumps
2. Place the rest on the fire
3. When it boils, thin the mixed flour with a little of the hot milk and then pour it into the milk while stirring vigorously back and forth
4. Add the finely sliced almonds and salt when serving
5. It is cooked for a good quarter hour and eaten with sugar, butter and cinnamon
6. This porridge is particularly delicious eaten cold; then take a little less flour and a couple of drops of vanilla, pour it into a mold from which it can be turned and eaten with fruit juice over it
7. As warm porridge without butter and almonds it is good for children

title: Rismels-Budding (Rice Flour Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) melted margarine
- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) rice flour
- 125 g (1/4 S) sweet, ground almonds
- 1 liter (1 pot) boiling milk
- 125 g (1/4 Q) powdered sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Bake the rice flour and sweet ground almonds in the melted margarine and dilute with 1 liter (1 pot) of boiling milk.
2. When the batter has cooled, stir it with the powdered sugar and 12 egg yolks, and finally add the stiffly beaten egg whites.
3. Serve the pudding with cream or fruit sauce. Sweet almonds may be replaced with a few bitter almonds.

title: Rismels-Omelet (Rice Flour Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups sweet milk
- 2 ounces butter
- 3 ounces white sugar
- 3 ounces rice flour
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Bring milk, butter, and sugar to a boil
2. Bake rice flour in the mixture
3. When cooled, add egg yolks first
4. Then fold in stiffly beaten egg whites
5. Bake for about half an hour
6. Serves 6 persons

title: Rismelsgrød (Rice Flour Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1⅓ kg rice flour
- 4½ L milk
- crushed almonds
- salt
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- beer, red wine, or fruit juice and water

## Directions

1. Mix the rice flour with ¼ L of the cold milk.
2. When the remaining milk boils, stir a little of the hot milk into the liaison before adding it to the pot with strong stirring.
3. Cook for 10 minutes.
4. Crushed almonds can be stirred in with the salt.
5. Serve with butter, sugar, cinnamon and either beer, red wine or fruit juice and water.

title: Rismelsgrød (Rice Flour Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg rice flour
- 4.5 liters milk
- salt
- crushed almonds (optional)
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- oil, red wine, or juice and water

## Directions

1. Mix the rice flour in 0.25 liters of cold milk
2. Bring the remaining milk to a boil
3. Stir a little of the hot milk into the flour mixture before adding it to the pot with strong stirring
4. Cook for 10 minutes
5. Optional: stir in crushed almonds with the salt
6. Serve with butter, sugar, cinnamon, and either oil, red wine, or juice and water

title: Rismelsgrød og Vælling (Rice Flour Porridge and Gruel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pots milk
- 1 pound rice flour
- ground cinnamon
- powdered sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Take as much of 4 pots of milk as needed to stir 1 pound of rice flour into
2. Boil the remaining milk
3. When it boils, add the rice flour while stirring constantly, as it tends to sink to the bottom
4. Serve sprinkled with ground cinnamon and powdered sugar and with butter on the side
5. For gruel, use half the amount of rice flour
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rismelskage (Rice Flour Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 0.75 pound powdered sugar
- zest and strained juice of 1 lemon
- lightly pounded almonds
- 0.75 pound rice flour
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks with the powdered sugar
2. Add the finely grated zest and strained juice of 1 lemon, some lightly pounded almonds, and 0.75 pound rice flour
3. Finally fold in the 6 egg whites beaten to peaks
4. Bake the entire mixture in a pan at even oven heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Risotto (Lunch) (Italian Recipe for 12 Persons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Two finely chopped onions
- 125 grams butter
- 1/2 kg rice, blanched
- Good meat broth or water
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Truffle (optional)
- Tongue, boiled and sliced
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Brown the chopped onions in butter.
2. Add the blanched rice along with good meat broth or water flavored with Maggi Bouillon cubes.
3. While the rice cooks, continually add more broth so the rice does not cook dry and become mealy, but remains whole and tender.
4. Risotto can be arranged in a Bordure ring (see recipe 334), and a wreath of cooked sliced tongue can be placed around it, garnished with parsley.
5. The dish can be improved by preparing it with truffles; slice the truffles and give them only a brief cooking in the rice before serving.
6. If truffles are used, omit the onions.

title: Risotto med Skinke (Risotto with Ham)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onions
- Rice
- Meat broth or water
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Cooked smoked ham, finely diced
- Parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. After the butter and onions are browned and the rice is cooked tender, stir in the finely diced cooked smoked ham.
2. Serve with grated Parmesan cheese.

title: Rissoler (Fried Meat Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough
- hash or a mixture of cooked picked fish
- bread crumbs
- cold melted butter
- a small cooked onion
- pepper
- salt
- egg yolks
- water
- egg
- bread crumbs for coating
- clarified butter for frying
- fried parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Roll out butter dough fairly thin and cut it into square pieces about 2 inches on each side. Place a teaspoon of hash or a similar amount of a mixture of cooked picked fish, one third as much grated bread crumbs, a little cold melted butter, a small cooked onion that is chopped, pepper, salt, and 2 egg yolks in the middle of each piece. When the rissole is filled, brush the edges with water. Wrap the dough around the filling, making sure the dough closes tightly at the edges. Brush the rissolers with egg, roll in crushed bread, fry in clarified butter and turn during frying until they become golden brown. Place them on paper or a sieve so most of the fat can drip off. Arrange them dry, garnished with fried parsley. Usually place a napkin on the platter under them.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rissoller (Fried Pastry Pockets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter Dough
- Filling (made the same way as for Roulettes)
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Palmin (cooking fat)

## Directions

1. Roll out the butter dough fairly thinly and cut into 6 to 7 centimeter (2½ inch) squares with a pastry cutter.
2. Prepare the filling in the same manner as for Roulettes, made stiff and cold.
3. Place a dessert spoonful of filling on one half of each piece of dough.
4. Brush the edges with egg, fold the dough together, and press firmly.
5. Brush the Rissoller with beaten egg, dip in breadcrumbs, and fry in Palmin.
6. Arrange in a pyramid on a platter lined with a folded napkin, and garnish with parsley.
7. Serve with a sauce suited to the filling, though they are most often eaten without sauce.

title: Rissoller af Kartoffel-Mos (Potato Rissoller)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled and cooked mealy potatoes
- Pepper
- Salt
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Shallots
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Palmin (cooking fat)

## Directions

1. Mash the peeled and cooked potatoes.
2. Mix in a little pepper, salt, finely chopped parsley, shallots, and a couple of eggs.
3. Roll in egg and breadcrumbs.
4. Fry in Palmin.
5. Serve hot with roasts or meat dishes.
6. May also be made with finely chopped ham.

title: Rissoller med Leverpostej (Rissoller with Liver Pâté)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter Dough
- Liver Pâté
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Palmin (cooking fat)

## Directions

1. Use liver pâté as the filling for the Rissoller.
2. Place a piece of liver pâté on each square of butter dough and treat as described in the basic Rissoller recipe.

title: Ristede Kastanier (Roasted Chestnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Chestnuts
- Coarse salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Make a cross at the point of each chestnut. Place them on a frying pan with plenty of coarse salt and roast them tender over a brisk fire until they easily slip from the shell. Serve very hot in a folded napkin and eat with softened butter.

title: Ristet Brød (Toasted Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Day-old French bread
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the bread from the previous day into slices that are not too thin.
2. Place the slices on an asbestos plate, turn them, and toast until they are light brown on both sides; the bread must be turned regularly.
3. Do not stack the toasted bread on top of each other as it will become tough; instead stand it on edge or in a stand made for this purpose.
4. Serve the bread warm, spread with butter.

title: Ristet Brød med Bananskiver (Toasted Bread with Banana Slices)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Toasted bread
- Banana slices
- Violets for sprinkling
- Orange pieces
- Venushair for garnish

## Directions

1. Spread the toasted bread with butter.
2. Cover with banana slices and sprinkle with violets.
3. Arrange on cake paper and garnish with orange pieces and venushair.

title: Ristoppe, fyldt med Grønsager (Rice Puffs Filled with Vegetables)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Cooked vegetables such as Brussels sprouts, peas, or asparagus
- Parsley or chervil, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Cook the rice as described in recipe 502, but omit the raisins
2. Wet small molds lightly and line them with the rice mixture
3. In the center, place cooked finely chopped vegetables such as Brussels sprouts, peas, or asparagus, or any cooked vegetables that suit soup
4. Add finely chopped parsley or chervil
5. Cover with another layer of cooked rice
6. Turn these small puddings out onto a warm platter while still warm
7. Serve with the soup, allowing one rice puff per person
8. Small timbale molds can be used

title: Risvælling (Rice Gruel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 grams rice
- 6 liters semi-skimmed milk
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Rinse and scald the grains beforehand
2. Pour the grains into the boiling milk, stirring until it comes to a boil
3. Place a tight-fitting lid on the pot to prevent a skin from forming
4. Cook for 2 hours
5. Serve with sugar and cinnamon

title: Roast-Beef (English Roast Beef)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib of beef or tenderloin roast
- Brown gravy
- Parsley
- Scraped horseradish

## Directions

1. Roast as beef roast in about 2 hours
2. Serve a strong brown gravy with the roast beef, which is garnished with parsley and scraped horseradish
3. Peel, rinse and scrape the horseradish lengthwise into thin shavings with a sharp knife
4. When scraping, press it firmly against the cutting board and scrape from top to bottom

title: Roasted Pork Shoulder (Stegt Svinekam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ kg (5 lbs) pork shoulder, with all fat trimmed
- Salt
- Water
- Flour
- Coloring

## Directions

1. Wash the pork shoulder in cold water, sprinkle with salt, and place on a roasting pan, which is set in a hot oven.
2. Add boiling water to the pan, and baste the shoulder well during roasting, which lasts 1½ to 2 hours, depending on its thickness.
3. If roasting in a pot, it must be browned first.
4. Strain the broth, and in some of the skimmed fat, cook the flour, which is thinned with the broth to a suitable smooth sauce, colored with food coloring.
5. Serve with vegetables and preserves as mentioned for roast pork belly.

title: Roasted Pork Shoulder with Apples and Prunes (Stegt Svinekam med Æbler og Svesker)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork shoulder
- Apples, peeled and chopped
- Prunes, scalded and pitted
- Broth from roasting
- Butter
- Flour
- Boiled potatoes or red cabbage

## Directions

1. At the thin side of the shoulder, that is at the ribs, loosen the meat so much that some few peeled and chopped apples can be inserted there as well as some scalded prunes without stones.
2. Sew the meat together and roast it either in a pot, but then it must be browned first, or in a good oven.
3. Apples and prunes can also be larded into the shoulder, just as one lards a lamb roast with parsley.
4. Serve with sauce made from the broth and browned potatoes or red cabbage.

title: Roasted Pork Shoulder à l'Anglaise (Stegt Svinekam å Fanglaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork shoulder with the fat layer
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth from roasting
- Paprika
- Pickles
- Sherry
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Mashed potatoes
- Onions
- Butter for browning onions
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Roast the shoulder with the fat layer, scoring the rind as on roast pork belly, and roast it like that in the oven. The rind must be crispy.
2. Make a good brown sauce from butter, flour, and broth, seasoned with paprika, chopped pickles, a little sherry, and Maggi bouillon cubes.
3. Slice the roast, then arrange the slices together and place them in the center of the platter; around it, garnish with mashed potatoes sprinkled with onions, sliced and browned in butter.
4. Decorate the dish with parsley. Serve the sauce separately.

title: Roasted White Fish (Ristet White Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- White fish
- Fine salt and pepper
- Cold butter
- Smoke from wood

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and dry the fish.
2. Sprinkle a little fine salt and pepper over it, place it with the inside down on a grate and roast over fresh, clear coals.
3. When it is nicely brown, turn it for a short time, then remove it and spread cold butter over it.
4. A little smoke under the fish enhances its flavor.

title: Rod-Grod (Berry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Berries (best of currants and raspberries together)
- Water
- white Sugar
- Isinglass
- blanched and sliced sweet Almonds

## Directions

1. Rod-grod is made from all sorts of berries, but is most delicious of currants and raspberries together.
2. Currants must be picked to avoid the taste of the stems.
3. Press the berries raw through a fine flannel cloth; when all the juice is wrung out, place it over the fire with equal amount of water as juice.
4. When hot, stir in sufficient white sugar.
5. Dissolve isinglass in lukewarm water, and when the juice is near boiling, stir this in.
6. It only needs to boil a couple of minutes to become thick, but must be stirred gently so it becomes smooth.
7. Thereafter pour it either into dishes or small moulds and cups, from which it can be turned out the next day.
8. Blanched and sliced sweet almonds seem pleasant to most in rod-grod.

title: Rod-Grod af Sago (Sago Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- best large Sago grains
- 4 to 5 Quart Water
- Juice
- Sugar

## Directions

1. The best large sago grains are particularly suited for rod-grod, in which no starch is then needed.
2. If you get the best sago, which swells the most, you can reckon 1 pound grains to 4 to 5 quarts water.
3. Add juice and sugar to taste.
4. The grains should be cooked more in the water before juice and sugar are added, and thereafter the whole gets a good boil.
5. Spoon a spoonful onto a plate to see if it is sufficiently thick.
6. If it is too thick, it can be thinned with a little boiling water, but if it is too thin, there is nothing else to do but smooth it with a spoonful of corn-starch dissolved in cold water.
7. The best pearl sago can cook out to a clear jelly, and it becomes best with juice; but if preferred, it can also be made with red wine and lemon juice.

title: Rodkaal (Red Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Red cabbage
- Vinegar
- Butter or fat
- Sugar
- Cherry juice or currant juice
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves and cores from the red cabbage, which is cut into fine strips and placed on the heat with some vinegar, which makes it red, and cook with a lid on over even heat. If the cabbage becomes dry, add more vinegar or soup, but not more than makes the cabbage moist. Before it is tender, add butter or fat from freshly made soup, and finally sugar, a little cherry juice or currant juice and salt.

title: Rollmops (Herring Rolls)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty fresh herring
- 6 coarsely chopped onions
- 25 gr (5 quintal) whole peppercorns
- 75 gr (15 quintal) sugar
- 100 gr (20 quintal) salt
- 15 to 20 bay leaves
- Vinegar water

## Directions

1. Clean the fresh herring and remove the bone
2. These herring, which are cut in the belly, are spread out
3. Mix 6 coarsely chopped onions with 25 gr (5 quintal) whole peppercorns, 75 gr (15 quintal) sugar and 100 gr (20 quintal) salt and distribute this mixture on the herring
4. Roll the herring from the tail upward and bind them with bands or gather them with a sausage pin
5. Cook the herring in vinegar water in which 15 to 20 bay leaves are added
6. The herring remain in the broth they are cooked in and are also arranged in this
7. Serve cold
8. If using cured herring instead of fresh herring, economize on the salt that is placed inside the herring and cook them in as little liquid as possible

title: Rolls in Sweet Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- flour or bread rolls
- water
- salt
- strong sweet sauce
- pears or apples
- dried fruit or various jams

## Directions

1. Make flour or bread rolls according to instructions
2. Cook them in water with a little salt and place them on a platter
3. Pour over them a strong sweet sauce in which pears or apples (which can be dried) or various jams have been cooked

title: Rom-Budding (Rum Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) icing sugar
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) half-skimmed boiling milk
- 12 leaves isinglass
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) white rum
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cream
- cold fruit juice sauce, preferably of raspberry juice

## Directions

1. Stir 5 egg yolks with 125 Gr (1/4 S) icing sugar and whisk into 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) half-skimmed boiling milk
2. Cook to custard consistency
3. Dissolve 12 leaves of isinglass in 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water and stir into the custard
4. When the custard has cooled, add 1/4 L (1 Pægl) white rum and cream foam from 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cream
5. When the mixture begins to set, pour it into a wetted mold
6. Make this pudding the day before
7. Serve with cold fruit juice sauce, preferably of raspberry juice

title: Rom-Fromage (Rum Fromage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 125 gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar
- 1 1/4 dl (1/2 pægl) boiling water
- 6 sheets isinglass
- 1/4 L (1 pægl) white rum
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) cream
- 1/4 L (1 pægl) cream for whipped topping

## Directions

1. Stir egg yolks with powdered sugar
2. Dissolve isinglass in boiling water and add to egg mixture along with white rum
3. Fold in whipped cream
4. Pour into a mold, turn out when set, and serve decorated with whipped cream

title: Rom-Omelet (Rum Omelet)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- 5 tablespoons milk
- egg whites
- butter
- powdered sugar
- rum

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with milk and stir in the foam from the beaten egg whites.
2. Melt a slice of butter on a pan and add the eggs.
3. The omelet, which should only be baked on one side, is folded together and sprinkled with a generous layer of powdered sugar.
4. Serve on a warm dish, pour a wine glass of rum over it and ignite. Set the omelet on fire at the table immediately.

title: Rombudding B (Rum Pudding B)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons finely ground melis sugar
- 1 pail rum
- 1 pot cream
- 10 quintals isinglass dissolved in boiling water

## Directions

1. Stir 4 egg yolks well with 4 tablespoons finely ground melis sugar.
2. Add 1 pail rum.
3. Whip 1 pot cream to thick foam and stir it into the egg yolks.
4. Finally add 10 quintals isinglass that has been cooled after dissolving in 1 pail boiling water.
5. Pour the mixture into a glass bowl and set to cool, and half an hour later the pudding is stiff and can be arranged.
6. Serve with sauce made from fruit juice.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Roræg (Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- Cream or good milk
- Butter
- Salt
- Parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Beat 8 eggs well, first alone, then with 8 small spoonfuls of cream or good milk.
2. Put it in the pot or frying pan with an ounce of butter and a little salt.
3. It tastes good to add a little finely chopped parsley.
4. There must not be a high flame and do not stir, but scrape continually from the bottom with a spatula; if you stir round, the pieces will break apart and that is not the intention.
5. When you find them suitably stiff, take them off the fire.
6. They must not be too hard.
7. You can add cooked asparagus heads in them.
8. Count 3 eggs per person.

title: Rosen-Eddike (Rose Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh rose petals
- good vinegar

## Directions

1. In summer, fill bottles halfway with fresh rose petals.
2. Fill the bottles with good vinegar and cork them.
3. Let stand for some time before the vinegar can be used.
4. Strain from the petals.
5. You will have an aromatic, light pink vinegar for salad, mayonnaise, and similar dishes.

title: Rosenkaal (Brussels Sprouts Pickled)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprout heads
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the heads from the stalks and take off all loose leaves.
2. Pour boiling water over the cabbage.
3. Let it stand for 5 minutes.
4. Pour off with cold water and drain on a colander so the water runs off.
5. Place the cabbage in jars, sprinkle with a little salt, and pour raw vinegar over it.
6. The next day, drain off the vinegar, boil it, and add sugar to taste.
7. Pour the hot vinegar over the cabbage in the jars.
8. Seal the jars.

title: Rosenkaal (Brussels Sprouts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- Water
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the small, firm, green heads from the stalks and remove all yellow or wilted leaves.
2. Rinse well.
3. Place in boiling water with salt and cook for 15 to 20 minutes.
4. The heads must remain whole when cooked.
5. Drain through a colander, then place in a warm ragout dish and pour melted butter over them.

title: Rosenkaal (Brussels Sprouts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled, firm heads of Brussels sprouts
- Cold water
- Cold brine

## Directions

1. Rinse the peeled, firm heads and cook for a couple of minutes
2. Cool with cold water and fill in the jars
3. Pour the usual cold brine over and cook the cabbage in the hermetically closed jars for 45 minutes and cool

title: Rosenkaal, Stuvet (Stewed Brussels Sprouts)
body:
## Ingredients

- small firm brussels sprout heads
- salt
- water
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves from the small firm heads and discard any loose leaves
2. Cook the heads tender in boiling salted water
3. Lay them for a moment on a colander and shake them while still warm with melted butter
4. Serve warm with salt dishes, or they can also be cooked in soup with a little butter
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rosenkager (Rose Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- 35 pounds finely ground powdered sugar
- cochineal dye
- rose oil
- butter for greasing
- colored coarse sugar for decoration

## Directions

1. Whip 6 egg whites over weak heat with 35 pounds finely ground powdered sugar until it becomes frothy
2. Remove from heat and whip until cold
3. Stir in a few drops of cochineal dye and rose oil
4. Grease the sheet as usual
5. Place the cakes on the sheet in a lukewarm oven to dry until they release easily from the sheet
6. Place on the sheet using a teaspoon in size of a five-øre coin
7. Can be decorated with colored coarse sugar and egg white
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Roulader med Fars (Meat Roulades with Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of veal or pork tenderloin
- veal or pork forcemeat
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter
- soup or gravy
- sago flour
- wine
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Pound the meat slices flat and coat with a layer of forcemeat.
2. Roll up and tie with string.
3. Dip in beaten egg and bread crumbs.
4. Brown in butter in a pot.
5. Pour a little soup or gravy over them and braise gently, basting frequently.
6. Strain the sauce and thicken with sago flour mixed with wine, adding mushrooms.
7. Alternatively, serve with vegetables and gravy sauce, omitting wine and mushrooms.

title: Rouletter (Cabbage Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage leaves
- Meat forcemeat
- Meat broth or water
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Half-cook nice white cabbage leaves, and when they are cold, take meat forcemeat and roll it in them
2. The thickness should be like a large chestnut ball and the length 3 inches
3. Bind them with thread, cook in meat broth or salted water, and brown them on the outside in butter
4. The thread cannot be removed until they are ready to be served
5. Serve with brown butter

title: Rouletter (Roulettes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- stew mixture (as used for crust cases)
- isinglass
- breadcrumbs
- eggs
- Claret or Palmin (for frying)
- parsley

## Directions

1. The same stews used for crust cases can be used for roulettes, only the sauce must be baked up to a thick paste; if it is too thin, the roulettes will burst.
2. While the sauce is still warm, add some dissolved isinglass.
3. Pour the stew into a layer about 2 to 3 centimeters thick on a flat dish and should preferably be made the day before so the roulettes can be formed better.
4. With a spoon, take as much of the mixture as the size of a walnut, roll it in ground bread into a ball, and continue until everything is formed.
5. Then dip the roulettes in beaten eggs and again in bread.
6. The roulettes are best cooked in a larger, flat saucepan or pot.
7. If you have a frying basket, it is very easy to place them in it during cooking; otherwise, use a skimming ladle to take them out.
8. The roulettes must be cooked in plenty of Claret or Palmin, cooked until they are brown and crisp, then taken up and placed on paper so the fat can drain off.
9. Serve on a dish with a napkin laid on it; arrange them in a pyramid and decorate either with parsley boiled in the fat until it is crisp, or, as many prefer, with fresh green parsley or head lettuce.
10. Begin cooking 10 to 15 minutes before serving.

title: Roulettes of Lobster
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster
- butter
- flour
- nutmeg
- sugar
- broth
- salt
- milk or water

## Directions

1. Cook the lobsters, remove the meat from the bones and cut it into cubes
2. Pound the shells with butter, sauté over the fire and add water
3. When it has cooked a quarter hour, strain it, and put the lobster butter back on the fire with flour, a little nutmeg and a little sugar and add broth until it becomes a thick sauce
4. Stir in the lobster meat and a little salt at the end
5. When cold, cook as described for roulettes of meat
6. The shells can also be cooked with milk, in which case the lobster butter is diluted with cream or good milk

title: Roulettes of Roasted or Boiled Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chopped onions
- 1.5 ounces butter
- 0.5 ounces flour
- 1 pint bouillon
- 1 pound cooked, finely chopped meat
- ground pepper
- salt
- ground bread
- eggs
- parsley
- 5 rusks
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Sauté two chopped onions in 1.5 ounces butter
2. Bake 0.5 ounces flour in it and gradually add 1 pint bouillon
3. Cook until smooth, then stir in 1 pound cooked, finely chopped meat, ground pepper and salt
4. When completely cold, take a little at a time and roll in ground bread, then in well-beaten eggs, then in bread again
5. Cook them in broth together with a little whole parsley to garnish
6. About 16 roulettes are made from this portion, using 5 rusks and 4 eggs
7. For a more piquant taste, 3 chopped anchovies can be sautéed with the onions, and a little white wine, a little lemon juice and nutmeg can be added to the mixture
8. Serve with a sharp sauce and boiled potatoes or eat with vegetables

title: Rug-Kiks, 1 (Rye Biscuits, 1)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram sifted rye flour
- 1/2 kilogram cake flour
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 tablespoon caraway seeds
- 625 grams cold, stiff margarine
- 1 small glass vinegar
- 3 3/4 deciliters cold water

## Directions

1. Place the rye flour and cake flour on a pastry board with the salt and caraway seeds
2. Take half of the cold margarine and chop it together with the flour using a knife until very finely crumbled
3. Work the vinegar and cold water into the dough with the knife
4. Gather the dough together with your hands and roll out with a rolling pin into a large sheet
5. Distribute the remaining margarine in small pieces over the dough
6. Fold the dough together and roll out 3-4 times as instructed for puff pastry (Nr. 22)
7. Let the dough rest in a cold place for a quarter of an hour
8. Roll out as thin as possible, dusted with flour without adding to the weight
9. Cut the biscuits into squares with a pastry cutter, prick with a fork, and place close together on a baking sheet
10. Bake light brown over good heat. Makes approximately 1 1/2 kilograms of excellent crispy biscuits that keep well for a long time, but should be placed briefly in a warm oven before serving

title: Rug-Kiks, 2 (Rye Biscuits, 2)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram sifted rye flour
- 125 grams margarine
- 1/4 liter cream
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Mix together the rye flour, margarine, cream, salt, and caraway seeds
2. Roll out thin and prick with a fork
3. Bake as the previous recipe. These are cheaper but not as crispy

title: Rugbrød (Rye Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds coarse rye flour (and if available, a couple of spoonfuls of gluten flour)
- 1 pound butter
- 4 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking soda
- 5 quarts baker's yeast
- a handful of salt
- a handful of caraway
- 5½ pails cooked, cooled water
- ½ pail vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together thoroughly the evening before baking to form a dough
2. Before baking, press the dough by hand onto the pan to the thickness of crackers
3. Roll thin and smooth with a bottle
4. Cut into round or square small cakes before placing the pan in the oven
5. When the dough has dried slightly, loosen all cakes with a thin knife
6. Later turn them so they become dry on the side facing the pan as well
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rugkiks (Rye Crackers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound rye flour
- 3/4 pound butter
- 1 cup cream
- crushed dried caraway
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Knead the rye flour, butter, cream, crushed dried caraway, and sugar to taste well together.
2. Roll out into thin dough and cut out crackers with a glass.
3. Prick with a fork and bake at even heat.
4. This yields approximately 100 pieces.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rulleaal (Rolled Eel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- one very large eel
- salt
- pepper
- allspice
- whole pepper
- bay leaves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Skin the eel, cut off the head and tail, and clip off the fins with scissors
2. Cut the eel lengthwise along the back and remove the bone
3. Rinse it well and dry it in a fish cloth
4. Sprinkle it with salt, pepper and allspice and roll it tightly together from the tail
5. Bind it with yarn and sew it into a thin cloth
6. Place it over the fire in cold water with salt, whole pepper, bay leaves and vinegar
7. When cooked, remove it and place it in a press like rolled sausage
8. When the eel is cold, remove the bands, cut it into slices and place on a platter
9. Pour aspic (see recipe No. 247), made from the broth in which the eel was cooked, over it
10. Garnish the dish with lemon and parsley

title: Rulleaal (Rolled Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large eel
- Finely chopped shallot
- Salt
- Pepper
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Twine
- Half vinegar and half water
- Bay leaves
- Peppercorns
- Chopped onion
- Isinglass (4.5 quint per pot of broth)

## Directions

1. Skin the eel and remove the head and tail
2. Split the eel along the backbone and remove the backbone
3. Sprinkle with finely chopped shallot, salt, pepper, allspice and cloves
4. Roll it lengthwise and bind it with twine
5. Cook the eel in half vinegar and half water with some bay leaves, peppercorns, chopped onion and salt
6. Place it in a light press, then remove the twine and cut it into slices across the fish
7. Place these slices in a dish
8. Prepare the broth into aspic as described in recipe 443, using 4.5 quint isinglass for each pot of broth
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rullepölser (Rolled Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat from beef or sheep
- Salt
- Ground saltpeter
- Ground pepper
- Ginger
- Cloves
- Allspice
- Fat meat for filling

## Directions

1. Spread out the meat and cut into pieces 1 quarter wide and 13 quarters long
2. The top surface should be scraped so it becomes evenly thick; on the thin spots, place flat slices of fat meat
3. Coat everywhere with salt, a little ground saltpeter, ground pepper, ginger, cloves and allspice
4. Roll together into a thick, oblong sausage, stuff well at the ends, and sew around with strong thread, with a loop at one end for those you want to smoke
5. Rub well with salt and saltpeter and place with the salted meat or in cooked brine
6. After 3 weeks, remove the sausages you want to smoke
7. Both salted and smoked rolled sausages must be soaked the day before they are to be used; cook very gently and place in a press between two wooden spatulas, with weight on the upper one
8. The next day remove from the press and strip off the outer skin and threads

title: Rullepølse (Rolled Sausage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef, veal, or lamb meat, preferably from the best cuts
- Salt
- 1 Teaspoon Saltpeter
- Ground pepper
- Allspice
- 2 finely chopped onions
- For veal sausages: fresh or salted, fatty bacon
- For lamb sausages: chopped parsley
- String for sewing

## Directions

1. Lay the outer, fatty layer of beef on a board
2. Where the meat is thickest, cut some of it away and distribute it in the sausage along with any other smaller meat pieces
3. Sprinkle salt mixed with 1 teaspoon saltpeter, ground pepper, allspice, and 2 finely chopped onions
4. Roll the sausage very tightly
5. Insert a fork through the middle of the sausage to hold it together while sewing
6. Begin sewing from one end using a sewing needle and very strong thread, sewing as tightly as possible so it holds together after cooking
7. Bind the beef rolled sausage tightly with sailcloth before cooking
8. Beef rolled sausages require up to 2 hours of cooking; test tenderness by pricking with a fork
9. From one beef cut, 3 rolled sausages can be made
10. For veal rolled sausages, add fresh or salted fatty bacon and the same seasonings as mentioned
11. Lamb cuts are usually sufficiently fatty on their own; they can be prepared like beef and veal sausages or with chopped parsley, pepper, salt, and saltpeter
12. Beef rolled sausages are salted within 10 to 12 days, while veal and lamb rolled sausages require only 5 to 6 days
13. When the sausages are cooked, remove them from the boiling broth and place them in a press between 2 bacon boards with sufficient weight on top, and leave them until the next day

title: Rullepølse (Rolled Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb shoulder
- Ground saltpeter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Ginger
- Finely chopped onion
- Meat (optional)

## Directions

1. The shoulder of lamb is best, though beef shoulder can also be used.
2. It is cut from the roast, laid on a board and sprinkled with ground saltpeter, salt, pepper, ginger and finely chopped onion.
3. If one thinks that the rolled sausage becomes too thin, some meat is laid in the middle.
4. Roll it up well and sew it on the side and at the ends and sprinkle with salt or place it in brine in the salt barrel with a press on top.
5. When it is to be used, place it first in soak in cold water and then cook it until it is tender.
6. Then place a press on it, which remains lying until the sausage is cold so that it becomes flat; finally remove the thread.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rum-Budding Nr. 1 (Rum Pudding No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- Half a pound white sugar
- 1.5 pints cream
- 1/2 ounce gelatine
- Cold water
- 3 gills good, preferably white rum

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks white with half a pound white sugar
2. Boil 1.5 pints cream and pour over 1/2 ounce gelatine, first dipped in cold water
3. When the gelatine is dissolved, set it back over the fire until it steams
4. Remove from heat, add 3 gills good white rum, and stir until cold
5. Pour into a mold rinsed with rum
6. Serve with red sauce

title: Rum-Budding Nr. 2 (Rum Pudding No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints cream
- 2 ounces gelatine
- A little boiling water
- 12 egg yolks
- 12 ounces white sugar
- 1 pint rum

## Directions

1. Place cream on the fire with 2 ounces gelatine, first dissolved in a little boiling water
2. When it boils, pour it in under rapid whisking into 12 egg yolks, first stirred thick with 12 ounces white sugar
3. Pour back into the saucepan and whisk until it just begins to boil
4. Remove from heat and stir in 1 pint rum
5. Pour into a dish and stir constantly until cold, then pour into the mold

title: Rungsteds Lyksalighed (Rungsted's Happiness)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg whites
- 10 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 2 tablespoons raspberry jam (without juice)
- 10 egg yolks
- 125 gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) boiling milk
- vanilla essence
- 4 sheets isinglass
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) heavy cream
- macarons

## Directions

1. Beat the 10 egg whites until very stiff.
2. Carefully stir in 10 tablespoons powdered sugar and 2 tablespoons raspberry jam without juice.
3. Pour onto flat baking sheets and dry in the oven like meringue. This must be done the day before.
4. On the day of serving, stir the 10 egg yolks (which have been covered) with 125 gr powdered sugar and add to 1/2 L boiling milk.
5. Make a good cream and add vanilla essence and 4 sheets isinglass dissolved in a small amount of water.
6. Stir the cream regularly until it is almost completely cold.
7. Pour over the egg whites and when set, top with whipped cream made from 1/2 L heavy cream.
8. Serve with macarons.

title: Ruskomsnusk (Root Vegetable Hash)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small cubes of lean, smoked bacon or ham
- Water
- Finely chopped carrots
- Finely chopped white turnips
- Finely chopped celery
- Finely chopped potatoes
- Shelled green peas, about double the amount of other vegetables
- Flour mixed with water for thickening, optional
- Cold butter
- Finely chopped parsley
- Sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook small cubes of lean, smoked bacon or ham until nearly tender in a little water
2. Add equal parts of finely chopped carrots, white turnips, celery and potatoes, and about double the amount of shelled green peas
3. Cook until tender together with the bacon
4. Optionally add a little thickening of flour mixed with water and bring to a boil
5. Finally add a piece of cold butter, finely chopped parsley, sugar and salt as needed
6. This dish can be served as an accompaniment to a meat course or filled in the middle of a ring of forcemeat, or as a dessert course

title: Russisk Abrikos-Marmelade (Russian Apricot Paste)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) apricot pulp from jelly straining
- 0.5 kg (1 lb) powdered sugar
- apricot kernels (optional)

## Directions

1. The scraps from apricot jelly are passed through a puree sieve, after which the pulp is weighed.
2. Stir the sugar into it, and place the pulp over low heat to reduce to a fairly firm mass.
3. Pour it out onto flat pans smeared with oil, dry and cut out as directed above.
4. The apricot kernels can be scalded, chopped and cooked in the jam.

title: Russisk Flambi (Russian Frozen Vanilla Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- 8 egg yolks
- half a pound powdered sugar
- 1 vanilla bean

## Directions

1. Make a vanilla cream from the sweet cream, egg yolks, powdered sugar, and vanilla bean
2. Set over heat and bring to a boil while whisking, then set aside to cool
3. When completely cold, add 2 pails of sweet cream that has been whipped to stiff peaks
4. Gently stir the mixture together so it appears striped
5. Pour into an ice bucket and put the lid on
6. Set in salt and ice and let stand for 3 hours, but do not turn it
7. Turn out onto a plate as usual and arrange whole with small meringues alongside
8. Instead of vanilla cream, thick sweet raspberry juice may be used, but the treatment is otherwise the same
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Russisk Hindbær-Marmelade (Russian Raspberry Paste)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) raspberry pulp from jelly straining
- 0.5 kg (1 lb) powdered sugar

## Directions

1. The raspberry refuse which remains in the jelly cloth is passed through the sieve.
2. The sugar is added, and the jam is reduced, poured out and dried as previously described.

title: Russisk Jordbær-Marmelade (Russian Strawberry Paste)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberry pulp from jelly straining
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Treat as Raspberry Jam.

title: Russisk Kvæde-Marmelade (Russian Quince Paste)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- quince fruit
- water
- 375 gr (0.75 lb) powdered sugar to 1/2 kg (1 lb) strained pulp
- almond kernels (optional)

## Directions

1. Dry the fruit, cut into 4 pieces, and cut away the stem, blossom and core house.
2. Cook the quinces until completely tender in water, remove and pass through the sieve.
3. Weigh the pulp and cook with the sugar until it is so firm that it does not immediately flow together after the spoon is drawn through it.
4. Scalded, broken almond kernels can be cooked in the jam.
5. Pour it out, dry and store as previously explained.
6. Scraps from quince jelly can be passed through the sieve; it is weighed and cooked with sugar and otherwise treated as the previously mentioned Russian jams.

title: Russisk Marmelade (Russian Quince Paste)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- Sugar
- Blanched and chopped nut kernels (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the quinces and cook them completely soft in water.
2. Press the cooked quinces through a sieve.
3. Weigh the resulting pulp and cook it with sugar until it is so firm that it does not immediately flow back together after a spoon is drawn through it.
4. If desired, blanched and chopped nut kernels may be cooked into the marmalade.
5. Spread it out, let it dry, and store as previously explained.
6. Any scraps from quince jelly may be pressed through a sieve, weighed, cooked with sugar, and treated as the other Russian marmalades.

title: Russisk Melon (Russian Melon)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Unripe melon, cut into smaller pieces
- Vinegar
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Cloves
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut an unripe melon into smaller pieces and cover with vinegar.
2. The next day, remove the melon and sprinkle it with sugar.
3. After 4 to 5 hours, put everything in the pot, and cook the melon until it is clear, before removing it and placing it in the jar.
4. Bring the liquid to a boil and pour it over. Add some whole cloves and whole cinnamon as desired.
5. Seal immediately.

title: Russisk Pandekage (Russian Pancake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Pound potato flour
- 0.5 Pound butter
- 1 Pint water
- 1 Pint cream
- 6 to 8 Egg yolks
- Egg whites, whipped
- A little vanilla

## Directions

1. Bake the potato flour in 0.5 pound butter and 1 pint water.
2. Set aside to cool.
3. When cooled, stir it up with 1 pint cream and 6 to 8 egg yolks, and finally with the whipped egg whites.
4. A little vanilla enhances the flavor.
5. Bake 3 pancakes on a pan until they are light brown.
6. Stack them on top of each other with preserves between them and powdered sugar on top.
7. Serve warm.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Russisk Ribs-Marmelade (Russian Currant Paste)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- currant pulp from jelly straining
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make as Raspberry Jam, No. 147. The currants must first be treated as described.

title: Russisk Salat (Russian Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked finely chopped celery
- grated horseradish
- oil

## Directions

1. Make according to the same recipe as Sildesalat (Herring Salad, recipe 285) with the addition of cooked finely chopped celery, a little grated horseradish and oil, stirred into the sauce

title: Russisk Salat (Russian Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablespoons vinegar
- 12 tablespoons strong gravy
- 14.5 tablespoons salad oil
- 3 chopped eggs
- Chopped onion equal in amount to the eggs
- 4 roughly chopped anchovies without bones
- Finely chopped beets and shallot
- 3 teaspoons ground pepper
- 2 knife-tips cayenne pepper
- 4 spoonfuls soy sauce
- Diced meat of roasted goose, duck, turkey or veal

## Directions

1. Fill a terrine with 6 tablespoons vinegar, 12 tablespoons strong gravy, 14.5 tablespoons salad oil, 3 chopped eggs, chopped onion equal in amount to the eggs, 4 roughly chopped anchovies without bones, finely chopped beets and shallot, 3 teaspoons ground pepper, 2 knife-tips cayenne pepper and 4 spoonfuls soy sauce
2. Place it in heat so everything melts together but does not become hot
3. Place diced meat of roasted goose, duck, turkey or veal in a dish and pour the sauce over it
4. Let this salad stand for several hours before serving
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Russisk Stikkelsbær-Marmelade (Russian Gooseberry Paste)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Gooseberry jelly scraps
- Sugar
- Powdered sugar (1½ kg to ½ kg of sieved pulp)
- Oil (for greasing)
- Optional: various essences, colors, chopped or sliced almonds, or nut kernels

## Directions

1. Press the scraps from gooseberry jelly through a sieve.
2. Cook the pulp with sugar until a fairly firm mass forms.
3. Color the mass before spreading it out on flat pans greased with oil.
4. Let the marmalade dry and store as explained in the first recipe.
5. The marmalade may have various essences and colors added to it, and chopped or sliced almonds or nut kernels may be stirred in if it is not to be used for cutting out as decoration on cakes and cheeses.

title: Russisk Æble-Marmelade (Russian Apple Paste)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Sugar
- Powdered sugar (1¼ kg to ½ kg of sieved pulp)
- Optional: vanilla or orange essence, candied orange peel, blanched and cut nuts, almonds, or walnuts

## Directions

1. Apples that are less good and not suitable for other uses may be employed.
2. Quarter the apples; remove the blossom, stem, and core, and place the fruit gradually in cold water until it is put in the pot to be cooked covered over very low heat so as not to scorch.
3. Press the cooked apples through a coarse sieve, weigh them, and return them to the pot to cook with sugar with constant stirring until it becomes a fairly firm marmalade.
4. The flavor may be varied by means of different additions such as vanilla or orange essence, or it may be cooked with finely cut candied orange peel, finely sliced lemon peel, blanched and cut nuts, almonds, or walnuts.
5. Spread out, let dry, and store as the preceding recipes.
