title: Russiske Pandekager (Russian Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Egg Yolks
- 4 Ounces Flour
- 1 Quart Milk
- 3 Tablespoons Sugar
- Egg Whites (whipped)

## Directions

1. Stir 8 Egg Yolks, 4 Ounces Flour, and 1 Quart Milk well together with 3 Tablespoons Sugar
2. Whip the Egg Whites and fold in last
3. Pour the batter very thin on the pan, and when the cake is baked on one side, fold it together without baking on the other, and lay it straight on the plate
4. Preserve can be placed inside
5. Brisk fire is needed

title: Rå Kirsebær Saft på Flasker (Raw Cherry Juice in Bottles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good freshly picked cherries
- Brandy

## Directions

1. The berries, which must be good and freshly picked, are crushed and wrung out in a cloth and placed in completely dry bottles
2. Pour as much brandy as stands a few inches above the juice
3. The bottles should preferably be small, as the juice does not keep once they have been opened

title: Ræddike-Salat (Radish Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- radishes

## Directions

1. Mix together oil, vinegar, pepper and salt with a little chopped parsley
2. Cut the radishes into slices and pour the sauce over them

title: Rödvins-Budding (Red Wine Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pints good red wine
- 1.5 pints clear raspberry juice, not cooked with sugar
- 1 pint water
- 1.5 pounds white sugar
- 1.5 ounces gelatine

## Directions

1. Cook the red wine, raspberry juice, water, and white sugar with gelatine
2. Let it cool and stir until completely cold
3. Pour into the mold
4. Serve with cream sauce

title: Rödvins-Budding (Red Wine Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 Pint good red wine
- 1.5 Pint clear raspberry juice, not cooked with sugar
- 1 Pint water
- 1.5 Pound whole sugar
- 1.5 Ounces gelatine, good quality

## Directions

1. Cook the red wine, raspberry juice, water, and sugar together with the gelatine
2. Allow it to cool and stir until it is quite cold
3. Pour it into the mold
4. Serve with Crème sauce
5. Serves 10 persons

title: Röget Skinke med Eg (Smoked Ham with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Smoked ham (the lean part only)
- Eggs
- Parsley (optional)

## Directions

1. Brown a little butter in the pan, in which fry slices of soaked smoked ham (but only the lean part) until it is slightly browned.
2. Break eggs and place them between the meat in the same way as for poached eggs, but so that it can form into a cake.
3. Bake until the eggs are set (but the yolks must not be hard), and with care so it does not burn.
4. Do not turn it, but slide it from the pan onto the plate.

title: Rød Biskop (Red Bishop)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle red wine
- ¼ pound fine sugar
- 25 quintals orange essence

## Directions

1. Dissolve ¼ pound fine sugar in 1 bottle red wine
2. Add 25 quintals orange essence, which is bought at the apothecary
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rød Sago-Suppe (Red Sago Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- good 1/4 L (good 1 Pægl) medium-fine sago grains
- 4 1/2 L (4 1/2 Pot) water
- whole cinnamon
- lemon peel
- preserved plums and cherries or juice of plums, cherries, currants, rhubarb, raspberries or strawberries

## Directions

1. Cook the soup with whole cinnamon and lemon peel.
2. Season with preserved plums and cherries or with juice of either plums, cherries, currants, rhubarb, raspberries or strawberries.
3. Sweeten to taste.

title: Rød Sagofuppe (Red Sago Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- sago grains
- water
- jam or preserves
- sweet almonds (optional)
- cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the same as the preceding soup, but instead of eggs and wine add jam or preserves and, if desired, sliced sweet almonds
2. Whipped cream, preferably whipped, can be offered at table so that each person can add some to their plate

title: Rød Vin-Gelé (Red Wine Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle red wine
- a little cherry juice
- sugar, water, isinglass (same proportions as White Wine Jelly)

## Directions

1. Prepare as White Wine Jelly.
2. The difference is only that 1 bottle of red wine is used, with a little cherry juice mixed in.
3. Serve with cream sauce or whipped cream.

title: Rød Vin-Kréme (Red Wine Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints red wine
- water
- lemon zest
- cinnamon
- 2 cardamom pods
- half pound white sugar
- 4 tablespoons potato starch
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Mix 2 pints of red wine with a little water and cook it with a little lemon zest, cinnamon and 2 cardamom pods.
2. Let it cook well through, strain it and set it again over the heat with half a pound of white sugar.
3. When it boils, dissolve 4 tablespoons of potato starch in a little water and whip it into the boiling mixture, after which it is poured onto a dish to become cold.
4. It is garnished with whipped cream, which is laid in strips over the cream and served with biscuits.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rød-Grød (Red Fruit Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Equal parts currants and raspberries, or currants and blackcurrants, or currants and cherries
- 2 L (2 pots) water
- Sugar to taste
- 60 gr (12 quintals) flour per liter (pot) of juice, half sago flour and half potato flour
- Chopped almonds (optional)
- Vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Pick and rinse the berries
2. Cook the berries in 2 L water
3. Strain the juice
4. Sweeten the juice
5. Thicken with 60 gr flour per liter of juice, using half sago flour and half potato flour
6. If using cherries, pound them in a mortar
7. Add chopped almonds to the strained juice before thickening, or sprinkle them on the finished pudding
8. Stir vanilla essence into the finished pudding
9. Serve with fresh cream or crème

title: Rødbeder (Beets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beets
- Salt
- Powdered sugar
- Caraway seeds, optional
- Vinegar, diluted

## Directions

1. Rinse the beets and cook in water, or place in a hay box to soften, which takes several hours
2. While warm, rub the skin off lightly by hand
3. Best results come from placing them on a dry pan and roasting in the oven until the skin can be easily rubbed off
4. As they are sliced, place them in a jar and sprinkle with powdered sugar and optionally a little caraway seeds
5. Finally pour diluted raw vinegar over them
6. The jar should preferably stand uncovered but with plenty of vinegar over the beets
7. Serve with roasted meat and use frequently in salads

title: Rødbeder (Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- water
- caraway
- salt
- powdered sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the beets in very little water
2. When tender, remove them
3. Scrape off the skin while hot so they remain whole
4. When cooled, cut into slices and place in a jar with a little caraway and salt if needed
5. Pour over with diluted vinegar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rødgrød (Red Fruit Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pots juice
- 1/4 to 1/2 pound sugar
- Thickening agent: 4 1/2 ounces sago flour, or 1/2 pound rice flour, or 1 pound sago grains
- Cold sweet cream or creme for serving
- Vanilla sugar
- Optional: A small handful of scalded and finely crushed almonds (some bitter)

## Directions

1. For the best Rødgrød, use 3 pots of black currant juice and 1 pot of raspberry juice, placed over heat to boil.
2. When boiling, add the thickening agent (mixed with a little of the cold juice) to the pot and cook with continuous stirring for 6 to 8 minutes; cooking is shortest with sago flour.
3. Before ladling the porridge into large flat bowls, test if it is stiff enough by placing a teaspoon on a saucer and setting it in cold water.
4. If the porridge is too thin, add a little more thickening agent; if too thick, thin it with boiling water.
5. Serve with cold sweet cream or creme and sprinkled vanilla sugar.
6. Alternatively, add a small handful of scalded and finely crushed almonds, including some bitter ones.
7. Other options for juice: exclusively black currant (from 3 pounds berries) or 2 pounds cherries and 1 pound black currant. For cooking the fruit, use 1 pot of water for each pound of fruit; from 3 pounds of berries, 4 pots of juice are obtained after straining.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rødkaal (Red Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 medium red cabbage heads
- Butter or lard
- Salt
- Sugar
- Vinegar (beet or regular)
- Caraway seeds (optional)
- Currant or cherry juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves and cut the cabbage in quarters and slice into fine strips, without including any of the core.
2. Melt a slice of butter or lard on the bottom of a pot, add a little salt and sugar.
3. Pour in several spoonfuls of beet vinegar or regular vinegar.
4. Steam the cabbage until tender in this for at least 2 hours, the last part without a lid and with regular stirring.
5. At serving time, season to taste with more vinegar, sugar, salt, and butter.
6. Many prefer to cook a little caraway seed with the cabbage and season it with currant or cherry juice; however, the juice is unnecessary, as the cabbage can be equally delicious without it.

title: Rødkaal, Stuvet som Surkaal (Stewed Red Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- firm red cabbage head
- water
- vinegar
- butter or meat fat
- salt
- sugar
- currant juice
- caraway seeds (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the outer loose leaves and the stalk from a firm head
2. Cut the cabbage into fine strips
3. Rinse in cold water and lay on a colander to drain
4. Place over heat in a little cold water and vinegar, not too much
5. Cook covered until nearly tender
6. Add a little butter or meat fat, salt and sugar to taste
7. If the color is not a good red, add a little currant juice
8. Many like to mix in a tablespoon of caraway before cooking, which is also healthful
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rødspætter i Karry (Plaice in Curry)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- plaice
- water
- salt
- butter
- flour
- curry
- fish stock
- boiled rice

## Directions

1. Cut and cook plaice as in boiled plaice recipe, but not in too much water so the fish stock can be used for the curry sauce
2. When fish is cooked, brown butter, flour and curry together
3. Add fish stock to make a smooth sauce
4. Pour sauce over fish at serving
5. Garnish with a ring of loose boiled rice sprinkled with dry curry

title: Rødvin-Sauce (Red Wine Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) red wine
- 1/4 liter (1 pint) cherry juice
- 1 1/4 deciliter (1/2 pint) water
- 1 shot glass Cognac
- Sago or potato starch
- Scalded, chopped almonds (optional)
- Sugar to taste (optional)

## Directions

1. Combine 1/2 liter red wine, 1/4 liter cherry juice, 1 1/4 deciliters water, and 1 shot glass Cognac.
2. When it boils, smooth it with a little mixed sago or potato starch.
3. Scalded, chopped almonds may be added to the sauce and more sugar to taste.

title: Rødvins-Sauce til Steg (Red Wine Sauce for Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- 2 tablespoons wheat flour
- Meat broth
- Skimmings from the roast
- Red wine
- Chopped onions
- Chopped anchovies
- Stirred mustard
- Pickled oyster sauce
- Pepper
- Soy sauce

## Directions

1. Brown a piece of butter in a roasting pan, but not too strongly
2. Add 2 tablespoons wheat flour and rub it out with the back of a spoon
3. Continue moving it from one spot in the pan to another so it does not rest long enough to burn
4. Keep the heat from being too strong under the pan
5. When it has acquired a nice brown color, gradually add meat broth and skimmings from the roast
6. The broth does not need to be very strong if you have skimmings
7. When this is mixed a few times but still too thick, add red wine until it becomes the desired thickness
8. Add chopped onions, chopped anchovies, a little stirred mustard, pickled oyster sauce to taste, and a little pepper
9. Cook all this well and then strain it
10. Place the sauce back in the pan to heat it and add a little soy sauce
11. If the roasting pan is only iron and not well mixed with steel, do not keep the wine in the pan, but when the sauce is mixed with broth, place it in a small tinned copper pan or glazed pot and prepare it there
12. This sauce is suitable for beef roast and can also be used for mutton roast; for goose, duck, and ham roast use more mustard

title: Rødvinsbudding (Red Wine Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle red wine (St. Julien)
- 1 pail Cognac
- 2 pails water
- 3/4 pound white sugar
- rind of 1 lemon
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 2 cardamom pods
- 10 quintals isinglass dissolved in boiling water

## Directions

1. Bring to a boil 1 bottle red wine, 1 pail Cognac, 2 pails water, 3/4 pound white sugar, the rind of 1 lemon, the juice of 2 lemons, 1 piece cinnamon, and 2 cardamom pods.
2. Dissolve 10 quintals isinglass in boiling water and add it cooled to the rest.
3. Mix the whole well, stir from time to time, strain, and pour into a jelly mold.
4. Let it stand for several hours on ice; if you do not have ice, the pudding must be made the day before it is to be used, and the mold should stand in cold water.
5. Serve with cream sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Røgede Aal (Smoked Eel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- Butter
- Spinach
- Peas
- Carrots
- Cauliflower
- Potato purée
- Scrambled eggs

## Directions

1. Rinse and dry the eel before cutting it into smaller pieces
2. Fry the pieces in butter in a frying pan
3. Serve the browned butter in which they were fried alongside the smoked eel
4. Serve stewed vegetables such as spinach, peas, carrots, cauliflower, potato purée, or scrambled eggs as garnish

title: Røgede Aal (Smoked Eel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- Butter
- Spinach, stewed
- Peas
- Carrots
- Cauliflower
- Potato puree
- Scrambled eggs

## Directions

1. Rinse and dry the eel before cutting into smaller pieces
2. Fry the pieces in butter in a frying pan
3. Serve with the browned butter in which they were fried
4. Serve with vegetables such as: stewed spinach, peas, carrots, cauliflower, potato puree, or scrambled eggs

title: Røget Gaasebryst (Smoked Goose Breast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- goose breasts
- salt
- sugar
- ground saltpeter
- thin cloth

## Directions

1. Remove the breasts from the geese and rub well with salt mixed with a little sugar and ground saltpeter.
2. Lay them in a barrel skin-side down on the bottom, with each successive layer skin-side up.
3. Place a weight on top and let them stand for 5 to 6 days, turning them daily so the top layer moves to the bottom.
4. When ready to smoke, sew the breast together like a sausage and sew it into a very thin cloth.
5. Hang in smoke for 5 to 6 days or send to a smokehouse.
6. Serve the smoked goose breast cut into thin slices with vegetables.
7. The carcasses from which the breast is removed are excellent for cooking with dried peas, cabbage, and similar dishes; eat the meat along with it. Goose carcasses can be smoked and kept for later in the winter.

title: Røget Makrel (Smoked Mackerel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked mackerel
- Butter

## Directions

1. Pat the smoked mackerel dry with a damp cloth
2. Cut into pieces
3. Toast in butter in a pan
4. Place pieces on a platter with skin side up
5. Reassemble in the fish's original form
6. Garnish with salad or parsley

title: Rønnebær-Gelé (Rowan Berry Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg rowan berries
- 625 gr sugar
- 1.75 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice
- Cognac

## Directions

1. Gather the berries when they have a beautiful red color and are juicy.
2. Remove them from their stems, rinse, and place them in a pot with 2.5 dl water for each 0.5 kg berries.
3. Cook them well before pouring into the cloth, and weigh the juice that has run through the next day.
4. Cook the specified amount of sugar and water as directed in No. 93, then pour in the juice.
5. Cook and skim for 10 minutes and then pour into jelly cups moistened with Cognac, which are sealed when the jelly has begun to set.
6. Serve especially with game. Rowan berry juice can be mixed with half the amount of currant juice or with juice of cooked, unripe apples, and the jelly tastes just as good as from rowan berry juice alone.

title: Rørt Bænkenkage (Stirred Bench Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarter-pounds butter
- 3 quarter-pounds powdered sugar
- 6 eggs
- 1 pound flour
- 1 quarter-pound finely ground sweet almonds
- 14 quintals deer antler salt
- egg white
- powdered sugar for topping
- 1 quarter-pound sweet almonds, sliced into strips

## Directions

1. Beat 3 quarter-pounds butter, 3 quarter-pounds powdered sugar and 6 eggs well together, then beat again with 1 pound flour, 1 quarter-pound finely ground sweet almonds and 14 quintals deer antler salt.
2. Grease a form well with butter and fill with the dough, then brush with egg white on top and sprinkle with powdered sugar.
3. Upon that lay 1 quarter-pound sweet almonds cut into strips.
4. Bake for 1 hour at even oven heat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rørt Smør (Whipped Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Water
- Butter and sweet cream (alternative method)
- Half a teaspoon cornstarch

## Directions

1. Stir butter strongly on heat with a drop of water until it becomes thick
2. This tastes best with asparagus and cabbage, but is expensive and has the disadvantage that it tends to turn into melted butter on a warm plate
3. Alternative method: take equal amounts of butter and sweet cream, knead half a teaspoon cornstarch into the butter, pour the cream into a small pot and stir until it boils
4. This is used for many types of fish

title: Rørt Smør til Asparges (Stirred Butter for Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Stir butter with a little nutmeg over gentle heat, without letting it melt, until it is white and smooth as a salve.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Rørt Æblekage (Stirred Apple Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 Pounds cold cooked potatoes
- 3 Pounds butter
- 8 Egg yolks
- 0.5 Pound powdered sugar (or white farina)
- 1 Lemon, juice and grated peel
- 2 Deep plates peeled apples, without cores, cut into pieces
- 8 Egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Grate the cold cooked potatoes and bake them in 3 pounds butter.
2. Beat the 8 egg yolks well with 0.5 pound powdered sugar (or white farina), the lemon juice, and grated lemon peel.
3. When well mixed, add the potatoes together with the 2 deep plates of peeled, cored, and cut apples.
4. Mix the whole together.
5. Finally, stir in the 8 whipped egg whites.
6. Bake the cake for one hour in a form with removable bottom, or even better in an omelet pan.
7. Serve sprinkled with powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Røræg (Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- sweet milk or sweet cream
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. For each egg, take 2 tablespoons of sweet milk or sweet cream, which is whisked well together with a little salt. The mixture is then poured into a saucepan with melted butter, but only when it foams.
2. When the eggs begin to set, they are done.
3. 15 quarters of butter is calculated for 10 eggs.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Røræg med hakkede, røgede Sild, Makrel eller Hornfisk (Scrambled Eggs with Chopped Smoked Herring, Mackerel, or Garfish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Scrambled eggs
- Smoked herring, mackerel, or garfish, chopped

## Directions

1. Place the set scrambled eggs in a bowl as they are taken from the pan. At serving, turn this mound over onto a smaller platter, and place the chopped fish around it.

title: Røræg med Hummer (Scrambled Eggs with Lobster)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh lobster
- Butter
- Water
- Beaten eggs
- Watercress

## Directions

1. If using fresh lobster, boil and pick the meat. Pound the shells together with a slice of butter. Heat this over the fire, boil it in water and strain it. Skim off the lobster butter that floats on top and put it in a pan to be used for the scrambled eggs. Mix the lobster meat into the beaten eggs. Make the scrambled eggs as usual. Garnish with watercress. Canned lobster can be used very well for this; replace the lobster butter with a little lobster and crayfish extract.

title: Røræg med Karse anrettet med Tomater (Scrambled Eggs with Watercress Arranged with Tomatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beaten eggs
- Watercress, rinsed and cut
- Small red tomatoes

## Directions

1. Mix the rinsed, cut watercress into the beaten eggs and make scrambled eggs as usual. Arrange them warm and place a wreath around them made of small red tomatoes, halved and laid with the round side up. A bunch of watercress is placed between each tomato.

title: Røræg med Kastanier (Scrambled Eggs with Chestnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted or cooked chestnuts
- Beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Mix the roasted or cooked chestnuts, whole or chopped, into the beaten eggs.

title: Røræg med Ost (Scrambled Eggs with Cheese)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beaten eggs
- Milk
- Swiss cheese, diced
- Watercress
- Small radishes

## Directions

1. Into the beaten eggs prepared with milk, add small dice of Swiss cheese. Spread the finished, set scrambled eggs out on a platter, and watercress garnishes the edge of the eggs. In the middle, place a wreath of very small radishes without leaves. Served with open-faced sandwiches.

title: Røræg med røget Laks (Scrambled Eggs with Smoked Salmon)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Scrambled eggs
- Watercress
- Smoked salmon

## Directions

1. Arrange the scrambled eggs on a platter and sprinkle with watercress. Roll slices of smoked salmon together like snails and place them around the eggs. Place small bunches of watercress between them.

title: Røræg med Sardel-Ringe (Scrambled Eggs with Anchovy Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Scrambled eggs
- Tomatoes, coarsely chopped
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt
- Anchovy rings (canned)
- Lettuce heart leaves

## Directions

1. Around the scrambled eggs spread on a small flat platter, place a border of coarsely chopped tomatoes tossed in a mixture of oil, vinegar, pepper and salt. On the tomatoes arrange the small anchovy rings (available in cans), and tuck the heart leaves of head lettuce around the edge of the platter.

title: Røræg med Skinke eller Tunge (Scrambled Eggs with Ham or Tongue)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover ham or tongue, diced
- Beaten eggs

## Directions

1. If using leftover ham or tongue, cut it into small dice and add to the eggs before they are placed over the heat.

title: Røræg med Suppeurter (Scrambled Eggs with Vegetable Soup Ingredients)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked soup vegetables, cut into strips
- Beaten eggs
- Chopped parsley or finely cut leek greens

## Directions

1. A portion of cooked soup vegetables cut into strips can be mixed into the egg mixture along with a little chopped parsley or finely cut leek greens, and then the scrambled eggs are made as usual.

title: Røræg med Tomat-Puré (Scrambled Eggs with Tomato Puree)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beaten eggs
- Tomato puree

## Directions

1. For each egg, use 1 tablespoon of tomato puree instead of milk. The scrambled eggs are served with open-faced sandwiches. Garnish with watercress.

title: Røræg med Trøfler (Scrambled Eggs with Truffles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Truffles
- Madeira
- Beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the truffles very finely and place them over the heat for a moment with a little Madeira. Add them to the beaten eggs.

title: Røræg og Porrer i Gratinskaller (Scrambled Eggs and Leeks in Gratin Shells)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Scrambled eggs
- Cooked leeks
- Bavarian sausage
- Butter

## Directions

1. Arrange the scrambled eggs in the warm shells, and place 2 steaming hot, cooked leeks in a cross over the scrambled eggs with 1 Bavarian sausage on each side. The sausages can be fried in butter in a pan. If serving this dish on a larger platter, place the eggs on one side and stripe the other alternately with sausages and leeks.

title: Røræg og Spinat i Gratinskaller (Scrambled Eggs and Spinach in Gratin Shells)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Scrambled eggs
- Cooked spinach
- Watercress
- Smoked salmon
- Hard-boiled egg, sliced

## Directions

1. These 2 types of prepared warm vegetables are placed side by side in a warm gratin shell. Over the eggs sprinkle watercress, and place 2 thin slices of smoked salmon rolled together like snails on the eggs. Cover the spinach with a slice of hard-boiled egg. Place the shells on a platter lined with a nicely folded napkin.

title: Saft-Gelée (Fruit Juice Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints cherry juice
- 2 pints raspberry juice
- 1 pint water, or more if the juice is very thick
- 12 quintal dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Boil the cherry juice, raspberry juice, and water together with the dissolved isinglass in an enameled pot
2. Strain through a new fine sieve
3. Set in a mold on ice to freeze for 2–3 hours
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Saft-Gelée (Juice Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints cherry juice
- 2 pints raspberry juice
- 1 pint water, or more if the juice is very thick
- 12 quarters dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Boil 2 pints cherry juice, 2 pints raspberry juice, and 1 pint water, or more if the juice is very thick, together with 12 quarters dissolved isinglass in an enameled pot.
2. Strain through a new flour sieve and set in the mold on ice to freeze 2-3 hours.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sago-Omelet (Sago Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet milk
- 1/4 pound sago grits
- 1/4 pound cold butter
- 15 drams powdered sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- grated lemon zest
- 10 drams ground sweet almonds
- 5 drams ground bitter almonds
- 10 egg whites
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Heat 1 pot sweet milk, and when boiling, add 1/4 pound well-cleaned sago grits
2. Cook with constant stirring until the porridge is thick and leaves the sides of the pan
3. Pour out and when slightly cooled, stir in 1/4 pound cold butter, first beaten to a salve with 15 drams powdered sugar
4. Stir in 10 egg yolks one at a time, together with the grated lemon zest, 10 drams ground sweet almonds and 5 drams ground bitter almonds
5. After stirring for half an hour, fold in the 10 egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks
6. Pour the entire mixture into a buttered dish, sprinkled with bread crumbs
7. Sprinkle powdered sugar over the omelet and bake immediately for 1 hour
8. Serve hot with either powdered sugar and jam or with sweet fruit sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sago-Øl (Sago Ale)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 bottles pale ale
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) fine sago grains
- lemon slices
- whole cinnamon
- 6 egg yolks
- 375 Gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Stir 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) fine sago grains into the boiling ale and cook together with lemon slices and whole cinnamon until the grains are clear.
2. Beat 6 egg yolks white with 375 Gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar.
3. Pour the sago ale into the eggs, stirring well with a whisk.

title: Sagovælling (Sago Gruel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 deciliters fine sago grains
- 4 liters semi-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Cook as Rice Gruel, but only until the grains are clear

title: Sagovælling med Vanille (Sago Gruel with Vanilla)
body:
## Ingredients

- sago
- 4 pots sweet boiling milk
- a couple of cinnamon sticks
- a little lemon peel
- a vanilla stick

## Directions

1. Bring 4 pots of sweet milk to a boil with a couple of cinnamon sticks, a little lemon peel, and a vanilla stick
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sagovælling med Vanille (Sago Porridge with Vanilla)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pots sweet milk
- A couple of cinnamon sticks
- A little lemon peel
- A vanilla stick
- Half a pound small, white sago grains, cleaned
- Salt to taste
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Bring 4 pots of sweet milk with cinnamon sticks, lemon peel, and vanilla stick to a boil over heat.
2. Simultaneously, whisk in half a pound of small, white sago grains as the milk comes to a boil.
3. The porridge cooks thereafter for approximately 3 quarters of an hour, until the grains are tender.
4. Add salt and sugar to taste.
5. Do not serve this porridge too hot.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Salat af kold Steg (Cold Roast Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold roast meat
- Shallot
- Cucumbers or pickles
- Vinegar
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the meat, but not too finely, and do not include any browned parts
2. Also chop shallot and mix in with finely sliced cucumbers or pickles
3. Dress with vinegar and pepper

title: Salat No. 2 (Salad No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mustard
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Rub together 2 teaspoonfuls of mustard and 2 hard-boiled egg yolks in 2 spoonfuls of oil; add vinegar, pepper and a little salt.
2. Treat the salad as before.

title: Salat No. 2 (Salad No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoons mustard
- 2 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Rub the mustard and hard-boiled egg yolks together in the oil until combined.
2. Add vinegar, pepper, and a little salt.
3. Treat the salad in the same manner as above.

title: Salmi af Fasan (Pheasant Salmi)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold roasted pheasant or leftovers thereof
- Game stock or soup
- Arrowroot
- Sherry or Madeira
- Coloring
- Truffles
- Toasted bread, bread croutons, or slices

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from the bones of a cold roasted pheasant or the leftovers in smaller pieces
2. Crush the bones and cook them in soup or game stock
3. Dilute arrowroot in Sherry or Madeira and stir into the stock along with coloring
4. Let the sauce boil for at least 10 minutes, then strain it
5. Add the meat to warm it through, but do not let it boil
6. Serve on a hot dish garnished with toasted bread, bread croutons, or slices
7. Truffles may be added to the sauce
8. The salmi may be served warm or cold

title: Salmon (Lax)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Bay leaves
- Whole pepper
- Parsley
- Butter
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Fine chopped parsley
- Oil, vinegar and pepper for cold service

## Directions

1. Salmon retains the scales and should not be split, but cut into fairly thick pieces crosswise.
2. In the water in which the pieces are boiled, add bay leaves, whole pepper and parsley.
3. They can be boiled whole, but if it is a thick middle piece, it takes a long time to cook through.
4. Serve with butter and vinegar; pepper and finely chopped parsley are added to the sauce at the table.
5. When cold, serve with oil, vinegar and pepper.

title: Salt Oksebryst (Salted Beef Brisket)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef brisket
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Boil the meat before it is salted to obtain clear, fresh broth
2. Place the beef brisket over heat in boiling water, skim it, and let it boil for a couple of hours
3. Remove it directly from the boiling broth and place it in the brine to salt for 8 to 10 days
4. When ready to serve, whether hot or cold, remove it from the brine and place it in a pot with gently boiling salted water
5. Cook for half an hour or longer depending on the thickness of the meat
6. If the meat is not to be served hot with vegetables, let it cool in the broth
7. This meat will not have as fine an appearance, but the flavor is good

title: Salt Oksetunge (Salted Beef Tongue)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tongue
- Brine (see Holstein Salt Brine recipe)

## Directions

1. Scald the tongue in boiling water
2. Let it lie in brine for 14 days
3. Place it over heat in boiling water
4. It is tender when the skin can be easily removed
5. Place the tongue back in the broth until cold, unless it is to be served warm
6. For serving as a cold cut, it is most economical to begin cutting from the tongue tip, as nothing goes to waste

title: Salt Oksetunge (Salted Beef Tongue)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lightly salted beef tongue
- Water

## Directions

1. Place the lightly salted beef tongue in boiling water over heat
2. Cook very gently for 2 to 3 hours
3. When the skin comes off easily, the tongue is usually tender
4. Place it back in the soup to keep it warm until serving
5. Cut slightly on the slant into almost 1 centimeter (1/4 inch) thick slices
6. Arrange the slices so the tongue lies whole on the platter with vegetables around it
7. The vegetables can either be tossed in butter after boiling or stewed
8. For serving cold, the tongue will be more juicy if cooled in the soup
9. Smoked beef tongue should be soaked for a few hours before cooking

title: Saltede Snittebonner (Salted String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh string beans
- Salt

## Directions

1. The beans must be freshly picked so they are fresh and crisp. Dry them in a cloth, remove the strings and cut them finely.
2. In a crock spread a thin layer of salt, then a layer of beans about three fingers thick, then a layer of salt thick enough to cover the beans, then beans, and so forth; press each layer firmly and the last must be salt
3. Cover with a piece of cloth, place a disk on top and on this a fairly heavy weight, which after some time, when the beans have become firm, can be removed
4. Tie the crocks, place them in a dry spot and they will keep until summer
5. When they are to be used, put them with the salt in sufficiently much boiling water and cook until tender, then strain through a sieve where the water is pressed out well, and stew them
6. If the beans, which is especially the case in early spring, should be too salty when the water is pressed from them, they can be placed for a short time in cold water with a little powdered sugar in it
7. The beans can also be salted whole in the same way after they are dried and have the strings removed. By adding a little prepared soda to the water in which the beans are cooked, they become tender quickly

title: Saltet Agurkesalat (Salted Cucumber Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- dry salt

## Directions

1. Cucumber salad can be preserved for a long time when peeled and sliced cucumbers in the usual way are placed in a jar, layered with dry salt and tied over.
2. When the salad is to be used, soak the pieces thoroughly and prepare them afterwards in the usual manner.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sammenblandet Frugtsaft (Mixed Berry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries
- raspberries
- currants
- cherries
- 37 parts juice
- 63 parts sugar (Melis)
- vanilla (optional)

## Directions

1. Crush the berries and let them ferment for approximately 3 days at a temperature of 15 to 20 degrees Reaumur.
2. When gas development stops, press out the juice.
3. Give it a brief boil and let it rest quietly for 2 days.
4. Filter it through filter paper, first wetted with water.
5. Weigh the drained juice.
6. Add 63 parts sugar (Melis) to 37 parts juice and dissolve it in.
7. Give it a brief boil and filter while still warm.
8. Give it another short boil and fill into bottles.
9. When boiling the juice a second time, a little vanilla may be added.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sand Cake (Sandkage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound clarified Butter
- 1 Pound powdered Sugar
- 1 Pound fine Flour
- 10 Egg yolks
- beaten Egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir the butter until creamy
2. Stir in the powdered sugar for a quarter hour
3. Stir in the fine flour and egg yolks one at a time
4. Lastly fold in the beaten egg whites
5. Pour into a greased form
6. Bake with moderate heat, especially at first, for 1½ hours

title: Sand-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 400 grams fine sugar
- 500 grams margarine
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- vanilla essence
- 500 grams cake flour
- egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Mix egg yolks, fine sugar, margarine, baking powder, and vanilla essence into a cream
2. Add cake flour
3. Fold in whipped egg whites last
4. Bake in a greased form for 1 hour

title: Sand-Kringler (Sand Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 105 grams (35 quintal) margarine
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 kg (1/10 lb) icing sugar
- a little cardamom
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) flour

## Directions

1. Mix the margarine with 2 eggs, 1/4 kg icing sugar and a little cardamom until it is a tough dough
2. Then mix in 1/2 kg flour
3. Form the dough into small rings
4. Bake until light brown at even heat

title: Sandart (Pike-Perch)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike-perch
- Salt
- Cooked potatoes
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Scale the fish, clean it and wash it well
2. Cut off the fins
3. Sprinkle with salt and let it stand for a couple of hours before cooking
4. Serve on a platter with a napkin, garnished with small cooked potatoes sprinkled with chopped parsley

title: Sandart à la General Blucher (Pike-Perch General Blucher Style)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike-perch
- Salt
- Water or thin soup
- Food coloring
- Butter
- Flour
- Paprika
- Madeira
- Mushrooms
- Butter pastry slices

## Directions

1. Clean and split the fish, remove the bone and cut into 2½ centimeter (about 1 inch) thick slices
2. Wash the slices and sprinkle with salt
3. Cook the head and backbone in water or thin soup, color it brown with food coloring
4. Brown a slice of butter and bake a good spoonful of flour in it
5. Dilute with the strained fish soup until there is enough to cook the fish in
6. Season the sauce to taste with paprika and Madeira
7. Rinse the fish and lay the pieces side by side in a flat casserole or pot
8. Pour the prepared sauce over them
9. Cook very gently and remove when done
10. Finish the sauce with an addition of mushrooms and pour over the fish
11. Garnish with butter pastry slices

title: Sandarten (Sandeel)
body:
## Ingredients

- sandeel
- salt
- cold water
- milk

## Directions

1. Scrape and clean the sandeel, clip off the fins, then let it soak in ice-cold water with salt for about an hour
2. Place it over the heat in cold water, salt, and milk
3. When the water boils, skim it
4. Cook over even heat for about an hour, then remove the pot from the heat and let the sandeel rest in the hot fish water for a quarter hour before arranging it
5. Serve with boiled peeled potatoes and sour butter sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sandkage (Sand Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound finely sifted flour
- a little hartshorn salt
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks for about an hour with 1 pound powdered sugar and 1 pound butter
2. Then stir in 1 pound finely sifted flour and finally fold in the 10 beaten egg whites
3. Mix a little hartshorn salt into the flour
4. Place the mixture in a form buttered and dusted with flour, and bake 1 hour in a moderately warm oven
5. When the cake is removed from the oven, let it rest in the form for an hour, covered with a warm folded cloth
6. Only when it is cold should it be turned out of the form, as otherwise it easily becomes sticky inside
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sandkage med Maizena (Sandcake with Cornstarch)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams margarine
- 135 grams cornstarch
- 325 grams Pillsbury flour
- 1.5 teaspoons baking powder
- 300 grams ground fine sugar
- 250 milliliters sweet milk
- a little vanilla essence
- 7 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Melt margarine and stir in cornstarch, Pillsbury flour mixed with baking powder, ground fine sugar, sweet milk, and vanilla essence
2. Fold in whipped egg whites last
3. Pour batter immediately into a form, well greased and sprinkled with breadcrumbs
4. Bake at moderate heat for 1 hour

title: Santé Budding (Santé Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 pails red wine
- juice of 1 lemon
- grated rind of 1/2 lemon
- 4 pounds sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 4.5 quintals dissolved isinglass
- 3/4 pail red liqueur
- 1.5 pails sweet cream, whipped to foam

## Directions

1. Bring to a boil 2.5 pails red wine, the juice of 1 lemon, and the grated rind of 1/2 lemon with 4 pounds sugar.
2. Mix 10 egg yolks with 4.5 quintals dissolved isinglass and whip together with the remaining ingredients until it is about to boil.
3. Add 3/4 pail red liqueur, then pour the entire pudding into a dish.
4. Whip 1.5 pails sweet cream to foam and add it, stirring until cold.
5. Serve with cream sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sauce d'Orleans (Orleans Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish stock
- Butter
- Flour
- Lobster and crayfish extract
- Cold butter
- Paprika

## Directions

1. Use fish stock to thin flour and butter to a smooth, white sauce
2. The sauce does not need to be bound, but add some lobster and crayfish extract, a piece of cold butter, and a little paprika

title: Sauce Financière (Financière Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Madeira sauce
- Chopped, cooked chestnuts
- Mushrooms
- Truffles
- Olives

## Directions

1. Prepare a Madeira sauce
2. Bring to a boil with chopped, cooked chestnuts, mushrooms, truffles and olives, using just a small amount of each type

title: Sauce Piquante (Piquant Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Very strong, clear, brown gravy
- Pickled pearl onions
- Paprika
- Sugar
- Salt
- Madeira
- Sago flour

## Directions

1. Add pickled pearl onions, paprika, sugar, salt and Madeira to taste to a very strong, clear, brown gravy
2. Boil the gravy for a few minutes
3. Thicken with sago flour stirred in water
4. Sauce piquante is a thin, clear sauce with a slightly sour taste
5. Suitable for roasted meat dishes, especially veal, lamb and pork, as well as for boiled and roasted chicken, spring chicken and turkey

title: Sauce til Grydesteg (Sauce for Pot Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ginger
- Whole peppercorns
- Onion
- Flour
- Brown butter
- Meat broth
- Sugar
- Vinegar
- Sour and sweet sauce ingredients

## Directions

1. The ordinary sauce is what forms itself in the pot when you knead the roast well before putting it in the brown butter and add whole pieces of ginger, whole peppercorns, and whole onion, which are then strained
2. Some prefer sour and sweet sauce, in which case less flour is put on the roast
3. Prepare a small browned thickening as for red wine sauce, dilute it with the broth and add sugar and vinegar
4. In this case use neither onion nor spices, only a little ginger

title: Sauce til Vildt (Sauce for Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cream
- cold Butter
- good Broth

## Directions

1. Put Cream, cold Butter and some good Broth together on the Fire and stir until it boils and has become smooth.

title: Sauce à l'Italienne (Italian Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A couple of very finely chopped onions
- A small amount of garlic
- Butter
- Flour
- Butter to brown
- Finely chopped mushrooms
- Olives

## Directions

1. Brown a couple of very finely chopped onions and just a small amount of garlic in butter
2. The butter should be browned and combined with flour for the Madeira sauce
3. Finely chopped mushrooms and olives are brought to a boil in it

title: Sauce à la Diable (Devil Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) strong, even gravy sauce
- A couple of chopped, lightly browned onions
- 4 tablespoonfuls of finely cut Piccalilly (Pickles), pickled in mustard
- 2 tablespoonfuls of mustard
- Salt
- Wine
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Heat the strong, even gravy sauce
2. Add the chopped, lightly browned onions and bring to a boil in the sauce together with the finely cut Piccalilly and mustard
3. Season with salt, wine and sugar to taste
4. The sauce should have a nice, brown color

title: Sauce-Béarnaise (Béarnaise Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Six egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- 2 tablespoons strong broth or fish broth
- 1 snapseglas Béarnaise essence
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) cold butter

## Directions

1. Stir together six egg yolks with 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley and 2 tablespoons strong broth, or fish broth if the sauce is to be served with a fish dish
2. Finally stir in 1 snapseglas Béarnaise essence (see Nr. 734) and 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) cold butter
3. Place the sauce in a water bath like Sauce-hollandaise, and add the butter in small portions in the same manner

title: Sauce-Mousseline (Mousseline Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Good, true Sauce-hollandaise (see Nr. 793)
- Whipped cream from 1 1/4 Dl (1/4 Pægl) cream

## Directions

1. Make a good, true Sauce-hollandaise (see Nr. 793), into which whipped cream from 1 1/4 Dl (1/4 Pægl) cream is stirred for 1/2 dozen people when it is to be served
2. Eaten with boiled vegetables
3. Lemon juice, which is used in hollandaise sauce, is omitted in this

title: Sauce-Pompadour (Pompadour Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Asparagus water
- Salt
- Lemon juice or white wine
- Nutmeg
- Egg yolks
- Cold butter
- Asparagus

## Directions

1. In 100 gr (20 Kvint) of butter, bake 50 gr (10 Kvint) of flour
2. Thin with 1/4 L (3 Pægle) of asparagus water
3. Bring the sauce to a boil, season with salt, juice of 1 lemon or a little white wine, and a pinch of ground nutmeg if desired
4. Just before serving, bind with 6 well-beaten egg yolks, then stir in 50 gr (10 Kvint) of cold butter
5. Pour the sauce immediately over the asparagus

title: Savojkaal (Savoy Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Savoy cabbage
- Water
- Salt
- Milk
- Sugar
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Cut into 4 pieces and remove some of the core.
2. After rinsing, cook only for a very short time in water with salt; if cooked too long, it loses its fine cabbage flavor.
3. Stew in milk and season with a little sugar and salt.
4. Drain the water well from the cabbage, place in a ragout dish, and pour the sauce over.

title: Scorzonerrødder, Stuvede (Stewed Salsify)
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify roots
- water
- vinegar
- milk
- soup stock
- flour
- butter or cream
- pepper
- nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. Scrape the roots well and immediately place them as you work in lukewarm water with a little vinegar and milk, otherwise they will turn black
2. Cut them into pieces of similar shape and size as asparagus to be stewed
3. Cook in soup stock and they will be tender in a moment
4. Stew them in a white sauce made from flour and butter or in cream with pepper and nutmeg to taste
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Scrambled Eggs with Asparagus and Shrimp (Røræg med Asparges og Rejer)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 2 tablespoons milk for each egg
- 1/2 liter shrimp
- 375 grams asparagus

## Directions

1. Place cooked finely cut asparagus together with peeled shrimp in the beaten eggs.

title: Scrambled Eggs with Bavarian Sausages (Røræg med Bajerske Pølser)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Bavarian sausages
- Eggs
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Arrange the scrambled eggs on a platter and garnish with the sausages, which have been cooked for at most 5 minutes.
2. Sprinkle the scrambled eggs with chopped parsley.
3. The sausages can also be roasted in butter in the pan.

title: Scrambled Eggs with Cauliflower (Røræg med Blomkaal)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Pick the cauliflower into small florets and cook.
2. Add it to the beaten eggs.

title: Scrambled Eggs with Green Peas (Røræg med Grønærter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked peas
- Eggs
- Parsley
- Butter

## Directions

1. Pour the cooked peas through a colander and when the water has drained, mix them into the beaten eggs.
2. The pea water can be used in meat soup or used in the scrambled eggs instead of milk or water.
3. Sprinkle the serving with chopped parsley.

title: Scrambled Eggs with Lobster (Røræg med Hummer)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh lobster
- Butter
- Eggs
- Watercress

## Directions

1. If using fresh lobster, cook and shell them and pound the shells together with a slice of butter.
2. Place it over the fire, cook down in water and strain, and the lobster butter, which floats on top, is skimmed off and put in the pan to be used for the scrambled eggs.
3. Stir the lobster meat into the beaten eggs.
4. Garnish the scrambled eggs with watercress.
5. Canned lobster can be used excellently for this, lobster butter is then replaced with some lobster and crayfish extract.

title: Scrambled Eggs with Milk (Røræg med Mælk)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Milk
- Butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. One egg and 2 tablespoons of milk are generally calculated for each person.
2. Beat the eggs well so that the yolks and whites come together.
3. In a flat saucepan or on a pan, place a slice of butter which is melted and pour in the eggs, which must be placed over very low heat.
4. As the scrambled eggs stiffen, scrape them from the bottom of the pan and place them on the warm platter on which they are to be arranged.
5. The scrambled eggs must not come to a boil and only very little salt should be added, as both of these cause them to easily become grainy and watery.

title: Scrambled Eggs with Mushrooms (Røræg med Champignons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Eggs

## Directions

1. The mushrooms used here must not be sour, that is, not preserved with vinegar or lemon juice.
2. Best are those that are preserved in butter.
3. Use the mushroom butter in the scrambled eggs.

title: Scrambled Eggs with Water (Røræg med Vand)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water
- Butter

## Directions

1. Scrambled eggs can be made with the same amount of water as milk is used above.
2. Made with water, the scrambled eggs become lighter and get a more beautiful yellow color, but in this case a little more butter must be used.

title: Seed-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams margarine
- 4 eggs
- 375 grams fine sugar
- 2 good teaspoons baking powder
- 500 grams Pillsbury flour
- 500 milliliters semi-skimmed milk
- cardamom
- vanilla essence
- orange essence

## Directions

1. Mix margarine, eggs, fine sugar, and baking powder well
2. Add Pillsbury flour and semi-skimmed milk
3. Season with cardamom, vanilla essence, and orange essence to taste
4. Bake for about 1 hour in a greased cake form

title: Seferi Suppe (Celery Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- a small wheat bread, about one pound by weight
- 6 large celeries
- meat stock
- white root vegetables, cut into pieces
- small meat balls

## Directions

1. Grate the wheat bread and cook it in a small meat stock together with 6 large celeries until they can easily be pressed apart
2. Dilute the whole mixture with meat stock, cook a little, press through a colander, then through a sieve and place it back on the fire with some cut white root vegetables
3. When it boils, add small meat balls
4. If meat balls are lacking, the soup can be eaten with toasted bread

title: Selleri, Fyldte og med Boller (Stuffed Celery with Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- large beautiful celery knobs
- salt
- water
- meat forcemeat
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- mace
- white wine
- meat balls

## Directions

1. Clean and peel large beautiful celery knobs and place them in salted water as you work to prevent them from turning black
2. Cut off a lid and hollow out the celery
3. Fill with meat forcemeat
4. Cook very little in water or soup until tender, then remove them
5. Melt butter and cook flour in it to make a white sauce, thin with bouillon and some of the water in which the celery was cooked
6. Add a little ground mace, salt and white wine
7. Warm and serve the celery in this sauce
8. Alternatively, cut the celery into fairly large pieces, cook in soup stock and serve in the above sauce together with fairly large meat balls that have been boiled in water
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Selleri-Salat (Celery Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked finely chopped celery
- lost mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Toss cooked finely chopped celery in lost mayonnaise
2. This salad is given with game roasts, woodcocks, pheasants, partridges, etc.
3. It can be arranged in shells cut from fresh cabbage leaves, or in small clam shells, which are placed around the roast

title: Selleri-Salt (Celery Salt)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 parts dried and grated celery root
- 1 part salt

## Directions

1. Mix 3 parts dried and grated celery root with 1 part salt.
2. Store in airtight tins or tightly closed glass bottles.

title: Selleri-Sauce (Celery Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Two celeries
- Salt
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Butter
- Flour
- 2 egg yolks
- A little sherry
- Ground white pepper
- Insignificant amount of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the celeries into small cubes and cook them tender in soup or in water with salt and Maggi bouillon cubes
2. Make a roux with butter and flour and dilute with the strained soup to a sauce that is not too thick, which is thickened with 2 egg yolks and seasoned with a little sherry, salt, ground white pepper and insignificant sugar
3. Put the cubes in the sauce, which is served with boiled fowl and with boiled beef, veal, and lamb

title: Selleri-Sauce (Celery Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Celery
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock or salted water
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut two celeries into small cubes and cook tender in stock or in salted water with Maggi bouillon cubes
2. Bake butter and flour and thin with the strained stock to a not too smooth sauce, which is bound with egg yolks

title: Selleri-Sauce for kogt Fugl eller Kalkun (Celery Sauce for boiled Fowl or Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Tablespoons Flour
- 1 Teacup Butter
- 1 Pint boiling Milk
- 3 Celery heads
- Water

## Directions

1. Mix 2 Tablespoons Flour with 1 Teacup Butter.
2. Have 1 Pint boiling Milk ready.
3. Stir the Flour and Butter into the Milk.
4. Boil 3 Celery heads cut into small pieces in Water, strain off the Water.
5. Put the Celery into the Milk and set it over the Fire for 5 to 10 Minutes, stirring constantly.

title: Selleri-Suppe (Celery Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small wheat bread, approximately 1 pound in weight
- 6 large celeries
- Meat broth
- White root vegetables
- Small meat dumplings

## Directions

1. Grate the bread and cook it in barely simmering water together with 6 large celeries until these can easily be pressed apart. Then dilute the whole with meat broth, cook a little, press through a colander, then through a sieve and place back on the fire with some cut white root vegetables. When it boils, add small meat dumplings. In the absence of meat dumplings, the soup can be eaten with toasted bread.

title: Selleri-Suppe (Celery Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 celeries
- 4 L (4 pots) broth
- 100 grams (20 skein) butter
- 100 grams (20 skein) flour
- 3 egg yolks
- ground white pepper
- Sherry
- salt
- water for cooking
- peas, asparagus or meat balls (optional)
- toasted bread
- wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the celeries, rinse them, cut them into 4 pieces and cook them tender in water.
2. Take one of them aside and cut it into smaller cubes, which are placed in the tureen.
3. Rub the others through a sieve.
4. Bake 100 grams (20 skein) butter and 100 grams (20 skein) flour and thin with the broth and 1 L (1 pot) of the celery water, after which the celery purée is added and brought to a boil.
5. Stir 3 egg yolks with a couple of spoonfuls of water, and bind the soup with this without bringing it to a boil.
6. Season it with ground white pepper, a little Sherry and salt.
7. Peas, asparagus or meat balls can be added to the soup. Serve with toasted bread. Wine can be added to the soup.

title: Sellerisuppe (Celery Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 good celery roots
- Some of the innermost fine leaves
- 5 pots bouillon
- A little nutmeg
- 3 egg yolks
- Toasted French bread slices

## Directions

1. Clean the celery roots and cut into pieces with some of the innermost fine leaves
2. Place over slow heat with 5 pots bouillon and cook until very tender
3. Press through a sieve while thinning with the soup
4. Return the soup to heat with a little nutmeg and bring to a boil
5. In the tureen, whisk 3 egg yolks
6. While continuously whisking, pour the soup into the egg yolks
7. Serve with toasted French bread slices
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Semoule-Fløde-Rand (Semolina Cream Ring)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 1/4 kg (1 1/2 Q) leftover cold semolina porridge
- 7 sheets isinglass
- 1 1/4 dl (1/2 pægl) boiling water
- 75 gr (15 quintal) powdered sugar
- 20 sweet, blanched and grated almonds
- 8 bitter, blanched and grated almonds
- 3 3/4 dl (1 1/2 pægl) cream for whipping
- fruit compote

## Directions

1. Add isinglass dissolved in boiling water to the leftover semolina porridge
2. Stir in powdered sugar and almonds
3. Fold in whipped cream with a spatula
4. Pour into 2 wetted ring molds
5. After a few hours, turn out and fill with fruit compote

title: Semoulegrød (Semolina Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg semolina grains
- 2.25 to 2.5 liters sweet or semi-skimmed milk
- essence (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare and serve as Rice Flour Porridge
2. Add essence if desired
3. Leftover porridge can be used for dessert the same way as rice porridge

title: Semoulegrød (Semolina Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pots milk
- 1 pound semolina grits
- salt to taste
- a little crushed almonds (optional)
- 1 tablespoon butter (optional)
- ground cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Heat 4 pots of milk over the fire
2. When it boils, sprinkle 1 pound of semolina grits into it while stirring constantly until the porridge boils
3. Add salt at the end
4. Optionally, add a little crushed almonds and 1 tablespoon of butter at the same time as the grits
5. Cooking takes approximately a quarter hour after the grits are added
6. Serve sprinkled with ground cinnamon and sugar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sennep (Mustard)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered mustard
- cooked, lukewarm water
- sugar
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Whether using Russian Sarepta, English Mustard, or Danish mustard, it is stirred in the same way, but not in too large portions at once, as it loses flavor and strength when standing.
2. It must never be stirred the same day it is to be used, as it will then have a bitter taste.
3. Stir the powdered mustard in cooked, lukewarm water and add sugar, salt and a little vinegar.
4. If it becomes too thick after standing for some time, it can be thinned with water or vinegar.

title: Sennep (Mustard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons mustard
- Boiling water
- Salt
- 1 tablespoon olive oil
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 egg yolk (well whipped)
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Pour a little boiling water over the mustard
2. Add salt
3. Stir in olive oil gently
4. Add sugar
5. Stir in a well-whipped egg yolk
6. Add vinegar to taste
7. Should be made the day before it is used

title: Sennep-Sauce til Fisk (Mustard Sauce for Fish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- A slice of butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Fish broth
- Water mustard
- Milk

## Directions

1. Melt a slice of butter and bake 1 tablespoon flour in it, which is diluted with fish broth
2. Stir in water mustard when it boils
3. The mustard sauce becomes milder when it is diluted with milk
4. Melted butter is served at the same time as the mustard sauce

title: Senneps-Sauce (Mustard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- ground mustard
- water
- butter
- flour
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Stir ground mustard with a little water and cook under constant stirring with a slice of butter.
2. Alternatively, add as much flour by weight as mustard, and twice as much butter, and thin the sauce with bouillon.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sherry Cobbler
body:
## Ingredients

- Ice
- 2 wine glasses Sherry
- 1 tablespoon Cognac
- 2 teaspoons crushed sugar
- Lemon peel
- Strawberries, if available

## Directions

1. Fill a large glass more than halfway with ice chunks. Add the remaining ingredients except the strawberries. Pour the mixture quickly several times from one glass to another, then add the strawberries. Drink through a glass straw or drinking tube.

title: Sigtebrød (Rye Sieve Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 Gr (20 Kvint) yeast
- a handful of kitchen salt
- 1 Kg (2 S) rye sieve flour
- 1 Kg (2 S) 'Best Bakery' flour
- 1 1/2 L (1 1/2 Pot) semi-skimmed milk
- optional: a handful of caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Mix yeast with a handful of kitchen salt
2. Add rye sieve flour, 'Best Bakery' flour and semi-skimmed milk
3. The dough can be kneaded the evening before and set on the kitchen table overnight
4. The next morning, knead it with a little flour not counted in the weight
5. Form into 1 larger and 2-3 smaller loaves, which must rise again
6. Brush with milk and bake 1/4 hour in a well-heated oven
7. A handful of caraway seeds can be kneaded into the dough

title: Sild (Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- Herring
- Flour
- Butter or clarified fat

## Directions

1. After the herring is cleaned, washed and dried, dip it in flour and fry in butter or clarified fat.
2. Brown butter in a pan and dip the herring in flour and place in the pan with one head to another's tail, as long as there is space, and press them so tightly together that they form a cake.
3. When one side is fried enough, carefully slide the cake onto a lid, or if you have a sufficiently large plate, place small pieces of butter on the unfried side, place it over the pan and turn it over.
4. Be careful to hold the lid firmly to the pan so there is no opening through which the cake can slide out.
5. Do not press the herrings too hard together, as the herrings might become soft and the flour white.
6. This must be checked as the cake is taken from the pan.
7. Serve hot with greens or cold as an open sandwich.

title: Sild (Herring) - Cooked
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the herring as usual
2. Cook and serve with peeled boiled potatoes and sour butter sauce
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sild (Herring) - Fried
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- salt
- beaten egg
- grated bread
- flour
- clarified butter
- parsley
- boiled potatoes
- fried sauce
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the herring
2. Make several cross-wise cuts and sprinkle with salt
3. Let them rest for 2-4 hours
4. Dry them, dip in beaten egg, roll in grated bread mixed with flour, and fry in clarified butter
5. Serve on a platter garnished with parsley, with peeled boiled potatoes, fried sauce, and vinegar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sild i Eddike (Herring in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good smoked herring
- Milk
- Shallot
- Powdered sugar
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove bones and skin from good smoked herring and split it along the backbone into two equal halves
2. Soak them in milk for a couple of hours
3. Remove and cut each half into 3 to 4 pieces across
4. Layer these pieces with thinly sliced shallot in a crock, sprinkling a very thin layer of powdered sugar over each layer of herring
5. Pour enough vinegar into the crock to cover the herring
6. Let them stand for a day before using
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sild i Marinade (Herring in Marinade)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cold fried herring
- onion slices
- white pepper
- salt
- vinegar
- sugar
- meat or fish stock

## Directions

1. Layer cold fried herring with onion slices, white pepper, salt, vinegar, a little sugar and meat or fish stock
2. In this marinade the herring can keep for several days
3. Serve as breakfast dish

title: Sild med Remoulade (Herring with Remoulade)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Four cured herring
- Milk
- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon stirred mustard
- 3 spoonfuls oil and vinegar
- 5 chopped shallots
- 1 bunch finely chopped parsley
- 1 chopped cucumber

## Directions

1. Lay the cured herring in milk the day before and prepare as for marinated herring
2. Stir together 3 hard-boiled egg yolks with 1 tablespoon stirred mustard and 3 spoonfuls oil and vinegar until it becomes a liquid sauce
3. Stir into this 5 chopped shallots and 1 bunch finely chopped parsley
4. Pour the sauce over the herring and sprinkle with 1 chopped cucumber

title: Sild; nedlagte som Anchiovis (Herring Preserved as Anchovies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 herring
- 1 good handful of salt
- 25 quints light powdered sugar
- 3 handfuls salt
- 3 quints Spanish hops
- 1 handful coarsely crushed pepper
- Cloves
- Bay leaves, cut into pieces
- 1 pot vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the gills from the herring and rinse in 6 changes of water
2. Dry them in a cloth
3. Choose the male herring or milk herring, which are the fattiest and best
4. Sprinkle with a good handful of salt and let stand for 24 hours
5. Dry again with a cloth
6. Mix together the light powdered sugar, salt, Spanish hops, coarsely crushed pepper, cloves, and bay leaves cut into pieces
7. Layer the herring in this mixture
8. Pour the vinegar over everything
9. Place a board with a stone on top
10. After 6 weeks they are ready to use
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Silde-Salat (Herring Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Stock
- Two egg yolks
- Two dessertspoons powdered sugar
- Beetroot vinegar
- French or Russian mustard
- Pepper
- Salt
- Wine
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- One grated onion
- Two apples
- Salted herring
- Meat
- Pickled cucumbers
- Potatoes
- Beetroots
- Chopped eggs for garnish
- Pickled cucumbers or small pickles for garnish
- Parsley or watercress

## Directions

1. Make a white, smooth sauce from butter and flour, diluted with good stock
2. Mix 2 egg yolks with 2 dessertspoons powdered sugar and pour in the warm sauce, stirring until cooled
3. Season with beetroot vinegar, French or Russian mustard, pepper, salt, a little wine, Maggi bouillon cubes, and one grated onion
4. Cut 2 apples, salted herring, meat, pickled cucumbers, potatoes, and beetroots in equal parts into 1½ centimeter (½ inch) long, narrow fillets
5. Turn these gently into the sauce
6. The salad should be made a few hours before serving
7. Garnish with chopped eggs and pickled cucumbers or small pickles arranged in a pattern, with parsley or watercress between
8. A few drops of red food coloring can be added if necessary

title: Silde-Salat (Herring Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fatty, good pickled herring
- Sweet milk
- Fine oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Chopped shallot
- Cooked beets
- Carrots
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Skin and bone the herring
2. Soak in sweet milk for one hour, strain and dry on a cloth
3. Cut into very small cubes and dress with fine oil, vinegar, pepper, and chopped shallot (though some dislike onion or oil, in which case use only vinegar and pepper)
4. For garnish, use cooked beets, carrots, and eggs, all cut into fine cubes and mixed with the salad if desired
5. Arrange garnishes on top in points or stripes, with each item separate
6. Chop hard-boiled egg whites and yolks separately, not finely
7. Can arrange alternating rows of egg white and beets, with egg yolk and carrots between, and can also show a stripe of herring

title: Silde-Øje (Herring Eye)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Herring (Matjes, cured, spiced, boneless herring or anchovies)
- Finely chopped onion
- One raw egg yolk

## Directions

1. Chop the herring and lay them in a ring on a small plate or compote plate
2. Around the chopped herring lay very little finely chopped onion; too much of it easily makes the flavor too prominent
3. In the middle of this arrangement lay one whole raw egg yolk and place it on the table thus
4. Place a spoon on each plate, and with it mix the whole together after sitting down to table
5. Herring Eye is eaten like caviar, spread on bread or biscuits, and is used in sandwiches

title: Sildesalat (Herring Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked herring
- Milk
- Onion
- Cooked potatoes
- Cooked beets
- Veal roast
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Mustard
- Powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Remove bones and skin from smoked herring and let it soak in milk for a couple of hours
2. Finely chop the herring, onion, cooked potatoes, cooked beets and veal roast, all cut into small cubes
3. Mix and stir with vinegar, pepper, mustard and a little powdered sugar if necessary
4. When serving, garnish the salad on top with small, neatly cut pieces of beets
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Silvia-Budding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart milk
- 5 Ounces blanched and finely cut almonds
- 2 Egg yolks
- 4 Spoonfuls white sugar
- 3 Spoonfuls corn starch
- 2 Egg whites, beaten
- 5 Pints rum
- Cranberry sauce or another red sauce

## Directions

1. Reserve a good half cup of the milk
2. Heat the remaining milk with the blanched and finely cut almonds
3. Mix the egg yolks with white sugar
4. Mix the corn starch with the cold milk
5. When the milk is about to boil, add the egg yolks, pouring them in gradually and then pouring into the pot
6. Then add the corn starch mixture
7. When this has come to a boil, remove the pot from the heat
8. Add the beaten egg whites and the rum
9. Let it come to a boil again and pour either into a mold or into small cups, from which it is turned out and eaten cold
10. Cranberry sauce is good with this, or any other red sauce
11. Alternatively it can be made without rum with lemon juice
12. Serves 5 persons

title: Simler (Rolls with Cross)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g margarine
- 1 kg Pillsbury flour
- 1 teaspoon salt with 25 g yeast dissolved in it
- 2 teaspoons icing sugar
- a very small amount of cardamom
- just over 6.5 dl half-skimmed milk
- milk for brushing

## Directions

1. Crumble the margarine into the flour
2. Add salt with dissolved yeast, icing sugar, cardamom, and milk
3. Roll the dough into balls
4. Score a cross on each with a sharp knife
5. Let rise
6. Brush with milk and bake for 15 to 20 minutes

title: Simpel Omelet (Simple Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- 0.5 teaspoon salt
- A pinch of pepper
- A piece of butter the size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Put egg yolks in one dish, egg whites in another
2. Add salt and pepper
3. Beat for half a minute
4. Heat butter in a clean omelet pan or frying pan over heat
5. Beat egg whites until stiff foam
6. Gently fold into yolks
7. Pour into pan
8. Stir with a fork along the bottom so omelet gathers in the center
9. When cooked, slide from one side of pan onto a warm plate
10. Serve immediately
11. An omelet of 3 eggs serves 2 persons; if more is wanted, make another omelet of 3 eggs

title: Simpel Sennep (Simple Mustard)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mustard
- Vinegar
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Stir mustard into vinegar
2. Add sugar to taste

title: Sirupskager (Syrup Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints syrup
- 3 quintal ground cinnamon
- 1 quintal ground ginger
- cloves and cardamom to taste
- 10 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 10 tablespoons melted butter
- 10 beaten eggs
- 6 quintal soda, dissolved in cold water
- flour as needed for a standard dough

## Directions

1. Give the syrup one boil with the cinnamon, ginger, cloves, and cardamom, then add the powdered sugar and melted butter. Whip it until it forms bubbles, and when the syrup has cooled, pour this mixture into the beaten eggs while constantly beating, then stir in the soda dissolved in cold water and as much flour as is needed for a standard dough. The dough must stand for a couple of days in a cold place before the cakes are baked in a well-heated oven.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Skarp Sauce (Sharp Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound powdered icing sugar
- 2 knife tips cayenne pepper
- 2 tablespoons curry
- 2 tablespoons salt
- 4 tablespoons Sarepta mustard
- 2 tablespoons ground nutmeg
- 1 tablespoon white pepper
- 2 tablespoons ground ginger
- 4 bottles tarragon vinegar
- Madeira (optional)

## Directions

1. Mix 1 pound powdered icing sugar, 2 knife tips cayenne pepper, 2 tablespoons curry, 2 tablespoons salt, 4 tablespoons Sarepta mustard, 2 tablespoons ground nutmeg, 1 tablespoon white pepper, and 2 tablespoons ground ginger.
2. Add enough tarragon vinegar so that the total makes 4 bottles full.
3. Ordinary vinegar or vinegar from mushrooms and asparagus can also be used.
4. A little Madeira enhances the flavor.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Skidne Æg (Dirty Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- mustard
- butter

## Directions

1. Thus called hard-boiled eggs, which are peeled and served in a sauce of water-thinned mustard and butter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Skildpadde-Suppe à la faux (Mock Turtle Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large calf's head
- Good brown stock
- Sago flour
- Wine to taste
- Fish balls
- Hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare the head stock and mix it with good brown stock, as the soup requires more liquid than a ragout.
2. Reduce the soup well.
3. Thicken with sago flour mixed in water.
4. Season with wine to taste.
5. Cut the head meat and tongue into dice and bring to a boil in the soup.
6. Serve with fish balls and hard-boiled eggs, either whole yolks or cut in pieces.

title: Skildpaddesuppe (Turtle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds lean veal, cut into square pieces
- 1 pound ham
- Roots
- Mixed vegetables, cut into pieces
- Spices
- Parsley
- 1 pint Madeira
- Turtle meat
- Turtle fat, cut into cubes
- Cayenne pepper
- 1 glass spice extract
- Lemon slices
- Lemon slices for serving
- Fish balls
- Egg balls

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into square pieces. Bring to a boil, then move over a gentler fire. Add 1 pint Madeira and continue cooking for 1 hour until the soup is clear and light yellow in color.
2. Skim the soup and strain it through a sieve into another casserole.
3. Blend it with the necessary amount of turtle meat and the lungs, cut into cubes.
4. Allow it to boil for 12 to 15 minutes, then add a little Cayenne pepper, a glass of spice extract and some of the turtle fat, cut into cubes.
5. Pour the soup into the tureen and serve with lemon slices.
6. Prepare the spice extract as follows: In a casserole, place 1 part dried thyme, the same amount of basil and of marjoram. Pour over a good half pint of boiling Madeira. Boil for 5 minutes, then strain through a sieve into the soup.
7. Garnish with fish balls and egg balls.

title: Skildpaddesuppe No. 2 (Scandinavian Turtle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 3 pounds smoked ham
- 5 onions
- 1 celery
- 1 leek, all cut into pieces
- 1 tablespoon peppercorns
- A little cloves
- Thyme
- Marjoram
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 pound flour
- 4 to 5 quarts turtle bouillon
- 1 flask rum
- 1 flask Madeira or sherry
- Salt
- Sugar
- Turtle meat
- Fish balls
- Egg balls

## Directions

1. Brown 1 pound butter in a casserole with 3 pounds smoked ham, 5 onions, 1 celery, 1 leek, all cut into pieces, and 1 tablespoon peppercorns, a little cloves, thyme, marjoram and 2 bay leaves.
2. Blend in 1 pound flour.
3. When well blended, add 4 to 5 quarts of turtle bouillon, 1 flask rum, 1 flask Madeira or sherry.
4. Stir over the fire until it boils, then set aside to cook slowly for 1 hour and skim well.
5. Season with salt and sugar to taste and strain into a casserole.
6. Cut the turtle meat into suitable pieces and heat in the strained soup.
7. Garnish with fish balls and egg balls.

title: Skinke i Gelee (Ham in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Beets
- Green leaves or parsley

## Directions

1. Since aspic from ham cannot achieve a beautiful white color, color it red with beets
2. Decorate simply with green leaves or parsley

title: Skinkeflæsk (Fried Ham Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices from a smoked ham
- fat bacon or butter
- flour
- pepper
- onion
- boiled peeled potatoes

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from slices cut from a smoked ham.
2. Beat the slices a little with a mallet and soak in water for a couple of hours.
3. Take them out and let the water drain off.
4. Add a little fat bacon or butter to the pan, depending on whether the ham is fatty or lean.
5. Turn the pieces in flour and sprinkle with a little pepper.
6. Add onion, sliced as for beef, in abundance.
7. Fry brown and serve with boiled, peeled potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Skinkesteeg (Roasted Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham roast
- Salt
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Requires roasting longer than veal roast; it must not be red inside.
2. After washing, the roast is rubbed very thoroughly with salt, which helps make the rind crispy, and this is cut so that the whole rind becomes narrow strips.
3. The oven must be very hot when the roast is put in, so that the rind can become crispy; and it is not basted.
4. The rind can also be cut off together with a layer of fat, if the ham is very fatty.
5. The roast is then put in to become warm, and taken out again, while it is sprinkled with crushed allspice mixed with crushed cloves, pepper and salt, after which it is put into the oven again and roasted.

title: Skopzoner-Rødder, stuvede i Mælk (Salsify Roots Braised in Milk)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Lukewarm water
- Wheat flour
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Milk
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Scrape the roots and gradually place them in lukewarm water in which wheat flour and some vinegar have been dissolved to prevent them from darkening
2. Cut the roots like asparagus
3. Place in boiling salted water and as soon as tender, drain on a colander
4. Place in a milk sauce like that for braised potatoes and asparagus

title: Skorzoner-Rødder, stuvede i Suppe (Salsify Roots Braised in Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Butter
- Flour
- Soup or cooking water from the roots
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Sugar
- Lemon juice

## Directions
