1. Make a sauce of butter and flour, diluted with soup or the water in which the roots were cooked
2. Add Maggi Bouillon cubes
3. Thicken with egg yolks
4. Season with salt, sugar and lemon juice
5. Warm the roots in the sauce

title: Skotske Mixed-Pickles (Scottish Mixed Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Small heads of Brussels sprouts
- Very small young carrots
- Young beans
- Green beans, shelled
- Small cucumbers
- Nasturtium seeds
- Radish skins
- Pearl onions
- Small shallots
- Whole lemons
- Horseradish
- Pepper
- Tarragon leaves
- Dill
- Bay leaves
- Wine vinegar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the first five items, each in its own way
2. Divide the carrots only once, leave the beans whole
3. Boil everything quite soft in water with some salt, but not too soft
4. Drain on a sieve
5. Wash the cucumbers the evening before, sprinkle with salt and dry the next day
6. Arrange everything alternately in square glass jars with wide mouths, layer by layer with spices between
7. Fill the glasses with hot and cooled wine vinegar

title: Skum-Budding (Foam Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Ounces corn starch
- 1 Quart milk
- Vanilla
- 1 Ounce white sugar
- 12 well-beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix the corn starch in some of the milk
2. Heat the remaining milk with vanilla and white sugar
3. When it boils, add the corn starch mixture
4. While still on the heat, add the beaten egg whites
5. The pot or saucepan must be large enough to have good space for mixing the egg whites quickly
6. Pour immediately into the mold, from which it can be turned out after a few hours
7. For the sauce, beat 12 egg yolks with white sugar until stiff, then add 2 teaspoons cognac and a little good wine, or use wine Crème sauce or strong sweet sauce
8. Serves approximately 12 persons

title: Skumsauce (Foam Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 whole eggs
- 11 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons powdered icing sugar
- 1/4 glass old white wine
- 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Whisk together 3 whole eggs and 11 egg yolks in a saucepan with 2 tablespoons powdered icing sugar.
2. Gradually add 1/4 glass old white wine and 1 glass water.
3. Set the saucepan over heat and continue whisking the sauce until smooth.
4. Remove from heat and keep in a warm place until needed.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sky (Brown Stock)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- tough meat such as neck, brisket or shank
- leftover meat from game, veal, lamb, and pork
- bones and sinews, all finely chopped
- plenty of fat
- peppercorns
- onions, cut into pieces
- slices of carrots
- celery

## Directions

1. Brown plenty of fat in a pot
2. Add meat, bones, etc., and brown together with peppercorns, onions cut into pieces, slices of carrots and celery
3. When well browned, pour water or thin stock over it; a little salt soup can be mixed in if available
4. Skim during cooking, which takes 5-6 hours
5. Strain the stock and set aside until the next day
6. Remove the fat when the stock is to be used
7. After straining, the stock can be reduced further if necessary

title: Skørdejg til Beklædning af Kageformer (Sugar Dough for Lining Cake Molds)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 lbs. butter
- 30 quintals white sugar
- 1 lb. flour
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Wash the salt out of 0.5 lbs. butter.
2. Work it together with 30 quintals white sugar, 1 lb. flour and 2 eggs.
3. When well kneaded together, roll it out on a board sprinkled with flour.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Slaabrok-Sild (Herring in Paper)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cured herring
- Milk
- Ground pepper
- Onions
- White writing paper, half sheets
- Butter
- Twine

## Directions

1. Soak the cured herring in milk the day before
2. Skin the herring and cut off the head and tail
3. Open them with a slit down the back and remove the bone, then scrape them clean in the belly
4. Lay the herring flat with the inside facing upward, sprinkle with ground pepper and cover with thin onion slices
5. Layer them back into their original form, wrap them in half sheets of white writing paper well buttered, and bind them with twine
6. Fry the herring on both sides either on a grill or in a pan with butter over a brisk fire until the paper is brown
7. Remove the bands and serve the herring in the paper
8. Eat with stewed potatoes

title: Slaaensaft (Sloe Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Sloes
- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar or Farin per 1 liter (1 Pot) juice
- Water

## Directions

1. The berries must have been exposed to frost before preserving.
2. Rinse the berries and place them in a pot with enough water to cover them.
3. Let them boil until they begin to burst, then pour them into a jelly cloth or a fine sieve.
4. Boil the strained juice with the sugar for a quarter of an hour and skim during cooking.
5. Pour the juice hot into dry bottles and bind them immediately.
6. The waste can be poured into a jar, covered with boiling water, and left until the next day.
7. Pour everything through the cloth and boil the juice obtained here with a little sugar; this must be used first.

title: Sliced Smoked Ham (Spegeskinke)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked ham
- Parsley or lettuce
- Braised vegetables or boiled vegetables
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the smoked ham into very thin slices, which are laid in strips on a flat platter and garnished with parsley or lettuce.
2. Serve with most types of braised vegetables. If boiled in water, serve them with melted butter or toss them in butter.
3. Sliced smoked ham can be served with onion sauce and boiled potatoes.
4. Smoked pork tenderloin can be sliced and used in place of sliced smoked ham.

title: Slik-Asparges (Green Asparagus)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salt
- Water
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Asparagus is usually picked from the middle of May to the end of June.
2. The largest are cooked as green asparagus.
3. Peel the asparagus from the middle up, without damaging the tips, but so that only the outermost tough skin is removed.
4. Rinse them, tie them in bundles with about twenty pieces in each, so that the tips point to the same side, and place them in boiling salted water.
5. Allow half an hour for cooking.
6. Arrange the asparagus on a platter with a folded napkin.
7. They can either be placed on a grill or lifted with a spoon, and the water is removed.
8. Melted butter is served with them.

title: Slik-Asparges (Stewed Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Water
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Scrape the asparagus very well while it is raw and place it in cold water as you scrape it; the tip or head is not scraped, so you hold it and scrape downward. It is best to cook large and small separately, as the large ones require longer cooking. Tie them in bundles and cook them in salt and water. They are eaten with melted butter.

title: Slik-Asparges (Tender Asparagus)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled fresh asparagus
- Boiling water
- Salt (10 gr or 2 Kvint per liter)
- Cold salt solution (10 gr or 2 Kvint per liter)

## Directions

1. Bind the peeled, fresh asparagus in bundles or stand them heads upward in a frying basket, all of the same length
2. Pour boiling salted water into the pot where the asparagus is arranged, but do not let the water reach the heads
3. Cook for 5 minutes, then remove the basket and pour cold water over the asparagus
4. Place them immediately in jars with the root ends down, cover with cold salt solution to 3 to 4 centimeters from the rim of the jar, close and place in a pot or steam cooker
5. Fill the pot with cold water up to the neck of the jars and cook for 1 hour
6. Leave the jars in the pot until cooled, or remove and cover with a woolen blanket

title: Slik-Porrer (Sweet Leeks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Large leeks, 1/4 to 1/5 per person
- Water
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Use the largest leeks for sweet leeks, which can be served at breakfast, as a first or second course at dinner, or in the evening
2. Cut away the upper green part of the leeks (which can be used in soup)
3. Thoroughly clean the lower part of soil, which is often found in abundance in leeks
4. Rinse the leeks, tie them in bundles like asparagus, and cook them tender in salted water for 1/2 to 1/4 hour
5. Arrange them as hot as possible on a platter with a napkin, and remove the bands
6. Serve sweet leeks with creamed butter

title: Slik-Ærter (Sugar Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin, fine sugar peas
- Salt
- Sugar
- Butter

## Directions

1. Rub the peas, rinse them, and tie them in bundles.
2. Place the bundles in boiling water with salt and sugar and cook until very tender.
3. Arrange them as hot as possible on a napkin placed on a hot dish.
4. Serve with stirred butter.

title: Sloe Berries (Slaaen)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Sloe berries
- Water

## Directions

1. The berries must not be picked before they have been touched by frost
2. Fill them into a barrel
3. Pour water over them and insert the bung
4. During the first month, roll the barrel daily across the floor
5. As the juice is used, more water can be added

title: Smaa Agurker (Small Pickled Gherkins)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 very small gherkins
- Strong salt brine
- 1 liter (1 pot) vinegar
- 125 grams (1/4 skålpund) powdered sugar (Melis)
- About 20 small peeled shallots
- 2 pods Spanish pepper
- 5 pieces long pepper
- 1 tablespoon whole yellow mustard

## Directions

1. Place the very small gherkins in strong salt brine
2. Leave until the next day, then remove and dry
3. Place in jars or crocks
4. Boil the vinegar with powdered sugar, small peeled shallots, Spanish pepper pods, long pepper, and whole yellow mustard
5. Pour the boiling vinegar over the gherkins
6. Cover with cloth and bind with parchment paper the next day

title: Smaa Boller (Small Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon powdered sugar
- 2 tablespoons sweet cream
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 6 tablespoons flour
- 2 crushed cardamom seeds

## Directions

1. Beat 2 eggs well with 1 tablespoon powdered sugar.
2. Add 2 tablespoons sweet cream, 2 tablespoons melted butter, 6 tablespoons flour, and 2 crushed cardamom seeds.
3. Work all together well and set aside for one hour to cool.
4. When the soup for which the balls are intended boils, place them in with a teaspoon.
5. Cook for 2 minutes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Smaa Butterdejgs-Kager (Small Puff Pastry Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry (see recipe 22)
- 1 egg
- sugar
- chopped almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Roll out a thin sheet of puff pastry
2. Cut out small cakes with a shot glass
3. Dip in egg and plenty of sugar
4. Bake until light brown in a well-heated oven
5. Chopped almonds can be sprinkled on the cakes, which are placed close together on the sheet
6. Best served the same day they are baked

title: Smaa Dyre-Fileter (Small Venison Fillets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison fillets from the back, nearly one inch thick
- Butter
- Flour
- Salt and pepper
- Game stock or water
- Color (food coloring)
- Currant, lingonberry, or redwine jelly (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut small fillets from the back, nearly one inch thick.
2. Chop the backbone into pieces, sauté in butter and cook in thin stock or water.
3. Brown the flour in butter for the sauce, dilute with the strained stock.
4. Color the sauce and finish with currant, lingonberry jelly or red wine if desired.
5. Beat the fillets lightly with a meat mallet, season with salt and pepper.
6. Fry on the pan in brown butter over high heat.
7. Variations in sauce can be made with different additions, given only one boil, but omit the jelly in these cases.

title: Smaa Marencer med Mandler (Small Macaroons with Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- egg white
- 5 cents gum tragacanth
- white sugar
- 2 ounces finely ground sweet almonds

## Directions

1. The evening before baking, pour egg white over 5 cents gum tragacanth and let it stand until the next morning.
2. Beat well and stir with as much white sugar as it can take and 2 ounces finely ground sweet almonds.
3. Roll small pieces of dough with hands against the cake board into cylinders, cut like peppernuts, and bake light brown on a buttered pan.

title: Smaa Postejer med Kalvebrissel (Small Pâtés with Veal Sweetbread)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough
- Egg yolk, beaten
- Well-washed and cooked veal sweetbread
- Butter
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Mushrooms, chopped
- Madeira or Sherry

## Directions

1. Roll out butter dough to 3 inches thick
2. Cut out with a round form or large wine glass
3. From a piece of the same size, cut out the center with a smaller glass to form a ring
4. Cut out a smaller third piece to form the lid
5. Brush beaten egg yolk on the bottom piece
6. Place the ring on top and brush the surface with egg (not the sides)
7. Brush the lids (baked separately) with egg as well
8. Wet the baking sheet with a little water before placing the pâtés on it
9. Bake in the oven at good heat
10. Remove the skin and membrane from the cooked veal sweetbread
11. Cut into pieces the size of a pea
12. Brown butter and flour together and thin with bouillon
13. Add salt, pepper, and chopped mushrooms
14. Add the chopped veal sweetbread
15. Bake everything together
16. Stir in a little Madeira or Sherry
17. Fill the warm pâtés with this mixture
18. Place the lids on top
19. Serve immediately
20. Allow 2 to 3 pâtés per person
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Smaa Sandkager (Small Sand Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound clarified butter
- 1 pound light, dried powdered sugar
- 1 pound and 6 tablespoons flour
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Clarify the butter and stir until stiff with 1 pound light, dried powdered sugar, 1 pound and 6 tablespoons flour, and 1 egg.
2. When too hard to stir, knead with hands until it releases from them, then roll with floured hands into small round cakes.
3. Flatten with a knife, place on a pan, and bake very light.
4. Makes 70 cakes.

title: Smaa Syltetøjskager (Small Jam Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg whites
- egg yolks
- 15 quintal finely ground powdered sugar
- 6 quintal fine flour
- paper for forms
- melted butter
- sugar and egg white for glazing
- jam for serving

## Directions

1. Whip 5 egg whites to stiff peaks and stir in the yolks
2. Add 15 quintal finely ground powdered sugar and 6 quintal fine flour
3. When well mixed, curl round pieces of paper at the edges, dip in melted butter
4. Place a small teaspoonful of batter in each paper cup
5. Insert into a moderately warm oven
6. When risen, remove from the paper and glaze with a mixture of sugar and egg white beaten together
7. Insert into a lukewarm oven to dry
8. Serve with jam
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Smaa Thekranse (Small Tea Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine flour
- ½ pound butter
- 1 cup cream
- a little yeast

## Directions

1. Knead together 1 pound fine flour, ½ pound butter, 1 cup cream, and a little yeast into a batter
2. Form small rings
3. Bake light brown
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Smaa Timbal'er med Hummer (Small Timbals with Lobster)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Fish forcemeat
- Butter
- Flour
- Fish stock made from fish bones
- Curry
- Lobster meat, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Butter the small timbal molds and line them with fish forcemeat
2. Make a roux with butter and flour and thin with fish stock made from fish bones, the meat being used for the forcemeat
3. Add curry to the sauce and finally the finely chopped lobster meat
4. Fill the molds with this stew, cover with forcemeat, and place a piece of buttered paper on top
5. Cook as recipe No. 51
6. Turn the molds out onto platters and pour curry sauce over them
7. Decorate with some of the remaining lobster meat
8. Serve with curry sauce or a lobster and crayfish sauce

title: Smaa Timbal'er med Vildt-Ragout (Small Timbals with Game Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Beef, veal, or game forcemeat
- Game ragout as recipe No. 11
- Game sauce with red currant jelly

## Directions

1. Butter the molds and line them with forcemeat made from beef, veal, or game
2. Fill them with game ragout as recipe No. 11
3. Treat and cook as described above
4. Serve with game sauce mixed with a little red currant jelly

title: Smaa Timbaler (Small Timbals)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Fish forcemeat
- Various stews mentioned previously

## Directions

1. Butter small cup molds and line them with fish forcemeat
2. Fill with various stews mentioned previously, covering them with forcemeat and paper
3. Place the molds in a hot oven in a baking pan, then fill the pan with boiling water
4. They will be ready in approximately ½ hour
5. Small timbals can be served as a course with sauce or as decoration around a larger timbal

title: Smaa Æggeboller (Small Egg Balls)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- 10 grams (2 dram) wheat flour

## Directions

1. Mash the 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
2. Stir with melted butter, a little salt and pepper, and wheat flour
3. Roll into small balls
4. Cover until ready to serve in brown or clear soup or on top of mock turtle

title: Smaa, sure Agurker (Small Sour Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers without black spots
- salt brine strong enough to float an egg
- soft water
- vinegar
- soda (natron)
- best quality vinegar
- small shallots
- white pepper
- horseradish, cut into squares
- small amount of sugar

## Directions

1. When choosing cucumbers, be careful that there are none with black spots among them, as these are always more or less bitter.
2. Brush the cucumbers clean, rinse them and place on a linen cloth so the water can drain from them.
3. They must then lie for 24 hours in a salt brine that is so strong it can float an egg. The water for this must be soft.
4. Place the cucumbers in the pickling pot with so much vinegar that it stands over them and cook for ¼ hour.
5. When you add a small amount of soda to the water, the color becomes stronger green without harm to health.
6. Remove the cucumbers and place in a jar.
7. Now boil best quality vinegar with some small shallots, white pepper, horseradish cut into squares and a tiny bit of sugar.
8. When it is well boiled, add the cucumbers, give them a brief boil, then place them in jars and pour the vinegar over them.
9. If you boil the cucumbers in a brass kettle, they will turn black.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Smaakager (Small Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound melted butter
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- grated lemon peel or vanilla
- 8 beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 pound melted butter, 1 pound powdered sugar, and 4 egg yolks with a little grated lemon peel or vanilla
2. Finally add the 8 beaten egg whites
3. Place on the sheet in small dollops using a teaspoon
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Smeltet Smør med Eddike (Melted Butter with Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons vinegar
- 4 tablespoons water
- ¼ kg (½ pound) cold butter

## Directions

1. Bring 4 tablespoons vinegar and 4 tablespoons water to a boil and remove from the heat.
2. Stir in ¼ kg cold butter while it stands by the heat, but it must not boil.

title: Små Agurker med raa Eddike (Small Cucumbers with Raw Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small cucumbers
- Strong salt brine
- White pepper
- Whole nutmeg
- Bay leaves
- Raw vinegar

## Directions

1. The cucumbers must be very small
2. Dry them and place in strong brine for 24 hours
3. Dry them again and place in a crock
4. For 100 small cucumbers, take 1 spoonful white pepper, a little whole nutmeg and 10 bay leaves
5. Cover everything with enough raw vinegar that it stands well over them
6. Tie them up immediately and they will keep well, though they will not be very green in appearance

title: Små fine Tvebakker (Small Fine Rusks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 175 gr powdered sugar
- 1/4 kg margarine
- 1 level teaspoonful ground cardamom
- 4 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- just under 1/4 L semi-skimmed milk
- 1 kg flour

## Directions

1. Mix the eggs well with the powdered sugar, margarine, ground cardamom, baking powder and salt.
2. Finally add the semi-skimmed milk and flour and knead into a dough.
3. Roll a piece of the dough into a long sausage shape and cut off pieces the size of a small walnut.
4. Roll into small, smooth balls, and if necessary dust lightly with flour, place on the sheet and bake pale brown in a well-heated oven.
5. When the balls have cooled, cut them through with a sharp knife and place again on the sheet with the soft, cut side upwards.
6. Put in a not-too-hot oven to dry out, brown and become crisp.
7. Yields about 1 1/4 kg rusks.

title: Små Kalvebrissel-Posteiер (Small Veal Sweetbread Pâtés)
body:
## Ingredients

- Morde dough
- 2 to 3 veal or lamb sweetbreads, blanched
- Ham
- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Spices
- 1 Gill red wine
- Egg yolk
- Meat stock

## Directions

1. Cut the sweetbreads into large cubes and mix with equal amounts of cubed ham and mushrooms
2. Sauté with butter over even heat for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring constantly
3. Add spices and wine, bring to a boil over brisk heat, then remove from heat
4. Line small fluted molds with dough
5. Lay a layer of forcemeat, then fill with the stewed mixture and cover with forcemeat and a lid of dough
6. Brush with egg yolk and bake in an evenly warm oven
7. When done, remove the lid, pour in a little sauce or stock, replace the lid, and serve

title: Små Postejer (Small Pastry Cases)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter Dough
- Egg
- Filling of choice

## Directions

1. Roll out butter dough to approximately ½ centimeter thickness.
2. Cut out rounds using a pastry cutter or glass at least 6 centimeters (2½ inches) in diameter.
3. Place half the rounds on a baking sheet.
4. Cut the other half in the center with a small glass to form rings.
5. Brush the bases with egg and place the rings on top.
6. Place the small lids beside them on the baking sheet.
7. Brush both the pastry cases and lids with egg, being careful not to let egg run down the sides as this will prevent them from rising.
8. Bake at high heat until light brown.
9. Carefully remove the risen butter dough from the center of each pastry case to make room for filling.
10. Fill with the same fillings used for Krustader, which should be hot before filling.
11. Alternatively, omit the lids and place a mushroom or olive on top to suit the filling.

title: Små Postejer, bagte i Forme (Small Pâtés Baked in Molds)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter pastry
- Peas for filling during baking
- Ragout filling (as previously mentioned)
- Egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Line small fluted or smooth molds with thinly rolled butter pastry
2. Fill with peas during baking to prevent collapse
3. Cut lids the size of the molds with a pastry wheel and bake them separately until light brown
4. When the pâtés are baked, drain the peas
5. Invert the mold bottom upward and release the pâtés easily from the molds
6. Fill with any of the previously mentioned ragouts
7. Place the lid on top and brush the pâté with egg
8. Return to the oven to dry and gain gloss
9. Arrange on a platter with a napkin
10. These pâtés can also be used for a cold buffet

title: Små Sandkager (Small Sand Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 3 pounds powdered sugar
- ¼ pound wheat flour
- 9 egg yolks
- 9 egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and remove the salt
2. When half cooled, stir in 3 pounds powdered sugar
3. Stir in ¼ pound wheat flour
4. Add 9 egg yolks
5. Finally fold in 9 whipped egg whites
6. Bake in small molds at fairly strong heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Små Vol au vent (Small Vol au Vent)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter Dough
- Egg
- Filling of choice

## Directions

1. Roll out butter dough to the same thickness as for small pastry cases.
2. Cut out rounds approximately 8 centimeters (3 inches) in diameter.
3. Brush with egg as described for pastry cases.
4. Use the same fillings as for pastry cases, though cut less finely.
5. Vol au vent do not have lids.

title: Smörgröd (Butter Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds butter
- 1 pound wheat flour
- almost 3 quarts boiling water
- salt

## Directions

1. Melt 3 pounds butter
2. Bake 1 pound wheat flour in it until it comes off the spoon easily
3. Gradually add almost 3 quarts boiling water, so the porridge blends well between each time it is stirred, and with vigorous stirring so it does not become lumpy
4. The porridge will be very thin after this amount of water, but it will thicken when served, which must happen as soon as it is cooked
5. Add salt last
6. Serves 8 to 9 persons

title: Smør med Eddike (Butter with Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- Vinegar
- Butter

## Directions

1. When the vinegar boils, add the butter
2. Remove from heat and stir until the butter is completely melted

title: Smør-Horn (Butter Horns)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g margarine
- 1/2 kg flour
- 1 tablespoon icing sugar
- 15 g yeast, mixed with 1 teaspoon salt
- just over 1/4 L half-skimmed milk
- egg for brushing
- sugar and chopped almonds for coating

## Directions

1. Crumble the margarine into the flour
2. Add the icing sugar, yeast mixed with salt, and milk
3. Mix everything together and let the dough rise
4. Roll out the dough to 1/4 inch thickness
5. Cut into small triangles
6. Roll up from the wide side, shape and curve like horns
7. Brush with egg and dip in sugar and chopped almonds
8. Bake at good heat for approximately 20 minutes

title: Smørgrød (Butter Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg butter
- 625 grams flour
- 4 liters boiling water
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Melt the butter
2. Roast the flour in the melted butter
3. Gradually add the boiling water, allowing the porridge to boil between each addition
4. Stir strongly to avoid lumps
5. Serve with butter, sugar, and cinnamon
6. Serve juice and water or sweet white beer on the side

title: Sne-Budding (Snow Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter (1 pot) heavy cream
- 5 sheets isinglass
- 1¼ dl (½ pægel) boiling water
- a little vanilla essence
- jam (preserved fruit)

## Directions

1. Whip the heavy cream into stiff foam.
2. Dissolve the isinglass in boiling water and stir into the cream foam along with a little vanilla essence.
3. Layer the whipped cream alternately with jam in a glass bowl or a mold from which the pudding can be turned out.
4. Make the same day it is to be eaten.

title: Sne-Budding (Snow Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Liter (1 Pot) cream
- 5 leaves isinglass
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water
- a little vanilla essence
- jam

## Directions

1. Whip 1 Liter (1 Pot) cream to stiff foam
2. Dissolve 5 leaves of isinglass in 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) boiling water and stir in along with a little vanilla essence
3. Layer the cream foam with jam in a glass bowl or in a mold from which the pudding can be turned out
4. Make on the same day it is to be served

title: Sneboller (Cream Puffs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 Gr (1/4 S) melted margarine
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 tt) flour
- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) boiling milk or water
- 8 eggs
- Palmin
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. In melted margarine, add 1/4 Kg (1/2 tt) flour, which is baked and thinned with 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) boiling milk or water.
2. When the dough has cooled, stir 8 eggs into it, after which it is dropped into small balls with a teaspoon or dessert spoon and placed in hot Palmin.
3. When the cream puffs have a nice brown colour and are cooked through, serve them as hot as possible, sprinkled with powdered sugar.
4. The dough can be flavoured with an essence.

title: Sneboller (Fried Cream Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 quintal butter
- 2 pails water
- 3/4 pound flour
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Bake the butter, water, and flour together and stir to combine
2. Mix in the eggs one at a time
3. Drop into boiling butter or fat as small round balls
4. Fry until light brown and serve hot
5. Sprinkle with powdered sugar before serving
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sneegsväling (Snow Egg Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- 20 ounces powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoon potato flour
- A few crushed almonds
- 6 pots sweet milk
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon flour
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- Salt to taste
- Ground cinnamon and powdered sugar for garnish

## Directions

1. Whisk 6 egg whites until foamy and blend in 20 ounces powdered sugar, 1 tablespoon potato flour, and a few crushed almonds.
2. Bring 6 pots of sweet milk to a boil over heat.
3. Using a spoon, drop balls of egg white mixture the size of an egg into the boiling milk, as many at once as will fit.
4. Let them cook for a few minutes, carefully turning them during cooking, then remove them.
5. Continue until all the egg white mixture is cooked into balls or snow eggs.
6. Mix the 6 remaining egg yolks, 1 teaspoon flour, and 1 tablespoon sugar until it resembles a paste.
7. Whisk this mixture into the boiling milk so it becomes smooth.
8. Add salt to taste, place the finished snow eggs in the milk, and serve the porridge with ground cinnamon and powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Snegle (Boiled Snails)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- snails in shells
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the snail shells well, boil them for several minutes in water with plenty of salt, until the snail easily pulls out with a larding needle or similar pointed object.
2. Remove the foot and the internal organs with a small knife.

title: Snegle (Boiled Snails)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Snail shells
- Salt
- Water
- 1 onion
- 1 bay leaf
- Thyme
- Black peppercorns
- Good bouillon

## Directions

1. Wash snail shells well
2. Boil for several minutes in salted water until snails can be easily pulled out with a larding needle or similar pointed object
3. Remove the mantle and viscera sacs with a small knife
4. Place snails in warm water and rinse in cold water
5. Press dry in a cloth
6. Place over heat in a little good bouillon
7. Cook together with snails 1 onion, 1 bay leaf, a small amount of thyme and a couple of whole black peppercorns for about 1 hour
8. Strain the broth from the cooked snails for use in further preparation

title: Snegle i Fiskefars (Snails in Fish Farce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked snails (from No. 572)
- Fish farce
- Water
- Salt
- Chopped mushrooms
- 1 grated onion
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Ground white pepper
- White wine
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. After the snails have undergone the cleaning and cooking described in No. 572, place them in fish farce, ensuring a little of the snail is visible above the smoothed farce
2. Place snail shells in a flat casserole with boiling salted water, farce side up
3. Cook for 10 minutes
4. Arrange on a napkin or on a dish covered with a layer of salt
5. Use the broth in which the snails were cooked to make a white, bound sauce
6. Before adding egg yolks, add some chopped mushrooms, 1 grated onion and 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley to the sauce
7. Season the sauce with salt, ground white pepper, white wine and lemon juice
8. Serve the sauce separately

title: Snegle-Kager (Snail Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) flour
- 375 gr (0.75 lb) margarine
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon hartshorn salt, dissolved in a little water

## Directions

1. Knead the flour, margarine, powdered sugar, eggs, and hartshorn salt solution together
2. Roll into very thin sticks
3. Twist into snail shapes, about the size of a two-crown coin
4. Place slightly apart on a baking sheet
5. Brush with egg and sprinkle with either chopped almonds or chopped candied citrus peel

title: Snegle-Ragout (Snail Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked snails
- Sauce (as in previous recipe)
- Chopped parsley
- Lemon
- Salad leaves

## Directions

1. Serve the cooked snails in a sauce similar to the previous dish
2. The sauce should be velvety and not too thin
3. Arrange on a flat dish, in gratin shells or in small casseroles
4. Sprinkle the ragout with chopped parsley, which is then omitted from the sauce
5. Garnish with lemon and salad leaves

title: Snittebonner (French Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- French beans
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Wheat flour
- Cream or milk
- Parsley
- Sugar
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Using the side of a knife, take hold of the stem and you can draw out a long thread all the way to the tip of the bean. This is called stringing the beans. Thereafter cut them on the bias, quite fine; cook them for a short time in water without salt, then press them dry in a sieve. They should not cook for more than just a short time, as they must not fall apart. Melt butter, stir in wheat flour and spread it with cream or milk and let it cook to a smooth sauce, after which the French beans get a brief cooking in this with a little chopped parsley, a piece of sugar, a little nutmeg and finally salt. A good spoonful of butter, half an ounce of wheat flour and a small pint of cream is approximately sufficient for a deep plate full of firmly pressed beans.

title: Snittebonner, Art. 1 (String Beans, Method 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- string beans
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place the beans in boiling water and cook without a lid until tender
2. Shake the water off them and place them again over heat with 1 ounce butter, 1 tablespoon chopped parsley, 1 teaspoon chopped onion and a little salt and pepper
3. Serve warm

title: Snittebonner, Art. 2 (String Beans, Method 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter beans
- water
- flour
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place the beans in boiling water and cook completely tender
2. Shake the water off them and let them have a brief cooking in a little flour and butter, salt and pepper

title: Snittebønner, hele (Whole String Beans)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- String beans
- Salt

## Directions

1. After drying, remove the seams from the back and front of the beans.
2. Pack the beans tightly on top of each other in a completely clean and dry bucket or in a stone jar, covered with a cloth.
3. Place a perforated bottom on top with a press.
4. Store in a dry place.
5. Cut the beans as needed for use.
6. Princesse beans are preserved in the same manner.

title: Snittebønner, snittede (Sliced String Beans)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- String beans
- Salt

## Directions

1. Choose beans that are not too coarse.
2. Dry, remove seams, and cut finely, placing them as cut in a bowl in layers with salt.
3. The next day, press down the layers and pack the beans tightly together in a stone jar, always with a little salt sprinkled between layers and over the top.
4. Place a piece of linen cloth on top, then a perforated wooden bottom fitting the size of the jar.
5. Finally, place a press on top and put the jar in a dry place.
6. Each time the jar is opened and beans are taken out, rinse the cloth in warm water and dry the inside of the jar.
7. It is common to cook the beans just as they are taken from the jar and rinsed in cold water, drying them after cooking if necessary.

title: Snittebønner, Stuvede og Saltede (Stewed and Salted String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- string beans
- water
- butter
- cream or milk
- flour
- parsley
- sugar
- pepper
- mace
- salt

## Directions

1. String the beans, in which the kernels should not be too large, and cut at slant angles into small fine strips
2. Cook in water until just tender
3. Pour into a sieve to drain the water
4. Make a white sauce from melted butter, cream or milk, flour, chopped parsley, a little sugar, pepper, mace and finally a little salt
5. Give the string beans a boil in this sauce and serve with it
6. To salt them: string the beans and place in a crock that has first been sprinkled with salt inside
7. Layer the beans with salt, either whole or usually cut in strips, and press down with a weight
8. When needed, rinse first in several changes of cold water or give a boil in boiling water which is then drained, then cook tender in fresh water and otherwise treat as fresh
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Snæpper (Roasted Woodcock)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock (2 for 3 people)
- Hearts, gizzards and livers of the woodcock
- Thin strips of bacon

## Directions

1. Pluck the woodcock, singe, remove the eyes, and pluck only the head.
2. When cleaning the birds, collect the hearts, gizzards and livers to prepare as a farce.
3. Dry the birds well inside with a cloth.
4. Cut off the feet and truss: press the thighs tight against the body and bend the head back, then insert the long pointed beak through the thigh and body and out again through the other thigh.
5. Lard or wrap the breast with thin strips of bacon.

title: Snæpper (Woodcock)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 woodcocks
- Fresh bacon slices
- Butter
- Red wine
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- French bread
- Heart, gizzard, and liver of the woodcocks

## Directions

1. Pluck the birds, singe them, remove the eyes, and only flay the head.
2. When the birds are cleaned, collect the hearts, gizzards, and livers to make a forcemeat.
3. Dry the birds thoroughly inside with a cloth, cut off the feet, and truss using their long, pointed beaks.
4. Press the thighs tightly against the body and bend the head back, then insert the beak through the thigh and body and out again through the other thigh.
5. Lard or wrap the breast with thin bacon slices.
6. Brown the birds and roast them like partridges, preferably in a pot and not much more than half an hour.
7. Serve garnished with woodcock bread made as follows: Finely chop the liver, gizzard, and heart with a little fresh bacon and parsley.
8. Sauté this in a slice of butter, then stir in a little red wine, a couple of egg yolks along with salt and pepper.
9. Spread this forcemeat on slices of French bread from which the crust has been removed and which have been fried in butter in a pan.
10. Place these in a well-heated oven so the forcemeat bakes quickly.
11. Serve with sauce as for pheasant.

title: Sobekaal (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- green, white, or red cabbage or spinach
- meat or beef
- flour
- butter
- meat balls
- potatoes (optional)
- sugar
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. First boil a piece of meat or beef, or a little of each
2. When this is tender and the broth strained, add the finely chopped cabbage with a little flour
3. When the cabbage is tender, add a binding of butter and flour together with meat balls
4. If desired, potatoes can be added, which are added at about the same time as the cabbage, or a little before
5. Previously cooked potatoes, either small or cut in pieces, browned in butter and sugar, are particularly suitable
6. For green and white cabbage: cook it in water with a little salt until it begins to soften, then squeeze out the water, chop it with flour and treat as before. This removes the bloating quality the cabbage contains without loss of flavor
7. The broth from smoked meat or fish can be used, but then the meat, after it has soaked half the time in water, must be carefully scraped, placed again in clean water and scraped again before boiling, and the fat must be skimmed off during cooking as the smoked taste is mostly there
8. Salt meat and pork must likewise be soaked before use

title: Soda-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams cold margarine
- 500 grams Pillsbury flour
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 250 grams fine sugar
- 375 milliliters semi-skimmed boiling milk
- 3 whole eggs, well whipped
- 65 grams chopped preserved orange peel or lemon drops
- 125 grams currants

## Directions

1. Rub cold margarine completely into Pillsbury flour together with baking soda and fine sugar
2. Add boiling semi-skimmed milk and whipped eggs
3. Add chopped preserved orange peel or lemon drops and currants
4. Grease form and bake for 1 hour

title: Sodakage (Soda Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 12 quintals butter
- 3 pounds powdered sugar or white farina
- a little finely ground bitter almonds
- 1 pound flour
- 3 quintals cream of tartar
- 15 quintals baking soda
- 2 pints cold sweet milk
- 4 egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg yolks white with 12 quintals butter and 3 pounds powdered sugar or white farina, a little finely ground bitter almonds and then with 1 pound flour which has first been mixed with 3 quintals cream of tartar and 15 quintals baking soda.
2. Gradually stir in 2 pints cold sweet milk and finally the 4 beaten egg whites.
3. Put the cake immediately in the oven and bake for 1 and a quarter hours at even heat.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sodſystede Meloner (Sweet-Pickled Melons)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small, not overly ripe musk melons
- One quart water
- Two tablespoons fine salt
- One quart water
- One pint good white vinegar
- One pound melons (prepared)
- One pound sugar
- One pint white wine

## Directions

1. Place the melons in one quart of water in which two tablespoons of fine salt is dissolved; let sit for 12 hours.
2. Transfer to a mixture of one quart water and one pint white vinegar; leave for 24 hours.
3. Remove, peel, and halve the melons; remove the pulpy parts.
4. Dry with fine cloth and cut into long pieces.
5. For 1 pound of melons, take 1 pound sugar and 1 pint white wine; cook sugar and wine together and skim well.
6. Add the cut melon and bring to a boil.
7. Pour into a glass and add the syrup while hot.
8. If it becomes thin after several days, cook it again and pour cold over the melons.

title: Soiré-Budding (Evening Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 375 grams (¾ pound) powdered sugar
- 1 liter (1 pot) boiling sweet milk
- 16 sheets isinglass
- 1¼ dl (½ pægel) water
- 3¾ dl (1½ pægel) Madeira wine
- ¼ dl (¼ pægel) cognac
- 125 grams (¼ pound) finely ground sweet almonds
- 125 grams (¼ pound) cleaned, scalded currants
- 3¾ dl (1½ pægel) heavy cream

## Directions

1. Stir egg yolks well with powdered sugar and pour into boiling sweet milk, whisking with a whisk while standing over heat until the cream is smooth but does not come to a boil.
2. Dissolve isinglass in water and add Madeira, cognac, ground almonds, and currants to it.
3. Pour this mixture into the cream, stirring until it is so stiff that it can bear the currants.
4. Then add whipped cream made from the heavy cream.
5. Pour the pudding into a wetted mold and preferably make it the day before.
6. Serve with cold fruit sauce.

title: Soja (Soy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- broth from previously prepared meat or pork
- 4 ox feet
- 1 pot water
- 3 quint ginger
- 3 quint pepper
- 3 quint allspice
- 1 pound peeled onions
- a little thyme and marjoram
- 3 pounds St. Croix sugar

## Directions

1. Use broth that has been used in winter for meat or pork. Boil it, skim it, and when clear, strain through a napkin.
2. Set 4 ox feet over heat and boil with 1 pot water, 3 quint ginger, 3 quint pepper, 3 quint allspice, 1 pound peeled onions and a little thyme and marjoram.
3. When the feet are tender, remove them, and the soup should then amount to about 1 pot.
4. Soften 3 pounds St. Croix sugar in water and boil until it becomes brown like caramel.
5. Dilute with 2 pots of the cooked broth and boil together with the soup from the ox feet for approximately 3 hours with 3 quint mace.
6. Then strain the mixture through a very fine sieve, pour into clean, dry bottles, cork them and keep for later use.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Solbær (Black Currants)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 gr powdered sugar
- 1/2 kg picked berries

## Directions

1. Pick the berries from the stems and if desired cut off the flowers
2. Weigh them, place on a colander to rinse well and then put in pot sprinkled with sugar
3. Cook the berries for 10 minutes, skim and remove on a colander
4. Reduce the juice until thick then add the berries to mix with it
5. Cool and seal immediately

title: Solbær-Gelé (Blackcurrant Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg blackcurrants
- 625 gr sugar
- 2.5 dl water per 0.5 kg berries
- 1.75 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice

## Directions

1. Rinse 0.5 kg blackcurrants and cook with 2.5 dl water until the berries are cooked apart, then pour into the jelly cloth.
2. When the juice has run through, weigh it.
3. Cook sugar and water (see No. 93), and add the juice at the end to cook for 10 minutes.
4. Skim well during cooking.
5. Pour the jelly into jars, which are sealed when it has begun to set.
6. The berries remaining in the cloth can be cooked with sugar and served with roast.

title: Solbær-Likør (Black Currant Liqueur)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 S) black currants
- 1 l (1 pot) white rum
- Ground icing sugar to taste, approximately 1/4 kg (1/2 S) per 1 l (1 pot) rum

## Directions

1. Place 1 kg (2 S) black currants in a jar and pour 1 l (1 pot) white rum over them.
2. Set the jar in the warmth for 4 to 5 weeks.
3. Strain the rum through a clear piece of cloth and sweeten with ground icing sugar to taste, approximately 1/4 kg (1/2 S) per 1 l (1 pot) rum.
4. Pour the black currant rum into dry bottles, cork them, and seal with gypsum.

title: Solbær-Suppe (Blackcurrant Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg blackcurrants
- 4.5 L water
- cloves
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- potato flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse blackcurrants and cook for half an hour with a few whole cloves, whole cinnamon and lemon peel
2. Strain the soup, sweeten, and when it boils again, thicken with potato flour mixed with water

title: Solbærrom (Blackcurrant Rum)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pails white rum
- 1 pound shelled blackcurrants
- 1 pound white sugar
- A little cinnamon
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Place the sugar in a kettle and pour enough water over it so it covers the sugar
2. Boil until it becomes like thin syrup and skim well during boiling
3. Add a little cinnamon and cloves along with the blackcurrants
4. When boiled, remove from the fire to cool
5. When cold, pour in the rum
6. Let the blackcurrant rum stand for 6 weeks in a well-corked jug and shake occasionally
7. After this time, strain and pour into bottles
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Solbærsaft (Blackcurrant Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 S) blackcurrants per batch
- 1/4 liter (1 Pægl) water per 1/2 kg blackcurrants
- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar Melis per 1 liter (1 Pot) juice

## Directions

1. Each 1/2 kg (1 S) blackcurrants is covered with 1/4 liter (1 Pægl) water and must boil completely through before pouring into the jelly cloth.
2. Measure the juice and boil it with the sugar for a quarter of an hour.
3. Skim it and pour it immediately into bottles, which are then bound.

title: Solbærsaft (Blackcurrant Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of blackcurrants
- 1 pot of water
- 0.75 measures of Cognac
- 1 pound of powdered sugar per pot of juice

## Directions

1. Cook the blackcurrants with the water and Cognac and skim well
2. After 1 hour the berries are cooked through, place them in a sieve
3. Press off the juice and cook the berries once more with two measures of water and mix this juice with the first
4. Cook the combined juice for half an hour with the sugar, skim, filter and pour into containers
5. The next day distribute the juice into bottles
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sorte Pølser (Black Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig blood
- Barley groats (3 measures of groats per pot of blood)
- Dried herbs (Basil, Marjoram and Thyme)
- Ground cloves
- Allspice
- 18 Quintals sugar
- 2 Pounds washed raisins or dried fruit
- Salt
- Ground cinnamon
- Finely chopped onion
- 4 Pounds pork lard cut into fairly large dice
- Intestines

## Directions

1. When the blood runs from the pig, stir it well so it does not solidify.
2. Then pour in medium-fine barley groats while stirring constantly, 3 measures of groats for each pot of blood.
3. Let the blood stand until the next day and then mix it with 2 tablespoons dried herbs (Basil, Marjoram and Thyme) that are rubbed to pieces between the hands and sifted, furthermore with 1 tablespoon ground cloves, the same amount of allspice, 18 Quintals sugar, 2 Pounds washed raisins or dried fruit, a little salt, ground cinnamon and finely chopped onion.
4. 4 Pounds of pork lard are cut into fairly large dice and mixed in with the rest.
5. The sausage mixture is then stuffed into well-cleaned intestines and boiled in water with a little salt in it.
6. They can be used both boiled and roasted on a grill or cut into slices and fried in a pan in butter, to which a little syrup can be added.
7. In the first two cases they are served with melted butter and white, ground sugar and cinnamon alongside, in the last the sauce from the pan is served with it.
8. Apple compote or apple porridge can also be served with black sausages.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sorte Ræddiker (Black Radishes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Black radishes
- Salt

## Directions

1. Peel and rinse the radishes
2. Cut into thin slices
3. Arrange on a plate and sprinkle with salt
4. Serve as part of a cold buffet

title: Souffle med Ananas (Pineapple Souffle)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 150 g (30 quintal) fine powdered sugar
- egg whites
- butter
- stewed pineapple, cut into very small pieces, without juice

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks until foamy with the fine powdered sugar.
2. Beat the egg whites to very stiff foam and carefully fold into the stirred egg mixture.
3. Butter a shallow baking dish that can withstand oven heat and pour in the egg mixture mixed with the stewed pineapple pieces.
4. Bake the omelet within 25 minutes. It must go straight from the oven to the table.
5. Arrange and sprinkle with powdered sugar.

title: Soufflé-Budding (Soufflé Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 4½ pounds sugar
- rind of 2 lemons
- juice of 2 lemons
- egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir together 10 egg yolks, 4½ pounds sugar and the grated rind of 2 lemons for approximately 1 hour.
2. Add the juice of the 2 lemons and the egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks.
3. Pour this mixture into a large, deep dish buttered well.
4. Place in a gently heated oven until it has risen.
5. Serve warm with a napkin around the dish and powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Soufflés (Cheese Soufflés)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams (1/4 pound) grated Parmesan cheese
- 50 grams (10 quintals) butter
- 50 grams (10 quintals) potato flour
- 4 eggs
- 3/4 deciliter (1/2 pint) milk

## Directions

1. Stir the flour and milk together and place in a saucepan along with the cold butter
2. Set over heat and bring to a boil with constant stirring until thickened
3. Lift from heat and stir in the cheese, egg yolks and a little salt
4. Let the dough cool
5. Only when ready to bake, fold in the beaten egg whites
6. Fill into paper capsules and bake in a hot oven approximately 1/4 hour
7. Arrange as quickly as possible in a pyramid on a folded napkin, placed on a platter

title: Sour Cherries (Syltede kirsebær)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pd. cleaned, ripe cherries
- 4 Pd. sugar
- 3/4 Pægl vinegar
- whole cinnamon
- whole cloves

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar with the vinegar, skimming the vinegar well during cooking
2. Add the cherries and boil until they are no longer sour at the pits
3. Remove the cherries and place in a colander so the liquid drains from them
4. Boil the syrup for 8 minutes until it is clear
5. Return the cherries to the syrup along with some whole cinnamon and boil together
6. Pour into preserve jars and add a little cognac to each
7. If the syrup becomes too thin after some time, boil it again with more sugar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sour Cherry Juice (Sur Kirsebærjaft)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover berries from a previous recipe
- Vinegar to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the berries with vinegar to taste for 8 to 10 minutes covered
2. Strain through a cloth
3. Cook the juice as you would the previous recipe

title: Sour Pears (Sure pærer)
body:
## Ingredients

- 23 Pd. Melis
- 5 Pægl vinegar
- 5 Pd. peeled, chopped gray pears

## Directions

1. Dissolve the Melis in vinegar
2. Add the chopped pears and boil for 3 hours at even heat, skimming them well
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Soya (Mushroom Condiment)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pickled mushroom trimmings
- Salt
- 1½ liters (1½ pots) mushroom liquid
- 10 chopped anchovies
- 1 teaspoon ground white pepper
- ¼ ounce ground cloves
- 2 bay leaves
- ½ kg (1 pound) darkly browned sugar
- ½ bottle red wine

## Directions

1. Rinse the mushroom trimmings well and chop finely.
2. Sprinkle with salt and let stand under a press for 3 to 4 days.
3. Strain the liquid through a cloth.
4. Take 10 chopped anchovies, 1 teaspoon ground white pepper, ¼ ounce ground cloves, 2 bay leaves, and ½ kg darkly browned sugar.
5. Cook all of this and skim it before pouring in ½ bottle of red wine.
6. Once it has boiled for at least a quarter hour after the wine is added, strain it and pour into dry bottles, which should be corked when cold.
7. This soya is very tasty in various brown, piquant sauces and in many meat dishes.

title: Soya (Soy Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 liters (3 pots) good meat broth
- water
- 3 teaspoons ground white pepper
- 1 teaspoon paprika
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 2 chopped red onions
- 3 bay leaves
- 1/4 kg (1 1/2 s) powdered sugar
- 1/2 bottle sherry
- potato starch

## Directions

1. Place 3 liters (3 pots) good meat broth over the fire and bring to a boil, skim well and strain through a fine cloth once it has finished skimming.
2. Dilute the broth with so much water that it becomes appropriately salty.
3. Return 3 L (3 pots) of this to the pot along with 3 teaspoons ground white pepper, 1 teaspoon paprika, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon cloves, 2 chopped red onions and 3 bay leaves.
4. Simmer the broth with these ingredients for at least 3 quarters of an hour and strain again.
5. While the soy sauce is cooking, brown 1/4 kg (1 1/2 s) powdered sugar in a pan and add it to the hot soy sauce to give it color. The sugar must be dark brown, but not burnt, as it can otherwise easily give a bad taste.
6. Finally add 1/2 bottle sherry.
7. If it is preferred to have the soy sauce smooth, stir potato starch in some of the wine and bring the soy sauce to a boil again.

title: Spanish Cherries (Yellow)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg cherries
- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 1/4 l water

## Directions

1. Either pit the cherries or preserve them as whole berries, of which only a little of the stems are cut off.
2. Boil sugar and water together to a thin syrup, add the cherries, boil and skim for 6 to 8 minutes, then remove them and place on a colander.
3. Pour the cherries into a jar after they have drained well, and set the juice over the fire to reduce until it is very thick, then pour it boiling over the fruit, which should be sealed as soon as possible.
4. A vanilla bean can be boiled in the juice to taste if desired.

title: Spanisk Sauce (Spanish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ounce smoked Ham
- 1 Ounce Fat
- 2 Ounces Carrot
- 2 Ounces Onion
- 2 Ounces dried Flour
- 1 Quart Bouillon or Water and 1/2 Pound finely chopped Meat
- 1 Teaspoon Salt
- Pepper
- 1 Bay leaf
- 1 Gill Wine

## Directions

1. Cut 1 Ounce smoked Ham into small pieces about 2 Inches in size and fry in 1 Ounce Fat.
2. As soon as it is brown, add 2 Ounces Carrot sliced and the same amount of Onion, likewise sliced.
3. Stir in 2 Ounces dried Flour and brown it.
4. Then add 1 Quart Bouillon; if you do not have it, then 1 Quart Water and 1/2 Pound finely chopped Meat.
5. Season with 1 Teaspoon Salt, a little Pepper and 1 Bay leaf.
6. Boil slowly for one Hour, and skim properly.
7. Then strain it and 1 Gill Wine may be added.
8. This Sauce can keep for over a Week in a cold Place and is used for all kinds of dark Meat, Game or fried Fish.

title: Spansk Vind (Spanish Wind)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- lemon zest, grated
- 6 tablespoons sugar
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Whip 4 egg whites to as stiff peaks as possible
2. Stir in grated lemon zest
3. Add 6 tablespoons sugar, one at a time
4. Place greased paper on the baking sheets and sprinkle with sugar
5. Set small cakes on this using a teaspoon, twice the size of a walnut
6. Space them a finger's width apart
7. Sprinkle with powdered sugar
8. Bake at even heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Spanske Kranse (Spanish Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 354 grams (1/4 pound) flour
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) margarine
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- Coarse sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Knead together the flour, margarine, and hard-boiled egg yolks
2. Form into rings
3. Dip them in coarse sugar
4. Place them not too close together on the baking sheet

title: Spare-Suppe (Economy Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- bones, tendons or trimmings of meat, already roasted or cooked
- salt
- 1 quart water for each round of bone and meat

## Directions

1. When you have bones, tendons or meat trimmings that have already been roasted or cooked but are cleanly handled, chop and cut them into small pieces
2. Cook them for a pair of hours with salt and 1 quart water to each round of bone and meat
3. This produces a soup that can be used for sauces, dishes and soups that do not need to be strong

title: Sparesuppe (Economical Stock)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Chopped raw, cooked or roasted beef, veal, lamb or pork bones
- Tendons
- Bacon scraps
- Meat scraps from raw, cooked and roasted meat
- Poultry carcasses
- Leftovers of white, smooth sauces
- Roll sausage and pork head soup and other soup leftovers
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place all bones, scraps, and leftovers in a pot with water and salt (if not already salted)
2. Add smoked items with caution as the flavor can become too overpowering and make the stock less versatile for all dishes
3. Use the same caution with herbs and spices, as their flavors do not always harmonize well with all dishes and the stock is better preserved without these additions
4. Roots and herbs should preferably only be added when the stock is to be used and the recipe requires it
5. When the stock is boiling and well-skimmed, it can be placed in a hay box for several hours, but in summer it should not sit overnight
6. Things that do not suit white, smooth soups can be used for brown stock instead

title: Spegepølse (Cured Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 Pounds lean pork
- 2 Pounds good beef
- 0.5 Pound salt
- 7 Quintals white pepper
- 4 Quintals saltpeter
- 5 Pounds bacon cut into fine dice
- Beef intestines

## Directions

1. Chop the lean pork, beef, salt, white pepper and saltpeter finely and knead until it becomes spongy.
2. Let it stand until the next day, then knead it again, after which the 5 Pounds of bacon cut into fine dice are kneaded in and mixed well with the rest of the mass.
3. Fill it into wide, well-cleaned beef intestines, stuff firmly, let it lie for 24 hours with salt, press together well again, tie it up and bring it to a boil.
4. In the country they are best preserved in malt in the malt box.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Spegepølser (Smoked Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds lean meat
- 6 pounds fresh beef from the inner loin
- 2 pounds salt
- 1½ ounces ground saltpeter
- ½ ounce ground pepper
- ½ ounce ground allspice
- ½ ounce ground cloves
- 3-4 pounds pork fat, cut into small cubes
- 5 bottles red wine
- Ox intestines

## Directions

1. Mince the lean meat and beef very finely
2. Place in a trough and knead with hands for ½ hour
3. Add 2 pounds salt, 1½ ounces ground saltpeter, ½ ounce ground pepper, ½ ounce ground allspice and ½ ounce ground cloves
4. Knead again for ½ hour and spread in the trough
5. Add 3-4 pounds pork fat cut into small cubes and pour in 5 bottles of red wine
6. Knead thoroughly
7. Use the widest and thickest ox intestines, cleaned and prepared like pork intestines
8. Sew one end closed and place in lukewarm water
9. Stuff very tightly so that no empty spaces remain
10. After stuffing, rub well with salt and keep for 24 hours
11. Then pack the sausage meat more tightly together and tie the end with twine to form a loop for hanging in smoke
12. Let lie another 24 hours, then dry off, wrap in thin paper and hang in smoke for 14 days
13. Then hang in a dry place
14. Do not make smoked sausages unless you are certain of having completely trichinosis-free meat

title: Spejl-Æg (Fried Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Eggs
- Stuffed oysters or mushrooms (optional)
- Sliced anchovies (optional)
- Green leaves for garnish (optional)

## Directions

1. Put butter in the holes of an omelet pan; when light brown, break an egg and slide gently into the butter
2. Fry only until the white is firm and the yolk is soft like poached eggs
3. Eggs can be placed on a small flat dish with stuffed oysters or mushrooms around each egg, or with sliced anchovies between the eggs
4. Can also be served alone
5. Alternatively, use a frying pan; when butter is light brown, break eggs gently side by side so they appear separate but connected like a cake
6. Garnish with green leaves around the edge

title: Spejlæg (Fried Eggs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Salt
- White pepper

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs out onto a plate so that the yolks are whole, rather than placing them on the pan one at a time
2. Place the frying pan over the heat with a little butter
3. When it boils, pour the eggs carefully onto it
4. As soon as the whites are set, they are done

title: Spejlæg (Fried Eggs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. To make the eggs lie more beautifully and become more evenly fried, it is best to first break them out onto a platter so that the yolks are whole, rather than placing them on the pan one at a time; for as often happens, the first ones are done before the last ones are even placed on. Place the frying pan over the heat with a little butter; when it boils pour the eggs carefully onto it, and as soon as the white is set, they are done.

title: Spejlæg (Fried Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Brown butter in a pan, and crack the eggs gently into it when the butter has stopped foaming.
2. They are fried until the white has become firm and can be arranged garnished with parsley.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Spejlæg med Champignons (Fried Eggs with Mushrooms)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried eggs
- Mushrooms
- Strong gravy
- Madeira
- Maggi bouillon cubes

## Directions

1. Give some mushrooms a boil in a strong gravy
2. Add a little Madeira and Maggi bouillon cubes
3. Pour the gravy around the fried eggs

title: Spejlæg med Madeira-Sky (Fried Eggs with Madeira Gravy)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried eggs
- Strong gravy
- Madeira
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Sago flour

## Directions

1. Make a strong, clear brown gravy
2. Add a glass of Madeira
3. The gravy can be made from Maggi bouillon cubes and thickened slightly with sago flour
4. Serve the eggs in a ring around the plate and pour the gravy in the center

title: Spejlæg med Skinke (Fried Eggs with Ham)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried eggs
- Cooked ham
- Butter
- Watercress or finely sliced leeks
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut cooked ham into slices
2. Fry on the pan in butter and arrange on a warm plate
3. Place one fried egg on each slice of ham
4. Watercress or finely sliced leeks are much appreciated with this dish; sprinkle lightly over the eggs

title: Spejlæg med Tomat-Sauce (Fried Eggs with Tomato Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried eggs
- Butter
- Flour
- Beef broth
- White pepper
- Sherry
- Tomato puree

## Directions

1. Arrange the fried eggs on a warm plate
2. Pour over a tomato sauce made of butter and flour, thinned with beef broth
3. Add white pepper, a glass of sherry, and tomato puree to taste

title: Spejlæg med Trøfler (Fried Eggs with Truffles)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fried eggs
- Truffles
- Strong gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the truffles into slices and give them a boil in strong gravy
2. Pour the gravy around the eggs

title: Spettet Kalvekjød (Larded Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin
- Pork fat for larding
- Butter
- Water or meat stock
- Cream (optional)
- Cold butter (optional)
- Ginger and pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. Take the finest loin veal and cut pieces about 13 inches long, two fingers thick and somewhat wider than the thickness.
2. Beat the pieces gently so they do not fall apart and lard them well with pork fat so that only one side remains bare.
3. Brown butter in a flat pot and brown the pieces on all the sides that have fat until the fat is cooked through.
4. Turn the bare side down and when it is brown, add a little water or meat stock and cook on this side until cooked through.
5. They are best when not too well cooked but still somewhat juicy.
6. When removing from the pot, stir a little cream sauce; alternatively, dip the pieces in ginger and pepper before larding, in which case use only a piece of cold butter to thicken the sauce instead of cream.
7. These can be served with vegetables like a carbonade.

title: Spinat (Spinach)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Salt
- A little baking soda
- Cream, milk or soup
- Butter
- Flour
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the spinach and remove the coarse stalks, rinse in several changes of water
2. Place in boiling water with salt and a little baking soda and cook quickly
3. Drain on a colander and rinse with cold water, pressing well to remove moisture
4. Spinach can be chopped very fine or pounded in a mortar
5. Make a sauce with butter and a little flour, thin with cream, milk or soup
6. Add the spinach and bring to a boil, season with sugar, salt and cold butter
7. Alternatively, chop the spinach with a spoonful of flour, place in a pot, bring to a boil and add cream, milk or soup plus butter, sugar and salt
8. Or chop raw spinach using a meat grinder and stir into a sauce as described above, then cook and reduce well
9. Arrange in a serving dish with a ring of sliced hard-boiled eggs around it

title: Spinat (Spinach)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh-picked spinach
- Water
- Salt
- Soda
- Cold brine
- Sugar
- Butter

## Directions

1. Rinse the spinach and cook in very little water with salt and soda for 3 to 4 minutes
2. Pack this cooked mass in canning jars, tapping them lightly against the table
3. If there is not enough liquid with the spinach, i.e. it does not cover the spinach in the jars, then add a little cold brine with sugar addition
4. Close the jars, cook for 30 minutes and cool
5. The spinach is stewed when it is to be used
6. It can also, after being cooked, be poured over with cold water, pressed well and chopped finely, then set over heat to reduce until the liquid with it is gone
7. Pack the spinach in jars or crocks and seal carefully with butter
8. The butter is used in the preparation of the dishes for which the spinach is used

title: Spinat (Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- a little flour
- nutmeg
- cream, milk or broth
- butter
- salt
- poached or hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the spinach and cook it until soft
2. Press the water out through a sieve and chop it fine with a little flour
3. Place it in the pot in which it will be stewed, with a little nutmeg, mix well and thin gradually with cream, milk or broth until it becomes like a thin porridge
4. Cook it briefly and finally add butter and salt
5. Serve with poached or hard-boiled eggs around it

title: Spinat med Smør (Spinach with Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked, chopped spinach
- Cold butter
- Salt
- A little sugar

## Directions

1. Place the cooked, chopped spinach in a pot and bring to a boil so that the water attached to it can evaporate.
2. Stir in cold butter, salt, and a little sugar.
3. Serve the spinach immediately.

title: Spinat med Smør (Spinach with Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked, chopped spinach
- Butter

## Directions

1. Place the cooked, chopped spinach in a pot and bring to a boil

title: Spinat, Stuvet (Stewed Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- New Zealand spinach
- water
- salt
- flour
- nutmeg
- sugar
- cream or milk
- butter

## Directions

1. So-called New Zealand spinach should be preferred as the best
2. After the spinach is picked and rinsed, cook it until tender in salted water
3. Lay in a sieve until the water has drained off
4. Chop it very fine with a little flour and stew with a little nutmeg and sugar in cream or milk
5. Finally add a little butter and salt
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Spinat-Suppe (Spinach Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts spinach
- Water for cooking
- Salt
- 2 quarts milk
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- A little white pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil the spinach in sufficient salted water.
2. When it is completely soft, so that it can easily be pressed, drain the water through a strainer; chop the spinach finely and rub it through a sieve with a spoon.
3. Boil 2 quarts of milk, stir the spinach into it, and when it is near boiling, thicken it with one tablespoon of cornstarch, mixed in cold milk.
4. Season with a little teaspoon of salt, a little white pepper and nutmeg.
5. It must be served as soon as it boils up.

title: Sponge Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 2¾ cups granulated sugar
- 1 cup boiling water
- 3 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Separate the egg yolks and whites
2. Stir 2¾ cups granulated sugar into the yolks
3. Add 1 cup boiling water, then 3 cups flour and 1 teaspoon baking powder sifted with the flour
4. Fold in the beaten egg whites last

title: Spouge Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 cups sifted flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 6 egg yolks with 2 cups sugar, then with 2 cups sifted flour with 2 teaspoons baking powder in it
2. Then add 1 teaspoon vanilla and the beaten whites

title: Sprængt Gaas (Pickled Goose)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned goose with head, neck, wings, and legs removed
- 2 to 3 large handfuls of salt
- 1 good teaspoon of ground saltpeter
- 1 tablespoon of sugar
- water for boiling
- vegetables for serving

## Directions

1. Rub the goose well inside and outside with salt mixed with ground saltpeter and sugar.
2. Set the goose in a cool place for 2 to 3 days.
3. Rinse the goose when ready to cook.
4. Place in boiling water and cook for approximately 2 hours.
5. Serve with vegetables. Use the broth for peas or cabbage.

title: Sprængt Gaas (Pickled Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose
- Salt
- Saltpeter
- Powdered sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. When the goose is cleaned, rub it inside and outside with a quarter pound of salt mixed with 1 tablespoon of saltpeter and 1 tablespoon of powdered sugar, then place it in a cool place for several days.
2. When ready to use, rinse it and boil in plentiful water for approximately 1.5 hours.
3. Serve sprinkled with salt and with beet roots or Brussels sprouts. The broth is used for mixing with other soups, such as giblet soup, split pea soup or similar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sprængt Lammebryst (Salted Lamb Breast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast or brisket
- salt
- 1 teaspoon saltpeter
- 2 teaspoons sugar

## Directions

1. Break the ribs in a couple of places, and rub the meat with salt.
2. Rub with 1 teaspoon of saltpeter and 2 teaspoons of sugar, and place on a flat dish to be regularly turned in the brine that draws from the meat.
3. Let it stand thus for 3-4 days.
4. Rinse the brine well from the meat before placing it in the pot in boiling water and cooking until tender.
5. Serve with stewed vegetables. The broth from the meat can be used for a smooth soup or purée.

title: Steg med kold Sauce (Roast with Cold Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Roast of any kind
- Butter
- Stock
- Cream
- Gelatine (if needed)

## Directions

1. Cut roast into pieces and arrange neatly on a flat dish
2. For the sauce, brown butter in a pan, stir in strong stock, and when it has boiled a little, remove from heat
3. Stir in enough good cream to make the sauce light
4. Return to heat and simmer, stirring constantly
5. Season and when smooth, continue stirring until nearly cold, then pour over the roast
6. If the sauce is not smooth, it can be stirred with a very little gelatine
7. Cut strong aspic or stiff stock into strips and arrange in strips around the roast
8. The aspic can also be melted and poured, nearly cold, over everything
9. Alternatively, strong stock can be melted and stirred with good cream until nearly cold and beginning to become smooth, then poured over diced roast

title: Stege Sauce (Roast Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Soy sauce or Sky
- Cold butter
- Sour or sweet cream or buttermilk (for beef or veal roast)

## Directions

1. Heat the pan before adding butter
2. When the butter is brown, stir in some soy sauce or Sky
3. Remove the pan from heat and stir in a piece of cold butter strongly until smooth
4. For beef or veal roast, sour or sweet cream or buttermilk can be used instead of cold butter
5. Remove the pan from heat while stirring in the cream, then place on heat again for a small boil while stirring constantly

title: Stegt Agerhøne (Roasted Partridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridge (1 bird for 2 portions)
- Twine
- Linen cloth
- Bacon, either for larding or in slices
- Butter
- Salt
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Currant jelly
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean the partridge as usual; do not cut off the head, but fasten it with twine to the legs.
2. Do not wash it, but dry it well with a linen cloth.
3. Either lard it, wrap it with bacon slices, or brown it first in a casserole with butter on both sides of the breast.
4. Then place it on its back in the pan, sprinkle with a little salt, and roast tender in about half an hour.
5. The less liquid under the partridges, the better.
6. Make the sauce by boiling down the pan drippings with bouillon, thicken it with a little flour, and stir it up with bouillon.
7. Serve with currant jelly and browned potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Agge-Plante (Fried Eggplant)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggplant
- Salt
- Water
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or fat

## Directions

1. Cut the plant into slices at least 1 inch thick; clean each piece and place it in salt and water, laying a plate on top to keep them submerged; let them stand for an hour or more. Dry each slice thoroughly, dip it in beaten eggs and breadcrumbs and fry in butter or fat until they are completely fried and brown.

title: Stegt And (Roasted Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck
- Butter
- Pepper
- Salt
- Apples
- Prunes
- Giblets
- Liver
- Wheat flour
- Bouillon
- Duck gravy
- Roasted potatoes
- Red cabbage

## Directions

1. Pluck the duck, singe and remove the entrails. Cut off the head and feet, rinse and rub inside with pepper and salt.
2. Brown butter in the pan and roast the duck at very even heat for approximately 1 good hour until it is a beautiful light brown.
3. Baste with the sauce from time to time, which is thinned with bouillon or gravy if necessary.
4. Like the goose, it can be filled with apples and prunes, and in that case place giblets and liver inside the duck, otherwise place them under the wings but they will roast too quickly then.
5. The duck should not be roasted too much; it is roasted enough when the leg easily loosens from the side.
6. Brown the sauce with flour, thin with duck gravy and strain. Serve with roasted potatoes and red cabbage.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt And i Sky (Roasted Duck in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- bouillon or gravy
- coloring
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- a glass of sherry
- tarragon vinegar
- isinglass
- egg whites
- eggshells
- cooked apples
- currant jelly
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Roast and portion the duck when cold according to the instructions for aspic
2. Skim the fat carefully from the gravy, which is mixed with good bouillon or gravy and boiled together
3. Give it a nice brown color with coloring and add Maggi bouillon cubes if needed
4. While it boils, add a glass of sherry along with a little tarragon vinegar and isinglass (12 leaves to 1 L)
5. Clarify with egg whites and eggshells
6. Pour a thin layer of gravy into the bottom of the mold, and when it is set, place the meat in it and cover with the rest of the gravy
7. The next day turn it out of the mold
8. Decorate the dish with a ring of cooked apples cut in 2 pieces; place a little currant jelly on each piece and garnish with lettuce leaves
9. If you have decorative skewers, arrange it in the middle of the mold by running it through a hard-boiled egg and binding it with lettuce leaves

title: Stegt Bederyg (Roast Venison Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison loin
- Pepper
- Ginger
- Salt
- Blood
- Vinegar
- Cold milk
- White writing paper
- Butter

## Directions

1. The venison loin must hang for several days before roasting; remove the internal fat and kidneys, then rub with ground pepper, ginger and salt.
2. Alternatively, soak it in cold blood mixed with vinegar and salt for several days. For each pound of meat, reckon 2 measures of blood and half a measure of vinegar and a small tablespoon of salt.
3. The blood should cover the loin, and all impurities that collect on the surface should be skimmed off daily.
