4. When you want to use the loin, remove it, rinse with cold, skimmed milk and soak in it for an hour.
5. When roasting, cover the loin with a piece of white writing paper smeared with butter; baste and roast as usual.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Dyrebov (Roasted Venison Haunch)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison haunch
- Lard strips
- Margarine or fat

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the haunch.
2. Lard the haunch well with fine strips of lard.
3. Cover with margarine or fat.
4. Roast and serve as with venison leg.

title: Stegt Dyrekølle (Roasted Venison Leg)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison leg from fallow deer
- Lard strips
- Margarine or fat
- Potatoes
- Currant jelly
- Apple sauce or apple compote

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the leg.
2. Lard densely with fine lard strips and cover with margarine or fat.
3. Roast and baste as with venison back, allowing 2 to 3 hours of roasting depending on size.
4. Serve with browned potatoes, currant jelly, apple sauce or apple compote.
5. Make the sauce as ordinary game sauce.

title: Stegt Dyrelever (Roasted Venison Liver)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison liver
- Salt and pepper
- Flour
- Butter
- Onion
- Game stock or soup

## Directions

1. Soak the liver.
2. Remove the skin and cut the liver into thin slices.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Dip in flour and fry in butter on the pan.
5. Slice onion, brown it in butter, and brown some flour for the sauce.
6. Dilute with game stock or other good soup or stock.
7. Pour the sauce over the liver at serving.
8. Serve with cooked, peeled potatoes.

title: Stegt Egge-Plante (Fried Eggplant)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- salt
- water
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Cut the eggplant into slices at least 1/4 inch thick
2. Clean each piece and place it in salt and water, laying a plate on top to keep them under the liquid
3. Let them stand an hour or more
4. Dry each slice, dip it in whisked eggs and breadcrumbs and fry in butter or fat until thoroughly fried and brown

title: Stegt Farseret Hvidkaal (Fried Stuffed White Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cabbage head
- forcemeat
- butter
- broth or water
- coloring
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Stuff the head as described in recipe 221
2. Brown the head in butter in a pot
3. Add broth or water, a little coloring, salt, and liquid as typically used for a pot roast
4. Turn the head once during cooking under a closed lid for 3.5 to 4 hours
5. Make the sauce from browned butter and flour, thinned with the gravy
6. Can also be roasted in the oven

title: Stegt Fasan (Roasted Pheasant)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 male pheasants
- Fresh bacon for larding
- Butter
- Flour
- Cream
- Milk
- Game stock or soup
- Salt
- Sugar
- Coloring
- Beef extract or Maggi bouillon cubes
- Bread for base
- Tinfoil and white paper for decoration
- Celery or herb salad

## Directions

1. Choose the male pheasant for its beautiful plumage
2. Hang the pheasant for several days before preparation as its meat is naturally dry
3. Pluck as other poultry, but keep the head, neck, wings, and tail to use as decoration around the roasted bird; remove these parts after plucking
4. Singe, remove innards, and dry both inside and outside with a cloth
5. Prepare as directed for poultry
6. Lard the breast and thighs with fine strips of fresh bacon
7. Brown in butter in a pot along with the giblets and heart
8. Make a sauce from browned butter and flour, diluted with cream, milk, and game stock or soup, seasoned with salt, a little sugar, and coloring, and pour over the bird so it is half covered
9. Cook gently over low heat for about 1 hour, turning the bird frequently and basting regularly
10. Strain the sauce and add a little cold butter, a few spoonfuls of raw cream, and if needed, beef extract or Maggi bouillon cubes
11. Prepare a bread base carved from rye or wheat bread with the same circumference as the bird and about 5 centimeters high; hollow slightly in the center where the bird will rest and cover with tinfoil and white paper cut into fringe around the edge
12. Insert a decorative skewer through the beak and neck and fix in the base
13. Attach the wings and tail in their respective places in the same manner
14. Slice the pheasant breast thinly and arrange on the base in its original form
15. Cover the pheasant with a spoonful of sauce and garnish with celery or herb salad

title: Stegt Fasan (Roasted Pheasant)
body:
## Ingredients

- pheasant
- butter
- salt
- bouillon
- white wine

## Directions

1. Pluck the pheasant with great care, singe over burning spirits, carefully remove the innards, and dry inside but do not wash.
2. Do not cut off the head but leave the feathers on it as decoration and wrap it with paper during roasting.
3. Usually place the tail feathers on the platter as well.
4. Rub the pheasant with butter before roasting, sprinkle a little salt on it, baste with butter, and toward the end with a little bouillon and white wine.
5. Roast for half an hour to three quarters of an hour over a clear but not too strong fire, being careful not to overcook it.
6. Do not lard or cover with bacon as this harms the flavor.
7. The pheasant must have hung for some time before roasting to develop the proper game flavor.
8. Serve on the platter with the breast up, with the head and neck positioned using a steel wire.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Flæsk med Bøf-Sauce (Fried Pork with Beef Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg (3 lb) striped side pork, mildly salted
- Onion slices
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Soup or broth
- Ground pepper
- Coloring (kulør)
- Potato puree or boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. The pork should preferably be mildly salted striped side pork, which is cut into thin slices with the rind removed.
2. Fry the slices on a hot pan until they are brown, then turn them and place them on the serving dish.
3. Pour the fat from the pan so that only the salty sediment remains, and rinse the pan before pouring some of the fat back to brown the onion slices in.
4. When the onions are brown and crispy, add 1 tablespoon of flour to the pan and dilute this with soup or broth and add ground pepper and coloring.
5. The sauce can be poured over the pork or served separately.
6. Serve with potato puree or boiled potatoes.
7. If the pork is very salty, the slices can be boiled in water in the pan before frying.

title: Stegt Flæsk med Fløde-Sauce (Fried Pork with Cream Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Striped pork
- Boiled potatoes
- Onion sauce or parsley sauce

## Directions

1. Fry the pork as instructed above and serve with boiled potatoes and either onion sauce or parsley sauce.

title: Stegt Gaas (Roasted Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose
- Apples
- Prunes
- Salt
- Pepper
- Little water
- Wheat flour
- Bouillon
- Potatoes
- Red cabbage

## Directions

1. Pluck the goose, singe over burning spirits, rinse and dry well.
2. Chop off the neck, wings and feet, which are used with giblets, liver and heart for giblet soup.
3. Open the goose and remove the entrails. Wash well and rub inside with salt and pepper, then fill with peeled and chopped apples and rinsed prunes, and sew together at both the neck and the rear.
4. Roast with very little water for 2 to 3 hours in a warm oven without basting until tender.
5. When serving, remove the thread. Skim the fat off the sauce, thicken with flour and bouillon and serve with the roast along with potatoes and red cabbage.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Gaas i Sky (Roasted Goose in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- aspic ingredients as for duck
- glazed onions
- parsley or lettuce

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as for duck in aspic
2. The dish can be garnished with glazed onions and parsley or lettuce

title: Stegt Gaas à l'anglaise (Roast Goose English Style)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose
- 6 fairly large onions
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the 6 onions into dice and brown them in a pan with butter, salt, and pepper.
2. Fill the cleaned and prepared goose with this hot onion mixture.
3. Roast the goose as an ordinary roast goose.
4. Make the sauce from butter, flour, and the pan drippings from the roast.
5. Serve with boiled potatoes.

title: Stegt Gaas, fyldt med Kastanier (Roast Goose Filled with Chestnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose
- about 750 grams chestnuts, shelled
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- water
- olives

## Directions

1. After cleaning the goose and rubbing the inside with salt and pepper, fill it with the 750 grams of chestnuts, whose shells have been removed.
2. Scald the chestnuts so that the thin brown skin can be removed.
3. Sew the goose closed and roast it.
4. Serve with ordinary brown pan sauce, to which olives can be added.
5. Serve the chestnuts with the roast.

title: Stegt Gedde (Fried Pike)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pike
- Lard strips
- Gravy
- Potato flour
- Sherry
- Paprika
- Chutney
- Oyster or lobster sauce

## Directions

1. The pike can be fried whole or split, but if split the backbone is removed
2. After cutting a crosswise slit at the nape, the skin is pulled off
3. After the pike is well rinsed, lard it thickly with fine, fresh lard strips and place it in a hot oven
4. Baste frequently with good gravy during roasting, otherwise it will easily become dry
5. When fried, strain the gravy, skim it, and thicken it with potato flour mixed in Sherry
6. Season the sauce further with paprika and chutney, which are very piquant with fried fish
7. Oyster or lobster sauce can also be used

title: Stegt Hare (Roasted Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare
- Lard
- Butter
- Boiling water
- Cream
- Wheat flour
- Milk
- Roasted potatoes
- Redcurrant jelly

## Directions

1. Hang the hare by the hind legs with head downward in a dry, cool place for several days before roasting.
2. Skin the hare when ready to use, remove all membranes, and cut off the head. Cut off the front legs but keep them for roasting. Cut away enough from the breast and ribs so the hare lies flat on the pan.
3. Wash the hare well and rinse the inside with boiling water using a teapot, holding the hare by the hind legs so the water drains immediately.
4. Lard densely with lard, including the front legs. Place on the pan, cover with butter and roast at even good heat with constant basting of the sauce, and when almost tender, also with cream.
5. Roasting takes 1 to 2 hours depending on size. When tender and removed from the pan, whisk the sauce with a little wheat flour, thin with cream, and immediately before serving stir in a piece of cold butter.
6. Serve with roasted potatoes and redcurrant jelly. If the hare has hung too long, after cleaning let it soak for 24 hours in milk.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt hel Gris (Roasted Whole Pig)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pig aged 4 to 6 weeks
- ground pepper
- salt
- butter or bacon fat
- bouillon
- apples
- prunes
- flour
- cold butter

## Directions

1. Wash, scald and scrape the pig.
2. Remove the eyes and insert a wooden spoon in their place.
3. Open the mouth slightly and keep it open during roasting with a stick.
4. Rub the inside with ground pepper and salt, the outside with salt.
5. Bend the legs under the pig.
6. Fill it with wooden spoons and chopped apples.
7. Sew it together.
8. Make the oven very hot.
9. When the pig has roasted about a quarter hour or has begun to heat, rub the skin well with butter or preferably with bacon fat or fat bacon rind.
10. Put a little bouillon in the bottom of the pan.
11. Roast for approximately 14 hours, regularly basting and rubbing the skin as described.
12. At serving, place a lemon in the mouth after removing the stick.
13. Brown the pan sauce with flour and strain it; finally stir a little cold butter into it, and serve this sauce with the pig, which is placed whole on the table after removing the thread.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Hjerpe (Roasted Ptarmigan)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ptarmigans, cleaned
- Salt
- Fine bacon
- Butter
- Flour
- Sweet cream
- Currant jelly
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. After cleaning the ptarmigans, scald them.
2. Pull the skin from the neck and breast downward and wrap it around the legs.
3. Set them up so the breast is high.
4. Lard with fine bacon and place them side by side, breast up.
5. Sprinkle with a little salt and roast in butter over gentle heat for about a quarter hour.
6. When roasted, remove them and brown the sauce with a little flour.
7. Thin it with sweet cream and strain it; finally stir in a piece of cold butter.
8. Serve with currant jelly and browned potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Hvidling (Fried Whiting)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Hvidling (Whiting)
- Salt
- Mel (Flour)
- Æg (Egg)
- Brød (Bread)
- Smør or Palmin (Butter or Margarine)
- brunt Smør (Brown butter)
- kogte Kartofler (Boiled potatoes)
- Salat or Persille (Salad or Parsley)
- Citronskiver (Lemon slices)

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and remove the head and fins.
2. Make a few cuts on the sides.
3. Rinse well and sprinkle with salt.
4. Before frying, dry thoroughly in a fish cloth.
5. Dip in flour, then in egg, then in bread.
6. Fry in butter or Palmin on a pan.
7. Serve with brown butter and boiled potatoes.
8. Garnish with salad or parsley and lemon slices.

title: Stegt Kalkun (Roasted Turkey)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- salt
- butter or margarine
- pan drippings or broth or salted water
- butter
- flour
- Sagomel (sago flour)

## Directions

1. The male turkey is best for roasting.
2. Clean and truss the turkey, lard the breast and thighs, sprinkle with salt and cover with butter or margarine.
3. Place breast-side up in the roasting pan and roast approximately 2.5 hours in the oven, but must be basted frequently with either pan drippings, broth or salted water.
4. If the breast begins to brown too much, cover it with paper spread with butter.
5. The sauce can be made from butter and flour, diluted with the pan drippings, or the drippings can simply be thickened with Sagomel, but for this it must be more reduced to be sufficiently strong.

title: Stegt Kalkun (Roasted Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- salt
- pepper
- liver
- gizzard
- heart
- bread crumbs
- eggs
- currants
- sugar
- butter
- anchovies
- lemons
- flour
- bouillon
- cream
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Slaughter the turkey 3 to 4 days before use.
2. Pluck, singe, and cut up the turkey.
3. Remove the innards and wash thoroughly.
4. Blanch in boiling water for 2 minutes, then wash in cold water.
5. Rub inside with salt and pepper.
6. Place the cleaned liver under one wing and the cleaned, prepared gizzard under the other, unless they are to be used for stuffing.
7. Tie the legs together over the rump.
8. For bread stuffing: remove the crust from an old French bread, soak in sweet milk, squeeze out the milk, let stand for one hour, then mix with 3 eggs, 2 tablespoons washed currants, salt, pepper, sugar, half a quarter pound of softened butter, and finely chopped liver.
9. For alternative stuffing: scrape the liver, gizzard, and heart, chop 4 anchovies, 2 lemons, and a French bread soaked in water, add 1 tablespoon flour, pepper, salt, and 2 eggs.
10. Lard the turkey with 3 fine strips on the breast and a star on the thigh, or cover with white writing paper buttered during roasting.
11. Roast for 1.5 to 2 hours depending on size in light, browned butter with frequent basting, being careful not to burn it.
12. Skim the fat from the sauce and thicken with flour, thin with bouillon, strain, and stir in a little cream.
13. Arrange the turkey with the breast up after removing the strings.
14. Serve with browned potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Kalkun i Sky (Roasted Turkey in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- aspic ingredients as for duck
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Prepare the turkey with aspic to which mushrooms have been added and given a boil
2. Place the mushrooms on the dish around the aspic

title: Stegt Kalvelever og Hjerte (Fried Calf's Liver and Heart)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh calf's liver
- Calf's heart
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Browned butter
- Onions
- Stock (Sky)
- Smoked bacon (optional)

## Directions

1. Place fresh calf's liver in cold water for a quarter hour, then rinse and remove the membrane.
2. Cut the liver into appropriate pieces, about the size of cutlets, and rinse these slices in several batches of cold water.
3. Beat the slices lightly on both sides with a meat mallet, sprinkle with salt, pepper and flour, and place in a pan with browned butter, turning and frying until cooked through.
4. Remove from the pan, and in the remaining butter, brown some chopped onions, then pour the sauce over the liver.
5. If necessary, thin the sauce with stock.
6. Instead of beating the liver, it can be hacked slightly with the back of a knife.
7. The liver gains in flavor when some slices of smoked bacon are fried alongside it.
8. Clean the heart and cut into slices like the liver, then fry it with the liver.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Kalvelever og Hjerte (Fried Calf's Liver and Heart)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh calf liver
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- butter
- onions
- stock

## Directions

1. Place the fresh calf liver in cold water for a quarter hour, then rinse and remove the membrane
2. Cut into slices of suitable size, approximately the size of cutlets, and rinse these slices in several changes of cold water
3. Beat lightly on both sides with a mallet, sprinkle with salt, pepper and flour, and place in a pan with browned butter
4. Turn and fry until completely cooked through
5. Remove from the pan and brown some chopped onions in the remaining butter, then pour this sauce over the liver
6. If necessary, thin the sauce with stock
7. The liver can also be chopped finely instead of being beaten
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Kanin (Roasted Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit
- Basilicum
- Thyme
- Lard
- Vinegar
- Sauce

## Directions

1. After the rabbit is skinned, cleaned and washed like a hare, place it for 24 hours in a cool place under a tightly fitting lid in a deep basin with basilicum and thyme.
2. Lard densely as the meat is dry, and roast as a hare (Nr. 159), but pour a little vinegar over it during roasting and baste very frequently.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Lammehoved (Roast Lamb's Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb's heads
- Salt
- Water
- Eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Butter
- Browned butter or parsley sauce
- Salad
- Vegetables

## Directions

1. After the heads are split and the brain removed, clean them, rinse and place in cold water for an hour.
2. Then boil them in water with fairly abundant salt until the skin can be removed, then clean them carefully.
3. Cut off the ears and remove the eyes, but do not remove the fat in the eye sockets behind the eye.
4. Split the tongues and fry them alongside the heads.
5. Brush these with beaten eggs and sprinkle with ground bread mixed with ground pepper, salt and finely chopped parsley.
6. Fry them a light golden brown in butter in a pan.
7. Serve lamb's heads either with browned butter or with parsley sauce, and serve salad and other vegetables alongside.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Pattegris (Roasted Suckling Pig)
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig, at least 6 weeks old
- Lard
- Cracker
- Wheat bread or flour
- Water
- Butter
- Pepper
- Thyme
- Marjoram
- Sage
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Select a pig at least 6 weeks old and have the butcher clean it completely inside and out.
2. Slit the skin crosswise over the back from head to tail; bend the knees and sew the udders together at the tips behind the head.
3. Rub it with lard all over and place a cracker in its mouth.
4. Make a stuffing from fresh wheat bread (without the crust) or flour (salted as for bread dough) and stir into a stiff dough with boiling water; when this is cooked, break it up and add 1 pound butter, a little pepper, thyme, marjoram and sage.
5. Fill the pig with this dough and sew it shut.
6. Place it on its knees in a roasting pan with enough water to baste it continuously until done.
7. Turn it during roasting as you would a turkey.
8. Make a sauce from the hot fat in the pan (strained through a fine sieve) and thicken with flour; add boiling water if needed, and stir in a tablespoon of finely chopped parsley.
9. Roasting takes about two hours, more or less.
10. Some use finely chopped lungs, liver and heart, and a pair of eggs in the dough before making the stuffing.

title: Stegt Rype (Roasted Willow Ptarmigan)
body:
## Ingredients

- Willow ptarmigans
- Butter
- Salt
- Ginger, crushed
- Cream, thin and thick, sour
- Currant jelly
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Roast as you would ptarmigans, but do not flay them and place them in the pan breast up.
2. When nicely browned, turn them.
3. Thin the sauce with a little boiling, thin cream in which a little salt and crushed ginger have been added.
4. Roast them tender under a lid and baste regularly with thick, sour cream.
5. Stir the sauce up with thick cream.
6. Serve with currant jelly and browned potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Skinke (Roasted Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Cloves
- Bay leaf
- Breadcrumbs
- Root vegetables (carrots and white roots)

## Directions

1. Test the ham as follows: run a thin-bladed knife close to the bone; if the smell you notice is sweet, the ham is good.
2. Soak it in cold water for twenty-four hours, changing the water once.
3. Scrape it well and remove all sinews.
4. Place it in enough cold water to cover it, with a lid in which 6 cloves and a bay leaf are stuck.
5. Boil for 4 hours.
6. Remove it, take off the skin, spread sifted breadcrumbs over it, and brown it in the oven.
7. It can be eaten either cold or warm; if to be served warm, it is often accompanied by sauce and vegetables arranged around it as follows: cut some large carrots and white roots into olive shapes and other forms.
8. Boil them in salted boiling water, then place in cold water until needed.
9. Heat them in the sauce and arrange around in small groups, pouring a little sauce over them.

title: Stegt Stenbider (Fried Catfish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- catfish
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- Palmin
- browned butter
- tomato slices
- cucumber salad

## Directions

1. Skin fish and cut from backbone into smaller pieces
2. Dip in eggs and breadcrumbs
3. Fry in Palmin
4. Serve with browned butter and garnish with tomato slices topped with cucumber salad

title: Stegt Svine-Mørbrad (Roasted Pork Tenderloin)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tenderloin
- salt
- ground pepper
- meat drippings
- wine
- boiled peeled potatoes
- stewed vegetables

## Directions

1. Roast the tenderloin whole; cut away the fat and membrane.
2. Sprinkle with salt mixed with a little ground pepper.
3. Roast finished under constant basting with drippings.
4. Serve with the drippings in which it was roasted, to which a little wine may be added, along with boiled, peeled potatoes and stewed vegetables.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegt Tjur og Røj (Roasted Capercaillie and Hen)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Capercaillie and hen (1 pair for 10 to 12 people)
- Lard
- Game sauce
- Cold butter
- Raw cream
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Lingonberry or currant jelly

## Directions

1. Thaw overnight in the kitchen if frozen.
2. Pluck and clean the birds and soak in milk for a few hours.
3. Lard and brown the birds, then roast like pheasant for about 2 hours.
4. Serve with the game sauce in which they were roasted, which can be improved with cold butter, raw cream, and Maggi bouillon cubes.
5. Carve the breast into thin slices and reassemble.
6. Serve with lingonberry or currant jelly.

title: Stegt, Farseret Kalvebryst (Roasted Stuffed Veal Breast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast
- Forcemeat of veal or pork
- Butter
- Broth or water
- Culinary coloring
- Salt
- Parsley and puff pastry for garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal breast the same way as for boiled stuffed veal breast.
2. Brown in butter and roast either in the oven or in a pot, basting with broth or water in which culinary coloring and salt have been added.
3. When serving, remove the thread and slice the meat, then reassemble the slices and garnish with parsley and puff pastry strips, or serve in a puff pastry border.
4. Make a sauce from the pan drippings as for veal roast, and serve with vegetables.

title: Stegte Aal (Fried Eel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- Salt
- Flour
- Egg or egg white
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or claret
- Lemon juice
- Parsley
- Boiled potatoes or potato purée

## Directions

1. After the eels are skinned, cut them into 8 centimeter (3 inch) long pieces
2. Rinse the pieces and sprinkle them with salt, let them stand for one hour, and then dry them in a fish cloth
3. Turn the pieces first in flour, then dip them in egg or just in egg white, and finally in breadcrumbs
4. Fry them in a pan in browned butter or claret
5. Serve with browned butter mixed with lemon juice and boiled potatoes or potato purée
6. Garnish the dish with parsley and lemon slices

title: Stegte Aal (Fried Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt
- flour
- eggs
- fine breadcrumbs
- butter
- boiled peeled potatoes
- frying sauce

## Directions

1. After the eel is slaughtered, skin it as follows: with a sharp knife, make an incision under the head and loosen the skin. Bind a thin, strong twine just under the head and hang up the eel. With a rough cloth, take the skin and pull it off.
2. Rub the skinned eel with salt, and cut off the fins with scissors, then remove the innards that are not used.
3. Rub the fish with salt and treat it as mentioned in the introduction, after first cutting it into pieces about 2 inches long and discarding the head.
4. After it has soaked for a couple of hours in water and the pieces are rinsed, dry them, roll in flour, then in beaten eggs, and then in fine sifted breadcrumbs.
5. Fry in a pan with very little butter and serve with boiled peeled potatoes and frying sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegte Agerhøns (Roasted Partridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 young partridges
- Fresh bacon for larding
- Butter
- Flour
- Cream
- Milk
- Game stock or soup
- Salt
- Sugar
- Coloring
- Potatoes for browning
- Salad or parsley for garnish
- Currant, lingonberry, or rhubarb jelly

## Directions

1. Pluck or skin the young partridges, which are identifiable by their yellow legs; remove and dry them thoroughly inside with a cloth
2. Lard the breasts with fine strips of fresh bacon
3. Brown the partridges in a pot and cover with a sauce made as for pheasant
4. Cook over low heat for about 1 hour
5. Strain the sauce and stir in a little cold butter
6. Split the partridges and arrange them again on a hot dish
7. Garnish with browned potatoes and decorate with salad or parsley
8. Serve with currant, lingonberry, or rhubarb jelly

title: Stegte Brokfugle, Pommerantsfugle, Regnspover, Brushøns eller Viber (Roasted Plovers, Dotterels, Ringed Plovers, Grouse, or Lapwings)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young birds (plovers, dotterels, ringed plovers, grouse, or lapwings)
- Cream
- Potatoes
- Currant jelly

## Directions

1. Handle and roast them as you would woodcock, but do not use the entrails and bend the head under the wing.
2. During roasting, baste them with cream.
3. Serve with browned potatoes and currant jelly.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegte Duer (Roasted Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- meat stuffing
- bacon slices
- cream

## Directions

1. Fill the pigeons with fine meat stuffing (recipe 75).
2. Cover with bacon slices on the breast.
3. Roast as chickens are roasted.
4. During roasting, baste with cream, and also stir cream into the sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegte Duer (Roasted Squabs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs
- Salt
- Butter
- Soup or water
- Coloring
- Heart, liver, and gizzard
- Salad or parsley
- Compote and salad for serving
- Butter
- Flour
- Strained gravy
- Raw cream

## Directions

1. Pluck the squabs, clean, and dress like other poultry
2. Lard the breast with 2 rows of lardoons
3. Brown the squabs in butter in a pot
4. Sprinkle with salt and pour in a little soup or water and coloring
5. Cook the heart, liver, and gizzard with the squabs
6. Roast for a good half hour, basting well during cooking
7. Split and arrange garnished with salad or parsley
8. Serve with compote and salad
9. For the sauce, brown butter and flour, dilute with the strained gravy, and add a little raw cream

title: Stegte Duer i Sky (Roasted Pigeons in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- aspic with isinglass
- small pickled pearl onions
- cooked salted tongue
- parsley or lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. When the pigeons are roasted, portion them in 2 or 4 pieces
2. Cover them with the aspic, mixed with isinglass (see roasted duck in aspic recipe), in which small pickled pearl onions have been cooked
3. On the dish where the mold is turned out, arrange a ring of cooked salted tongue
4. Between the tongue pieces, insert parsley or lettuce leaves

title: Stegte Høns (Roasted Chicken)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large chicken
- butter
- gravy, stock, or water
- salt
- coloring
- sago flour

## Directions

1. Select preferably capons or young roosters. After cleaning, dry them in a cloth and lard the breast with 2-3 rows of fat.
2. Brown a good slice of butter in a pot, in which the meat is browned without becoming tough or dry.
3. Pour in only a little gravy, stock, or water with salt and coloring.
4. Roast gently under a lid and must be frequently basted.
5. Strain the gravy and reduce it until smooth, or improve it with Maggi bouillon cubes and thicken with sago flour.
6. Alternatively, serve a smooth gravy sauce to which butter and flour are added, diluted with the gravy and mixed with a little raw cream, along with compote, salad, and browned potatoes.

title: Stegte Høns à l'anglaise (Roasted Chicken à l'anglaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- cooked, salted beef tongue
- cooked vegetables (peas, asparagus, cauliflower, carrots, beans)
- sago flour

## Directions

1. Rub the cleaned and well-dried chickens inside with pepper and salt.
2. Place a walnut-sized piece of butter inside each chicken.
3. Lard the breast and thighs and roast as instructed in recipe 931.
4. When serving, cut the chickens into 4-6 pieces depending on size and arrange in strips on the platter, alternating with strips of sliced, warm, cooked, salted beef tongue.
5. Garnish around with various cooked vegetables such as peas, asparagus, cauliflower, carrots, beans, or only some of these.
6. Purée of vegetables can also be served with this.
7. Reduce the gravy to make a sauce and thicken with sago flour.

title: Stegte islandske Ryper (Roasted Icelandic Ptarmigan)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Icelandic ptarmigan
- 1¼ dl (½ pint) vinegar
- 1 l (1 pot) water
- Milk
- Sauce (see Pheasant, No. 963)

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the ptarmigan and soak for 4 to 5 hours in a mixture of 1¼ dl vinegar to 1 l cold water.
2. Rinse well and soak in milk overnight.
3. Lard and brown the ptarmigan, then braise in a pot and drip with a previously made sauce (see Pheasant, No. 963).
4. Roast over gentle heat for approximately 1 hour, which will result in excellent flavour.

title: Stegte Jærper (Roasted Capercaillie)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 capercaillie
- Butter
- Claret
- Flour
- Game stock or soup
- Cream
- Coloring
- Potatoes for browning
- Apples, halved and cooked
- Currant or gooseberry jelly
- Apple sauce

## Directions

1. Skin the capercaillie, remove innards, and rinse well
2. If frozen, soak them in milk for a few hours before larding
3. Lard them and brown in butter or claret in a pot
4. During roasting, which lasts approximately 1 hour, baste frequently with game stock, soup, or cream
5. Strain the gravy and use it to make a thin sauce with the browned butter and flour, stirring in a little cold butter and coloring to achieve a nice golden-brown color
6. Alternatively, roast them as pheasants in a pre-prepared sauce, stirring in only a little cold butter at service
7. Serve the breast, which is split lengthwise, as the back is rarely served at dinner
8. Garnish with browned potatoes and with a wreath of cooked apple halves filled with currant or gooseberry jelly
9. Apple sauce may be served with the capercaillie

title: Stegte Karusser (Fried Crucian Carp)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small crucian carp
- Salt
- Flour
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Browned butter or Palmin

## Directions

1. Scrape off the scales and clean the fish thoroughly, then rinse.
2. Sprinkle with salt.
3. Dry the fish in a cloth before frying.
4. Dip in flour, egg, and breadcrumbs.
5. Fry in browned butter or Palmin.
6. Serve with browned butter and lemon, or English fish sauce, accompanied by boiled potatoes.

title: Stegte Kramsfugle og Smaafugle (Roasted Thrushes and Small Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrushes or small birds
- Bacon slices, thin
- Butter
- Salt
- Bouillon or cream

## Directions

1. Pluck the birds and remove the entrails.
2. Cover them with thin slices of bacon and roast them in butter that has first been browned in a casserole.
3. After adding a little salt, roast them over brisk heat for about 10 minutes, basting and dripping with bouillon or cream.
4. Stir the sauce up with sweet cream and a piece of butter and strain it.
5. Smaller birds are roasted in a similar manner.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegte Kyllinger i Sky (Roasted Chickens in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted chickens
- aspic
- herb salad

## Directions

1. Portion roasted chickens in 4 pieces and place in a ring mold
2. Pour the prepared aspic over them
3. When the mold is turned out onto the dish, place an herb salad in the middle

title: Stegte Kyllinger, Kapuner og Høns (Roasted Chickens, Capons, and Hens)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens or capons or hens
- parsley leaves
- butter
- salt
- bouillon or water
- cream

## Directions

1. After the birds are plucked, singed, and the innards removed and feet cut off, wash and dry them.
2. Fill with picked parsley leaves and cold butter.
3. Brown butter lightly in a casserole and place the birds breast-side down.
4. When they have roasted somewhat and are golden brown, turn them, sprinkle a little salt on them, and pour a couple of spoonfuls of warm bouillon or boiling water over them.
5. Continue roasting for approximately another half hour, total roasting time about one hour.
6. Thicken the sauce with sweet cream and strain.
7. Serve with potatoes and cucumber salad.
8. For capons and young hens: lard with bacon or cover with bacon slices and roast over high, clear heat with constant basting with butter.
9. Skim the fat from the sauce, thicken with a little flour, thin with bouillon, and strain.
10. Garnish the platter with salad leaves and curly parsley.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegte Lammehoveder (Fried Lamb Heads)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb heads (or mutton heads if from young sheep)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Beaten egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or lard
- Brown butter
- Salad or other vegetables
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Although lamb heads are best, mutton heads can be used if from young animals.
2. Scald, pluck, and clean them, remove the brains, wash well and soak for an hour in cold water to let the blood drain out.
3. Put them in boiling salted water and cook until the skin can be removed.
4. Pick them carefully, cut off the ears and sinews, remove the palate and split the tongue and fry it along with the heads if desired.
5. Fry the heads in butter or lard after dipping them in beaten egg and rolling in ground breadcrumbs mixed with salt and pepper.
6. Fry them light brown and serve with brown butter and salad or other vegetables and potatoes.

title: Stegte norske Ryper (Roasted Norwegian Ptarmigan)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Norwegian ptarmigan
- Browned potatoes
- Currant or lingonberry jelly

## Directions

1. Pluck and lard the ptarmigan and prepare as for grouse.
2. Divide the ptarmigan into 2 parts.
3. Serve with browned potatoes and currant or lingonberry jelly.

title: Stegte Rødspætte-Fileter (Fried Plaice Fillets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- plaice fillets
- salt
- flour
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- Palmin or lard

## Directions

1. Let cut fillets stand for about an hour sprinkled with salt
2. Dry in fish cloth
3. Dip first in flour, then in egg, and finally in finely sifted, pounded breadcrumbs
4. Fry in Palmin or lard, or fry in pan until fish is crispy and has a nice brown color
5. Serve immediately
6. Alternatively, place fillets in a frying basket and lower into boiling fat

title: Stegte Rødspætter (Fried Plaice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- plaice
- flour
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- lemon
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut plaice into pieces as desired - crosswise into 3-4 pieces, lengthwise into 2 halves, or cut out as fillets
2. Treat and fry as for fried plaice fillets
3. Serve with browned butter, lemon and boiled potatoes, or with vegetables such as spinach, peas, asparagus or stewed carrots, or with browned butter and potato salad, or with browned butter, chutney sauce and boiled rice

title: Stegte Sild (Fried Herring)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- flour
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- Palmin
- browned butter
- vinegar
- lemon
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean herring
2. Make several cuts on the sides with a knife
3. Dry in fish cloth
4. Dip in flour, eggs and breadcrumbs
5. Fry in butter or Palmin
6. Serve with browned butter with vinegar or lemon and boiled potatoes

title: Stegte Snepper eller Bekkasiner (Roasted Woodcock or Snipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock or snipe
- Alcohol for singing
- Forcemeat (fars)
- Peppercorn
- Bacon slices, thin
- Butter
- Cream
- Bread, finely ground (Tvebak)
- Pepper
- Salt
- White bread, toasted

## Directions

1. Pluck the birds and singe them over burning alcohol.
2. Remove the windpipe, but keep the head attached to the bird.
3. Flay the skin with feathers from the head.
4. Scald the feet and cut off the outermost joint with the toes.
5. Bend the feet in under the thigh and tie them with twine.
6. Fill the eye sockets with forcemeat containing a peppercorn.
7. Bend the head so the beak passes through both thighs, which are then bent well upward.
8. Wrap the birds with thin bacon slices.
9. Brown butter in a casserole and roast the birds in it with frequent basting until tender, about a quarter hour.
10. Remove them and stir the roasting sauce with cream and a piece of butter; serve with the snipe.
11. Remove the twine.
12. Mince the entrails and liver (but not the stomach) very fine and brown them with butter, a little finely ground bread, and a very little pepper and salt in a casserole for a few minutes.
13. Spread this on thinly toasted white bread, which is placed on the platter around the snipe.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegte Svaner, Edderfugle, Vildgæs og Vildænder (Roasted Swans, Eiders, Wild Geese, and Wild Ducks)
body:
## Ingredients

- Swans, eiders, wild geese, or wild ducks
- Butter
- Cream
- Potatoes
- Currant jelly
- Milk

## Directions

1. These birds have a more or less fishy taste. Those with a broad, flat bill are the best, especially young ducks raised in fresh water.
2. Young ducks need not be flayed; before roasting, they may be boiled for 1 hour.
3. More fishy species must, after plucking and singeing, be flayed, then have the head, feet, and tips of the wings cut off.
4. Then drain and wash them well in cold water and place them in milk for several hours.
5. Lard them and roast for about an hour until tender in butter and cream.
6. Sharpen the sauce with a little cream.
7. Truly only the breast is worth serving.
8. Serve with browned potatoes and currant jelly.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegte Tjurer, Røier eller Urhøns (Roasted Grouse, Black Grouse, or Capercaillie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young birds, cleaned and rinsed
- Butter
- Salt
- Bouillon
- Cream
- Currant jelly
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. After removing the entrails, clean and rinse the birds.
2. Lard the birds and place them in a pan in which butter has been browned with a little salt.
3. During roasting, place them breast-down and baste with bouillon and cream.
4. Turn them from time to time.
5. When done, stir a piece of butter into the sauce, skim it, and strain it.
6. Serve with currant jelly and browned potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stegte Vildeænder (Roasted Wild Ducks)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild ducks
- Thin slices of bacon
- Sauce
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare wild ducks like other fowl; do not stuff them
2. Bind thin slices of bacon over the breast and place in the oven
3. Calculate about 15 minutes roasting time for each pound the fowl weighs
4. Serve with sauce and lemon slices
5. Note: The book recommends they be fully roasted as half-roasted wild duck is considered unpalatable

title: Stegte Vildænder (Roasted Wild Ducks)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild ducks
- Thin slices of bacon
- Sauce
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the wild ducks as you would other fowl.
2. Do not stuff them, but bind thin slices of bacon over the breast.
3. Place in the oven and roast for approximately 15 minutes per pound of weight.
4. Serve with sauce and lemon slices.

title: Stegte Østers (Fried Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large oysters
- Clean cloth
- Butter
- Beaten egg
- Bread crumbs or crackers
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Use the largest and best oysters.
2. Place them on a clean cloth and press another cloth over them to remove all moisture.
3. Melt enough butter in a frying pan to nearly cover the oysters.
4. Dip the oysters first in beaten egg and then roll them in bread crumbs or crushed crackers.
5. Fry them light brown.
6. Serve dry on a hot dish with lemon slices around them.

title: Stenbider (Lumpfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- lumpfish
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- peppercorns
- bay leaves
- cream and horseradish sauce or sour sauce
- flour
- light brown butter
- butter
- flour
- soup or water
- finely chopped onion
- white liver
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. To cook: skin the fish, rinse it, and dry it in a clean cloth
2. Place it to cook with water, salt, a little vinegar, some peppercorns, and a couple of bay leaves
3. Serve with cream and horseradish sauce or sour sauce
4. To fry: flour the fish as usual and fry it in light brown butter
5. For the sauce: bake a good spoonful of butter and a spoonful of flour together over the heat and thin with soup or water
6. Add finely chopped onion, the white liver (blanched), and lemon slices, cook well together, then strain the sauce
7. Slice the fried fish thinly and lay it in this sauce, which is then brought to a boil
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stenbider kogt (Boiled Catfish)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- catfish
- salt
- water
- hollandaise sauce
- pickles
- mustard

## Directions

1. Skin the fish
2. Remove entrails and cut from backbone into 2 fillets
3. Let stand sprinkled with salt for 1/2 hour
4. Place in cold salted water
5. Serve covered with a broken hollandaise sauce in which a little finely cut pickles preserved in mustard can be stirred

title: Stewed White Beans (Stuvede, hvide Bønner)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans cooked as in recipe 590
- butter
- flour
- cooking broth from the beans
- 2 egg yolks per 0.5 kg beans
- salt
- ground white pepper
- lemon juice
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the beans in broth as in recipe 590
2. When tender, drain the broth and reserve it
3. Brown the butter and flour together and thin with the reserved cooking broth
4. Thicken the sauce with 2 egg yolks per 0.5 kg of beans
5. Season the sauce with salt, ground white pepper, lemon juice, and chopped parsley
6. Turn the beans in the sauce to coat

title: Stikkelsber-Grod (Gooseberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Gooseberries
- Water
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Corn-Starch (if needed)

## Directions

1. If the berries are unripe and very sour, they should stand a while and infuse in hot water, which is then strained off and fresh water placed on the fire.
2. For 2 quarts berries take 3 quarts water.
3. The berries must be cooked well out and often stirred from the bottom, after which the jelly is made with sugar and cinnamon.
4. Light powdered sugar can well be used for gooseberry jelly, but white sugar gives finer taste and prettier appearance.
5. Alternatively, the berries can be cooked without letting them extract first; more water is taken for the same portion of berries, and as it thereby becomes too thin, it is smoothed with a couple of spoonfuls of corn-starch dissolved in cold water.
6. This saves both berries and sugar.
7. A third way is to take more berries and not let them cook quite to jelly, but strain them when it is like a purée.
8. The juice is then placed over again, made with sugar and cinnamon, and smoothed with corn-starch.
9. Gooseberries kept in bottles can also be used for jelly, but always need a thickening of corn-starch as they lose the jelly-like quality by being kept.

title: Stikkelsber-Grod til Steg (Gooseberry Jelly for Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- unripe Gooseberries
- a little Water
- washed Butter
- whole Cinnamon
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Take unripe berries, but do not scald them first.
2. When the stems and seed-cases are removed, place them on the fire with very little water and a piece of washed butter the size of a walnut for 1 quart berries.
3. They should only be cooked more, but not broken.
4. A few pieces of whole cinnamon are cooked with the berries, and the sugar is added when the jelly is cooked, but it should not be very sweet.

title: Stikkelsber-Suppe (Gooseberry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Quart Gooseberries
- 2 Quart Water
- 3 Egg yolks
- white Sugar
- whole Cinnamon
- Bread

## Directions

1. This can be made from both unripe and ripe gooseberries. It is best made with eggs.
2. Place 1 quart gooseberries over the fire in 2 quarts water; they need not be picked.
3. Meanwhile whisk 3 egg yolks in the tureen with much white sugar.
4. When the berries are cooked, strain the soup and place it on again with a little whole cinnamon and sugar, as what is in the eggs will scarcely be sufficient to diminish the berries' sourness.
5. When it has cooked a little, whisk in the eggs and serve with bread.

title: Stikkelsberkage (Gooseberry Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart unripe gooseberries
- 6 egg yolks
- cinnamon
- powdered sugar
- old wheat bread
- 2 small cups of grated bread
- 3 tablespoons butter
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Pick, rinse, and place unripe gooseberries in the oven until they turn yellow, or alternatively simmer in water and strain through a colander until dry
2. Stir 6 egg yolks well with a little ground cinnamon and powdered sugar to taste
3. Grate old wheat bread and use 2 small cups full, baking it in 3 tablespoons butter until slightly browned
4. Stir the bread mixture into the eggs along with the gooseberries and whipped egg whites
5. Bake for one hour
6. Serves 8 persons

title: Stikkelsbær-Gelee (Gooseberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart unripe good gooseberries
- 1 pint water
- 4 quarts berries (to yield 1 quart juice)
- 2 pounds loaf sugar
- 1 pint water

## Directions

1. Cook 1 quart unripe good gooseberries in 1 pint water until they burst
2. Pour into a flannel jelly bag that has been dipped in hot water and wrung out in a dry cloth while still warm
3. Let the bag hang quietly until the juice has run off, which may take until the next day
4. For 1 quart juice (typically obtained from 4 quarts berries), take 2 pounds loaf sugar and cook it into syrup with 1 pint water
5. Add the juice to the syrup and cook until stiff when tested on a cup
6. If the juice is not very thick, you may add the sugar to it and let it cook until stiff when tested
7. The spent berries can be used for gooseberry soup

title: Stikkelsbær-Gelé (Gooseberry Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg green unripe gooseberries
- 625 gr sugar
- 1.75 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice
- 2.5 dl water per 0.5 kg berries

## Directions

1. For jelly use the large green unripe berries, the so-called glass berries.
2. The berries, which do not need to be picked over, are rinsed and placed over heat with 2.5 dl water for each 0.5 kg and cooked until they burst.
3. Pour them into the jelly cloth and leave thus until the next day; then weigh the strained juice.
4. Cook the specified amount of sugar and water as described in No. 93, and only when it is cooked completely dry, pour in the juice and cook for 10 minutes, while skimming well at the same time.
5. Even if the jelly should be less firm in the first days after it is preserved, it matters nothing; seal it and it will then be firm after a short time.
6. If the gooseberries remaining in the cloth are to be used for Russian marmalade, they must be rubbed through a coarse sieve or through a food mill.

title: Stikkelsbær-Gelé (Gooseberry Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 liters (3 pots) green gooseberries
- 1 L (1 pot) water
- 625 g (1 1/4 S) powdered sugar
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) water
- 20 sheets isinglass

## Directions

1. Hull, rinse, and cook the gooseberries with 1 L (1 pot) water and pass through a jelly bag.
2. Cook 625 g (1 1/4 S) powdered sugar and 1/2 L (1/2 pot) water to a syrup, and melt 20 sheets of isinglass in it.
3. Pour the isinglass mixture into the juice.
4. Serve and arrange as Rhubarb Jelly.

title: Stikkelsbær-Grød (Gooseberry Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg gooseberries
- ½ kg sugar
- ¼ L water
- Sago flour
- Wine or water for thinning
- Vanilla essence (optional)
- Fresh cream or crème for serving

## Directions

1. For unsieved pudding: melt the sugar in water
2. Cook the picked and rinsed berries in this liquid until they begin to fall apart
3. Stir in a little sago flour thickening, thinned in wine or water
4. The pudding will then come to a boil
5. Adjust sweetness to taste according to the tartness of the fruit
6. For sieved pudding: cook the berries in 2 L water, strain, sweeten, and thicken
7. Add vanilla essence and serve with raw cream or crème

title: Stikkelsbær-Grød til Steg (Gooseberry Sauce for Roasted Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- unripe berries
- very little water
- a piece of rinsed butter, the size of a walnut, per quart of berries
- a few pieces of whole cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take unripe berries, but do not peel them first
2. After stems and flower buds have been removed, place them on the fire with very little water and a piece of rinsed butter the size of a walnut per quart of berries
3. They should only be cooked until soft, but not broken
4. Cook a few pieces of whole cinnamon with the berries
5. Add sugar after the sauce is cooked, but it should not be very sweet

title: Stikkelsbær-Kompot (Gooseberry Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) green, thin-skinned gooseberries
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 Q) Melis sugar
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægel) water

## Directions

1. Use green gooseberries with thin skins
2. Remove the stems and blossoms
3. Rinse the berries
4. Make a syrup from the Melis sugar and water
5. Give the berries a brief boiling in the syrup, but they must remain whole
6. The syrup can be thickened with stirred sago flour
7. If the berries are particularly sour, they can be given a brief boiling in water before being put in the syrup

title: Stikkelsbær-Marmelade (Gooseberry Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Very ripe gooseberries
- Powdered sugar (575 gr to 1/2 kg berries)

## Directions

1. Put the very ripe gooseberries in a jar, and place this in a pot of boiling water, which must be kept boiling until the berries are completely soft and the juice can easily be pressed through a coarse sieve.
2. The strained juice is then cooked with the sugar until one finds the jam sufficiently stiff.
3. Fill it into jars, which are covered with paper and tied up.
4. What remains in the sieve can have water added to it and be cooked down to gooseberry porridge.

title: Stikkelsbær-Suppe (Gooseberry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 kg green gooseberries
- water
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- egg yolks
- sugar
- sago grains

## Directions

1. Rinse gooseberries without removing husks and cook in water with a little lemon peel and whole cinnamon
2. Strain and thicken
3. Season with egg yolks like rhubarb soup
4. Can cook fine sago grains in the strained gooseberry soup

title: Stikkelsbærgelée (Gooseberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pots of well-cleaned, fully developed but unripe gooseberries
- 2 pots of water
- 2 pounds of powdered sugar per pot of juice
- Vanilla to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the gooseberries with the water until they fall apart and are cooked into a pulp
2. Press this through a jelly bag
3. Cool the juice, place it on the fire again and cook and skim it for 10 minutes
4. Add 2 pounds of powdered sugar and vanilla to taste for each pot of juice
5. Cook this for half an hour and treat it afterwards in the usual manner
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stikkelsbærgrød (Gooseberry Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pots unripe, green gooseberries
- 2 pots water
- The peel of one lemon
- A cinnamon stick
- 1 pot white wine
- Powdered sugar
- Thickening agent: 30 ounces sago flour mixed with a little cold water
- Cold sweet cream for serving
- Vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Pit and rinse the green gooseberries well in cold water, then cook them with 2 pots of water, the lemon peel, and cinnamon stick until completely cooked.
2. Press through a colander several times and then through a fine sieve.
3. Place the 3 pots of strained juice over heat to boil with 1 pot of white wine and powdered sugar to taste.
4. Thicken like Rødgrød with 30 ounces of sago flour mixed first with a little cold water.
5. Serve cold with cold sweet cream in which a little vanilla sugar has been mixed.
6. The wine can be omitted.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stikkelsbærgrød (Gooseberry Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pots unripe green gooseberries
- 2 pots water
- peel of 1 lemon
- 1 stick cinnamon
- 1 pail white wine
- crushed sugar (about 14 pounds), to taste
- 30 quints sago flour
- cold sweet cream
- a little vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Pick and rinse the gooseberries well in cold water
2. Cook them with 2 pots water together with the lemon peel and cinnamon stick until completely cooked
3. Press through a colander several times and then through an open sieve
4. Heat the 3 pots of strained liquid with 1 pail white wine and crushed sugar to taste
5. Thicken like rødgrød with 30 quints sago flour, first mixed in a little cold water
6. Serve cold with cold sweet cream in which a little vanilla sugar has been added
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stikkelsbærkage (Gooseberry Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pots gooseberries, barely fully ripe
- powdered sugar, same weight as the berries
- a little ground Ceylon cinnamon
- a little grated lemon zest
- 1 pint French white wine
- 3/4 pound clarified butter
- 16 egg yolks
- 3/4 pound powdered sugar
- 60 quintals crushed rusks
- 16 egg whites, beaten
- paper dipped in melted butter
- powdered sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Weigh 3 pots gooseberries, barely fully ripe, and give them a brief boil, then drain in a hair sieve to let the water run off.
2. Boil powdered sugar of the same weight as the berries with a little ground Ceylon cinnamon, a little grated lemon zest and 1 pint French white wine to a very thick syrup, and add the gooseberries, which are cooked to marmalade.
3. Beat 3/4 pound clarified butter white, then beat again with 16 egg yolks, 3/4 pound powdered sugar and 60 quintals crushed rusks.
4. When this is well beaten together, pour in the gooseberries and stir carefully.
5. Finally stir in the 16 beaten egg whites.
6. Line a form with paper dipped in melted butter, then fill with the dough and bake slowly.
7. When baked through, carefully turn out onto a serving dish, remove the paper, and sprinkle with white powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stikkelsbærsaft (Gooseberry Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 S) unripe, rinsed, unpeeled berries per batch
- 1/2 liter (1/2 Pot) water per 1/2 kg berries
- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar Melis per 1 liter (1 Pot) juice

## Directions

1. For each 1/2 kg (1 S) unripe, rinsed, but unpeeled berries, take 1/2 liter (1/2 Pot) water.
2. The berries boil until they burst.
3. Pour them into the jelly cloth.
4. Only the next day, measure the juice and boil it for 10 minutes with the sugar.
5. Skim it during cooking and pour it into bottles, which are bound immediately.

title: Stikkelsbærsaft (Gooseberry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pots gooseberries, green or white glass berries preferred
- water to cover the berries well
- 10 pounds sugar (Melis)

## Directions

1. Pick the gooseberries when fully developed but not ripe.
2. Remove the flowers and stems.
3. Pour enough water over the berries so it stands well above them.
4. Cook for 1 hour.
5. Mash the berries.
6. Strain the whole mixture through a jelly bag.
7. This yields approximately 9 pots of juice.
8. Cook the juice for 1 hour with 10 pounds sugar.
9. Skim thoroughly and pour into bottles.
10. The next day, fill the cooled juice into bottles.
11. From each pot of berries, expect to get about 1 bottle of juice.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stikkelsbærsaft (Gooseberry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fully developed but unripe gooseberries
- Water

## Directions

1. Pick the gooseberries when they are fully developed but not ripe. The green or white ones are the best
2. Remove the flower and stem
3. Pour water over them so that it reaches a certain level (recipe text cuts off)
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stikkelsbærvin (Gooseberry Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- 32 pounds ripe berries, preferably yellow or white and clear
- 24 pounds powdered sugar
- Cold water
- Old drawn-off gooseberry wine, white French wine (Graves), or Sherry for topping up

## Directions

1. Crush the berries in a clean kettle or new bucket
2. Add 24 pounds powdered sugar and stir well together with the mashed berries
3. Pour the juice into a barrel that is well cleaned and sulfured
4. Rinse the berries with cold water until the juice comes out of them; pour this water also into the barrel, which should not be completely full so the wine has room to ferment
5. When the juice has finished fermenting and no more sound is heard in the barrel, fill it with old drawn-off gooseberry wine, white French wine (Graves), or Sherry and seal
6. Seal the bunghole and tap with clay
7. Around New Year's time the wine will be ready for drawing off into bottles
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stiv Druesaft (Stiff Grape Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Grapes
- Whole sugar, 2½ pounds per quart of juice
- Vanilla drops

## Directions

1. Place the berries in a jar and treat as stiff currant juice
2. However, since grape juice difficult becomes stiff, take 2½ pounds whole sugar per quart of juice, which is dipped in the juice and boiled to syrup according to instructions
3. Lift off the white of the egg while the remaining juice is stirred in together with a few vanilla drops
4. Cook this now for half an hour
5. It seldom becomes so stiff that it can be turned out of a cup

title: Stiv Ribsſaft (Thick Currant Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- Nearly 2 pounds whole sugar per quart of juice
- Half pint water

## Directions

1. Pick the currants and place them in a well-glazed earthenware crock, which does not need to be new if it is well cleaned.
2. Set the crock covered (so that the topmost ones do not become hard) in a hot oven where it stands, without being allowed to boil, until the currants sink, change color, and a little juice appears when you gently press on them with a spoon.
3. Then take out the crock and pour the currants together with the juice that is with them into a bag, which hangs still until the juice has run off.
4. For 1 quart of juice take nearly 2 pounds of whole sugar, which is cooked into a syrup with half pint water, but a little longer than according to directions, so that the syrup begins to become white, but is by no means thick or crystallized.
5. Lift the pot from the fire while the currant juice is stirred into it, set it again on and cook the juice evenly, not hard for 6 to 7 minutes, during which time it is skimmed, then poured into crocks or bottles, which the next day are covered with paper dipped in brandy, and tied up.
6. To get the juice lighter, one can let one-fourth of the currants be white.

title: Stor Fricandeau (Large Fricandeau)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg meat from inner loin (for 8 to 10 persons)
- Butter or fat for larding
- Water or broth
- Flour
- Cream
- Brown sauce
- Salt and pepper
- Browned potatoes
- Currant jelly or apple compote for serving

## Directions

1. Cut the meat piece from the inner loin.
2. Remove the skin and blanch the fricandeau.
3. Lard the fricandeau and roast it like veal roast, but it is best roasted in a pot.
4. Make the sauce as a brown, smooth gravy sauce, to which a little cream is added just before serving.
5. Browned potatoes, currant jelly or apple compote are served with the fricandeau.

title: Stor Iskage (Large Ice Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- butter (equal weight to eggs)
- powdered sugar very finely ground (equal weight to eggs)
- flour (equal weight to eggs)

## Directions

1. Weigh the eggs and take the same weight of each of the following: butter, very finely powdered sugar, and flour
2. Mix the egg yolks and sugar together
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stor Iskage (Large Ice Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- Equal weight of butter
- Equal weight of very finely powdered sugar
- Equal weight of flour
- 7 to 8 egg whites
- Equal amount of sifted sugar to egg whites, measured in tablespoons
- Vanilla ice cream (Recipe 413)

## Directions

1. Weigh 12 eggs, and take the same weight of each of the following: butter, very finely powdered sugar, and flour.
2. Beat the egg yolks and sugar together in a large bowl until it becomes a white salve.
3. Stir the flour into it and finally the butter, which has first been melted and somewhat cooled.
4. Beat the whites until white foam and blend with the rest.
5. Butter 8 to 10 pieces of paper of the size the ice cake should have, and spread a finger-thick layer of the batter on them, with a suitable opening in the middle so the baked cake has the form of a fairly broad wreath.
6. When the cakes are baked, take them from the paper and lay them on top of each other.
7. Beat 7 to 8 egg whites until foam and blend with the same number of tablespoons of sifted sugar, and brush the cakes with this both on the sides and on top.
8. Put it in a moderately warm oven to dry and lay it on the serving dish.
9. Immediately before serving, fill the opening in the middle with vanilla ice cream (Recipe 413).
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Stor Kringle (Large Kringle)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 Gr (10 Kvint) yeast
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 level teaspoon cardamom
- 5 eggs
- 175 Gr (35 Kvint) margarine
- 1/4 Kg (1 1/2 S) flour
- 3 1/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) thin cream
- finely chopped candied citrus peel and raisins to taste
- egg for brushing
- powdered sugar
- sliced almonds

## Directions

1. Mix yeast with salt
2. Knead together cardamom, eggs, margarine, flour and thin cream
3. Add finely chopped candied citrus peel and raisins to taste
4. Mix the dough the evening before, place in a cloth and bind with a band, but so that the dough has ample space to rise during the night
5. Place the cloth with the dough in a bowl or crock with ample cold water and set until the next morning
6. On the baking board place a little flour and pour out the dough, which with a light hand is rolled into a sausage
7. Form it as a kringle by placing it on the sheet and pressing it fairly flat
8. Brush with egg, sprinkle with abundant powdered sugar and cover with sliced almonds
9. When it has risen well again, place it in a well-heated oven and bake 1/2 hour

title: Stor Vol au vent (Large Vol au vent)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sheets puff pastry
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Roll out two sheets of puff pastry to just under 1 centimeter (1/4 inch) thick.
2. Take a measurement of the dish on which the vol au vent will be served; this is easiest done by cutting a shape from paper and using it to cut the pastry with a pastry wheel.
3. As the dough shrinks during baking, the dough must be at least 1.5 centimeters (1/2 inch) larger in circumference than the measurement.
4. Place the thinner sheet of pastry on a baking sheet and brush with egg white.
5. Cut the second, slightly thicker sheet into a ring approximately 5 to 6 centimeters (2 inches) wide, and place it on top of the base sheet and brush with egg as well, but ensure nothing gets on the edges.
6. Cut the remaining dough into small strips or decorative figures; bake these together with the vol au vent in a well-heated oven and use them for garnishing.
7. When the puff pastry is to be a nice light brown, it is often necessary to turn the vol au vent so that it bakes evenly.

title: Strandkaal au naturel (Coastal Cabbage, Plain)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Coastal cabbage stalks with leaves
- Water
- Salt
- Butter or hollandaise sauce

## Directions

1. Rinse the stalks with leaves.
2. Split lengthwise if needed.
3. Cook immediately in water with salt under a tight-fitting lid.
4. Many use milk to cook them in.
5. Arrange on a napkin like asparagus, but as hot as possible.
6. Serve with stirred butter or hollandaise sauce.

title: Strawberries (Jordber)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- A few spoonfuls brandy
- 1 pound strawberries
- 1 pound large, not too ripe strawberries
- 1/2 pound sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar into syrup with a few spoonfuls of brandy
2. Add 1 pound of strawberries and bring to a boil once
3. Pour out and let stand 3 to 4 days
4. Take large, not too ripe strawberries and pour the juice from the first batch over them
5. Let stand several more days
6. Carefully remove the strawberries into a sieve
7. Strain the juice again so the seeds come out and bring to a boil with 1/2 pound sugar
8. Pour this cold over the berries
9. The strawberries should be picked the same day they are preserved, otherwise they will taste bitter when preserved

title: Strawberries (Jordbær)
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- 1 pound powdered sugar per pound of berries
- brandy

## Directions

1. Clean the berries and for each pound use 1 pound powdered sugar, which is sprinkled in layers between the berries the day before preserving.
2. The next day, place the berries over the fire.
3. When the sugar is well dissolved, carefully pour the berries into a colander and cook the drained syrup for 1/2 hour while skimming frequently.
4. Then add the berries to the syrup and cook the whole for 8 minutes, then remove the berries again and cook the syrup alone for 1 hour more while skimming.
5. Now return the berries to the syrup and stir gently with 4 tablespoons of brandy.
6. Pour everything into a large jar and let stand until the next day, then divide into smaller glasses.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Strawberries No. 2 (Jordeber Nr. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound pitted strawberries
- 1 pound white sugar
- 1 gill water or young wine

## Directions

1. Cook sugar into a syrup with a gill of water or young wine
2. Add the berries and cook 15 to 20 minutes with a couple of boils
3. Remove the berries and cook the juice 4 to 5 minutes
4. Pour the juice hot over the berries
5. The next day put the whole thing on the fire, and when the berries have cooked a little, strain them into a pot
6. Cook the juice until thick, skim, and pour cold over the berries
7. The next day cover with paper dipped in brandy and tie up
8. Powdered sugar may also be used for the berries

title: Straßburger Gäseleverpâté (Strasbourg Goose Liver Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose liver
- Pork fat (lard)
- Truffles
- 2 finely chopped shallots
- 2 tablespoons finely chopped parsley
- Grated nutmeg
- Ground pepper
- Salt
- Butter
- Goose or pork offal (fat)

## Directions

1. Clean the truffles well, peel and cut into thick slices, then the shell is finely chopped.
2. Remove any spots carefully, and 1/2 pound of the goose liver is pounded into a fine farce with an equal amount of pork fat, an onion, a spoonful of parsley, the truffle shells and a little of the seasonings.
3. Cut the rest of the liver into 6 to 8 pieces, which are larded with slices of truffle that have been previously fried in butter with the rest of the onion and parsley.
4. Line the bottom of a fairly tall, cylindrical cooking vessel of porcelain or faience with thin slices of butter, over it a finger-thick layer of farce, then the larded slices of liver, which are sprinkled with seasonings, then another finger-thick layer of farce, thin slices of butter, and on top slices of pork fat. Cover the cooking vessel with paper and a lid, place on a baking sheet and bake in the oven for 2.5 to 3 hours.
5. Remove the paper and the pork fat slices.
6. Fill the cooking vessel completely with goose or pork offal, and a paper is placed anew under the lid.
7. Keeps for a long time in a cool place. All butter and offal used should be unsalted.

title: String Beans (Snittebønner)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh tender bean pods with underdeveloped beans
- salt
- a small amount of baking soda
- butter
- flour
- good milk
- chopped parsley
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select freshly picked thin pods with underdeveloped beans
2. Dry and rub them
3. Stack several pieces and cut crosswise with a sharp knife into very thin, fine strips
4. Cook tender in water with salt and a small amount of baking soda
5. Brown butter and flour together and thin with good milk to make a smooth sauce
6. Season the sauce with chopped parsley, salt, and sugar
7. Stir in the string beans without adding any liquid

title: Stuevede Krebse og Hummer (Stewed Crabs and Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- Crabs or lobster
- Salad oil
- Bread crumbs
- Nutmeg
- Cayenne pepper
- Lemon juice
- Butter
- Sweet cream
- Parsley
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the crabs and carefully remove the meat from the shell, keeping the shell intact.
2. Rub the shell with salad oil.
3. Mix bread crumbs (the same amount as the meat), a little nutmeg, cayenne pepper, lemon juice, butter, and a little sweet cream.
4. For lobster, rub the shell with cream instead.
5. Place the meat back in the shells, lightly cover with bread crumbs and butter, and bake brown in the oven.
6. Arrange with parsley and lemon slices around it.

title: Stuevede Østers (Stewed Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Oysters
- 2 cups cream or milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix butter with flour.
2. Place this mixture and the oysters in a glazed pot and stir until it boils.
3. Gradually add cream or milk, stirring constantly.
4. Season with salt to taste.
5. The oysters' own liquid is not used when stewing them.

title: Stufato (Italian Braised Beef)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kg beef chuck or brisket
- ¼ kg bacon strips
- Salt
- Pepper
- Finely chopped onions
- Palmin or claret
- ½ L white wine
- 1 L soup or water
- Coloring
- 3 to 4 apples
- Flour
- Butter
- Sugar
- Cooked macaroni
- Grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Remove all the tallow and then beat the meat well with a meat mallet and lard with bacon strips dipped in salt, pepper and finely chopped onions
2. Rub the meat with salt and brown in palmin or claret in a pot
3. Pour ½ L white wine and 1 L soup or water with added coloring over the meat and cook it until tender, turning it regularly
4. One hour before serving, add 3 to 4 apples to the pot and cook together with the meat
5. When the meat is tender, remove it; cooking usually takes 3 hours
6. Strain the gravy and with it dilute the baked flour and butter to make the sauce, which is seasoned with salt and a little sugar
7. Serve cooked macaroni with grated Parmesan cheese with the meat

title: Stuffed Pork Tenderloin
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork tenderloin
- Pork filling (see recipe 202)

## Directions

1. Trim the pork tenderloin and use the trimmed pieces for the filling.
2. Slit open the tenderloin and place the filling inside.
3. Sew it closed and lard it.
4. Roast as in the previous recipe.
5. Serve with potato salad and brown sauce made from the gravy.

title: Stuved Strandkaal (Braised Sea Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Sea cabbage stalks
- Water
- Salt
- Sauce for braised asparagus (recipe 587-589)

## Directions

1. Cut the stalks into smaller pieces.
2. Boil them in water with salt.
3. Prepare sauce as for braised asparagus (recipe 587-589).
4. Turn the cabbage in the sauce.

title: Stuvede Agge-Vlanter (Stewed Eggplant)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggplant
- Salt
- Water
- Pepper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Place them in cold water with a little salt, and cook until they can be cut through with the side of a fork; strain the water once. Mash them, crush them and heat them in hot water, pepper and butter.

title: Stuvede Artiskokker (Stewed Artichokes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Strong broth
- Paprika
- Lemon juice
- Mushrooms
- Madeira
- Sugar
- Salt
- Meat glaze if needed
- Artichoke hearts
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Brown the butter and cook flour in it, then add a strong broth to make a smooth sauce, seasoned with paprika and a little lemon juice.
2. Add mushrooms, Madeira, sugar, salt and meat glaze if the sauce needs more body.
3. The hearts, which have previously been blanched in bouillon, are taken up with a spoon, arranged on a warm platter, and the sauce is poured over them.

title: Stuvede Asparges og nye Gulerodder (Stewed Asparagus and Young Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Carrots
- Water
- Bouillon
- Butter
- Flour
- Salt
- Parsley
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scrape both parts, cut them into short pieces and cook them for a short time in half water, half bouillon; of the asparagus cook only what is tender. Asparagus in this country is on the whole not good, and one must nearly always cut a piece of the hardest part away. The heads become tender faster than the rest and should therefore be added later. They are not cooked in more water and bouillon than is needed for the sauce. When they are tender, make a roux of butter and flour and a little salt. You can stew both parts each separately in the same way, and then add a little parsley to the carrots and nutmeg to the asparagus.

title: Stuvede Botfeldske Roer (Stewed Beet Field Roots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Beet field roots
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk

## Directions

1. Cook and stew as Stewed White Roots (Nr. 700)

title: Stuvede Egge-Planter (Stewed Eggplant)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- cold water
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Place them in cold water with a little salt and cook until they can be cut through with the side of a fork
2. Change the water once
3. Peel them, mash them and warm them in salt water, pepper and butter

title: Stuvede Grønærter og Gulerødder (Stewed Green Peas and Carrots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dekaliters green peas
- Carrots, quantity to taste
- Sugar
- Salt
- 125 grams butter
- 2 good tablespoons flour
- Parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots and cut them into small, fine cubes about the size of a pea; rinse them.
2. Cook the carrots together with the peas in very little water with sugar and salt.
3. Melt the butter and brown 2 good tablespoons of flour in it.
4. Thin this with the pea water and add the peas together with finely chopped parsley.
5. Bring to a boil and serve.

title: Stuvede Gulerodder (Stewed Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- butter
- sugar
- parsley
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots, cut them into slices and place them in boiling water
2. Cook until they are so tender that you can press them through a sieve with a spoon
3. Place them again over heat with a piece of butter and sugar and let them have a brief boiling
4. When arranged, sprinkle chopped parsley over
5. Some like a little vinegar with this dish

title: Stuvede Gulerødder (Stewed Carrots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- water
- Maggi bouillon cubes or thin broth
- butter
- flour
- ground white pepper
- salt
- chopped parsley
- sugar

## Directions

1. Small carrots are preferred for stewed carrots
2. If using larger carrots, cook them whole first, then cut them
3. They can be split and cut into smaller pieces or into slices, preferably with a fluted vegetable knife
4. Scrape the carrots and place in enough water with Maggi bouillon cubes or thin broth to provide sufficient liquid for stewing
5. Make a roux of butter and flour, dilute with the carrot broth
6. Season with ground white pepper, salt, chopped parsley, and if needed, a little sugar

title: Stuvede Hvideroer (Stewed White Roots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- White roots (turnips)
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Parsley
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Roots are not considered a particularly healthy food and can often have a bitter taste, so as soon as they come to a boil, discard the first water
2. Peel and cut them into smaller pieces and cook tender in salted water
3. Stew them like potatoes in butter, flour, and milk
4. Prepare the sauce with chopped parsley, sugar, and salt
