5. Drain the roots well on a colander before placing them in the stew

title: Stuvede Kartofler (Stewed Potatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Salt
- Sugar
- Potatoes
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Brown butter and flour and thin with milk until it becomes a proper thick sauce, which is prepared with salt and a little sugar. Add very small, cooked, peeled potatoes or larger ones, cut into 2 to 4 pieces. Heat the potatoes in the sauce. A little chopped parsley in the sauce is good for new potatoes. For all vegetables that are stewed in milk, it has a good effect if a little cold butter is stirred into the sauce just before serving.

title: Stuvede Kartofler (Stewed Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Milk or broth
- Cooked potatoes
- Nutmeg
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Salt

## Directions

1. Melt butter, add flour and milk or bouillon; when this has boiled until smooth, give the cooked and peeled potatoes a boiling in it together with nutmeg, pepper and parsley.
2. Add salt at the end.
3. Alternatively, peeled potatoes can be placed on the fire with milk or thin soup and cooked until the sauce becomes smooth, then stir in butter, seasonings and salt.

title: Stuvede Kastanier (Stewed Chestnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Scalded chestnuts
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock
- Salt
- Sugar
- Madeira

## Directions

1. Scalded chestnuts, from which the shell and brown skin have been removed, are boiled tender in stock. Brown butter and flour and dilute with the stock in which they are cooked. Season with salt, sugar and Madeira. Give the chestnuts a brief cooking in this before serving.

title: Stuvede Radiſer (Stewed Radishes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Radishes
- Bouillon
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Chopped parsley
- Coarsely ground white pepper

## Directions

1. Remove stems and small roots
2. Wash the radishes and cook quickly in bouillon or strong meat stock with fresh butter and spices
3. Thicken the sauce with flour and parsley and bring to a boil, then add egg yolks
4. Serve, typically with cutlets

title: Stuvede Selleriknolde (Braised Celery Root)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled and cut celery root
- Water or soup
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the peeled and cut celery root until tender in water or soup with salt
2. Brown butter and flour together and thin with the strained celery cooking liquid to make a sauce
3. Thicken the sauce with a couple of egg yolks
4. Add salt, pepper and a little sugar to taste

title: Stuvede Syrer (Stewed Sorrel)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 bushel sorrel
- A little natron
- Butter
- Flour
- Soup stock
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Rub the sorrel, rinse it, and place it in plenty of boiling water with a little natron.
2. When the leaves are tender, drain them on a colander and press out the water.
3. Chop the sorrel finely or press it through a coarse sieve.
4. The sorrel mash can either be stewed like spinach, for which butter and flour are browned and thinned with soup stock, and the sorrel is stirred in and prepared with sugar; or a piece of cold butter is simply stirred into the mash along with sugar to taste.
5. Bring the sorrel to a boil with this and serve with roasted meat, cutlets, etc.

title: Stuvede Tomatoes (Stewed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes
- Sugar
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place the tomatoes in hot water and scald them; then peel them finely, cook them in barely enough water, and add sugar to taste.

title: Stuvede Tomatoes, Art. 1 (Stewed Tomatoes, Method 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tomatoes
- hot water
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Place ripe tomatoes in hot water and peel them
2. Cut them up and cook further, then season with butter, pepper and salt

title: Stuvede Tomatoes, Art. 2 (Stewed Tomatoes, Method 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- hot water
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place the tomatoes in hot water and peel them
2. Cut them small, cook tender in barely enough water and add sugar to taste

title: Stuvet Corn (Stewed Corn)
body:
## Ingredients

- Corn
- Milk
- Flour
- Butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the husks and strip the kernels from the cob.
2. Boil the kernels; afterwards stew them in good milk.
3. Make a roux of flour and butter; add a little salt at the end.

title: Stuvet Hvidkaal (Stewed White Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- White cabbage head
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Ground white pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves from the cabbage head and cut the head into 4 pieces, cutting away the core.
2. Cook the cabbage until tender in water with salt.
3. Place on a colander and press the water out of it.
4. Make a roux by baking butter and flour together, then dilute with milk to make a stewing sauce.
5. Season the sauce with salt and often with ground white pepper.
6. Cut the cooked cabbage into smaller pieces or, as many do, chop it coarsely.
7. Add the cabbage to the sauce.

title: Stuvet Makaroni (Stewed Macaroni)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni, broken into pieces and cooked
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Salt
- A little sugar

## Directions

1. Make a sauce with butter and flour.
2. Dilute with good milk and season with salt and a little sugar.
3. Turn the cooked macaroni into the sauce.

title: Stuvet Rosenkaal (Stewed Brussels Sprouts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked Brussels sprouts
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth or water
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Egg yolks
- Sugar
- Salt
- Browned potatoes

## Directions

1. After the Brussels sprouts are cooked and the water is well drained, pour over a sauce made of butter and flour.
2. Bake together, then dilute with broth or water with Maggi bouillon cubes and bind with 1 egg yolk per head of cabbage.
3. Season the sauce with sugar and salt to taste and toss the cabbage in it.
4. Serve browned potatoes around the cabbage or separately.
5. Alternatively, Brussels sprouts can be stewed in milk.

title: Stuvet Strandkaal (Stewed Coastal Cabbage)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Coastal cabbage stalks
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk or cream

## Directions

1. Cut the stalks into smaller pieces and cook in water with salt.
2. Use a sauce like that for stewed asparagus to serve with it.

title: Suder (Brill)
body:
## Ingredients

- brill
- hot water
- salt
- sour butter sauce or cream and horseradish sauce
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Scrape and scald the brill in several changes of hot water to remove the slime
2. Clean them and cook whole
3. Serve with sour butter sauce or cream and horseradish sauce
4. If they are muddy, cook some bread crumbs in the water to remove the muddy taste
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sugar Bread Cake (Sukkerbrodskage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 Egg yolks
- 1 Pound powdered Sugar
- Egg whites
- 2 Ounces Corn Starch

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks with the powdered sugar for half an hour
2. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks
3. Mix the corn starch with the beaten whites
4. Carefully fold into the egg yolks
5. Pour into a form lined with buttered paper
6. Bake for a few hours with low heat at first, then more

title: Sukat af Græskar eller Agurker (Candied Pumpkin or Cucumber)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pumpkin or large cucumbers, still green
- Puddered sugar
- Vinegar
- Ginger

## Directions

1. Cut into large pieces, give one boiling in water, and place on a clean cloth to dry. The outer skin should remain on, where it is not on the pumpkin too thick and hard
2. Dark powdered sugar is moistened with so much vinegar that it can melt; it is cooked until it is skimmed and clear, with 1 teaspoon ground ginger to each 2 pounds
3. Now the pieces are given a good boiling in the sugar, upon which they are taken up, placed in a crock, and the sauce is poured over warm. If it does not stand over, then more must be cooked
4. Since the pieces tend to float up, press them down with a tablespoon or a small round plate that is placed in the crock with some weight on it. After 8 days take them up to place the top ones at the bottom. When they have stood until you can taste that they are penetrated with sugar, they are taken up and placed in a dry crock or a deep vessel, and covered between each layer with dry powdered sugar
5. This also becomes syrup, and both this and the first can be used to candy other pumpkins with. The candied pieces are now placed in a light press between two large spoons with paper over and under. When they are pressed, thread a string through them and let them hang in an airy place to dry, but not in the sun
6. Finally place them in a crock with ground white sugar between each layer. The fruit must not be cooked too tender

title: Sukat af Meloner (Candied Melon)
body:
## Ingredients

- A melon that is not fully ripe
- Sugar brine

## Directions

1. Peel a melon that is not fully ripe, hollow it out and cut into finger-long pieces, about an inch wide and half as thick, weigh them, place in a brine that is so strong that it can just make an egg float, and let stand for 4 days
2. Then take the pieces out and place for 3 days

title: Sukat af Meloner (Candied Melon)
body:
## Ingredients

- One melon, not fully ripe
- One pound melon (prepared)
- One pound white sugar
- One-third pint water
- One egg (for testing brine density)

## Directions

1. Peel the melon, remove the seeds, and cut into finger-length pieces, about one inch wide and half as thick.
2. Weigh the pieces and place in a brine that is strong enough to float an egg; let sit for 4 days.
3. Remove the pieces and soak in cold water for 3 days, changing the water daily.
4. Place on heat in cold water and cook covered until tender enough to pierce with a stick; remove and dry thoroughly in cloth.
5. For 1 pound of melon, take 1 pound white sugar and cook with one-third pint water to make a syrup.
6. Add the melon to the syrup, cook for 5 to 6 minutes, pour into a jar and cover; let stand for 4 days.
7. Pour off the syrup, cook it for a few minutes, and pour hot over the melon; let stand for three days.
8. Spread on a sieve to drain for a few hours, then place on a plate.
9. Cook the syrup again until thick enough to pour over the melon; turn the melon in it and let stand several days in an airy place to dry.
10. Store in a dry place.

title: Sukkeragurker (Sugar Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- Sugar
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers and split lengthwise
2. Place in cold water for a while and drain on a sieve
3. Boil water and place the cucumbers in for a preliminary cooking and drain on a cloth
4. Take 2 parts vinegar to 1 part sugar by weight

title: Sukkeragurker (Sugar Pickled Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- Sugar
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, split them lengthwise, soak them in cold water for a while and drain on a sieve
2. Boil water, place the cucumbers in it for one boiling and drain on a cloth
3. Cook together 2 parts vinegar, 1 part sugar by weight, some whole cloves and a little cinnamon to make a syrup, then cook the cucumbers in this syrup until done

title: Sukkerbrodskage (Sugar Bread Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- egg whites
- 2 ounces corn starch

## Directions

1. Stir 16 egg yolks with 1 pound powdered sugar for half an hour
2. Whip the egg whites until stiff peaks form
3. Blend the whipped egg whites with 2 ounces corn starch
4. Carefully stir the corn starch mixture into the egg yolks
5. Pour into a form lined with buttered paper
6. Bake for a couple of hours with gentle heat at first, then increased heat later

title: Sukkerdrops (Sugar Drops)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1/2 pound butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 gill rose water

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together.
2. Place on paper with a teaspoon.
3. Makes approximately 60.

title: Sukkerkage (Sugar Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 egg yolks
- 55 quintal powdered sugar
- 25 quintal best flour
- 12 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks together with 55 quintal powdered sugar for an hour
2. Then add 25 quintal best flour, first dried and sifted
3. Beat 12 egg whites to peaks and fold in with the rest
4. Pour the whole into a form lined with butter paper and bake 1 hour in a moderately warm oven
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sukkerkrandse (Sugar Wreaths)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 raw egg yolks
- 1/4 pound white or powdered sugar
- 1/4 pound clarified butter
- 1/2 pound flour

## Directions

1. Rub the hard-boiled yolks and mix well with the 4 raw yolks, 1/4 pound white or powdered sugar, 1/4 pound clarified butter, and 1/2 pound flour.
2. Mix to a stiff dough.
3. Take small pieces, dip in flour, roll with hands, and form into small wreaths.
4. Brush with egg white on one side, sprinkle with white coarse sugar, and bake light brown.
5. Makes 30 wreaths.

title: Sukkerol (Sugar Ale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 5 quarts cold fresh water
- 1 bottle lager

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, water, and ale together for 1 hour. Pour into bottles without cooking beforehand and cork well. After 10 days it is ready to serve.

title: Sukkerpleskener (Sugar Sponge Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 1 pound patent flour

## Directions

1. Beat 12 eggs and 1 pound powdered sugar well together.
2. Stir in 1 pound patent flour.
3. When well mixed, stick out small cakes with a spoon, place on a buttered pan, sprinkle with sugar, and bake.

title: Sultana-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 250 grams margarine
- 250 grams fine sugar
- 125 grams pitted and blanched sultana raisins
- 50 grams finely chopped candied orange peel
- cardamom
- orange essence
- 500 grams Pillsbury flour
- 500 milliliters semi-skimmed milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix eggs, margarine, and fine sugar well
2. Add pitted and blanched sultana raisins, finely chopped candied orange peel, cardamom, and orange essence to taste
3. Stir in Pillsbury flour, semi-skimmed milk, and baking powder
4. Bake in a greased form for 1 hour

title: Sundheds-Suppe à la Santé (Health Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- A couple of handfuls of sorrel
- A handful of chervil
- The same amount of lettuce
- A little parsley
- 1/4 pound butter
- 5 pots chicken broth
- Small chicken dumplings or toasted French bread

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and finely chop the sorrel, chervil, lettuce, and parsley
2. Place over heat with 1/4 pound butter and braise for a quarter hour
3. Add 5 pots chicken broth and cook for 3/4 hour
4. Serve with small chicken dumplings or with toasted French bread
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Suppe med Karri (Curry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- nearly 1 pound beef, veal, or lamb meat, or about double that amount of tender veal meat per portion
- rice
- cold water
- salt
- soup vegetables
- curry powder to taste
- sour onion sauce

## Directions

1. This soup is primarily cooked with veal or lamb meat.
2. The treatment is otherwise quite as described in No. 5, except that the meat barely tolerates cooking as long, and instead of dumplings, rice is used.
3. Soak the rice in cold water, and renew this repeatedly while stirring well with your hands.
4. When all impurities have been removed in this way, place the rice over heat in cold water, bring to boil and skim repeatedly.
5. Then pour it into a colander and rinse with cold water.
6. Now pour the soup onto the rice, which is given a boil until it is tender, but still whole.
7. Add curry powder to taste.
8. Serve the soup first and then the soup meat, to which sour onion sauce can be served.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Suppe med ægte Kastanier (Chestnut Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Pund ægte Kastanier eller Maroner
- Et Fjerdingspund Smør
- en Spiseskefuld Sukker
- omtrent en Pægl Sky
- 3 Potter Sky
- Sagomel
- Rødvin
- lidt Supperødder

## Directions

1. Koges 2 Pund ægte Kastanier eller Maroner et Øjeblik i Vand, lægges paa et Dørslag og overhældes med koldt Vand.
2. Paa denne Maade bliver man i Stand til at pille dem.
3. Smeltes Et Fjerdingspund Smør i en Pande sammen med en Spiseskefuld Sukker.
4. Glasseres Kastanierne heri.
5. Tilsættes der lidt efter lidt omtrent en Pægl Sky, hvormed Kastanierne koges, indtil de ere møre.
6. Koges 3 Potter Sky, skummes og jævnes lidt efter lidt med Sagomel, saa den ikke klumper, men bliver saa jævn som tynd Havresuppe.
7. Kommes Rødvin deri efter Smag og derpaa Kastanierne tilligemed den Sky, hvori de ere kogte.
8. Om man synes kan ogsaa tilsættes lidt Supperødder, der ere skaarne i Terninger af Størrelse som Kastanierne.
9. Kogningen maa ske ved langsom Ild og under jævnlig Omrystning og Omrøring, da Kastanierne ere tilbøjelige til at brænde paa Bunden.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Suppe paa Kalkunkraaſer (Turkey Giblet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Giblets from 3 turkeys
- 7 to 8 leeks
- 1 bunch chervil
- 1 pint Madeira wine
- 4 to 5 quarts veal bouillon
- Toasted bread cubes

## Directions

1. Bring the bouillon to a boil and add the mixed turkey giblets, except for the livers.
2. Pour in the wine and continue cooking over moderate heat.
3. Cook the white part of the leeks in very little butter, cut into pieces and place in the soup tureen together with the cleaned and cut giblets and the cooked livers.
4. Skim the soup and strain through a fine sieve, pour over them and add the chervil.
5. Serve with toasted bread cubes.

title: Suppe à l'espagnole (Spanish Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 Kg (1 Sj) pearl onions
- 75 Gr (15 Quintal) Butter
- 1 Dessertspoonful Powdered sugar
- Soup
- Paprika
- Wine
- 1/2 Kg (1 tt) fish forcemeat
- Parsley or watercress, chopped

## Directions

1. Scald and peel the onions
2. Brown 75 Gr (15 Quintal) butter, and shake the onions in it until they begin to brown
3. Sprinkle 1 dessertspoonful of powdered sugar over them and shake the onions in it until they are glazed
4. Cook them tender in a little of the soup, which is later mixed with the remainder
5. Just before serving, season with paprika and wine
6. Place small balls of 1/2 Kg (1 tt) fish forcemeat, mixed with chopped parsley or watercress, on top of the soup

title: Suppe à l'Hollandaise (Dutch-Style Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 L (5 pots) economical stock
- 4 carrots
- 4 parsnips
- 0.5 L (0.5 pot) shelled peas
- 125 g (1/4 lb) butter
- 125 g (1/4 lb) flour
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the carrots and parsnips into small dice and cook tender in some of the stock together with 0.5 L (0.5 pot) shelled peas
2. Brown 125 g (1/4 lb) butter with 125 g (1/4 lb) flour and dilute with 5 L (5 pots) stock in which the vegetable stock has been mixed
3. When it has cooked, remove from heat while 6 well-beaten egg yolks are stirred in

title: Suppe à l'Hollandaise (Hollandaise Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 L (5 pots) vegetable stock
- 4 carrots
- 4 turnips
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) shelled peas
- 125 g (1/4 lb) butter
- 125 g (1/4 lb) flour
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/4 L (1 pint) raw cream

## Directions

1. Cut 4 carrots and 4 turnips into small cubes and cook until tender in some of the stock together with 1/2 L (1/2 pot) shelled peas.
2. In a pot, bake 125 g (1/4 lb) butter and 125 g (1/4 lb) flour together and thin with 5 L (5 pots) stock in which the vegetable stock has been mixed.
3. When it has boiled, remove from heat.
4. Whisk together 6 well-beaten egg yolks with 1/4 L (1 pint) raw cream and whisk into the soup.
5. Return the pot to the heat and bring the soup to a boil with strong stirring, then pour immediately into the tureen over the vegetables.

title: Suppe à la Clairmont
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 onions
- Butter
- 4 L (4 pots) meat soup cooked with herbs and vegetables
- 60 g (12 drams) tapioca grains
- Finely chopped chervil
- Carved cooked carrots
- Poached eggs

## Directions

1. Slice 4 onions and sauté lightly in butter until they do not darken
2. Pour 4 L (4 pots) of meat soup cooked with herbs and vegetables over the onions
3. As soon as the soup comes to a boil, sprinkle 60 g (12 drams) tapioca grains while stirring continuously
4. Cook the soup until the grains become clear, which takes about a quarter hour
5. Stir in a small amount of finely chopped chervil and bring to a boil once
6. Arrange with carved cooked carrots
7. Serve with poached eggs

title: Suppe à la Douglas (Douglas Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L (4 Pots) soup
- Maggi Bouillon cubes
- Sago flour
- Water
- Sherry or Madeira
- 1/2 Kg (1 S) finely diced asparagus
- Veal forcemeat

## Directions

1. Take a good, clear, brown spare soup and add Maggi Bouillon cubes if needed
2. Thicken with sago flour mixed in water
3. Season with a little Sherry or Madeira to taste
4. Add 1/2 Kg (1 S) finely diced asparagus and some very small balls of veal forcemeat (Nr. 201) to the soup
5. Serve with small water pastries

title: Suppe à la Jardiniére
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L (4 pots) clear soup
- 4 carrots
- 2 turnips
- 3 leeks
- 1 celery
- 1 head of lettuce
- 50 g (10 drams) butter
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- Small whole onions
- Cooked vegetables of the season: asparagus, peas, cauliflower, Brussels sprouts, etc.
- Bread-croutons (recipe No. 500)

## Directions

1. Prepare as for the Julienne or Vegetable Soup
2. In addition to the vegetables, sauté some small whole onions together with the other vegetables
3. Add cooked seasonal vegetables such as asparagus, peas, cauliflower, Brussels sprouts, etc. to the tureen
4. Serve with bread-croutons (recipe No. 500)

title: Suppe à la Pompadour (Pompadour Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Celery
- 2 Parsley roots
- 2 Root leeks
- 4 Carrots
- Butter
- 4 L (4 Pots) good brown spare soup
- Toasted bread or small water pastries

## Directions

1. Cut the celery, parsley roots, root leeks and carrots into small cubes
2. Fry in a very little butter
3. Pour 4 L (4 Pots) good brown spare soup over the vegetables
4. Boil for a quarter hour
5. Serve with toasted bread or small water pastries

title: Sur Flöde-Omelet (Sour Cream Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints sour cream
- 0.5 pint sweet cream
- 6 egg yolks
- White sugar
- 6 tablespoons flour
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat sour cream and sweet cream to a foam
2. Combine with egg yolks, white sugar, and flour
3. Fold in beaten egg whites just as it goes into oven

title: Sur Kirsebær Saft (Sour Cherry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wrung out berries from the previous recipe
- Vinegar
- Powdered sugar, ½ pound per pint

## Directions

1. The wrung out berries that remain from the previous one can be cooked with vinegar to taste
2. Since only very little juice comes from this, it is best to crush some more berries and cook them with the vinegar
3. When it has boiled well for 8 to 10 minutes under a lid, wring it in a cloth
4. Then boil the juice like the previous one

title: Sur Smørsauce (Sour Butter Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- cold butter

## Directions

1. Boil vinegar, and when it boils, remove it from the heat.
2. Stir in cold butter until it melts.
3. The sauce should be fairly smooth.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Surbrød (Sour Rye Bread)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 Gr (10 Kvint) yeast
- 1/2 Kg (1 S) rye sieve flour
- 1/4 L (3 Pægle) semi-skimmed milk
- salt
- 1/2 Kg (1 S) rye sieve flour
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Crumble yeast into 1/2 Kg (1 S) rye sieve flour
2. Stir in 1/4 L (3 Pægle) semi-skimmed milk and a little salt
3. Let the dough rise for 3-4 hours
4. Knead with 1/2 Kg (1 S) rye sieve flour
5. Form into 2-3 loaves, which rise again
6. Brush with milk or egg and sprinkle with caraway seeds
7. Bake well for 1/4 hour

title: Sure Agurker
body:
## Ingredients

- Agurker, ikke overmodne
- Grovt salt
- Charlotter
- Sort peber
- Hvidt peber
- Rødt peber
- Dill
- Allehaande
- Eddike

## Directions

1. Skræl agurkerne og flek dem
2. Tag indmaden ud med en sploffe
3. Nedlæg dem lagvis med dygtig grovt salt imellem i en bale eller andet rent kar
4. Dagen efter tag dem op og tør dem omhyggeligt
5. Nedlæg dem i krukkerne lagvis, idet man mellem hvert lag lægger en god mængde charlotter, sort, hvidt og rødt peber samt dill og allehaande
6. Kog så meget eddike, som man kan beregne vil stå over agurkerne, og slå den hed over dem
7. Hvis man har gjort dette om morgenen, lad dem stå til næste dags eftermiddag, tilbundet med et klede
8. Hæld eddiken fra dem i gryden igen, og giv agurkerne efterhaanden et ganske fort opkog deri
9. Til sidst holdes eddiken over dem igen

title: Sure Asier (Pickled Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- salt brine strong enough to float an egg
- best quality wine vinegar
- Spanish pepper
- black pepper
- cloves
- small shallots
- horseradish, cut into small cubes

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and hollow them out.
2. Wipe them dry with a linen cloth.
3. Leave them for 24 hours in a salt brine strong enough to float an egg, made with soft water.
4. Remove them and place on a colander.
5. Leave them for another 24 hours in fresh salt brine.
6. Put best quality wine vinegar, Spanish pepper, black pepper, cloves, some small shallots, and horseradish cut into small cubes in an enameled pot and heat over the fire and cook briefly.
7. Place the pears in the pot, but do not let them boil, and remove after a moment.
8. Place them in jars and pour the vinegar over them.
9. There must be enough vinegar to stand well over the pears.
10. If the vinegar evaporates, cook a little more and pour it cold over them.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sure Valnødder til Saucer (Sour Walnuts for Sauces)
body:
## Ingredients

- Walnuts, picked at same time and of same size as preserved walnuts
- Salt brine
- Vinegar
- Shallots
- Garlic
- Ginger
- Horseradish
- Nutmeg
- Cloves
- Mustard seed
- Pepper
- Mixed spices

## Directions

1. The walnuts are placed in a cold brine which is so strong that it can bear an egg, and must be fresh and well stirred. They stand in this for 6 days, are taken out and placed in a fresh and equally strong, cold brine, in which they again stand for 6 days
2. Strain them through a sieve where the brine is well drained, and place in a crock
3. Then place on the fire so much vinegar that it completely stands over the walnuts; to this for 100 walnuts add 4 shallots, a small piece of garlic, 3 pieces of ginger, 2 pieces of thin horseradish, 5 to 6 pieces of nutmeg, 10 cloves, 6 tablespoons whole mustard seed, 2 tablespoons pepper and 2 tablespoons mixed spices
4. When all this has boiled, strain it, cover with a cloth and pour cold over the walnuts, which are then tied up very securely, as air would make them soft
5. After a year they are ready to use and keep for several years. They are quite dark in color, but give a pure taste to sauce, especially in melted butter

title: Surkaal (Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized and white cabbage heads
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Caraway seeds
- Barberries
- Sourdough or rye flour and vinegar dough

## Directions

1. Remove outer leaves from cabbage heads and cut off the stumps, then cut the cabbage very finely
2. Line a well-cleaned wooden barrel or tub with a little firm sourdough or dough made from rye flour and vinegar on the bottom and sides
3. Layer the shredded cabbage in the barrel, sprinkling each layer with vinegar and a little salt, caraway, and barberries
4. Pack the cabbage tightly with a wooden pestle so it releases its juice
5. Place a wooden cover with a weight on top
6. Put the barrel or tub in a warm room so the cabbage ferments and becomes sour, which usually takes 1 to 2 weeks
7. The fermentation produces acid that prevents further spoilage and makes it suitable for long storage
8. If mold appears, remove it carefully
9. Place the barrel in the cold to freeze; it can then be kept all winter

title: Sursteeg (Sour Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds beef (top round)
- Bacon for larding
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth
- 1 quart thin oil
- 3 pints vinegar
- White or black pepper
- Salt
- 3 to 4 bay leaves
- Cream
- 1 ounce sugar
- Juniper berries

## Directions

1. Free the meat from bone and rub with salt and ground pepper.
2. Mix oil and vinegar with some pepper grains, bay leaves, salt, and 50 juniper berries.
3. Place meat in this brine and let stand 2 to 3 days.
4. When ready to roast, rinse the meat in fresh water.
5. Lard with bacon strips rolled in salt and pepper.
6. Brown the meat well in butter in a roasting pan.
7. Add broth and roast under a lid.
8. Add sugar and cream from time to time, and baste occasionally with the sauce.
9. Thicken the sauce with a little flour, bring to a boil, strain, and serve.

title: Sursteg (Sour Pot Roast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg beef sirloin
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Cloves
- ¼ L vinegar
- 1 L beer
- Whole peppercorns
- 3 bay leaves
- 2 onions
- Butter or palmin
- Water
- Soya or coloring

## Directions

1. Beat the meat well with a meat mallet and rub with a mixture of salt, ground pepper and a little cloves
2. Place the meat for 3 to 4 days in a brine of ¼ L vinegar, 1 L beer, some whole peppercorns, 3 bay leaves and 2 onions cut into slices, turning it regularly
3. Brown a good slice of butter or palmin in a pot and place the meat in it after the brine has drained well
4. When sufficiently browned, pour water and a little soya or coloring over it
5. Baste the roast regularly with the gravy, turn it and roast very gently covered for 2½ to 3 hours
6. Strain the gravy

title: Sursteg (Sour Roast)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg beef (inner loin, for 12 persons)
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- A little cloves
- 1/4 L (1 pægl) vinegar
- 1 L (1 pot) beer
- A few whole peppercorns
- 3 bay leaves
- 2 onions, sliced
- A good slice of butter or Palmin
- Water
- A little soy sauce or color
- Flour for the sauce
- Sugar
- Currant juice
- Color if necessary
- Boiled potatoes for serving

## Directions

1. Beat the meat well with a meat mallet and rub with a mixture of salt, ground pepper and a little cloves.
2. Place the meat in a marinade of 1/4 L (1 pægl) vinegar, 1 L (1 pot) beer, a few whole peppercorns, 3 bay leaves and 2 onions sliced into slices for 3 to 4 days and turn regularly.
3. Brown a good slice of butter or Palmin in a pot, and place the meat in it after the marinade has drained well.
4. When sufficiently brown, pour in water and a little soy sauce or color.
5. Baste the roast regularly with the gravy, turn it and roast very slowly covered for 2.5 to 3 hours.
6. Strain the gravy and skim the fat.
7. In the fat that is skimmed, brown flour for the sauce and dilute with the gravy and season with salt, a little sugar, currant juice and color if necessary.
8. Boiled potatoes can be served alongside.
9. Sursteg can also be roasted in milk.

title: Sv eder med Eddike til Steg (Quinces with Vinegar for Serving)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces, well ripe
- Small pieces of cinnamon
- Cloves, peeled
- Two pounds quinces (prepared)
- One pint water
- Three pints vinegar
- One and one-third pound powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces, which must be well ripe, and cut into 4 to 6 pieces according to size.
2. Remove the hard matter and weigh the pieces; stud with small pieces of cinnamon and peeled cloves.
3. For 2 pounds quinces, take 1 pint water, 3 pints vinegar, and 1 and one-third pound good powdered sugar.
4. If the quinces are not completely covered, take a bit more of each ingredient.
5. Place everything together on heat until the quinces are tender; remove the quinces and place in a jar.
6. Cook the syrup until completely even, cool it, and pour over the quinces; tie the next day.

title: Sveder med Eddike til Steg (Preserved Quinces with Vinegar for Roasting)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces, well ripened
- Cinnamon pieces
- Cloves
- 2 pounds quinces
- 1 pint water
- 3 pints vinegar
- 1½ pounds powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces, which must be well ripened, cut them into 4 to 6 pieces according to their size, remove the cores
2. Weigh them and stud with small pieces of cinnamon and cloves
3. For 2 pounds quinces, take 1 pint water, 3 pints vinegar, and 1½ pounds good powdered sugar
4. If the quinces are not completely covered, take a little more of each ingredient
5. Heat until the quinces are soft, then remove them and place in a jar
6. Boil the liquid until it is perfectly smooth, cool it, and pour over the quinces
7. Seal the jar the following day

title: Svedskesuppe (Prune Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound washed and soaked pearl barley
- 3 pots boiling water
- several sticks cinnamon
- 1.5 pounds prunes
- a couple of lemon slices
- sugar to taste
- 1/2 pint sharp French wine

## Directions

1. Put 1 pound of washed and soaked pearl barley in 3 pots of boiling water and cook until tender, together with several sticks of cinnamon.
2. Meanwhile, wash 1.5 pounds of prunes well in several changes of cold water and add them to the soup along with a couple of lemon slices (with pits removed).
3. Add sugar to taste and 1/2 pint of sharp French wine.
4. If the soup becomes too thick, thin it with boiling water.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Svensk Budding (Swedish Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter (1 pot) heavy cream
- 125 grams (¼ pound) finely chopped candied citrus peel
- 6 to 8 tablespoons cherry jam without juice
- 125 grams (¼ pound) small macaroons
- a little vanilla essence
- 8 sheets isinglass
- 1¼ dl (½ pægel) boiling water
- 1¼ dl (½ pægel) maraschino liqueur
- ¼ liter (1 pægel) heavy cream for decoration
- a few drops of fruit coloring

## Directions

1. Whip 1 liter of heavy cream into stiff foam.
2. Stir in the chopped citrus peel, cherry jam without juice, small macaroons, and a little vanilla essence.
3. Dissolve isinglass in boiling water and stir into the whipped cream along with the maraschino liqueur.
4. When the pudding is so stiff that it can bear the cherries and citrus peel, pour it into the wetted mold.
5. It can be made a few hours before it is to be eaten and decorated with whipped cream made from ¼ liter heavy cream, mixed with a few drops of fruit coloring.
6. Small macaroons or biscuits are served separately.

title: Svensk Knækbrød (Swedish Crisp Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 measures sweet milk
- 1/2 pound butter
- half wheat flour and half rye flour
- 2 measures good yeast
- two handfuls caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Place 2 measures sweet milk and 1/2 pound butter over heat and warm.
2. Work half wheat flour and half rye flour with 2 measures good yeast together into a dough and knead well until it begins to rise.
3. Add more flour and two handfuls caraway seeds and knead with the hands until the dough releases from the hands and the dough trough.
4. Let it rise.
5. When it has risen, punch it down.
6. Roll out a piece of dough the size of a goose egg to the thickness of a straw, trim the edges with a pastry wheel and prick with a fork so it does not puff up during baking.
7. Let the cakes rise further and bake in the oven at good heat.
8. Store in a dry place.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sveske-Grød (Prune Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ kg prunes
- Very little water
- Sugar to taste
- Sago flour

## Directions

1. Scald the prunes
2. Remove the stones before cooking, unless using stone-free prunes
3. Cook the prunes in very little water under a lid over low heat
4. When tender, sweeten them
5. Thicken with sago flour
6. Pour into glass bowls
7. Serve cold with raw cream or crème

title: Sveske-Tærte (Plum Tart)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Two thin layers of butter dough
- 1/2 kilogram (1 pound) prunes
- Sugar
- A few drops of lemon oil
- Egg for brushing
- Cream (see recipe 1120)
- Whipped cream for topping

## Directions

1. Roll out two thin layers of butter dough and cut to the size of the serving pan
2. Lattice one layer with thin strips of butter dough, brush with egg, and bake in a hot oven
3. Blanch the prunes, remove the pits, and cook them until tender in a small amount of water with sugar and a few drops of lemon oil, then chop them
4. Place this mixture along with a layer of cream between the cake layers
5. Decorate the top with whipped cream peaks

title: Sveskeblomme-Kompot (Prune Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1.5 dl water for 0.5 kg fruit

## Directions

1. Place the plums in a sieve and pour boiling water over them.
2. Cook the sugar and water to a thin syrup.
3. Give the plums only a couple of minutes of boiling, as they must remain whole when filled into jars.
4. Reduce the syrup for 6 to 8 minutes and pour immediately over the plums.
5. Fit the jars with sticks and sulfur, light them, and then seal the jars.

title: Sveskeblomme-Kompot (Prune Preserve)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- 125 grams powdered sugar
- 1.25 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Place the plums in a sieve and pour boiling water over them.
2. In the sugar and water, which have been boiled to a thin syrup, give the plums only a couple of minutes of cooking, as they must remain whole when filled into the jars.
3. Reduce the syrup for 6 to 8 minutes and pour it immediately over the plums.

title: Sveskeblommer med Arrak (Dried Plums with Arrack)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) dried plums, pitted
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) Demerara sugar
- 1/2 l (1/2 pot) water
- arrack

## Directions

1. Place the dried pitted plums over heat with the specified amount of water.
2. Remove them as soon as they begin to boil and place in a sieve.
3. Once the water has drained well, place the plums in a crock.
4. Put the sugar in a pot together with the water from the plums and boil until it becomes a syrup that hangs in threads from the spoon.
5. Pour the boiling syrup over the plums and let them sit until the next day.
6. The next day, pour everything into the pot and remove the plums as soon as they boil.
7. Reduce the syrup before pouring it over the fruit.
8. The next day, pour enough good arrack over the plums to completely cover them and seal the crocks.

title: Sveskeblommer med Arrak (Prunes with Arrack)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ kg Demerara sugar
- ½ l water
- prunes

## Directions

1. Set the dried prunes, from which the stones have been removed, over heat with the stated measure of water.
2. Remove them as soon as they begin to boil, and place in a sieve.
3. When the water has drained well, put the prunes in the jar.
4. Put the sugar in the pot together with the water from the prunes and cook to syrup, which should be smooth enough to hang in threads from the spoon; pour it boiling over the prunes.

title: Sveskeblommer med Eddike (Prunes with Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 gr Demerara sugar
- ¾ dl vinegar
- prunes
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cover the prunes with boiling water and place on a sieve.
2. When sugar and vinegar are cooked to a thin syrup, add the prunes, but not more at once than can be ensured they do not break during cooking.
3. Cook only a couple of minutes, remove with a slotted spoon and spread on flat dishes until they have cooled and a glaze has formed over them; only then are they placed in the jar.
4. A little whole cinnamon is added to the liquid, which is cooked until smooth, which takes about 1 hour.
5. Treated in this way the prunes become excellent, and both pricking and drying are avoided.
6. If the liquid boils sufficiently long, it need not be reduced more often, and the prunes are sealed.
7. Should it happen that the liquid has become thin after some days, it is only the top that needs to be poured off and reduced.

title: Sveskeblommer uden Skind og Stene (Prunes without Skin and Stones)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ kg Demerara sugar
- ½ l water
- ripe prunes
- 50 gr succade
- 50 gr candied orange peel
- peel of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Place ripe prunes in boiling water until the skin comes off easily.
2. With a knife, cut a slit lengthwise along the prunes, through which the stones are removed; these can be crushed and the kernels cooked together with the prunes.
3. Cook sugar and water to syrup together with 50 gr succade, 50 gr candied orange peel, and the peel of 1 lemon, all cut into very fine, narrow strips.
4. When the syrup is cooked and skimmed, add the prunes, but not too many at once.
5. They must only just come to a boil and remain whole when removed with a slotted spoon and placed in the jar.
6. Reduce the syrup until smooth; then pour it over the prunes and seal.
7. After a week, check them, and should the liquid be too thin, pour off only the top, which is reduced and poured hot over the prunes. At the bottom of the jar the liquid will always be sufficiently thick.

title: Sviiie-Nyrer, stegt som Bøf (Pork Kidneys Fried as Beefsteak)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork kidneys
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Onion slices
- Stock or soup
- Coloring
- Cold butter
- Boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Place kidneys in cold water
2. Remove the skin
3. Cut into long slices
4. Soak lightly and place on an inverted sieve, press in a cloth
5. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
6. Fry in butter on a fairly hot pan over brisk heat
7. A good portion of onion slices can be browned before the kidneys are fried and distributed over the fried meat
8. Deglaze the pan with a little stock or soup and a little coloring
9. Stir in a piece of cold butter
10. Pour this reduced stock over the meat
11. Serve with boiled potatoes
12. Veal kidneys can be prepared the same way

title: Svine-Kotelet (Pork Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork chops with bone
- salt
- ground pepper
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the chops from each other so that a bone follows with each one.
2. Beat them well with a meat mallet.
3. Sprinkle with salt and a little ground pepper.
4. Dip in beaten eggs.
5. Turn in grated bread.
6. Fry golden brown in butter.
7. Serve with mashed potatoes.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Svine-Koteletter (Pork Chops)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg pork shoulder
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- bread
- brown butter or lard

## Directions

1. Cut from the shoulder as mentioned for veal chops, sprinkle with salt and pepper, dip in egg and bread, and fry in brown butter or lard.
2. The chops can be served with brown butter and the gravy from the pan, or with spiced sauces and vegetables, for example: stewed cauliflower and brown butter, red cabbage and gravy sauce, browned potatoes, apple sauce and gravy sauce, potato croquettes and pickles sauce, mashed potatoes and piquant sauce, or macaroni and tomato sauce or lemon sauce.

title: Svinekødfars (Pork Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg pork
- 2 tablespoons potato flour
- 1/2 liter milk
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. The meat must be cut pork, pork tenderloin or small cuts; this is bought most cheaply and as a rule there is sufficient fat with the meat.
2. Prepare approximately 1 kg of forcemeat from 1/2 kg pork, 2 tablespoons potato flour, about 1/2 liter milk and 1 egg.

title: Svineryg-Suppe (Pork Loin Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg (8 lb) pork loin
- 1 medium head of cabbage
- 1 celery
- 4 carrots
- 20 peppercorns
- 1 bunch of herbs
- Salt

## Directions

1. Rinse the pork loin and place in a pot of cold salted water over heat
2. Once it comes to a boil and is well skimmed, add 1 large head of cabbage cut into 4 pieces, 1 celery, 4 carrots, 20 peppercorns, and a good bunch of herbs
3. Simmer for approximately 3 hours
4. When the cabbage is tender, remove it and cut away from the core into smaller pieces
5. Clean and cut the carrots into pieces and place in the tureen
6. Strain the soup and skim off the accumulated fat
7. Cut the pork loin into smaller pieces and serve hot with the soup
8. Leftover pork loin can be prepared with a good curry sauce (recipe No. 780)

title: Sweet Pears (Søde pærer)
body:
## Ingredients

- Gray pears
- 1 Pd. Melis per 1 Pd. pears
- 1 Pægl water
- vanilla
- optional: Madeira or Sherry

## Directions

1. Peel the pears; if large, cut in 2 pieces and remove the core
2. Boil the Melis to syrup with water
3. Add the pears and boil until tender
4. Remove the pears and place on a colander
5. Boil the syrup with a piece of vanilla and skim until clear and thick
6. When the syrup is cooled, pour over the pears which have been placed in preserve jars
7. Seal the jars the next day
8. It is recommended to add a little Madeira or Sherry to the syrup after the final boiling to enhance the taste
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Swiss Pudding (Swiss Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1 heaping teaspoon baking powder
- Salt
- 4 ounces or 6 large tablespoons granulated sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 egg
- 0.5 teaspoon lemon extract
- 0.5 pint milk

## Directions

1. Sift the flour together with baking powder and salt.
2. Rub the sugar and butter together well; when mixed but not creamy, gradually add the flour.
3. Make a hollow in the center of the dough and add an egg, lemon extract, and milk.
4. Mix to a soft dough, place in a form greased well with butter and sprinkled well with flour.
5. Boil for approximately 3 hours; test with a stiff straw pushed down into it; if nothing sticks to the straw, it is done.
6. Turn the pudding out of the form and serve with crème sauce.

title: Sy Sauce (Meat Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Skimmings from the roast
- Butter
- Finely chopped shallots
- Soy sauce
- Mushroom slices
- Mushroom juice

## Directions

1. Take as much skimmings from the roast as possible by pouring everything out of the roasting pan or pot when the sauce has become strong
2. Add a small piece of new butter
3. When the poured-out sauce has stood for a while, skim off all the fat from it and pour it back on the roast so that only the strength remains
4. Place this in a pan with finely chopped shallots and cook well
5. When ready to serve, remove the pan from heat and stir in a good piece of cold butter
6. This makes the sauce smooth; add a little soy sauce for color
7. You can also add strips of mushrooms and a little mushroom juice
8. This is good for all roasts

title: Sylf ide-Omelet (Sylphide Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 35 drams rice flour
- 3 measures cream or sweet milk
- 35 drams butter
- 35 drams powdered sugar
- 25 drams ground sweet almonds
- 12 egg yolks
- 1/4 pound washed currants
- 1/4 pound finely cut candied citrus peel
- 12 egg whites
- liqueur

## Directions

1. Mix 35 drams rice flour with 3 measures cream or sweet milk
2. Bake well together with 35 drams butter, 35 drams powdered sugar and 25 drams ground sweet almonds, heating the butter gently but not hot
3. Gradually stir into the mixture 12 egg yolks, first beaten white
4. Stir in 1/4 pound washed currants and 1/4 pound finely cut candied citrus peel
5. Finally fold in the 12 egg whites, first beaten to stiff peaks
6. As an alternative to the dried fruits, half a measure of liqueur can be added to the mixture
7. Bake the omelet and serve with sweet fruit sauce, liqueur can be added to taste according to preference
8. This amount makes 10-12 portions
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sylte (Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig head
- Water
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Ginger
- Finely chopped onion
- Linen cloth
- Laurel leaves
- Allspice

## Directions

1. A pig head is best; but slices of boiled, striped bacon can also in a similar way be made into pressed aspic.
2. Split the head lengthwise, take out the eyes, and clean the ears well, after which it is set over the fire in cold water.
3. When it is about to come to a boil, pour out this water and put fresh water and a little salt on it.
4. Cook it with this until it is tender, then take it up and place it in cold water.
5. Take the meat from the bones and lay it in layers in a clean linen cloth, alternating with salt, finely ground pepper, ginger and finely chopped onion.
6. Fold the linen cloth together and bind it firmly, after which the aspic is placed in a press.
7. If one wishes to have the aspic in gelée, one can reduce the soup with salt, laurel leaves and allspice, skim off the fat and pour the reduced, cold soup over the aspic that has first been cut into appropriate pieces.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Sylte (Head Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's head
- Pig's feet
- Salt
- Whole pepper
- Bay leaves
- Cloves
- Vinegar
- Pepper for serving

## Directions

1. Fleece the head, remove the ears and brain, cut off the jowls, and clean the place where they were situated
2. Remove unusable parts from inside the head, wash it and soak for a few hours in cold water so the blood can drain out
3. Boil it together with the feet in warm water and scrape well
4. Split the feet and put everything together in a pot on the heat in cold water with salt
5. Skim and cook until tender, remove, and while still warm take the bones out of the head, pick it clean, and when cold place it and the feet in a cold brine seasoned with whole pepper, bay leaves and a little cloves
6. Remove as needed for use and serve with vinegar and pepper

title: Sylte i Gelee (Head Cheese in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork head
- Veal knuckle
- Water
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Bay leaves
- Whole pepper
- Egg whites and shells

## Directions

1. Cook head and other parts with a veal knuckle in water
2. Simmer, then add salt, a little vinegar, bay leaves and whole pepper
3. Cook slowly; then remove
4. Remove bones, pick head clean and cut into smaller pieces, separate feet
5. Rinse slightly in broth and place either gathered in one pot or in several smaller ones
6. Skim fat well off the broth, which is heated over fire; if it is not clear, cook a couple of egg whites and shells in it
7. Pour it through a fine sieve over the head cheese, which is lifted a little so the aspic can penetrate everywhere
8. The head cheese is eaten cold with vinegar and pepper

title: Syltede Ananas (Preserved Pineapple)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar per pound of fruit
- Nearly 1 cup water per pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the fruit, free it from the cores and cut it into slices.
2. Place it layer by layer in the pot with the addition of nearly 1 cup water per pound of sugar.
3. As soon as it has just begun to boil, take up the fruit and spread it on platters exposed to the sun.
4. The syrup continues to boil and simmer for half an hour. Then add the fruit again and let it boil for a quarter hour.
5. Then put the fruit in crocks and pour the boiling syrup over it. Cover it with a cloth until it is cold, then place paper soaked in cognac over it, and tie up the crocks.

title: Syltede Charlotte- eller Perleløg (Pickled Pearl Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- pearl onions
- sweet milk
- cold water
- vinegar
- white pepper
- nutmeg
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the onions with the help of a silver or glass knife and place them gradually in sweet milk as they are peeled
2. Rinse them completely clean in cold water
3. Heat vinegar with a little white pepper and nutmeg over heat, boil and skim
4. Place the onions in this and cook until they are tender
5. If you want the onions to be sweet and sour, boil the vinegar the first time with sugar in the proportion of 1 pound granulated sugar to 4 pails vinegar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Syltede Citron Skaller (Preserved Lemon Peels)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lemons
- Water
- White sugar, 1 pound per pound of peels
- ¼ pint of the water the peels were cooked in

## Directions

1. Peel the lemons and remove the pulp, place them in soft water for 9 days, giving them fresh water every day
2. Put them to cook in the same water until they are so soft that a straw can be pierced through them
3. For 1 pound peels, take 1 pound white sugar, which with ¼ pint of the water the peels were cooked in, boil to a syrup
4. Give the lemon peels one cooking in this syrup; four days later boil the syrup and repeat this twice more

title: Syltede Crabebler (Preserved Crabapples)
body:
## Ingredients

- The best, red crabapples
- 12 pounds sugar per pound of fruit
- 1 small cup water per pound of fruit
- Juice of 1 lemon per 3 pounds of fruit

## Directions

1. Choose the best red crabapples. With the stems on, place them in the pot and pour warm water over them, enough to cover them.
2. Bring it to a boil and boil slowly until the skin cracks. Then cool, drain and peel them, then remove the cores from the top where the flower sits with a penknife.
3. Bring water and sugar to a boil for each pound of fruit (using 12 pounds sugar and 1 small cup water), and skim it well.
4. Then add the fruit and boil evenly under a lid until it is clear, soft and red.
5. Then take up the apples with a fish slice and spread them out on platters to cool and become firm.
6. To the syrup in the pot add the juice of 1 lemon for every 3 pounds of fruit and cook until it is smooth and clear.
7. Fill the fruit into crocks or glasses with enough room above to pour the syrup over, and when it is cold, tie them up.

title: Syltede Ferskener (paa Amerikansk) (Preserved Peaches, American Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Five pounds brown sugar
- One gallon raw cider vinegar
- Peaches
- Whole cloves
- A piece of cinnamon
- Peach stones (optional)

## Directions

1. Take 5 pounds brown sugar and one gallon raw cider vinegar; boil strongly for 30 minutes, constantly skimming until clear.
2. Throw the peaches into boiling water, immediately remove, and rub the skin off quickly with a flannel cloth.
3. Add some whole cloves and a piece of cinnamon to the boiling syrup.
4. If the stones are in the peaches, add them to the syrup and cook for 15 to 20 minutes; if the stones have been removed, place the peaches in jars and pour the hot syrup over them.
5. Place a small saucer or plate over them to keep them submerged in the syrup and tie them.
6. After four days, check them; if necessary, cook the syrup again and pour hot over them.
7. Store in a cool place while the weather is warm to prevent them from becoming sour.

title: Syltede Ferskener (Preserved Peaches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches, nearly ripe
- One pound peaches
- One pound sugar
- One-quarter pint white wine
- Peach kernels, sliced

## Directions

1. Peel the peaches and remove the stones.
2. For each pound of peaches, take 1 pound sugar, dip in water and cook to a syrup.
3. When the syrup is ready, add one-quarter pint white wine for each pound of sugar.
4. Add the peaches a few at a time, bring to a boil, and carefully remove with a fish slice; place on a colander to drain.
5. When the syrup has drained, return it to the pot and cook again.
6. Skim well, then add the kernels, sliced into thin slices.
7. Place the peaches in glass jars and pour the syrup over them.
8. If the syrup becomes watery after a few days, cook it again and pour cold over the peaches.

title: Syltede Gulerodder (Pickled Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound dark red Carrots
- 4 Lemons
- cold Water approximately 1 Quart
- 1 Pound white Sugar
- 1 Pint of water from cooking the carrots
- Juice of two Lemons
- 3 Ounces Sugar
- Lemon juice to taste
- Cognac paper

## Directions

1. Choose dark red carrots from August
2. Scrape clean, cut into slices as thick as a straw, then cut these into strips of equal thickness and weigh
3. Peel lemons very thinly so none of the white remains, then cut the peelings into thin black strips
4. Place the peelings and carrots in cold water, approximately 1 Quart, cook covered for about 1.5 hours or until carrots are soft but do not fall apart
5. Drain on a colander
6. Cook 1 pound white sugar to a thin syrup with 1 pint of the water in which the carrots were cooked
7. Add the carrots and juice of two lemons and cook about 10 minutes
8. Place in a crock and let stand two days
9. Drain on a colander and cook the syrup for 8-10 minutes with 3 ounces sugar and more lemon juice to taste
10. Place carrots in but do not cook further
11. Place in jars and cover with cognac paper and bind

title: Syltede Kirsebær (Preserved Cherries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds cherries with or without pits
- 1 quart vinegar
- 2 pounds sugar
- 3 ounces cinnamon
- ½ ounce cloves

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar together with the vinegar and spices (these crushed and tied in a piece of muslin)
2. Pour the whole hot over the berries

title: Syltede Log (Pickled Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small to medium-sized fine white Onions
- strong Brine
- new Brine
- cold Water
- white Peppercorns
- Cloves
- whole Nutmeg Flower
- Vinegar
- 1 Cup Sugar per Gallon of Vinegar

## Directions

1. Choose smaller or medium-sized fine white onions
2. Peel and place in strong brine for four days, changing the brine twice
3. Bring new brine to a boil, add onions and cook for 3 minutes
4. Place in cold water for 4 hours
5. Remove and pack in crocks, layering with white peppercorns, cloves and whole nutmeg flower
6. Cook vinegar with 1 cup sugar per gallon of vinegar and pour hot into the crocks while still warm, then bind
7. After one month the onions can be used but are better later

title: Syltede Muslinger (Pickled Mussels)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels
- Nutmeg
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Bay leaves

## Directions

1. Cook the mussels in the usual manner
2. Remove them from the shells and discard the beard and black intestine
3. Place the mussels in a crock
4. Cook the mussel broth with nutmeg, pepper, cloves and bay leaves, skim it and strain it
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Syltede Muslinger (Pickled Mussels)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels, cooked
- Nutmeg
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Bay leaves
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the mussels in the usual manner (see recipe 116)
2. Remove the mussels from their shells
3. Remove the beard and the black intestine
4. Place the mussels in a jar
5. Cook the mussel broth with nutmeg, pepper, cloves and bay leaves
6. Skim and strain the broth
7. Blend the broth with vinegar
8. Pour the mixture over the mussels
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Syltede Tomater (Pickled Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 pails vinegar
- 1 pound granulated sugar
- cinnamon sticks
- ginger
- cloves
- 2 pounds tomatoes, peeled and cut into smaller pieces or slices

## Directions

1. Boil vinegar and granulated sugar with cinnamon sticks, ginger, and cloves until a smooth syrup forms
2. Add the prepared tomatoes to the syrup
3. Boil and skim for 10 minutes
4. Pour into jars and bind when the tomatoes have cooled
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Syltede Tomater (Pickled Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 gills vinegar
- 1 pound sugar (Melis)
- cinnamon sticks
- ginger
- cloves
- 2 pounds tomatoes, not fully ripe, peeled and cut into smaller pieces or slices

## Directions

1. The tomatoes must not be fully ripe.
2. Cook 15 gills vinegar and 1 pound sugar with some pieces of cinnamon, ginger and cloves to a smooth syrup.
3. Place 2 pounds peeled tomatoes, cut into smaller pieces or slices, in it.
4. Cook and skim for 10 minutes.
5. Pour into jars and tie them when the tomatoes have cooled.
6. They go well with roast.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Syltede Æbler, Nr. 1 (Preserved Apples, No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good juicy apples
- Water
- 1 quart water per pound apples
- Cinnamon sticks
- White sugar, 1 pound per pound apples
- ½ pint of the water the apples were cooked in
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. The apples must be good and juicy
2. Peel them, cut into 4 pieces, remove the cores, and weigh them
3. Place 1 pound apples over the fire with 1 quart water and a couple of pieces of cinnamon
4. When it boils, add the apples, and when they have had 2 boilings, place them on a coarse strainer so the water can drain off
5. Boil 1 pound white sugar to syrup with ½ pint of the water in which the apples were cooked
6. Add these along with a little lemon juice, and boil very gently until they are clear, usually 5 minutes
7. Then remove the pot from the fire, let the apples stand a little in it, then remove them and place in a jar
8. If the juice is not stiff when cold, boil it a little more and pour cold over the apples
9. Seal the next day
10. These preserved apples can only keep for a couple of months

title: Syltede Æbler, Nr. 2 (Preserved Apples, No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good ripe apples
- White wine
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel good ripe apples and cut into 2 or 4 pieces
2. Give them one cooking in white wine and sugar
3. Then boil whole sugar fast and thick and pour lukewarm over the apples; the wine is not used but can be used for soup
4. They should not be placed in a narrow jar the first time, but the sauce should be poured over them in a deep dish where they do not lie much on top of each other
5. Two days later they can be placed in the jar
6. The sauce is then given one cooking and poured cold over
7. They cannot be kept as long as other sweet preserves

title: Syltede Østers (Pickled Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Salt
- Water
- Vinegar
- Whole pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the oysters and throw them into strongly boiling salted water, stew them well and place in cold water
2. Bring vinegar and whole pepper to a boil and then cool
3. Place the oysters in a stone crock; to each quart of the water in which the oysters were scalded, take one cup of vinegar, both cold, which is poured over the oysters so it stands over them

title: Syltet Græskar (Pickled Pumpkin)
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkin
- wine vinegar
- 1 pound sugar (Melis) per pound pumpkin
- ginger
- cinnamon sticks
- cloves
- cognac and white wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the pumpkin in half and then into smaller pieces.
2. Peel the skin well and remove the soft interior.
3. Cut the pieces into smaller, approximately equal-sized cubes.
4. Cook them tender in good wine vinegar until they can be pierced with a straw.
5. For each pound of pumpkin, take 1 pound sugar, dip it in cold water and cook to a thick syrup.
6. Cool it and pour cold over the pumpkin pieces.
7. Add small pieces of ginger, some cinnamon sticks and a little cloves to taste to the cold syrup.
8. Let the pumpkin stand thus for 8 days.
9. Give the syrup a brief boil, if needed with more sugar, until it becomes very smooth.
10. Pour it cold over the pumpkin pieces again.
11. Alternatively, when cooking the sugar the first time, dip it in half cognac and half white wine instead of water.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Syltet Hvidkaal (Pickled White Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Gallons Vinegar
- 1 Pint white Mustard Seeds
- 1 Ounce Allspice
- 3 Ounces Peppercorns
- 4 Ounces Ginger
- 1 Ounce Nutmeg Flower
- 1 Ounce Nutmeg
- 2 Ounces Cloves
- 2 Ounces Turmeric
- 1 Handful chopped Garlic
- 1 Handful grated Horseradish
- 4 Pounds Sugar
- 2 Ounces Celery Seeds
- 3 Lemons thinly sliced
- white Cabbage
- Brine
- Salt
- cold Vinegar
- soaked Turmeric

## Directions

1. Mix the vinegar, mustard seeds, allspice, peppercorns, ginger, nutmeg flower, nutmeg, cloves, turmeric, garlic, horseradish, sugar, celery seeds and sliced lemons together well and expose to the sun for 3 days
2. Remove the outer green leaves from the cabbage, cut into 4 pieces and cook for 3 minutes in a kettle with boiling brine
3. Remove and sieve and cover with a good layer of salt
4. Spread in the sun to dry
5. Shake off the salt and place in cold vinegar that has been colored with soaked turmeric
6. Let stand for 2 weeks
7. Then place in the spiced vinegar
8. Can be used after 5-6 weeks

title: Syltet Ingefær (Preserved Ginger)
body:
## Ingredients

- White ginger, fine and smooth
- Water
- Soda lye
- Powdered sugar, 1 pound per pound of ginger

## Directions

1. Beat the white ginger for a while to check if it is dried; discard any dried pieces, but keep the good ones
2. Weigh the good ginger and place in cold water for 24 hours
3. Prepare a weak soda lye; when the liquid is strong enough that it feels smooth to the fingers, strain off the clear liquid
4. Place the ginger in this liquid until it feels soft to the touch
5. Rinse it for 8 days, changing the water every day
6. Weigh the sugar, 1 pound for every pound of ginger
7. Boil the sugar and water clear as usual, then boil the ginger in it until it is so soft that it can be pierced with a fine straw
8. Pour together but do not seal until it has cooled

title: Syltet Melon (Pickled Melon)
body:
## Ingredients

- melon, fully grown but not ripe
- 4 gills vinegar
- 1 pound sugar (Melis)

## Directions

1. The melon must be fully grown but not ripe.
2. Peel it and cut it into 8 pieces.
3. Remove the interior.
4. Give the pieces a brief boil in water, remove and place on a clean linen cloth.
5. Meanwhile cook a sugar brine of 4 gills vinegar and 1 pound sugar.
6. Place the melon pieces in it to cook a little until the brine is as thick as syrup.
7. Place the melon pieces in a jar and pour the brine over them, as warm as it is.
8. Tie the jar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Syrer (Sorrel)
body:
## Ingredients

- sorrel
- butter
- sugar
- salt
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Remove the greenest stems from the sorrel and cook them in barely enough water if they are not very sour, but if they are quite sour use plenty of water, some of which should be poured off before they are fully cooked
2. When tender, pour off the water and press them through a sieve and place on heat with a good piece of butter, sugar to taste and a little salt

title: Syrer, Stuvede (Stewed Sorrel)
body:
## Ingredients

- sorrel
- flour
- butter
- bouillon
- spinach (optional)

## Directions

1. The treatment is nearly the same as for spinach (No. 537), but stew with flour, butter and bouillon
2. If they are very sour, they can be mixed with a little spinach
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Söd Omelet (Sweet Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- 1 tablespoon powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks and whites separately
2. Instead of salt and pepper, add powdered sugar to yolks
3. Cook the same way as simple omelet
4. While omelet cooks, clean the fire shovel and make it red-hot
5. When omelet is slid onto plate, sprinkle with powdered sugar
6. Stiffen the top with the hot iron, browning the sugar without burning it
7. Serve hot

title: Sød Kirsebær Saft (Sweet Cherry Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Damaged cherries not suitable for whole preservation
- Powdered sugar, ½ pound per pint juice

## Directions

1. All the worst berries that are not suitable for preserving whole are pitted, crushed, and wrung out in a cloth
2. Weigh the juice, place on the fire with ½ pound powdered sugar per pint juice
3. Skim it, boil well for 10 to 15 minutes and pour onto bottles

title: Søde Agurker til Steg
body:
## Ingredients

- Agurker
- Salt
- Eddike
- Sukker
- Nelliker
- Kanel
- Friske laurberblade

## Directions

1. Når agurkerne er førelede, overskaarne og udhulede, gnides de med salt og står således i 24 timer
2. Tør dem og tryk dem godt i tykke kleder
3. Skær dem i strimler, vej dem og nedlæg dem i en krukke
4. Til hver pund agurker koges 1 pint eddike, som hældes hed på dem, og hvori de atter står i 24 timer, hvorefter de igen tørres
5. Dernæst koges igen til hver pund agurker 1/2 pint frisk eddike med 1/4 pund sukker, 3 nelliker, nogle små stykker kanel og 6 a 8 friske laurberblade
6. Når det har kumuleret, kommes agurkerne deri

title: Søde Asier (Sweet Pears)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 gr Farine or powdered loaf sugar
- 1¾ dl vinegar
- 1¾ dl water
- peeled pears
- whole cloves
- cinnamon sticks

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, split them, hollow them out and then cover them repeatedly with boiling water, in which they must stand until they are cold.
2. Pour them onto a sieve and dry them in a cloth, before cutting them into strips as before.
3. Cook sugar, vinegar and water to syrup together with a few whole cloves and a couple of pieces of whole cinnamon.
4. Cook the pears in this for about 5 minutes, pour into a jar and cover.
5. After 3 to 4 days, pour off the syrup and reduce it until very smooth.
6. Pour it hot over the pears and seal.

title: Søde Asier (Sweet Pickled Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- soft water
- wine vinegar
- sugar (Melis)
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Halve the pears, hollow them out and wipe them clean.
2. Place them in a jar and pour boiling soft water over them.
3. Let them stand for 5 to 6 hours.
4. Remove them, place on a colander, and when the water has drained, press them gently with a linen cloth.
5. Bring wine vinegar to a boil and briefly boil the pears in it.
6. Remove them and place them layered in a jar on whose bottom has been spread a layer of ground sugar.
7. Between each layer of pears place a layer of sugar; the topmost layer must be sugar.
8. Pour the wine vinegar over the pears.
9. It gives a pleasant taste if the vinegar is boiled with cinnamon and cloves, but the pears will become dark.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Søde Asier til Steg (Sweet Pickled Cucumbers for Roasts)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers, peeled, halved and hollowed
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Fresh bay leaves

## Directions

1. Salt the peeled, halved and hollowed cucumbers and let them stand for 24 hours
2. Dry them and press firmly in thick cloths
3. Cut them into strips, weigh them and place them in a crock
4. For each pound of cucumbers, boil 1 pint of vinegar and pour it hot over them
5. Let them stand for 24 hours, then dry them again
6. For each pound of cucumbers, boil 1 pint fresh vinegar with 1/2 pound sugar, 3 cloves, some small pieces of cinnamon and 6 to 8 fresh bay leaves
7. When it has simmered, add the cucumbers, and when it comes to a boil again, pour it out and cover with a cloth
8. After a few days, strain off the liquid and boil it until it has simmered and is clear
9. Pour it hot over the cucumbers, cover with a cloth and tie up the next day

title: Sødt Sauce (Sweet Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- red Juice, preferably Raspberry with a little Cherry
- Water
- Sugar
- 1 Pint
- 1 Tablespoon Corn Starch
- cold Water
- Preserves or Raisins
- sliced Almonds

## Directions

1. Take red Juice, preferably Raspberry with a little Cherry, Water and Sugar to taste.
2. For 1 Pint of this take 1 Tablespoon Corn Starch, which is stirred out in cold Water and put in the Juice when it boils.
3. You may add Preserves or Raisins, also sliced Almonds in it; these last Things are boiled in the Juice before the Thickening is added.
4. The Preserve is added after it is boiled, and must not boil.
5. Such Sauce is used for most Puddings.

title: Søg-Suppe (Egg Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 plate full of chopped eggs
- butter
- 6 tablespoons flour
- meat stock

## Directions

1. When a plate full of chopped eggs is browned in butter, stir in 6 tablespoons flour; thereafter gradually add stock and let it cook for an hour
2. Strain it, skim off the fat, and give it another boil
3. Eaten with baked bread

title: Søsterkage (Sister Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound butter
- salt
- 2 cups milk
- 5 tablespoons yeast
- flour
- 0.75 pound light powdered sugar or powdered sugar
- cardamom
- lemon zest
- ground cinnamon
- cut candied peel to taste
- 12 eggs
- 1 pound good raisins

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and drain off the salt
2. In the warm butter place 2 cups milk and then 5 tablespoons yeast, and gradually pour this mixture into the flour and knead well together with 0.75 pound light powdered sugar or powdered sugar, a little cardamom, grated lemon zest, ground cinnamon, and cut candied peel to taste
3. Then add 12 eggs, 2 at a time, and when the dough is nearly done kneading, add 1 pound good raisins
4. When the dough is ready, pour it into a buttered and floured form, cover well, and set in a warm place to rise, as its quality depends essentially on its being sufficiently risen before it is placed in the oven or cake pan
5. Bake for about 1.5 hours and it should be a beautiful light brown
6. Serve with coffee and tea
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Taalmodighedskager (Patience Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds melted and clarified butter
- 5 eggs
- ¾ pound powdered sugar
- 3 pounds wheat flour
- chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Stir melted and clarified butter well with eggs and ¾ pound powdered sugar until even
2. Work in 3 pounds wheat flour
3. Spread the dough into small oblong cakes on a greased pan
4. Sprinkle with chopped almonds
5. Bake light brown at slow heat
6. When taken out, curl them over a bottle
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Tapioca Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Ounces tapioca
- 1 Pint milk
- 3 Eggs
- 0.5 Pound sugar

## Directions

1. Put the tapioca over the heat in the milk
2. Let it heat and become soft gradually, stirring often so it does not burn
3. When the grains are quite soft, beat the eggs with half a pound of sugar
4. Mix the cooked tapioca and half a pint of cold milk together
5. Bake the pudding for half an hour in a moderately warm oven
6. Serve warm or cold

title: Tapioca-Suppe (Tapioca Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L (4 pots) good, strong bouillon
- 125 g (1/4 lb) tapioca grains
- Toasted bread

## Directions

1. Sprinkle 125 g (1/4 lb) tapioca grains into 4 L (4 pots) of boiling bouillon while stirring constantly
2. Simmer for at least a quarter hour or until the grains are clear
3. Fine sago grains can be used instead of tapioca
4. Serve with toasted bread

title: Tarteletter (Tartlet Shells)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) flour
- 1/4 kg (1/2 pound) cold butter or margarine
- 1 1/2 dl (1/10 pint) cold water
- 3 egg yolks
- a pinch of salt
- dried peas for filling forms during baking

## Directions

1. Mix flour, cold butter or margarine, cold water, egg yolks, and salt together quickly and knead together
2. Place in a cloth and set in a cold place to rest for at least 1/2 hour before using
3. Roll out the dough thinly and line the inside of small fluted cake forms with it
4. Fill the forms with dried peas to prevent the dough from collapsing during baking
5. Place the forms on a baking sheet and bake at good heat until light brown
6. Pour out the peas and invert the forms to remove the shells easily

title: Tarteletter (Tartlets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough
- Peas
- Various fillings as specified

## Directions

1. Roll out the butter dough very thinly
2. Line small tartlet forms with the dough
3. Fill with peas
4. Bake until golden
5. When the tartlets are baked and cooled, fill with one of the following: Chocolate Fromage decorated with piped whipped cream, Nougat Cream served when set with pale red whipped cream roses around the edge, Rum Fromage or a set Vanilla Cream mixed with a glass of rum while still liquid
6. For Swedish Tartlets, fill the baked tartlets with compote without liquid or fruit puree, covered with Cream, with a whipped cream rose in the center

title: Te-Kringler (Tea Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 grams (1/4 lb) flour
- 175 grams (35 quintal) margarine
- 125 grams (1/4 lb) icing sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon baking soda

## Directions

1. Mix together the flour, margarine, icing sugar, egg and baking soda
2. Form into rings
3. Dip in egg and sugar
4. Bake until light brown

title: Teljekringler (Boiled Ring Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine flour
- 4 measures sweet milk
- 5 whole eggs
- 4 spoonfuls sugar
- a little crushed cardamom
- 1 spoonful dissolved yeast

## Directions

1. Work 1 pound fine flour, 4 measures sweet milk, 5 whole eggs, 4 spoonfuls sugar, a little crushed cardamom and 1 spoonful dissolved yeast together well until it shows eyes and begins to rise.
2. Form small rings from the dough and immediately place them in boiling water.
3. When they float up, remove them from the water and let them drain for a moment.
4. Place them on a buttered pan and bake in a moderately warm oven.
5. When they are removed from the oven, brush them with boiling water to make them glossy.
6. These rings can be stored for a long time without spoiling.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: The-Brod (Tea Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds flour
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 ounces raisins
- 4 ounces sugar
- 1 pint lukewarm milk
- 2 cents yeast
- butter
- cinnamon
- currants
- sliced almonds

## Directions

1. Knead 6 egg yolks, 4 ounces raisins, 4 ounces sugar, 1 pint lukewarm milk and 2 cents yeast dissolved in a little of the lukewarm milk into 2 pounds flour.
2. Knead for a good half hour and set overnight covered in a cold place.
3. Next day turn the dough out onto a baking sheet smeared with butter and set for half an hour in the heat.
4. While it stands there, pour some melted butter over the bread, and stick small pieces of butter here and there into it, then sprinkle it with sugar, crushed cinnamon, currants and sliced almonds.
5. Bake at even heat light brown for approximately half an hour.

title: The-Creme (Tea Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- strong extract of tea
- 1 quart fresh cream
- 5 ounces sugar
- 10 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Prepare and serve the same as coffee cream, only using strong tea extract instead of coffee.
2. It can be made without eggs; in that case use less tea extract for the same portion.

title: Thekage (Tea Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound butter
- 0.75 pound powdered sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 pounds rice flour
- 0.5 pound flour
- 10 quintal bitter almonds
- 4 quintal powdered cardamom or a little grated lemon zest
- 1 baking or raising powder
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Wash the butter well and stir together with 0.75 pound powdered sugar
2. Stir in 6 egg yolks, 2 at a time, then 4 pounds rice flour, 0.5 pound flour, 10 quintal bitter almonds, 4 quintal powdered cardamom or a little grated lemon zest, 1 baking or raising powder
3. Finally fold in the 6 egg whites beaten to peaks
4. Bake 1 hour at even good heat in a prepared form
5. Do not remove from the form immediately, but let it rest a moment, wrapped with a warm piece
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Thekranse (Tea Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound fine flour
- 36 quintal butter
- 12 quintal finely ground powdered sugar
- 1 egg yolk
- egg white for dipping
- coarse white powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Knead together ½ pound fine flour, 36 quintal butter, 12 quintal finely ground powdered sugar, and 1 egg yolk
2. Form into rings
3. Dip in egg white and coarse white powdered sugar
4. Bake at slow heat
