5. From this you will get about 60 rings
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Timbal (Baked in Oven)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter pastry
- Decorated pastry figures
- Brown coloring or soya
- Butter
- Water
- Paper for baking
- Ragout filling

## Directions

1. Butter a smooth mold (timbal mold) with cold butter
2. Cut figures from thinly rolled butter pastry, color with brown coloring or soya, press firmly against the mold sides, and brush with water
3. Roll out a larger sheet of pastry and cut out a bottom piece to line the mold
4. Line the sides with a thin layer of rolled pastry, pressing carefully against the figures
5. Butter a piece of paper the size of the mold
6. Place the buttered paper buttered side down in the mold

title: Timbal med Brissel (Timbal with Sweetbreads)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pairs of sweetbreads
- Butter
- Truffles, sliced
- Veal forcemeat, approximately 2½ centimeters thick
- Sweetbread ragout
- Mushroom sauce
- Egg yolks
- Salad or parsley
- Sweetbread cutlets
- Breaded tomatoes

## Directions

1. Use a pudding mold without a center post if a timbal mold is not available
2. Butter the mold well with cold butter and decorate with truffle slices pressed firmly against the sides
3. Line the mold with a layer of veal forcemeat approximately 2½ centimeters thick, pressing it firmly against the truffles with a spoon dipped in hot water without disturbing the truffles
4. Fill the mold with sweetbread ragout prepared as recipe No. 6, cutting the sweetbreads into pieces of at least 2½ centimeters
5. Cover the ragout with a layer of forcemeat and place a piece of buttered paper on top
6. Put the lid on and cook like any other pudding for approximately 1 hour
7. Turn the timbal out onto the serving platter, let it stand briefly, then carefully lift the mold and remove any butter that has collected on the platter
8. Serve with mushroom sauce held back from the ragout, thinned and bound with egg yolks
9. Garnish with salad or parsley, sweetbread cutlets, and breaded tomatoes

title: Timbal med Fiske-Filet (Timbal with Fish Fillet)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 to 8 plaice
- Butter
- Flour
- Fish stock made from fish bones
- Egg yolks
- Madeira or Graves wine
- Mushrooms
- Lemon
- Tomato slices

## Directions

1. Prepare a timbal mold as described in the general timbal instructions
2. Arrange cooked fish fillets high in the center of the timbal
3. Make mushroom sauce from butter and flour, thin with fish stock made from fish bones
4. Bind the sauce with egg yolks and add Madeira or Graves wine
5. Pour the mushroom sauce over the fish fillets
6. Decorate the top of the timbal with mushrooms
7. On the serving platter, arrange a wreath of fried fish fillets decorated with lemon and raw tomato slices
8. Different types of fish can be used, either as a stew or as fillets

title: Timbal med Fiskefars og Filet (Timbal with Fish Forcemeat and Fillet)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 to 6 plaice
- Butter
- Fish forcemeat
- Fish fillets
- Wine
- Egg yolks
- Crayfish
- Optional: mushrooms, lobster, crayfish extract, mussels, shrimp, asparagus, or tomato puree

## Directions

1. Butter the mold and line it with fish forcemeat
2. Fill the center with fillets prepared as described in recipe No. 37
3. Treat and cook as instructed in recipe No. 48
4. Thin the sauce from the fillets and bind with eggs, adding more wine
5. Decorate the timbal with crayfish
6. The sauce can be varied in many different ways, such as with mushrooms, lobster, crayfish, lobster and crayfish extract, mussels, shrimp and asparagus, or tomato puree

title: Timbal med Høns (Timbal with Chicken)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 chickens
- Butter
- Cooked, salted beef tongue, sliced
- Fish or veal forcemeat
- Chicken ragout

## Directions

1. Prepare the mold as in recipe No. 48
2. Instead of truffles, use slices of cooked, salted beef tongue
3. Line the mold with either fish or veal forcemeat
4. Fill the center with a good chicken ragout prepared as recipe No. 35
5. Cover with forcemeat and paper and cook as the previous recipe
6. Serve with the same sauce used for the ragout, but thinned

title: Toast
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Bread
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the eggs for 6 to 7 minutes, as they must not be completely hard-boiled
2. Cover with cold water, shell and cut into slices
3. Spread hot toasted bread with butter and place the egg slices on it
4. Sprinkle with salt and pepper

title: Tobiser (Fried Smelts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- small fish
- salt
- flour
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- Palmin (cooking fat)
- parsley
- lemon
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and sprinkle with a small amount of salt and let it stand briefly.
2. Dry the smelts, dip them in flour, then in egg and breadcrumbs, and fry in Palmin.
3. Arrange them garnished with parsley and lemon.
4. Place a dish of soft butter in the center of the platter, which can be either Tartar or maître d'hôtel butter.
5. Alternatively use browned butter and boiled potatoes.

title: Toddy
body:
## Ingredients

- Spirits
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Mix spirits, water, and sugar together. Without sugar, the drink is called 'Grog'.

title: Tomat-Gelé (Tomato Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kg ripe red tomatoes
- 625 gr sugar
- 2 dl water per 2 kg tomatoes
- 2.25 dl water per 0.5 kg fruit juice

## Directions

1. Wipe the ripe red tomatoes and place over heat with 0.5 l water per 2 kg tomatoes.
2. Cook until they can be easily passed through a sieve.

title: Tomat-Gelé (Tomato Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg (3 pounds) stone sugar
- 3/4 dl (3/4 cup) water for 1/2 kg (1 pound) fruit juice
- 2 kg (4 pounds) ripe red tomatoes
- 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) water to 2 kg (4 pounds) tomatoes

## Directions

1. Wipe the ripe red tomatoes and place them over heat with 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) water to 2 kg (4 pounds) tomatoes.
2. Cook until they can be completely mashed with ease.
3. Pour them into a jelly cloth.
4. The juice that has run through by the next day is weighed.
5. Cook sugar and water as described in recipe 93.
6. Pour the juice into the sugar after it has been cooked completely dry.
7. Cook for 10 minutes, then pour into jelly cups and bind as soon as it has partially set.

title: Tomat-Marmelade (Tomato Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes
- Powdered sugar (1/4 kg to 1/2 kg tomatoes)

## Directions

1. Wipe the tomatoes dry, cut them in 4 parts and cook over low heat.
2. When they are completely cooked down, press the pulp through a coarse sieve and pour it back into the pot to be cooked together with the sugar until the jam is as stiff as desired.
3. Fill it into jars, cover with paper and tie up.

title: Tomat-Puré (Tomato Purée)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr (0.25 S) salt to 3 kg (6 S) tomatoes
- Beautiful, red tomatoes without bruised spots
- Salicylic acid

## Directions

1. Dry beautiful, red tomatoes without bruised spots, cut them once through and place them gradually in the preserving pot, sprinkled with salt
2. As soon as they are cooked out, rub the mash through a coarse sieve and pour it back into the pot to just bring to a boil
3. Dissolve 1 teaspoon of salicylic acid in the purée after it is lifted from the heat
4. Pour it boiling onto jars or bottles and bind immediately with parchment paper
5. If salicylic acid is added, the jars only need to be bound with parchment paper, and then the Hercules jar is not necessary, but if the salicylic acid is omitted, the tomato purée must absolutely go into canning jars and be canned, otherwise it is ruined
6. Cook for 15 to 20 minutes

title: Tomat-Salat (Tomato Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- raw tomatoes
- oil
- tarragon vinegar or lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- lettuce leaves
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Toss slices of raw tomatoes in a sauce of oil, tarragon vinegar or lemon juice, salt, pepper and chopped parsley
2. Lay the tomato slices in a heap in the salad bowl, garnish it with lettuce leaves and let the heart leaves of a head of lettuce form the top figure
3. Hard-boiled eggs, cut into slices, are stood on edge along the sides of the salad

title: Tomat-Sauce (Tomato Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Plain white sauce
- Tomato puree
- Ground white pepper
- Salt
- Sugar
- Wine

## Directions

1. Stir tomato puree into a plain white sauce to taste and bring it to a boil
2. Season the sauce with ground white pepper, salt, sugar and wine
3. It can be served with dishes of boiled and fried fish as well as with boiled fowl, veal, and lamb
4. The sauce does not need to be thickened with egg

title: Tomat-Sauce (Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tomatoes
- salt
- mustard
- pepper
- allspice
- cloves
- gray pepper
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut ripe tomatoes in half and bring to a boil.
2. Press them through a sieve to separate skin and seed.
3. For every 3 pots of strained tomatoes take 4 tablespoons salt, 3 tablespoons mustard, 2 tablespoons pepper, 1 tablespoon allspice, 4 tablespoons cloves, and 4 tablespoons gray pepper, as well as 3 pints wine vinegar.
4. Boil the whole for 4 hours and then fill into hot bottles, which are immediately corked.
5. The sauce is used to strengthen other sauces.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Tomat-Smør (Tomato Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ kg (½ pound) stirred butter
- 4 to 6 tablespoons thick, strongly reduced tomato purée
- Paprika

## Directions

1. ¼ kg stirred butter is given 4 to 6 tablespoons of thick, strongly reduced tomato purée and a little paprika.
2. Served with roasted meat and fish.

title: Tomat-Suppe 1 (Tomato Soup 1)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 S) large, red tomatoes
- 5 L (5 pots) broth
- 0.25 kg (0.5 S) rice
- salt
- 2 onions
- Sherry
- paprika
- finely chopped parsley
- fish balls, flour balls, or small pieces of leeks cooked in the soup (optional)

## Directions

1. Scald 0.25 kg (0.5 S) rice and cook it tender in water with salt as a thin porridge.
2. Cook the tomatoes together with 2 onions in some of the broth and rub them through a sieve together with the rice porridge and the remaining broth.
3. Place it over heat and bring to a boil and season with Sherry, paprika and finely chopped parsley.
4. Fish balls, flour balls, or small pieces of leeks cooked in the soup can be added to the tomato soup.

title: Tomat-Suppe 2 (Tomato Soup 2)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 L (5 pots) white spare broth
- 125 grams (0.25 S) butter
- 125 grams (0.25 S) flour
- 1 whisk
- tomato purée
- paprika
- wine
- cauliflower, fish balls, flour balls, or loose cooked rice (optional)

## Directions

1. Melt 125 grams (0.25 S) butter, and bake the same amount of flour in it, which is thinned with the broth in which a whisk has been cooked.
2. Add tomato purée (see the preserve book) to the soup to taste as well as paprika and a little wine.
3. It can be served with cauliflower, fish balls, flour balls, or loose cooked rice.

title: Tomater (Preserved Tomatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 medium tomatoes
- 0.5 kg powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoon whole white pepper
- a few fresh bay leaves
- 10 peeled shallots
- raw vinegar

## Directions

1. Layer the tomatoes in a jar alternately with the powdered sugar, whole white pepper, bay leaves, and peeled shallots.
2. Pour enough raw vinegar over the tomatoes to cover them.
3. Cover the jar and let stand for 3-4 days.
4. Drain off the vinegar and heat it to boiling.
5. Pour the hot vinegar over the tomatoes.
6. Cover with cloth and seal the jar the next day with parchment paper.
7. Serve with meat as a pickle.

title: Tomater (Preserved Tomatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes
- Salt (1/2 kilogram per 7 1/2 liters of water)
- Water

## Directions

1. Make a good brine of salt and water, bring to a boil, then cool completely.
2. Place well-dried tomatoes in the cooled brine; to keep them well, it is necessary that some of the stem remains attached and that they are kept submerged under the brine with the aid of a light press.
3. Tomatoes can be preserved in salt brine until well into summer.
4. Even if a fungal growth forms on them toward spring, this is of no consequence—simply remove it and the tomatoes are still good.
5. They can be used in soup, ragout, sauce, etc., but the dish for which they are used should not be salted too much, since the tomatoes are already salted.

title: Tomater (Tomatoes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Firm, not overripe tomatoes
- Cold salt brine
- 10 gr (2 Kvint) salt to 1 l (1 Pot) boiling water
- Icing sugar

## Directions

1. Dry firm, not overripe tomatoes and place them in the canning jars
2. Pour the cold salt brine, 10 gr (2 Kvint) to 1 l (1 Pot) boiling water, and a very little icing sugar over them
3. Close the jars with ring and clamp and cook in the kettle for 15 minutes
4. Cool in the kettle or under a woolen blanket

title: Tomater (Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tomatoes
- boiling water
- sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over ripe tomatoes
2. Peel them and serve with sugar and vinegar to taste
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Tomater med Eddike (Tomatoes with Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar or Demerara sugar
- 1 kg (2 S) tomatoes
- 1/2 kg (1 S) powdered sugar
- Vinegar
- Cinnamon stick pieces
- Whole cloves
- 1/4 kg (1/2 pound) sugar
- Salicylic acid, dissolved

## Directions

1. Select large, partly ripe tomatoes and dry them in cloth, then cut them in half
2. Dip 1/2 kg (1 S) powdered sugar directly into vinegar and place in a pot to cook down to a syrup
3. Add as many tomatoes at once as can lie side by side without stacking on top of each other
4. Shortly after the tomatoes begin to boil in the syrup, the skin will split; then immediately remove them and place in glasses with a few pieces of whole cinnamon and some whole cloves
5. Add the remaining 1/4 kg (1/2 pound) sugar to the liquid and boil it down until fairly thick
6. Pour the boiling liquid over the tomatoes, then pour a little dissolved salicylic acid over them as described in recipe Nr. 81
7. Seal the glasses immediately

title: Tomater med Eddike (Tomatoes with Vinegar)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg soft sugar
- 1 kg tomatoes

## Directions

1. Large partly ripe tomatoes are dried in a cloth and cut in half
2. 1/2 kg soft sugar is dipped directly into vinegar and put in pot to cook to syrup
3. Then as many tomatoes as can lie side by side without lying on top of each other are added
4. Shortly after the tomatoes are put in the syrup and begin to cook the skin bursts and they are then removed immediately and placed in glasses with a few pieces of whole cinnamon and some whole cloves
5. The juice is mixed with the remaining 1/4 kg sugar and reduced until fairly thick

title: Tomater med Ingefær (Tomatoes with Ginger)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 Q) crushed powdered sugar
- 25 gr (5 Kv.) whole ginger
- 3/4 dl (1/4 Pægl) vinegar
- 1/2 kg (1 Q) tomatoes
- Rum with dissolved salicylic acid

## Directions

1. Peel the skin from very large ripe tomatoes, then section them like oranges
2. Layer them with sugar in a crock and pour vinegar over them
3. Let them sit for one day
4. Pour off the syrup and boil it for 10 minutes together with the blanched and scraped ginger
5. Pour the boiling liquid over the tomatoes
6. The next day, pour off the liquid and boil it again
7. On the third day, remove the tomatoes, place them in jars, and pour the boiling, newly reduced liquid over them
8. Pour a generous amount of rum with dissolved salicylic acid into each jar (1/2 teaspoon to 2 1/2 to 3 kg (5 to 6 S) tomatoes)
9. Seal immediately

title: Tomater med Vanille (Tomatoes with Vanilla)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg (1 tb) crushed powdered sugar
- 1 1/4 dl (3/4 Pægl) water
- 1/4 vanilla bean
- 1/2 kg (1 S) tomatoes
- Vinegar
- Salicylic acid

## Directions

1. Blanch beautiful, ripe tomatoes and peel away the skin
2. Place them over heat with enough vinegar to cover them
3. Give them only one boil and place on a sieve to let the vinegar drain off
4. Boil the sugar, water, and vanilla together
5. Give the tomatoes one boil in this syrup, being careful to keep them whole as they will be placed in the jars
6. If the liquid is not thick enough, reduce it further before pouring over the fruit
7. Seal as soon as the preserve is poured into the jars
8. Dissolve 1/2 teaspoon salicylic acid in some of the liquid for every 3 kg (6 Q) fruit

title: Tomater, Stuvede (Stewed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tomatoes
- flour
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. To remove the skin from ripe tomatoes, pour boiling water over them, then drain it off at once and cover with cold water
2. When peeled, stew them with flour, butter and pepper in an enameled pot or earthenware vessel
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Tomato Catchup
body:
## Ingredients

- good ripe Tomatoes
- boiling Water
- Salt
- ground Mustard
- short Pepper
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Cayenne Pepper
- white Wine or Cider Vinegar

## Directions

1. Choose good ripe tomatoes
2. Throw in boiling water and rub through a coarse sieve to remove skin and seeds
3. When cooled to each gallon add 4 Tablespoons salt, 3 tablespoons ground mustard, 2 tablespoons short pepper, 1 tablespoon allspice, 4 tablespoons cloves, 4 tablespoons cayenne pepper and 1 pint white wine or cider vinegar
4. Simmer gently for 4 hours
5. Place on bottles and stopper well

title: Tomato Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pint Tomatoes
- 1 Ounce Carrot
- 1 Ounce Onion
- 1 Ounce salt Meat
- 1 Bay leaf
- 4 Cloves
- 1 Teaspoon Salt
- 1/4 Teaspoon Pepper
- 1 Gill Soup, Syrup or Water

## Directions

1. In a thick Saucepan or Casserole put 1 Pint Tomatoes, 1 Ounce Carrot and the same amount of Onion sliced, further 1 Ounce salt Meat cut into small pieces, 1 Bay leaf, 4 Cloves, 1 Teaspoon Salt, 1/4 Teaspoon Pepper and 1 Gill Soup, Syrup or Water.
2. Boil slowly for about one Hour and strain through a Sieve with a Spoon.

title: Tomato-Suppe (Tomato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart tomatoes
- 1 quart water
- 1 quart milk
- butter
- salt
- red pepper
- 1 teaspoon saleratus
- crackers

## Directions

1. Boil the tomatoes in the water
2. Heat the milk to boiling point in a bowl set over a pot of hot water
3. When the tomatoes are soft, add 1 teaspoon of saleratus, which will cause strong effervescence
4. It is best to place the pot first in a pan or other vessel to prevent spillage
5. When it has cooled again, add the milk, butter, and seasonings
6. The soup becomes better when more milk than water is used, and cream instead of butter is used
7. Serve with crackers

title: Top-Øllebrød (Topped Beer Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beer prepared as in Norsk Øllebrød
- 1 pound grated rye bread
- 20 drams browned butter
- a quarter pound powdered sugar
- a little ground cinnamon
- a little grated lemon peel
- half a quarter pound washed currants

## Directions

1. Prepare the beer as in Norsk Øllebrød
2. For the bread topping: bake 1 pound of grated rye bread in 20 drams of light brown browned butter in a pan together with a quarter pound of powdered sugar, a little ground cinnamon, a little grated lemon peel, and half a quarter pound of washed currants until it resembles a good dough and is almost dry
3. Let it cool slightly in the pan, and when half-cold, press it into small cups that have been moistened inside
4. When cold, loosen it around the edges with a knife and turn out onto a plate
5. Serve the bread topping with the beer soup
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Torsk (Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- salt
- cold water
- vinegar
- boiled potatoes
- melted butter or mustard sauce or sour butter sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the cod thoroughly with salt and cold water
2. If the liver is beautifully white and the roe is firm, cook both with the fish
3. Cut the cleaned fish into suitable pieces, sprinkle with salt, and let it rest for a quarter hour
4. Place it in boiling water with salt and a couple of spoonfuls of vinegar
5. Cook thoroughly, but not so much that the meat falls from the bones
6. Remove carefully and arrange on a platter
7. Serve with freshly peeled boiled potatoes and melted butter, or mustard sauce, or sour butter sauce
8. The head is considered the best piece
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Torsk i Gratinskaller (Cod in Gratin Shells)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cod fish
- butter
- flour
- fish stock
- egg yolks
- white pepper, ground
- salt
- wine (Sherry or Graves)
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. The fish is cooked and picked from the bones in larger pieces while still warm.
2. Butter and flour are baked and thinned with fish stock to a fairly smooth sauce, which is bound with a couple of egg yolks.
3. To this is added ground white pepper, salt, wine (either Sherry or Graves) and a little lemon juice.
4. The mixture is placed in the shells and baked. Salmon, pike and halibut can be prepared in a similar manner.

title: Torsk med Tomat-Puré i Gratinskaller (Cod with Tomato Purée in Gratin Shells)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cod fish
- butter
- flour
- fish stock
- tomato purée
- wine

## Directions

1. The cod is prepared as in the previous recipe. If the sauce is well smooth, it does not need to be bound with egg yolks, but only tomato purée and a glass of wine are added.

title: Torsk paa Fad (Cod au Gratin)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- small cod
- salt
- butter
- flour
- fish stock from fish heads
- egg yolks
- shallots
- lemon juice
- white pepper
- crushed hardtack (Tvebak)
- puff pastry strips

## Directions

1. Clean small cod, cut them in pieces, and cut off the heads. After rinsing the fish, sprinkle with salt.
2. Let them stand with salt for about an hour, then rinse the fish pieces and place them on a slightly deep dish that can withstand heat in the oven.
3. Make the sauce from butter and flour, which are baked and thinned with fish stock cooked from the fish heads.
4. Bind it with a few egg yolks and season with shallots, lemon juice, salt, and ground white pepper to taste.
5. Pour the sauce over the fish, which is sprinkled with crushed hardtack and drizzled with melted butter.
6. Place the dish in a warm oven for a good quarter hour.
7. Serve the fish dish on the platter, which is garnished with puff pastry strips.

title: Torsk à la norvegienne (Cod à la Norvegienne)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cod
- Salt
- 1.5 pints cream
- About 3 pounds butter
- 1 to 2 ounces flour

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, rinse and cut into slices, which are placed in cold water with 1 pound fine salt for 6 hours.
2. Boil 8 quarts water separately with 12 ounces salt. Place the fish in it and bring to a boil, then remove the kettle from the heat and let it stand for 5 minutes.
3. Arrange and serve with sauce made from the above ingredients, which are whisked together during boiling and strained.

title: Torske-Filet (Fried Cod Fillets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- very small cod
- salt
- flour
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- Palmin (cooking fat)
- parsley
- lemon
- capers sauce
- mushroom sauce
- tomato sauce
- chutney sauce
- butter
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. From very small cod, cut off the head and remove the innards; scrape the fish, split it, clean it, and cut off the fins.
2. Rinse the fillets several times in cold water and sprinkle with a little salt.
3. After drying in a fish cloth, dip them in flour, then in egg and breadcrumbs, and fry in Palmin until crispy and nicely brown.
4. Arrange them immediately, as they otherwise become soft.
5. As soon as the fish is cleaned, cook the heads and backbones for the sauce, which is prepared while the fish is frying.
6. Garnish the fish with parsley and slices of fresh tomatoes.

title: Torske-Kaviar med Karse-Smør (Cod Roe with Watercress Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod roe
- Watercress butter (butter mixed with chopped watercress)
- Butter
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Remove the roe from the tin or jar and press it into a couple of small, moistened molds or cups
2. Turn it out onto plates and decorate these small puddings with watercress butter
3. Decorate with piped butter and place small rosettes of it on lemon slices, which are laid around the roe
4. It should be very cold

title: Torske-Rogn-Koteletter (Cod Roe Cutlets)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cod roe
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- pepper
- butter or Palmin (cooking fat)
- lemon
- pickled cucumber
- tomato slices
- cucumber salad

## Directions

1. The roe should be cooked, and the membrane is removed from the cold roe, which is cut into slices, not too thin, dipped in egg and breadcrumbs mixed with salt and pepper, and fried in butter or Palmin.
2. Home-cooked cod roe is treated the same way and served freshly fried on open-faced sandwiches, just as one serves fried fish.
3. Top with lemon, pickled cucumber, or tomato slices topped with cucumber salad.

title: Torske-Suppe med Champignons (Cod Soup with Mushrooms)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 L (2 pots) fish broth
- 2 L (2 pots) good white meat broth
- 1 good bunch
- some carrots
- 1 celery
- 125 gr (1/4 S) butter
- 125 gr (1/4 S) flour
- 1/2 kg (1 S) fresh or preserved mushrooms
- 5 egg yolks
- ground white pepper
- chopped parsley
- Graves or sherry
- lemon juice
- small fish balls

## Directions

1. Mix the fish broth and meat broth together, and cook 1 good bunch, some carrots and 1 celery in it.
2. Melt 125 gr (1/4 S) butter, and bake 125 gr (1/4 S) flour in it, which is thinned with the strained broth.
3. Add 1/2 kg (1 S) fresh or preserved mushrooms to the soup and bring to a boil, after which it is thickened with 5 egg yolks and seasoned with ground white pepper, chopped parsley, Graves or sherry and lemon juice.
4. Small fish balls are added to the soup.

title: Torske-Suppe med Tomat-Puré (Cod Soup with Tomato Purée)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 L (2 pots) fish broth
- 2 L (2 pots) good white meat broth
- tomato purée

## Directions

1. Prepare as No. 418.
2. Instead of mushrooms, add tomato purée to taste; but this soup does not need to be thickened.

title: Tournedos (Small Beef Filet Pieces)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Small beef pieces cut from the filet
- Salt
- Pepper
- Brown butter
- Gravy
- Vegetables for garnish

## Directions

1. Small beef pieces cut from the filet, which are altogether handled, fried and served like French Beef No. 134.
2. Tournedos can be garnished with vegetables.

title: Tranebær (Cranberries)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 gr (3/4 S) powdered sugar or Demerara sugar
- 2 1/2 dl (1 Pægl) water
- 1/2 kg (1 tt) fruit

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and water to a thin syrup
2. Add the cleaned and rinsed berries and cook until they change color
3. Remove them and place in a crock, then reduce the liquid until very thick
4. Mix the berries with the liquid
5. Pour into jars and seal immediately
6. American cranberries can be preserved the same way

title: Transparent Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 0.5 pound sugar
- 0.25 pound butter
- 3 tablespoons cream
- 0.5 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Divide the sugar in two parts, stir half into the butter and the other half into the egg yolks
2. Mix in the cream, half a teaspoon vanilla, and fold in the beaten egg whites
3. This mixture is enough for 2 pies
4. Line the molds preferably with fine dough, then pour in the mixture and bake in a not too hot oven

title: Tremont-Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 3/8 pints warm milk
- 1 tablespoon fat
- 2 tablespoons white sugar
- salt
- 1/2 cake compressed yeast or 1 cup homemade yeast
- 2 quarts sifted flour
- melted butter
- 1/2 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Make a well in the center of 2 quarts sifted flour.
2. Add 1 3/8 pints warm milk, 1 tablespoon fat, 2 tablespoons white sugar, salt, and 1/2 cake compressed yeast or 1 cup homemade yeast, and blend together to form a dough.
3. Let it rise. When light, knead again and set to rise again.
4. When risen again, knead lightly and roll out 1 inch thick.
5. Cut with a biscuit cutter; in the absence of one, use a small glass.
6. Dampen the corners slightly with melted butter and fold together with the point to the center.
7. Place on a bread pan with about 1 inch spacing.
8. Let them rise about half an hour in a warm place, then bake in a quick oven for 10 to 15 minutes.
9. If making for afternoon tea, set the dough in the morning. If for breakfast, set around 9 p.m., and as soon as you rise in the morning, add 1/2 teaspoon soda. They will then be ready for baking. They are considered very delicate when well made.

title: Tricolore
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg chocolate
- 1 L (1 pot) sweet milk
- 6 sheets isinglass
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) heavy cream
- cherry jam
- small cakes

## Directions

1. Cook chocolate with 1 L sweet milk.
2. After removing from heat, dissolve 6 sheets isinglass in it.
3. Pour the chocolate and stir until it begins to set.
4. Fill into a glass bowl and let become completely cold.
5. Before serving, cover with whipped cream and decorate with cherry jam.
6. Serve small cakes on the side.

title: Trifle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound macarons with some bitter almonds
- 1 pint good wine
- preserved fruit
- whipped cream
- vanilla
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place 1 pound of macarons, in which there are some bitter almonds, in a deep dish, not too thickly, and cover with 1 pint of good wine.
2. Let them stand covered for one hour, but turn them when they have stood half the time.
3. Then take them out of the wine, place them closely together in the dish in which they are to be served, and cover with preserved fruit and then with stiff whipped cream mixed with vanilla and sugar.

title: Trifle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound Macaroons with some bitter almonds
- 1 Pint good wine
- Jam
- Whipped cream
- Vanilla
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Place the macaroons in a deep dish, not too crowded, and cover with 1 pint good wine
2. Let them stand covered for one hour, turning them after half the time
3. Remove them from the wine and layer them tightly in the dish in which they shall be served
4. Cover with jam and then with stiff whipped cream mixed with vanilla and sugar

title: Trifles
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- macarons
- sherry or rum
- cream (vanilla, liqueur, or with lemon or orange essence)
- whipped cream
- jam (optional in this English version)

## Directions

1. Place some macarons dipped in sherry or rum on the bottom of a glass bowl.
2. Pour a cream over them, which can be vanilla or liqueur cream, or cream with lemon or orange essence.
3. Over the set cream, layer whipped cream.
4. Note: In this English version, jam is omitted, though it is generally layered with macarons and cream in the traditional recipe.

title: Tréffli (Trifle)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds small macaroons
- 1 pint Madeira
- jam or preserves
- 1 pot cream
- 10 egg yolks
- 3 quintals isinglass
- half a pint water
- half a pound white sugar
- vanilla
- 1 pint cream
- colored sugar crystals

## Directions

1. Lay 5 pounds small macaroons in a bowl, pour over 1 pint Madeira and let stand covered for a couple of hours until they are thoroughly soaked but not dissolved.
2. Place them in a glass bowl and cover with a layer of jam.
3. Pour over a cream made of 1 pot cream, 10 egg yolks, 3 quintals isinglass dissolved in half a pint water, half a pound white sugar, and vanilla prepared as directed in No. 272.
4. Whip 1 pint cream to stiff peaks, spread over the whole, and sprinkle with colored sugar crystals.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Trøffel-Sauce (Truffle Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- Madeira
- bouillon
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. Rinse the truffles, peel them, and cut into small pieces.
2. Bring to a boil in Madeira and bouillon.
3. Add to a smooth brown sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Trüffel-Sauce (Truffle Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) strong gravy sauce
- 2 tablespoonfuls finely cut truffles
- Madeira

## Directions

1. Take 2 tablespoonfuls of finely cut truffles for 1/2 L of strong gravy sauce
2. Steam the truffles in Madeira before adding them to the sauce

title: Tunger (Sole)
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- brown butter
- lemon slices
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean and treat the sole as flatfish are treated
2. Fry them, then serve with brown butter, lemon slices, and boiled potatoes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Tunger (Tongues)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tongues
- Brown butter
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. They are best in spring and early summer.
2. Serve fried with brown butter, in which a little lemon juice is pressed.

title: Tutti-Frutti Nr. 1 (Rumtopf)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 kg (10 S) crushed powdered sugar
- 1 bottle Jamaica rum
- 5 kg (10 S) fruit: strawberries (1/2 kg (1 S) berries and 1/2 kg (1 S) sugar), raspberries, various types of cherries (pitted), apricots (halved and pitted), peaches (skin removed and pitted), mirabelles (halved and pitted), Reine-Claude plums (halved and pitted), prune plums (halved and pitted), melon (peeled and cut into small pieces), mulberries, blackberries

## Directions

1. Select fruits that are completely spotless and not overripe
2. Do not peel them until just before layering with sugar and rum
3. Begin with strawberries: place 1/2 kg (1 S) berries and 1/2 kg (1 S) sugar plus some rum on the bottom of a large crock and stir once so the sugar can dissolve
4. Cover the crock with a well-fitting lid and wrap with parchment paper
5. Continue layering with other berries and fruits throughout the summer in the same manner
6. Stir occasionally
7. After one month from when the last fruit was added, it is ready for use
8. Serve with roasts as a compote or use as a dessert
9. The liquid that forms can be strained through a fine sieve if not used with the fruit and served as an aromatic liqueur

title: Tvebakker (Twice-Baked Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 whole eggs
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 6 ounces washed butter
- half a pint milk
- 1 cent yeast
- 1 pound fine, sifted flour

## Directions

1. Whip the 4 whole eggs well with 3 tablespoons sugar.
2. Melt the 6 ounces butter and add half a pint milk in which 1 cent yeast is dissolved and 1 pound fine, sifted flour, which is all stirred together with the whipped eggs.
3. Knead it properly and set it aside to rise.
4. Then knead the dough a little, but without more flour, and cut it into small pieces, which are rolled into round buns and placed on a baking sheet, covered with a thin cloth and set aside again in the heat.
5. When they have risen, salt them in the oven and bake for half a dozen minutes, then remove the sheet, and when the buns are cold, cut them across with a thin, sharp knife, place them with the cut side up and put them in a warm, not hot oven, until the upper side is light brown.

title: Tvebakker A (Twice-baked Biscuits A)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.5 pounds fine wheat flour
- 1 pound butter
- 1 measure sweet milk
- spices and sugar to taste
- 16 quints pressed yeast

## Directions

1. Dissolve 16 quints pressed yeast in 1 measure sweet milk.
2. Work together 3.5 pounds fine wheat flour, 1 pound butter, the milk with yeast, spices and sugar to taste well until the dough releases from the hands.
3. Let it rise a little.
4. Form small buns, place on a pan and let them rise a little before baking.
5. Continue with the treatment described in Nr. 558.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Tvebakker B (Twice-baked Biscuits B)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds fine wheat flour
- 2 measures cream
- 15 quints melted butter
- 1 pound sugar
- 2 tablespoons yeast

## Directions

1. Warm 2 measures cream slightly.
2. Work together 2 pounds fine wheat flour, the warm cream, 15 quints melted butter, 1 pound sugar and 2 tablespoons yeast well until the dough releases from the hands.
3. Let it rise in a warm place.
4. When the dough seems sufficiently risen, form it into small, round buns and place them on a pan.
5. Cover with a thin cloth and let them rise again in a warm place.
6. Place them in the oven and bake light brown, then remove and let cool.
7. When the buns are cold, cut them in half with a sharp knife and place them back on the pan with the cut side up.
8. Place them in a warm, but not too hot oven, until the cut side is light brown.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Tydske Müred Pickles (German Mixed Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young fresh asparagus
- Small carrots
- Cauliflower heads
- Green beans
- Artichokes
- White beans
- Turkish wheat or corn
- Shallots
- Young parsley roots
- Green Spanish pepper pods
- Pearl onions
- Brussels sprouts
- Small cucumbers
- Very small whole decorative gourds
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Water
- Red pepper
- French tarragon vinegar
- Spices

## Directions

1. Prepare and clean all the pieces
2. Cut the asparagus into pieces, keep the carrots and parsley roots with a little tuft of heart leaves
3. Separate the cauliflower by the skin, trim the beans and cut into pieces if large
4. Slice the artichokes, scald the large beans and clean them by the skin
5. The corn should be the smallest, and the washed cucumbers are salted and placed in water overnight
6. Shell the shallots and pearl onions by the outer skin and free the Brussels sprouts of the outer loose leaves
7. Boil everything, each separately, with vinegar and water, first sprinkling well with salt, drain on a sieve
8. Arrange according to colors with strips of red Spanish pepper in glasses
9. Pour over with French tarragon vinegar, which has been heated with some spices and cooled, filling the glasses
10. Close with stoppers and paper, and dip in sealing wax

title: Tynd Druesaft (Thin Grape Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Grapes, completely ripe
- Water
- White or brown sugar, 1 pound per quart of juice

## Directions

1. The grapes can be completely ripe; treat them like the previous ones
2. Strain the juice in a cloth and boil for half an hour with 1 pound white or brown sugar per quart of juice
3. Then put it in small bottles and cork

title: Tynd Ribsſaft (Thin Currant Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Currant juice
- 3 pounds powdered sugar per quart of juice
- Brandy

## Directions

1. When the juice is prepared and measured, set it on the fire and boil and simmer well for 8 to 10 minutes.
2. Then add 3 pounds powdered sugar per quart of juice, boil and skim for 1 quarter hour, pour out and stand uncovered until the next day.
3. If thick juice has accumulated on top of the thin, remove this, put in a crock and can be used as thick simple juice, but preferably first in the winter.
4. Pour the thin juice into bottles, into which pour brandy, then cork them.
5. One can cook this juice with 3 pounds sugar per quart of juice, but then for it to be able to keep, it must boil half an hour with the sugar, and since the juice loses some in the longer cooking, little is gained by saving sugar here.

title: Tynde Pandekager (Thin Pancakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) fine flour or Pillsbury flour
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) oil
- just over 1/2 L (just over 1/2 Pot) milk
- sugar
- salt
- cardamom
- egg whites

## Directions

1. In fine flour or Pillsbury flour, stir 4 egg yolks, 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) oil, just over 1/2 L (just over 1/2 Pot) milk, sugar, salt and cardamom to taste, lastly the beaten egg whites.
2. On the hot pan, add either a little butter, margarine or Palmin and a spoonful of the dough.
3. Turn the pan quickly so the dough can spread out into a thin cake, which is baked on both sides.
4. Jam or compote and powdered sugar are served with it.

title: Tynde Pandekager (Thin Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Pints sweet cream
- A little sugar
- 2 Pints flour
- 4 Egg yolks
- 2 Pints melted butter
- 1 Pint cold water

## Directions

1. Whip the sweet cream with a little sugar.
2. Add 2 pints flour, 4 egg yolks, and 2 pints melted butter.
3. Thin with 1 pint cold water.
4. Bake these pancakes so thinly that you do not need to turn them, and they will still be stiff.
5. If you wish to serve them rolled up, roll them warm around a wooden stick or similar.
6. Grease the pan with butter only the first time; not afterwards.
7. Serve with powdered sugar sprinkled over.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Tynde Sukker-Kringler (Thin Sugar Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg (1/2 lb) flour
- 125 grams (1/4 lb) icing sugar
- 175 grams (35 quintal) margarine
- 1 egg
- 1 level teaspoon baking soda, dissolved in a little water
- a little ground cardamom

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Roll out the dough into very thin sticks and form as rings
3. Roll very flat with a cookie roller
4. Dip in egg mixed with cream, then in plenty of soft icing sugar
5. Place far apart on the baking sheet as they spread considerably
6. Bake until light brown and remove quickly from the sheet while still warm from the heat
7. Can also be sprinkled with caraway seeds

title: Tyttebær med Pærer, Æbler eller Græskar (Lingonberries with Pears, Apples or Squash)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 gr (40 Kv.) Demerara sugar
- 1/2 kg (1 S) lingonberries
- Peeled and cut fruit (pears, apples or squash)
- Whole cinnamon pieces

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the lingonberries, layer them with sugar in the pot and bring to a boil with constant stirring
2. Now add the peeled and cut fruit along with some whole cinnamon and cook together with the lingonberries for about 1/4 hour
3. Pour the preserve into crocks and seal as soon as it has cooled
4. Use pear varieties such as Graapærer, Bergamottes, or Figenpærer, which are commonly cooked with lingonberries, though any cooking pear variety can be used
5. Peel the fruits, cut them into 4 pieces, remove the cores, rinse them, then add to the pot to cook with the lingonberries
6. Pears can be bought when cheapest, peeled, cut and cooked with the lingonberries

title: Tyttebær-Gelé (Lingonberry Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) stone sugar
- 3/4 dl (3/4 cup) water for 1/2 kg (1 pound) fruit juice
- ripe, picked, and rinsed lingonberries

## Directions

1. Place the ripe, picked, and rinsed berries over gentle heat before they burst.
2. Pour them into a jelly cloth.
3. The juice that has run through by the following day is weighed.
4. After sugar and water have been cooked as for the previous jellies, add the juice and cook and skim for 10 minutes.
5. Pour into cups and bind when it begins to set.
6. The berries remaining in the cloth can be cooked with sugar and served with roast, or cooked in water and used for lingonberry pudding.

title: Tyttebær-Grød (Lingonberry Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ kg lingonberries
- 1½ L water
- 1 piece of cinnamon
- Sugar to taste
- Potato flour and sago flour

## Directions

1. Pick and rinse the berries
2. Place over heat with 1½ L water, cinnamon stick, and sugar to taste
3. When cooked through, thicken the juice with a little thinned potato flour and sago flour
4. Serve cold with cream or crème
5. Alternatively, the pudding can be strained and thickened like other fruit puddings

title: Tyttebær-Suppe (Lingonberry Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 kg lingonberries
- 5 L water
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- potato flour
- sugar
- pears
- apples

## Directions

1. Cook berries with lemon peel and whole cinnamon and strain after cooking for half an hour
2. Thicken soup with potato flour
3. Peel pears, divide in 4 pieces and cook in very little water with sugar
4. When tender, add to soup and sweeten further
5. Can also add apples

title: Tyttebærsaft (Lingonberry Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Lingonberries
- 1/2 liter (1/2 Pot) water per 1/2 kg berries
- 1/4 kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar or Melis per 1 liter (1 Pot) juice

## Directions

1. Stem, weigh, and rinse the berries.
2. When the water has drained well, place them over heat with 1/2 liter (1/2 Pot) water per 1/2 kg berries.
3. Cook them through, pour into the jelly cloth.
4. Measure the juice that has run through.
5. Boil it with the sugar for 10 minutes, skim during cooking, and pour into bottles, which are bound immediately.

title: Tyttebærsaft (Lingonberry Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter (1 pint) water
- 2 kg (4 pounds) berries

## Directions

1. Pick and rinse the berries
2. Place them in a preserving pot together with the water
3. They must be completely cooked before pouring into a jelly cloth or fine sieve so the juice can drain
4. The next day, pour into bottles and cork them
5. Wrap with hay and boil for 5 minutes in a kettle
6. Seal with plaster the next day

title: Tørrede Abrikoser med Makroner og Flødeskum (Dried Apricots with Macarons and Whipped Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg dried apricots
- macarons
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) heavy cream

## Directions

1. Soak the apricots the evening before.
2. Cook the apricots and sweeten to taste.
3. Layer the cooled apricots with macarons in a glass bowl.
4. Decorate with whipped cream made from 1/2 L (1/2 pot) heavy cream.

title: Tørrede Abrikoser med Makroner og Flødeskum (Dried Apricots with Macaroons and Whipped Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 575 gr (1/4 lb) dried apricots
- Macaroons
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) cream for foam

## Directions

1. Soak the apricots the evening before, cook and sweeten to taste.
2. Layer them cold with macaroons and garnish with foam from 1/2 L cream.

title: Tørrede, russiske Ærter (Dried Russian Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram dried Russian peas
- Sugar
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Rinse the peas in several changes of water and soak them in lukewarm water overnight; if soaked for 2 days they will be better.
2. Cook them in water with sugar, salt, and a good slice of butter.
3. The cooking time depends on the quality of the peas, but usually at least 2 hours are needed.
4. Stew them as green peas and can be prepared with finely chopped parsley.

title: Umoden Melon (Unripe Melon)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Unripe melon, peeled and cut into oblong pieces
- Powdered loaf sugar
- Water
- Alum

## Directions

1. Place the peeled melon, cut into oblong pieces, in boiling water with a slight amount of alum, and bring to a boil.
2. When the melon is soft, remove it onto a colander and cover with cold water.
3. Place the fruit in a jar and cover with a thin syrup, made from the specified amount of sugar and water.
4. The next two days, pour off the liquid, cook and skim it, but the last time place the fruit in it to bring to a boil.
5. After the melon is removed, the liquid drained, and the fruit placed in the jar, reduce the liquid further to make it completely even.
6. Pour it warm over the melon.
7. Seal as soon as the preserve has cooled.

title: Umodne Ferskener (Unripe Peaches)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) unripe peaches
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) powdered loaf sugar
- 1.5 dl (1/3 pint) water

## Directions

1. Dry the peaches and prick or peel them.
2. Split them and remove the pits.
3. Boil and skim the sugar and water before adding the fruit to boil for several minutes, depending on its ripeness.
4. Remove the peaches and place them in the jars, and boil the syrup again for several minutes before pouring it over the fruit.
5. After 3 to 4 days have elapsed, pour off the syrup, skim it and boil it until smooth; then pour it over the peaches.
6. Seal the jars as soon as the preserve has cooled.

title: Umodne Ferskener til Steg (Unripe Peaches for Serving)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound unripe peaches, peeled
- One pound white sugar
- One gill vinegar
- One half pint water (scant)

## Directions

1. Peel 1 pound unripe peaches, divide into 4 parts, and remove the stones.
2. Take 1 pound white sugar and cook with 1 gill vinegar and a scant half pint water.
3. When this is skimmed, add the peaches and cook about 5 minutes (longer if very unripe); remove and place in a jar.
4. Cook the syrup a bit longer, about 6 minutes, pour hot over the peaches, and cover with cloth.
5. After several days, cook the syrup again about 10 minutes, skim well, pour hot over, and cover again with cloth.
6. Tie as usual.

title: Urte-Salat (Herb Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- lost mayonnaise
- Russian mustard
- beet vinegar
- cooked finely chopped celery
- cooked beets
- watercress

## Directions

1. Add Russian mustard and beet vinegar to lost mayonnaise
2. Add equal parts of cooked finely chopped celery and beets
3. Garnish the salad with watercress
4. It can be served as a vegetable side dish with meat

title: Uægte Kaviar (Mock Caviar)
body:
## Ingredients

- Roe from pike, cod, or preferably salmon
- Fine, dry salt
- Per half pound of roe: 3 finely chopped shallots
- Crushed pepper
- Crushed cloves
- Elderberry paste (optional, for darkening)

## Directions

1. Remove the membranes from the roe very carefully
2. Sprinkle with fine, dry salt and beat with it
3. Let stand a few hours to stiffen
4. To half a pound of roe, add 3 very finely chopped shallots, a little crushed pepper, and crushed cloves
5. Mix well together and let stand for 1 day, after which it is ready to use
6. The color will be light, but if you wish it to be dark like genuine caviar, you can purchase a little elderberry paste from the apothecary and stir it in
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Uægte Majonaise (False Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of butter
- flour
- strong bouillon
- 4 egg yolks
- fine culinary oil
- mustard
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Bake a piece of butter with flour and dilute with strong bouillon so it becomes fairly thick.
2. Let it boil clear, and when cold, stir in 4 egg yolks one at a time along with fine culinary oil, a little mustard, salt, pepper and fairly substantial lemon juice.
3. Finally carefully fold in 3 egg whites whipped to foam.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vafler (Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 Pound washed butter
- 1 Pound fine wheat flour
- 3 Pints lukewarm water
- A little powdered sugar
- A little cardamom
- 1 scant pint cold water

## Directions

1. Stir the washed butter to a salve.
2. Then stir with 1 pound fine wheat flour, and when well mixed, with 3 pints lukewarm water, a little powdered sugar, and a little cardamom.
3. Finally add 1 scant pint cold water.
4. Meanwhile heat the waffle iron and grease with butter, but only for the first waffle, after which baking begins.
5. Bake light brown over brisk heat.
6. Serve hot with caster sugar.
7. The quantity given makes about 16 pieces.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vafler (Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter, clarified
- 8 egg yolks, 1 at a time
- 1 pint cream
- ½ pound flour
- 5 egg whites, whipped to foam

## Directions

1. Clarify and stir 1 pound butter until stiff
2. Stir in 8 egg yolks, 1 at a time
3. Stir in 1 pint cream and ½ pound flour
4. Whip 5 egg whites to foam and add when beginning to bake
5. Grease the iron from time to time
6. This makes approximately 18 waffles
7. The butter can also be rinsed out and stirred to cream, in which case stir in 4 egg yolks, ½ pound flour, 1 pint warm milk, and the whipped whites; grease the iron for each cake

title: Vafler af Flöde (Cream Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cream
- 2 pounds flour
- a little white sugar
- 4 beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Whip 1 quart cream to foam
2. Constantly remove the foam from the top and there remains a little thin part, in which stir 2 pounds flour
3. Mix in the foam with a little white sugar and 4 beaten egg whites
4. Grease the iron from time to time and bake the cakes light brown
5. They are equally good whether the cream is sweet or sour, or take half of each kind
6. The egg whites can be omitted
7. This makes approximately 18 waffles

title: Valnødder (Pickled Walnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg walnuts
- 625 gr ground icing sugar
- 51/4 dl water

## Directions

1. Pick walnuts in the first half of July before the stone has hardened
2. Pierce the meat and outer green shell with a coarse larding needle or bacon needle
3. Place in cold water, which must be renewed daily
4. After standing thus for about ten days, place over heat in cold water and cook covered until tender enough to pierce easily with a needle
5. Remove and place on a cloth, then weigh them
6. Boil sugar and water to a thin syrup and pour over the nuts, which stand thus for a couple of days
7. Drain off the juice, reduce well, and give the walnuts another boil in it
8. Place in glasses and reduce the juice further before pouring over
9. The walnuts can be flavored with various additions: either cook a stick of vanilla in the syrup, or stick the nuts with small pieces of cinnamon and some whole cloves after they have been cooked tender in the water. The spices mentioned can also be simply boiled in the syrup

title: Valnødder (Pickled Walnuts)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- walnuts
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- whole black pepper
- ground ginger
- shallots

## Directions

1. Prick the walnuts with a fork and place them in a brine made of 0.5 kg salt to 1.25 liters water.
2. Every third day, pour fresh brine over the nuts.
3. After 9 days total, remove the nuts, pat them dry, and place on trays.
4. Set the trays in the sun until the nuts are completely black after 3-4 days.
5. Place them in jars.
6. Heat enough vinegar to cover the walnuts and boil for 10 minutes.
7. For each liter of vinegar, add 50 gr whole black pepper, 25 gr ground ginger, and 5 shallots.
8. Pour the hot vinegar with spices over the walnuts, ensuring they are completely covered.
9. When the vinegar cools, seal the jar.
10. After one month, the walnuts are ready to eat and can be stored for several years.
11. Use in brown sauces, with fish and meat.

title: Valnødder (Preserved Walnuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- Walnuts, half-grown
- Cinnamon
- Cloves
- White sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Walnuts are usually suitable for preserving in late July. They must only be half-grown and not yet have a hard shell, which can be tested by pricking with a larding needle; if there is nothing hard in them, they are good and are placed for 5 days in well water, which is renewed each day
2. Then prick them all around with a larding needle, and place them again in water for 4 days, which is likewise renewed each day
3. Then place them on the fire in cold water and cook under a lid until they are so tender that a pointed piece of cinnamon can be stuck through them, then place them on a cloth to drain and weigh them
4. Stick each walnut with 4 pieces of cinnamon and 2 cloves, and place in a crock
5. For each pound of nuts take 1 pound white sugar, which is cooked to a syrup with 3 gills water, and poured immediately over the walnuts, which then stand covered for 4 to 5 days
6. Then strain them, and the syrup is cooked with an addition of ½ pound sugar for 10 minutes, or until it is smooth, and is then poured immediately over the walnuts, which the next day are covered with paper dipped in brandy, and tied
7. If the syrup after about 3 weeks has become thin, it must be cooked again, but is then poured cold over the walnuts

title: Valnødder med Honning (Walnuts with Honey)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg cooked walnuts
- 1/2 kg honey

## Directions

1. Prick the walnuts, place in cold water and cook until tender in water as described in the previous recipe
2. Weigh them and can be studded with cloves and cinnamon if desired
3. Bring the honey to a boil and give the nuts another boil in it
4. Let stand overnight, then drain the honey
5. Bring the honey to a boil and pour immediately over the nuts
6. Boil the honey a third time and finally give the walnuts another boil in it before placing in glasses and covering with the boiling honey
7. Bind immediately

title: Vandbakkelseloer (Water Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound flour
- 1/2 pound clarified butter
- 1 pint boiling water
- 10 eggs

## Directions

1. These cakes require strong heat to be quick, but must not become brown at all.
2. Bake the flour well in 1/2 pound clarified butter.
3. Add 1 pint boiling water and when it has baked with it, cool it a little, stir for half an hour with 10 eggs, two at a time, and place preferably in small molds, but if not available, with a spoon in small lumps on the pan.
4. Makes 25 cakes.
5. They can be filled with Vanilla Cream or Pastry Cream.

title: Vandbakkelser (Water Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 quintal melted butter, with the salt removed
- 3/4 pound flour
- 2 pints boiled water
- 10 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Bake the melted butter with the salt removed together with the flour and thin it with the boiled water. Bake it until it releases from the pan, then pour it out to cool. When it has cooled somewhat, stir in 10 whole eggs, 2 at a time, and continue stirring for half an hour. Grease the baking sheet with cold butter, and with a teaspoon place small, round cakes, about 25 in all, on it and bake at good oven heat for 1.5 hours. While they bake, the oven must not be opened, as they will fall. They can be served sprinkled with powdered sugar and with a dollop of currant jelly on each plus cream sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vandbakkelser (Water Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 ounces melted butter
- ¾ pound wheat flour
- 2 pints boiled water
- 10 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Bake 40 ounces melted butter (with salt removed) together with ¾ pound wheat flour and thin with 2 pints boiled water
2. Bake until the dough releases from the pan
3. Pour out to cool
4. When somewhat cooled, stir in 10 whole eggs, 2 at a time
5. Continue stirring for half an hour
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vandbakkelser (Water Puffs)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams margarine
- 250 grams Pillsbury flour
- 500 milliliters boiling water
- 10 egg yolks
- egg whites, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. Melt the margarine and bake the flour in it
2. Add the boiling water gradually
3. When cooled, stir well with egg yolks
4. Finally add the stiffly beaten egg whites
5. Place the dough with a teaspoon on a cold baking sheet, not too close together
6. Bake in a very hot oven for approximately half an hour
7. Make a small opening in the cakes with a knife and fill with cream or whipped cream, which can be flavored with various things such as chopped pineapple, candied ginger, pineapple, vanilla, or a liqueur
8. Jam is served separately if used
9. Alternatively, the dough can be filled into a pastry bag and piped into oblong cakes, which can also be filled with whipped cream
10. For small water puffs for soup, place the dough on the sheet in very small drops the size of a large nut and bake quickly

title: Vandchokolade på Parisermaade (Parisian-style Water Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Unzer fin Chokolade
- 1 Pint Band
- 2 Æggeblommer
- Sukker (optional)

## Directions

1. Dissolve 4 Unzer fine chocolate in 1 Pint water
2. Pour into a high kettle and place on gentle heat and boil
3. After it has boiled for 10 minutes, whisk it with an egg whisk, which is twirled between the hands therein, until it froths strongly
4. Pour gradually into 2 beaten egg yolks, which have been beaten throughout the work
5. Return to the kettle and whisk continuously with the egg whisk
6. All the thin foam is placed with a skimmer in the heated cups, which are placed in a warm spot, and this is continued until it stands piled up in the form of a dome over the cups
7. Hold a red-hot fire shovel very close over the foam until a kind of crust forms, after which the chocolate is served
8. Note: The chocolate froths even better when some sugar is stirred into the egg yolks, or if foam balls are used instead, prepared as follows: A sufficient number of egg whites are whisked to very stiff foam, and therein is stirred fine powdered sugar until the mass forms a stiff dough, which is spread on a baking sheet, and when it is nearly dried, small balls are formed of the size of a medium nut, which are then dried completely in the sun or at room temperature. After the egg yolks are whipped together with the chocolate in the manner described above, for each cup one such foam ball is placed in the chocolate pot and whisked on the fire until the entire chocolate mass is dissolved into stiff foam. One must observe the precaution that the chocolate is only heated until nearly boiling; it must absolutely not boil.

title: Vandgrød (Water Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- barley grits or rice grains

## Directions

1. Instead of milk, use water to cook barley grits or rice grains in the proportions given for those dishes
2. To improve the flavor, cook a tablespoon of washed caraway seeds with barley water porridge or some washed currants with rice water porridge
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vandkringle A (Water Wreath A)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 pounds fine wheat flour
- 1/2 pound butter
- 5 eggs
- 15 cups cold, sweet milk
- 2 tablespoons yeast
- 1/2 pound sugar
- washed raisins
- finely cut citron peel
- a little cardamom
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Knead 2.5 pounds fine wheat flour, 1/2 pound butter, 5 eggs, 15 cups cold sweet milk, and 2 tablespoons yeast well together the evening before the wreath is to be baked.
2. Wrap the dough in a linen cloth and let it lie in cold water overnight.
3. The next morning, knead in 1/2 pound sugar, washed raisins, finely cut citron peel, and a little cardamom.
4. Shape as a wreath, brush with melted butter, and sprinkle with powdered sugar before baking.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vandkringle B (Water Ring Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds wheat flour
- 4 pounds butter
- 6 eggs
- 2 measures milk
- a little crushed cardamom
- grated lemon zest
- 2 tablespoons yeast
- 1 small cup crushed powdered sugar
- 3/4 pound cleaned raisins
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Work 2 pounds wheat flour, 4 pounds butter, 6 eggs, 2 measures milk, a little crushed cardamom, grated lemon zest, 2 tablespoons yeast and 1 small cup crushed powdered sugar together cold with the hand until it comes together.
2. Place the dough in a strong, firm cloth strewn with flour and submerge it in a pail with cold water.
3. Let it stand from evening to the next morning.
4. Knead 3/4 pound cleaned raisins into the dough.
5. Form the dough into a large ring cake, brush it with melted butter and sprinkle with sugar before baking.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vanille Creme (Vanilla Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cream
- 2 tablespoons vanilla
- 8 egg yolks
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Place the cream on heat with 2 tablespoons vanilla.
2. When the cream boils, remove it from heat and stir in 8 egg yolks that have first been stirred white with a little white sugar.
3. Return to heat and stir constantly until it is just about to boil and has become thick.
4. Pour out and stir evenly until it is so cold that it no longer forms a skin, then pour into a dish and serve.
5. If the cream becomes thick quickly while on heat and begins to separate, pour it out at once and stir strongly so that it can come together again; it helps to stir in a little cold cream or good milk and pour it through a sieve.
6. Add a little salt and serve with small cakes.
7. You can cook egg whites according to instructions and place them over the cream.
8. For 18 persons.

title: Vanille Kranse (Vanilla Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound fine flour
- 3 pounds finely ground powdered sugar
- ¼ pound washed butter
- 2 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 2 raw egg yolks
- vanilla sugar for 25 øre
- coarse white powdered sugar
- chopped almonds
- flour for rolling

## Directions

1. Knead ½ pound fine flour, 3 pounds finely ground powdered sugar, ¼ pound washed butter, 2 hard-boiled and 2 raw egg yolks, and vanilla sugar for 25 øre together
2. Roll with flour into rings
3. Dip in coarse white powdered sugar and chopped almonds
4. Bake at even heat on a floured sheet
5. Best served freshly baked
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vanille Kréme (Vanilla Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- 1 vanilla pod
- 12 egg yolks
- 36 quarters powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pot of sweet cream with 1 vanilla pod.
2. Whip 12 egg yolks white with 36 quarters of powdered sugar.
3. When the cream has boiled but is cooled enough that it no longer boils, pour it with whisking into the egg yolks, which are placed in the pot and again under constant whisking are set over the heat and warmed until the mixture is just about to boil.
4. Remove it from the heat and pour it through a large sieve, stirring it through into a terrine.
5. You must continue to stir it in the terrine until it is cold, otherwise it will separate.
6. The cream is then set to cool.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vanille-Budding (Vanilla Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g (20 quintal) margarine
- 100 g (20 quintal) flour
- 3 3/4 dl (1 1/2 pint) boiling milk
- 7 egg yolks
- 125 g (1/4 S) powdered sugar
- egg whites, beaten stiff
- vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Melt the margarine and bake the flour in it, then dilute with about 3 3/4 dl (1 1/2 pint) of boiling milk.
2. When the batter has cooled, stir in the 7 egg yolks together with the powdered sugar, and finally add the stiffly beaten egg whites.
3. Vanilla essence may be stirred into the batter.
4. Serve with chocolate sauce (Nr. 803) or fruit sauce.

title: Vanille-Cookies (Vanilla Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup cold water
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons vanilla
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup butter and 2 cups sugar well together.
2. Add 1 cup cold water, 1 teaspoon soda, 2 teaspoons vanilla, and flour to make the dough very stiff.
3. Roll very thin, cut, and bake the cakes brown.

title: Vanille-Créme (Vanilla Cream)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 L (1 pot) sweet milk
- 5-6 egg yolks
- 100 gr (20 quintal) powdered sugar
- 10 sheets isinglass
- 1 1/4 dl (1/2 pægl) boiling water
- vanilla flavoring to taste
- 1/2 L (1/2 pot) cream for whipping

## Directions

1. Heat the milk and pour it in a thin stream while whisking into the egg yolks mixed with powdered sugar
2. Return the cream to the pot and whisk well until it just comes to a boil, then remove from heat quickly
3. Stir in isinglass dissolved in boiling water and vanilla flavoring to taste
4. Continue stirring until the cream is approximately cold
5. Fold in whipped cream
6. Pour into a wetted mold sprinkled with powdered sugar and chill on ice or in a cool place until ready to turn out and serve
7. Can be decorated with: whipped cream, pineapple, stewed fruits, candied fruits, sugared fruits, Russian marmalade, grapes, meringue, macaroons, small cakes, or biscuits

title: Vanille-Is (Vanilla Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 egg yolks
- 4.5 pounds powdered sugar
- 13 pots good sweet cream
- 1 vanilla bean

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks until white with the powdered sugar
2. Boil the sweet cream and scrape the inside of a vanilla bean into it while the outside is boiled with it but then removed
3. When the cream is boiled, remove from heat and pour slowly into the egg yolks while whisking constantly
4. Put the whole mixture back in the pot and bring to a boil while whisking continuously
5. When the cream is done, let it cool and pour into an ice bucket to freeze
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vanille-Is (Vanilla Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 egg yolks
- 4½ pounds powdered sugar
- 1 pot good, sweet cream
- 1 vanilla bean

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks white with 4½ pounds powdered sugar.
2. Boil 1 pot good, sweet cream and scrape the inside of a vanilla bean into it, while the outside is well boiled with it, but then taken out.
3. When the cream is boiled, take it off the heat and pour it little by little and with constant beating into the egg yolks.
4. Put the whole into the pot and bring to a boil with uninterrupted beating.
5. When the cream is ready, let it stand until cold, then pour it into the ice cream freezer to freeze.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vanille-Is med Puré af friske Frugter (Vanilla Ice Cream with Fresh Fruit Purée)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) fruit purée for 1 L (1 Pot) cream or milk
- cream as Nr. 1068
- fresh fruit (strawberries, raspberries, cherries, apricots, or peaches)

## Directions

1. Cook the cream as Nr. 1068 and freeze, but only after it is frozen to the point that it is a thick mass, add 1/2 Kg (1 S) purée of one or another sort of fruit.
2. Stir it well around in the mixture and then pack it in the freezer.
3. The fruit must be fully ripe and pressed through a sieve.
4. It can be either strawberries, raspberries, cherries, apricots, or peaches.

title: Vanille-Kager (Vanilla Cakes)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 gr (0.75 lb) margarine
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar, crushed
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon hartshorn salt
- vanilla essence
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 lb) Pillsbury flour
- 1/4 Kg (1/6 lb) potato flour

## Directions

1. Mix the margarine well with the powdered sugar, eggs, hartshorn salt, and vanilla essence
2. Stir in the Pillsbury flour and potato flour
3. Place on a cold baking sheet in small dollops with spacing between them
4. Bake light brown

title: Vanille-Kranse (Vanilla Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram flour
- 375 grams (3/4 pound) margarine
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) powdered sugar
- 125 grams (1/4 pound) sweet, blanched, and finely ground almonds
- 1 egg
- Vanilla essence
- A small amount of deer salt, optional

## Directions

1. Knead together the flour, margarine, powdered sugar, almonds, egg, and vanilla essence
2. The dough can be piped through a pastry bag or meat grinder and formed into small rings
3. The rings can be placed fairly close together on the baking sheet
4. If a small amount of deer salt is added, they become equally good
5. Vanilla rings and almond rings can be baked using special baking flour, adding only water and margarine

title: Vanille-Soufflé (Vanilla Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 measures sweet cream
- 60 drams white sugar
- vanilla
- 14 egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Boil 3 measures sweet cream and 60 drams white sugar with a vanilla stick, split lengthwise
2. Beat 14 egg yolks and pour the boiling cream into them
3. Pour everything back into the pan and bring to a boil with constant beating
4. Pour out to cool and beat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vanille-Soufflé (Vanilla Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups sweet cream
- 60 quintals white sugar
- 1 vanilla pod, split lengthwise
- 14 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites, whipped to stiff peaks

## Directions

1. Bring the sweet cream and white sugar to a boil with the split vanilla pod.
2. Whip the egg yolks and pour the boiling cream mixture into them.
3. Pour the entire mixture back into the saucepan and bring to a boil while constantly whisking.
4. Pour into a dish to cool, whisking constantly until cold.
5. Gently fold in the whipped egg whites.
6. Bake the soufflé in a gentle oven heat and serve as in Nr. 292, dusted with powdered sugar.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vanillekager (Vanilla Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- ½ pound finely ground powdered sugar
- ½ stick vanilla, ground
- 1 pound potato flour
- beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 6 egg yolks carefully with ½ pound finely ground powdered sugar and half a stick ground vanilla
2. When mixed, add 1 pound potato flour
3. Finally add the beaten egg whites
4. Bake in even oven heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vanillekringler (Vanilla Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds fine flour
- 1 egg
- ¼ pound washed butter
- ¾ pound finely ground powdered sugar
- ½ stick ground vanilla
- 1 pound sweet almonds, finely ground
- 3 quintal bitter almonds, finely ground
- flour for rolling

## Directions

1. Knead on the baking board 3 pounds fine flour with 1 egg
2. Add ¼ pound washed butter that has been patted dry in cloth
3. Add ¾ pound finely ground powdered sugar
4. Add ½ stick ground vanilla
5. Add 1 pound finely ground sweet almonds and 3 quintal finely ground bitter almonds
6. Knead all together well until it leaves your hands
7. Roll in flour on the board into small rings or form using a pastry bag
8. Bake slowly until nicely brown
9. From the stated amount you will get about 70 rings
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Varm Fløde-Peberrod-Sauce (Warm Cream Horseradish Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Thin cream or sweet milk
- Salt
- A little sugar
- Grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Make a roux with butter and flour and dilute with thin cream or sweet milk until the sauce is properly smooth
2. Then season it with salt, a little sugar and grated horseradish to taste
3. Cream horseradish sauce is served with boiled fowl, turkeys and with boiled veal and lamb

title: Varm Skumbudding af tiloversblevne Æggehvider (Warm Foam Pudding from Leftover Egg Whites)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pots sweet milk
- ½ pound sago flour
- 6 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1 wine glass rum
- a little chopped orange peel
- vanilla
- a little raspberry jam
- 9 egg whites
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Bring 2 pots sweet milk to boil.
2. Mix ½ pound sago flour in a third pot of milk and bring it to a boil in the first pot of milk.
3. Add 6 tablespoons powdered sugar, 1 wine glass rum, a little chopped orange peel, vanilla and a little raspberry jam.
4. While this boils, gently stir in 9 egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks, and bring it to a boil.
5. At the very end add a little salt.
6. Turn the pudding out of the mold and serve with fruit sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Veal Cutlets
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8.5 to 9 kg (7 to 8 lb) veal rib section
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Egg
- Bread
- Gravy or water
- Salad or parsley
- Vegetables, compote, or salad

## Directions

1. Use the rib section, cut so that the cutlet bones are 8 to 10 centimeters (3 to 4 inches) long.
2. Remove the vertebrae and the thick membrane covering the fillet.
3. Generally allow one bone per cutlet, though individual pieces without bone can sometimes be cut.
4. Loosen and scrape the meat from the top of the bone, beat with a meat mallet and shape with a knife into a rounded form.
5. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and roast approximately 6 to 8 minutes in browned butter over a brisk fire.
6. The cutlets can also be dipped in egg and bread.
7. When serving, place a little cold butter under them on the platter, place the bones toward the center and garnish with a bouquet of salad or parsley.
8. Pour a little gravy or water on the pan on which the cutlets were roasted to cook out the strength, which is then poured over them.
9. Serve with either vegetables, compote, or salad.
10. Paper frills can be placed around the bones of the roasted cutlets.

title: Veal Cutlets à la Maintenon
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Egg
- Bread
- Parsley
- Shallots
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Gravy
- Potatoes cooked in clarified butter

## Directions

1. Dip the cutlets first in egg, then in a mixture of crushed bread, chopped parsley, shallots, salt, and pepper and roast in butter.
2. Cook the pan with gravy, which is poured over the cutlets.
3. Garnish with potatoes cooked in clarified butter.

title: Veal Cutlets à la Perigord
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Butter
- Strong gravy
- Chopped truffles
- Madeira
- Warm boiled salted beef tongue
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Treat and roast the cutlets as in recipe 153.
2. Add chopped truffles and Madeira to a strong gravy and bring to a boil.
3. When serving, place a slice of warm boiled salted beef tongue on each cutlet and pour the sauce with truffles over it.

title: Veal Rissole (Veal Patties)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal shoulder or chopped veal
- Fatty pork
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg
- Bread
- Butter
- Gravy made from meat sinews
- Food coloring
- Vegetables, browned potatoes, compote, or salad
- Macaroni
- Paper frills

## Directions

1. Use either shoulder or chopped veal finely scraped or ground once through the meat grinder; preferably half should be fatty pork.
2. When ground and collected with a knife, form it into the shape of cutlets.
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, dip in egg and bread and roast in browned butter.
4. Pour gravy made from the meat sinews into the butter on the pan.
5. The sauce must be well reduced, mixed with food coloring and poured over the rissoles or served in a sauce bowl.
6. Serve with vegetables, browned potatoes, compote, or salad.
7. Small pieces of macaroni wrapped with a small piece of cut paper or paper frills can be inserted into the rissole after it is roasted as mock bones; these can be bought ready-made or can be easily cut oneself.

title: Vestindisk Æggemælk (West Indian Eggnog)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 liters semi-skimmed milk
- 6 egg yolks
- 0.25 kg powdered sugar
- 5 to 6 teaspoons cornstarch
- 2.5 deciliters white rum
- vanilla essence
- additional rum and sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg yolks white with 0.25 kg powdered sugar
2. Bring the milk to a boil
3. Mix 5 to 6 teaspoons cornstarch in 2.5 deciliters white rum
4. Pour the boiling milk over the egg mixture while whisking continuously
5. Add vanilla essence to taste, and more rum and sugar if needed
6. Serve with small rusks

title: Vildeænder paa Skandinavisk (Scandinavian-style Wild Ducks)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild ducks
- Butter
- Milk

## Directions

1. Pluck and flay them completely; the gamey flavor often found in wild ducks is in the skin
