2. Roast in one hour with butter, which is browned, and into which is added enough good milk so it comes halfway up the roasting bird, which is constantly

title: Vildsvinehoved (Wild Boar Head)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar head
- Red wine
- Stock
- 2 onions
- 1 celery
- 2 carrots
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 lemon
- Whole peppercorns
- Juniper berries
- Butter
- Lemon juice
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Black peppercorns
- Green salad
- Stock (for reduction)
- Red currant or raspberry jelly

## Directions

1. Cut off the head at the shoulder blades as the neck is the greatest delicacy.
2. The hairs can either be singed or scalded off; then soak it overnight in cold water to draw out the blood.
3. Remove the eyes and clean the head very thoroughly and wrap it in thin cloth.
4. Boil for at least 3 hours in a marinade of red wine and stock with 2 chopped onions, 1 celery, 2 carrots, 2 bay leaves, 1 lemon sliced, some whole peppercorns and a little juniper berries.
5. When cooked, set aside and only when cold, remove from the soup, unwrap, and garnish for serving.
6. Cut the neck meat into slices, reassemble them, and arrange the head on a platter garnished with green salad.
7. Place a whole lemon in the snout and replace the eyes with 2 slices of hard-boiled eggs with a black peppercorn in each.
8. Mix fat and butter with lemon juice and pipe through a piping bag to create decorative figures on the head, which can also be covered with stiff stock and cut vegetables or simply wrapped with flowers stuck into the head.
9. Serve Cumberland Sauce on the side: reduce some of the soup in which the head was cooked until it becomes a strong stock, add the juice of 1 or 2 lemons along with the peel of 1 orange with the white skin well removed, bring to a boil, and stir in red wine, mustard and paprika to taste, strain and thicken with dissolved tapioca flour.

title: Vildt-Puré (Game Purée)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- leftovers of all kinds of game
- 3 carrots
- 1 celery
- 2 onions
- whole peppercorns
- salt
- water or thin brown spare broth
- 125 grams (0.25 S) butter
- 125 grams (0.25 S) flour
- Sherry
- game balls (no. 204)
- bread croutons (no. 500)

## Directions

1. The meat is picked from the bones, which are chopped and cooked for an hour in water with salt or in thin brown spare broth together with 3 carrots, 1 celery, 2 onions and some whole peppercorns.
2. The meat is finely pounded in a mortar and rubbed through a sieve with some of the broth.
3. Brown 125 grams (0.25 S) butter, and bake 125 grams (0.25 S) flour in it, which is thinned with the sieved broth.
4. Add the purée and bring to a boil and add Sherry to taste.
5. Game purée can be served with small balls of game farce (no. 204). Bread croutons (no. 500) can be served with it.

title: Vildt-Salat (Game Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover game or poultry
- Good game sauce
- Red wine
- French mustard (moutarde)
- Watercress
- Tartlets filled with apple compote, apple sauce, lingonberry jelly, or currant jelly

## Directions

1. Cut leftover game or poultry meat into 2 to 3 centimeter (1 inch) long, fine strips.
2. Make a good, smooth game sauce and add red wine and a little French mustard.
3. Turn the meat in this sauce and arrange it, garnished with watercress.
4. Place the salad bowl on a platter and arrange a wreath of tartlets filled with apple compote, apple sauce, lingonberry jelly, or currant jelly around the edge.

title: Vildt-Salat (Game Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover venison or game bird
- Good game sauce
- Red wine
- French mustard
- Watercress for garnish
- Tartlets with apple compote

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover meat into 2-3 centimeter (1 inch) long, fine strips
2. Prepare a good, smooth game sauce and add red wine and a little French mustard to it
3. Turn the meat into it and arrange garnished with watercress
4. Place the salad bowl on a platter and arrange a ring of tartlets filled with apple compote around the edge

title: Vildt-Sauce (Game Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Gravy from the game
- Cream or unsweetened condensed milk
- Cold butter
- Salt
- Coloring
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Currant jelly

## Directions

1. Brown butter and bake just a little flour in it, thin with the gravy from the game to make a thin sauce
2. Add good cream or unsweetened condensed milk and cook well
3. Finally stir in a slice of cold butter together with salt, coloring and Maggi bouillon cubes if needed
4. Currant jelly stirred into the sauce gives an excellent taste

title: Vildtfars (Game Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg game meat
- 125 g cold butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Treat as for beef forcemeat.
2. It can be stirred up with either red wine, gravy, milk or cream.

title: Vildtfars (Game Forcemeat)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg game meat
- 125 grams cold butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Treat as beef forcemeat
2. The forcemeat can be mixed with either red wine, gravy, milk, or cream

title: Vildtsauce (Game Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cream
- butter
- gravy or soy sauce
- red currant jelly (optional)

## Directions

1. Put cream and butter in a saucepan over heat and stir until it boils.
2. Add gravy or soy sauce.
3. The sauce should be fairly smooth.
4. A little red currant jelly can also be stirred in, though this is better served unmixed with the roast.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vildænder (Roasted Wild Duck)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild duck (1 for 2 to 3 people)
- 1¼ dl (½ pint) vinegar
- 1 l (1 pot) cold water
- Milk
- Sauce (see Pheasant, No. 963)
- Cold butter
- Browned potatoes
- Compote or jelly

## Directions

1. Use preferably the broad-billed variety. Remove the head with neck and tips of wings, cut open, clean and rinse well inside.
2. If freshly shot, lard and roast immediately. If hung for a time, soak in a mixture of 1¼ dl vinegar to 1 l cold water for 4 to 5 hours after plucking.
3. Rinse and soak in milk for a few hours.
4. Lard and brown, then braise best in a pot for about 5 quarters of an hour.
5. Prepare the sauce first and roast the duck in it (see Pheasant, No. 963).
6. If roasting a larger number in the oven, do not brown first but place breast side up and drip well with a thin, baked sauce.
7. The sauce can be given more colour at serving and thickened with a piece of cold butter.
8. At serving, remove the back and divide the breast into 4 parts, which are reassembled.
9. Garnish with browned potatoes and serve with compote or jelly.

title: Vin-Gelée (Wine Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 5 lemons
- Zest of 2 lemons
- 2 pounds white sugar
- 2 pints water
- 12 quarters isinglass
- 4 egg whites
- 1 bottle Rhine wine
- 1 pint Madeira

## Directions

1. Set the strained juice of 5 and zest of 2 lemons, 2 pounds white sugar, 2 pints water, 12 quarters isinglass, and 4 egg whites over heat in an enameled pot.
2. Bring to a boil in the manner mentioned in Recipe 421 and skim.
3. Then add 1 bottle Rhine wine and 1 pint Madeira, and pour the whole through a filter bag to clarify.
4. Arrange the jelly preferably in tall glass bowls as cold as possible.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vin-Koldskaal (Wine Cold Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- small macaroons
- lemon slices
- a couple of sticks cinnamon
- white wine
- ice-cold pure water
- sugar to taste
- ice (if available)

## Directions

1. An hour before the cold soup is to be served, put small macaroons in a tureen together with some lemon slices without pits and a couple of sticks of cinnamon.
2. Pour over them white wine mixed with ice-cold pure water and sugar to taste.
3. If you have some pieces of clean ice, add them.
4. Allow 1 to 1.5 pints per portion.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vindrue-Gelé (Grape Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 625 grams (1 1/2 pounds) stone sugar
- 1 1/2 dl (1/2 cup) water for 1/2 kg (1 pound) fruit juice
- partially ripe grapes

## Directions

1. Pick the grapes from the stems and fill them into a jar which is placed in a pot of boiling water that must be kept boiling until the grapes burst and release their juice well.
2. Pour the entire contents into a jelly cloth.
3. Weigh the juice that has run through.
4. Cook sugar and water as in recipe 93.
5. Pour the juice, which is then poured into the pot, and cook for 10 minutes, skim and pour into cups moistened with cognac.
6. Bind as soon as the jelly is cooled and has begun to set.

title: Vindruer (Preserved Grapes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds half-ripe grapes
- ½ pound of the same kind of grapes
- 1 pound white sugar
- 2 ounces sugar per pound (later)
- Brandy
- Paper dipped in brandy

## Directions

1. Place 2 pounds half-ripe grapes in a jar; crush ½ pound of the same kind of grapes
2. Strain the juice and mix with 1 pound white sugar and pour over the whole berries, which stand covered for 2 days
3. Then place the whole over the fire and boil until the grapes have changed color
4. Remove them and place in the jar
5. Boil the juice a little longer, skim it, and pour hot over the grapes, which are covered with a cloth and stand again for 2 days
6. Put the whole over the fire again; when it begins to boil, remove the grapes and place in the jar
7. Boil the juice well, skim it, and pour hot over it, covered with a cloth and sealed the next day
8. When they have stood for 8 days, the juice is usually thin; then boil it with an addition of 2 ounces sugar per pound and pour cold over the grapes
9. Cover with paper dipped in brandy and seal
10. They can be filled in the same way with powdered sugar, and are then good for cakes, sweet soup, and sauces

title: Vindruesaft (Grape Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Grapes
- 1/2 kg (1 S) powdered sugar Melis per 1 liter (1 Pot) juice

## Directions

1. Crush the grapes in a wooden or stone mortar and pour them into the jelly cloth so that the juice can run off.
2. Measure the juice and boil it with the sugar until no more foam appears during cooking.
3. Then pour it out and fill into bottles, which are bound immediately.
4. This juice is particularly suitable for jelly.

title: Vinsauce (Wine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- zest of 1 lemon
- 1/4 pound white sugar
- juice of 2 lemons
- 2 glasses white wine
- 1 glass water
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon potato flour

## Directions

1. Beat together the grated zest of 1 lemon and 1/4 pound white sugar with the juice of 2 lemons, 2 glasses white wine, 1 glass water, 4 egg yolks and 1 teaspoon potato flour.
2. With constant whisking bring to a boil until it froths.
3. Remove the sauce from heat and continue whisking until it is approximately cooled.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vinsuppe (Wine Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints French white wine
- 4.5 pints water
- a couple of lemon slices
- 1 stick cinnamon
- 4 egg yolks
- 6 spoonfuls pounded powdered sugar
- small rusks

## Directions

1. Cook 3 pints of French white wine and 4.5 pints of water with a couple of lemon slices (with pits removed) and a stick of cinnamon.
2. Beat 4 egg yolks with 6 spoonfuls of pounded powdered sugar until they are white.
3. While stirring constantly, add a few spoonfuls of the soup to the eggs, and continue this until all the soup is whisked together with the eggs in a tureen.
4. Serve small rusks with it.
5. The soup must be served as soon as it is ready.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vinter-Salat (Winter Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked macaroni
- carrots
- celery or salsify roots
- pickled gherkins
- cooked green peas
- pickled onions
- lost mayonnaise
- mustard
- watercress

## Directions

1. Cut cooked macaroni, carrots, celery or salsify roots and pickled gherkins small, and mix in cooked green peas and small sliced pickled onions
2. Mix all these ingredients in lost mayonnaise made with mustard
3. Sprinkle the salad with watercress

title: Vinter-Salat (Winter Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked macaroni
- Carrots
- Celery or salsify roots
- Pickled gherkins
- Cooked green peas
- Small pickled onions, sliced
- Mayonnaise made with mustard
- Watercress

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked macaroni, carrots, celery or salsify roots, and pickled gherkins into small pieces.
2. Mix in the cooked green peas and small sliced pickled onions.
3. Blend all these ingredients into the diluted mayonnaise made with mustard.
4. Sprinkle the salad with watercress.

title: Vol au vent med Duer (Vol au vent with Pigeons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted pigeons
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock
- Pickled pearl onions
- Shallots
- Pickles
- Olives
- Champignons

## Directions

1. Place roasted pigeons, portioned into 2 or 4 pieces, in the vol au vent.
2. Make a brown sauce from butter, flour, and stock.
3. Pickled pearl onions, shallots, pickles, olives, or champignons may be added to the sauce.
4. Pour the sauce over the pigeons, arrange them in the vol au vent, and serve as hot as possible.

title: Vol au vent med Høns og Grønærter (Vol au Vent with Chicken and Green Peas)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- 1 Kg (2 lb) peas
- Butter or fat from chicken broth
- Flour
- Chicken broth
- Pea cooking water
- 4 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clean and blanch the chickens, then cook until tender.
2. Leave them in the broth to cool (removing them immediately makes the meat dry).
3. When cold, cut the chicken meat into small pieces without bones.
4. Use the butter or fat skimmed from the chicken broth to make the sauce.
5. Make a sauce with flour and dilute with broth and pea water to make a good fricassée sauce.
6. Bind the sauce with 4 egg yolks for this portion.
7. Make enough sauce to fill the sauce boats before heating the chicken meat in it.
8. Arrange the fricassée high in the center of each small vol au vent and garnish with peas on top.
9. If using canned peas, simply heat the can in boiling water. It is unnecessary to sauté the peas in butter for this dish.

title: Vol au vent med Kalvebrissel (Vol au Vent with Veal Sweetbreads)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pairs of veal sweetbreads
- Filling for Krustader

## Directions

1. Clean, blanch, and cook the sweetbreads in the same manner as described in recipe 6.
2. Prepare the filling as for Krustader, preferably cutting the sweetbreads into slightly larger pieces.
3. Garnish the vol au vent filled with sweetbreads with mushrooms.

title: Vol au vent med Kalvebrissel (Vol au vent with Veal Sweetbreads)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pairs veal sweetbreads
- Champignons

## Directions

1. Clean the sweetbreads, blanch and cook in the same manner as Nr. 6.
2. Prepare the filling in the same way as for Croustades, though the sweetbreads should preferably be cut into somewhat larger pieces.
3. Fill the vol au vent with sweetbreads and garnish with Champignons.

title: Vol au vent med Skildpadde-Ragout (Vol au vent with Turtle Ragout)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's head
- Small fish balls
- Eggs
- Chestnuts
- Truffles
- Champignons
- Olives

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into cubes of approximately 3 centimeters (1 inch) in size.
2. Prepare the ragout according to Nr. 9.
3. Serve the ragout in vol au vent.
4. Garnish with very small fish balls arranged in a star pattern on top, or with eggs, chestnuts, truffles, champignons, or olives.
5. Alternatively, use leftovers of goose and duck roasts, boiled or roasted turkey, pigeons, or any kind of game for the ragout filling.

title: Vol-au-vent (Puff Pastry Case)
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared puff pastry dough
- eggs
- meat or fish

## Directions

1. Roll out a base from ready-made puff pastry dough according to the size of the dish.
2. Around it place a strip a couple of fingers wide, brush it with beaten eggs and fasten it to the edge around the base.
3. Place it in a hot oven to bake.
4. When done, take it out and place a meat or fish dish in the opening.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Vrøvl (Vrøvl)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 lb) Pillsbury flour
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 lb) margarine
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 lb) powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon hartshorn salt
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix the flour, margarine, powdered sugar, hartshorn salt, and eggs together
2. Form into various small shapes, not placed too close on the baking sheet
3. Bake. Note: Vrøvl can be baked from baking flour made for vanilla or almond wreaths. Even better is half almond and half vanilla baking flour, baked in wreaths

title: Walesboller (Welsh Buns)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Water puff dough
- Butter dough
- Water glaze

## Directions

1. Place a teaspoon of water puff dough on a small piece of square, thinly rolled butter dough
2. Bend the four pointed corners up around the dough
3. Bake as water puffs
4. While lukewarm, brush the cakes with water glaze

title: Walter Scott's Postej (Walter Scott's Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. well-scraped beef
- 1 lb. finely cleaned chopped kidney fat
- 2 tablespoons wheat flour
- salt
- ground pepper
- nutmeg
- pale ale
- bouillon
- short crust dough
- roasted hens or other birds
- onions
- butter

## Directions

1. Pound 2 lbs. beef and 1 lb. finely cleaned chopped kidney fat very finely in a mortar together with 2 tablespoons wheat flour, a little salt, ground pepper, nutmeg, pale ale and bouillon until it becomes spongy and smooth.
2. Line a mold with short crust dough (Nr. 184).
3. Spread the forcemeat on it and place roasted hens or other birds, cut into pieces, on top together with onions that have been braised in butter and cut into strips, and continue in this manner.
4. On top place a layer of short crust dough.
5. Place the pie in a hot oven and bake for 1.5 hours.
6. There must be an opening in the middle of the lid, as otherwise the pie easily cracks.
7. Serve with brown sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Wax Beans in Tomato Sauce (Voksbønner i Tomat-Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- wax beans or green beans
- butter
- flour
- meat broth or cooking water from beans
- tomato purée
- salt
- pepper
- Maggis bouillon cubes

## Directions

1. Rub, rinse the beans, and if small cook them whole, otherwise cut them once
2. Brown butter and flour together and thin with meat broth or the water in which the beans were cooked
3. Stir tomato purée, salt, pepper, and Maggis bouillon cubes into the sauce to taste
4. Pour the sauce over the warm beans or turn the beans in the sauce without breaking them

title: Wble-Budding (Apple Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds apples
- A little water
- Lemon zest and cinnamon
- Sugar
- Half a pound wheat bread
- Milk
- 2 tablespoons clarified butter
- 4 egg yolks
- A good half cup sugar
- A little bitter almonds, blanched and finely chopped
- Cardamom and cinnamon
- Egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Peel and cook the apples in a little water with lemon zest and cinnamon
2. Press through a sieve and sweeten with sugar, but not too sweet
3. Grate half a pound of wheat bread and mix with milk, not too thin
4. Add clarified butter, egg yolks, sugar, almonds, cardamom, and cinnamon
5. When well mixed, add the apple puree and finally the beaten egg whites
6. Cook for 1.5 hours
7. Serve with sugar
8. Instead of apples, gooseberries or rhubarb stalks can be used

title: Wedding Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 5 eggs
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup coffee
- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 nutmeg
- a little candied peel
- cinnamon
- cloves
- allspice
- 1 glass brandy

## Directions

1. Dissolve the soda in the coffee
2. Mix all ingredients together
3. Bake in a hot oven

title: White Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- ½ cup butter
- whites of 4 eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3 cups flour
- 3 small teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Stir sugar and butter until cream
2. Then stir in the milk and flour, little by little
3. Finally fold in the lightly beaten whites
4. The cake must be stirred well before the whites are added

title: White Scones
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kilogram Pillsbury flour
- 200 grams margarine
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 65 grams powdered sugar
- a little cardamom
- 2 eggs
- 125 grams sultana raisins
- 3 3/4 deciliters half-skimmed milk
- milk

## Directions

1. Mix the Pillsbury flour, margarine, baking powder, powdered sugar, and cardamom together
2. Stir in the eggs, sultana raisins, and half-skimmed milk
3. Knead the batter and roll out into 6 round, flat cakes of 1/4 inch thickness
4. Cut each cake into 4 pieces
5. Place the triangles on a baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes in a hot oven
6. 5 minutes before removing from the oven, brush with milk to brown
7. Serve as fresh as possible, split while warm, spread with butter, and eat folded together

title: Whole Preserved Raspberries (At fylte Hindbar hele)
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries
- Powdered sugar at 2 pounds per pound of berries

## Directions

1. Layer the raspberries in an earthenware pot alternating with powdered sugar, with sugar on top and bottom
2. Let stand 2 days
3. Put on a gentle fire and cook very slowly for a short time
4. After several days, drain the juice and bring to a boil

title: Whole Roasted Pork Tenderloin with Compote and Salad
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) pork tenderloin
- Butter, clarified butter, or Palmin
- Thin broth or water
- Food coloring
- Salt
- Flour
- Maggi bouillon cubes
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Remove fat and membranes from the pork tenderloin, beat lightly with a meat mallet and lard if desired.
2. Brown a slice of butter, clarified butter, or Palmin in a pot and place the meat inside.
3. When sufficiently browned, drip either thin broth or water mixed with food coloring and salt over it.
4. Roast over low heat.
5. When the meat is tender, strain the gravy and skim off the fat; use this fat to brown the flour, which is then thinned to make a good sauce, which can be given more strength with the help of Maggi bouillon cubes.
6. When serving, coat the pork tenderloin with some of the sauce, garnish with parsley and serve with compote and salad.

title: Wiener-Schnitzel
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- veal or mixed veal and pork
- pepper
- salt
- butter or Palmin
- gravy
- lemon
- herring or anchovies
- capers
- potato salad or browned potatoes
- grated horseradish

## Directions

1. The meat used can be either cut veal, cut like fricandeau, or minced veal and pork, prepared like veal carbonade. Sprinkle with pepper and salt and fry in butter or Palmin on a pan.
2. When the meat is fried, add more butter and gravy to the pan; boil it down to a good gravy sauce, which at serving is poured over the meat.
3. On each piece place a slice of lemon and in the center of it a rolled, boneless herring or a flaked anchovy along with some capers.
4. Either potato salad or browned potatoes and grated horseradish can be eaten with this. Wiener-Schnitzel can be made from pork tenderloin, but then the gravy must be mixed with Maggi bouillon cubes.

title: Wiener-Schnitzel (Viennese Breaded Veal Cutlet)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cutlet from the inner side of shoulder
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- crushed zwieback
- butter
- stock
- lemon
- horseradish
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from the inner side of the shoulder near to the bone in the shape and thickness of cutlets
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, brush with beaten eggs using a feather and turn in crushed zwieback
3. Fry to a light golden brown in a pan with lightly browned butter
4. Serve with a sauce made from the pan drippings thinned with stock
5. Place a lemon slice on top of each piece when serving and garnish the dish with grated horseradish
6. Serve with boiled, peeled potatoes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Wienerbrød (Viennese Pastry)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg flour
- 75 gr powdered sugar
- 1/2 L lukewarm milk
- 50 gr yeast
- salt
- 1/4 kg margarine
- ground cardamom
- egg
- chopped almonds
- powdered sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Mix the flour and powdered sugar with the lukewarm milk and yeast mixed with a little salt.
2. Roll out the dough on a board and cover with margarine distributed in lumps, sprinkle with ground cardamom and fold together as for butter dough (see No. 22), repeating 3 to 4 times.
3. Set aside in a cool place to become firmer and easier to handle, then roll out to 1/4 inch thickness.
4. Cut into strips 1 inch wide.
5. Twist and shape as rings, spirals, or braids, or cut the dough into squares.
6. Place on baking sheets, brush with egg, sprinkle with chopped almonds and set to rise.
7. As soon as sufficiently risen, sprinkle with plenty of powdered sugar and bake in a very hot oven.
8. The finished, lukewarm bread can be glazed.

title: Wienerkage (Viennese Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pound Butter
- 8 Egg yolks
- 2 Pounds powdered sugar
- 2 Pounds fine wheat flour
- 1/4 Kilo hart's horn salt
- Grated lemon peel
- 8 Egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Clarify the butter and stir it white.
2. Beat the egg yolks with 2 pounds powdered sugar and add to the butter.
3. Add 2 pounds fine wheat flour that has first been mixed with 1/4 kilo hart's horn salt and a little grated lemon peel.
4. When this is well mixed together, carefully fold in the 8 whipped egg whites.
5. Bake the cakes in paper forms like layer cake.
6. Place cream between the individual layers.
7. Glaze the top with white sugar and decorate with preserves.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Wienerkage (Viennese Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 2 pounds powdered sugar
- 2 pounds flour
- 4 ore deer antler salt
- a little grated lemon zest
- 8 egg whites, beaten
- cream for layering
- white sugar for glazing
- jam for decoration

## Directions

1. Clarify 1 pound butter and beat white.
2. Beat 8 egg yolks with 2 pounds powdered sugar and add to the butter, likewise 2 pounds flour which has first been mixed with 4 ore deer antler salt and a little grated lemon zest.
3. When well beaten together, carefully add the 8 beaten egg whites.
4. Bake the cakes in paper forms as layer cake, and between each layer place cream.
5. Glaze the top with white sugar and decorate with jam.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Wienerkage (Viennese Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound washed butter
- 1 pound wheat flour
- 1 pound finely ground white or powdered sugar
- 1 pound sweet almonds finely ground
- a few bitter almonds
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Knead together the butter, flour, sugar, sweet almonds, bitter almonds, and lemon zest well on a board
2. Roll out with flour into thin sheets
3. Cut to the size of a plate or whatever size suits the pan where the cake will be served
4. Bake the sheets slowly, one at a time, until light brown
5. Trim the edges while still warm
6. When cold, layer them with different jams, apple pulp, or very thick cream between each layer
7. Do not bake the assembled cake, but it can be covered with icing sugar and placed in a lukewarm oven to dry
8. It is more palatable to cover the whole cake with whipped cream just before serving
9. Serves approximately 20 persons

title: Wildsvineryg, stegt som Dyreryg (Roasted Wild Boar Back like Venison)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar back
- Butter or margarine
- Red wine
- Good stock
- Butter
- Flour
- Lemon or orange peel
- Red currant or raspberry jelly

## Directions

1. Cut the back as you would venison back, remove the skin, and lard the back thickly and cover with ample butter or margarine.
2. During roasting, baste it with a mixture of red wine and good stock.
3. For the sauce, brown butter and flour, thin with the stock from which the fat has been skimmed.
4. Add a very small amount of lemon or orange peel, bring to a boil, and remove before serving.
5. Stir red currant or raspberry jelly into the sauce.

title: Wreath Cake (Krandskage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ Pounds sweet almonds
- 2 Ounces bitter almonds
- 1½ Pounds dry powdered Sugar
- Zest of 1 Lemon, grated
- 6 Egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks

## Directions

1. Blanch and dry the sweet and bitter almonds
2. Grind them slightly
3. Mix with the dry powdered sugar
4. Add the grated lemon zest
5. Fold in the beaten egg whites
6. Form wreaths of the batter, each one smaller than the previous, so they can be stacked on top of each other when they are done

title: Wygte engelſk Plum-Budding (English Plum Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound currants
- 1 pound raisins, pitted
- 1 pound almonds, blanched and chopped
- 1 pound chopped suet
- 1 pound flour
- 1 pound breadcrumbs
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon and cloves mixed
- Zest of 2 lemons
- 2 pints rum
- 12 well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Mix together currants, raisins, almonds, suet, flour, breadcrumbs, cinnamon and cloves, lemon zest, and rum
2. Add 12 well-beaten eggs
3. Boil in a napkin well dusted with flour for 4.5 hours
4. Serve with melted butter with sherry and sugar, or brandy sauce

title: Yankee Pork and Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart small white beans
- Water
- Soda
- Lard
- Molasses or syrup
- Salt pork
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Soak one quart of small white beans in cold water overnight.
2. The next morning, strain them into fresh water and let them dry.
3. Place them in cold water with 3 tablespoons of soda over the fire; let them boil for approximately half an hour and drain the water.
4. Take an earthenware pot and place a slice of lard in the bottom, then the beans with two or three tablespoons of molasses.
5. Next place in the center 1 to 3 pounds of salt pork with the skin scored crosswise; add a little pepper and, if needed, a little salt.
6. Pour boiling water over until it covers the meat.
7. Let it bake 6 hours or more over an even, moderate fire, adding boiling water as needed to keep it moist as evaporation requires.
8. It cannot easily be baked too long.
9. Keep the lid on to prevent scorching on top, but remove it for one hour before serving so the pork becomes crispy.

title: Yellow Cherries (Gule Kirsebær)
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- powdered sugar equal in weight to the berries

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and extract the stones using the help of a cut quill.
2. Weigh the berries and take as much powdered sugar as they weigh.
3. Cook the sugar in the usual manner into a thick syrup.
4. Place the berries in the syrup and cook for 1/4 hour, skim well, and remove with a slotted spoon, placing them in a deep dish.
5. Cook the syrup again until it is like a fairly thick syrup.
6. Return the berries to the syrup and give it one more boil.
7. Remove the pot from the fire and shake the berries well, then let them rest until they appear clear and full, then divide into smaller jars and treat in the usual manner.
8. If desired, the stones can be crushed, the kernels removed and mixed with the berries.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Yelva-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams margarine
- 250 grams fine sugar
- 3 eggs
- a little cardamom
- a few drops bitter almond oil
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 750 grams Pillsbury flour
- 250 milliliters semi-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Mix margarine, fine sugar, eggs, cardamom, bitter almond oil, and baking powder well
2. Add Pillsbury flour and semi-skimmed milk
3. Bake in a greased form for 1 hour 15 minutes

title: Yelva-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kilogram margarine
- 1/4 kilogram powdered sugar
- 3 eggs
- a little cardamom
- a few drops bitter almond oil
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 3/4 kilogram Pillsbury flour
- 1/4 liter half-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Mix the margarine, powdered sugar, eggs, cardamom, bitter almond oil, and baking powder well together
2. Add the Pillsbury flour and half-skimmed milk
3. Bake in a greased form for 5 quarters of an hour

title: York-Cake
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams margarine
- 3 eggs
- 1/4 kilogram powdered sugar
- a few drops lemon oil
- a little cardamom
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 kilogram Pillsbury flour
- 1/2 liter half-skimmed milk

## Directions

1. Mix the margarine with the eggs, powdered sugar, lemon oil, cardamom, and baking powder
2. Stir in the Pillsbury flour and half-skimmed milk
3. Pour the batter into a greased form and bake for 1 hour

title: Zigeunerbudding (Gypsy Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pot sweet cream
- 6 quintals isinglass dissolved in water
- 1 quarter-pound sugar
- currant or raspberry jam

## Directions

1. Whip 1 pot sweet cream to foam.
2. Bring to a boil 6 quintals isinglass dissolved in water and 1 quarter-pound sugar.
3. When almost cold, carefully stir this into the whipped cream along with some currant or raspberry jam.
4. Line the bottom of a mold with jam and place some oval sugar cookies around the sides.
5. Fill the pudding into the mold and let it stand in a cold place for several hours.
6. Turn the pudding out of the mold onto a dish and place some sugar cookies around it.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æble Sauce (Apple Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Water
- sweet Almonds
- Lemon Juice and Shell
- Sugar
- Corn Starch
- Water

## Directions

1. Cut the Apples into pieces and boil them in Water together with some crushed sweet Almonds.
2. Then rub them through a Colander, put them back on with a little Lemon Juice and Shell, Sugar and a little Corn Starch, stirred in Water, and boil quite dry.

title: Æble-Gelee (Apple Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 pounds peeled and cut sour apples
- 1 pint water
- 1/2 ounce red gelatine
- 1/2 pint white wine
- rind of 1/2 lemon
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook the 1 1/2 pounds of peeled and cut sour apples very soft in an enameled pot with 1 pint of water.
2. Then press them through a sieve and blend while warm with 1/2 ounce of red gelatine dissolved in 1/2 pint of white wine.
3. Add the grated peel of 1/2 and the juice of 2 lemons, 1 pound of powdered sugar, and some cinnamon.
4. Keep the whole warm while stirring constantly and pour it into a mold first rinsed with salad oil.
5. When completely cold, turn it out onto a chilled dish and vanilla sauce can be served with it.

title: Æble-Gelé (Apple Jelly)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg (3 pounds) stone sugar
- 3/4 dl (3/4 cup) water for 1/2 kg (1 pound) fruit juice
- tart apples
- 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) water to each 1/2 kg (1 pound) apples

## Directions

1. Use tart apples; do not peel them as the skin especially contains the jelly substance.
2. Cut them into 4 to 6 pieces and remove the core, blossom, stem, and bruised spots.
3. Rinse the fruit and cook completely soft in 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) water to each 1/2 kg (1 pound) apples.
4. Pour into a jelly cloth where it remains overnight.
5. The next day weigh the clear juice.
6. Cook sugar and water as in recipe 93 and, after it is again dry, pour the juice into it, cook and skim for 10 minutes.
7. Pour the jelly into cups and bind when it has begun to thicken.

title: Æble-Gelé med Citron, Appelsin, Vin eller Vanille (Apple Jelly with Lemon, Orange, Wine or Vanilla)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- apples, cooked as described previously
- lemon, orange, wine, or vanilla as flavoring

## Directions

1. After the apples have been cooked as described above, add one of the aforementioned flavorings to the clear juice, and then weigh it.
2. Cook the jelly as with the other types of jelly.
3. Cook the apple pulp with sugar to make Russian marmalade.

title: Æble-Grød (Apple Pudding)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg good large apples
- ¼ L water
- Sugar to taste
- Rice flour (optional for thickening)

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and cut away the core
2. Rinse them
3. Place in a well-enameled pot with ¼ L water on the bottom
4. Cook the apples over low heat without stirring until tender
5. Mash them, sweeten, and pour into bowls
6. Can be thickened with a little thinned rice flour
7. Alternatively, cook the apples with the skin on and press the pudding through a coarse sieve
8. Serve lukewarm with cream or crème

title: Æble-Grød (Apple Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- whole apples
- water
- sugar
- corn starch (if needed)

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, then cook them in water or preferably by steaming
2. As the apples are peeled, cast them into water to prevent them from turning red
3. Discard this water
4. When the apples are soft, press them through a coarse sieve so the core remains behind
5. Place them back on the fire to prepare with sugar
6. Alternatively, slice the peeled apples into small pieces after they have lain in cold water, and place them over the fire with little water added as the apples themselves are watery
7. Let them cook quite evenly under a covered lid without stirring until they are fully cooked
8. Then stir vigorously and add white sugar according to the apples' sourness
9. Stir briskly during the cooking the sauce now undergoes, and reserve it to be eaten cold with sugar and cream
10. If it becomes too thin, take a couple of spoonfuls of corn starch mixed in a little cold water, which is added after the sugar and allowed to cook only a couple of minutes
11. To prevent it from sticking to the bottom, while the pot is clean and dry, grease it inside with cold butter and then rinse it with cold water
12. Do not place it over a flame fire

title: Æble-Kompot (Apple Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 gr powdered sugar
- 1.5 dl water for 0.5 kg fruit

## Directions

1. The apples used must be fine and firm apples that do not easily fall apart.
2. Peel them, place in water with vinegar and cook as pears.
3. Since fresh apples can be obtained throughout the winter, it is probably the fruit least used in the household for preserving as compote.

title: Æble-Kompot (Apple Compote)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Kg (2 lb) good, not too sweet apples
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) Melis sugar
- 1/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægel) water
- a little vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, cut in half, and remove the cores
2. Rinse in water with a little vinegar
3. Make a syrup from 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) Melis sugar and 1/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægel) water
4. Place the apples in the syrup
5. Watch carefully during cooking, remove each piece as it becomes cooked, and place in the serving bowl
6. If the juice is not sufficiently smooth, boil it a bit longer and strain before pouring over the apples
7. Just before serving, the hollow where the core was removed can be filled with a little currant, gooseberry, or apple jelly, but the fruit pieces must not lie on top of each other

title: Æble-Kompot (Apple Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh, juicy apples
- white wine
- water
- lemon peel
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, cut them into 4 pieces, and remove the cores
2. Treat as for pear compote, but they do not tolerate boiling as long as pears and must be removed carefully so they do not fall apart
3. If making a larger portion, divide the apples into more pieces and cook a batch at a time in the same sauce
4. The sauce must be reduced slightly before pouring over the apples
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æble-Marmelade (Apple Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe apples
- water

## Directions

1. The treatment for this is the same as in the preceding recipe; the difference is only that the pears are omitted.

title: Æble-Marmelade (Apple Jam)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Powdered sugar (125 gr to 1/2 kg apple pulp)
- Water (1 1/4 dl per 1/2 kg apples)

## Directions

1. The apples for this can either be peeled first with the seed cavity and blossom end removed, or they can simply be wiped dry and cut in 4 pieces.
2. The cooking must take place over very low heat and covered until the apples are completely cooked down.
3. Press the pulp through a coarse sieve and weigh it.
4. The sugar must be added only after the pulp has been strained through the sieve.
5. Cook the jam down until it is sufficiently stiff.
6. Fill it into jars, cover with paper and tie up.
7. The English often add a portion of lemon juice to the apple pulp. Chopped or grated almonds or nuts can be stirred into the jam when using it.

title: Æble-Marmelade med Ingefær (Apple Jam with Ginger)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Chopped apples with seed cavity and blossom end removed
- Powdered sugar (1 1/2 kg to 2 kg apple pulp)
- Water (1/2 l)
- Ground ginger (50 gr), bound in a cloth

## Directions

1. Put the chopped apples in the pot together with 1/2 l water and the ground ginger, bound in a piece of cloth.
2. When the apples are completely cooked, press the pulp through the sieve.
3. Weigh it and cook with the sugar until it is sufficiently stiff; then fill into jars, cover with paper and tie up.

title: Æble-Marmelade med Kirsebærsaft (Apple Jam with Cherry Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Powdered sugar (125 gr)
- Cherry juice (1/4 l to 1/2 kg pulp)

## Directions

1. After the apples are cooked and the pulp pressed through the sieve, weigh it, then add sugar and cherry juice.
2. Cook the jam down to a suitable consistency, fill into jars or crocks, cover with paper and tie up.

title: Æble-Omelet (Apple Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large apples
- Water
- 0.5 pound butter
- 0.5 pound white sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Peel and slice apples, remove cores
2. Cook to a pulp with a little water
3. When cooked, stir in butter and white sugar
4. When cooled, stir in egg yolks
5. Finally fold in beaten egg whites
6. Sprinkle form or pan with bread crumbs
7. Bake to a light brown
8. Serves about 8 persons
9. Serve warm with powdered sugar

title: Æble-Omelet å l'anglaise (Apple Omelet à l'anglaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- sugar
- essence
- 8 good apples
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and add sugar and essence as mentioned in Nr. 1012.
2. Peel and cut 8 good apples into smaller pieces and fry them on a pan in butter and as much sugar as the tartness of the apples requires.
3. When they are lightly browned, pour the egg mixture over them and turn and bake the omelet on both sides.
4. Sprinkle with sugar and serve immediately on a warm dish.

title: Æble-Pyramide (Apple Pyramid)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- good, beautiful apples of equal size
- 1/2 kg powdered sugar
- 3.75 dl water
- ladyfinger cake, macarons, or small cakes

## Directions

1. Rinse the apples, remove the blossom, scrape the stem, and cook without peeling.
2. Boil 1/2 kg powdered sugar with 3.75 dl water to make a syrup.
3. Cook the apples tender in the syrup.
4. Arrange the apples in a pyramid while as hot as possible.
5. Strain the syrup in which they were cooked and pour over them.
6. Serve immediately with ladyfinger cake, macarons, or small cakes.

title: Æble-Soufflé (Apple Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Water
- Lemon zest
- Sugar
- Egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the apples in a little water with lemon zest and press through a colander.
2. Blend with sugar to taste.
3. Finally fold in egg whites that have been whipped to stiff peaks with sugar.
4. Treat otherwise as with other soufflés.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æble-Suppe (Apple Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg apples
- 5 L water
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- rice flour
- wine
- sugar

## Directions

1. Use sour apples, do not peel but divide in 4 pieces removing blossom, stem and core
2. Cook in 1 L water with cinnamon and lemon peel, then press through a coarse sieve with the remaining 4 L water
3. Thicken with rice flour mixed with water and cook at least 5 minutes
4. Season with wine and sweeten to taste

title: Æble-Suppe (Apple Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg dried apples
- 5 L water
- Whole cinnamon
- Lemon peel
- Rice flour
- Sherry

## Directions

1. Rinse the apples, soak them overnight and cook together with whole cinnamon and lemon peel in the water in which they have been standing.
2. When the apples are completely tender, pour them into a coarse sieve and press through.
3. Thicken the soup with a little rice flour stirred in water, sweeten and prepare with sherry.
4. Serve with rusks, baked bread or hardtack.

title: Æblegelée (Apple Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour apples
- Powdered sugar (equal weight to the strained juice)

## Directions

1. The treatment is entirely as described in recipe 475, only the apples are not peeled
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æblegrød (Apple Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 pounds good apples (not too juicy)
- 1 pot water
- A couple of cinnamon sticks
- A little lemon peel
- Vanilla
- Cold butter
- Powdered sugar
- Cold sweet cream or creme for serving

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the apples into pieces, removing the cores, and place the pieces in cold water.
2. Smear the bottom of an enameled pot with cold butter and place it over heat with 1 pot of water, a couple of cinnamon sticks, a little lemon peel, and vanilla.
3. When the water boils, add the apple pieces and cook until completely tender.
4. Remove from heat and mash the pieces into a smooth porridge, or press them through a colander; in the latter case, give the porridge a boil.
5. Add powdered sugar and serve either lukewarm or cooled with cold sweet cream or creme.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æblegrød med Mandeldejg (Apple Sauce with Almond Paste)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 kg apples
- powdered sugar
- 1/4 kg blanched, sweet ground almonds
- bitter almonds
- 1/4 kg powdered sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites (beaten)

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, cut into 4 pieces, remove the core, rinse, and cook over heat with a little water.
2. When cooked, sweeten the apple sauce with powdered sugar.
3. Make an almond paste from 1/4 kg blanched, sweet ground almonds, a few bitter almonds, 1/4 kg powdered sugar, 6 egg yolks, and finally the beaten egg whites.
4. Spread the apple sauce on flat baking sheets, cover with the almond paste, and place in a moderate oven.
5. Serve lukewarm, decorated with whipped cream.

title: Æblegrød med stødte Makroner (Apple Sauce with Crushed Macarons)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- apple sauce
- crushed macarons
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Cook the apple sauce and arrange lukewarm as in the previous recipe.
2. Instead of almond paste, top with a layer of crushed macarons.
3. Top with whipped cream.
4. Do not place in the oven.

title: Æblekage med Brød (Apple Cake with Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- lemon zest
- cinnamon
- sugar
- white wine or water
- 1 pound grated French bread
- butter
- powdered sugar
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and remove the cores
2. Cook them with lemon zest, cinnamon, sugar, and a little white wine or water into a compote or porridge
3. Bake 1 pound grated French bread golden brown in butter
4. Butter a form and dust with grated bread
5. In the bottom of the form lay first a layer of bread, then a layer of apple porridge, over which sprinkle a layer of powdered sugar, then bread again, apple porridge, sugar, and so forth, until the form is full
6. Place it in the oven and bake 1 hour
7. When done and somewhat cooled, turn it onto a dish and serve sprinkled with white powdered sugar and with whipped cream on the side
8. Instead of sugar, one can also place sweet preserves, for example, raspberry marmalade, between the layers
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æblekage med Mandeldejg (Apple Cake with Almond Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- wine or water
- butter
- powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 3 pounds sweet almonds
- 3 quintal bitter almonds
- 8 egg whites
- whipped cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and remove the cores carefully
2. Cook the apple pieces tender with very little wine or water and a piece of butter on the bottom of the cooking pot, stirring only when tender
3. When tender, add powdered sugar to taste
4. Lay the porridge on the bottom of the dish in which it is to be baked, in a layer about 2 fingers thick
5. Make a batter of 8 egg yolks, 3 pounds sweet almonds and 3 quintal bitter almonds and finally the 8 beaten egg whites, which is stirred well together and poured over the apple porridge
6. Bake 1 hour until it is a beautiful light brown, and serve warm with whipped cream with sugar over it
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æblekage med Tvebakker (Apple Cake with Rusks)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 kilograms (5 pounds) apples
- Rusks for 30 øre
- 1/4 kilogram (1/2 pound) melted butter
- Jam, if desired
- Whipped cream from 1/2 liter (1/2 pot) cream
- Red currant jelly for decoration

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, cut them into quarters, and remove the cores
2. Rinse and cook them until tender in a small amount of water under a closed lid over low heat
3. Sweeten them and layer them in a greased form alternating with crushed rusks, starting and ending with crushed rusks
4. Press the cake firmly together with a spoon before pouring 1/4 kilogram of melted butter over it
5. Jam can be placed between the layers
6. Place the cake in a warm oven for about half an hour
7. Once cold, turn it out of the form and decorate with whipped cream and red currant jelly
8. The cake does not need to be placed in the oven if the bread has been previously baked in butter on a griddle, and butter can be saved in that case

title: Æblekranse (Apple Fritters)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg good, fine, large apples
- 1/4 kg flour
- 3 egg yolks
- 50 g melted margarine
- 1/4 L semi-skimmed milk
- a little sugar
- salt
- cardamom
- egg whites, whipped

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and cut them into slices about 1/4 inch thick, removing the core from the center
2. Make a batter from the flour, egg yolks, melted margarine, semi-skimmed milk, a little sugar, salt, cardamom and the whipped egg whites
3. Take the apple slices with a fork and dip them in the batter, making sure they are completely covered
4. Fry them in boiling clarified butter until they have a nice light brown color
5. The batter covering the center can be shaped so that the wreaths are nicely formed
6. Serve very hot with powdered sugar

title: Æblekranse (Apple Rings)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 Kg (3 S) good, fine, large apples
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) fine flour
- 3 egg yolks
- 50 Gr (10 Kvint) melted margarine
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) half-skimmed milk
- a little sugar
- salt
- cardamom
- egg whites
- clarified butter

## Directions

1. Peel 1 1/2 Kg (3 S) good, fine, large apples and cut them into 1/4 inch thick slices, from which the core is removed from the middle.
2. For this portion, stir a dough of 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) fine flour, 3 egg yolks, 50 Gr (10 Kvint) melted margarine, 1/4 L (1 Pægl) half-skimmed milk, a little sugar, salt, cardamom and the beaten egg whites.
3. Take the apples with a fork and dip them into the dough, which must completely cover them; from there, place them in boiling clarified butter until they have a nice, light brown colour.
4. The dough covering the middle can be removed so that the rings are nicely shaped.

title: Æbler (Dried Apples)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Firm, slightly tart and partly ripe apples
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and dry them whole, cut into 4 pieces, or cut into rings with the core removed using a core remover
2. As the fruit is peeled, immediately place it in salt water (6 to 8 grams salt to 1 liter cold water)
3. Before drying, place the fruit on a sieve so all water can drain off
4. Place whole apples in the oven side by side with the stem end facing upward
5. Place quartered pieces with the round side against the tray
6. Lay rings in rows so they overlap slightly, as they will shrink as they dry
7. Drying time for whole fruits is up to 4 to 5 hours, while cut pieces take at most 2 to 3 hours
8. The rings should be spongy when broken to check if they are finished
9. Store the apples in boxes or linen bags

title: Æbler i Gelée (Apples in Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wine Jelly (Nr. 424)
- apples, peeled and cored
- sugar
- cinnamon
- white wine

## Directions

1. Pour the Wine Jelly into small molds and set to stiffen
2. Using a silver spoon dipped in hot water, remove enough jelly from the center of each mold to make room for a piece of apple
3. Peel the apples, remove the cores, and cook the pieces with sugar and cinnamon in a little white wine
4. Melt the removed jelly and use it to fill the holes where the apple pieces are placed
5. Set the jelly to cool
6. When ready to serve, quickly dip the molds in hot water, dry them, and turn the jelly out onto a dish so that the whole forms a pyramid
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æbler à la Baloise (Apples à la Baloise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 good apples
- dough as for cream puffs
- 1 tablespoon powdered sugar
- a little vanilla essence
- clarified butter

## Directions

1. Peel 8 good apples, cut them into very small pieces and stir into a dough as for cream puffs (Nr. 1024); add 1 tablespoon powdered sugar and a little vanilla essence.
2. Drop it with a dessert spoon into oblong balls, which are cooked light brown in clarified butter.
3. Serve hot, sprinkled with powdered sugar.

title: Æblesaft (Apple Juice)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Various types of apples
- Generous 1/2 liter (1/2 Pot) water per 1/2 kg apples
- 1/2 kg (1/2 S) powdered sugar Melis per 1 liter (1 Pot) juice

## Directions

1. Wipe the apples, cut them into 4 to 6 pieces, and cook them out in generous 1/2 liter (1/2 Pot) water per 1/2 kg apples.
2. Pour the mass into the jelly cloth.
3. Only the next day, measure the juice, boil it with the sugar for 10 minutes, and fill into bottles that are bound immediately.
4. The apple juice will have a beautiful clear color and makes an excellent dessert.

title: Æbleskiver (Apple Pancakes) Nr. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- half a Cup Butter
- half a Cup Sugar
- 4 Egg Yolks
- 1 Quart Milk
- 2 Cents Yeast
- Flour as needed
- Egg Whites (whipped)

## Directions

1. Stir half a Cup Butter well with half a Cup Sugar, then with 4 Egg Yolks
2. Warm 1 Quart Milk and dissolve 2 Cents Yeast in it
3. Add the stirred mixture and sift in as much Flour as will make the batter properly stiff (somewhat thicker than for pancakes)
4. Fold in the whipped Egg Whites
5. Beat well in a crock which, covered, stands by the heat for a couple of hours so the batter can rise
6. When the pan is hot, put a little Butter in each hole and fill the hole a little more than half
7. When baked enough on one side, turn them with two Forks

title: Æbleskiver med Kjærnemælk (Buttermilk Apple Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Pints buttermilk
- 4 Eggs
- 1 Pound wheat flour
- 2 Teaspoonfuls sodium bicarbonate

## Directions

1. Stir 4 eggs into 5 pints buttermilk.
2. Add 1 pound wheat flour and 2 teaspoonfuls sodium bicarbonate.
3. Bake and serve in the usual manner.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æbleskiver med Æbler (Apple Pancakes with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled apples, without cores, cut into pieces the size of a large nut
- 5 Eggs
- 1 Pint cream
- 0.5 Pint cold water
- 1 Pint melted butter
- 1 Pint fine wheat flour

## Directions

1. Beat 5 eggs with 1 pint cream and 0.5 pint cold water.
2. Add 1 pint melted butter and 1 pint fine wheat flour.
3. When the æbleskiver pan is well heated, put butter in it.
4. Dip the apple pieces in the batter and place them in the holes.
5. Fill the holes with more batter.
6. Bake beautifully golden brown.
7. Turn the æbleskiver and serve hot with caster sugar over.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æbleskiver med Æbler (Apple Pancakes with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled apples, without cores
- 5 Eggs
- 1 Pint cream
- 0.5 Pint cold water
- 1 Pint melted butter
- 1 Pint flour

## Directions

1. Peel apples, remove the cores, and cut into pieces the size of a large nut.
2. Beat 5 eggs with 1 pint cream and 0.5 pint cold water.
3. Add 1 pint melted butter and 1 pint flour.
4. When the æbleskiver pan is hot, put butter in it.
5. Dip the apple pieces in the batter and lay them in the holes.
6. Fill the holes with more batter.
7. Bake to a beautiful golden brown.
8. Turn the æbleskiver and serve warm with caster sugar over.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æbleskiver Nr. 2 (Apple Pancakes Nr. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 Pints Milk
- 6 small Spoonfuls Butter
- 1 Pound Flour
- 6 Eggs
- grated Lemon Peel
- 2 Tablespoons white Sugar
- 2 Cents Yeast

## Directions

1. Set 1.5 Pints Milk on the fire with 6 small Spoonfuls Butter and when melted, add it little by little to 1 Pound Flour and beat well with 6 Eggs (one at a time), a little grated Lemon Peel, 2 Tablespoons white Sugar, and finally 2 Cents Yeast dissolved in a little warm Milk
2. Treat as the preceding recipe
3. If you wish Apples baked in, peel and hollow out the Apples, cut them in round slices and cook in Water with Sugar and, if desired, Wine; on the pan put first a little Batter, then a little Apple and then again a little Batter

title: Æbleskiver. 1. (Apple Pancake Balls 1)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 tb) fine flour
- 1 L (1 Pot) half-skimmed milk
- 4 egg yolks
- a little sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- lemon or orange essence
- cardamom
- 1 teaspoon Hjortetaksalt
- egg whites
- clarified butter or butter

## Directions

1. In fine flour, stir 1 L (1 Pot) half-skimmed milk, 4 egg yolks, a little sugar, 1 teaspoon salt, lemon or orange essence, cardamom and 1 teaspoon Hjortetaksalt together, and lastly add the beaten egg whites.
2. The apple pancake ball pan must be hot, and the holes well supplied with clarified butter or butter or a mixture of both, which is easiest done if melted in a small pot.
3. The dough is best poured from a pitcher, and the holes are filled halfway with dough.
4. When the apple pancake balls are baked on one side, before they are turned, a little apple sauce, a piece of cooked apple or rhubarb compote can be placed in each, and the apple pancake balls are then baked finished on the other side, but it is preferable to serve the fruit separately, as the apple pancake balls will rise better that way.
5. They must be served as hot as possible and sprinkled with sugar.
6. If yeast is used for the apple pancake balls, use 40 Gr (8 Kvint), 4 eggs, 2 to 3 tablespoons powdered sugar, cardamom, 1/2 Kg (1 S) flour and 1/2 L (1/2 Pot) lukewarm milk, in which 75 Gr (15 Kvint) margarine is melted. Set to rise for a couple of hours.

title: Æbleskiver. 2. (Apple Pancake Balls 2)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Kg (1 S) fine flour
- 1 L (1 Pot) buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- salt
- grated lemon zest
- cardamom
- Palmin or butter

## Directions

1. In fine flour, stir 1 L (1 Pot) buttermilk, 1 teaspoon baking soda, salt, grated lemon zest and cardamom to taste together and bake immediately in generous Palmin or butter.

title: Æblesmør (Apple Butter)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Pear or Apple Syrup (before it is completely reduced)
- Cooked, strained apple sauce without any sugar added

## Directions

1. Before the Pear or Apple Syrup is completely reduced, stir in the cooked, strained apple sauce without any sugar added
2. Cook the juice a while longer before pouring into jars
3. The apple sauce produces an excellent effect; this is called Apple Butter and will certainly become highly valued as it becomes more known
4. Spread on French bread or crackers it tastes refreshing and is also a very healthy food; can be preserved for years

title: Æblesuppe (Apple Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds apples
- 3 pots water
- a little lemon peel
- a couple of sticks cinnamon
- a little rice flour or potato flour
- sugar to taste
- lemon juice
- 1 spoonful old wine
- small rusks

## Directions

1. For this soup, one preferably uses dried apples, though fresh ones can also be used.
2. Peel and cut 3 pounds of apples into pieces and cook under a lid with 3 pots of water and a little lemon peel, until they are so tender that they can be mashed through a sieve.
3. Put the strained soup back over the fire with a couple of sticks of cinnamon, let it come to a boil, and thicken it with a little rice flour or potato flour.
4. Before serving, add sugar and lemon juice according to how sweet or sour the soup is, and 1 spoonful of old wine.
5. Serve the soup with small rusks.
6. For fresh fruit, large juicy varieties are preferable; a smaller portion can also be pears.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æg i Sky (Eggs in Aspic)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Hard-boiled eggs
- Good aspic
- Isinglass
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs until hard, then peel them.
2. Cut a little off one end so the eggs can stand upright on the plate.
3. Pour good aspic stiffened with isinglass between the eggs.
4. Alternatively, halve the eggs and place them with the cut side down.
5. Place a little parsley on top of each egg.

title: Æg i smaa Jydepotter (Eggs in Small Earthenware Pots)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Salt
- White pepper
- Parmesan cheese
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Butter the earthenware pots
2. Pour an egg into each one
3. Sprinkle with a little salt, white pepper, and grated Parmesan cheese if desired
4. Place the pots in a hot oven in a baking pan with boiling water poured in
5. When the eggs are set, serve immediately
6. Sprinkle with chopped parsley

title: Ægge-Omelet (Egg Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 measure sweet cream or milk
- 1 teaspoon flour
- ground pepper
- salt
- 14 drams butter

## Directions

1. Beat 6 eggs well with 1 measure sweet cream or milk, 1 teaspoon flour, and a little ground pepper and salt
2. Melt 14 drams butter in a pan
3. Pour the egg mixture into the melted butter
4. When stiff, the omelet is ready
5. Serve with powdered sugar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ægge-Sauce (Egg Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Six egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 125 Gr (1/4 tb) cold butter
- 3 3/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) cold water
- Insignificant amount of sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Stir together six egg yolks with 1 tablespoon flour, the juice of 1 lemon, 125 Gr (1/4 tb) cold butter, 3 3/4 Dl (1 1/2 Pægl) cold water and insignificant sugar
2. Stir continuously until the sauce comes to a boil
3. Then season it with salt and with more acid if needed
4. This sauce is eaten with boiled meat and fish dishes

title: Ægge-Soufflé (Egg Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 measures sweet cream
- 1/2 pound white sugar
- 4 tablespoons pulverized orris root
- 8-10 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- pearl sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 2 measures sweet cream with 1/2 pound white sugar and 4 tablespoons pulverized orris root
2. Beat 8-10 egg yolks and pour the boiling cream into them
3. Pour the mixture back into the pan and heat with constant beating until thick
4. Pour out and continue beating until cold
5. Fold in 6 beaten egg whites
6. Put the soufflé in a gentle oven heat to rise slowly
7. Serve on a cake dish, sprinkled with pearl sugar
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ægge-Suppe (Egg Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 L (4 Potter) water
- whole cinnamon
- lemon peel
- 125 Gr (1/4 S) sago flour stirred in 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cold water
- 8 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- 375 Gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar
- 1/4 L (1 Pægl) sherry
- 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) rhubarb juice or white wine
- small rusks

## Directions

1. Boil the water for a few minutes with whole cinnamon and lemon peel and thicken with 125 Gr (1/4 S) sago flour stirred in 1/4 L (1 Pægl) cold water.
2. In a tureen, beat 8 egg yolks and 3 egg whites with 375 Gr (1/4 S) powdered sugar until the egg mixture is white and foaming.
3. Pour the hot soup into the egg mixture under vigorous whisking with a whisk.
4. Add 1/4 L (1 Pægl) sherry and 1 1/4 Dl (1/2 Pægl) rhubarb juice or white wine to the soup.
5. Serve small rusks with it.

title: Æggebag med Brød (Egg Cake with Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 6 tablespoons milk
- grated lemon zest
- 6 egg whites
- 1 French bread
- butter
- powdered sugar
- raspberry jam

## Directions

1. Beat the 6 egg yolks until white
2. Mix with 2 tablespoons flour, 6 tablespoons milk, and grated lemon zest
3. Beat the 6 egg whites until stiff and fold in
4. Slice the French bread into small pieces and bake in butter in a pan
5. When baked, pour the egg mixture over the bread slices
6. Bake the cake until light brown
7. Serve with powdered sugar and raspberry jam
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggeblommer til brun Suppe (Egg Ball Dumplings for Brown Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites
- 10 tablespoons sweet cream
- a little grated nutmeg
- a very little sugar
- a couple of tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Beat 10 egg yolks, 5 egg whites, and 10 tablespoons sweet cream well together.
2. Stir in a little grated nutmeg, a very little sugar, and a couple of tablespoons butter.
3. Pour the mixture into a mold that has been coated with butter and breadcrumbs.
4. Cook or bake like a pudding, then cut into oblong pieces and place in the soup when serving.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggegrød (Egg Porridge)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 175 grams potato starch
- 175 grams wheat flour
- 4.5 liters semi-skimmed milk
- butter
- cinnamon
- sugar
- red wine or juice and water

## Directions

1. Heat the milk to boiling
2. Mix the flour with the eggs in some of the cold milk to make a smooth mixture
3. As soon as the milk boils, stir the mixture in quickly
4. Bring to a boil again while stirring continuously
5. Cook for only 5 minutes
6. The porridge should be thin, as it becomes stiff quickly after being served
7. Season with salt and serve immediately
8. Serve with butter, cinnamon, and sugar
9. Serve red wine or juice and water on the side

title: Æggehvide-Kage (Egg White Cake)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kilogram Pillsbury flour
- 1/4 kilogram margarine
- 1/4 kilogram powdered sugar
- a little cardamom
- 3 3/4 deciliters half-skimmed milk
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 125 grams currants
- chopped candied citrus peel
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix together the Pillsbury flour, margarine, powdered sugar, cardamom, half-skimmed milk, baking powder, currants, and chopped candied citrus peel
2. Beat the egg whites and fold into the batter at the end
3. Pour into a greased form and bake for approximately 1 1/4 hours

title: Æggehvide-Kager (Egg White Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound clarified butter
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- ½ pound wheat flour
- 5 egg whites, whipped to stiff peaks
- chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Stir clarified butter for 20 minutes with 1 pound powdered sugar
2. Gradually stir in ½ pound wheat flour
3. Finally fold in 5 egg whites whipped to stiff peaks
4. Place on the pan with a teaspoon
5. Sprinkle with chopped almonds
6. Bake at even oven heat
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggehvidekager (Egg White Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 7 pounds finely ground powdered sugar
- 4 pounds fine flour
- 5 beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Warm 1 pound butter slightly and stir to a salve
2. Stir in 7 pounds finely ground powdered sugar
3. Add 4 pounds fine flour
4. Finally add 5 beaten egg whites
5. Place on the sheet in small dollops
6. Curl slightly
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggekage med Brød (Omelet with Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 6 tablespoons milk
- lemon zest
- 6 egg whites
- French bread
- butter
- powdered sugar
- raspberry jam

## Directions

1. Stir 6 egg yolks until they are white, and then mix with 2 tablespoons of flour, 6 tablespoons of milk, a little grated lemon zest, and 6 egg whites whipped to foam.
2. A French bread is cut into small slices, which are baked in butter in a pan, and when they are baked, pour the egg yolks over them, after which the cake is baked beautifully light brown.
3. It is served with powdered sugar and raspberry jam.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggekage med Flæsk (Omelet with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 8 tablespoons sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon flour
- egg whites
- 3 ounces bacon

## Directions

1. Whip 10 egg yolks until they are thick, into which then stir 8 tablespoons of sweet milk, in which 1 tablespoon of flour has first been dissolved, and finally the whipped egg whites.
2. Fry 3 ounces of bacon in slices in a pan, and drain the fat.
3. Pour the egg batter over the bacon, and the bacon cake is baked until it is light brown on the underside.
4. It can now either be served as is or with the help of a lid be turned and baked light brown on the other side as well.
5. Some like to sprinkle the bacon cake with chopped chives before baking.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggekranse (Egg Wreaths)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons powdered sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix the egg yolks and powdered sugar together very well, always stirring the same direction, until the mixture becomes thick
2. Stir in enough flour to make a loose dough
3. Roll out and cut into long strips
4. Form small wreaths from the strips
5. Fry until golden in melted butter
6. When removed and drained
7. Sprinkle with powdered sugar before serving
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggepunsch (Egg Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 whole eggs
- 9 egg yolks
- 1½ pounds ground, sifted white sugar
- peel of 1 lemon
- juice of 4 lemons
- 5 measures white wine
- 6 measures boiled water
- 4 measures arrack

## Directions

1. Whisk 4 whole eggs and 9 egg yolks to foam in a large saucepan
2. Add 1½ pounds ground, sifted white sugar, to which the peel of a lemon has been grated
3. Whisk again until quite white
4. Press the juice of 4 lemons into it
5. Pour in 5 measures white wine, 6 measures boiled water and 4 measures arrack
6. Set the whole over embers
7. Continue whisking until it begins to rise and foam up
8. Pour the punch into a tureen and serve immediately warm
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggeskaaler (Egg Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure good, thick cream
- 4 eggs
- fine flour
- melted butter
- light powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Work 1 measure good, thick cream and 4 eggs together well with enough fine flour so that the dough can be rolled very thin to the thickness of a straw.
2. Cut into square pieces, double the size of a playing card.
3. Brush with melted butter and sprinkle with light powdered sugar.
4. Bake light brown.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggesuppe (Sago Egg Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound large sago grains
- cold water
- 2 quarts boiling water
- zest of half a lemon
- 1/2 cup raisins
- 3 eggs
- white sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 glass white wine

## Directions

1. Rinse the sago grains in cold water and sprinkle into 2 quarts of boiling water
2. When it has boiled until the grains are swollen, add the zest of half a lemon and 1/2 cup of raisins
3. Stir 3 egg whites in the terrine with white sugar, then with the juice of 1 lemon and 1 glass of white wine
4. When the grains are clear and cooked, heat the soup to boiling over the eggs, stirring constantly and briskly in the terrine
5. It is a wholesome and good-tasting dish and is made in about half an hour's time
6. The wine can be omitted

title: Æggesøbe (Egg Ale Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon ale
- 12 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- sugar
- rye bread cubes
- butter

## Directions

1. Place 1 gallon of ale over the heat
2. Stir 12 egg yolks and 4 whites with sugar until white and thick
3. When the ale boils, pour it in under strong whisking, first a little, then more
4. Continue whisking for a couple of minutes, then pour into the terrine
5. Rye bread cubes fried in butter and sugar are served with it
6. If only lager ale or beer is available, it should first be diluted with water
7. Serves 8 persons

title: Æggesøbe (Egg Drink)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 bottles pale ale
- 7 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- 1/2 Kg (1 S) sugar
- rye bread or French bread cubes baked in butter and sugar, or small rusks

## Directions

1. While the ale is brought to a boil, beat 7 egg yolks and 3 egg whites with 1/2 Kg (1 S) sugar in a tureen until foaming.
2. When pouring, place the tureen with the egg mixture down on the floor, and pour the ale into it under constant whisking with a whisk.
3. At first hold the pot right down to the tureen, but raise it as high as possible during pouring. In this way the egg drink foams well.
4. Serve with rye bread or French bread cubes baked in butter and sugar, or with small rusks.

title: Æggesøbe (Egg Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolks from 10 eggs
- powdered sugar
- 3 pots light beer or double beer
- rye bread or wheat bread cubes, toasted

## Directions

1. Whisk the egg yolks from 10 eggs with powdered sugar until they resemble a white salve
2. Heat 3 pots of light beer or double beer over the fire
3. As soon as it boils, add a little of it to the whisked eggs
4. While whisking continuously, pour the egg mixture into the beer until it begins to boil, then immediately remove from heat and pour into a serving bowl
5. Serve with toasted rye bread or wheat bread cubes
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Æggetoddy (Egg Toddy)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar
- Cognac, rum or arrack
- Egg yolks
- Boiling water

## Directions

1. Stir 1 to 2 egg yolks well with 3 to 4 tablespoons of ground sugar, then pour in gradually while stirring constantly

title: Ægte Majonaise (True Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 raw or hard-boiled egg yolks or preferably one of each kind
- a knife point of salt
- 1 tablespoon fine culinary oil
- juice of half a lemon
- white wine
- salt
- white pepper (optional)
- white powdered sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Stir well 2 raw or hard-boiled egg yolks or preferably one of each kind and a knife point of salt with a wooden spoon.
2. Gradually let 1 tablespoon fine culinary oil drip down drop by drop onto the eggs with constant stirring until the mixture is properly smooth.
3. Finally add juice of half a lemon, white wine and salt.
4. The sauce, which should be properly thick and smooth, is then cooled.
5. It can also have a little powdered white pepper and white powdered sugar added, but this is not necessary.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Ægte Sauce-Hollandaise (True Hollandaise Sauce)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons water
- Juice of one lemon or an equivalent amount of vinegar
- 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) cold butter
- Salt
- White pepper
- Fish broth or cream

## Directions

1. Carefully separate the whites from the yolks, which are whisked well together with 4 tablespoons water and the juice of one lemon or an equivalent amount of vinegar
2. Pour the egg mixture into a smaller saucepan, which is placed inside a larger one filled with enough boiling water to come up the sides of the inner saucepan
3. Place over heat and stir continuously in the eggs, stirring in 1/4 Kg (1/2 S) cold butter in small portions
4. When the sauce has become smooth, salt it and add white pepper and dilute with fish broth or cream until it reaches the proper consistency
5. The sauce cannot boil in this way, and in this manner one avoids it separating
6. Stir the sauce continually until it is to be served

title: Ægte Skildpaddesuppe (Genuine Turtle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turtle
- Beef and veal meat
- 2 ounces ham
- 1 hen
- Onions
- Carrots
- Celery and parsley roots
- Seasoning extract
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Salt
- ½ pint Madeira wine
- 2 bottles white wine
- Bouillon
- 3 to 4 lemons
- 40 pounds beef and veal shank (for 60-pound turtle)
- 8 onions
- 2 carrots
- 2 celeries
- 2 leeks
- Thyme
- 3 bay leaves
- Peppercorns
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Place the turtle on a straw-covered board and pull out the neck with a rope as far as possible, then cut off the head
2. If the turtle is large, allow 10 to 12 hours for the blood to drain
3. Open it with a knife inserted between the belly and back shell
4. Cut off the flippers, neck and tail as close to the body as possible
5. Remove the intestines first, then the meat and bones, and finally the fat attached to the shell
6. Cut the belly and back shells into large squares and cook for a few minutes until the fine outer shell can be removed
7. Treat the flippers, tail and head the same way; cook everything in water until the meat can be loosened from the bones, which are set aside for serving
8. Scald the greenish fat with water and cook it for 3 quarters of an hour with wine and bouillon; the reddish meat is cooked tender in the bouillon but is not served
9. Brown approximately 40 pounds of beef and veal shank in butter or good fat in a large pot together with 8 onions, 2 carrots, 2 celeries, 2 leeks, a little thyme, 3 bay leaves, some peppercorns and cloves
10. Pour it up with the bouillon in which the turtle was cooked, with the addition of salt
11. After 5 hours of cooking, remove the meat and strain the soup through a fine sieve
12. A few hours before serving, pour about 4 quarts of turtle bouillon (calculated for 12 persons) into a casserole
13. Add a hen browned together with the giblets, 3 pounds lean veal in square pieces, ¼ pound ham, cut vegetables, seasonings and parsley
14. Bring to a boil, move to a gentler heat, add ½ pint Madeira and continue cooking for 1 hour; the soup should then be clear and pale yellow in color
15. Skim and strain through a sieve into another casserole
16. Mix it with the necessary amount of turtle meat and the flippers cut into cubes
17. When it has cooked with these for 12 to 15 minutes, add a little cayenne, a glass of seasoning extract and some of the turtle fat cut into cubes
18. Pour the soup into the tureen and serve with lemon slices

title: Ærtebælge-Suppe (Pea Pod Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 4.5 L (4 pots) soup
- Pea pods
- Water
