- Bicarbonate of soda
- 125 grams (0.25 lb) butter
- 125 grams (0.25 lb) flour
- Whole cooked peas
- Sliced carrots
- Spinach juice (optional for color)
- Baked bread

## Directions

1. Cook pea pods 1/2 hour in water with a little bicarbonate of soda.
2. Place in a hay box for 4-5 hours to become very tender.
3. Press through a sieve with stock.
4. Brown 125 grams butter and 125 grams flour together and dilute with the strained soup.
5. Add some whole cooked peas and sliced carrots.
6. For a nice green color, add juice from pressed spinach strained through a sieve.
7. Serve with baked bread.

title: Ærter og Makaroni (Peas and Macaroni)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover cooked macaroni
- Green peas, fresh or preserved
- Pea water
- Butter
- Salt
- Parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover cooked macaroni very finely and mix it with an equal portion of green peas, fresh or preserved.
2. Add some of the pea water, a slice of butter, salt, and finely chopped parsley.
3. Heat the vegetables through and serve with ham.

title: Ærter å l'Anglaise (Peas à l'Anglaise)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked peas
- Cold butter

## Directions

1. Drain the water from the cooked peas.
2. Pour them into a warm ragout dish.
3. Place a slice of cold butter on top.

title: Ærter å la française (Peas à la Française)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked peas
- Melted butter
- Parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Drain the water from the cooked peas.
2. Toss them in melted butter together with a little finely chopped parsley.
3. This preparation is commonly used as a garnish around cutlets, roasts, fillets, and similar dishes.

title: Öflers-Suppe Mo. 3 (Oyster Soup No. 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups good, not very salty, melted butter
- 2 cups flour
- a little cayenne pepper
- a little salt
- 1 gallon nearly boiling milk
- 1 quart fresh oysters
- water
- cold water

## Directions

1. Stir 2 cups of melted butter with 2 cups flour, a little cayenne pepper, and a little salt
2. Gradually add 1 gallon of nearly boiling milk to the roux while stirring well to prevent lumps
3. Pour a little water over 1 quart of fresh oysters, set over good heat and simmer gently
4. Pour the water with the oyster liquor into the soup
5. Rinse the oysters in cold water to clean them of all small shells
6. Bring the soup to a boil and remove immediately from heat so it does not curdle
7. Add the oysters to the soup
8. Have everything at hand before beginning, as the soup must be stirred constantly

title: Östers i Skallerne (Oysters in the Shell)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters in shells
- Hardtack

## Directions

1. Wash the shells and bake in a hot oven; then open the shells with an oyster knife, being careful not to spill any of the juice, and arrange them quickly on warm plates with small hardtack.
2. Oysters can in the same way be baked by steam in the shells.
3. If you want to preserve oysters in the shell, they should be placed in a cool cellar, in upright ascending piles, and from time to time sprinkled with cornmeal and sprinkled with salt water.
4. If you also ensure that they do not freeze, they can keep for a long time.

title: Øllebrød (Ale Bread Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 bottles pale ale
- 3/4 L (3 Pægle) cream
- old rye and wheat bread
- lemon peel
- whole cinnamon
- smoked herring or anchovies
- raw cream

## Directions

1. Soak old rye and wheat bread (mixed if desired) for a few hours in water and cook it out.
2. Press it through a coarse sieve and dilute with the ale.
3. The ale bread soup can be cooked for a few minutes with lemon peel and whole cinnamon and sweetened to taste.
4. Serve smoked herring or anchovies and raw cream with it. Like with bread soup, the sugar can be stirred with 3 to 4 egg yolks.

title: Ørekjødpostei (Ox Cheek Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- For 5 persons: Pastry dough
- 2 pounds meat
- Flour
- 3 teaspoons pepper
- 1 crushed bay leaf
- Salt
- chopped onion and parsley, 2 teaspoons of each
- Butter
- 1 gill water

## Directions

1. Roll out the dough to 1/4 inch thickness and line a well-buttered mold with it.
2. Cut the meat into slices, pound them, dip in flour and layer them in the mold.
3. Between each layer place some of the seasonings.
4. Pour in the water, cover the mold with dough and bake in a moderately hot oven for 3 hours.
5. When done, shake it gently so that the sauce mixes, then turn out the pâté.
6. Can be made from any kind of meat.

title: Øresteg tillavet som Vildt (Beef Prepared as Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef from the hind leg
- Vinegar
- Bacon strips
- Butter
- Brandy
- Cream

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef from the hind leg, pound it well on all sides, and place it in vinegar overnight.
2. Remove it and dry it, but do not stuff it, and then lard it densely with bacon strips.
3. Brown butter in a pot large enough to accommodate the meat, which should be appropriately sized in proportion to the meat.
4. Brown the meat in the butter, add a little brandy to the butter, and cook it gently until it is tender.
5. When done, stir a little cream into the sauce.
6. For tough and lean meat, you can cut it into elongated pieces about two fingers thick and treat them in the same way.

title: Østers au naturel i Gratinskaller (Oysters au Naturel in Gratin Shells)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Lemon pieces
- Salt
- Endive salad, parsley or maidenhair fern for garnish

## Directions

1. Calculate six oysters per person for dinner service
2. Serve either after the soup or begin the meal with them
3. Arrange in gratin shells in their juice
4. Place the shell on a medium or dessert plate on which a small napkin has been laid
5. Garnish with lemon pieces and a salt cellar
6. Decorate with endive salad, parsley or maidenhair fern

title: Østers i Skaller (Oysters in Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large shells (Coquille shells)
- Oysters
- Bread crumbs
- White wine
- Lemon juice
- Butter
- Cooked and boned Cod (optional)

## Directions

1. Place 4 oysters in each large shell, with the beard removed.
2. Add a little bread crumbs, white wine, a few drops of lemon juice and a little butter to each shell.
3. Cover with a thin layer of bread crumbs with small pieces of butter on top.
4. Keep the oyster liquid in the shells.
5. Bake in the oven for about a quarter hour until the bread crust is a nice light brown.
6. Optionally, the above ingredients can be mixed with a little fine, cooked and boned cod.
7. Cook the beards in a little water, strain it and store in bottles for use in oyster sauce.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Østers Pie (Oyster Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Puff pastry
- Large oysters
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks, finely chopped
- Bread crumbs
- 1/2 pint cream
- Flour

## Directions

1. Butter a large pie dish and line the sides and corners with puff pastry.
2. Use the largest and finest oysters possible and strain all liquid from them.
3. Season the oysters with pepper, salt, and butter.
4. Place them on the dish with as much of their own sauce as desired, and sprinkle with the chopped hard-boiled egg yolks and grated bread crumbs.
5. Cover with a pastry lid and press the edges with a fork.
6. Bake at high heat.
7. You may also add 1/2 pint of cream mixed with a little flour.

title: Østers à la Crème (Oysters in Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart oysters
- 1 pint cream
- flour
- cold milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place the oysters in an enameled pot and boil until the oyster liquid begins to flow out.
2. Strain the oysters and place the cream over the fire.
3. When the cream is nearly boiling, thicken it with flour mixed in a little cold milk.
4. Add the oysters and boil for five minutes.
5. Serve hot with toast.

title: Østers, raa (Raw Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Serve raw oysters with lemon slices.
2. They look best when they stay on the flat shell when opened, but the liquid is better when left in the deep shell.
3. They must be fresh; this is known by the shells being tightly closed.
4. They are only good in winter.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land

title: Østers-Salat (Oyster Salad)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauce (as per Nr. 13)
- Raw oysters
- Cooked cod
- Lemon slices
- Watercress

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as Nr. 13.
2. Cut the raw oysters in half and add them along with a little cooked cod to the sauce when it is cold.
3. Place some whole oysters and lemon slices on top of the salad.
4. Garnish with a little watercress.

title: Østers-Sauce (Oyster Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Water or thin Soup
- Butter
- Flour
- White Wine
- Lemon Juice
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Meat Soup
- small Fish balls
- Chestnuts
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Remove the beards from the Oysters, boil them very briefly in a little Water or thin Soup and strain.
2. Melt a piece of Butter, stir the Flour into it, and thin it with the strained Soup, together with white Wine, a little Lemon Juice, Salt, Nutmeg and so much Meat Soup that it becomes appropriately thick.
3. Add the Oysters last, and they must not be boiled, but only become warm.
4. Very small Fish balls may be added, which must be boiled in the Soup before it is used for the Sauce.
5. Boiled and peeled Chestnuts may also be added together with the Oysters.
6. Thicken it with a little Egg yolk.

title: Østers-Suppe (Oyster Soup)
author: Kristine Marie Jensen
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 oysters
- 3 L soup stock
- 2 L boiling water
- oyster liquid and beards
- 125 gr butter
- 125 gr flour
- lemon juice
- fish, veal or spare soup
- 6 egg yolks
- Rhine wine
- small fish balls (optional)

## Directions

1. Open the oysters and collect the water from the shells in a large saucepan along with the removed beards
2. Place the oysters in a colander set over the saucepan containing the beards
3. Pour 2 L boiling water over the oysters and place them in a tureen
4. Cook the beards for a few minutes with a little lemon juice in the oyster water
5. Strain the soup and mix with 3 L fish, veal or spare soup
6. Brown 125 gr butter and 125 gr flour together and thin with the soup
7. When it boils, thicken it with 6 beaten egg yolks and season with Rhine wine
8. Pour the boiling soup over the oysters in the tureen
9. Serve with Butter dough slices or Simels

title: Østers-Suppe No. 1 (Oyster Soup No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart water
- 1 teacup butter
- 1 pint milk
- 2 teaspoons coarse salt
- 0.5 teaspoon pepper
- 1 quart oysters

## Directions

1. Let the whole come to a rapid boil and then add one quart of oysters, let it boil rapidly again and then remove it from the fire.

title: Østers-Suppe No. 2 (Oyster Soup No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart boiling water
- 1 quart good milk
- 1 teacup of grated crackers
- Pepper and salt to taste
- 1 quart good, fresh oysters
- 1 piece of good butter the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Mix one quart of boiling water in the pot with one quart of good milk; stir in one teacup of grated crackers along with a little pepper and salt to taste.
2. When it boils, add one quart of good, fresh oysters; you must stir so that it does not scorch.
3. Finally add a piece of good butter the size of an egg, and as soon as it boils up, the soup is ready and is removed from the fire.

title: Østers-Suppe No. 3 (Oyster Soup No. 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups good, not very salty melted butter
- 2 cups flour
- A little Cayenne pepper
- A little salt
- 1 gallon nearly boiling milk
- 1 quart fresh oysters
- Water

## Directions

1. In 2 cups of good, not very salty melted butter, stir 2 cups of flour, a little Cayenne pepper and a little salt.
2. Pour in one gallon of nearly boiling milk, a little at a time; stir well so that the flour does not lump.
3. Pour a little water over 1 quart of fresh oysters, place it over a good fire and skim clean; strain this water with the oyster liquid into the soup, then rinse the oysters in cold water to free them from all small shells.
4. The soup gets a boil and is then immediately removed from the fire so that it does not curdle, and the oysters are added to it.
5. Everything should be at hand before you begin, as you must constantly stir in the soup.

title: Østerssauce (Oyster Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 oysters with their beards
- 18 quint butter
- 12 quint flour
- oyster liquor
- white wine
- lemon juice
- soup or broth
- 2 egg yolks (optional for binding)

## Directions

1. Set the beards of 30 oysters along with all the liquor collected carefully to boil, then strain.
2. Stir 18 quint butter with 12 quint flour, and pour the oyster liquor onto it with white wine, a little lemon juice and enough soup so the whole becomes properly smooth.
3. Add the 30 oysters and warm through without allowing to boil.
4. The sauce may be bound with 2 egg yolks.
source_title: Illustreret kogebog for by og land
